english_verse: Theophilus, I first wrote to you about all that Jesus did and taught from the very first
translation_verse: Teofilo gɛ ɛdɛmɛ nɔ, nɛ mɛ keplukpa kukuɔ mɛɛ, ɛfɔnɔ kɛkpɛ gɛ Yesu akpɛ yɛaxɔ̃ɛ nɛ̀ bɔdɔ̃ kakasɛ esi

english_verse: until he was taken up to heaven. But before he was taken up, he gave orders to the apostles he had chosen with the help of the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: flogo tɛ bebe feke yɛ ewunu avɛnɔ kititíɛ, akɔ banunɔ gɛ ebú asɛ yɛanɔkpɛkpɛ akɔ ɔsha ɔgana Ɔfɔfuĩa gbangbã mɛ.

english_verse: While he was still with them, he said: Don't leave Jerusalem yet. Wait here for the Father to give you the Holy Spirit, just as I told you he has promised to do.
translation_verse: Kibeɛ tinikpo gɛ azaŋã bɔdɔ̃ nɛ̀ alɛɛ, akɔ alɛ ɔsha ɔlɛ asɛ, “Nɔtɛshɛ nɛ Yerusalem, pɔ nɔzã tɛ nozopo kɛgɛ Mɛakaa atɛɖɔ elu asɛ abakɔ pɛ ɛzɛɖɔ tseɖe wɔnɔɔ.

english_verse: John baptized with water, but in a few days you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit. Jesus Is Taken to Heaven
translation_verse: Bulĩ Yohanes alɔkɔ aakpɛ Owusoso Akaa bulĩ nɛ kɛtakpu akɔ banunɔ, pɔ nɛ ewui gulũi teli gɛ abà mɛɛ, Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã babà lɔkɔ akpɛ bulĩɛ akɔ wɔnɔ xɔnɔ nɔ̀.”

english_verse: While the apostles were still with Jesus, they asked him, “Lord, are you now going to give Israel its own king again?”
translation_verse: Gɛ bazatyɔaa, bebisi yɛasɛ, “Apalete, kibè ɛlɛ mɛ wobodzinɔ bukusiŋaŋã ɔkɔ Israelanɛ dza?”

english_verse: Jesus said to them, “You don't need to know the time of those events that only the Father controls.
translation_verse: Yesu ebuiliulũ akɔ alɛ asɛ, “Wɔnɔ xɔnɔɔ, kɛma ɔsɛ tɛ̀ noti kibeɛ alo ewuiɛ gɛ Akaa atɔɖɔ elu nɛ yɛohumi nulũɔ.

english_verse: But the Holy Spirit will come upon you and give you power. Then you will tell everyone about me in Jerusalem, in all Judea, in Samaria, and everywhere in the world.”
translation_verse: Onupɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa gbangbã ɔba wɔnɔ abhaa, nɔbɔ yɛkɛ ohumi, kɛlɛ nobo dzi banɔ gɛ nɔbɔ mɔ ebui alɛ ɔɖɔ nɛ mɛishu nɛ Yerusalem nɛ̀ Yudea petee ɔvɛnɔ Samaria ɔdɛtɔ̃ kanɔ nɔ̀ ɔtɔ̃kɔ.”

english_verse: After Jesus had said this and while they were watching, he was taken up into a cloud. They could not see him,
translation_verse: Gɛ aɖɔ ebui alɛ ebekeɛ, kɛdɔ̃ tili bafeke yɛewunɔ ɔvɛnɔ kititíɛ nɛ balɛ amɛ, kɛlɛ ɔmɔlɔ toli obokpũi.

english_verse: but as he went up, they kept looking up into the sky. Suddenly two men dressed in white clothes were standing there beside them.
translation_verse: Pɔ gɛ batsyɔɛ awuhũɔ akpɛ kɛlɛɛ nɔ mɛ gɛ avɛɛ, nɛ balɛ matimati mɛɛ, ɔmɔ banyɛ taabha, gɛ bazã bɔdɔ̃ kpɛkpɛ frɛrɛ mɛ, pɛ bebeli itití nɛ alɛ ɔbha.

english_verse: They said, “Why are you men from Galilee standing here and looking up into the sky? Jesus has been taken to heaven. But he will come back in the same way you have seen him go.” Someone To Take the Place of Judas
translation_verse: Pɛ baɖɔ asɛ, “Galileanɛ, kiɖe gɛkɔ ɔsɛ nozhioli pɛ noɖi kɛlɛɛ nɔ mɛ ana dza? Yesu alɛ gɛ befeke ashɛnɔ nɛ wɔnɔ ɔbha avɛnɔ kititiɛ, aza babà asɛ kɛmɔ gɛ nɔmɔ yɛ pɛ avɛ kititíɛ ana.” Bebu Matia Akpɛ Yuda Yɛinye Mɛ

english_verse: The Mount of Olives was about a kilometer from Jerusalem. The apostles who had gone there were Peter, John, James, Andrew, Philip, Thomas, Bartholomew, Matthew, James the son of Alphaeus, Simon, known as the Eager One, and Judas the son of James. After the apostles returned to the city, they went upstairs to the room where they had been staying.
translation_verse: Gɛ bediku ɔma mɛɛ, bewu avɛ kabha buvũɔ mɛ gɛ bazazãa. Bagɛ bazã ɛkɛlɛ balɛ enu Petro, Yohanes, Yakobo nɛ̀ Andrea, Filipo nɛ̀ Toma, Bartolomeo nɛ̀ Mateo, Yakobo, Alfeo yɛeyiɛ nɛ̀ Simon, Ishuahɔɛmɛ zazã pɛpɛelete Kɛzamɛanɔ, nɛ̀ Yuda Yakobo yɛeyiɛ.

english_verse: The apostles often met together and prayed with a single purpose in mind. The women and Mary the mother of Jesus would meet with them, and so would his brothers.
translation_verse: Banɔ alɛ nyɔ̃ɔ abhɛtɛ tenikpo nɛ Owusoso Akaa tikukulu mɛ kibe nyia be ɔplɛnɔ badzɛ, nɛ̀ Maria gɛenu Yesu yɛelĩ nɛ̀ yɛadzya alɛ.

english_verse: One day there were about
translation_verse: Nɛ ewui alɛ mɛɛ, Petro alɔɛtití nɛ bayɛyɛkɛŋã nɛishi

english_verse: Then Judas bought some land with the money he was given for doing that evil thing. He fell headfirst into the field. His body burst open, and all his insides came out.
translation_verse: (Yuda alɔkɔ kɛbhɛ gɛ babuia akɔɛ nɛ yɛ kɛtakpu mɛ gagalĩɛ ebuiɛ shuɔ edzi kesiakpa nɔ, nɛ ɛkɛlɛ yɛeditsu oliesi, ata yɛɛ kɛlɛ yɛyamɛ bɔdɔ̃ tafala nɔ nɛ ɛkɛlɛ.

english_verse: When the people of Jerusalem found out about this, they called the place Akeldama, which in the local language means “Field of Blood.”
translation_verse: Anɔ nyia anɔ nɛ Yerusalem elũ kɛgɛ ba ɛlɛ mɛ. Kliso bakɔ kesiakpa alɛ ninye asɛ, “Akeldama” gɛ kɛlɛ esi kɛlɛinu ɔsɛ, Dzyosu Kesiakpa.)

english_verse: In the book of Psalms it says, “Leave his house empty, and don't let anyone live there.” It also says, “Let someone else have his job.”
translation_verse: Petro aɖɔ asɛ, “Ɔmɔsɛ bafɔnɔ elu nɛ ɛdzɛ zhazhã keplukpa mɛ elu asɛ, “ ‘Yɛɔpã badzi bɔpã yɛyɛɛ, anɔ nanà atɛzã bɔlɛ mɛ,’ kɛlɛ, “ ‘Anɔ bhlatɔ abayɛkɛ yɛ banɔlũazazã ɔzãkɔɔ.’

english_verse: So we need someone else to help us tell others that Jesus has been raised from death. He must also be one of the men who was with us from the very beginning. He must have been with us from the time the Lord Jesus was baptized by John until the day he was taken to heaven.
translation_verse: odenɔ kibèɛ gɛ Yohanes azakpɛ bulĩɛ ɔvɛnɔ kibeɛ gɛ befeke Yesu avɛnɔ kititíɛ. Ɔmɔsɛ kɛlɛ ɔsɛ tɛ banɔ alɛ ɔmayɛmɛ tenikpo edzi anɔ gɛ abaplɛnɔ pɛ bɔbàɖɔ ebuiɛ nɛ Yesu yɛ tɛlɔlɔ odenɔ bekpuɔ mɛ shu.

english_verse: Two men were suggested: One of them was Joseph Barsabbas, known as Justus, and the other was Matthias.
translation_verse: Kliso bedzu kɛwɔɛ nɛ banyɛ taabha abha, balɛenu, Yosef, gɛ bezekulu tsyɛ asɛ Barsaba, nɛ̀ Matia.

english_verse: Then they all prayed, “Lord, you know what everyone is like! Show us the one you have chosen
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ bekulu Akaa aɖɔ asɛ, “Apalete wonyi anɔ nyia anɔ yitúɔ mɛ. Kasɛ ɔlɔ banɔ alɛ ɔmayɛmɛ nɔnɔ gɛ obuɔ

english_verse: to be an apostle and to serve in place of Judas, who got what he deserved.”
translation_verse: ɔsɛ tɛ abavɛ banɔkpɛkpɛ axɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa nabha gɛ Yuda ashɛnɛ ahɔɛ elú pɛ avɛ ɛkɛlɛ gɛ ashɔnɔ asɛ tɛ avɛɛ.

english_verse: They drew names, and Matthias was chosen to join the group of the eleven apostles.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ batá kebisì, kɛlɛ kadɔ Matia abha, kliso balɔkɔɛ aplɛnɔ banɔkpɛkpɛ lɔfɔ nɛ̀ toliɛ.

english_verse: On the day of Pentecost all the Lord's followers were together in one place.
translation_verse: Gɛ Pentekoste kiwuiɛ baa, balɛ petee bazã kitsù nɛ ɔkɔ tonikpo.

english_verse: Suddenly there was a noise from heaven like the sound of a mighty wind! It filled the house where they were meeting.
translation_verse: Nɛ balɛ matìmatì mɛ, ozĩ oyì asɛ kɛlɛɛ gagalĩi tifufù ana odenɔ kititíɛ pɛ kɛyɛkɛ bɔpã petee mɛ gɛ bazãa.

english_verse: The Holy Spirit took control of everyone, and they began speaking whatever languages the Spirit let them speak.
translation_verse: Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã otsoku balɛ pete mɛ, gɛ kɛkɔ ɔsɛ bakpà beluhũ ediedi uhu asɛ gɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔkɔ balɛ kidzo ana.

english_verse: Many religious Jews from every country in the world were living in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Nɛ kibè ɛlɛ, Yudanɛ teli gɛ badɛmɛ nɔ nɛ̀ Owusoso Akaa bede ɛmadi ka nyia ka mɛ nɛ kesiakpa nabha abazã Yerusalem.

english_verse: And when they heard this noise, a crowd gathered. But they were surprised, because they were hearing everything in their own languages.
translation_verse: Gɛ belũ ozĩ ɔlɛɛ, banɔɛza gbagbla tili bashogò kɛlɛ kɛbhɛtɛ balɛibi, ɔmɔsɛ anɔ nyia anɔ elũ ebuiɛ gɛ baɖɔɔ nɛ yɛulũhũ mɛ.

english_verse: They were excited and amazed, and said: Don't all these who are speaking come from Galilee?
translation_verse: Gɛ kɛbhɛtɛ balɛ kibi soɔ, bebisi asɛ, bagɛ baɖɔaɖɔ alɛ nyɔ̃ɔ nɔ pɔ, anɛ Galelianɛ benuɔ mɔ dza?

english_verse: Then why do we hear them speaking our very own languages?
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ kɛzã pɛ bɔlɔ ɔmayɛmɛ aka nyia aka elũ ebuiɛ mɛ nɛ yɛulũhũɔ mɛ̀ dza?

english_verse: Some of us are from Parthia, Media, and Elam. Others are from Mesopotamia, Judea, Cappadocia, Pontus, Asia,
translation_verse: Bededeba ede Partia, Media, nɛ̀ Elam, beteli ede Mesopotamia, Yudea nɛ̀ Kapadokia, Pontio nɛ̀ Asia,

english_verse: Everyone was excited and confused. Some of them even kept asking each other, “What does all this mean?”
translation_verse: Gɛ kɛbhɛtɛ balɛ ibi pɛ betinyi kɛgɛ babà bhɛtɛ soɔ, bebisi bagɔlɔ asɛ, “Kibui ɛlɛ niesi sɛ ɛlɛ?”

english_verse: Others made fun of the Lord's followers and said, “They are drunk.” Peter Speaks to the Crowd
translation_verse: Beteli xɔnɔ aaɖɔ amɔɛbui nɛ balɛishu kɛlɛ baɖɔ asɛ, “Bunu bawɔ̃ɛ ebuku. Petro Aɖɔ Kaɖɔ Akɔ Banɔɛza

english_verse: Peter stood with the eleven apostles and spoke in a loud and clear voice to the crowd: Friends and everyone else living in Jerusalem, listen carefully to what I have to say!
translation_verse: Petro nɛ̀ yɛɔhayɛ lɔfɔ nɛ̀ toliɛ alɔɛtití pɛ aɖɔaɖɔ etseɖe banɔɛza asɛ, “Mɛɔhayɛ Yudanɛ nɛ̀ wɔnɔ bagɛ alɛ Yerusalemuɔ petee, nɔkɔ mɛibu ebui alɛ esi ɔkɔ wɔnɔ, nɔtaatɔ tɛ̀ nolũ kɛgɛ ɛbaɖɔ tseɖe wɔnɔ piapii.

english_verse: You are wrong to think that these people are drunk. After all, it is only nine o'clock in the morning.
translation_verse: Gɛnɛ bunu banɔ alɛ adɔ asɛ gɛ noɖiɛ, kɛvɔlɔ ɛɖa tizhita nɛlɛ ko gɛta.

english_verse: But this is what God told the prophet Joel to say,
translation_verse: Ao, kibui ɛlɛ baɖɔ ɔgana ebuiɖɔluanɔ Yoel abha asɛ,

english_verse: “When the last days come, I will give my Spirit to everyone. Your sons and daughters will prophesy. Your young men will see visions, and your old men will have dreams.
translation_verse: “ ‘Owusoso Akaa aɖɔ asɛ, nɛ ewui gɔgɔɛ mɛɛ, mɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ ɔbɔdɔ banunɔ petee abha. Wɔnɔ beyianyɛ nɛ̀ beyiadzɛ abaɖɔ ebui elu, wɔnɔ bayɛsɔ̃ abamɔ ebui nɛ bɔlala mɛ, wɔnɔ banɔ sheshèɛ babaza balalá.

english_verse: In those days I will give my Spirit to my servants, both men and women, and they will prophesy.
translation_verse: Ɛbalɔkɔ mɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ ɔkɔ maxɔ̃ɛdɛ gɛenu banyɛ nɛ̀ badzɛ tsyɛ, nɛ ewui alɛ mɛ, kɛlɛ babàɖɔ ebui elú.

english_verse: “I will work miracles in the sky above and wonders on the earth below. There will be blood and fire and clouds of smoke.
translation_verse: Ɛbabu bɔdɔ̃ gɛbhɛtɛ kibi ɔkasɛ baba mɔ nɛ kititíɛ, ɛbabu ebui ɔkasɛ nɛ kesiakpa nɛ̀ kesiakpa niesi, dzyosu, kifu nɛ̀ efutù gɛibù ɔnyɔ̃.

english_verse: The sun will turn dark, and the moon will be as red as blood before the great and wonderful day of the Lord appears.
translation_verse: Kiwuiɛ batsyina dzi otumũ kɛlɛ woele nɔ ebetsyina edzi dzyosu, flogo pɛ Apaleteɛ yɛiwui gbagbla nɛ̀ kiwui dadalɛ banà.

english_verse: Then the Lord will save everyone who asks for his help.”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ aka nyia aka gɛekulu Apalete neinyeɛ, babayɛkɛ yɛ elú nɛ̀ ɛkpã.

english_verse: Now, listen to what I have to say about Jesus from Nazareth. God proved he sent Jesus to you by having him work miracles, wonders, and signs. All of you know this.
translation_verse: “Israelanɛ, nɔtaatɔ̃ tɛ noɔlũ kibui ɛlɛ, Yesu Nazareteyiɛ enu anɔ gɛ Owusoso Akaa alɔkɔ akpɛ ahɔɛ akɔ wɔnɔ ɔsɛ tɛ̀ abhɛtɛ atsyindzye ebui, ebui gɛ labhɛtɛ kibì, tɛ̀ ebù ebui akasɛ, ebui alɛ enu ebui gɛ Owusoso Akaa abhɛtɛ nɛ wɔnɔɛshi ɔgana yɛabha asɛ gɛ nonyi ana.

english_verse: God had already planned and decided that Jesus would be handed over to you. So you took him and had evil men put him to death on a cross.
translation_verse: Owusoso Akaa yɛalɔkɔ anyɛ alɛ akpɛ ahɔɛ akɔ wɔnɔ nɛ yɛ tɛpɛpɛ nɛ̀ kɛgɛ apɛ asɛ tɛ̀ kɛba ɛlɛ mɛ̀ nulũ. Kɛlɛ wɔnɔɔ, atakpu mɛ gagalĩ elete aplɛnɔ ɔnɔ pɛ noyii ɔganà yɛ tɛkpakpalà nɛ osi shu.

english_verse: But God set him free from death and raised him to life. Death could not hold him in its power.
translation_verse: Pɔ Owusoso Akaa efeke yɛ odenɔ bekpuɔ mɛ, kɛlɛ ebui aganà butsíɛ bɔlɛ bɔtɔ mɔmɔ mɛ, ɔmɔsɛ butsí gɛmɔ kidzo tɛ bɔkɔ yɛawɔ bɔlɛ ahɔɛ mɛ.

english_verse: What David said are really the words of Jesus, “I always see the Lord near me, and I will not be afraid with him at my right side.
translation_verse: David aɖɔ elu nɛishu asɛ, “ ‘Ɛɛmɔ̀ Apaleteɛ nɛ mɛotugbà kibe nyiɛ be. Ɔmɔsɛ alɛ mɛ kɛŋã mɛ, Kifù tɛbà yimɛ.

english_verse: Because of this, my heart will be glad, my words will be joyful, and I will live in hope.
translation_verse: Kliso mɛ kitúɔ mɔ same, kɛlɛ mɛinekeɛ zhã same ɛdzɛ, kɛlɛ mɛishudzya baɖi otugbà mɛ tɛpɛpɛ,

english_verse: The Lord won't leave me in the grave. I am his holy one, and he won't let my body decay.
translation_verse: ɔmɔsɛ ɔtɛbɔ shɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ mɛishu ɔkɔ butsi alo pɛ ɔbɔkɔ ɔsɛ wɔ ayɛ gɛ yɛishu dalɛ atsɔmɔ ezĩ.

english_verse: He has shown me the path to life, and he makes me glad by being near me.”
translation_verse: Wɔkɔ mɛitì ɛkpã kidzoɔ, ɔbɔkɔ ɔsɛ same eyukù mɛ nɛ wɔamɛ.’

english_verse: My friends, it is right for me to speak to you about our ancestor David. He died and was buried, and his tomb is still here.
translation_verse: “Mɛadzya alɛ mɔ, ɛpɛ sɛ tɛ mɛdalɛ mɛishumɛ ɔkɔ wɔnɔ tɛ̀ noti ɔsɛ ashuĩ David etsí, kɛlɛ bavɔbɔɛ, kɛlɛ yɛodzeɖe ɔlɛ ɛkɛlɛ̀ ɔmana oboɔ.

english_verse: But David was a prophet, and he knew that God had made a promise he would not break. He had told David someone from his own family would someday be king.
translation_verse: Pɔ edzì ebuiɖɔluanɔ gɛetì asɛ Owusoso Akaa atanà bɔdɔ̃ alɔkɔ atɔɖɔ kibui elú akɔɛ asɛ abatɔɖɔ yɛeshui teli nɛ yɛukusi kegbu nabha.

english_verse: David knew this would happen, and so he told us Christ would be raised to life. He said God would not leave him in the grave or let his body decay.
translation_verse: Gɛ David amɔ kɛgɛ batɔɖɔ elu akɔɛ soɔ, aɖɔaɖɔ nɛ Kristo yɛ tɛlɔlɔ nɛ bekpuɔ mɛ shu asɛ, “Batɛ bà shɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ Kristoɔ shu nɛ bekpuɔ mɛ alo pɛ babàkɔ asɛ tɛ atsɔmɔ.

english_verse: All of us can tell you that God has raised Jesus to life!
translation_verse: Owusoso Akaa efeke Yesu alɛ edenɔ bekpuɔ mɛ amana ɛkpã mɛ gɛ bɔlɔ pete zãmɔ kibui ɛlɛ ɔsɛ kɛba ɛlɛ mɛ kɛgbã.

english_verse: Jesus was taken up to sit at the right side of God, and he was given the Holy Spirit, just as the Father had promised. Jesus is also the one who has given the Spirit to us, and this is what you are now seeing and hearing.
translation_verse: Gɛ befeke yɛavɛnɔ Owusoso Akaa kɛŋãa mɛ ebekeɛ, ayɛkɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã gɛ batɔɖɔ elu akɔɛ odenɔ Akaa ɔkɔ kɛlɛ yɛtsyɛ ashɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ ɔlɛ shu asɛ gɛ nɔmɔ pɛ nolũɔ ana.

english_verse: David didn't go up to heaven. So he wasn't talking about himself when he said, “The Lord told my Lord to sit at his right side,
translation_verse: David agɛvɛ kitití, pɔ aɖɔ asɛ, “Apaleteɛ etseɖe Mɛapalete asɛ, “Zã kesi nɛ mɛ kɛŋãa mɛ

english_verse: until he made my Lord's enemies into a footstool for him.”
translation_verse: ɔvɛnɔ kibeɛ gɛ ɛba lɔkɔ bagɛ atɛ adɛmɛ wɔɛbuiɛ tɛ bedzi kɛdɔ̃ gɛ ɔbɔtɔɖɔ ɛtsɛɖɛ nɛ ɛlɛ abha.” ’

english_verse: Everyone in Israel should then know for certain that God has made Jesus both Lord and Christ, even though you put him to death on a cross.
translation_verse: “Kliso kɔ Israelanɛ beti kɛlɛ piapii asɛ, Owusoso Akaa abhɛtɛ Yesu alɛ̀ gɛ nɔkpalà nɛ butsi osiɛ shu yɛedzi Apalete nɛ̀ Kristo.”

english_verse: When the people heard this, they were very upset. They asked Peter and the other apostles, “Friends, what should we do?”
translation_verse: Gɛ banunɔ elũ kibui ɛlɛ, kibhui balɛ kitúɔ ohui, kɛlɛ betseɖe Petro nɛ̀ banɔkpɛkpɛ gɔgɔɛ nɔ asɛ, “Mɛadzya alɛ mɔ, kiɖe bɔbabhɛtɛ?”

english_verse: Peter said to them, “Turn to God and be baptized, every one of you, in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins, and you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Petro ebuiliulũ asɛ, “Notsyina etúɔ mɛ, tɛ bakpɛ bulĩ akɔ wɔnɔ ɔmayɛmɛ ka nyia ka nɛ Yesu Kristo yɛinye mɛ̀, tɛ balɔkɔ wɔnɔ ebui kpa etsye wɔnɔ. Kɛlɛ nɔbɔ yɛkɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã ɛdɔ̃ kɔkɔ.

english_verse: This promise is for you and your children. It is for everyone our Lord God will choose, no matter where they live.”
translation_verse: Tɛtɔtɔɖɔ ɛlɛ lɛ ɔkɔ wɔnɔ nɛ̀ wɔnɔ beyi alɛ nɛ̀ bagɛ balɛ kitsí fãa nɔ, gɛ benu banɔ nɔ nyɔ̃ɔ gɛ bɔlɔ Apaleteɛ Owusoso Akaa ebekuluɔ.”

english_verse: Peter told them many other things as well. Then he said, “I beg you to save yourselves from what will happen to all these evil people.”
translation_verse: Alɔkɔ ebui bhlatɔ etseɖe nɔ nɛ̀ balɛ, kɛlɛ akɔ balɛ kɛtakpu asɛ, “Nobu wɔnɔ ishu odenɔ kibè kpa kanɔ alɛ mɛ.”

english_verse: They spent their time learning from the apostles, and they were like family to each other. They also broke bread and prayed together. Life among the Lord's Followers
translation_verse: Balɔkɔ balɛishu ashɔkpɛ banɔkpɛkpɛ bɔdɔ̃ kakasɛ nɛ̀ ɔha kpɛkpɛ mɛ, ɔvɛnɔ kifufulũɔ tɛyaya nɛ̀ Owusoso Akaa tikukulu mɛ.

english_verse: Everyone was amazed by the many miracles and wonders that the apostles worked.
translation_verse: Kifu zã anɔ nyia anɔ mɛ, kɛlɛ banɔkpɛkpɛ abhɛtɛ atsyindzye ebui nɛ̀ ebui gɛ labhɛtɛ kibì gelee

english_verse: All the Lord's followers often met together, and they shared everything they had.
translation_verse: Bagɛ ayɛkɛ aŋã petee eshogò kɛlɛ bakpɛ ebuiɛ petee tinikpo abhɛtɛ.

english_verse: They would sell their property and possessions and give the money to whoever was in need.
translation_verse: Badɛ̀ balɛ ahɔɛmɛ bɔdɔ̃, kɛlɛ balɔkɔ akɔ aka nyia aka asɛ kɛpɛɛ gɛ apɛɛ ulũ.

english_verse: Day after day they met together in the temple. They broke bread together in different homes and shared their food happily and freely,
translation_verse: Bazatɔ̃ kɛtakpu nɛ Owusoso Akaavũɔ shu kibe nyia be. Bazaŋã bɔdɔ̃ nɛ kesugba tienikpo mɛ nɛ balɛ bapãmɛ nɛ same nɛ̀ etú dadalɛ mɛ,

english_verse: while praising God. Everyone liked them, and each day the Lord added to their group others who were being saved.
translation_verse: pɛ bazakpɛ Owusoso Akaa nɛinyè nɛitití kɛlɛ balɛ ebuiɛ ezeku banunɔ petee awɔ̃. Kɛlɛ Apaleteɛ akɔ bagɛ ayɛkɛ elú nɛkpã abaplɛnɔ balɛ bekpè atɔɖɔ kiwui nyia wui.

english_verse: The time of prayer was about three o'clock in the afternoon, and Peter and John were going into the temple.
translation_verse: Kiwui tinikpo Petro nɛ̀ Yohanes baavɛ Akaavũɔ mɛ, nɛ kibeɛ gɛ bekulu Owusoso Akaa-gɛenu kemuze ɛɖa tɛta mɛ ana.

english_verse: A man who had been born lame was being carried to the temple door. Each day he was placed beside this door, known as the Beautiful Gate. He sat there and begged from the people who were going in.
translation_verse: Anyɛ teli gɛenu kɛkpatà odenɔ yɛmɔbèɛ, banɔ teli alɔkɔ yɛ ededzu akaa buvũɔ niedulũɔ gɛ bekulu asɛ Kedulũ Gbangbã, ɛkɛlɛ bedzu yɛ kibe nyia bepɛ ebisi bagɛ bavɛ Owusoso Akaavũɔ mɛ bɔdɔ̃.

english_verse: The man saw Peter and John entering the temple, and he asked them for money.
translation_verse: Gɛ amɔ Petro nɛ̀ Yohanes gɛ bavɛ editsoku Akaavũɔ mɛɛ, ebisi balɛ asɛ tɛ bakɔɛ ehoeyi.

english_verse: But they looked straight at him and said, “Look up at us!”
translation_verse: Petro nɛ̀ Yohanes belu awɔ̃ eɖii, kɛlɛ Petro etseɖe yɛasɛ, “Ɖi bɔlɔ!”

english_verse: The man stared at them and thought he was going to get something.
translation_verse: Kliso anyɛ etsyina yɛamɛ akpɛ balɛ ɔkɔ, pɛ eɖi kidzo mɛ asɛ babà kɔyɛ kɛdɔ̃ tili.

english_verse: But Peter said, “I don't have any silver or gold! But I will give you what I do have. In the name of Jesus Christ from Nazareth, get up and start walking.”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ Petro etseɖe yɛasɛ, “Sikãfufú nɛ̀ sikã ama mahɔɛ pɛ ɛbakɔ wɔ, pɔ kɛgɛ lɛ mahɔɛ mɛba kɔwɔ. Nɛ Yesu Kristo Nazareteyiɛ nɛinyeɛ mɛɛ, lɔgã.”

english_verse: Peter then took him by the right hand and helped him up. At once the man's feet and ankles became strong,
translation_verse: Gɛ Petro anyana yɛŋã ahɔɛɛ, aplɛnii pɛ alɔ, odunũ ɔlɛ koɔ, yɛtsɛɖɛ nɛ̀ bɔtsɛɖɛkɔ̀ mɛ gagalĩ.

english_verse: and he jumped up and started walking. He went with Peter and John into the temple, walking and jumping and praising God.
translation_verse: Etso epoele nɛ yɛtsɛɖɛ nabha kɛlɛ akpàa gã. Kɛlɛ atɔnɔ alɛ avɛ Owusoso Akaavũɔ shu pɛ aagã, pɛ etsopoele pɛ akpɛ Owusoso Akaa nɛinyè nɛitití.

english_verse: Everyone saw him walking around and praising God.
translation_verse: Gɛ banunɔ nyɔ̃ɔ amɔɛ gɛ agãfala pɛ akpɛ Owusoso Akaa nɛinye nɛ kititiɛ,

english_verse: They knew he was the beggar who had been lying beside the Beautiful Gate, and they were completely surprised. They could not imagine what had happened to the man. Peter Speaks in the Temple
translation_verse: babakà asɛ yɛedzi anyɛ gɛ azazã kedulũɔ gɛ beekulu asɛ kedulũ Gbangbã pɛ ezebisi bɔdɔ̃, kɛlɛ kɛbhɛtɛ balɛibi nɛ kɛgɛ ba yɛabha shu. Petro Aɖɔ Kaɖɔ Ɔkɔ Banɔɛzaa

english_verse: While the man kept holding on to Peter and John, the whole crowd ran to them in amazement at the place known as Solomon's Porch.
translation_verse: Kibeɛ gɛ anyɛ gɛ ezebisi bɔdɔ̃ atanyanã Petro nɛ̀ Yohanesiɛ, kɛbhɛtɛ banɔ nɔ petee ibi, kɛlɛ balɛ petee bu tsɛtsɛɖɛ mɛ avɛ balɛ ɔbha, nɛ ɛkɛlɛ gɛ beekulu asɛ, “Salomo Kagba mɛ.”

english_verse: Peter saw that a crowd had gathered, and he said: Friends, why are you surprised at what has happened? Why are you staring at us? Do you think we have some power of our own? Do you think we were able to make this man walk because we are so religious?
translation_verse: Gɛ Petro amɔ kibui ɛlɛ, ebisi balɛ asɛ, “Israelanɛ, kiɖe sopɛ kibui ɛlɛ bhɛtɛ wɔnɔɛ bi ana? Kiɖe sopɛ noyelè bɔlɔ ana asɛ onupɛ bɔlɔ ahɔɛ ohumi alo Owusoso Akaa mɛ tɛzazã so pɛ kɛkpatà ɛlɛ gã dza?”

english_verse: The God that Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and our other ancestors worshiped has brought honor to his Servant Jesus. He is the one you betrayed. You turned against him when he was being tried by Pilate, even though Pilate wanted to set him free.
translation_verse: Abraham, Isak nɛ̀ Yakob balɛ Owusoso Akaa, bɔlɔ ashuĩ alɛ Owusoso Akaa yɛadalɛ yɛaxɔ̃ɛdɛ Yesu shu. Pɔ wɔnɔ xɔnɔ ɔlɔkɔɛ ɔkpɛ ahɔɛ asɛ beyii kɛlɛ nɔɖɔ nɛ Pilato yɛamɛ asɛ notinyi yɛ, pɔ yɛ xɔnɔ apɛ asɛ tɛ ashɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ yɛishu flogo.

english_verse: You rejected Jesus, who was holy and good. You asked for a murderer to be set free,
translation_verse: Nɔɖɔ ɔsɛ nɔotinyi Yesu geɛnu anɔ dadalɛ pɛ nobisi ɔsɛ Pilato ashɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ anyɛ gɛeyi anunɔ boŋ shu akɔ wɔnɔ.

english_verse: and you killed the one who leads people to life. But God raised him from death, and all of us can tell you what he has done.
translation_verse: Noyi anɔ nɔ gɛ akɔ banɔ ɛkpaã, pɔ Owusoso Akaa efeke yɛ edenɔ bekpu nɛishi. Bɔzã mɔ kibui ɛlɛ.

english_verse: You see this man, and you know him. He put his faith in the name of Jesus and was made strong. Faith in Jesus made this man completely well while everyone was watching.
translation_verse: Ɔgana Yesu yɛinye nabha tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛɛ, bakɔ anyɛ alɛ gɛ nonyi pɛ nɔmɔ amɔ ishumɛ gagalĩ. Yesu yɛinyeɛ nɛ̀ yɛabha tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã gɛide koeyi nɛ yɛ mɛɛ, kɛlɛ kɔ anyɛ alɛ ishumɛ gagalĩ kɔɛ asɛ gɛ wɔnɔ petee ɔmɔ ana.

english_verse: My friends, I am sure you and your leaders didn't know what you were doing.
translation_verse: “Mɛadzya alɛ mɔ, inyi ɔsɛ wɔnɔ nɛ̀ bagɛ baagbanà wɔnɔɔ otinyi flogo pɛ nɔbhɛtɛ.

english_verse: But God had his prophets tell that his Messiah would suffer, and now he has kept this promise.
translation_verse: Pɔ, kana Owusoso Akaa abhɛtɛ kɛgɛ aɖɔ elúɔ ɔgana yɛebuiɖɔluanɔ nabha gɛ aɖɔ asɛ, yɛ Kristoɔ abamɔ bɔtɔ.

english_verse: So turn to God! Give up your sins, and you will be forgiven.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ, notsyina etúɔ mɛ, tɛ notsyina ɔvɛ Owusoso Akaa ɔkɔ ɔsɛ tɛ beplu wɔnɔ ebui kpa, ɔmɔsɛ kibe ɛlɛ ɛkpã vɔvɔ titsyitsyina badɛ Apaleteɛ ɔkɔ ɔba.

english_verse: Then the time will come when the Lord will give you fresh strength. He will send you Jesus, his chosen Messiah.
translation_verse: Sɛ tɛ asɛsɛ Kristoɔ, anɔ gɛ yɛabha batɔɖɔ ahɔɛ akɔ wɔnɔ, gɛenu Yesuɔ.

english_verse: But Jesus must stay in heaven until God makes all things new, just as his holy prophets promised long ago.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ ɔsɛ tɛ Yesu azã kititiɛ, ɔvɛnɔ sɛ kibeɛ na ɔsɛ tɛ Owusoso Akaa edzinɔ kɛka nyia ka ewoese, asɛ gɛ atɔɖɔ elú abafalanɔ ɔgana yɛebuiɖɔlunɛ gɛ balɛishuɔ dalɛ nabha.

english_verse: Moses said, “The Lord your God will choose one of your own people to be a prophet, just as he chose me. Listen to everything he tells you.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ Mose aɖɔ asɛ, ‘Apaleteɛ gɛenu wɔnɔ Owusoso Akaa abakɔ yɛebuiɖɔluanɔ teli ede koeyi nɛ yɛanɔ nɔ̀ mɛ asɛ mɛ ana. Nɔtaatɔ̃ nɛ̀ kɛka nyia ka gɛ aɖɔ etseɖe wɔnɔ.

english_verse: No one who disobeys this prophet will be one of God's people any longer.”
translation_verse: Anunɔ gɛ egilũ atɔ̃ nɛ yɛɛ, bebèbu yɛ eyinɔ nɛ yɛ banunɔ nɛishi.’

english_verse: Samuel and all the other prophets who came later also spoke about what is now happening.
translation_verse: “Kibui gbã kinu ɔsɛ, ebuiɖɔluanɔ nɔ pete gɛaɖɔ ebuiɛ elu, odenɔ Samuel abha ɔmana bagɛ baza aɖɔaɖɔ eluɔ petee aɖɔ ebui nɛ obo kanɔ alɛ shu.

english_verse: You are really the ones God told his prophets to speak to. And you were given the promise God made to your ancestors. He said to Abraham, “All nations on earth will be blessed because of someone from your family.”
translation_verse: Wɔnɔ onu kaŋãsi eleteɛ ɔkɔ ebuiɖɔluanunɔ nɛ̀ Owusoso Akaa yɛ olugbũnyanyã gɛ anya nɛ̀ wɔnɔ akaalɛ. Etseɖe Abraham asɛ, ‘Gana woeyi mɔmɔ alɛ nabha, bebèyula tutu esi akɔ banunɔ petee nɛ kesiakpa nabha.’

english_verse: God sent his chosen Son to you first, because God wanted to bless you and make each one of you turn away from your sins.
translation_verse: Kliso gɛ Owusoso Akaa efeke yɛ yɛaxɔ̃ɛdɛ, akɔ yɛebhoso aba wɔnɔ ɔkɔ tɛ ebeyulà akɔ wɔnɔ ɔganà wɔnɔ ɔmayɛmɛ aka nyia aka titsyitsyina edenɔ yɛ kɛtakpu mɛ gagalĩ edzo nabha.”

english_verse: The apostles were still talking to the people, when some priests, the captain of the temple guard, and some Sadducees arrived.
translation_verse: Banɔlũazazã nɛ̀ agɛ yɛeɖi Akaavũɔ nabha nɛ̀ Zadukianɛ aba Petro nɛ̀ Yohanes ɔkɔ nɛ kibeɛ gɛ baɖɔaɖɔ ɔkɔ banunɔ.

english_verse: These men were angry because the apostles were teaching the people that the dead would be raised from death, just as Jesus had been raised from death.
translation_verse: Kɛkpɛ Zadukianɛ buluhũ, ɔmɔsɛ banɔkpɛkpɛ azakasɛ banɔ ɔdɔ̃, pɛ bazaɖɔaɖɔ adalɛ Yesu shu, nɛ banunɔ tɛlɔlɔ odenɔ bekpuɔ mɛ shu.

english_verse: It was already late in the afternoon, and they arrested Peter and John and put them in jail for the night.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ, bevũ Petro nɛ̀ Yohanes kɛlɛ gɛ ɔwlago tsyɛ ɔtɛna soɔ, bakpɛ alɛ buvũ ɔvɛnɔ kelizaza mɛ.

english_verse: But a lot of people who had heard the message believed it. So by now there were about
translation_verse: Pɔ, banɔ kpekpèɛ gɛelũ balɛ ibui ɖɔɖɔɔ ayɛkɛ aŋã, kɛlɛ banunɔ tɛkaklà mɛ bakpè tɔɖɔ asɛ ihotu titì ana.

english_verse: The next morning the leaders, the elders, and the teachers of the Law of Moses met in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Gɛ keli azaa, bekusiɛ, besheshèɛ nɛ̀ ɔshadɔkasɛ, badɛtsyɔa nɛ Yerusalem.

english_verse: The high priest Annas was there, as well as Caiaphas, John, Alexander, and other members of the high priest's family.
translation_verse: Hana gɛedzi banɔlũazazã gbagbla tsyɛ azã ɛkɛlɛ, kana Kayafa, Yohanes nɛ̀ Aleksandro, nɛ̀ banɔ gɔgɔ̃ɔ gɛede banɔlũazazã gbagbla yɛɔpã mɛ azã ɛkɛlɛ.

english_verse: They brought in Peter and John and made them stand in the middle while they questioned them. They asked, “By what power and in whose name have you done this?”
translation_verse: Bakɔ bagbanà Petro nɛ̀ Yohanes amana balɛ amɛ, kɛlɛ bakpa balɛ ebui ubisi asɛ, “Gana ohumi ɖuwoe abha alo kinye ɖuwoe nɔlɔkɔ ɔbhɛtɛ̀ kibui ɛlɛ nɔ?”

english_verse: Peter was filled with the Holy Spirit and told the nation's leaders and the elders:
translation_verse: Petro gɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã eyukui mɛɛ, etseɖe balɛ asɛ, “Bekusiɛ nɛ̀ ɔma besheshèɛ!

english_verse: You are questioning us today about a kind deed in which a man who could not walk was healed.
translation_verse: Onupɛ bekulu bɔlɔ obuɔ ɔsɛ bɔbaɖɔaɖɔ nɛ kibui gbangbã gɛ bɔbhɛtɛ kɔ kɛkpatà tɛ bebisì kɛbhɛtɛ gɛ bɔbhɛtɛ pɛ yɛishumɛ gagalĩɛ,

english_verse: But there is something we must tell you and everyone else in Israel. This man is standing here completely well because of the power of Jesus Christ from Nazareth. You put Jesus to death on a cross, but God raised him to life.
translation_verse: kɛlɛ wɔnɔ nɛ̀ Israelanɛ petee notì kibui ɛlɛ ɔsɛ, kinù kibui gɛgã Yesu Kristo Nazareteyiɛ yɛinyeɛ mɛ, anunɔ gɛ nɔdɛ kpalà nɛ osi shuɔ pɛ Owusoso Akaa efeke yɛ edenɔ bekpuɔ mɛɛ, yɛakɔ asɛ anyɛ alɛ gɛeli wɔnɔ amɛ ishumɛ gagalĩ.

english_verse: He is the stone you builders thought was worthless, and now he is the most important stone of all.
translation_verse: “ ‘Yesu alɛ yɛenu, kɛklagbuɔ gɛ wɔnɔ bevũbhɔ nɔtɛ oluɔ, pɛ kɛlɛ badzi odekrekpu iklagbuɔ.

english_verse: Only Jesus has the power to save! His name is the only one in all the world that can save anyone.
translation_verse: Batɛ bamɔ anɔ yɛyɛkɛ elù nɛ̀ ɛkpã ɔgana ananà abha, ɔmɔsɛ kinyè nanà bhama kanɔ alɛ mɛ gɛ babà alɔkɔ ayɛkɛ bɔlɔ elu nɛ̀ ɛkpã nɔ.”

english_verse: The officials were amazed to see how brave Peter and John were, and they knew that these two apostles were only ordinary men and not well educated. The officials were certain these men had been with Jesus.
translation_verse: Gɛ bamɔ kitèkpɛkpɛ nɛ Petro nɛ̀ Yohanes mɛ pɛ bamɔ asɛ betinu buplukpaeleteɛ, pɔ banɔ etee ko benuɔ, kɛbhɛtɛ balɛibi kɛlɛ babakà asɛ banɔ alɛ azã aplɛnɔ Yesu.

english_verse: But they could not deny what had happened. The man who had been healed was standing there with the apostles.
translation_verse: Pɔ gɛ bamɔ kɛkpatà gɛ bakɔ kɛlɛ shu mɛ gagalĩɛ eli kitití nɛ ɛkɛlɛ, bagamɔ kɛnɔnɔ gɛ babaɖɔ.

english_verse: The officials commanded them to leave the council room. Then the officials said to each other,
translation_verse: Kliso bakɔ asɛ bekoeyì nɛ ɔhaesheshèɛ namɛ, kɛlɛ batɔ̃ kɛtakpu kitsù.

english_verse: “What can we do with these men? Everyone in Jerusalem knows about this miracle, and we cannot say it didn't happen.
translation_verse: Bebisi asɛ, “Kiɖe bɔbabhɛtɛ nɛ̀ banɔ alɛ? Ɔmɔsɛ aka nyia aka gɛ alɛ Yerusalem enyi asɛ babhɛtɛ atsyindzye kibui, kɛlɛ bɔtɛ balà kɔɛlɛ asɛnɛ.

english_verse: But to keep this thing from spreading, we will warn them never again to speak to anyone about the name of Jesus.”
translation_verse: Pɔ tɛ kibui ɛlɛ taayɛ̀ɛ ɔvɛ nɛ banunɔ mɛɛ, kɛlɛ sɛ bɔkpɛ ɔsha kɔ banɔ alɛ tɛ̀ batɛ adaɖɔ kibui ɛlɛ etseɖe ananà nɛ kinye ɛlɛ mɛ.

english_verse: So they called the two apostles back in and told them they must never, for any reason, teach anything about the name of Jesus.
translation_verse: Kliso bedzini balɛ ekulu amana buvũɔ mɛ, kɛlɛ bakpɛ balɛ ɔsha asɛ bagamɔ kidzo aɖɔaɖɔ alo tɛ̀ bakasɛ bɔdɔ̃ nanà nɛ Yesu yɛinyeɛ mɛ.

english_verse: Peter and John answered, “Do you think God wants us to obey you or to obey him?
translation_verse: Pɔ, Petro nɛ̀ Yohanes ebuiliulũ asɛ, “Nobhui kibui mɛ ɔkɔ shu ɔsɛ, kiɖuwoe gɛpɛ nɛ Owusoso Akaa namɛ ɔsɛ bɔbhɛtɛ, tɛ bulũ batɔ̃ akɔ wɔnɔ pɛnɔsɛ bulũ batɔ̃ ɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa.

english_verse: We cannot keep quiet about what we have seen and heard.”
translation_verse: Pɔ, bɔtɛ balà yɔlɔ kaɖɔɖɔɖɔ nɛ agɛ alɛ bɔmɔ pɛ buluɔ̃ shu.”

english_verse: The officials could not find any reason to punish Peter and John. So they threatened them and let them go. The man who was healed by this miracle was more than
translation_verse: Kɛkpatà gɛ bakɔ kɛlɔ gã nɛ atsyindzye ibui bhɛbhɛtɛ mɛɛ yɛkɛ agɔ afɛtalɛ nɛ̀ tɔtɔɖɔ. Bayɛyɛkɛŋã Owusoso Akaa Tikukulu

english_verse: As soon as Peter and John had been set free, they went back and told the others everything the chief priests and the leaders had said to them.
translation_verse: Gɛ bashɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ Petro nɛ̀ Yohanes shu koɔ, betsyina aba balɛ ɔhayɛ nɛishi kɛlɛ badɛkɔ balɛ tititì nɛ kɛgɛ banɔlũazazã gbagbla, nɛ̀ besheshèɛ etseɖe balɛ shu.

english_verse: When the rest of the Lord's followers heard this, they prayed together and said: Master, you created heaven and earth, the sea, and everything in them.
translation_verse: Gɛ belũ kibui ɛlɛ, bakpɛ ozĩ kitsù nɛ balɛ Owusoso Akaa tikukulu mɛ. Baɖɔ asɛ, “Apaleteɛ gɛ aŋã bukusiɛ,” wɔbhɛtɛ kititì, kesiakpa, aƒu nɛ̀ kɛka nyia ka gɛ kpasɛ balɛ mɛ.

english_verse: And by the Holy Spirit you spoke to our ancestor David. He was your servant, and you told him to say: “Why are all the Gentiles so furious? Why do people make foolish plans?
translation_verse: Ɔɖɔ kaɖɔ nɛ̀ Ɔfɔfuĩa gbangbã ganà wɔaxɔ̃ɛdɛ, gɛenu bɔlɔ ashuĩ David abha asɛ, “Kiɖuwoe gɛkɔ ɔsɛ amadiɛ alɔɛtití pɛ batɔɖɔ ebui kpa nɛ kɛlɛɛ mɛ?

english_verse: The kings of earth prepare for war, and the rulers join together against the Lord and his Messiah.”
translation_verse: Kesiakpa abha ekusiɛ batɔ̃ kɛtakpu kɛlɛ beekusiɛ beshogò nɛ Apaleteɛ shu nɛ̀ agɛ yɛasara bɔbɔ ɔkɔɔ,

english_verse: Here in Jerusalem, Herod and Pontius Pilate got together with the Gentiles and the people of Israel. Then they turned against your holy Servant Jesus, your chosen Messiah.
translation_verse: Kibui gbã kinu ɔsɛ Herodes nɛ̀ Pontio Pilato badɛtɔ̃ kɛtakpu aplɛnɔ bagɛ betinu Yudanɛ nɛ̀ Israelanɛ nɛ ɔmagbagbla ɔlɛ mɛ tɛ batɔɖɔ kibui kpa nɛ axɔ̃ɛdɛ dadalɛ Yesu shu, anunɔ gɛ yɛ wɔ sara bɔbɔ ɔkɔɔ.

english_verse: They did what you in your power and wisdom had already decided would happen.
translation_verse: Babhɛtɛ̀ kɛgɛ wɔ ohumi nɔ pɛ nɛ̀ kɛgɛ wɔɛbuiɛ tɔɖɔ lùɔ tɛ̀ kɛba ɛlɛ mɛ.

english_verse: Lord, listen to their threats! We are your servants. So make us brave enough to speak your message.
translation_verse: Otsi alɛ, Apalete, ɖi kɛkpɛ gɛ bakpɛ balɔ ɛkpã nɛifùɔ tɛ̀ ɔkpɛ̀ wɔaxɔ̃ɛdɛ niemi tɛ aɖɔaɖɔ nɛ bupù hohoe mɛ.

english_verse: Show your mighty power, as we heal people and work miracles and wonders in the name of your holy Servant Jesus.
translation_verse: Boele wɔahɔɛ mɛ tɛ obu banyãa, tɛ ɔbhɛtɛ atsyindzye ebui nɛ̀ ebui gɛ labhɛtɛ ibi ɔkasɛ alɛ ɔgana woeyi dadalɛ Yesu Kristo yɛinyeɛ mɛ.”

english_verse: After they had prayed, the meeting place shook. They were all filled with the Holy Spirit and bravely spoke God's message. Sharing Possessions
translation_verse: Gɛ bekulu Owusoso Akaa ebekeɛ, ɛkɛlɛ gɛ beshogò azã oshukù. Kɛlɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã oyuku balɛ petee mɛ kɛlɛ baɖɔ Owusoso Akaa nɛibuiɛ nɛ bupù hohoe mɛ. Bayɛyɛkɛŋã Alɔkɔ Balɛ Bɔdɔ̃ Akpɛitsu

english_verse: The group of followers all felt the same way about everything. None of them claimed that their possessions were their own, and they shared everything they had with each other.
translation_verse: Bayɛyɛkɛŋã pete azã tenikpo nɛ kitú nɛ̀ ekpeɖekpeɖe mɛ. Balɛ ɔmayɛmɛ ananà agɛ lɔkɔ yahɔɛmɛ kɛdɔ̃ asɛ yɛ kɛdɔ̃ kinu, pɔ beebu kɛka nyia ka gɛzã balɛ ahɔɛ mɛ akɔ bagɔlɔ.

english_verse: In a powerful way the apostles told everyone that the Lord Jesus was now alive. God greatly blessed his followers,
translation_verse: Nɛ ohumi gbagbla mɛɛ, banɔkpɛkpɛ azaɖɔaɖɔ adalɛ Apalete Yesu tɛlɔlɔ odenɔ bekpuɔ mɛ shu, kɛlɛ bebhui balɛ petee ebú.

english_verse: and no one went in need of anything. Everyone who owned land or houses would sell them and bring the money
translation_verse: Anɔ gɛ apɛ kɛdɔ̃ nanà agɛzã balɛishi. Ɔmɔsɛ odenɔ keliza ɔvɛnɔ keliza mɛɛ, bagɛ busiakpa alo bevu azaã balɛ ahɔɛ adɛ balɛ, kɛlɛ balɔkɔ ehoeyiɛ gɛ bamɔ edenɔ bɔdɔ̃ dɛdɛ mɛ amana

english_verse: to the apostles. Then they would give the money to anyone in need.
translation_verse: kɛlɛ balɔkɔ akpɛ ahɔɛ akɔ banɔkpɛkpɛ kɛlɛ banɔkpɛkpɛ bakɔ anɔ nyia anɔ nɛ yɛ tɛpɛpɛ ulũ.

english_verse: Ananias and his wife Sapphira also sold a piece of property.
translation_verse: Anyɛ teli azã gɛ bezekulu yɛasɛ Anania nɛ̀ yɛedzini Safira tsyɛ adɛ balɛ ahɔɛmɛ kɛdɔ̃ tuli.

english_verse: But they agreed to cheat and keep some of the money for themselves. So when Ananias took the rest of the money to the apostles,
translation_verse: Yɛedzini enyi kibui odenɔ kɛlɛ shu asɛ ebu ehoeyiɛ teli gɛ babuia akɔ alɛ shu kɛlɛ alɔkɔ agɔgɔɛ nɔ amana banɔkpɛkpɛ.

english_verse: Peter said, “Why has Satan made you keep back some of the money from the sale of the property? Why have you lied to the Holy Spirit?
translation_verse: Petro ebisi yɛasɛ, “Anania, kiɖeso pɛ Abosam etsoku wɔ kitúɔ mɛ pɛ ɔsɛ asɛnɛ otseɖe Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã pɛ ɔlɔkɔ ehoeyiɛ teli balɛ ɔkɔ wɔshu nɛ kesiakpa gɛ ɔdɛɛ mɛ dza?

english_verse: The property was yours before you sold it, and even after you sold it, the money was still yours. What made you do such a thing? You didn't lie to people. You lied to God!”
translation_verse: Gɛnɛ wɔayɛ kenu flogo pɛ ɔdɛ kalɛ dza? Kɛlɛ gɛɔdɛ kalɛ obekeɛ, gɛnɛ wɔahɔɛ mɛ ehoeyiɛ azã dza? Kiɖuwoe gɛkɔ ɔsɛ tɛ̀ ɔbhɛtɛ ekpeɖekpeɖe alɛ nɛidi mɛ? Gɛnɛ banɔ wɔwɔɛ pɔ Owusoso Akaa wɔwɔɛ.

english_verse: As soon as Ananias heard this, he dropped dead, and everyone who heard about it was frightened.
translation_verse: Gɛ Anania elũ kibui lɛ koɔ, eglebe adɔ kesiakpa pɛ etsi. Kɛlɛ kifù hohoe ba bagɛ belũ kɛgɛ ba ɛlɛ mɛ abha popopo.

english_verse: Some young men came in and wrapped up his body. Then they took it out and buried it.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ bayisɔ̃ abamànà kɛpɔtɛ nɛishu pɛ balɔkɔ yɛ adɛvɔbɔ.

english_verse: Three hours later Sapphira came in, but she did not know what had happened to her husband.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔasɛ ɔɖa tatá tɛtá edeɛ, yɛedzini tsyɛ ebeyì, gɛ etinyi kɛgɛ ba ɛlɛ mɛ soɔ,

english_verse: Peter asked her, “Tell me, did you sell the property for this amount?” “Yes,” she answered, “that's the amount.”
translation_verse: Petro ebisi yɛasɛ, “Tseɖe mɛ, ehoeyi alɛ ete wɔnɛ woenyineɛ ɔmɔ odenɔ kesiakpa tɛdɛdɛ̀ mɛ dza? Ebuiliulũ asɛ “Ee, kedziɛ niaalɛ.”

english_verse: Then Peter said, “Why did the two of you agree to test the Lord's Spirit? The men who buried Ananias are by the door, and they will carry you out!”
translation_verse: Petro ebisi yɛasɛ, “Kiɖuwoe gɛkɔ ɔsɛ wɔnɔ olugbũ ɔshɔ̃ pɛ nofeke Apaleteɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ oɖi? Ɖio! Banyɛ ɛtsɛɖɛ gɛdɛ vɔbɔ wɔenyineɛ akashɛ nɛ kedulũɔ, kliso babà lɔkɔ wɔ tsyɛ avɛnɔ.”

english_verse: At once she fell at Peter's feet and died. When the young men came back in, they found Sapphira lying there dead. So they carried her out and buried her beside her husband.
translation_verse: Odunu ɔlɛ koɔ, awɔlɛ adɔ nɛ Petro otugbà pɛ etsí. Kɛlɛ gɛ bayisɔ̃ abamɔ asɛ etsíɛ, bamanà kɛpɔtɛ nɛitsyɛ shu adɛ vɔbɔ nɛ yɛenyine ɔbha.

english_verse: The church members were afraid, and so was everyone else who heard what had happened. Peter's Unusual Power
translation_verse: Kifù gbagbla yí ɔhaa nɛ̀ banɔ bhlatɔ pete gɛelũ ebui alɛ gɛ laba ɛlɛ mɛ. Banɔkpɛkpɛ Ebú Banyã Akɔ Banɔ Kpekpè

english_verse: The apostles worked many miracles and wonders among the people. All of the Lord's followers often met in the part of the temple known as Solomon's Porch.
translation_verse: Banɔkpɛkpɛ abhɛtɛ atsyindzye ebui gelee, nɛ̀ ebui gɛ labhɛtɛ kibì nɛ banunɔ nɛishi bamɔ. Kɛlɛ bayɛyɛkɛŋã petee ezeshogò nɛ Salomo Kagbaa mɛ.

english_verse: No one outside their group dared join them, even though everyone liked them very much.
translation_verse: Anɔ nanà agɛ kpɛ kemi pɛ etsoku balɛishi, pɔ banunɔ nyɔ̃ɔ azakpɛ kɛtakpu esi kɔ alɛ flogo.

english_verse: Then sick people were brought out to the road and placed on mats. It was hoped that Peter would walk by, and his shadow would fall on them and heal them.
translation_verse: Nɛ balɛ axɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa shu soɔ, banɔ agbana balɛ banyanyã amana betipɔ nabha pɛ belu balɛ nɛ bapã nɛ̀ iputso nabha. Sɛ tɛ Petro kɛtɔwuiɛ ete dɔ balɛ banɔ teli abha gɛ onupɛ abafala.

english_verse: A lot of people living in the towns near Jerusalem brought those who were sick or troubled by evil spirits, and they were all healed. Trouble for the Apostles
translation_verse: Banɔaza ezeshogò nɛ ɛmaa gɛlɛ Yerusalem ɔbha mɛ̀, pɛ bagbana balɛ banyanyã nɛ̀ bagɛ ɛfɔfuĩa kpa zɛkpɛ balɛ buhũɔ amana, kɛlɛ bebu banyã kɔ balɛ. Kititilɔlɔ Nɛ Banɔkpɛkpɛ shu

english_verse: The high priest and all the other Sadducees who were with him became jealous.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ banɔlũazazã gbagbla nɛ̀ yɛɔbhialɛ gɛenu Zadukianɛ kɛza mɛ anunɔ, balɔkɔ kabhã wɔawlɔakɛ.

english_verse: They arrested the apostles and put them in the city jail.
translation_verse: Bevũ banɔkpɛkpɛ kɛlɛ balɔkɔ alɛ adɛkpɛ nɛ balɛ ɔɖavũɔ ɔkpɛkɔ.

english_verse: But that night an angel from the Lord opened the doors of the jail and led the apostles out. The angel said,
translation_verse: Pɔ gɛ elishiɛ anaa, Apaleteɛ yɛaxɔ̃ɛdɛ teli adɛ dalɛ ɔɖavũɔ budulũɔ kɛlɛ agbanà alɛ ekoeyì.

english_verse: “Go to the temple and tell the people everything about this new life.”
translation_verse: Etseɖe balɛ asɛ, “Nɔvɛ ɔdɛ lɔ kititì nɛ Owusoso Akaavũɔ shu tɛ̀ nɔɖɔ ɛkpã vɔvɔ ɛlɛ shu ebui otseɖe banunɔ.”

english_verse: So they went into the temple before sunrise and started teaching. The high priest and his men called together their council, which included all of Israel's leaders. Then they ordered the apostles to be brought to them from the jail.
translation_verse: Kelizaza nɛvɔlɔɔ, banɔkpɛkpɛ avɛ Owusoso Akaavũɔ shu kɛlɛ bakpà banunɔ bɔdɔ̃ ɔkasɛ asɛ gɛ betseɖe alɛ ana. Gɛ banɔlũazazã gbagbla nɛ̀ yɛɔbhia alɛ ebekuɔ, bekulu ɔhamɛesheshèɛ, gɛinu banɔ sheshè kɛzaa petee nɛ Israel, kɛlɛ bakpɛ banɔ nɛ banɔkpɛkpɛ gɛbakpɛ nɛ ɔɖavũɔ ɔkɔ.

english_verse: The temple police who were sent to the jail did not find the apostles. They returned and said,
translation_verse: Pɔ gɛ bavɛ ediku ɔɖavũɔ shuɔ, ɔɖavũabha eɖiɖieleteɛ agɛ damɔ banɔkpɛkpɛ nɛuvũɔ mɛ, kliso betsyina avɛ edɛkɔ tititì,

english_verse: “We found the jail locked tight and the guards standing at the doors. But when we opened the doors and went in, we didn't find anyone there.”
translation_verse: “Bɔdɛmɔ ɔsɛ budulũɔ pete dãa piapii, kɛlɛ bagɛ epo kɛlɛ tsyɛ eli kititì nɛ budulũɔ, pɔ gɛbɔ dalɛ̃ buvuɔ̃, bagɛmɔ ananà nɛ bɔlɛ mɛ̀.”

english_verse: The captain of the temple police and the chief priests listened to their report, but they did not know what to think about it.
translation_verse: Gɛ belũ kibui ɛlɛ, kɛbhɛtɛ agɛ eɖi akaavũɔ nabha nɛ̀ banɔlũazazã gbagbla nɛibi, gɛ begilũ kɛlɛ esi kɛlɛ bazã nɛ̀ ɔwɔ̃nyɔnɔ nɛ kɛgɛ badekoeyì nɛ kibui ɛlɛ mɛ.

english_verse: Just then someone came in and said, “Now those men you put in jail are in the temple, teaching the people!”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ anɔ teli aba ebetseɖe balɛ asɛ, “Noɖio, banyɛ gɛ novũ ɔkpɛ ɔɖavũɔ, nɛ alɛ balɛ eli akaa vũɔ shu pɛ baakasɛ banunɔ ɔdɔ̃.”

english_verse: The captain went with some of the temple police and brought the apostles back. But they did not use force. They were afraid that the people might start throwing stones at them.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ, agɛeɖi akaavũɔ nabha ɔplɛnɔ yɛaxɔ̃ɛwɔaa avɛ adɛgbana banɔkpɛkpɛ amana. Bagɛ gbanà balɛ nɛ tɛgagalĩ mɛ, ɔmɔsɛ beesi kifù asɛ bagɛ akpasɛ bɔdɔ̃ ebehu alɛ aklagbu.

english_verse: When the apostles were brought before the council, the high priest said to them,
translation_verse: Gɛ bagbana banɔkpɛkpɛ amana, bakɔ balɔɛititi nɛ ɔhamɛesheshèɛ namɛ tɛ banɔlũazazã gbagbla ebisi balɛ ebui.

english_verse: “We told you plainly not to teach in the name of Jesus. But look what you have done! You have been teaching all over Jerusalem, and you are trying to blame us for his death.”
translation_verse: Etseɖe alɛ asɛ, “Bɔkpɛ ɔsha kɔ wɔnɔ gagalĩ ɔsɛ nɔtɔkɔ kasɛ bɔdɔ̃ nɛ kinye ɛlɛ mɛ, pɔ, nɔkɔ wɔnɔ bɔdɔ̃ kakasɛ tsyɔa Yerusalem kɛlɛ nɔpɛ ɔsɛ tɛ̀ bɔŋã kɛpɔ nɛ anyɛ alɛ dzyosu nɔ shu.”

english_verse: Peter and the apostles replied: We don't obey people. We obey God.
translation_verse: Petro nɛ̀ banɔkpɛkpɛ gɔgɔɛ ebuiliulũ asɛ, “kɛlɛ ɔsɛ bulũ batɔ̃ ɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa ɔdɛ banɔdzyrame!

english_verse: You killed Jesus by nailing him to a cross. But the God our ancestors worshiped raised him to life
translation_verse: Bɔlɔ akaalɛ Owusoso Akaa, yɛefeke Yesuɔ edenɔ bekpuɔ mɛ, anɔ gɛ nɔkpiisɔ̃ nɛ busi shu pɛ noyii.

english_verse: and made him our Leader and Savior. Then God gave him a place at his right side, so that the people of Israel would turn back to him and be forgiven.
translation_verse: Owusoso Akaa akpii nɛitití pɛ alɔkɔɛ edzu yɛŋã mɛ asɛ Ekusi nɛ̀ anɔ gɛ ebe Bhui banɔ ebù asɛ tɛ akɔ Israelanɛ etsyina etúɔ mɛ, tɛ balɔkɔ balɛ ebui kpa etsye alɛ.

english_verse: We are here to tell you about all this, and so is the Holy Spirit, who is God's gift to everyone who obeys God.
translation_verse: Bɔlɔ nɛ̀ Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã gɛ Owusoso Akaa akɔ bagɛ elũ yɛatɔɔ̃ bɔlɔ zãmɔ ebui alɛ.”

english_verse: When the council members heard this, they became so angry they wanted to kill the apostles.
translation_verse: Gɛ belũ kibui ɛlɛ, ɔmɔpɔɛ oyí alɛ, kɛlɛ bapɛ asɛ tɛ beyi alɛ.

english_verse: But one of the members was the Pharisee Gamaliel, a highly respected teacher. He ordered the apostles to be taken out of the room for a little while.
translation_verse: Pɔ Farisieyi teli gɛ bezekulu asɛ Gamaliel gɛ edzì ɔshadɔkasɛ pɛ banɔ adɛmɛ nɛ yɛɛ, aba alɔɛtití nɛ ɔhaesheshèɛ namɛ kɛlɛ akɔ ɔsha asɛ bebù banɔkpɛkpɛ eyinɔ ɔtɔ̃ ɛbɔɛ.

english_verse: Then he said to the council: Men of Israel, be careful what you do with these men.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ aɖɔ etseɖe alɛ asɛ, “Israelanɛ, noɖi piapii nɛ kɛgɛ nɔɔtɔɖɔ ɔsɛ tɛ nɔbhɛtɛ nɛ̀ banɔ alɛ shu mɔ.

english_verse: Later, when the people of our nation were being counted, Judas from Galilee showed up. A lot of people followed him, but he was killed, and all his followers were scattered.
translation_verse: Nɛ yiedeɛ, Galilea eyì teli tsyɛ gɛ bezekulu asɛ Yuda ebekoeyì, nɛ ewui alɛ mɛ gɛ bazakalà banunɔ kɛlɛ agbana banɔ kpekpè aplɛnɔ yɛishu pɛ bagalĩ batɔ̃ mɛ. Beyii tsyɛ, kɛlɛ bɔpɔ̃ɛ yiede azazã petee tsyɛ akanà.

english_verse: So I advise you to stay away from these men. Leave them alone. If what they are planning is something of their own doing, it will fail.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ kibui ɛlɛ otsiɛ nɔ, ihu buluhũ ɔkɔ wɔnɔ ɔsɛ, Nɔshɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ banɔ alɛ shu! Nɔkɔ bagavɛ! Ɔmɔsɛ onupɛ kɛgɛ batɔɖɔ nɛ̀ balɛ ebui bhɛbhɛtɛ mɛ enu banɔdzyrame ayɛɛ, latɛ bamɔ otugbà vɛvɛ.

english_verse: But if God is behind it, you cannot stop it anyway, unless you want to fight against God. The council members agreed with what he said,
translation_verse: Pɔ, onupɛ Owusoso Akaa ɔkɔ ledeɛ xɔnɔɔ, nɔtɛ bɔlà ɔbhɛtɛ ɔsɛ banɔ alɛ bazɔkɔ yɔlɔ eɖi, pɔ nɔbɔmɔ wɔnɔ ishu ɔsɛ nɔgɔlɛ̃ iboe nɛ̀ Owusoso Akaa.”

english_verse: and they called the apostles back in. They had them beaten with a whip and warned them not to speak in the name of Jesus. Then they let them go.
translation_verse: Yɛibui ɖɔɖɔ kɔ belũ ebui esi, klisoɔ bekulu banɔkpɛkpɛ amana kɛlɛ bakɔ behuisi alɛ. Kɛlɛ bakpɛ ɔsha akɔ alɛ ɔsɛ batɛ aɖɔaɖɔ nɛ Yesu yɛinyeɛ mɛ, kɛlɛ bashɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ balɛishu pɛ bashɛ.

english_verse: The apostles left the council and were happy, because God had considered them worthy to suffer for the sake of Jesus.
translation_verse: Banɔkpɛkpɛ ashɛ nɛ kebhu okulukɔ nɛ̀ same, ɔmɔsɛ bakalà balɛ asɛ banɔ gɛ bashɔnɔ asɛ bamɔ bɔtɔ nɛ̀ awuĩ nɛ Yesu yɛinyeɛ nɛ takpu.

english_verse: Every day they spent time in the temple and in one home after another. They never stopped teaching and telling the good news that Jesus is the Messiah.
translation_verse: Keliza, elizaa mɛɛ, banɔkpɛkpɛ azã bɔdɔ̃ kakasɛ nabha nɛ akaavũɔ shu, nɛ̀ odenɔ bɔpã mɛ ɔvɛnɔ bɔpã mɛ, kɛlɛ bagɛ zɔkɔ yɔlɔ nɛ̀ balɛ bɔdɔ̃ kakasɛ nɛ̀ kibui gbangbã tɛɖɔɖɔ ɔsɛ Yesu yɛenu Kristoɔ.

english_verse: A lot of people were now becoming followers of the Lord. But some of the ones who spoke Greek started complaining about the ones who spoke Aramaic. They complained that the Greek-speaking widows were not given their share when the food supplies were handed out each day.
translation_verse: Nɛ ewui alɛ mɛ, gɛ badɔkpasɛanɛ tɛkaklà mɛ zikpè tɔɖɔɔ, Helanɛ gɛenu Yudanɛ gɛazã balɛishiɛ bazakpamɛ ishu nɛ Hebrianɛ gɛenu Yudanɛ shu, ɔmɔsɛ beebhui Helanɛ bavɛvɛ edzyini alɛ bho kibe nyia be nɛ bɔŋaŋã tibubú ɔkɔ alɛ mɛ.

english_verse: The twelve apostles called the whole group of followers together and said, “We should not give up preaching God's message in order to serve at tables.
translation_verse: Kliso banɔkpɛkpɛ lɔfɔ nɛ̀ taabha ekulu badɔkpasɛanɛ petee eshogò pɛ betseɖe alɛ asɛ, “Kɛma ɛlɛ ɔzãkɔ ɔsɛ bɔshɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ Owusoso Akaa ebuiɛ tɛɖɔɖɔ shu, tɛ̀ buziɖi bɔŋaŋã tɛkɔkɔ anɔ ebui shu.

english_verse: My friends, choose seven men who are respected and wise and filled with God's Spirit. We will put them in charge of these things.
translation_verse: Kliso, mɛadzya alɛ mɔ, nobu banyɛ tegegene nɛ wɔnɔɛshi gɛ nonyi ɔsɛ, Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã nɛ̀ bubutì kpasɛ alɛ mɛ, bɔbà lɔkɔ kɛxɔ̃ɛ ɛlɛ kpɛ ahɔɛ ɔkɔ alɛ

english_verse: We can spend our time praying and serving God by preaching.”
translation_verse: tɛ kɛkɔ bɔmɔ kibè ɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa tikukulu nɛ̀ yɛibuiɛ tɛɖɔɖɔ.”

english_verse: This suggestion pleased everyone, and they began by choosing Stephen. He had great faith and was filled with the Holy Spirit. Then they chose Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and also Nicolaus, who worshiped with the Jewish people in Antioch.
translation_verse: Kɛzaa pete adɛmɛ atɔɖɔ kibui ɛlɛ gɛ bakpɛ amana nabha. Bebu Stefano, anɔ gɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã nɛ̀ Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã oyuku yɛ mɛ nɛ̀ Filipo, Prokoro, Nikanora, Timona, Parmena nɛ̀ Nikanao, anɔ gɛ akpɛ takpu esi akɔ ibudi pɛ ebetsyina edzi Yudaeyi gɛ ede Antioxia.

english_verse: These men were brought to the apostles. Then the apostles prayed and placed their hands on the men to show they had been chosen to do this work.
translation_verse: Bebu banɔ alɛ akasɛ banɔkpɛkpɛ, kɛlɛ bekulu Owusoso Akaa pɛ batɔɖɔ balɛ ahɔɛ nɛ balɛ abha.

english_verse: God's message spread, and many more people in Jerusalem became followers. Even a large number of priests put their faith in the Lord. Stephen Is Arrested
translation_verse: Kidzo ɛlɛ nabha Owusoso Akaa ibuiɛ yɛɛvɛ, kɛlɛ badɔkpasɛanɛ nɛ Yerusalem bezekpè atɔɖɔ ziazia, kɛlɛ banɔlũazazã kpekpè teli ebelũ batɔ esi akɔ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã. Stefano Tivuvũ

english_verse: God gave Stephen the power to work great miracles and wonders among the people.
translation_verse: Stefano anɔ gɛ Owusoso Akaa tibhui bhuibu nɛ̀ ohumi ɔzã yɛmɛ, abhɛtɛ atsyindzye ebui nɛ̀ ebui gɛ labhɛtɛ ibì nɛ banunɔ nɛishi bamɔ.

english_verse: But some men from Cyrene and Alexandria were members of a group who called themselves “Free Men.” They started arguing with Stephen. Some others from Cilicia and Asia also argued with him.
translation_verse: Pɔ kititì lɔlɔ babá, odenɔ ɔtsyɔakɔ banunɔ gɛ bamɔ ishuahɔɛmɛ zazãa, gɛenu Yudanɛ gɛede Kirene nɛ̀ Aleksandro, kɛlɛ beteli tsyɛ ede Kilikia nɛ̀ Asia bɔdɔ̃ mɛ. Banɔ alɛ akpà ebui wlɔakɛ nɛ̀ Stefano,

english_verse: But they were no match for Stephen, who spoke with the great wisdom that the Spirit gave him.
translation_verse: pɔ, bagɛ lazã yɛubutì nɛ̀ Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ gɛ ɔlɛ yɛalɔkɔ azaɖɔaɖɔ nulũ.

english_verse: So they talked some men into saying, “We heard Stephen say terrible things against Moses and God!”
translation_verse: Kliso nɛ tikpukpũ mɛɛ, bawɔɛ banɔ teli ulũ asɛ baɖɔ asɛ, “Bulũ Stefano yɛaɖɔ ebuso ebui nɛ Mose nɛ̀ Owusoso Akaa shu.”

english_verse: They turned the people and their leaders and the teachers of the Law of Moses against Stephen. Then they all grabbed him and dragged him in front of the council.
translation_verse: Kliso baɖɔaɖɔ etsuifú akpɛ ɔma bɔnɔ besheshèɛ nɛ̀ ɔshadɔkasɛ mɛ. Bevũ Stefano kɛlɛ bagbana yɛamana Ɔhaesheshèɛ ɔkɔ.

english_verse: Some men agreed to tell lies about Stephen, and they said, “This man keeps on saying terrible things about this holy temple and the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Baɖɔ asɛnɛ ebui nɛishu asɛ, “Anyɛ alɛ atɛ ayɔlɔ̀ ebui kpa tɛɖɔɖɔ nɛ ɔzãkɔ dadalɛ ɔlɛ nɛ̀ ɔshaa shu.

english_verse: We have heard him claim that Jesus from Nazareth will destroy this place and change the customs Moses gave us.”
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ bulũ yɛaɖɔ asɛ Yesu Nazareteyi alɛ abayɛ ɔzãkɔ ɔlɛ, kɛlɛ ebetsyina ɛkɔɔ gɛ Mose akpɛ eluɔ akɔ ɔlɔ.”

english_verse: Then all the council members stared at Stephen. They saw that his face looked like the face of an angel.
translation_verse: Ɔhamɛesheshèɛ gɛ bazã ɛkɛlɛ petee edzu awuhũ nɛ yɛabha, kɛlɛ bamɔ asɛ yamɛ edzì asɛ axɔ̃ɛdɛ ayɛ mɛ̀ ana.

english_verse: The high priest asked Stephen, “Are they telling the truth about you?”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ ɔzãkɔanɔ gbagbla ebisi yɛasɛ, “Ebui alɛ gɛ balɔkɔ ebhui wɔlũɔ nɔ enu ebui gbã dza?”

english_verse: Stephen answered: Friends, listen to me. Our glorious God appeared to our ancestor Abraham while he was still in Mesopotamia, before he had moved to Haran.
translation_verse: Ebu kibui ɛlɛ ulũ asɛ, “Mɛadzya alɛ, nɛ mɛakaa alɛ, nɔtaatɔ nɛ mɛ! Nɛ kibe ɛlɛ gɛ bɔlɔ ashuĩ Abraham azã Mesopotamia nɔ, Owusoso Akaa Ishudadalɛ eleteɛ ebu yɛishu akasɛ yɛ nɛ ɛkɛlɛ flogo pɛ Abraham ebetú avɛ Xaram.

english_verse: God told him, “Leave your country and your relatives and go to a land that I will show you.”
translation_verse: Owusoso Akaa aɖɔ asɛ, ‘Tu ɔshɛ nɛ wɔ kesiakpa, nɛ̀ wɔ eshopo alɛ ɔbha tɛ ɔvɛ kesiakpa gɛ ɛbakasɛ wɔ nabha.’

english_verse: Then Abraham left the land of the Chaldeans and settled in Haran. After his father died, Abraham came and settled in this land where you now live.
translation_verse: Kliso etú ashɛ nɛ Kaldea kesiakpa nabha pɛ adɛzã Xaram. Nɛ yɛakaa butsi niedeɛ, Owusoso Akaa asɛsii nɛ kesiakpa alɛ abha gɛ nɔlɛ otsiɛ nɔ.

english_verse: God didn't give him any part of it, not even a square meter. But God did promise to give it to him and his family forever, even though Abraham didn't have any children.
translation_verse: Agɛ kɔɛ kaŋãsi nanà nɛ ɛkɛlɛ pɛ oboyi kesiakpa kalɛ katsɛɖɛkpɔ tidzedze mɛ ete tsyɛ. Pɔ Owusoso Akaa atɔɖɔ akɔɛ asɛ, yɛ nɛ̀ yɛeshui alɛ abayɛkɛ kesiakpa alɛ nɛ kibe ɛlɛ tsyɛ, eyi nanà agɛzã Abraham ahɔɛ flogo.

english_verse: God said Abraham's descendants would live for a while in a foreign land. There they would be slaves and would be mistreated
translation_verse: Owusoso Akaa aɖɔaɖɔ etseɖe yɛ̀ nɛ kidzo ɛlɛ nabha asɛ, ‘Woeshui alɛ ebedzi befù nɛ kesiakpa tieli gɛ ketinu balɛ ayɛ nabha. Kɛlɛ babalɔkɔ balɛ asɛ banɔ dzidzi ana, kɛlɛ babakpɛ alɛ bɔtɔ nɛ agɔ akpã talɛ mɛ.

english_verse: But he also said, “I will punish the nation that makes them slaves. Then later they will come and worship me in this place.”
translation_verse: Owusoso Akaa aɖɔ asɛ, ɛbapɔ̃ batɔ̃ ɔkɔ kɛmadiɛ gɛ bàbhɛtɛ alɛ asɛ banɔ dzidzi ana. Kidegbaa bebè koeyi nɛ kɛmadi ɛlɛ mɛ, kɛlɛ babakpɛ kɛtakpu esi akɔ mɛ nɛ ɛkɛlɛ̀.’

english_verse: God said to Abraham, “Every son in each family must be circumcised to show you have kept your agreement with me.” So when Isaac was eight days old, Abraham circumcised him. Later, Isaac circumcised his son Jacob, and Jacob circumcised his twelve sons.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ anyã olugbũ nɛ̀ Abraham nɛ kɛhɔ̃a bhui bhui shu. Kliso gɛ Abraham amɔ Isakiɛ, ebhui kɛhɔ̃a akɔɛ nɛ kiwui sɛmì nabha. Kidegbaa Isak abamɔ Yakob kɛlɛ Yakob tsyɛ abamɔ́ bashuĩ lɔfɔ nɛ̀ taabha.

english_verse: These men were our ancestors. Joseph was also one of our famous ancestors. His brothers were jealous of him and sold him as a slave to be taken to Egypt. But God was with him
translation_verse: Gɛ balɔkɔ kawɔ̃kpɛ nɛ Yosef shu soɔ, badɛ̀ yɛakɔ Egipteanɛ asɛ anɔ dzidzì ana. Pɔ, Owusoso Akaa azã aplɛnii,

english_verse: and rescued him from all his troubles. God made him so wise that the Egyptian king Pharaoh thought highly of him. The king even made Joseph governor over Egypt and put him in charge of everything he owned.
translation_verse: kɛlɛ ebui edenɔ yɛɔtɔ ka nyia ka mɛ. Akɔ Yosef bubutì gɛ kɛkɔ ɔsɛ yɛibutìɛ ku Egipteanɛ ekusi Farao awɔ̃.

english_verse: Everywhere in Egypt and Canaan the grain crops failed. There was terrible suffering, and our ancestors could not find enough to eat.
translation_verse: Kliso akɔ yɛaŋã bukusi nɛ Egipte kesiakpa nɛ̀ yɛukusi bɔpã petee abha.

english_verse: But when Jacob heard there was grain in Egypt, he sent our ancestors there for the first time.
translation_verse: Gɛ Yakob elũ asɛ nikpèe alɛ Egypteɛ, akpɛ bɔlɔ ashuĩ alɛ nɛ ɛkɛlɛ dɛdɛ gɛiputɔ.

english_verse: It was on their second trip that Joseph told his brothers who he was, and the king learned about Joseph's family.
translation_verse: Gɛ baza avɛ bɔbhalà, Yosef etseɖe yɛadzya alɛ anɔ nɔ̀ ɛdi gɛenuɔ, kɛkɔ ɔsɛ Farao tsyɛ eti Yosef yɛeshopo alɛ.

english_verse: Joseph sent for his father and his relatives. In all, there were
translation_verse: Nɛ kɛlɛ̀ niedeɛ, Yosef akpɛ banɔ asɛsɛ yɛakaa Yakob nɛ̀ yɛɔpã petee, banɔ asɛ afɛtegegene nɛ̀ teti ana.

english_verse: His father went to Egypt and died there, just as our ancestors did.
translation_verse: Kliso Yakob ebezhi avɛ Egipte, ɛkɛlɛ yɛ nɛ̀ bɔlɔ ashuĩ alɛ editsí.

english_verse: Later their bodies were taken back to Shechem and placed in the tomb that Abraham had bought from the sons of Hamor.
translation_verse: Balɔkɔ balɛ etsitsiɛ amana Sixem adɛ vɔbɔ nɛ odzeɖeɛ gɛ Abraham edzi nɛ̀ ehoeyi kpekpè teli edenɔ Xamɔ yɛeyi alɛ ahɔɛ mɛ nɛ Sixem.

english_verse: Finally, the time came for God to do what he had promised Abraham. By then the number of our people in Egypt had greatly increased.
translation_verse: “Gɛ kibeɛ nà ɔsɛ tɛ Owusoso Akaa abhɛtɛ atɔɖɔ olugbũɔ gɛ anyã nɛ̀ Abraham abhaa, bɔlɔ banunɔ nɛ Egipte ebekpè atɔɖɔ.”

english_verse: Another king was ruling Egypt, and he didn't know anything about Joseph.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ ekusi bhlatɔ gɛ yɛetinyi kɛnanà nɛ Yosef shuɔ ebedzi ekusi nɛ Egipte.

english_verse: He tricked our ancestors and was cruel to them. He even made them leave their babies outside, so they would die.
translation_verse: Ekusi alɛ abhɛtɛ̀ kɛtakpu mɛ gagalĩ nɛ̀ bɔlɔ banunɔ, kɛlɛ akpɛ bɔlɔ ashuĩ alɛ ɔtɔ pɛ ezetũilɛ akpɛ balɛilũ asɛ bátɛɛ balɛ beyivɔvɔ gɛ bamɔ asɛ tɛ betsí.

english_verse: During this time Moses was born. He was a very beautiful child, and for three months his parents took care of him in their home.
translation_verse: “Nɛ kibe ɛlɛ bamɔ Mose, gɛetinu eyi ete ko. Banyɔ nii woele tata nɛ yɛakaa pãmɛ.

english_verse: Then when they were forced to leave him outside, the king's daughter found him and raised him as her own son.
translation_verse: Gɛ bebui eyinɔ ɔtɔɔ̃, Farao yɛeyi adzɛ alɔkɔɛ anyɔnɔ asɛ yɛahɔɛmɛ anyɛnuvɔɛ ana.

english_verse: Moses was given the best education in Egypt. He was a strong man and a powerful speaker.
translation_verse: Balɔkɔ Egipteanɛ bubutiɛ akasɛ Mose bɔdɔ̃ nɔ. Kɛlɛ amɔ ohumi nɛ kaɖɔ ɖɔɖɔ nɛ̀ ebui bhɛbhɛtɛ mɛ.

english_verse: One day he saw an Egyptian mistreating one of them. So he rescued the man and killed the Egyptian.
translation_verse: Amɔ Israeleyi teli gɛ Egipteyi azawɔɛ nyɔnyɔ̃, kɛlɛ avɛ asɛ tɛ̀ adɛ wlɔakɛ yɛtakpu, gana kɛlɛ mɛɛ ebeyi Egipteyiɛ.

english_verse: Moses thought the rest of his people would realize God was going to use him to set them free. But they didn't understand.
translation_verse: Mose eɖi asɛ yɛelé alɛ abamɔɛ asɛ anɔ gɛ Owusoso Akaa awɔɛ shu axɔ̃ɛ asɛ tɛ awlɔakɛ balɛ takpu, pɔ begilũ kɛliesi ana.

english_verse: The next day Moses saw two of his own people fighting, and he tried to make them stop. He said, “Men, you are both Israelites. Why are you so cruel to each other?”
translation_verse: Gɛ keli azaa, Mose ewlui azaba batɔnɔ Israelanɛ taabha gɛ bagɔ̃lɛ, apɛ asɛ tɛ evũ iboe nɛ balɛishi, ebisi asɛ, ‘Baanyɛ, baadzya nonu, kiɖeso pɛ nɔpɛ ɔsɛ tɛ nɔkɔ bagɔlɔ kibiɛ dza?’

english_verse: But the man who had started the fight pushed Moses aside and asked, “Who made you our ruler and judge?
translation_verse: Pɔ anyɛ gɛ yɛawɔa yɛɔhagbo nyɔnyɔ̃ nɔ etii Mose avɛnɔ kɛwɔã abha, pɛ ebisi yɛasɛ, “Afana gɛ edzu wɔ ekusi nɛ̀ kebhukulu nɛ bɔlɔ ulũ?

english_verse: Are you going to kill me, just as you killed that Egyptian yesterday?”
translation_verse: Ɔpɛ ɔsɛ tɛ oyi mɛ tsyɛ asɛ kiyiɛ gɛ oyi Egipteyiɛ kivũɔ dza?

english_verse: When Moses heard this, he ran away to live in the country of Midian. His two sons were born there.
translation_verse: Gɛ Mose elũ kibui ɛlɛ, ebu tɛtsɛɖɛ mɛ avɛ Midianɛ kesiakpa nabha, ɛkɛlɛ yɛadɛzã asɛ efu ana, kɛlɛ amɔ beyianyɛ nuvɔɛ taabha nɛ ɛkɛlɛ.

english_verse: Forty years later, an angel appeared to Moses from a burning bush in the desert near Mount Sinai.
translation_verse: “Agɔ afɛtalɛ edeɛ, axɔ̃ɛdɛ teli ebu yɛishu akasɛ Mose nɛ ewu kifutù tili gɛ zɛtã kifúɔ, nɛ oxuiɛ gɛotso ɔplɛnɔ Sinai ketukpɔ.

english_verse: Moses was surprised by what he saw. He went closer to get a better look, and the Lord said,
translation_verse: Gɛ amɔ kibui ɛlɛ, kɛbhɛtɛ yɛibi. Gɛ etso aplɛnɔ asɛ tɛ̀ amɔ piapii nɔ, ebelũ Apalete nɛaɖɔ asɛ,

english_verse: “I am the God who was worshiped by your ancestors, Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.” Moses started shaking all over and didn't dare to look at the bush.
translation_verse: ‘Mɛinu wɔashuĩ alɛ Owusoso Akaa, Abraham, Isak nɛ̀ Yakob balɛ Owusoso Akaa.’ Mose ebeshuku gbagbà nɛ̀ kifu sisi mɛ, kliso aga kpɛ kitè eɖi ɛkɛlɛ.

english_verse: The Lord said to him, “Take off your sandals, because the place where you are standing is holy.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ Apaleteɛ, etseɖe yɛasɛ, “Buwɔ ɛtsɛɖɛkɔlɔ, ɔmɔsɛ ɛkɛlɛ gɛ oliɛ onu ɔdadalɛkɔ.

english_verse: With my own eyes I have seen the suffering of my people in Egypt. I have heard their groans and have come down to rescue them. Now I am sending you back to Egypt.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛmɔ̃ ɔtɔ mɛ̀ ɔkɔ mɛ banunɔ nɛ Egipteɛ kɛgbã. Ilũ balɛ tìninimiɛ kɛlɛ izhiba ɔsɛ tɛ mɛ bàkɔ alɛ ishu ahɔɛmɛzazã, kliso ba otsiɛ tɛ midzinì wɔkpɛ nɛ Egypte.’

english_verse: This was the same Moses that the people rejected by saying, “Who made you our leader and judge?” God's angel had spoken to Moses from the bush. And God had even sent the angel to help Moses rescue the people and be their leader.
translation_verse: “Mose alɛ gɛ bagɛ yɛkɛ yɛebuiɛ gɛ azaɖɔ asɛ, ‘Afana gɛedzu wɔ bukusi nɛ̀ kebhukulu anɔ nɛ bɔlɔ ulũɔ dza?’ Yɛ Owusoso Akaa akpɛ tɛ edzi balɛ ekusi nɛ̀ anɔ gɛ ebebù alɛ ɔgana axɔ̃ɛdɛ gɛebu yɛishu akasi nɛ ayakpa mɛ.

english_verse: In Egypt and at the Red Sea and in the desert, Moses rescued the people by working miracles and wonders for
translation_verse: Agbana alɛ ekoeyì nɔ nɛ Egipte, kɛlɛ abhɛtɛ atsyindzye ebui nɛ̀ ebui gɛ abhɛtɛ ibì nɛ Egipte nɛ̀ aƒu dzadzã shu nɛ agɔ afɛtalɛ mɛ.

english_verse: Moses is the one who told the people of Israel, “God will choose one of your people to be a prophet, just as he chose me.”
translation_verse: “Mose alɛ yɛetseɖe Israelanɛ asɛ, ‘Owusoso Akaa aba sɛsɛ wɔnɔ ebuiɖɔluanɔ asɛ mɛ ana, odenɔ wɔnɔ ahɔɛ mɛ banunɔ ɔkɔ.’

english_verse: Moses brought our people together in the desert, and the angel spoke to him on Mount Sinai. There he was given these life-giving words to pass on to us.
translation_verse: Yɛazã Israel kɛza nɛishi nɛ oxuiɛ, aplɛnɔ axɔ̃ɛdɛ gɛ aɖɔaɖɔ etseɖe yɛ nɛ̀ yɛashuĩ alɛ nɛ Sinai ketukpɔ nabha, kɛlɛ ayɛkɛ ebui gɛ ɛkpã kpasɛ alɛ mɛ abakpɛ ahɔɛ akɔ bɔlɔ.

english_verse: But our ancestors refused to obey Moses. They rejected him and wanted to go back to Egypt.
translation_verse: “Pɔ, bɔlɔ ashuĩ alɛ egilũ yɛatɔ̃. Pɔ balɛ kitúɔ xɔnɔ zã Egipte shu.

english_verse: Then they made an idol in the shape of a calf. They offered sacrifices to the idol and were pleased with what they had done.
translation_verse: Kibe ɛlɛ babhɛtɛ obudi gɛ lodzi asɛ nɛntsyɔɛ eyi ana. Balɔkɔ bɔdɔ̃pɔpɔɛ amana ɔlɛ kɛlɛ baŋã kiwui akpɛ samɛ nɛ kɛdɔ̃ gɛ balɔkɔ balɛ ahɔɛ abhɛtɛ shu.

english_verse: God turned his back on his people and left them. Then they worshiped the stars in the sky, just as it says in the Book of the Prophets, “People of Israel, you didn't offer sacrifices and offerings to me during those
translation_verse: Klisoɔ Owusoso Akaa ashɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ balɛishu asɛ tɛ̀ bakpɛ kɛtakpu esi akɔ kititì bɔdɔ̃ɔ. Kibui ɛlɛ shɔnɔ kɛgɛ bafɔnɔ nɛ bebuiɖɔluanɛ keplukpa mɛ asɛ, “ ‘Oo Israel, bɔdɔ̃pɔpɔɛ nɛ̀ bɔdɔ̃ kɔkɔ wɔ mana mɛ nɛ agɔ afɛtalɛ kidzo gagã mɛ nɛ oxuiɛ dza?

english_verse: Instead, you carried the tent where the god Molech is worshiped, and you took along the star of your god Rephan. You made those idols and worshiped them. So now I will have you carried off beyond Babylonia.”
translation_verse: Nɔkpɛ Molox ibudiuvũɔ nɛitití nɛ̀ Romfan wɔnɔ awɔ̃ɛ̀ obudiɛ, ibudiɛ gɛ nɔbhɛtɛ pɛ nɔkpɛ kɛtakpu esi ɔkɔ. Kliso ɛbakɔ bagbanà wɔnɔ ashɛnɔ avɛnɔ Babilonia ede asɛ bɔnɔ gɛ keyù eté alɛ ana.

english_verse: The tent where our ancestors worshiped God was with them in the desert. This was the same tent that God had commanded Moses to make. And it was made like the model that Moses had seen.
translation_verse: “Trɛwɔa kagba gɛkɔ bɔlɔ ashuĩ alɛ abakà ebuiɛ gɛ baɖɔ etseɖe alɛ nɛ oxuiɛ azã balɛ ahɔɛ. Babhɛtɛ kalɛ tɛzazã mɛ nɛ kɛshɔrɔ gɛ Owusoso Akaa ashɔrɔ Mose pɛ amɔ kalɛ nulũ.

english_verse: Later it was given to our ancestors, and they took it with them when they went with Joshua. They carried the tent along as they took over the land from those people that God had chased out for them. Our ancestors used this tent until the time of King David.
translation_verse: Gɛ bayɛkɛ trɛwɔa kagbaa, bɔlɔ ashuĩ alɛ gɛ Yosua agbana, balɔkɔ kalɛ amana, nɛ kibeɛ gɛ bayɛkɛ kesiakpa nɛ amadiɛ gɛ Owusoso Akaa akaka nɛ balɛ otugbàa ahɔɛ. Kazã balɛ kesiakpa nabha ɔlɔkɔ mana David ibe

english_verse: He pleased God and asked him if he could build a house of worship for the people of Israel.
translation_verse: anɔ gɛ amɔ tɛpɛplɛnɔ odenɔ Owusoso Akaa ahɔɛ mɛ, kɛlɛ ebisi asɛ tɛ edzu kagba kɔ Yakob Owusoso Akaa.

english_verse: And it was finally King Solomon who built a house for God.
translation_verse: Pɔ Salomo yɛaba bhɔ buvũɔ akɔɛ.

english_verse: But the Most High God doesn't live in houses made by humans. It is just as the prophet said, when he spoke for the Lord,
translation_verse: Pɔ Kititiayɛ Gbagbla xɔnɔ atɛzã beevũ gɛ banɔ abhɔ mɛ. Asɛ gɛ ebuiɖɔluanɛ aɖɔ asɛ,

english_verse: “Heaven is my throne, and the earth is my footstool. What kind of house will you build for me? In what place will I rest?
translation_verse: “ ‘Kititiɛ kɛlɛinu mɛ bukusi kegbuɔ, pɛ kesiakpa enu mɛ ɔtsɛɖɛ odzukɔ. Bɔpã ɖuwoedi nɔbɔ bhɔ ɔkɔ mɛ? Alo fakɔ mɛ omoelekɔ ɔbɔzã? Kana Apaleteɛ aɖɔ.

english_verse: You stubborn and hardheaded people! You are always fighting against the Holy Spirit, just as your ancestors did.
translation_verse: “Wɔnɔ batɔ̃mɛ gagalĩ elete! Wɔnɔ etú mɛ̀ nɛ̀ batɔ gɛegetsyina elete! Nɔodzi asɛ wɔnɔ akaalɛ. Nɔtɛ ɔpɛɛ ɔsɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbãa ɔba!

english_verse: Is there one prophet that your ancestors didn't mistreat? They killed the prophets who told about the coming of the One Who Obeys God. And now you have turned against him and killed him.
translation_verse: Ebuiɖɔluanɔ teli atɛzã wɔnɔɛshi gɛ wɔnɔ akaalɛ agɛ tɔnɔ yiede eɖiɛ dza? Noyí bagɛ aɖɔ elú asɛ Anɔ Dadalɛ ababaa tsyɛ. Kɛlɛ nɔkpɛ Anɔ dadalɛ ahɔɛ pɛ noyii,

english_verse: Angels gave you God's Law, but you still don't obey it. Stephen Is Stoned to Death
translation_verse: wɔnɔ bagɛ nɔyɛkɛ ɔsha gɛ balɔkɔ akpɛ axɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa mɛ ɔganà baxɔ̃ɛdɛ nabha pɔ nɔgɛlà ɔlɛ abha ɔbhɛtɛ tsyɛ.” Batá Aklagbu Ehu Stefano Eyii

english_verse: When the council members heard Stephen's speech, they were angry and furious.
translation_verse: Gɛ belũ stefano yɛebui alɛ, ɔmɔpɔɛ oyi alɛ kɛlɛ batã ene nɛishu.

english_verse: But Stephen was filled with the Holy Spirit. He looked toward heaven, where he saw our glorious God and Jesus standing at his right side.
translation_verse: Pɔ gɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã ɔzã Stefano mɛ soɔ, eɖi kititiɛ kɛlɛ amɔ Owusoso Akaa yɛ tɛdadalɛ mɛ̀, nɛ̀ Yesu gɛalɛ Owusoso Akaa yɛŋã mɛ.

english_verse: Then Stephen said, “I see heaven open and the Son of Man standing at the right side of God!”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ Stefano aɖɔ asɛ, “Noɖio! ɛmɔ kititíɛ gɛ dalɛ li kɛlɛ ɛmɔ Anɔdzyrame Yɛeyiɛ gɛ elititíɛ nɛ Owusoso Akaa yɛŋã mɛ.”

english_verse: The council members shouted and covered their ears. At once they all attacked Stephen
translation_verse: Bashɔ bɔdɔ̃kpɛ balɛ batɔ̃mɛ gɛ belũ kibui ɛlɛ, bakpɛ ozĩ gagalĩi kɛlɛ bezhieku yɛabha,

english_verse: and dragged him out of the city. Then they started throwing stones at him. The men who had brought charges against him put their coats at the feet of a young man named Saul.
translation_verse: betii yɛebu eyinɔ nɛ ɔmamɛ, kɛlɛ bakpà aklagbu atahui. Nɛ kibe ɛlɛ bagɛ bazã amɔ ebui alɛ alɔkɔ balɛ bakpɛkpɛ elú ayisɔ̃ teli gɛ bekulu yɛasɛ Saulo otugbà.

english_verse: As Stephen was being stoned to death, he called out, “Lord Jesus, please welcome me!”
translation_verse: Kibeɛ gɛ baata aklagbuɔ ehu Stefanoɔ, ekulu Owusoso Akaa asɛ, “Apalete Yesu, yɛkɛ mɛɔfɔfuĩa nɔ.”

english_verse: He knelt down and shouted, “Lord, don't blame them for what they have done.” Then he died.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ edzu ekluisi esi pɛ akpɛ ozĩ gagalĩi asɛ, “Apalete tɛbhɛtɛ kibui kpa ɛlɛ ɔkpɛ kɛliulũ ɔkɔ alɛ.” Gɛ aɖɔ kibui ɛlɛ ebeke koɔ kɛlɛ adã olugbũ.

english_verse: Saul started making a lot of trouble for the church. He went from house to house, arresting men and women and putting them in jail. The Good News Is Preached in Samaria
translation_verse: Pɔ Saulo akpɛ ɔha tɛyɛyɛ esi. Agã edenɔ bɔpã mɛ ɔvɛnɔ bɔpã mɛ, pɛ agbanà banyɛ nɛ̀ baadzɛ avɛnɔ ɔɖavũmɛ. Anɔkpɛkpɛ Filipo Avɛnɔ Kibui Gbangbã Nɛ Samaria

english_verse: The Lord's followers who had been scattered went from place to place, telling the good news.
translation_verse: Bagɛ apɔ̃ɛ kanà aɖɔ kibuiɛ nɛ ɔkɔ nyia ɔkɔ gɛ bavɛ.

english_verse: Philip went to the city of Samaria and told the people about Christ.
translation_verse: Filipo ediku ɔma toli mɛ̀ nɛ Samaria pɛ aɖɔ Kristo shu kibuiɛ etseɖe alɛ nɛ ɛkɛlɛ.

english_verse: They crowded around Philip because they were eager to hear what he was saying and to see him work miracles.
translation_verse: Gɛ banɔɛza elũ Filipo yɛebuiɛ pɛ bamɔ atsyindzye ebuiɛ gɛ azabhɛtɛ akasɛɛ, balɛ pete ayɛkɛ yɛibuiɛ gɛ azaɖɔɔ.

english_verse: Many people with evil spirits were healed, and the spirits went out of them with a shout. A lot of paralyzed and lame people were also healed.
translation_verse: Abhɛtɛ akɔ ɛfɔfuĩa kpa kpɛ ozĩ koeyi nɛ banɔ gelee mɛ kɛlɛ banɔ gɛ bagbã nɛ̀ akpatà lamɔ ishumɛ gagalĩ.

english_verse: Everyone in that city was very glad because of what was happening.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ same gbagbla azã ɔmã ɔlɛ mɛ. Simon Anɔ Gɛenu Ehuinɛ

english_verse: For some time a man named Simon had lived there and had amazed the people of Samaria. He practiced witchcraft and claimed to be somebody great.
translation_verse: Nɛ kibè tilì anyɛ teli gɛ bezekulu asɛ Simon, edzì ehuinɛ gɛ kɛkɔ ɔsɛ kɛbhɛtɛ Samarianɛ pete ibi. Efeke yɛishu asɛ anɔ gbagbla teli idì yɛenu,

english_verse: Everyone, rich and poor, crowded around him. They said, “This man is the power of God called ‘The Great Power.’ ”
translation_verse: kɛlɛ banunɔ petee, besheshèɛ nɛ̀ bunuvɔɛnɔ pete ezelu awɔ̃ nɛ yɛabha pɛ bazaɖɔ asɛ, “Anyɛ alɛ yɛenu kititiayɛ ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ gɛ beekulu asɛ Ohumi Gbagbla nɔ.”

english_verse: For a long time, Simon had used witchcraft to amaze the people, and they kept crowding around him.
translation_verse: Bazã yiede ɔmɔsɛ nɛ kibè dzlolo nɛ ɛlɛ gɛ yɛihui ebui bhɛbhɛtɛ azabhɛtɛ alɛ ibi.

english_verse: But when they believed what Philip was saying about God's kingdom and about the name of Jesus Christ, they were all baptized.
translation_verse: Pɔ gɛ bayɛkɛ Filipo abha aŋã nɛ kibui Gbangbã gɛaɖɔ nɛ Owusoso Akaa bukusiŋaŋã shu nɛ̀ Yesu Kristo yɛinyeɛ mɛ soɔ, banyɛ nɛ̀ badzɛ pete akɔ bakpɛ bulĩ nɛ ɛtakpu akɔ alɛ.

english_verse: Even Simon believed and was baptized. He stayed close to Philip, because he marveled at all the miracles and wonders.
translation_verse: Simon tsyɛ ayɛkɛ aŋã kɛlɛ bakpɛ bulĩ nɛ kɛtakpu akɔɛ. Kɛlɛ azã Filipo kede ɔvɛnɔ ɔkɔ nyia ɔkɔ gɛ avɛɛ, ɔmɔsɛ ebui bubukasɛ nɛ̀ atsyindzye ebuiɛ gɛ amɔɔ abhɛtɛ yɛibi.

english_verse: The apostles in Jerusalem heard that some people in Samaria had accepted God's message, and they sent Peter and John.
translation_verse: Gɛ banɔkpɛkpɛ nɛ Yerusalem elũ asɛ Samarianɛ ayɛkɛ Owusoso Akaa nɛibuiɛ, bakpɛ Petro nɛ̀ Yohanes nɛ balɛ ɔkɔ.

english_verse: When the two apostles arrived, they prayed that these people would be given the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Gɛ bediku ɛkɛlɛɛ, bekulu Owusoso Akaa nɛ balɛ ɛtakpu asɛ tɛ bayɛkɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa gbangbã,

english_verse: Before this, the Holy Spirit had not been given to any of them, though some of them had been baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: banya tɛkpɛ bulĩ akɔ alɛ ete nɛ Apalete Yesu kinyeɛ mɛ, pɔ Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã xɔnɔ ɔkɔ ba balɛ ɔmayɛ nanà abha.

english_verse: Peter and John then placed their hands on everyone who had faith in the Lord, and they were given the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ Petro nɛ̀ Yohanes atɔɖɔ ahɔɛ nɛ balɛ abha, kɛlɛ bayɛkɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã.

english_verse: Simon noticed that the Spirit was given only when the apostles placed their hands on the people. So he brought money
translation_verse: Gɛ Simon amɔ asɛ banɔkpɛkpɛ ahɔɛ tɔtɔɖɔ balɛ abha akɔ asɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ ba alɛ abha soɔ, ebu ehoeyi akɔ alɛ

english_verse: and said to Peter and John, “Let me have this power too! Then anyone I place my hands on will also be given the Holy Spirit.”
translation_verse: pɛ etseɖe alɛ asɛ, “Nɔkɔ mɛtsyɛ ohumi ɔlɛ nɛidi nɛ̀ ɔlɛ axɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa, sɛ tɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã ɔba anɔ nyia anɔ abha gɛ ɛtɔɖɔ ahɔɛɛ.”

english_verse: Peter said to him, “You and your money will both end up in hell if you think you can buy God's gift!
translation_verse: Petro ebuiliulũ akɔɛ asɛ, “Tsofala nɛ̀ wɔnɛ̀ woehoeyi, ɔmɔsɛ oɖi ɔsɛ ɔbɔlà Owusoso Akaa kɛdɔ̃ kɔkɔ odzi nɛ̀ woehoeyiɛ.

english_verse: You don't have any part in this, and God sees that your heart isn't right.
translation_verse: Wɔ ɔbhɛtɛkɔ nɛ̀ wɔ ɔŋãkɔ nanà ɔma bɔlɔ axɔ̃ɛdɛdɛ alɛ mɛ, ɔmɔsɛ wɔɛtũɔ mɛ gɛdalɛ nɛ Owusoso Akaa namɛ.

english_verse: Get rid of these evil thoughts and ask God to forgive you.
translation_verse: Tsyinà nɛ wɔɛtakpumɛ gagalĩ ebuiɛ alɛ shu tɛ okulu Apaleteɛ. Ɔdɛtsyɛ abalɔkɔ ekpeɖekpeɖeɛ gɛ wɔbhɛtɛ odenɔ wɔɛtúɔ mɛ etsye wɔ.

english_verse: I can see that you are jealous and bound by your evil ways.”
translation_verse: Ɛmɔsɛ ɔmɔpɔɛdi tilì kpasɛ wɔ mɛ̀, kɛlɛ odzi anɔ gɛ kibui kpa ŋã wɔabha.

english_verse: Simon said, “Please pray to the Lord, so that what you said won't happen to me.”
translation_verse: Simon ebuiliulũ asɛ, “Nokulu Apaleteɛ nɛ mɛ ɛtakpu, ɔsɛ tɛ ebui alɛ gɛ nɔɖɔɔ nanà atɛba mɛabha.

english_verse: After Peter and John had preached about the Lord, they returned to Jerusalem. On their way they told the good news in many villages of Samaria. Philip and an Ethiopian Official
translation_verse: Gɛ baɖɔ kibuiɛ alɔkɔ adalɛ Apaleteɛ shu ebekeɛ, Petro nɛ̀ Yohanes etsodzi etsyina avɛ Yerusalem, pɛ badaaɖɔ kibui gbangbã nɛ Samaria ɛma bhlui gelee nɔ mɛ. Filipo Nɛ̀ Etiopiaeyiɛ

english_verse: The Lord's angel said to Philip, “Go south along the desert road that leads from Jerusalem to Gaza.”
translation_verse: Apaleteɛ yɛaxɔ̃ɛdɛ teli ebetseɖe Filipo asɛ, “Lɔ tɛ ɔvɛ kidzoɔ gɛlɛ kanɔ nɔ̀ bɔpialɛ ɔkɔ, oxui kidzoɔ gɛizhi odenɔ Yerusalem pɛ kɛvɛ Gaza nɔ.”

english_verse: So Philip left. An important Ethiopian official happened to be going along that road in his chariot. He was the chief treasurer for Candace, the Queen of Ethiopia. The official had gone to Jerusalem to worship
translation_verse: Kliso akpɛ kidzogagã niesi kɛlɛ gɛ avɛ nɛ kidzoɔ mɛɛ, adɛtsyana Etiopiaeyi teli, anɔ gbagbla gɛ batsarà pɛ eɖi Etiopianɛ adzɛ ekusiɛ Kandake yɛɔdɔ̃ pete abhaa. Anyɛ alɛ avɛ Yerusalem asɛ tɛ adɛkpɛ kɛtakpu esi,

english_verse: and was now on his way home. He was sitting in his chariot, reading the book of the prophet Isaiah.
translation_verse: kɛlɛ gɛ azã apaka ohuɔ mɛ pɛ etsyina avɛ kepiɛɛ, azakalà ebuiɖɔluanɔ Yesaya keplukpaa.

english_verse: The Spirit told Philip to catch up with the chariot.
translation_verse: Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ otseɖe Filipo ɔsɛ, “Vɛ apaka ohu ɔlɛ shu, tɛ ɔdɛzã ɔlɛ ɔbhà.”

english_verse: Philip ran up close and heard the man reading aloud from the book of Isaiah. Philip asked him, “Do you understand what you are reading?”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ Filipo ebu tsɛtsɛɖɛ mɛ avɛ apaka ohuɔ shu, kɛlɛ elũ gɛ anyɛɛ aakalà ebuiɖɔluanɔ Yesaya keplukpa. Filipo ebisi anyɛ asɛ, “Olũ kɛgɛ ɔɔkalà niesiɛ dza?”

english_verse: The official answered, “How can I understand unless someone helps me?” He then invited Philip to come up and sit beside him.
translation_verse: Ebuiliulũ asɛ, “Kɛlɛ mɛbalà lũ alɛ esi, gɛ onupɛ ananà egibu alɛ̀ esi akɔ mɛ dza?” Kliso etseɖe Filipo asɛ tɛ ewu aba tɛ abazã yɛɔbha.

english_verse: The man was reading the passage that said, “He was led like a sheep on its way to be killed. He was silent as a lamb whose wool is being cut off, and he did not say a word.
translation_verse: Anyɛ gɛ batsarà azakalà Owusoso Akaa ebui alɛ ɔsɛ, “Bagbana yɛ asɛ obhoshi ana avɛnɔ obhoshi eyiyieleteɛ ɔkɔ, nɛ̀ asɛ obhoshieyi gɛ lɔdã olugbũ nɛ agɛ yɛasɔ̃ tɛkã nɛ ɔlɛ shuɔ shu, kidzo ɛlɛ nabha yɛtsyɛ adã yɛolugbũ.

english_verse: He was treated like a nobody and did not receive a fair trial. How can he have children, if his life is snatched away?”
translation_verse: Nɛ gɛezhinɔ yɛishu esi soɔ, begikulu kebhu gbangbã akɔɛ. Afana gɛ abalà kaɖɔ ɔɖɔ̀ nɛ yɛeshui alɛ shu? Ɔmɔsɛ bebu yɛkpã eyinɔ nɛ kesiakpa alɛ abha.”

english_verse: The official said to Philip, “Tell me, was the prophet talking about himself or about someone else?”
translation_verse: Anyɛ gɛ batsarà ebisi Filipo asɛ, “Ɛkɔwɔ ɛtakpu, afana shu ebuiɖɔluanɔ aɖɔaɖɔɔ? Yɛahɔɛ ishu pɛnɔsɛ anɔ bhlatɔ shu dza?”

english_verse: So Philip began at this place in the Scriptures and explained the good news about Jesus.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ Filipo akpɛ esi edenɔ yɛibuiɛ gɛ akalà nabha kɛlɛ etseɖe Yesu shu kibui gbangbã.

english_verse: After they had come out of the water, the Lord's Spirit took Philip away. The official never saw him again, but he was very happy as he went on his way.
translation_verse: Gɛ bekoeyì nɛ bulĩɛ mɛɛ, nɛ matìmatì mɛɛ, Apaleteɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ ɔbɔgbana Filipo ashɛnɔ, kɛlɛ anyɛ aga mɔɛ eɖi, pɔ avɛ yɛidzo gagã nabha nɛ̀ same mɔmɔ mɛ.

english_verse: Philip later appeared in Azotus. He went from town to town, all the way to Caesarea, telling people about Jesus.
translation_verse: Filipo edikoeyi nɛ ɔma toli gɛ bekulu asɛ Azotoɔ mɛ, kɛlɛ azanyɔ afala pɛ aaɖɔ kibui gbangbã nɛ ɔma ka nyia ka mɛ, ɔvɛnɔ kibeɛ gɛ ediku Kaisarea.

english_verse: Saul preached with such power that he completely confused the Jewish people in Damascus, as he tried to show them that Jesus is the Messiah.
translation_verse: Pɔ ohumi nɔ gɛ lɔzã Saulo mɛ lɔzɔ gagalĩ ɔtɔɖɔ, kɛlɛ kɛbhɛtɛ Yudanɛ gɛazã Damasko nɛibi gɛ azadalɛ kɛlɛ mɛ ɔsɛ Yesu yɛenu Kristoɔ.

english_verse: Now get up and go into the city, where you will be told what to do.”
translation_verse: “Lɔ otsiɛ tɛ̀ ɔvɛ ɔmahohoeɛ mɛ, kɛlɛ bebètseɖewɔ kɛgɛ̀ kɛlɛ ɔsɛ̀ wɔbhɛtɛɛ.”

english_verse: I will show him how much he must suffer for worshiping in my name.”
translation_verse: Ɛbakasɛ yɛ bɔtɔ nɔnɔ́ɛ ɛdi gɛ kɛlɛ ɔsɛ amɔ nɛ mɛinye nɛ takpu.”

english_verse: When Saul arrived in Jerusalem, he tried to join the followers. But they were all afraid of him, because they did not believe he was a true follower.
translation_verse: Gɛ Saulo ebeku Yerusalemuɔ, apɛ asɛ tɛ̀ etsoku badɔkpasɛnɛ nɛishi, pɔ balɛ nyɔ̃ɔ ezesi yɛifu, bagɛza ayɛkɛ aŋã asɛ adɔkpasɛnɛ gɛ babà yɛkɛ yɛabha aŋã yɛenu.

english_verse: and he has seen a vision. He saw a man named Ananias coming to him and putting his hands on him, so he could see again.”
translation_verse: Nɛ bɔlala mɛɛ, Saulo amɔ anyɛ teli gɛ bekulu asɛ Anania gɛaba yɛɔbha pɛ abatɔɖɔ ahɔɛ nɛ yɛabha asɛ tɛ amɔ bɔdɔ̃.”

english_verse: Suddenly something like fish scales fell from Saul's eyes, and he could see. He got up and was baptized.
