translation_verse: Tsiɔ̃ ɔlɛ koɔ, kɛdɔ̃ tili asɛ akɔlɔ ana wɔlɛ nɛ Saulo yɛawuhũ nabha, kɛlɛ azakpa bɔdɔ̃ mɔ. Alɔ kɛlɛ bakpɛ bulĩ nɛ kɛtakpu akɔɛ,

english_verse: Saul kept on threatening to kill the Lord's followers. He even went to the high priest
translation_verse: Nɛ kibe ɛlɛ, Saulo ezewlui akãkɔ Apaleteɛ yɛadɔkpasɛanɛ. Atɔnɔ banɔlũazazã gbagbla avɛ

english_verse: and asked for letters to the leaders of the synagogues in Damascus. He did this because he wanted to arrest and take to Jerusalem any man or woman who had accepted the Lord's Way.
translation_verse: kɛlɛ edibisi buplukpa asɛ bakɔɛ tɛ avɛ ɛtsyɔakɔ nɛ Damasko, sɛ tɛ onupɛ amɔ balɛ ɔmayɛ teli nɛ Kidzo Ɛlɛ Nabha, onupɛ benu banyɛ alo badzɛ tsyɛ, tɛ evũ alɛ asɛ ɔɖavũmɛ elete ana avɛnɔ Yerusalem.

english_verse: When Saul had almost reached Damascus, a bright light from heaven suddenly flashed around him.
translation_verse: Gɛ Saulo agã idzoɔ pɛ etso aplɛnɔ Damasko ebekeɛ, nɛ yɛ matìmatì mɛɛ, kifu boele odenɔ kititíɛ bakana yɛishu.

english_verse: He fell to the ground and heard a voice saying, “Saul! Saul! Why are you so cruel to me?”
translation_verse: Eglebe adɔ kesiakpa pɛ elũ ozĩ toli gɛ lotseɖe yɛɔsɛ “Saulo, Saulo! Kiɖeso pɛ ɔtɔnɔ miede ana?”

english_verse: “Who are you?” Saul asked. “I am Jesus,” the Lord answered. “I am the one you are so cruel to.
translation_verse: Saulo ebisi asɛ, “Apalete, wonu afana?” Ebuiliulũ akɔɛ asɛ, “Mɛinu Yesu, anɔ nɔ̀ gɛ yiede wɔtɔnɔɔ.”

english_verse: The men with Saul stood there speechless. They had heard the voice, but they had not seen anyone.
translation_verse: Banunɔ gɛazã gã kidzoɔ ɔplɛnɔ Saulo awɔeli kititi kaɖɔ maɖɔmaɖɔ, belũ kaɖɔ, pɔ bagɛmɔ anɔ nanà.

english_verse: Saul got up from the ground, and when he opened his eyes, he could not see a thing. Someone then led him by the hand to Damascus,
translation_verse: Saulo alɔ nɛ kesiakpa, pɔ gɛ adalɛ yɛawuhũɔ, lagɛ zamɔ kɛdɔ̃ nanà. Kliso banyana yɛahɔɛ pɛ bagbana yɛ avɛnɔ Damasko ɔmamɛ.

english_verse: and for three days he was blind and did not eat or drink.
translation_verse: Nɛ ewui tata mɛɛ, Saulo edzi awuhũpu, kɛlɛ agɛŋã bɔdɔ̃ alo pɛ awɔ̃ɛ kɛdɔ̃ nanà tsyɛ.

english_verse: A follower named Ananias lived in Damascus, and the Lord spoke to him in a vision. Ananias answered, “Lord, here I am.”
translation_verse: Nɛ Damasko ɔma mɛɛ, adɔkpasɛnɛ teli azã ɛkɛlɛ gɛ bezekulu yɛasɛ Anania. Apaleteɛ ebu yɛishu akasi nɛ bɔlala mɛ asɛ, “Anania!” Adɛmɛ akɔɛ asɛ, “Apalete, mɛ nɛ alɛ.”

english_verse: The Lord said to him, “Get up and go to the house of Judas on Straight Street. When you get there, you will find a man named Saul from the city of Tarsus. Saul is praying,
translation_verse: Apaleteɛ etseɖe yɛasɛ, “Lɔ tɛ ɔvɛ Yuda bɔpã mɛ nɛ Tipɔɔ Tĩi nabha tɛ odibisi anyɛ teli gɛede Tarso pɛ bekulu asɛ Sauloɔ nɛtɔ, ɔmɔsɛ eekulu Owusoso Akaa.”

english_verse: Ananias replied, “Lord, a lot of people have told me about the terrible things this man has done to your followers in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Anania ebuiliulũ asɛ, “Apalete, bakɔ mɛ tititì gelee nɛ anyɛ alɛ shu, nɛ̀ bɔdɔ̃ gɛ ezĩ nɛ banɔ dadalɛ shu nɛ Yerusalemuɔ.

english_verse: Now the chief priests have given him the power to come here and arrest anyone who worships in your name.”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ aba ɛkɛlɛ nɛ̀ ohumi edenɔ banɔlũazazã agbagbla ɔkɔ asɛ tɛ ebevũ anɔ ka nyia ka gɛekulu wɔɛnyeɛ.”

english_verse: The Lord said to Ananias, “Go! I have chosen him to tell foreigners, kings, and the people of Israel about me.
translation_verse: Pɔ Apaleteɛ etseɖe Anania asɛ, “Gavɛ! Anyɛ alɛ enu anɔ gɛ ibú ɔsɛ tɛ̀ alɔkɔ mɛinyeɛ avɛnɔ bagɛ betinu Yudanɛ nɛ̀ balɛ bekusiɛ nɛ̀ Israel kɛmadiɛ ɔkɔ.

english_verse: Ananias left and went into the house where Saul was staying. Ananias placed his hands on him and said, “Saul, the Lord Jesus has sent me. He is the same one who appeared to you along the road. He wants you to be able to see and to be filled with the Holy Spirit.”
translation_verse: Kliso Anania avɛ bɔpã mɛ, kɛlɛ editsoku bɔlɛ̀ mɛ. Atɔɖɔ ahɔɛ nɛ Saulo abha pɛ etseɖe yɛasɛ, “Mɛadzya Saulo, Apalete Yesuɔ gɛ ebu yɛishu akasɛ wɔ nɛidzoɔ mɛ gɛɔba ɛkɛlɛ, yɛakpɛ mɛ nɛ wɔkɔ, tɛ̀ wɔ awuhũɔ adalɛ tɛ̀ Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã oyuku wɔ mɛ.”

english_verse: Then he ate and felt much better. Saul Preaches in Damascus For several days Saul stayed with the Lord's followers in Damascus.
translation_verse: kɛlɛ gɛ bakɔɛ kɛdɔ̃ tili yɛaŋã ebekeɛ, azamɔ yɛohumi nɔ. Saulo Aɖɔ Owusoso Akaa Kibuiɛ Nɛ Damasko Nɛ̀ Yerusalem Saulo azã aplɛnɔ badɔkpasɛanɛ nɛ Damasko ewui gelee.

english_verse: Soon he went to the synagogues and started telling people that Jesus is the Son of God.
translation_verse: Odunu ɔlɛ koɔ, Saulo akpɛ bɔdɔ̃ kakasɛ esi nɛ ɛtsyɔakɔ asɛ Yesu yɛenu Owusoso Akaa Yɛeyiɛ.

english_verse: Everyone who heard Saul was amazed and said, “Isn't this the man who caused so much trouble for those people in Jerusalem who worship in the name of Jesus? Didn't he come here to arrest them and take them to the chief priests?”
translation_verse: Kɛbhɛtɛ banunɔ gɛ bazataatɔ̃ elũ yɛebuiɛ petee nɛibi kɛlɛ bebisi asɛ, “Gɛnɛ anyɛ alɛ yɛaza atɔɖɔ kɛtakpu mɛ̀ zizĩ nɛ banunɔ gɛ beze kulu Yesu kinye ɛlɛ dza? Pɔ gɛnɛ yɛazaba ɛkɛlɛ̀ ebeevũ banunɔ pɛ azagbanà alɛ avɛnɔ banɔlũazazã gbagbla ɔkɔ asɛ ɔɖavũmɛ azazãa dza?”

english_verse: Later some of them made plans to kill Saul,
translation_verse: Gɛ ewui gelee teli abafala, Yudanɛ atɔɖɔ asɛ tɛ beyìi,

english_verse: but he found out about it. He learned that they were guarding the gates of the city day and night in order to kill him.
translation_verse: pɔ Saulo elũ balɛ ebui tɔtɔɖɔ nɛishuɔ. Yudanɛ ezepo budulũɔ elishi nɛ̀ kemuze tɛbevũi eyí.

english_verse: Then one night his followers let him down over the city wall in a large basket. Saul in Jerusalem
translation_verse: Pɔ yiede azazã adɛgbana yɛ elishi pɛ bakpɛ yɛ nɛ kashɔ̀ mɛ pɛ bezhini yɛ aganà ozĩ toli gɛ lɔzã kigiliɛ mɛ.

english_verse: Then Barnabas helped him by taking him to the apostles. He explained how Saul had seen the Lord and how the Lord had spoken to him. Barnabas also said when Saul was in Damascus, he had spoken bravely in the name of Jesus.
translation_verse: Pɔ Banaba agbanà yɛ kɛlɛ alɔkɔ yɛavɛnɔ banɔkpɛkpɛ. Etseɖe balɛ kana gɛ Saulo amɔ Apaleteɛ nɛ yɛidzo gagã mɛ, kana gɛ Apaleteɛ aɖɔaɖɔ niyɛ, ɔplɛnɔ kana gɛ adɛɖɔ kibui Gbangbã nɛ kifu masi masi mɛ nɛ Damasko nɛ Yesu kinyeɛ mɛɛ.

english_verse: Saul moved about freely with the followers in Jerusalem and told everyone about the Lord.
translation_verse: Kliso Saulo azã balɛ ɔbha pɛ azagã afala nɛ ishu yɔyɔ mɛ̀ nɛ̀ Yerusalem ɔmamɛ pɛ azaɖɔaɖɔ nɛ Apalete nɛinyeɛ mɛ.

english_verse: He was always arguing with the Jews who spoke Greek, and so they tried to kill him.
translation_verse: Aɖɔ kaɖɔ alɔkɔ apɔ̃ɛ ebui mɛ nɛ̀ Yudanɛ gɛenu Helanɛ, pɔ bapɛ asɛ tɛ beyii.

english_verse: But the followers found out about this and took Saul to Caesarea. From there they sent him to the city of Tarsus.
translation_verse: Gɛ badzya ebetì kibui ɛlɛ, bagbana yɛ ezhinɔ avɛnɔ Kaisarea, kɛlɛ odenɔ ɛkɛlɛ, basɛsii nɛ Tarso.

english_verse: The church in Judea, Galilee, and Samaria now had a time of peace and kept on worshiping the Lord. The church became stronger, as the Holy Spirit encouraged it and helped it grow. Peter Heals Aeneas
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ ɔhaa petee gɛzã Yudea, Galilea nɛ̀ Samaria ɔdɔ̃ mɛɛ abamɔ ishu yɔyɔ nɛ kibè ɛlɛ. Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã ɔkpɛ kemì ɔkɔ ɔhaa, kɛlɛ ɔhamɛ banunɔ ekpè atɔɖɔ kɛlɛ bazã nɛ̀ ɛkpã pɛ beesi Apaleteɛ nifù. Ainea Nɛ̀ Tabita

english_verse: While Peter was traveling from place to place, he visited the Lord's followers who lived in the town of Lydda.
translation_verse: Gɛ Petro anyɔ afala nɛ kesiakpa alɛ nabhaa, avɛ asɛ ediɖi banɔ dadalɛ nɛ Lida ɔmamɛ.

english_verse: There he met a man named Aeneas, who for eight years had been sick in bed and could not move.
translation_verse: Ɛkɛlɛ yɛadɛ̀ tɔnɔ anyɛ teli gɛ bezekului asɛ Ainea, anɔ gɛ agbã pɛ azã oputso abha agɔ tasɛ nɛ alɛ.

english_verse: Peter said to Aeneas, “Jesus Christ has healed you! Get up and make up your bed.” At once he stood up.
translation_verse: Petro etseɖe yɛasɛ, “Ainea, Yesu Kristo ebu bɔnyãa akɔ wɔ. Lɔ tɛ̀ olù wɔoputso nɔ.” Odunũ ɔlɛ koɔ, kɛlɛ Ainea alɔ.

english_verse: Many people in the towns of Lydda and Sharon saw Aeneas and became followers of the Lord. Peter Brings Dorcas Back to Life
translation_verse: Bagɛ bazã Lida nɛ̀ Saron kibeɛ bamɔ yɛ kɛlɛ betsyina atɔnɔ Apaleteɛ.

english_verse: In Joppa there was a follower named Tabitha. Her Greek name was Dorcas, which means “deer.” She was always doing good things for people and had given much to the poor.
translation_verse: Nɛ Yopa ɔmamɛɛ, adɔkpasɛnɛ teli gɛ bezekulu yɛasɛ Tabita azã ɛkɛlɛ. Tabita azabhɛtɛ ebui gbangbã pɛ alɔkɔ aplɛnɔ betsyuitsyuiɛ nɔ.

english_verse: But she got sick and died, and her body was washed and placed in an upstairs room.
translation_verse: Nɛ kibe ɛlɛ abadɔ bɔnyã kɛlɛ etsí, kɛlɛ bewulu niyɛ etsitsíɛ pɛ bavɛnɔ yɛedi lu kabhavũ tulì mɛ.

english_verse: Joppa wasn't far from Lydda, and the followers heard that Peter was there. They sent two men to say to him, “Please come with us as quickly as you can!”
translation_verse: Lida ɔmãa otsò ɔplɛnɔ Yopa, kliso gɛ badɔkpasɛanɛ elũ asɛ Petro azã Lida ɔmãmɛɛ, bakpɛ banɔ taabha nɛ yiede tɛ̀ betseɖe yɛasɛ, “Budzu kɛtakpu esi kɔ wɔ, ba tsĩɔɔ ɔlɛ!”

english_verse: At once, Peter went with them. The men took Peter upstairs into the room. Many widows were there crying. They showed him the coats and clothes that Dorcas had made while she was still alive.
translation_verse: Petro avɛ nɛ̀ alɛ, kɛlɛ gɛ ediku ɛkɛlɛɛ, bagbani yɛavɛnɔ kabhavũɔ mɛ. Kɛlɛ bavɛvɛ edziniɛ alɛ pete abalɔɛtití nɛ Petro ɔbha, pɛ bezebi yoyo kɛlɛ balɔkɔ bɔdɔ̃ kpɛkpɛ gɛ Tabita eyú nɛ kibeɛ gɛ azã balɛ ɔbha aakasɛ alɛ.

english_verse: After Peter had sent everyone out of the room, he knelt down and prayed. Then he turned to the body of Dorcas and said, “Tabitha, get up!” The woman opened her eyes, and when she saw Peter, she sat up.
translation_verse: Petro akakà banunɔ pete ebu nɛ buvũɔ mɛ, kɛlɛ edzu ekluisi esi pɛ ekulu kitití ayɛ. Kɛlɛ gɛ etsyina yɛamɛ akpɛ adzɛ ɔkɔɔ, etseɖe yɛasɛ, “Tabita, lɔɛtití!” Adalɛ yɛawuhũɔ kɛlɛ gɛ amɔ Petroɔ, alɔ azã kititì.

english_verse: He took her by the hand and helped her to her feet. Peter called in the widows and the other followers and showed them that Dorcas had been raised from death.
translation_verse: Petro anyana yɛahɔkpɔɔ, pɛ aplɛni yɛalɔ azã yɛtsɛɖɛ nabha. Kɛlɛ ekulu bayɛyɛkɛŋã nɛ̀ bavɛvɛ edzini alɛ pɛ alɔkɔɛ akpɛ ahɔɛ akɔ alɛ enunù.

english_verse: Everyone in Joppa heard what had happened, and many of them put their faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Kibui ɛlɛ bayìfala nɛ Yopa bɔdɔ̃ mɛ, kɛlɛ banɔ kpekpè abayɛkɛ Apalete nabha aŋã.

english_verse: Peter stayed on for a while in Joppa in the house of a man named Simon, who made leather.
translation_verse: Petro azã bagãpi axɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa elete teli gɛ bezekulu asɛ Simon ɔkɔ nɛ Yopa ɔdɛ nanà kibè tili.

english_verse: In Caesarea there was a man named Cornelius, who was the captain of a group of soldiers called “The Italian Unit.”
translation_verse: Nɛ Kaisarea nɔ, anyɛ teli azã gɛ bezekulu yɛasɛ Kornelio, edzi anɔ gɛ agbana keyuiza asɛ banɔ akpã gɛ bezekulu asɛ Italia Keyuizaa.

english_verse: Cornelius was a very religious man. He worshiped God, and so did everyone else who lived in his house. He had given a lot of money to the poor and was always praying to God.
translation_verse: Yɛnɛ̀ yɛɔpãa petee lɔkɔ balɛishu akɔ pɛ Owusoso Akaa kifùsisi zã alɛ mɛ. Azabhɛtɛ ɛyamɛ gbangbã ɔkɔ betsyuitsyuiɛ pɛ azã Owusoso Akaa tikukulu abha kibe nyia be.

english_verse: One afternoon at about three o'clock, Cornelius had a vision. He saw an angel from God coming to him and calling him by name.
translation_verse: Kiwui tinikpo asɛ kemuze ɔɖatàta tɛtà mɛ anaa, bebu kibui tili akasɛ yɛ. Amɔ Owusoso Akaa axɔ̃ɛdɛ teli gɛ aba yɛɔkɔ pɛ ebetseɖe yɛasɛ, “Kornelio!”

english_verse: Cornelius was surprised and stared at the angel. Then he asked, “What is this all about?” The angel answered, “God has heard your prayers and knows about your gifts to the poor.
translation_verse: Kornelio akpɛ awɔ̃ eɖi axɔ̃ɛdɛ nɛ kifu sisi mɛ pɛ ebisi yɛasɛ, “Apalete, kibui ɖuwoe?” Axɔ̃ɛdɛ ebuiliulũ akɔɛ yɛasɛ, “Kikuluɔ gɛ okulu Owusoso Akaa nɛ̀ kana gɛ ɔkɔ betsyuitsyuiɛ bɔdɔ̃ɔ bayì nɛ Owusoso Akaa namɛ kɛlɛ kiku yɛawɔ̃.

english_verse: Now send some men to Joppa for a man named Simon Peter.
translation_verse: Kpɛ̀ banɔ ɔsɛsɛ Yopa tɛ̀ bekulu anyɛ teli gɛ bekulu yɛasɛ Simon, pɛ bekèkulu tsyɛ asɛ Petro ɔmana.

english_verse: He is staying with Simon the leather maker, who lives in a house near the sea.”
translation_verse: Alɛ Simon gɛenu bagãpi axɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa eleteɛ nɔpãmɛ, anɔ gɛ yɛɔpã lɛ aƒuɔ shuɔ.”

english_verse: After saying this, the angel left. Cornelius called in two of his servants and one of his soldiers who worshiped God.
translation_verse: Gɛ axɔ̃ɛdɛ gɛ aɖɔaɖɔ nɛ yɛ ashɛɛ, Kornelio ekulu yɛaxɔ̃ɛdɛ taabha, nɛ̀ keyuadɛdɛ tenikpo gɛ adɛ yɛaxɔ̃ɛ, pɛ ekènu anɔ gɛ aplɛnɔ yɛ tsyɛ amana.

english_verse: He explained everything to them and sent them off to Joppa.
translation_verse: Etseɖe balɛ kɛka nyia ka gɛba ɛlɛ mɛɛ, kɛlɛ akpɛ alɛ nɛ Yopa. Petro Yɛɔlalà Zaza

english_verse: The next day about noon these men were coming near Joppa. Peter went up on the roof of the house to pray
translation_verse: Gɛ keliɛ aza asɛ ɔɖa tatá lɔfɔ nɛ̀ taabha mɛ gɛ bazã kidzo gagã nabha pɛ betso aplɛnɔ ɔmaa, Petro ewu avɛ buvũɔ adamɛ ɔkɔ tɛ edikulu Owusoso Akaa.

english_verse: and became very hungry. While the food was being prepared, he fell sound asleep and had a vision.
translation_verse: Bɔbhɛ ziyii kɛlɛ apɛ kɛdɔ̃ tili tɛ aŋã, kɛlɛ gɛ batɔ bɔdɔ̃ɔ, ezhi eku taɖa mɛ.

english_verse: He saw heaven open, and something came down like a huge sheet held up by its four corners.
translation_verse: Amɔ gɛ kititíɛ gɛ dalɛ dzi, kɛlɛ amɔ kɛdɔ̃ tili gɛ kidzi asɛ kɛpɔtɛ hohoe tili ana, gɛ bezhinɔ ɛlɛ esi nɛ kɛlɛ batɔ̃ talɛ mɛ.

english_verse: In it were all kinds of animals, reptiles, and birds.
translation_verse: Bɔtsɛɖɛkpɔ tɔɔlɛ̃ bagã ediedi, bagã gɛetsì nɛ kesiakpa, ɔplɛnɔ kititì bɔzhɔɛ ediedi azã kɛpɔtɛ ɛlɛ mɛ.

english_verse: A voice said to him, “Peter, get up! Kill these and eat them.”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ ozĩ toli otseɖe yɛɔsɛ, “Petro, lɔ tɛ oyi bagã alɛ ɔtã!”

english_verse: But Peter said, “Lord, I can't do that! I've never eaten anything that is unclean and not fit to eat.”
translation_verse: Petro ebuiliulũ asɛ, “Apalete, ɛtɛbalà, ɛka ŋã kɛdɔ̃ nanà gɛ kɛlɛishu gɛdalɛ alo pɛ kigibhui oɖi.”

english_verse: The voice spoke to him again, “When God says that something can be used for food, don't say it isn't fit to eat.”
translation_verse: Kaɖɔ ezeyì bɔbhalà etseɖe Petro asɛ, “Tɔokulu kɛdɔ̃ nanà gɛ Owusoso Akaa abhɛtɛ pɛ kɛdalɛ sɛ kɛlɛishu gibhui.”

english_verse: This happened three times before the sheet was suddenly taken back to heaven.
translation_verse: Kibui ɛlɛ ba ɛlɛ mɛ̀ bɔba tɛta, kɛlɛ odunu ɔlɛ koɔ, bedzini kɛpɔtɛɛ efeke ewunɔ avɛnɔ kititíɛ.

english_verse: Peter was still wondering what all this meant, when the men sent by Cornelius came and stood at the gate. They had found their way to Simon's house
translation_verse: Kibeɛ gɛ bɔlala lɛ zɛbhɛtɛ Petro ibiɛ, banunɔ gɛ Kornelio akpɛ adɛmɔ ɛkɛlɛ gɛ Simon bɔpãa lɛ kɛlɛ bawɔeli kedulũɔ.

english_verse: and were asking if Simon Peter was staying there.
translation_verse: Bebisi nɛ̀ ozĩ tɛ betì, onupɛ bɔpãlɛ mɛ Simoniɛ gɛ bekulu asɛ Petroɔ alɛ.

english_verse: While Peter was still thinking about the vision, the Holy Spirit said to him, “Three men are here looking for you.
translation_verse: Kibeɛ gɛ Petro aza bhɛtɛ ekpeɖekpeɖe mɛ nɛ bɔlala shuɔ, Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ otseɖe yɛɔsɛ, “Simon, banyɛ tatá abapɛ wɔ.

english_verse: Hurry down and go with them. Don't worry, I sent them.”
translation_verse: Kliso lɔ tɛ̀ ozhi ɔvɛ kesiɛ. Tizĩ kibè nɛ tɛvɛvɛ nɛ alɛ ɔmɔsɛ mɛkpɛ alɛ nɛ wɔkɔ.”

english_verse: Peter went down and said to the men, “I am the one you are looking for. Why have you come?”
translation_verse: Petro ezhi avɛ banunɔ ɔbha kɛlɛ editseɖe balɛ asɛ, “Mɛinu anunɔ gɛ nɔpɛ nɔ. Kiɖuwoe sopɛ nɔba?”

english_verse: They answered, “Captain Cornelius sent us. He is a good man who worships God and is liked by the Jewish people. One of God's holy angels told Cornelius to send for you, so he could hear what you have to say.”
translation_verse: Banyɛ ebuiliulũ asɛ, “Bude Keyuiza otugbànɔ Kornelio ɔkɔ. Enu anɔ gɛ yɛishu dalɛ pɛ esi Owusoso Akaa nɛifù, anɔ gɛ Yudanɛ petee adɛmɛ yɛibui. Axɔ̃ɛdɛ dadalɛ teli etseɖe yɛasɛ akɔ wɔba yɛɔpã mɛ tɛ̀ ebelũ wɔebuiɛ gɛ ɔpɛ ɔsɛ tɛ̀ ɔɖɔ otseɖe yɛ.”

english_verse: Peter invited them to spend the night. The next morning, Peter and some of the Lord's followers in Joppa left with the men who had come from Cornelius.
translation_verse: Kliso Petro ayɛkɛ banyɛ akpɛ yɛɔpã mɛ asɛ yefù ana. Petro Avɛ Kornelio Pãmɛ Gɛ keliɛ azaa, Petro avɛ nɛ̀ balɛ, kɛlɛ badzya teli nɛ Yopa tsyɛ atɔnɔ balɛ avɛ.

english_verse: The next day they arrived in Caesarea where Cornelius was waiting for them. He had also invited his relatives and close friends.
translation_verse: Bediku Kaisarea nɛ kelizazaa mɛ̀. Kornelio ezeɖi balɛ kidzo mɛ kliso ekulu yɛeshopo alɛ nɛ̀ yɛɔbhia alɛ eshogò.

english_verse: When Peter arrived, Cornelius greeted him. Then he knelt at Peter's feet and started worshiping him.
translation_verse: Gɛ Petro etsoku bɔpã mɛɛ, Kornelio abatsyana yɛ kɛlɛ edzu ekluisi esi nɛ yɛotugbà tɛ alɔkɔ akasɛ ishu esi zhizhinɔ ɔkɔɛ.

english_verse: But Peter took hold of him and said, “Stand up! I am nothing more than a human.”
translation_verse: Pɔ Petro efeke yɛ ewunɔ pɛ aɖɔ asɛ, “Lɔɛtití, anɔdzyrame ete mɛtsyɛ inu.”

english_verse: As Peter entered the house, he was still talking with Cornelius. Many people were there,
translation_verse: Kibeɛ gɛ azaɖɔaɖɔ niyɛ̀ɛ, Petro etsoku kɛmɛɛ kɛlɛ adɛ tɔnɔ banɔɛza gbagbla tili gɛishogò dzì.

english_verse: and Peter said to them, “You know that we Jews are not allowed to have anything to do with other people. But God has shown me that he doesn't think anyone is unclean or unfit.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ Petro etseɖe alɛ asɛ, “Nonyi piapii ɔsɛ, ɔsha ogɛkɔ kidzo ɔsɛ Yudaeyi nanà akpɛ ɔha nɛ̀ anunɔ gɛetinu Yudaeyi alo tɛ̀ avɛ yɛɔkɔ tsyɛ. Pɔ Owusoso Akaa ebui ɛlɛ akasɛ mɛ asɛ, ɛtakà kulu anɔ nanà ɔsɛ̀ yɛishu gɛdalɛ alo ligibhui.

english_verse: I agreed to come here, but I want to know why you sent for me.”
translation_verse: Kliso gɛ nɔkpɛ banɔ nɛ miedeɛ, igishukù mɛtakpu pɛ ɛɖɔ ɔsɛ ɛtɛbà. Otsiɛ nɔkɔ mɛitì kɛnɔnɔ shu so pɛ nɔkpɛ banɔ nɛ miedeɛ.”

english_verse: Cornelius answered: Four days ago at about three o'clock in the afternoon I was praying at home. Suddenly a man in bright clothes stood in front of me.
translation_verse: Kornelio ebuiliulũ asɛ, “Ewui talɛ ede nɛ alɛ gɛizi kulu kitití ayɛ nɛ mɛɔpã mɛ, nɛ kemuze ɔɖa tatá tɛta mɛ. Nɛ matì matì mɛɛ, ɛmɔ anyɛ teli nɛ bɔdɔ̃ kpɛkpɛ gitséɛ mɛ gɛ eli kititì nɛ mɛotugbà.

english_verse: He said, “Cornelius, God has heard your prayers, and he knows about your gifts to the poor.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ aɖɔ asɛ, ‘Kornelio, Owusoso Akaa elũ wɔ tikukuluɔ gɛ okulu yɛ, kɛlɛ abakà ɛyamɛ gbangbã gɛ ɔbhɛtɛ ɔkɔ betsyuitsyuiɛ.

english_verse: Now send to Joppa for Simon Peter. He is visiting in the home of Simon the leather maker, who lives near the sea.”
translation_verse: Kpɛ banɔ nɛ Yopa tɛ̀ bedikulu Simon gɛ bekulu tsyɛ asɛ Petro ɔmana. Atsɛ nɛ Simon gɛenu bagãpi axɔ̃ɛwɔaelete ɔkɔ, anɔ gɛ yɛɔpã lɛ aƒuɔ shuɔ.’

english_verse: I sent for you at once, and you have been good enough to come. All of us are here in the presence of the Lord God, so that we can hear what he has to say.
translation_verse: Kliso odunũ ɔlɛ koɔ kɛlɛ mɛkpɛ banɔ nɛ wuiede, kɛlɛ apɛ asɛ ɔba. Bɔlɔ pete lɛ Owusoso Akaa namɛ pɛ bɔtaatɔ̃ nɛ̀ kɛka nyia ka gɛ Apaleteɛ akpɛ wɔahɔɛ asɛ tɛ̀ otseɖe bɔlɔɔ.”

english_verse: Peter then said: Now I am certain that God treats all people alike.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ Petro akpa kaɖɔɖɔ asɛ, “Otsi alɛ ilũ kɛlɛ esi piapii ɔsɛ, Owusoso Akaa etiɖì banɔ amɛ

english_verse: God is pleased with everyone who worships him and does right, no matter what nation they come from.
translation_verse: pɔ ayɛkɛ banɔ edenɔ kɛmadi ka nyia ka mɛ gɛ esì yɛifu pɛ abhɛtɛ kɛgɛ ɛlɛ kɛlɛ ɔzãkɔ.

english_verse: This is the same message that God gave to the people of Israel, when he sent Jesus Christ, the Lord of all, to offer peace to them.
translation_verse: Nonyi kibuiɛ gɛ Owusoso Akaa asɛsɛ Israelanɛ, asɛ tɛ baɖɔ ishu yɔyɔ kibui gbangbã ɔgana Yesu Kristo abha gɛenu Apalete akɔ kɛka nyia ka.

english_verse: You surely know what happened everywhere in Judea. It all began in Galilee after John had told everyone to be baptized.
translation_verse: Nonyi kɛgɛ ba ɛlɛ mɛ nɛ Yudea petee, bakpɛ kɛlɛ esi odenɔ Galilea, nɛ bulĩkpɛkpɛ kibui gbangbã gɛ Yohanes aaɖɔ niede,

english_verse: God gave the Holy Spirit and power to Jesus from Nazareth. He was with Jesus, as he went around doing good and healing everyone who was under the power of the devil.
translation_verse: kana gɛ Owusoso Akaa asara bɔbɔ akɔ Yesu Nazareteyiɛ nɛ̀ Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã nɛ̀ ohumi, nɛ kana gɛ azagã fala pɛ azabhɛtɛ ebui gbangbã, pɛ ebu banyã nɛ bagazã abosam yɛ ohumi niesi pete mɛ, ɔmɔsɛ Owusoso Akaa azãplɛnii.

english_verse: We all saw what Jesus did both in Israel and in the city of Jerusalem. Jesus was put to death on a cross.
translation_verse: “Bɔzã mɔ kɛka nyia ka gɛ abhɛtɛ nɛ Yudanɛ kesiakpa nabha nɛ̀ Yerusalem. Pɔ bakpalà yɛ nɛ osi shu yɛetsí.

english_verse: But three days later, God raised him to life and let him be seen.
translation_verse: Pɔ Owusoso Akaa efeke yɛeyinɔ nɛ bekpuɔ mɛ nɛ kiwui tramɛ nabha, kɛlɛ ebui yɛakasɛ banɔ.

english_verse: Not everyone saw him. He was seen only by us, who ate and drank with him after he was raised from death. We were the ones God chose to tell others about him.
translation_verse: Gɛnɛ anɔ ka nyia ka yɛamɔɛ. Pɔ bagɛ Owusoso Akaa ebu elu pɛ bamɔ ebuiɛ gɛ abhɛtɛɛ, nɛ bɔlɔ gɛ bɔŋã pɛ bɔwɔɛ nɛyɛ nɛ kibeɛ gɛ alɔ edenɔ bekpuɔ mɛ ede.

english_verse: God told us to announce clearly to the people that Jesus is the one he has chosen to judge the living and the dead.
translation_verse: Akpɛ ɔlɔ asɛ bɔɖɔ Kibui Gbangbã tseɖe banunɔ, tɛ̀ bɔkɔ beti asɛ yɛenu anunɔ gɛ Owusoso Akaa asara bɔbɔ akɔ asɛ tɛ ekulu kebhu nɛ̀ benunuɔ nɛ̀ betsitsíɛ petee.

english_verse: Every one of the prophets has said that all who have faith in Jesus will have their sins forgiven in his name.
translation_verse: Bebuiɖɔluanɛ pete aɖɔ nɛishu asɛ, anɔ ka nyia ka gɛ ayɛkɛ yɛabha ŋãa, babàlɔkɔ yɛ ebui kpa etsye yɛ gana yɛinye mɛ.”

english_verse: While Peter was still speaking, the Holy Spirit took control of everyone who was listening.
translation_verse: Kibeɛ gɛ Petro azaɖɔ ebui alɛ, Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã ɔba banunɔ gɛelũ kibuiɛ petee abha.

english_verse: Some Jewish followers of the Lord had come with Peter, and they were surprised that the Holy Spirit had been given to Gentiles.
translation_verse: Kɛbhɛtɛ bayɛyɛkɛŋã gɛ bebhui kɛhɔ̃a kɔ alɛ pɛ batɔnɔ Petro nɛibi ɔsɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã tɛyɛyɛkɛ ba bagɛ betinu Yudanɛ tsyɛ abha.

english_verse: Now they were hearing Gentiles speaking unknown languages and praising God. Peter said,
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ belũ gɛ bezehu beluhũ vɔvɔ pɛ baakpɛ Owusoso Akaa nɛinyè nɛitití. Kɛlɛ Petro aɖɔ asɛ,

english_verse: “These Gentiles have been given the Holy Spirit, just as we have! I am certain that no one would dare stop us from baptizing them.”
translation_verse: “Anɔ teli abalà abhɛtɛ ɛlɛ asɛ tɛ batɛ kpɛ bulĩ akɔ banɔ alɛ dza?” Balɛ tsyɛ ayɛkɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã asɛ kana gɛ bɔyɛkɛ̀ ɔlɛ ana.

english_verse: Peter ordered them to be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ, and they asked him to stay on for a few days.
translation_verse: Kliso akɔ bakpɛ bulĩ akɔ alɛ nɛ Yesu Kristo yɛinyeɛ mɛ. Kɛlɛ bebisi edenɔ Petro ɔkɔ asɛ tɛ̀ azã balɛ ɔbha ewui bhlui teli.

english_verse: The apostles and the followers in Judea heard that Gentiles had accepted God's message.
translation_verse: Banɔkpɛkpɛ nɛ̀ bagɛ ayɛkɛ aŋã nɛ Yudea bɔdɔ̃ mɛ pete ebelũ asɛ bagɛ betinu Yudanɛ tsyɛ ayɛkɛ Owusoso Akaa nɛibuiɛ.

english_verse: So when Peter came to Jerusalem, some of the Jewish followers started arguing with him. They wanted Gentile followers to be circumcised, and
translation_verse: Kliso gɛ Petro avɛ ediku Yerusalemuɔ, Yudanɛ gɛ bebhui kɛhɔ̃a akɔ baɖɔaɖɔ alɔɛtití nɛishu.

english_verse: they said, “You stayed in the homes of Gentiles, and you even ate with them!”
translation_verse: kɛlɛ baɖɔ asɛ, “Ɔdɛ́ bagɛ begibhui kɛhɔ̃ akɔ alɛ bapãmɛ pɛ ɔdɛŋã bɔdɔ̃ nɛ alɛ.”

english_verse: I was in the town of Joppa and was praying when I fell sound asleep and had a vision. I saw heaven open, and something like a huge sheet held by its four corners came down to me.
translation_verse: “Ɛzã Yopa ɔma mɛ pɛ izikulu Owusoso Akaa, kɛlɛ asɛ taɖamɛ anaa, ɛmɔ tibubu kasɛ. Ɛmɔ kɛdɔ̃ tilì gɛiyì kɛpɔtɛ gbagbla tili gɛ kizhi odenɔ kititiɛ ɔganà kɛlɛ batɔ̃ talɛ̃ mɛ, kɛlɛ kizhi ba ɛkɛlɛ gɛ ɛzãa.

english_verse: When I looked in it, I saw animals, wild beasts, reptiles, and birds.
translation_verse: Iɖi kɛpɔtɛ mɛ kɛlɛ ɛmɔ kesiakpa abha bagã ɛtsɛɖɛkpɔ tɔlɛ bagãa, bagãhoe, bagã gɛ bèetsì nɛ kesiakpa nɛ̀ kititì bɔzhɔɛ nɔ.

english_verse: I heard a voice saying to me, “Peter, get up! Kill these and eat them.”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ ilũ ozĩ toli gɛotseɖe mɛ ɔsɛ, ‘Petro lɔ, yi alɛ tɛ̀ ɔtã.’

english_verse: But I said, “Lord, I can't do that! I've never taken a bite of anything that is unclean and not fit to eat.”
translation_verse: “Ibuiliulũ ɔsɛ, ‘Apalete, ɛtɛ balà! Kɛdɔ nanà ma gɛ kɛlɛishu gɛdalɛ alo pɛ kɛlɛishu gibhuiɛ tiku mɛ olugbũ mɛ oɖi.’

english_verse: The voice from heaven spoke to me again, “When God says that something can be used for food, don't say it isn't fit to eat.”
translation_verse: “Kaɖɔ ezeyì bɔbhalà odenɔ kititíɛ ɔsɛ, ‘Tɛokulu kɛdɔ̃ nɔnɔ gɛ Owusoso Akaa adalɛ kɛlɛishuɔ sɛ kɛdɔ̃ gɛgi bhui.’

english_verse: This happened three times before it was all taken back into heaven.
translation_verse: Kibui ɛlɛ ba ɛlɛ mɛ bɔba tɛ̀ta, kɛlɛ beze feke kɛpɔtɛ ewunɔ avɛnɔ kititíɛ.

english_verse: Suddenly three men from Caesarea stood in front of the house where I was staying.
translation_verse: “Nɛ kibe ɛlɛɛ koɔ, banyɛ tatá gɛ bakpɛ edenɔ Kaisarea nɔ abawɔ eli bɔpã mɛ gɛ ɛzãa.”

english_verse: The Holy Spirit told me to go with them and not to worry. Then six of the Lord's followers went with me to the home of a man
translation_verse: Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ otseɖe mɛ ɔsɛ, ɛlɔ tɔnɔ̀ alɛ ɔvɛ. Badzya tehlo alɛ tsyɛ avɛ aplɛnɔ mɛ, kɛlɛ budiku anyɛ nɔpã mɛ.

english_verse: who told us that an angel had appeared to him. The angel had ordered him to send to Joppa for someone named Simon Peter.
translation_verse: Etseɖe bɔlɔ kana gɛ amɔ axɔ̃ɛdɛ ebu yɛishu akasɛ yɛ nɛ bɔpã mɛ pɛ etseɖe yɛasɛ, ‘Kpɛ anɔ nɛ Simon gɛbekulu tsyɛ asɛ Petroɔ ɔkɔ nɛ Yopa tɛ̀ aba.

english_verse: Then Peter would tell him how he and everyone in his house could be saved.
translation_verse: Abà mana wɔ ɔklasɔ̃, kɛlɛ gana ɔlɛ mɛɛ, wɔ nɛ̀ wɔpãa bɔmɔ ɛkpã.’

english_verse: After I started speaking, the Holy Spirit was given to them, just as the Spirit had been given to us at the beginning.
translation_verse: “Kibeɛ gɛ ɛɖɔ kaɖɔ koɔ, Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã ɔbɔdɔ̃ balɛ abha asɛ gɛ lɔba ɔlɔ tsyɛ abha nɛ kesikpɛkpɛ mɛ.

english_verse: I remembered that the Lord had said, “John baptized with water, but you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ ɛbaka kibuiɛ gɛ Apaleteɛ aɖɔ asɛ, ‘Yohanes akpɛ bulĩ nɛ kɛtakpu akɔ anɔ nɛ̀ bulĩ, pɔ wɔnɔ xɔnɔ Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã babà lɔkɔ akpɛ bɔlɛ akɔ wɔnɔ nɔ̀.’

english_verse: God gave those Gentiles the same gift that he gave us when we put our faith in the Lord Jesus Christ. So how could I have gone against God?
translation_verse: Kliso onupɛ Owusoso Akaa akɔ alɛ kɛdɔ̃ kɔkɔ asɛ gɛ akɔ bɔlɔ tsyɛ ana, bɔlɔ asɛ bagɛ ayɛkɛ Yesu Kristo abha aŋãa, kɛlɛ afana mɛinu pɛ ababhɛtɛ mɛ asɛ ɛbalà lɔɛtití nɛ Owusoso Akaa shu?”

english_verse: When they heard Peter say this, they stopped arguing and started praising God. They said, “God has now let Gentiles turn to him, and he has given life to them!” The Church in Antioch
translation_verse: Gɛ belũ kibui ɛlɛɛ, ananà egefekè kibui nanà, pɔ bakpɛ Owusoso Akaa nɛinye nɛitití pɛ baɖɔ asɛ, “Kɛlɛ́ otsiɛnɔ, Owusoso Akaa akɔ bagɛ̀ betinu Yudanɛ tsyɛ etumɛ tsyitsyina tɛ bamɔ ɛkpã.” Ɔhaa Nɛ Antioxia

english_verse: Some of the Lord's followers had been scattered because of the terrible trouble that started when Stephen was killed. They went as far as Phoenicia, Cyprus, and Antioch, but they told the message only to the Jews.
translation_verse: Banunɔ gɛ bapɔ̃ɛ alɛ mɛ akanà ɔganà balɛ ede tɛtɔtɔnɔ gɛkpà ɔplɛnɔ Stefano yɛ tiyiyiɛ, bagã kidzo avɛnɔ idzlolo ɔkɔ asɛ Foenisia ana, Kipro nɛ̀ Antoxia pɛ badaaɖɔ Kibui Gbangbã eetseɖe Yudanɛ ete.

english_verse: Some of the followers from Cyprus and Cyrene went to Antioch and started telling Gentiles the good news about the Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: Beteli nɛ balɛ mɛ, banɔ gɛede Kipro nɛ̀ Kirene, bavɛ Antioxia pɛ badaaɖɔ kibuiɛ etseɖe Helanɛ tsyɛ, betseɖe balɛ kibui gbangbã nɛ Apalete Yesu shu.

english_verse: The Lord's power was with them, and many people turned to the Lord and put their faith in him.
translation_verse: Apaleteɛ ɔhɔɛ ɔzã balɛ abha, kliso banɔ kpekpè teli ayɛkɛ aŋã pɛ betsyina avɛ Apaleteɛ ɔkɔ.

english_verse: News of what was happening reached the church in Jerusalem. Then they sent Barnabas to Antioch.
translation_verse: Kibui ɛlɛ baku ɔhaa gɛ̀ lɔlɛ̀ Yerusalemuɔ atɔ̃mɛ, kɛlɛ basɛsɛ Barnaba nɛ Antioxia.

english_verse: When Barnabas got there and saw how God had blessed them with undeserved grace, he was very glad. So he begged them to remain faithful to the Lord with all their hearts.
translation_verse: Gɛ ediku pɛ amɔ asɛ kibui gbã kinu ɔsɛ Owusoso Akaa ebhui balɛ ebuɔ, same ekui, kɛlɛ akpɛ balɛ petee kemi asɛ tɛ̀ balɔkɔ̀ balɛ etúɔ nyɔ̃ɔ azã nɛ̀ ɛkpã ɔkɔ Apaleteɛ.

english_verse: Barnabas was a good man of great faith, and he was filled with the Holy Spirit. Many more people turned to the Lord.
translation_verse: Barnaba edzi anɔ gbangbã teli gɛ̀ Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã ɔzã yɛmɛ̀ pɛ̀ ayɛkɛ aŋã, kɛlɛ bagbana banɔ kpekpè teli amana Apaleteɛ ɔkɔ.

english_verse: Barnabas went to Tarsus to look for Saul.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ Barnaba abavɛ Tarso asɛ tɛ̀ adɛpɛ Saulo,

english_verse: He found Saul and brought him to Antioch, where they met with the church for a whole year and taught many of its people. There in Antioch the Lord's followers were first called Christians.
translation_verse: kɛlɛ gɛ adɛmɔɛ, agbani amana Antioxia. Nɛ kɛgɔ zɛzɛ petee mɛ̀, Barnaba nɛ̀ Saulo atsyɔa nɛ̀ ɛháa pɛ bazakasɛ banɔ kpekpè teli bɔdɔ̃. Antioxia ɔha mɛ beputɔ ekulu badɔkpasɛanɛ asɛ Kristoanɛ.

english_verse: During this time some prophets from Jerusalem came to Antioch.
translation_verse: Nɛ kibe ɛlɛ, bebuiɖɔluanɔ teli ede Yerusalem aba Antioxia.

english_verse: One of them was Agabus. Then with the help of the Spirit, he told that there would be a terrible famine everywhere in the world. And it happened when Claudius was Emperor.
translation_verse: Balɛ ɔmayɛmɛ tenikpo gɛ bezekulu asɛ Agaboɔ, abalɔɛtití, kɛlɛ ɔgana Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ mɛɛ, aɖɔ elú asɛ bɔbhɛ gagalĩ tuli badɔ nɛ Roma kɛmadiɛ petee mɛ.

english_verse: The followers in Antioch decided to send whatever help they could to the followers in Judea.
translation_verse: Kliso badɔkpasɛanɛ asɛ kana gɛ balɛ ɔmayɛmɛ aka nyia aka abalà akɔ̀, batɔɖɔ ɛlɛ asɛ babàkɔ ɔhamɛanɛ gɛ̀ balɛ̀ Yudea dɔ mɛ tɛpɛplɛnɔ.

english_verse: So they appointed Barnabas and Saul to take their gifts to the church leaders in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Kɛgɛ babhɛtɛ kɛlɛinu ɔsɛ, basɛsɛ besheshèɛ bɔdɔ̃ ɔganà Barnaba nɛ̀ Saulo abha.

english_verse: At that time King Herod caused terrible suffering for some members of the church.
translation_verse: Kibe ɛlɛ Ekusi Herodes Agripa evũ ɔhamɛ banunɔ teli, kɛlɛ azatɔɖɔ ɛlɛ asɛ tɛ̀ atɔnɔ balɛ edè.

english_verse: He ordered soldiers to cut off the head of James, the brother of John.
translation_verse: Akɔ beyi Yakobo, gɛenu Yohanes yɛadzya nɛ̀ bɛtɛ̀.

english_verse: When Herod saw that this pleased the Jewish people, he had Peter arrested during the Festival of Thin Bread.
translation_verse: Gɛ amɔ asɛ kibui ɛlɛ ku Yudanɛ sameɛ, ebu katsɛɖɛ edivũ Petro tsyɛ. Kibui ɛlɛ ba ɛlɛ mɛ nɛ Amawɔɛ Gɛ Agɛnyanyã Kiwui ŋaŋã nabha.

english_verse: He put Peter in jail and ordered four squads of soldiers to guard him. Herod planned to put him on trial in public after the festival.
translation_verse: Gɛ evũ Petroɔ ebekeɛ, akpɛ yɛɔɖavũ, pɛ alɔkɔɛ akpɛ ahɔɛ akɔ keyuiza ivolo tɛlɛ gɛ̀ buyuadɛdɛ talɛ azã balɛ ulũ. Herodes atɔɖɔ ɛlɛ asɛ tɛ̀ agbanà yɛ amana kɛtsalɛ mɛ ɔkɔ kebhu kukulu gɛ̀ onupɛ baŋã Amawɔɛ Gɛagɛnyanyã Kiwuiɛ ebeke.

english_verse: While Peter was being kept in jail, the church never stopped praying to God for him. Peter Is Rescued
translation_verse: Kliso Petro azã ɔɖavũɔ mɛ, pɔ ɔhaa ezekulu Owusoso Akaa nɛ yɛtakpu gagalĩi nɛ̀ awuhũdzadzã.

english_verse: The night before Peter was to be put on trial, he was asleep and bound by two chains. A soldier was guarding him on each side, and two other soldiers were guarding the entrance to the jail.
translation_verse: Elishi alɛ flogo tɛ̀ Herodes abagbanà yɛ amana kebhuɔ oklukɔɔ, banyã Petro nɛ̀ ɛɖahui tɛbhà pɛ azadɔ taɖa nɛ buyuadɛdɛ taabha nɛishi, flogo pɛ buyuadɛ bhlatɔ tsyɛ azã azãepo kedulũɔ.

english_verse: Suddenly an angel from the Lord appeared, and light flashed around in the cell. The angel poked Peter in the side and woke him up. Then he said, “Quick! Get up!” The chains fell off his hands,
translation_verse: Nɛ balɛ matì matì mɛɛ, Apaleteɛ yɛaxɔ̃ɛdɛ ebekoeyì, kɛlɛ kifu dɛkɛ ku ɔɖavũɔ mɛ. Axɔ̃ɛdɛ etũ Petro kɛdɔ̃ nɛ yɛɔwɔã mɛ̀ pɛ anyɛmii. Etseɖe yɛasɛ, “Hɔa, tɛ ɔlɔ!” kɛlɛ ɛɖahuiɛ wɔlɛ̀ nɛ Petro yɛɔhɔkpɔ.

english_verse: and the angel said, “Get dressed and put on your sandals.” Peter did what he was told. Then the angel said, “Now put on your coat and follow me.”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ axɔ̃ɛdɛ etseɖe yɛasɛ, “Lɔkɔ wɔ̀ bɔdɔ̃ kpɛkpɛ nɛ̀ ɔtsɛɖɛkɔlɔ ɔkpɛ.” Kɛlɛ Petro abhɛtɛ ana. Eze tseɖe yɛasɛ, “Lɔkɔ wɔakpɛkpɛ dzloloɔ ɔplɛnɔ wɔshu tɛ̀ ɔtɔnɔ mɛ.”

english_verse: Peter left with the angel, but he thought everything was only a dream.
translation_verse: Petro azã yiede ekoeyì nɛ ɔɖavũɔ mɛ, pɔ egitì asɛ̀ kɛgɛ axɔ̃ɛdɛ aabhɛtɛ nu kibui gbã, ezeɖi asɛ bɔlala mɛ ebui yɛamɔ anà.

english_verse: They went past the two groups of soldiers, and when they came to the iron gate to the city, it opened by itself. They went out and were going along the street, when all at once the angel disappeared.
translation_verse: Bagã fala nɛ buyuadɛ kɛza gɛiputɔ, nɛ̀ kɛbhalà shu kɛlɛ bebèku ɔɖa kedulũɔ gɛ bagã ekoeyì avɛ ɔmahohoeɛ mɛ. Kadalɛ nɛ kalɛishu ahɔɛ mɛ̀, kɛlɛ̀ bagãfala nɛ kalɛ mɛ. Gɛ bagã tipɔ tenikpo idzo ebekeɛ, nɛ yɛ matì matì mɛɛ axɔ̃ɛdɛ ayala nɛ yɛamɛ.

english_verse: Peter now realized what had happened, and he said, “I am certain that the Lord sent his angel to rescue me from Herod and from everything the Jewish leaders planned to do to me.”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ̀ Petro yɛamɛ adalɛ kɛlɛ aɖɔ asɛ, “Kɛlɛ otsi alɛ ayɛkɛ aŋã asɛ Apaleteɛ yɛasɛsɛ yɛaxɔ̃ɛdɛ asɛ tɛ̀ ebebu mɛ eyinɔ nɛ Ekusi Herodes yɛawɔ̃fɔnɔ mɛ̀, nɛ̀ kɛka nyia ka gɛ Yudanɛ atɔɖɔ asɛ tɛ babhɛtɛ̀ nɛ mɛishuɔ.”

english_verse: Then Peter went to the house of Mary the mother of John whose other name was Mark. Many of the Lord's followers had come together there and were praying.
translation_verse: Gɛ kibui ɛlɛ mɛ badalɛ ɔkɔɛɛ, avɛ Maria gɛenu Yohanes gɛ beekulu Markoɔ yɛeliɛ ɔpãmɛ ɛkɛlɛ gɛ̀ banɔ kpekpè teli eshogò pɛ beze kulu Owusoso Akaa.

english_verse: Peter knocked on the gate, and a servant named Rhoda came to answer.
translation_verse: Petro ebho ɔtɔ̃ kedulũ opupuɔ, kɛlɛ adzɛnuvɔɛ axɔ̃ɛdɛ teli gɛ bezekulu yɛasɛ Roda elũ pɛ avɛ ɔlɛ̀ shu.

english_verse: When she heard Peter's voice, she was too excited to open the gate. She ran back into the house and said Peter was standing there.
translation_verse: Gɛ Roda elũ asɛ Petro yɛaɖɔaɖɔɔ, same ekui falanɔɛfú, kliso ebu tɛtsɛɖɛmɛ ashɛ gɛ agɛ dalɛ opupuɔ, kɛlɛ edetseɖe ɔhaa asɛ, “Petro alɛ kedulũɔ!”

english_verse: “You are crazy!” everyone told her. But she kept saying it was Peter. Then they said, “It must be his angel.”
translation_verse: Banunɔ ebuiliulũ akɔɛ asɛ, “Ɔma piapii.” Pɔ gɛ etũilɛ akpɛ asɛ̀ kɛkpasɛ ɛlɛ mɛ anaa, baɖɔ asɛ, “Kɛlɛ́ɛ ebedzi yɛaxɔ̃ɛdɛ yɛaba.”

english_verse: But Peter kept on knocking, until finally they opened the gate. They saw him and were completely amazed.
translation_verse: Pɔ Petro azã kedulũɔ pɛ ezebho opupuɔ, kɛlɛ gɛ badalɛ ɔlɛ pɛ bamɔɛ, kɛbhɛtɛ balɛ ibi popopo.

english_verse: Peter motioned for them to keep quiet. Then he told how the Lord had led him out of jail. He also said, “Tell James and the others what has happened.” After that, he left and went somewhere else.
translation_verse: Petro akasɛ alɛ ahɔɛ asɛ bataatɔ̃ kɛlɛ aɖɔ etseɖe alɛ nɛ kibuɔ gɛ Apaleteɛ ebui eyinɔ nɛ ɔɖavũɔ mɛ. Etseɖe balɛ asɛ, “Notseɖe Yakobo nɛ̀ yɛadzya alɛ nɛ kibui ɛlɛ shu kɛlɛ ashɛ avɛ ɔkɔ bhlatɔ.”

english_verse: The next morning the soldiers who had been on guard were terribly worried and wondered what had happened to Peter.
translation_verse: Gɛ keliɛ azaa, kawlɔ̃akɛ azã buyuadɛdɛ nɛishi nɛ kɛgɛ ba ɛlɛ mɛ̀ nɛ Petroɔ shu.

english_verse: Herod ordered his own soldiers to search for him, but they could not find him. Then he questioned the guards and had them put to death. After this, Herod left Judea to stay in Caesarea for a while. Herod Dies
translation_verse: Gɛ Ekusi Herodes akɔ bapɛ yɛ piapii pɛ bagɛ mɔ yɛ ɔgãkɔɔ, edzini buyuadɛdɛ gɛepo ɛkɛlɛ ebisi piapii, kɛlɛ akɔ ɔsha asɛ tɛ̀ beyi alɛ. Ekusi Herodes Agripa Yɛutsi Kɛlɛ Ekusi Herodes alɔ ede Yudea pɛ avɛ Kaisarea pɛ adɛzã ɛkɛlɛ nɛ kibè tili.

english_verse: Herod and the people of Tyre and Sidon were very angry with each other. But their country got its food supply from the region that he ruled. So a group of them went to see Blastus, who was one of Herod's high officials. They convinced Blastus that they wanted to make peace between their cities and Herod,
translation_verse: Herodes azapɛ ebela nɛ̀ Tiro nɛ̀ Sidoanɛ, pɔ otsi alɛ xɔnɔ babhɛtɛ tenikpo pɛ bekulu banɔ pɛ̀ bazã kesi nii. Gɛ bamɔ tɛpɛplɛnɔ odenɔ Blasto gɛenu Ekusiɛ yɛaxɔ̃ɛdɛ gɛ yɛabha yɛ ayɛkɛ aŋãa ɔkɔɔ, bebisi ishu yɔyɔ, ɔmɔsɛ ekusiɛ yɛesiakpa mɛ bamɔ bɔdɔ̃ gɛ baŋãa edenɔ.

english_verse: and a day was set for them to meet with him. Herod came dressed in his royal robes. He sat down on his throne and made a speech.
translation_verse: Nɛ kiwuiɛ gɛ batɔɖɔ elu nabhaa, Herodes akpɛ yɛekusi akpɛkpɛ dzloloɔ, azã yɛekusi kegbu nabha, kɛlɛ azaɖɔaɖɔ etseɖe banunɔ nɛ ɔtsyɔakɔ.

english_verse: The people shouted, “You speak more like a god than a man!”
translation_verse: Banunɔ akpɛ ozĩ asɛ, “Gɛnɛ anɔdzyrame kaɖɔ nɛ alɛ, pɔ obudì ayɛ.”

english_verse: At once an angel from the Lord struck him down because he took the honor that belonged to God. Later, Herod was eaten by worms and died.
translation_verse: Gɛ Herodes agɛkpɛ Owusoso Akaa nɛinyè nɛitití soɔ, tsiɔ ɔlɛ koɔ, Apaleteɛ yɛaxɔ̃ɛdɛ teli ebehui ebho, kɛlɛ asɔ̃ abaŋãyɛ pɛ etsi.

english_verse: And after Barnabas and Saul had done the work they were sent to do, they went back to Jerusalem with John, whose other name was Mark.
translation_verse: Gɛ Barnaba nɛ̀ Saulo adɛ kɛxɔ̃ɛ gɛ bakpɛ alɛ ebekeɛ, bagbana Yohanes gɛ bezekulu tsyɛ asɛ Markoɔ evunɔ edenɔ Yerusalem amana.

english_verse: The church at Antioch had several prophets and teachers. They were Barnabas, Simeon, also called Niger, Lucius from Cyrene, Manaen, who was Herod's close friend, and Saul.
translation_verse: Nɛ Antioxia ɔhaa mɛɛ, bebuiɖɔluanɔ nɛ̀ badɔkasɛ teli azã ɛkɛlɛ gɛ̀ balɛenu, Barnaba, Simeon gɛ bezekulu tsyɛ asɛ Anɔtutũ, Lukio gɛede Kirene, Manahem ɔlɔkɔ ɔplɛnɔ Saulo.

english_verse: While they were worshiping the Lord and going without eating, the Holy Spirit told them, “Appoint Barnabas and Saul to do the work for which I have chosen them.”
translation_verse: Kibeɛ gɛ bazanyã olũgbu pɛ baakpɛ kɛtakpu esi akɔ Apaleteɛ, Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã otseɖe balɛ ɔsɛ, “Nobu Barnaba nɛ̀ Saulo ɔtɔ ɔkɔ kɛxɔ̃ɛ gɛ kɛlɛishu mɛikulu alɛ.”

english_verse: Everyone prayed and went without eating for a while longer. Next, they placed their hands on Barnabas and Saul to show that they had been appointed to do this work. Then everyone sent them on their way. Barnabas and Saul in Cyprus
translation_verse: Kliso gɛ banyã olũgbu pɛ bekulu Owusoso Akaa ebekeɛ, batɔɖɔ balɛ ahɔɛ nɛ alɛ abha pɛ bakpɛ alɛ kidzo. Barnaba Nɛ̀ Saulo Avɛ Kipro

english_verse: After Barnabas and Saul had been sent by the Holy Spirit, they went to Seleucia. From there they sailed to the island of Cyprus.
translation_verse: Gɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã ɔkpɛ balɛidzo pɛ bashɛɛ, bediku Seleukia kɛlɛ beku kelĩabha ohu agã aƒu nabha pɛ bavɛ Kipro.

english_verse: They arrived at Salamis and began to preach God's message in the synagogues. They also had John as a helper.
translation_verse: Gɛ bediku Salamiɛ, baɖɔ Owusoso Akaa nibuiɛ nɛ Yudanɛ ɛtsyɔakɔ. Yohanes Marko azã balɛ ɔbha pɛ azaplɛnɔ alɛ.

english_verse: Barnabas and Saul went all the way to the city of Paphos on the other end of the island, where they met a Jewish man named Bar-Jesus. He practiced witchcraft and was a false prophet.
translation_verse: Bagã afala nɛ aƒumɛ ketukpɔ petee abha, odiyinɔ kibeɛ gɛ bediku Pafo. Ɛkɛlɛ badɛ tɔnɔ̃ Yudaeyi teli gɛ kebisì azã yɛahɔɛ pɛ edzì asɛnɛ ebuiɖɔluanɔ gɛ beekulu yɛasɛ Bar Yesu ,

english_verse: He also worked for Sergius Paulus, who was very smart and was the governor of the island. Sergius Paulus wanted to hear God's message, and he sent for Barnabas and Saul.
translation_verse: anyɛ alɛ azadɛ axɔ̃ɛ akɔ ekusi Sergio Paulo. Ɔma ekusi alɛ edzì ebuitinɛ teli idi kliso akpɛ banɔ nɛ Barnaba nɛ̀ Saulo ɔkɔ, ɔmɔsɛ apɛ asɛ tɛ elũ Owusoso Akaa nɛibuiɛ.

english_verse: But Bar-Jesus, whose other name was Elymas, was against them. He even tried to keep the governor from having faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Pɔ Elima alɔɛtití nɛ balɛ shu kɛlɛ apɛ asɛ tɛ̀ etsyina ekusiɛ edenɔ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã shu.

english_verse: Then Saul, better known as Paul, was filled with the Holy Spirit. He looked straight at Elymas
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ Saulo gɛ bezekulu tsyɛ asɛ Paulo gɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã oyukù yɛmɛ, edzu awuhũ nɛ ehuinɛ Elima yamɛ pɛ etseɖe yɛasɛ,

english_verse: and said, “You son of the devil! You are a liar, a crook, and an enemy of everything that is right. When will you stop speaking against the true ways of the Lord?
translation_verse: “Ɔfɔfuĩa zizĩ eyi wonu, nɛ̀ anɔ gɛ atɔɖɔ kibui kpa nɛ kibui ka nyia ka gɛpɛ shu! Asɛnɛ nɛ̀ bɔshɛ bhɛbhɛtɛ ebui nyɔ̃ɔ alɛ yuku wɔ mɛ. Ɔgɛ zɔyɔlɔ Apaleteɛ nidzoɔ gɛpɛ titsyitsyina dza?

english_verse: The Lord is going to punish you by making you completely blind for a while.” Suddenly the man's eyes were covered by a dark mist, and he went around trying to get someone to lead him by the hand.
translation_verse: Otsi alɛ Apaleteɛ yɛahɔɛ alɔɛtití nɛ wɔshu. Obopu awuhũ, kɛlɛ odi yinɔ kibe tiliɛ, ɔzɔ la ɔmɔ kiwiɛ gɛinyeɛ.” Tsiɔ ɔlɛ koɔ, ɔmɔlɔ nɛ̀ otumũ ɔba yɛabha, kɛlɛ akanà yɛahɔɛ pɛ apɛ anɔ gɛ abagbana yɛ.

english_verse: When the governor saw what had happened, he was amazed at this teaching about the Lord. So he put his faith in the Lord. Paul and Barnabas in Antioch of Pisidia
translation_verse: Gɛ ɔma ekusiɛ amɔ kɛgɛ ba ɛlɛ mɛɛ, ayɛkɛ aŋã ɔmɔsɛ Apaleteɛ shu bɔdɔ̃ kakasɛ bhɛtɛ yɛibi. Barnaba Nɛ̀ Saulo Nɛ Antioxia Kawɔ̃pɛ Gɛalɛ Pisidia

english_verse: Paul and the others left Paphos and sailed to Perga in Pamphylia. But John left them and went back to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Gɛ balɔ ede Pafoɔ, Paulo nɛ̀ yɛɔhayɛ eku kelĩabha ohu avɛ Perge nɛ Pamfilia bɔdɔ̃ mɛ, ɛkɛlɛ Yohanes Marko xɔnɔ ashɛ ayɔlɔ balɛ pɛ etsyina avɛ Yerusalem.

english_verse: The rest of them went on from Perga to Antioch in Pisidia. Then on the Sabbath they went to the synagogue and sat down.
translation_verse: Denɔ Pergeɛ, bavɛ Pisidia nɛ Antioxia. Nɛ Kimoelewui nabhaa, bavɛ balɛ ɔtsyɔakɔ pɛ badɛ zã kesi.

english_verse: After the reading of the Law and the Prophets, the leaders sent someone over to tell Paul and Barnabas, “Friends, if you have anything to say that will help the people, please say it.”
translation_verse: Gɛ bakalà kibuiɛ odenɔ Mose ɔsha nɛ ebuiɖɔluanɛ buplukpa mɛ ebekeɛ, bagɛ beɖi ɔtsyɔakɔ nabha asɛsɛ alɛ ɔklasɔ̃ asɛ, “Bɔlɔ adzya alɛ mɔ, onupɛ kibui tili lɛ wɔnɔ ahɔɛ pɛ nɔbɔ lɔkɔ ɔkpɛ ɔha kemi nɔ, nɔɖɔ ɛlɛ otseɖe bɔlɔ.”

english_verse: Paul got up. He motioned with his hand and said: People of Israel, and everyone else who worships God, listen!
translation_verse: Gɛ Paulo alɔɛtitíɛ, abhɛtɛ ahɔɛ akasɛ alɛ pɛ aɖɔ asɛ, “Wɔnɔ Israelanɛ nɛ̀ bagɛ notinu Yudanɛ pɛ nɔkpɛ kɛtakpu esi ɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa, nɔtaatɔ̃ nɛ mɛ!

english_verse: The God of Israel chose our ancestors, and he let our people prosper while they were living in Egypt. Then with his mighty power he led them out,
translation_verse: Israelanɛ Owusoso Akaa ebu bɔlɔ ashuĩ alɛ, akɔ banunɔ amɔ otugbà vɛvɛ nɛ kibeɛ gɛ bazã Egipteɛ, ɔgana ohumi hohoe mɛɛ Owusoso Akaa agbana alɛ eyinɔ edenɔ kesiakpa alɛ mɛ.

english_verse: Make sure what the prophets have said doesn't happen to you. They said,
translation_verse: Noɖi piapii ɔsɛ tɛ ebuiɛ gɛ ebuiɖɔluanɛ aɖɔ eluɔ nanà atɛba wɔnɔ abha asɛ,

english_verse: He destroyed seven nations in the land of Canaan and gave their land to our people.
translation_verse: Atsɛ bekusi tegegene nɛ kegbu abha nɛ Kanan, kɛlɛ alɔkɔ kesiakpa akɔ Israelanɛ asɛ kaŋãsi ana.

english_verse: Later, God removed Saul and let David rule in his place. God said about him, “David the son of Jesse is the kind of person who pleases me most! He does everything I want him to do.”
translation_verse: Gɛ atsɛ Saulo nɛ ekusi kegbu nabhaa, alɔkɔ David edzu bukusi akɔ alɛ. Aɖɔ kaɖɔ elú nɛ David shu asɛ, ‘Ɛmɔ David gɛenu Isai yɛeyianyɛ, anɔ gɛ ɛdɛmɛ nɔ nɛ̀ mɛitúɔ, ababhɛtɛ kɛka nyia ka gɛ itseɖe yɛ sɛ abhɛtɛɛ.’

english_verse: God promised that someone from David's family would come to save the people of Israel, and this one is Jesus.
translation_verse: Gana anyɛ alɛ yɛeshui alɛ nabhaa, Owusoso Akaa amana Yesu, anɔ gɛ ebebu alɛ asɛ kɛtɔɖɔ gɛ atɔɖɔ elu ana.

english_verse: But before Jesus came, John was telling everyone in Israel to turn back to God and be baptized.
translation_verse: Flogo tɛ Yesu ababáa, Yohanes eputɔ aɖɔ kitu mɛ titsyitsyina shu ibui nɛ̀ bulĩkpɛkpɛ ɔkɔ Israelanɛ pete.

english_verse: Then, when John's work was almost done, he said, “Who do you people think I am? Do you think I am the Promised One? He will come later, and I am not good enough to untie his sandals.”
translation_verse: Kibeɛ gɛ Yohanes aawɔa yɛaxɔ̃ɛ ebekeɛ, aɖɔ asɛ, ‘Afana noɖi ɔsɛ mɛinu? Gɛnɛ mɛinu anɔ alɛ́. Ao, pɔ alɛ miede aba, gɛ ɛgɛ shɔnɔ ɔsɛ tɛ̀ mɛiboele yɛɔtsɛɖɛkɔlɔ ihuiɛ ete tsyɛ.’

english_verse: Now listen, you descendants of Abraham! Pay attention, all of you Gentiles who are here to worship God! Listen to this message about how to be saved, because it is for everyone.
translation_verse: “Mɛadzya alɛ mɔ, Abraham yɛeshui alɛ, nɛ̀ wɔnɔ bagɛ notinu Yudanɛ pɛ nɔkpɛ kɛtakpu esi ɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa, bɔlɔ so pɛ basɛsɛ kibui ɛlɛ gɛ kɛyɛkɛ banɔ lù nɛ ɛkpã.

english_verse: The people of Jerusalem and their leaders didn't realize who Jesus was. And they didn't understand the words of the prophets they read each Sabbath. So they condemned Jesus just as the prophets had said.
translation_verse: Banunɔ nɛ Yerusalem nɛ̀ balɛ bekusiɛ egitì Yesuɔ, pɔ ganà yɛpɔ bubu mɛɛ, kɛkɔ ɔsɛ ebuiɛ gɛ bebuiɖɔluanɔ aɖɔ elu, pɛ nɔɔkalà alɛ nɛ Kimoelewui ka nyia ka abhaa ba ɛlɛ mɛ.

english_verse: They did exactly what the Scriptures said they would. Even though they couldn't find any reason to put Jesus to death, they still asked Pilate to have him killed. After Jesus had been put to death, he was taken down from the cross and placed in a tomb.
translation_verse: Gɛ babhɛtɛ kɛka nyia ka nɛ kɛfɔ̀nɔ gɛ bafɔnɔ elu nɛ yɛishu ebekeɛ, bebu yɛ nɛ osiɛ shu, pɛ badɛ vɔbɔɛ nɛ odzeɖeɛ mɛ.

english_verse: But God raised him from death!
translation_verse: Pɔ Owusoso Akaa efeke yɛ edenɔ bekpuɔ mɛ,

english_verse: Then for many days Jesus appeared to his followers who had gone with him from Galilee to Jerusalem. Now they are telling our people about him.
translation_verse: kɛlɛ nɛ ewui gele mɛɛ, ebu yɛishu akasɛ bagɛ bazagã niyɛ edenɔ Galilea amana Yerusalem. Balɛenu banunɔ gɛ bazãmɔ kibuiɛ otsiɛ gɛ baɖɔ etseɖe bɔlɔkɔ ayɛ.

english_verse: God made a promise to our ancestors. And we are here to tell you the good news
translation_verse: “Butseɖe wɔnɔ Kibui Gbangbã, kɛgɛ Owusoso Akaa atɔɖɔ elu akɔ bɔlɔ akaa alɛ

english_verse: that he has kept this promise to us. It is just as the second Psalm says about Jesus, “You are my son because today I have become your Father.”
translation_verse: akɔ kɛba ɛlɛ mɛ ɔkɔ bɔlɔ ginu yɛeyi alɛ, gɛ akɔ Yesu alɔ edenɔ bekpuɔ mɛ. Asɛ kɛfɔ̀nɔ gɛ bafɔnɔ elu nɛ ɛdzɛ keplukpa bhalà mɛ asɛ, “ ‘Wonù mɛeyianyɛ, oboɔ itsyina dzì woakaa.’

english_verse: God raised Jesus from death and will never let his body decay. It is just as God said, “I will make to you the same holy promises that I made to David.”
translation_verse: Kɛkpasɛ kɛlɛ mɛ ɔsɛ Owusoso Akaa efeke yɛ edenɔ bekpuɔ mɛ asɛ tɛ atɛba tsɔmɔ̃, bafɔnɔɛ ɛlɛ elu nɛ ebui alɛ mɛ asɛ, “ ‘Ɛba kɔwɔ anɔ dadalɛ nɛ̀ tiyuyulà gɛ batɔɖɔ elu akɔ David pɛ kɛtɛbà tsyina.’

english_verse: And in another psalm it says, “God will never let the body of his Holy One decay.”
translation_verse: Kliso bazaɖɔ ɛlɛ nɛ ɛkɔ bhlatɔ tsyɛ asɛ “ ‘Ɔtɛ bɔkɔ ɔsɛ wɔanɔ dadalɛ ahɔhɔ̃.’

english_verse: When David was alive, he obeyed God. Then after he died, he was buried in the family grave, and his body decayed.
translation_verse: “Gɛ David abhɛtɛ atɔɖɔ Owusoso Akaa yɛ tɛpɛpɛ nɛ yɛibeɛ ebekeɛ, adɔ taɖa, bavɔbɔ yɛ nɛ yashuĩ alɛ nɛwɔ̃a kɛlɛ yɛetsitsíɛ atsɔmɔ̃.

english_verse: But God raised Jesus from death, and his body did not decay.
translation_verse: Pɔ anɔ nɔ̀ gɛ Owusoso Akaa efeke yɛ edenɔ bekpu nɛishi xɔnɔ agɛ hɔhɔ̃.

english_verse: My friends, the message is that Jesus can forgive your sins! The Law of Moses could not set you free from all your sins.
translation_verse: “Kliso mɛadzya alɛ mɔ, ɛpɛ sɛ noti ɔsɛ, gana Yesu abhaa, baɖɔ ebui kpa tɛlɔlɔkɔ tsye ebui etseɖe wɔnɔ.”

english_verse: “Look, you people who make fun of God! Be amazed and disappear. I will do something today that you won't believe, even if someone tells you about it!”
translation_verse: “ ‘Ɖimɔ, wɔnɔ bagɛ nozĩ banɔ shu, nɔkanà tɛ notsí, ɔmɔsɛ ɛba bhɛtɛ kibui tili nɛ wɔnɔ kibe ɛlɛ mɛ gɛ nɔtɛ bɔyɛkɛ ɔŋã oɖi, gɛ onupɛ anɔ teli yɛetseɖe wɔ tsyɛ.’ ”

english_verse: As Paul and Barnabas were leaving the synagogue, the people begged them to say more about these same things on the next Sabbath.
translation_verse: Kibeɛ gɛ Paulo nɛ̀ Barnaba ekoeyì ashɛɛ nɛ ɔtsyɔa kɔɔ, banunɔ ezebisi edenɔ bala ahɔɛ asɛ bewlui abaɖɔ ebui alɛ nɛidi etseɖe alɛ nɛ Kimoelewuiɛ gɛbàa nabha.

english_verse: After the service, many Jews and a lot of Gentiles who worshiped God went with them. Paul and Barnabas begged them all to remain faithful to God, who had treated them with undeserved grace.
translation_verse: Gɛ banunɔ ekoeyì nɛ kɛtakpu ɔtɔ̃kɔɔ, Yudanɛ kpekpèɛ nɛ̀ bagɛ etsyina eku Yudanɛ ɔha mɛ atɔnɔ Paulo nɛ̀ Barnaba. Bazaɖɔaɖɔ nɛ̀ alɛ pɛ azakpalà alɛ asɛ bazã kɛlɛ abha nɛ balɛ Owusoso Akaa tibhui bhuibuɔ mɛ.

english_verse: The next Sabbath almost everyone in town came to hear the message about the Lord.
translation_verse: Nɛ Kimoelewuiɛ gɛzɛba nabhaa, banunɔ pete kloe nɛ ɔmamɛ edi shogò tɛ belũ Apalete nɛibuiɛ.

english_verse: When the Jewish people saw the crowds, they were very jealous. They insulted Paul and spoke against everything he said.
translation_verse: Gɛ Yudanɛ amɔ banɔɛza gɛishogòɔ, kabhã wɔawlɔakɛ eku balɛ mɛ, kɛlɛ bakpà akwalatu ebui aɖɔ nɛ kɛgɛ Paulo azaɖɔɔ shu.

english_verse: But Paul and Barnabas bravely said: We had to tell God's message to you before we told it to anyone else. But you rejected the message! This proves that you don't deserve eternal life. Now we are going to the Gentiles.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ Paulo nɛ̀ Barnaba ebu balɛ ishu ulũ nɛ kifù masi masi mɛ asɛ, “Wɔnɔ kɛlɛ ɔsɛ buputɔ ɖɔ Owusoso Akaa ibuiɛ ɔkɔ. Pɔ gɛ nɔgɛ yɛkɛ ɛlɛ pɛ nɔmɔ ɔsɛ nɔshɔnɔ ɔkɔ ɛkpã tihɔ nɔ soɔ, butsyina ɛlɛ ɔvɛnɔ bagɛ etinu Yudanɛ ɔkɔ.”

english_verse: The Lord has given us this command, “I have placed you here as a light for the Gentiles. You are to take the saving power of God to people everywhere on earth.”
translation_verse: Kɛgɛ Apalete atɔɖɔ akɔ bɔlɔ kɛlɛinu ɔsɛ, “Ɛbhɛtɛ wɔnɔ nɔodzi kifù ɔkɔ bagɛ etinu Yudanɛ sɛ tɛ nɔyɛkɛ banunɔ nɛ kesiakpa nabha olu nɛkpã ɔvɛnɔ kanɔ nɔ̀ ɔtɔ̃kɔ.’

english_verse: This message made the Gentiles glad, and they praised what they had heard about the Lord. Everyone who had been chosen for eternal life then put their faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Gɛ bagɛ etinu Yudanɛ elũ kibui ɛlɛ, same eku balɛ kɛlɛ bakpɛ Apalete nɛibuiɛ nɛitití, kɛlɛ bagɛ Owusoso Akaa ebu elu akɔ ɛkpã tihɔɔ nɔ mɛ tsyɛ ayɛkɛ aŋã.

english_verse: The message about the Lord spread all over this region.
translation_verse: Apaleteɛ nɛibuiɛ tsakɛ fala nɛ kɛma ɛlɛ pete mɛ.

english_verse: But the Jewish leaders went to some of the important men in the town and to some respected women who were religious. They turned them against Paul and Barnabas and started making trouble for them. They even chased them out of this part of the country.
translation_verse: Pɔ Yudanɛ ezetsu kifu akpɛ badzɛ awɔpɔ̃nɔ gɛ bakpɛ kɛtakpu esi akɔ Owusoso Akaa nɛ̀ baanyɛ awɔpɔ̃nɔ teli tsyɛ nɛ ɔma. Bakɔ asɛ balɔɛtití nɛ Paulo nɛ̀ Barnaba shu kɛlɛ bakakalɛ ebu nɛ kɛma ɛlɛ mɛ.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas shook the dust from that place off their feet and went on to the city of Iconium.
translation_verse: Nɛ kititi lɔlɔ nɛ balɛishu soɔ, bexoelɛ osubhududu nɔ nɛ balɛ ɔtsɛrɛkpɔ shu elu kɛlɛ bashɛ avɛ Ikonio.

english_verse: But the Lord's followers in Antioch were very happy and were filled with the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ badɔkpasɛanɛ amɔ same pɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã oyuku balɛ mɛ.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas spoke in the synagogue in Iconium, just as they had done at Antioch, and many Jews and Gentiles put their faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Nɛ Ikonio nɔ, Paulo nɛ̀ Barnaba avɛ Yudanɛ ɔtsyɔakɔ asɛ gɛ babhɛtɛɛ ana. Ɛkɛlɛ badɛ ɖɔ kaɖɔ nɛ̀ ohumi, gɛ kɛkɔ ɔsɛ banɔ kpekpèɛ gɛenu Yudanɛ nɛ̀ bagɛ etinu Yudanɛ tsyɛ ayɛkɛ aŋã.

english_verse: But the Jews who did not have faith in him made the other Gentiles angry and turned them against the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Pɔ Yudanɛ gɛ bagɛ pɛ asɛ ayɛkɛ aŋã akpɛ ebèlà nɛ balɛishi pɛ bezĩ batɔ̃ akɔ bagɛ ayɛkɛ aŋã.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas stayed there for a while, having faith in the Lord and bravely speaking his message. The Lord gave them the power to work miracles and wonders, and he showed that their message about his gift of undeserved grace was true.
translation_verse: Kliso Paulo nɛ̀ Barnaba azã ɛkɛlɛ nɛ ewui gele mɛ, pɛ bazaɖɔaɖɔ nɛ Apaleteɛ shu nɛ̀ ohumi. Kɛlɛ Apaleteɛ akɔ bamɔ yɛ tibhui bhuibu ibuiɛ ɔganà atsyindzye nɛ̀ ebui gɛ abhɛtɛ ibi gɛ akɔ babhɛtɛ mɛ.

english_verse: The people of Iconium did not know what to think. Some of them believed the Jewish group, and others believed the apostles.
translation_verse: Banunɔ nɛ ɔmamɛ ebu ɔtɔ nɛ bagɔlɔ shu, kawɔ̃pɛ tieli azã Yudanɛ ede, kɛlɛ tieli tsyɛ azã banɔkpɛkpɛ niede.

english_verse: Finally, some Gentiles and Jews, together with their leaders, decided to make trouble for Paul and Barnabas and to stone them to death.
translation_verse: Kibui kpa tɔtɔɖɔ zã Yudanɛ nɛ̀ bagɛ betinu Yudanɛ ɔlɔkɔ ɔplɛnɔ balɛ besheshèɛ teli mɛ, tɛ bakpɛ balɛ bɔtɔ̀ tɛ behu alɛ aklagbu.

english_verse: In Lystra there was a man who had been born with paralyzed feet and had never been able to walk.
translation_verse: Anyɛ teli gɛedzi kɛkpatà azã Listra ɔmamɛ, anɔ gɛ azanyã odenɔ yɛmɔbe pɛ akagã eɖi.

english_verse: The man was listening to Paul speak, when Paul saw that he had faith in Jesus and could be healed. So he looked straight at the man
translation_verse: Azataatɔ̃ nɛ̀ Paulo nɛ kibeɛ gɛ aza ɖɔaɖɔɔ. Paulo edzu awuhũ nɛ yɛabha gagalĩi kɛlɛ amɔ asɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã zã yɛmɛ asɛ tɛ̀ bebù bɔnyã akɔɛ

english_verse: and shouted, “Stand up!” The man jumped up and started walking around.
translation_verse: kliso Paulo akpɛ ozĩ ekulu yɛasɛ, “Lɔɛtití nɛ wɔtsɛɖɛ nabha!” Nɛ kibui ɛlɛ̀ tɛɖɔɖɔ shuɔ anyɛ alɔ, etsopoele kɛlɛ akpa gã.

english_verse: When the crowd saw what Paul had done, they yelled out in the language of Lycaonia, “The gods have turned into humans and have come down to us!”
translation_verse: Gɛ banɔɛza mɔ kɛgɛ Paulo abhɛtɛɛ, bakpɛ ozĩ nɛ Likonia buluhũɔ mɛ asɛ, “Ibudiɛ zhiba bɔlɔ ishi nɛ anɔdzyrame tɛzazã mɛ!”

english_verse: The people then gave Barnabas the name Zeus, and they gave Paul the name Hermes, because he did the talking.
translation_verse: Bakɔ Barnaba kinye asɛ Zeus kɛlɛ bakɔ Paulo xɔnɔ asɛ Herme, ɔmɔsɛ yɛedzi anɔ gɛ azaɖɔaɖɔɔ.

english_verse: The temple of Zeus was near the entrance to the city. Its priest and the crowds wanted to offer a sacrifice to Barnabas and Paul. So the priest brought some bulls and flowers to the city gates.
translation_verse: Obudi Zeus yiulũazazã, gɛ yɛobudi buvũɔ ɔzã ɔma nɔwatɔ alɔkɔ banɛtsyɔɛnyè nɛ̀ osi efufù kɛgã amana ɔma niedulũɔ, ɔmɔsɛ yɛnɛ banɔɛza pɛasɛ abhɛtɛ bɔdɔ̃ pɔpɔɛ akɔ alɛ.

english_verse: When the two apostles found out about this, they tore their clothes in horror and ran to the crowd, shouting:
translation_verse: Pɔ gɛ banɔkpɛkpɛ Barnaba nɛ̀ Paulo elũ kibui ɛlɛ, batsyɔɛ balɛ akpɛkpɛ nɛishuɔ nɛ ɔmɔpɔɛ mɛ, kɛlɛ bebu tɛtsɛɖɛmɛ ediku kɛzaa mɛ nɛ̀ ozĩ asɛ,

english_verse: Why are you doing this? We are humans just like you. Please give up all this foolishness. Turn to the living God, who made the sky, the earth, the sea, and everything in them.
translation_verse: “Bɔlɔ adzya alɛ amɔ, kiɖe so pɛ nɔbhɛtɛ ana? Bɔlɔ tsyɛ banɔdzyrame bunu asɛ wɔnɔ ana. Bɔmana wɔnɔ kibui gbangbã tɛ butseɖe wɔnɔ tɛ̀ notsyina nɛ ebui alɛ gɛ lama kɛnana mɛ niede, tɛ nɔtɔnɔ Owusoso Akaa enunuɔ niede, anunɔ gɛ yɛabhɛtɛ kititi, kesiakpa, aƒu nɛ̀ kɛka nyia ka gɛkpasɛ alɛ mɛ.

english_verse: In times past, God let each nation go its own way.
translation_verse: Nɛ kuku ibeɛ, akɔ ɛmadi ka nyia ka azã nɛ ɛkpã nɛ balɛ tɛpɛpɛ ulũ.

english_verse: But he showed he was there by the good things he did. God sends rain from heaven and makes your crops grow. He gives food to you and makes your hearts glad.
translation_verse: Pɔ ebui gbangbã gɛ abhɛtɛ aɖɔaɖɔ nɛishu. Abhɛtɛ ɛyamɛ gbangbã, gɛ akɔ subha adɔ edenɔ kititíɛ kɛlɛ bɔŋaŋã ba nɛ bɔlɛ kibe. Akɔ bɔlɔ kɛŋaŋã kpekpè kɛlɛ akɔ same mɔmɔ aba bɔlɔ etúɔ mɛ.”

english_verse: Even after Paul and Barnabas had said all this, they could hardly keep the people from offering a sacrifice to them.
translation_verse: Nɛ ebui alɛ niesi bubú ɔkɔ alɛ tsyɛ elo, kɛgagalĩ ɔkɔ Barnaba nɛ̀ Paulo tɛ betsodzì banɔɛza asɛ batɛpɔɛ bɔdɔ̃ akɔ alɛ.

english_verse: Some Jewish leaders from Antioch and Iconium came and turned the crowds against Paul. They hit him with stones and dragged him out of the city, thinking he was dead.
translation_verse: Yudanɛ teli ede Antioxia nɛ̀ Ikonio aba ɛkɛlɛ ebetsyina banɔɛza ekpeɖekpeɖe mɛ. Banɔɛza ata aklagbu ehu Paulo kɛlɛ betsìi nɛ kesiakpa avɛnɔ ɔma nɔwatɔ, beɖi asɛ etsí.

english_verse: But when the Lord's followers gathered around Paul, he stood up and went back into the city. The next day he and Barnabas went to Derbe. Paul and Barnabas Return to Antioch in Syria
translation_verse: Pɔ kidegba gɛ badɔkpasɛnɛ ebeshogo nɛishuɔ, alɔ kɛlɛ etsyina avɛ ɔmamɛ. Gɛ keli azaa, yɛ nɛ̀ Barnaba abashɛ avɛ Derbe. Paulo Nɛ̀ Barnaba Etsyina Avɛ Atioxia Nɛ Siria

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas preached the good news in Derbe and won some people to the Lord. Then they went back to Lystra, Iconium, and Antioch in Pisidia.
translation_verse: Baɖɔ Kibui Gbangbã nɛ Derbe, kɛlɛ bamɔ badɔkpasɛnɛ kpekpè teli nɛ ɛkɛlɛ. Kidegba betsyina adɛgã Listra, Ikonio nɛ̀ Antioxia,

english_verse: They encouraged the followers and begged them to remain faithful. They told them, “We have to suffer a lot before we can get into God's kingdom.”
translation_verse: pɛ bazakpɛ badɔkpasɛnɛ ohumi nɛ̀ kemi tɛ̀ bazã tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã gɛinu kibui gbã nabha. “Kɛlɛ ɔsɛ bɔgã bɔtɔ̀ gele mɛ flogo tɛ bɔmɔ kidzo ɔvɛ Owusoso Akaa bukusi ŋaŋã mɛ.”

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas chose some leaders for each of the churches. Then they went without eating and prayed that the Lord would take good care of these leaders who had trusted in the Lord.
translation_verse: Paulo nɛ̀ Barnaba edzu ɔhamɛ besheshè akɔ alɛ nɛ ɔha ka nyia ka mɛ, kɛlɛ nɛ Owusoso Akaa tikukulu nɛ̀ olugbũnyanyã mɛɛ, balɔkɔ balɛ akpɛ ahɔɛ akɔ Apaleteɛ gɛ yɛabha bayɛkɛ aŋãa.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas went on through Pisidia to Pamphylia,
translation_verse: Gɛ badɛ gãfala nɛ Pisidia bɔdɔ̃ mɛɛ, bebekoeyì nɛ Pamfilia,

english_verse: where they preached in the town of Perga. Then they went down to Attalia
translation_verse: kɛlɛ gɛ baɖɔ kibui nɛ Perge ebekeɛ, bagã ediyi nɛ Atalia.

english_verse: and sailed to Antioch in Syria. It was there that they had been placed in God's care for the work they had now completed.
translation_verse: Denɔ Atalia nɔ beku kelĩ abhaohu nɛ ɛkɛlɛ pɛ bebeyì nɛ Antioxia. Nɛ ɛkɛlɛ, balɔkɔ balɛishu adɛ akpɛ ahɔɛ akɔ Owusoso Akaa nɛ kɛxɔ̃ɛ gɛ ebhui alɛ ebu bawɔa ebekeɛ shu.

english_verse: After arriving in Antioch, they called the church together. They told the people what God had helped them do and how he had made it possible for the Gentiles to believe.
translation_verse: Gɛ bediku Antioxia nɔ, beshogo ɔha kɛlɛ bakɔ alɛ tititì nɛ ebuiɛ gɛ Owusoso Akaa abhɛtɛ aganà balɛ abha, nɛ̀ kana gɛ adalɛ idzo akɔ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã ɔkɔ bagɛ etinu Yudanɛ.

english_verse: Then they stayed there with the followers for a long time.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ bazã ɛkɛlɛ nɛ̀ badɔkpasɛanɛ kibè dzlolo.

english_verse: Some people came from Judea and started teaching the Lord's followers that they could not be saved, unless they were circumcised as Moses had taught.
translation_verse: Banyɛ teli ezhi ede Yudea aba Antioxia pɛ baba kasɛ ɔhamɛanɛ bɔdɔ̃ asɛ, “Onupɛ begibhui kɛhɔ̃a akɔ anɔ nɛ ɔkɔɔ gɛ Mose akasɛ ɔlɔ nulũɔ, atɛ bɔmɔ ɛkpã.”

english_verse: This caused trouble, and Paul and Barnabas argued with them about this teaching. So it was decided to send Paul and Barnabas and a few others to Jerusalem to discuss this problem with the apostles and the church leaders. The Church Leaders Meet in Jerusalem
translation_verse: Kibui ɛlɛ̀ badzì kawlɔ̃akɛ nɛ̀ ebui mɛ tsatsaɖakɛ nɛ Paulo, Barnaba nɛ̀ banunɔ nɛishi. Kliso bebu Paulo nɛ̀ Barnaba ɔplɛnɔ bayɛyɛkɛŋã teli, asɛ bavɛ Yerusalem tɛ bavɛ adɛmɔ banɔkpɛkpɛ nɛ̀ ɔhamɛ beshesheɛ nɛ kibui ɛlɛ shu.

english_verse: The men who were sent by the church went through Phoenicia and Samaria, telling how the Gentiles had turned to God. This news made the Lord's followers very happy.
translation_verse: Ɔhaa abakpɛ balɛidzo, kɛlɛ gɛ bagã kidzoɔ afala nɛ Foenisia nɛ̀ Samaria nɔ, betseɖe alɛ kana gɛ bagɛ etinu Yudanɛ tsyɛ etsyina balɛ etúɔ mɛ. Kibui ɛlɛ kɔ bayɛyɛkɛŋã amɔ same popopo.

english_verse: When the men arrived in Jerusalem, they were welcomed by the church, including the apostles and the leaders. They told them everything God had helped them do.
translation_verse: Gɛ Paulo nɛ̀ Barnaba ediku Yerusalemuɔ, ɔha, banɔkpɛkpɛ nɛ̀ Yerusalemuɔ ɔhamɛ beshesheɛ ayɛkɛ alɛ. Paulo nɛ̀ Barnaba abakɔ alɛ tititì nɛ kɛka nyia ka gɛ Owusoso Akaa abhɛtɛ aganà balɛ abha shu.

english_verse: But some Pharisees had become followers of the Lord. They stood up and said, “Gentiles who have faith in the Lord must be circumcised and told to obey the Law of Moses.”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ bayɛyɛkɛŋã teli gɛede Farisianɛ kɛzaa mɛ alɔ kititi kɛlɛ baɖɔ asɛ, “Kɛlɛ ɔsɛ bebhui kɛhɔ̃a akɔ bagɛ betinu Yudanɛ tɛ̀ bebisi edenɔ balɛ ahɔɛ mɛ asɛ babhɛtɛ atɔɖɔ Mose ɔsha nabha.”

english_verse: The apostles and church leaders met to discuss this problem about Gentiles.
translation_verse: Banɔkpɛkpɛ nɛ̀ ɔhamɛ beshesheɛ adɛtɔ̃ kɛtakpu tɛ̀ babhɛtɛ ekpeɖekpeɖe mɛ nɛ kibui bibisi ɛlɛ shu.

english_verse: They had talked it over for a long time, when Peter got up and said: My friends, you know that God decided long ago to let me be the one from your group to preach the good news to the Gentiles. God did this so they would hear and obey him.
translation_verse: Gɛ bapɔɛ kɛlɛishu ebekeɛ, Petro alɔ pɛ aɖɔ etseɖe alɛ asɛ, “Mɛadzya alɛ mɔ, nɔbaka asɛ kibe tili gɛba falaa, Owusoso Akaa ebu banɔ teli edenɔ wɔnɔɛshi ɔsɛ tɛ̀ bagɛ etinu Yudanɛ tsyɛ elũ Kibui Gbangbã ɛlɛ odenɔ mɛolugbũ mɛ̀ tɛ̀ bayɛkɛ aŋã.

english_verse: He knows what is in everyone's heart. And he showed he had chosen the Gentiles, when he gave them the Holy Spirit, just as he had given his Spirit to us.
translation_verse: Owusoso Akaa gɛenyi anɔdzyrame kitúɔ mɛ, akasɛ asɛ ayɛkɛ balɛ tsyɛ ɔgana Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã tɛkɔkɔ alɛ mɛ asɛ kɛkɔ gɛ akɔ bɔlɔ tsyɛ ana.

english_verse: God treated them in the same way that he treated us. They put their faith in him, and he made their hearts pure.
translation_verse: Egibu ɔtɔ nanà nɛ bɔlɔ nɛ̀ balɛishi, pɔ adalɛ balɛ etúɔ mɛ ɔgana tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛ.

english_verse: Now why are you trying to make God angry by placing a heavy burden on these followers? This burden was too heavy for us or our ancestors.
translation_verse: Otsiɛnɔ pɔ, kiɖe so pɛ nɔpɛ ɔsɛ tɛ nofeke Owusoso Akaa oɖi? Kiɖeso pɛ nɔlɔkɔ etukuɔ gɛ bɔlɔ alo bɔlɔ akaa alɛ tsyɛ agɛlà etukùɔ, ɔlɔkɔ tɔɖɔ badɔkpasɛanɛ xɔnɔ atakpu dza?

english_verse: But we believe that we will be saved by the gift of undeserved grace from our Lord Jesus Christ, just as the Gentiles are.
translation_verse: Ao! bɔyɛkɛ ŋã ɔsɛ gana bɔlɔ Apalete Yesu tibhui bhuibuɔ mɛɛ, kɛlɛ bayɛkɛ akɔ bɔlɔ asɛ balɛ ana.”

english_verse: Everyone kept quiet and listened as Barnabas and Paul told how God had given them the power to work a lot of miracles and wonders for the Gentiles.
translation_verse: Kɛza petee abadɔ kananáa gɛ belũ kaɖɔ gɛ Barnaba nɛ̀ Paulo aɖɔ etseɖe alɛ nɛ atsyindzye ebuiɛ gɛ labhɛtɛ ibiɛ gɛ Owusoso Akaa abhɛtɛ nɛ bagɛ etinu Yudanɛ nɛishi ɔgana balɛ abha.

english_verse: After they had finished speaking, James said: My friends, listen to me!
translation_verse: Gɛ bebhui balɛ kaɖɔɔ, Yakobo tsyɛ aɖɔaɖɔ asɛ, “Mɛadzya alɛ mɔ, nɔtatɔ̃ nɛ mɛ.

english_verse: Simon Peter has told how God first came to the Gentiles and made some of them his own people.
translation_verse: Simon gɛenu Petroɔ ashɔrɔ bɔlɔ kana gɛ putɔnɔ Owusoso Akaa akasɛ yɛ tili tɛlɔlɔkɔ nɛ kibuɔ gɛ ebu banɔ edenɔ bagɛ etinu Yudanɛ mɛ akɔ yɛishu.

english_verse: This agrees with what the prophets wrote,
translation_verse: Owusoso Akaa ebuiɖɔluanɛ ebuiɛ lashɔnɔ kibui ɛlɛ gɛ bafɔnɔ elu asɛ,

english_verse: “I, the Lord, will return and rebuild David's fallen house. I will build it from its ruins and set it up again.
translation_verse: “ ‘Nɛ kɛlɛ niedeɛ ɛbatsyina ba tɛ̀ ɛbadzinì David kagba gayɛ nɔ dzu. Ɛbadzini kivũkpuɔ dzu, kɛlɛ ɛbadzini kalɛ bhɛtɛ avɔvɔ,

english_verse: Then other nations will turn to me and be my chosen ones. I, the Lord, say this.
translation_verse: ɔsɛ tɛ banɔ gɔgɔɛ nɔ tsyɛ atɔnɔ Apaleteɛ niede, ɔplɛnɔ bagɛ etinu Yudanɛ petee gɛ mɛinyeɛ lɛ balɛishuɔ. kana Apaleteɛ gɛ abhɛtɛ ebui alɛ aɖɔ,

english_verse: I promised it long ago.”
translation_verse: ebui gɛ benyi edenɔ kibè tili ke.

english_verse: And so, my friends, I don't think we should place burdens on the Gentiles who are turning to God.
translation_verse: “Kinu mɛ kebhu kukulu ɔsɛ, kɛma ɔsɛ tɛ̀ bɔkɔ ebuiɛ agagalĩ ɔkɔ bagɛ etinu Yudanɛ gɛ betsyina aba Owusoso Akaa niede.

english_verse: We should simply write and tell them not to eat anything that has been offered to idols. They should be told not to eat the meat of any animal that has been strangled or that still has blood in it. They must also not commit any terrible sexual sins.
translation_verse: Pɔ nɔkɔ bɔfɔnɔ keplukpa ɔkɔ alɛ boŋ tɛ̀ bayɔlɔ ibudi bɔdɔ̃ ŋaŋã gɛ bɔlɛishu gɛdalɛ, kɛgɔlɔ ŋaŋã, nɛ̀ bɔdzya nɔnɔ gɛide bagã gɛ bedzinì balɛ ili nɛ̀ butsì tɛŋaŋã mɛ.

english_verse: We must remember that the Law of Moses has been preached in city after city for many years, and every Sabbath it is read in our synagogues. A Letter to Gentiles Who Had Faith in the Lord
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ bakala Mose ɔsha ɔkɔ banunɔ nɛ ɔma ka nyia ka mɛ odenɔ kuku ibeɛ, kɛlɛ bakalà ɔlɛ nɛ balɛ ɛtsyɔakɔ nɛ Kimoelewui ka nyia ka abha.” Ɔhamɛ Besheshèɛ Eplukpa Gɛ Bafɔnɔ Asɛsɛ Bagɛ Etinu Yudanɛ

english_verse: The apostles, the leaders, and all the church members decided to send some men to Antioch along with Paul and Barnabas. They chose Silas and Judas Barsabbas, who were two leaders of the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ banɔkpɛkpɛ nɛ̀ beshesheɛ ɔlɔkɔ ɔplɛnɔ ɔhaa petee adɛmɛ asɛ bebebu balɛ ahɔɛmɛ banɔ teli nɛ kɛzaa mɛ aplɛnɔ Paulo nɛ̀ Barnaba tɛ basɛsɛ alɛ nɛ Antioxia. Banunɔ gɛ bebuɔ balɛ enu, Yuda, nɛ̀ Sila, banɔ taabha alɛ enu otugbà banɔ nɛ ɔhaa nɛishi.

english_verse: They wrote a letter that said: We apostles and leaders send friendly greetings to all of you Gentiles who are followers of the Lord in Antioch, Syria, and Cilicia.
translation_verse: Keplukpa gɛ bafɔnɔ akpɛ balɛ ahɔɛ mɛ ebuiɛ alɛ enu asɛ, Bɔlɔ banɔkpɛkpɛ nɛ̀ beshesheɛ gɛenu wɔnɔ adzya alɛ, Bɔfɔnɔ keplukpa alɛ ɔsɛsɛ bagɛ etinu Yudanɛ nɛ Antioxia, Siria nɛ̀ Kilikia nɔ, Bɔsɛnɛ wɔnɔ.

english_verse: We have heard that some people from here have terribly upset you by what they said. But we did not send them!
translation_verse: Bulũ ɔsɛ bɔlɔ ɔmayɛ teli ekoeyì nɛ bɔlɔ ishi, gɛ bɔgɛ kpɛ alɛ pɛ baba alɔkɔ ebui teli akpɛ wɔnɔ buluhũ nɔ, babakpɛ wɔnɔ buluhũ nɛ̀ balɛ ebui ɖɔɖɔ.

english_verse: So we met together and decided to choose some men and to send them to you along with our good friends Barnabas and Paul.
translation_verse: Kliso bɔlɔ petee badɛmɛ ɔtɔɖɔ ɛlɛ abha ɔsɛ tɛ̀ bubu banɔ teli ɔkpɛ wɔnɔ ɔkɔ aplɛnɔ bɔlɔ ɔbhia alɛ gɛ bɔdɛmɛ nɔ gɛenu Barnaba nɛ̀ Paulo,

english_verse: These men have risked their lives for our Lord Jesus Christ.
translation_verse: banɔ gɛ balɔkɔ balɛ ɛkpã etsye nɛ bɔlɔ Apalete Yesu Kristo ɛtakpu.

english_verse: We are also sending Judas and Silas, who will tell you in person the same things that we are writing.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ bɔkpɛ Yuda nɛ̀ Sila nɛ wɔnɔ ɔkɔ tɛ̀ babà ɖɔaɖɔ atɔɖɔ kɛklagbu nɛ kɛgɛ ɛlɛ ishu bɔfɔnɔ keplukpa alɛ akɔ wɔnɔ.

english_verse: The Holy Spirit has shown us that we should not place any extra burden on you.
translation_verse: Kɛpɛ̃ ɔkɔ Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã nɛ̀ bɔlɔ tɛ bɔtɛ tɔɖɔ wɔnɔ kituku nanà gɛdɛ ebui alɛ gɛ bɔba tseɖe wɔnɔ.

english_verse: But you should not eat anything offered to idols. You should not eat any meat that still has the blood in it or any meat of any animal that has been strangled. You must also not commit any terrible sexual sins. If you follow these instructions, you will do well. We send our best wishes.
translation_verse: Nobu ishu oyinɔ odenɔ bɔŋaŋã gɛ batɔ akɔ ibudiɛ, dzyosu nɛ̀ bɔdzya gɛide bagã gɛ bedzyinì balɛ iliɛ mɛ, ɔlɔkɔ ɔplɛnɔ kɛgɔlɔ ŋaŋã. Nɔkpɛ kemi tɛ̀ nɔshɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ ebui alɛ nɛidi shu. Bɔsɛnɔ wɔnɔ piapii.

english_verse: The four men left Jerusalem and went to Antioch. Then they called the church members together and gave them the letter.
translation_verse: Bakpɛ banɔ nɔ nɛidzo pɛ bashɛ avɛ Antioxia. Ɛkɛlɛ, bedishogò ɔha pɛ balɔkɔ keplukpa akɔ alɛ.

english_verse: When the letter was read, everyone was pleased and greatly encouraged.
translation_verse: Banunɔ akalà keplukpa kɛlɛ same eku alɛ nɛ kalɛ mɛ ebuiɛ shu.

english_verse: Judas and Silas were prophets, and they spoke a long time, encouraging and helping the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Yuda nɛ̀ Sila gɛedzi ebuiɖɔluanɔ baɖɔ ebui gele alɔkɔ akpɛ badzya kemi nɛ̀ ohumi nɔ.

english_verse: The men from Jerusalem stayed on in Antioch for a while. And when they left to return to the ones who had sent them, the followers wished them well.
translation_verse: Gɛ baza ɛkɛlɛ nɛ ewui teli edeɛ, ɔha ɔkpɛ balɛ kidzo betsyina ashɛ nɛ̀ tiyuyulà nɛ̀ ishu yɔyɔ ɔsɛsɛ bagɛ akpɛ balɛ ɔkɔ.

english_verse: But Paul and Barnabas stayed on in Antioch, where they and many others taught and preached about the Lord. Paul and Barnabas Go Their Separate Ways
translation_verse: Paulo nɛ̀ Barnaba xɔnɔ awɔ Antioxia, ɛkɛlɛ balɛ nɛ̀ banunɔ teli azakasɛ bɔdɔ̃ pɛ bazaɖɔ Apalete nibuiɛ. Kawlɔ̃akɛ Eku Paulo Nɛ̀ Barnaba Ɛshi

english_verse: Sometime later Paul said to Barnabas, “Let's go back and visit the Lord's followers in the cities where we preached his message. Then we will know how they are doing.”
translation_verse: Kidegba, Paulo ebetseɖe Barnaba asɛ, “Kɔ butsyina ɔvɛ tɛ budiɖi bɔlɔ adzya alɛ nɛ ɛma mɛ gɛ bɔɖɔ Apalete nibuiɛ kana tɛ̀ buɖì kana gɛ balɛ.”

english_verse: Barnabas wanted to take along John, whose other name was Mark.
translation_verse: Barnaba apɛ asɛ tɛ agbana Yohanes, gɛ bezekulu tsyɛ asɛ Markoɔ avɛ aplɛnɔ alɛ,

english_verse: But Paul did not want to, because Mark had left them in Pamphylia and had stopped working with them.
translation_verse: Pɔ kigiku Paulo xɔnɔ awã ana, ɔmɔsɛ atɛ shɛnɛ ɔhɔɛ nɛ balɛ ishu elu eɖi nɛ Pamfilia kɛlɛ, agavɛ ɛlɛ abha awɔa axɔ̃ɛ nɛ alɛ.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas argued, then each of them went his own way. Barnabas took Mark and sailed to Cyprus,
translation_verse: Kibui ɛlɛ kɔ kawlɔ̃akɛ nyagãa eku Paulo nɛ̀ Barnaba ishi, kɛkɔ ɔsɛ bebu ɔtɔ nɛ bagɔlɔ shu. Barnaba agbana Marko aplɛnɔ yɛishu kɛlɛ beku kelĩabhaohu avɛ Kipro.

english_verse: but Paul took Silas and left after the followers had placed them in God's care.
translation_verse: Paulo xɔnɔ ebu Sila aplɛnɔ yɛishu, kɛlɛ gɛ ɔha alɔkɔ balɛ akpɛ ahɔɛ akɔ Apaleteɛ asɛ ebhui balɛ ebuɔ, batɔnɔ kidzo ashɛ.

english_verse: They traveled through Syria and Cilicia, encouraging the churches.
translation_verse: Agã ekoeyì nɛ Siria nɛ̀ Kilikia pɛ azakpɛ ɔhamɛanɛ ohumi.

english_verse: Paul and Silas went back to Derbe and Lystra, where there was a follower named Timothy. His mother was also a follower. She was Jewish, and his father was Greek.
translation_verse: Paulo agã edikoeyi nɛ Derbe nɛ̀ Listra, ɛkɛlɛ adɔkpasɛanɛ teli gɛ bezekulu asɛ Timoteo azã, anɔ gɛ yɛelĩ enu Yudadzi nɛ̀ anɔ gɛ ayɛyɛkɛŋã, pɔ yɛakaa edzi Helanɔ.

english_verse: The Lord's followers in Lystra and Iconium said good things about Timothy,
translation_verse: Badzya nɛ Listra nɛ̀ Ikonio nɔ baɖɔaɖɔ gbangbã nɛ Timoteo shu.

english_verse: and Paul wanted him to go with them. But Paul first had him circumcised, because all the Jewish people around there knew that Timothy's father was Greek.
translation_verse: Paulo apɛ asɛ tɛ agbana yɛ evunɔ soɔ, ebhui kɛhɔ̃a akɔɛ nɛ Yudanɛ gɛ balɛ kibé ɛlɛ shu so, ɔmɔsɛ balɛ petee enyi asɛ yɛakaa enu Helaeyi.

english_verse: As Paul and the others went from city to city, they told the followers what the apostles and leaders in Jerusalem had decided, and they urged them to follow these instructions.
translation_verse: Gɛ bazagã afala nɛ ɛmaa mɛɛ, baaɖɔ kibuiɛ gɛ banɔkpɛkpɛ nɛ̀ beshesheɛ aɖɔ nɛ Yerusalem etseɖe alɛ tɛ baabhɛtɛ tɔɖɔ kɛlɛ abha.

english_verse: The churches became stronger in their faith, and each day more people put their faith in the Lord. Paul's Vision in Troas
translation_verse: Kliso bakɔ ɔhaa amɔ ohumi nɛ balɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛ kɛlɛ balɛ tɛkaklà mɛ zikpè tɔɖɔ kibe nyia be. Paulo Bɔlala Gɛaza Nɛ Anyɛ Gɛaza Makedonia Shu

english_verse: Paul and his friends went through Phrygia and Galatia, but the Holy Spirit would not let them preach in Asia.
translation_verse: Paulo nɛ̀ yɛɔbhia alɛ azagã kidzo fala nɛ Frigia nɛ̀ Galatia, pɔ Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã gɛkɔ balɛ kidzo asɛ baɖɔ kibui gbangbã nɛ Asia bɔdɔ̃ mɛ.

english_verse: After they arrived in Mysia, they tried to go into Bithynia, but the Spirit of Jesus would not let them.
translation_verse: Gɛ bediku Misia bubò nabhaa, bapɛ asɛ etsoku Bitania, pɔ Yesu Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ ɔgɛkɔ balɛ kidzo ana.

english_verse: So they went on through Mysia until they came to Troas.
translation_verse: Kliso bagafala nɛ Misia pɛ bavɛ Troa.

english_verse: During the night, Paul had a vision of someone from Macedonia who was standing there and begging him, “Come over to Macedonia and help us!”
translation_verse: Nɛ elishi alɛ bebu kɛdɔ̃ akasɛ Paulo nɛ anyɛ teli gɛ alɛ Makedonia gɛ eli kitití pɛ ebisi yɛɔdɔ asɛ, “Tsakɛ ba Makedonia tɛ̀ ɔbɔplɛnɔ ɔlɔ.”

english_verse: After Paul had seen the vision, we began looking for a way to go to Macedonia. We were sure that God had called us to preach the good news there. Lydia Becomes a Follower of the Lord
translation_verse: Gɛ bebu ebui alɛ akasɛ Paulo ebekeɛ, bɔbalɛ tsiɔ ɔlɛ kɛlɛ bɔvɛ Makedonia, bɔyɛkɛ kɛlɛ ɔsɛ Owusoso Akaa yɛekulu bɔlɔ ɔsɛ bɔdɛɖɔ kibui gbangbã ɔkɔ alɛ. Lidia Yɛ Titsyitsyina Nɛ Filipia

english_verse: We sailed straight from Troas to Samothrace, and the next day we arrived in Neapolis.
translation_verse: Denɔ Troa nɔ buku kelĩabhaohu kɛlɛ bɔtsakɛ tĩi vɛ Samotrake, kɛlɛ nɛ kelizaza mɛɛ, bɔshɛ vɛ Neapoli.

english_verse: From there we went to Philippi, which is a Roman colony in the first district of Macedonia. We spent several days in Philippi.
translation_verse: Denɔ ɛkɛlɛ bɔgã kidzo ɔvɛ Filipi gɛenu Romanɛ kesiakpa abha ɔma gbagbla ɔdɛ nɛ Makedonia bɔdɔ̃ mɛ. Kɛlɛ bɔzã ɛkɛlɛ ewui gelee.

english_verse: Then on the Sabbath we went outside the city gate to a place by the river, where we thought there would be a Jewish meeting place for prayer. We sat down and talked with the women who came.
translation_verse: Nɛ Kimoelewui nabhaa, bɔvɛ ɔmaa niedeɛ nɛ kelĩ tieli shu, tɛ bɔdɛ pɛɛ ɔkɔ toli ɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa tikukulu. Bɔzã kesi kɛlɛ bɔkpa kaɖɔ ɖɔtseɖe badzɛ gɛ eshogò nɛ ɛkɛlɛ.

english_verse: One of them was Lydia, who was from the city of Thyatira and sold expensive purple cloth. She was a worshiper of the Lord God, and he made her willing to accept what Paul was saying.
translation_verse: Balɛ ɔmayɛmɛ tenikpo gɛ azataatɔ̃ nɛ ɔlɔ yɛenu Lidia, anɔ gɛ azadɛ apɔtɛ gɛ layɛkɛ kedzì, pɛ ehoeyi azã yɛahɔɛ pɛ ede Tiatira pɛ enu anɔ gɛ akpɛ kɛtakpu esi akɔ Owusoso Akaa. Apaleteɛ adalɛ yɛ kitúɔ mɛ ɔsɛ tɛ̀ ayɛkɛ Paulo ebui gɛ amana.

english_verse: Then after she and her family were baptized, she kept on begging us, “If you think I really do have faith in the Lord, come stay in my home.” Finally, we accepted her invitation. Paul and Silas Are Put in Jail
translation_verse: Gɛ bakpɛ bulĩ akɔ yɛ nɛ yɛɔpã mɛ anɛ, ayɛkɛ bɔlɔ akpɛ yɛɔpã mɛ. Aɖɔ asɛ “Onupɛ nɔdɛmɛ ɔsɛ inu anɔ gɛ ayɛkɛ Apalete nabha aŋãa, kɛlɛ nɔba tɛ nɔbɔzã mɛ bɔpã mɛ.” Kɛlɛ ebu ebui mɛ akɔ bɔlɔ. Bakpɛ Paulo Nɛ̀ Sila Nɛ Ɔɖavũmɛ

english_verse: One day on our way to the place of prayer, we were met by a slave girl. She had a spirit in her that gave her the power to tell the future. By doing this she made a lot of money for her owners.
translation_verse: Kiwui tinikpo gɛ bɔvɛ ɛkɛlɛ gɛ bukulu Owusoso Akaa, bɔtsyana adzɛnuvɔɛ dzidzi teli gɛ ɔfɔfuĩa toli ɔzãyɛahɔɛ gɛ azaɖɔ ebui gɛ laba ɛlɛ mɛ kidegba. Amɔ ehoeyi kpekpè akɔ yɛapalete alɛ nɛ yɛ kebisìɛ shu.

english_verse: The girl followed Paul and the rest of us, and she kept yelling, “These men are servants of the Most High God! They are telling you how to be saved.”
