translation_verse: Adzɛnuvɔɛ nɔ atɔnɔ Paulo nɛ̀ bɔlɔ bagɔgɔɔ pɛ akpɛ ozĩ asɛ, “Banyɛ alɛ enu Kititiayɛ Gbagbla yɛaxɔ̃ɛdɛ, gɛ etseɖe wɔnɔ kidzo nɔnɔ abha gɛ baba yɛkɛ wɔnɔ elú ɛkpã.”

english_verse: This went on for several days. Finally, Paul got so upset that he turned and said to the spirit, “In the name of Jesus Christ, I order you to leave this girl alone!” At once the evil spirit left her.
translation_verse: Azã kibui ɛlɛ abha nɛ ewui gelee mɛ̀. Kidegba kɛbadɛ Paulo ulũ kɛlɛ etsyina kɛlɛ etseɖe ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ asɛ, “Nɛ Yesu Kristo kinye mɛɛ, ɛkpɛ ɔsha akɔwɔ ɔsɛ tɛ okoeyi nɛ adzɛnuvɔɛ nɔ mɛ!” Tsiɔ̃ ɔlɛ koɔ, ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ koeyi nɛ adzɛnuvɔɛ nɔ mɛ.

english_verse: When the girl's owners realized they had lost all chances for making more money, they grabbed Paul and Silas and dragged them into court.
translation_verse: Kibeɛ gɛ adzɛnuvɔɛ dzidzì alɛ yɛapalete alɛ amɔ asɛ, ɛkɛlɛ gɛ bagã amɔ ehoeyiɛ ozĩɛɛ, bevũ Paulo nɛ̀ Sila avɛnɔ balɛ kedziɛ mɛ tɛ̀ badɛtsyɔa nɛ̀ ɔshaeleteɛ.

english_verse: They told the officials, “These Jews are upsetting our city!
translation_verse: Bagbana balɛ amana bebhukulu namɛ pɛ abaɖɔ asɛ, “Banɔ alɛ enu Yudanɛ kɛlɛ bamana buluhũ nɛ bɔlɔ ɔmamɛ

english_verse: They are telling us to do things we Romans are not allowed to do.”
translation_verse: baba kasɛ ɔsha tili gɛma ɛlɛ ɔzãkɔ ɔkɔ bɔlɔ Romanɛ tɛ bɔbhɛtɛ ɛlɛ abha.”

english_verse: The crowd joined in the attack on Paul and Silas. Then the officials tore the clothes off the two men and ordered them to be beaten with a whip.
translation_verse: Banɔɛza taplɛnɔ alɛ nɛ kititì lɔlɔ ɛlɛ nɛ Paulo nɛ̀ sila shu, kɛlɛ bebhukuluɔ akɔ ɔsha ɔsɛ bebú bɔdɔ̃ kpɛkpɛ nɛ balɛishu tɛ behuisi alɛ.

english_verse: After they had been badly beaten, they were put in jail, and the jailer was told to guard them carefully.
translation_verse: Gɛ behuisi alɛ ɖɔtɔ ɖɔtɔ ebekeɛ, bakpɛ alɛ nɛ ɔɖavũ mɛ, kɛlɛ agɛ ebú balɛ kɛpɔ akpɛ ɔsha akɔ ɔɖavũɔ abha eɖiɖieleteɛ asɛ tɛ belu awɔ̃ nɛ̀ alɛ piapii.

english_verse: The jailer did as he was told. He put them deep inside the jail and chained their feet to heavy blocks of wood.
translation_verse: Gɛ bayɛkɛ ɔshakɔkɔ ɔlɛ ebekèɛ, bakpɛ alɛ nɛ ɔɖavũɔ nɛishitɛbhà kɛlɛ bashɔ balɛ ɛtsɛɖɛ kpɛ isi tili mɛ gɛ bebu izĩ akpɛɛ.

english_verse: About midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing praises to God, while the other prisoners listened.
translation_verse: Nɛ elishi tetiɛ Paulo nɛ̀ Sila bezekulu Owusoso Akaa pɛ baazhã ɛdzɛ akɔɛ, kɛlɛ ɔɖavũmɛ azazã gɔgɔɛ nɔ aza taatɔ̃ nɛ alɛ.

english_verse: Suddenly a strong earthquake shook the jail to its foundations. The doors opened, and the chains fell from all the prisoners.
translation_verse: Nɛ balɛ matì matì mɛɛ, kesiakpa ebeshukù gagalĩi akɔ asɛ ɔɖavuɔ itsuru nɔ tsyɛ bashukù. Odunu ɔlɔ koɔ ɔɖavũɔ budulũɔ petee badalɛ, kɛlɛ aka nyia aka yɛɖahuiɛ boele.

english_verse: When the jailer woke up and saw that the doors were open, he thought that the prisoners had escaped. He pulled out his sword and was about to kill himself.
translation_verse: Ɔɖavũ abha eɖiɖi eleteɛ aba nyɛmɛ, kɛlɛ gɛ amɔ asɛ ɔɖavũ dulũɔ dalɛ liɛ, etso yɛ bɛtɛ asɛ tɛ̀ eyì yɛishu ɔmɔsɛ eɖi asɛ ɔɖuvũazazã ebu tɛtsɛɖɛmɛ ashɛ.

english_verse: But Paul shouted, “Don't harm yourself! No one has escaped.”
translation_verse: Pɔ Paulo abakpɛ ozĩ asɛ, “Tɛkpɛ ishu kibì! Bɔlɔ pete lɛ ɛkɛlɛ.

english_verse: The jailer asked for a torch and went into the jail. He was shaking all over as he knelt down in front of Paul and Silas.
translation_verse: Ɔɖavũ abha eɖiɖieleteɛ ebisi asɛ bamana efú, kɛlɛ abhɛtɛ zia vɛ edi zhi ekumu pɛ eshuku gbagbà nɛ Paulo nɛ̀ Sila otugbà.

english_verse: After he had led them out of the jail, he asked, “What must I do to be saved?”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ Agbana balɛ eyinɔ pɛ ebisi balɛ asɛ, “Bapalete, kɛlɛ mɛbabhɛtɛ pɛ ɛba mɔ ɛkpã?”

english_verse: They replied, “Have faith in the Lord Jesus and you will be saved! This is also true for everyone who lives in your home.”
translation_verse: Bebuiliulũ asɛ, “Yɛkɛ Apalete Yesu abha ɔŋã kɛlɛ ɔbɔ mɔ ɛkpã, wɔ nɛ̀ wɔ bɔpã mɛ anɛ.”

english_verse: Then Paul and Silas told him and everyone else in his house about the Lord.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ baɖɔ Apalete nɛibuiɛ etseɖe yɛ nɛ̀ banɔ gɔgɔɛ nɔ nɛ̀ yɛ bɔpã mɛ.

english_verse: While it was still night, the jailer took them to a place where he could wash their cuts and bruises. Then he and everyone in his home were baptized.
translation_verse: Nɛ elishi ɔɖa tatá ɔlɛ mɛɛ, ɔɖavũ abha eɖiɖi eleteɛ agbanà alɛ avɛnɔ adɛbɔ apɔ̃ɔ akɔ alɛ, kɛlɛ tsiɔ̃ ɔlɛ koɔ bakpɛ bulĩ nɛ ɛtakpu akɔɛ nɛ̀ yɛɔpãmɛanɛ.

english_verse: They were very glad they had put their faith in God. After this, the jailer took Paul and Silas to his home and gave them something to eat.
translation_verse: Ɔɖavũ abha eɖiɖieleteɛ agbana balɛ avɛnɔ yɛɔpã mɛ pɛ edidzu ɔkpɔlɔ̃ akɔ alɛ, same mɔmɔ eku yɛmɛ ɔmɔsɛ ayɛkɛ Owusoso Akaa nabha ŋã, yɛ nɛ̀ yɛɔpã mɛ anɛ petee.

english_verse: The next morning the officials sent some police with orders for the jailer to let Paul and Silas go.
translation_verse: Gɛ keliɛ azaa, behukuluɔ akpɛ banɔ gbagbla asɛsɛ ɔɖavũabha eɖiɖi eleteɛ ɔkɔ asɛ, “Shɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ banyɛ alɛ shu.”

english_verse: The jailer told Paul, “The officials have ordered me to set you free. Now you can leave in peace.”
translation_verse: Ɔɖavũ abha eɖiɖi eleteɛ etseɖe Paulo asɛ, “Bebhukuluɔ akɔ ɔsha asɛ bɔshɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ wɔ nɛ̀ Sila shu. Otsi alɛ nɔbɔlà ɔshɛ. Nɔgavɛ nɛ ishu yɔyɔ mɛ.”

english_verse: But Paul told the police, “We are Roman citizens, and the Roman officials had us beaten in public without giving us a trial. They threw us into jail. Now do they think they can secretly send us away? No, they cannot! They will have to come here themselves and let us out.”
translation_verse: Pɔ Paulo etseɖe banɔ gbagbla asɛ, “Behuisi bɔlɔ nɛ kɛtsalɛ mɛ pɛ bakpɛ bɔlɔ nɛ ɔɖavũmɛ, bunu Romanɛ, pɔ begikulu kebhu nɛ̀ bɔlɔ. Kɛlɛ otsi bapɛ asɛ tɛ̀ bashɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ bɔlɔ shu nɛ tikpukpũ mɛ dzã? Ao! Nɔkɔ baba ɛkɛlɛ tɛ̀ bebebú bɔlɔ eyinɔ nɛ ɔma mɛ.”

english_verse: When the police told the officials that Paul and Silas were Roman citizens, the officials were afraid.
translation_verse: Banɔ gbagbla avɛ adɛkɔ behukuluɔ tititì, kɛlɛ gɛ belũ asɛ Paulo nɛ̀ Sila enu Romanɛ, kifù dɛ alɛ.

english_verse: So they came and apologized. They led them out of the jail and asked them to please leave town.
translation_verse: Babà yɔ balɛ ishumɛ kɛlɛ bagbanà alɛ eyinɔ nɛ ɔɖavũɔ mɛ, pɛ betseɖe balɛ asɛ bekoeyì nɛ ɔma gbagbala mɛ.

english_verse: But Paul and Silas went straight to the home of Lydia, where they saw the Lord's followers and encouraged them. Then they left.
translation_verse: Gɛ Paulo nɛ̀ Sila ekoeyì nɛ ɔɖavũɔ mɛɛ, bavɛ Lidia bɔpã mɛ ɛkɛlɛ badɛtsyɔa nɛ̀ ɔhaa kɛlɛ bakpɛ balɛ kemi. Kɛlɛ bashɛ.

english_verse: After Paul and his friends had traveled through Amphipolis and Apollonia, they went on to Thessalonica. A synagogue was in that city.
translation_verse: Gɛ bagã edi yi nɛ Amfipoli nɛ̀ Apolonia nɔ, bebe yi nɛ Tesalonika, ɛkɛlɛ Yudanɛ ɔtsyɔakɔ toli ɔzã.

english_verse: So as usual, Paul went there to worship, and on three Sabbaths he spoke to the people. He used the Scriptures
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ asɛ gɛ abhɛtɛɛ ana, Paulo avɛ ɔtsyɔakɔ, kɛlɛ nɛ Emoelewui tata abhaa, azagã Owusoso Akaa ebuiɛ mɛ nɛ alɛ,

english_verse: to show them that the Messiah had to suffer, but that he would rise from death. Paul also told them that Jesus is the Messiah he was preaching about.
translation_verse: pɛ ezebu ebui mɛ pɛ ebu akasɛ asɛ kɛlɛ ɔsɛ Yesuɔ amɔ bɔtɔ flogo tɛ̀ befeke yɛ edenɔ bekpuɔ mɛ. Aɖɔ asɛ, “Yesu alɛ gɛ yɛibuiɛ mɛɖɔɔ yɛenu Kristoɔ.

english_verse: Some of them believed what Paul had said, and they became followers with Paul and Silas. Some Gentiles and many important women also believed the message.
translation_verse: Kesilulũ abakɔ Yudanɛ teli kɛlɛ betsyina atɔnɔ Paulo nɛ̀ Sila, kidzo ɛlɛ nabha Helanɛ kpekpèɛ teli gɛ besi Owusoso Akaa nɛifùɔ ɔlɔkɔ ɔplɛnɔ ɛzãkɔ elete gɛenu badzɛ kpekpèɛ tsyɛ abhɛtɛ ɛlɛ ana.

english_verse: The Jewish leaders were jealous and got some troublemakers who hung around the marketplace to start a riot in the city. They wanted to drag Paul and Silas out to the mob, and so they went straight to Jason's home.
translation_verse: Pɔ kabhã wɔawlɔakɛ afala eku Yudanɛ teli mɛ, kliso bagbana banɔ kpa teli nɛ kedziɛ mɛ pɛ babhɛtɛ kɛza tili gɛ ɔmɔpɔɛ ɔzã balɛ mɛ, pɛ badɛkpɛ kititì lɔlɔ esi nɛ ɔmamɛ. Bebu tɛtsɛɖɛmɛ avɛnɔ Yason bɔpã mɛ tɛ̀ badɛpɛɛ Paulo nɛ̀ Sila tɛ bagbana alɛ amana banɔɛza ɔkɔ.

english_verse: But when they did not find them there, they dragged out Jason and some of the Lord's followers. They took them to the city authorities and shouted, “Paul and Silas have been upsetting things everywhere. Now they have come here,
translation_verse: Pɔ gɛ bagɛ damɔ alɛɛ, bagbana Yason nɛ̀ ɔhamɛanɛ teli amana balɛ banɔ gbagbla namɛ nɛ̀ ozĩ kpɛkpɛ asɛ, “Banyɛ alɛ gɛ bamana buluhũ nɛ kanɔ nɔ̀ petee mɛ tsyɛ atɛba ɛkɛlɛ,

english_verse: and Jason has welcomed them into his home. All of them break the laws of the Roman Emperor by claiming that someone named Jesus is king.”
translation_verse: kɛlɛ Yason ayɛkɛ balɛ akpɛ yɛɔpã mɛ. balɛ petee etso Kaizaro Klauda yɛɔshaa, pɛ baaɖɔ asɛ ekusi bhlatɔ alɛ gɛ bekulu yɛasɛ Yesu yɛenu ekusiɛ.”

english_verse: The officials and the people were upset when they heard this.
translation_verse: Gɛ belũ kibui ɛlɛ, ebela ebeku banɔɛza nɛ̀ ɔma banɔ gbagbla nɛishi.

english_verse: So they made Jason and the other followers pay bail before letting them go. People in Berea Welcome the Message
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ bakɔ Yason nɛ̀ banɔ gɔgɔɛ abhɛ kɛlɛ bashɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ balɛishu. Paulo Avɛ Beroea

english_verse: That same night the Lord's followers sent Paul and Silas on to Berea, and after they arrived, they went to the synagogue.
translation_verse: Gɛ keliɛ ebele koɔ, badzya akpɛ Paulo nɛ̀ Sila nɛ Beroea. Gɛ bediku ɛkɛlɛ, bavɛ Yudanɛ ɔtsyɔakɔ.

english_verse: The people in Berea were much nicer than those in Thessalonica, and they gladly accepted the message. Day after day they studied the Scriptures to see if these things were true.
translation_verse: Otsi nɔ Beroeanɛ bezhinì balɛ ishu esi ɔdɛ Tesalonikanɛ, ɔmɔsɛ bayɛkɛ kibuiɛ nɛ̀ samè kɛlɛ bazapɔɛ kɛlɛ mɛ kiwui nyiɛ wui tɛ̀ beɖi asɛ kibuiɛ gɛ Paulo aɖɔ kpasɛ kɛlɛ mɛ ana dza.

english_verse: Many of them put their faith in the Lord, including some important Greek women and several men.
translation_verse: Yudanɛ kpekpèɛ ayɛkɛ aŋã, kana ɛzãkɔ elete gɛenu Helanɛ badzɛ nɛ̀ ɛzakɔ elete banyɛ tsyɛ ayɛkɛ aŋã.

english_verse: When the Jewish leaders in Thessalonica heard that Paul had been preaching God's message in Berea, they went there and caused trouble by turning the crowds against Paul.
translation_verse: Kibeɛ gɛ Yudanɛ gɛ alɛ Tesalonika elũ asɛ Paulo aɖɔ Owusoso Akaa nɛibuiɛ nɛ Beroea nɔ, bavɛ ɛkɛlɛ tsyɛ, kɛlɛ badaakpɛ ebelà nɛ balɛishi.

english_verse: At once the followers sent Paul down to the coast, but Silas and Timothy stayed in Berea.
translation_verse: Odunu ɔlɛ badzya akpɛ Paulo asɛsɛ aƒuɔ shu, pɔ Sila nɛ̀ Timoteo xɔnɔ azã Beroea.

english_verse: Some men went with Paul as far as Athens, and then returned with instructions for Silas and Timothy to join him as soon as possible. Paul in Athens
translation_verse: Banyɛ gɛ bagbana Paulo evunɔɔ agbana yɛ avɛnɔ Atene kɛlɛ Paulo akɔ banyɛ ɔklasɔ̃ asɛ tɛ̀ betseɖe Sila nɛ̀ Timoteo asɛ tɛ badɛ taplɛnii odunu ɔlɛ. Paulo Avɛ Atene

english_verse: While Paul was waiting in Athens, he was upset to see all the idols in the city.
translation_verse: Kibeɛ gɛ Paulo ezepo Sila nɛ̀ Timoteo nɛ Ateneɛ, kɛbà tɔɖɔ yɛabha gɛ amɔ asɛ ibudi yuku ɔmamɛ.

english_verse: He went to the synagogue to speak to the Jews and to anyone who worshiped with them. Day after day he also spoke to everyone he met in the market.
translation_verse: Kliso ababhɛtɛ ekpeɖekpeɖe mɛ nɛ̀ Yudanɛ nɛ ɔtsyɔakɔ. Ɔplɛnɔ Helanɛ gɛesi Owusoso Akaa nifùɔ nɛ̀ bagɛ azã budziɛ mɛ kiwui nyiɛ wui ɔlɔkɔ nɛ̀ bagɛ alɛ ɛkɛlɛ.

english_verse: Some of them were Epicureans and some were Stoics, and they started arguing with him. People were asking, “What is this know-it-all trying to say?” Some even said, “Paul must be preaching about foreign gods! That's what he means when he talks about Jesus and about people rising from death.”
translation_verse: Epikoroanɛ teli gɛ beshogò ishu ɔplɛnɔ Stoanɛ teli gɛenu bebutìnɛ abakpà kibui wlɔãkɛ nɛ̀ Paulo. Balɛ mɛ beteli ezebisi yɛasɛ, “Kinuvɔ ibui ɖuwoe idi yɛaɖɔ ana?” Bagɔgɔɔ tsyɛ aɖɔ asɛ, “Abhɛtɛ asɛ aɖɔaɖɔ tɛ alɔkɔ ebu amadi bhlatɔ ibudi ulũ nɔ.” Baɖɔ kibui ɛlɛ ɔmɔsɛ Paulo azaɖɔ kibui gbangbã nɛ Yesu shu, nɛ̀ tɛlɔlɔ odenɔ bekpuɔ mɛ shu.

english_verse: They brought Paul before a council called the Areopagus, and said, “Tell us what your new teaching is all about.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ bagbana yɛ amana Areopago besheshèɛ kɛtakpu ɔtɔ̃kɔ toli, ɛkɛlɛ betseɖe yɛasɛ, “Ɔtɔɔkɔ buti kibui tili odenɔ bɔdɔ̃ kakasɛ vɔvɔlɛ gɛ bɔlɛishu wɔɖɔ kaɖɔ dzã?

english_verse: We have heard you say some strange things, and we want to know what you mean.”
translation_verse: Ɔmana ebui teli gɛ bɔkà lũɖi ɔbɔkpɛ bɔlɔ atɔ̃mɛ, kɛlɛ bɔpɛ ɔsɛ tɛ̀ bulũ alɛ esi.”

english_verse: So Paul stood up in front of the council and said: People of Athens, I see that you are very religious.
translation_verse: Paulo aba lɔɛtití nɛ beshesheɛ kɛtakpu ɔtɔ̃kɔ pɛ aɖɔ asɛ, “Atenanɛ banyɛ, ɛmɔsɛ kɛtakpu esi kpɛkpɛ kpasɛ wɔnɔ mɛ̀ nɛ kawɔ̃pɛ ka nyia ka.

english_verse: As I was going through your city and looking at the things you worship, I found an altar with the words, “To an Unknown God.” You worship this God, but you don't really know him. So I want to tell you about him.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ kibeɛ gɛ ɛgã fala pɛ iɖi bɔdɔ̃ɔ gɛ nɔkpɛ kɛtakpu esi ɔkɔɔ, ɛmɔ bɔdɔ̃ ɔpɔɛkɔ toli gɛ bafɔnɔ atɔɖɔ asɛ, akaa gɛ butinyiɛ ɔdɔ̃ ɔpɔɛkɔ. Otsiɛ kɛdɔ̃ gɛ nɔkpɛ kɛtakpu esi ɔkɔ pɛ nɔotinyi kɛlɛ, ɛba ɖɔaɖɔ nɛ kɛlɛ shu ɔkɔ wɔnɔ.

english_verse: This God made the world and everything in it. He is Lord of heaven and earth, and he doesn't live in temples built by human hands.
translation_verse: “Owusoso Akaa gɛ abhɛtɛ kanɔ nɔ̀ nɛ̀ kɛka nyia ka nɛ kalɛ mɛ yɛenu Apalete akɔ kititiɛ nɛ̀ kesiakpa kɛlɛ atɛzã okulukɔ gɛ babhɔ nɛ̀ anɔdzyrame ahɔɛ.

english_verse: He doesn't need help from anyone. He gives life, breath, and everything else to all people.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ gɛnɛ anɔdzyrame ahɔɛ balɔkɔ akpɛ kɛtakpu esi akɔɛ nɔ, asɛ gɛ onupɛ apɛ kɛdɔ̃ tili ana, ɔmɔsɛ yɛakɔ banɔdzyrameɛ petee ɛkpã, ɔfɔfuĩa nɛ̀ kɛka nyia ka.

english_verse: From one person God made all nations who live on earth, and he decided when and where every nation would be.
translation_verse: Gana anɔ tenikpo mɛ yɛabhɛtɛ banunɔ petee nɛ̀ amadiɛ, sɛ tɛ̀ beyuku kanɔ nɔ̀ mɛ petee. Kɛlɛ yɛatɔɖɔ kibeɛ kɔ alɛ nɛ ɔkɔ nyia ɔkɔ gɛ babazã shu.

english_verse: God has done all this, so that we will look for him and reach out and find him. He isn't far from any of us,
translation_verse: Owusoso Akaa abhɛtɛ kibui ɛlɛ asɛ tɛ banɔ apɛ yɛidzo tɛ̀ bamɔɛ, pɔ gɛnɛ gɛ alɛ odzlolo ɔkɔ nɛ bɔlɔ ɔmayɛmɛ nanà ɔbha flogo.

english_verse: and he gives us the power to live, to move, and to be who we are. “We are his children,” just as some of your poets have said.
translation_verse: Asɛ kana gɛ bagɛ bafatɛ ɛdzɛ nɛ wɔnɔ ɛshiɛ aɖɔ asɛ, ‘Ɔmɔsɛ yɛmɛ bɔlɛ nɛ̀ ɛkpã pɛ bɔfalà pɛ bɔlɛ ishu ahɔɛ mɛ.’ ‘Yɛmɛ bude koeyì.’

english_verse: Since we are God's children, we must not think that he is like an idol made out of gold or silver or stone. He isn't like anything that humans have thought up and made.
translation_verse: “Klisoɔ gɛ Owusoso Akaa mɛ bude koeyiɛ, kɛma ɔsɛ tɛ buziɖi ɔsɛ ɔfɔfuĩa mɛ anɔ nɔ̀ edzi asɛ sikãɛkpã, sikãfufu alo kɛklagbu ana, bɔdɔ̃ tata gɛ anɔdzyrame alɔkɔ yɛahɔɛ abhɛtɛ nɔ.

english_verse: In the past, God forgave all this because people did not know what they were doing. But now he says that everyone everywhere must turn to him.
translation_verse: Kuku ibeɛ, Owusoso Akaa ezedzini awɔ̃ atɔɖɔ ebui alɛ gɛ noɔtinyiɛ nabha, pɔ otsiɛ xɔnɔɔ akpɛ ɔsha akɔ aka nyia aka asɛ betsyina nɛ balɛ etúɔ mɛ.

english_verse: He has set a day when he will judge the world's people with fairness. And he has chosen the man Jesus to do the judging for him. God has given proof of this to all of us by raising Jesus from death.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ atɔɖɔ kiwuiɛ gɛ ebekulu kebhu nɛ̀ kanɔ nɔ̀ mɛ petee nɛ̀ kebhuɔ gɛ kalɛ alɛ ɔzãkɔ ɔgana anunɔ gɛ yɛabha yɛatɔɖɔ ahɔɛ. Ebu kibui ɛlɛ akasɛ banunɔ petee ɔgana yɛ tifefeke odenɔ bekpuɔ mɛ shu.”

english_verse: As soon as the people heard Paul say a man had been raised from death, some of them started laughing. Others said, “We will hear you talk about this some other time.”
translation_verse: Gɛ belũ kibuiɛ odenɔ bekpuɔ tɛlɔlɔ shuɔ, beteli amɔ̃ɛ yɛ, pɔ bagɔgɔ xɔnɔ aɖɔ asɛ, “Bɔpɛ ɔsɛ buwlui lũ wɔ kaɖɔ ɖɔɖɔ nɛ kibui ɛlɛ shu kidegba.”

english_verse: When Paul left the council meeting,
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ Paulo abashɛ nɛ kazãa ɔbha.

english_verse: some of the men put their faith in the Lord and went with Paul. One of them was a council member named Dionysius. A woman named Damaris and several others also put their faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Banɔ bhlui teli ebedzi Paulo yiedeazazã kɛlɛ bayɛkɛ aŋã. Balɛ mɛ banɔ nɔ teli balɛenu, Dionisio gɛenu Areopago yɛ kɛtakpu ɔtɔ̃kɔ anɔ, nɛ̀ adzɛ teli tsyɛ gɛ bezekulu yɛasɛ Damaris nɛ̀ banɔ kasasà tieli.

english_verse: Paul left Athens and went to Corinth,
translation_verse: Nɛ kibui ɛlɛ niedeɛ, Paulo ashɛ nɛ Atene avɛ Korinto.

english_verse: where he met Aquila, a Jewish man from Pontus. Not long before this, Aquila had come from Italy with his wife Priscilla, because Emperor Claudius had ordered the Jewish people to leave Rome. Paul went to see Aquila and Priscilla
translation_verse: Ɛkɛlɛ yɛadɛ tɔnɔ Yudaeyi teli gɛ bezekulu yɛasɛ Akwila, anɔ gɛede Ponto. Yɛnɛ yɛedzini Priskila ede Italia aba ɛkɛlɛ kiwui kadze, ɔmɔsɛ ekusi Kaisaro Klauda akɔ ɔsha asɛ Yudanɛ petee ashɛ nɛ Roma. Paulo avɛ asɛ ediɖi alɛ,

english_verse: and found out they were tent makers. Paul was also a tent maker, so he stayed with them, and they worked together.
translation_verse: kɛlɛ gɛ enu anɔ gɛ enyi kagbabhɛtɛ asɛ balɛ soɔ, azã balɛ ɔbha pɛ awɔa axɔ̃ɛ nɛ balɛ.

english_verse: Every Sabbath, Paul went to the synagogue. He spoke to Jews and Gentiles and tried to win them over.
translation_verse: Azabhɛtɛ ekpeɖekpeɖe mɛ aplɛnɔ balɛ nɛ Kimoelewui ka nyia ka abha, pɛ apɛ asɛ tɛ̀ ebu ebui mɛ kɔ Yudanɛ nɛ̀ Helanɛ.

english_verse: But after Silas and Timothy came from Macedonia, he spent all his time preaching to the Jews about Jesus the Messiah.
translation_verse: Kibeɛ gɛ Sila nɛ̀ Timoteo ede Makedonia aba, Paulo alɔkɔ yɛishu akɔ kibuiɛ tɛɖɔɖɔ, pɛ adalɛ kɛlɛ mɛ ɔkɔ Yudanɛ ete ɔsɛ Yesu yɛenu Kristoɔ.

english_verse: Finally, they turned against him and insulted him. So he shook the dust from his clothes and told them, “Whatever happens to you will be your own fault! I am not to blame. From now on I am going to preach to the Gentiles.”
translation_verse: Pɔ gɛ Yudanɛ alɔɛtití nɛ Paulo shu nɛ̀ akwalatu ebui soɔ, eshuku yɛakpɛkpɛ akasɛ balɛ asɛ, “Wɔnɔ dzosu nɔ aba wɔnɔ atakpu nabha! Ɛbhɛtɛ mɛ ɔbhɛtɛ ɔkɔ. Odenɔ otsiɛ ɔvɛnɔɔ, ɛbavɛ bagɛ betinu Yudanɛ ɔkɔ.”

english_verse: Paul then moved into the house of a man named Titius Justus, who worshiped God and lived next door to the synagogue.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ Paulo ashɛ nɛ ɔtsyɔakɔ avɛ bɔpã gɛlɛ Tito Yusto, anɔ gɛ akpɛ kɛtakpu esi ɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa yɔɛpa nɔbha mɛ.

english_verse: Crispus was the leader of the synagogue. He and everyone in his family put their faith in the Lord. Many others in Corinth also heard the message, and all the people who had faith in the Lord were baptized.
translation_verse: Krispo, anɔ gɛ eɖi ɔtsyɔakɔ nabha, nɛ̀ yɛɔpã petee yɛkɛ Apalete nabha aŋã, kɛlɛ Korintoanɛ kpekpè teli gɛ belũ yɛɔdɔ ayɛkɛ aŋã kɛlɛ bakpɛ bulĩ akɔ alɛ.

english_verse: One night, Paul had a vision, and in it the Lord said, “Don't be afraid to keep on preaching. Don't stop!
translation_verse: Elishi tenikpo Apaleteɛ aɖɔaɖɔ etseɖe Paulo nɛ kɛdɔ̃ mɔmɔ mɛ ɔsɛ, “Tisì kifù, zã kɛlɛ abha tɛ̀ ɔzɔɖɔ kaɖɔɔ, tɛ̀ dã olugbũ.

english_verse: I am with you, and you won't be harmed. Many people in this city belong to me.”
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ ɛlɛ ɔplɛnɔ wɔ, kɛlɛ anɔ nanàa etibè zhieku wɔabha pɛ abakpɛ kɛpɔ̃ nɛ wɔshu, ɔmɔsɛ banɔ kpekpè alɛ mahɔɛ nɛ ɔmã hohoe ɔlɛ mɛ.”

english_verse: Paul stayed on in Corinth for a year and a half, teaching God's message to the people.
translation_verse: Kliso Paulo azã ɛkɛlɛ kɛgɔ zɛzɛ nɛ̀ kapɛ, pɛ azakasɛ alɛ Owusoso Akaa nɛibuiɛ.

english_verse: While Gallio was governor of Achaia, some of the Jewish leaders got together and grabbed Paul. They brought him into court
translation_verse: Kibeɛ gɛ Galio azaŋã bukusi nɛ Roma kapɛ tieli abha, Yudanɛ abhɛtɛ tenikpo kɛlɛ bezhi eku Paulo abha pɛ bagbana yɛ amana kebhu okulukɔ.

english_verse: and said, “This man is trying to make our people worship God in a way that is against our Law!”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ bebhui yɛulũ nɛ̀ kibui ɛlɛ ɔsɛ, “Anyɛ alɛ ebù ebuimɛ ɔkɔ banunɔ asɛ tɛ bakpɛ̀ kɛtakpu esi ɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa nɛ edzo teli abha gɛ alɔɛtití nɛ ɔshaa shu.”

english_verse: Even before Paul could speak, Gallio said, “If you were charging this man with a crime or some other wrong, I would have to listen to you.
translation_verse: Kibeɛ gɛ Paulo apɛ asɛ tɛ̀ aɖɔaɖɔ koɔ, Galio etseɖe Yudanɛ asɛ, “Pɛ otɛɔsɛ wɔnɔ Yudanɛ nɔkpamɛ ishu nɛ banɔ yiyí alo kibui kpa tili shuɔ, tsyɛ alo ekpeɖekpeɖe mɛ atazã ɛlɛ mɛ akɔmɛ ɔsɛ tɛ ɛtaatɔ̃ nɛ̀ wɔnɔ.

english_verse: But since this concerns only words, names, and your own law, you will have to take care of it yourselves. I refuse to judge such matters.”
translation_verse: Pɔ gɛ kinu kibui gɛ kɛkpà ɔplɛnɔ ebui, enyè nɛ̀ wɔnɔ ɔsha shu soɔ, nowoese wɔnɔ ahɔɛ ibui, itɛbadzi anɔ gɛ ɛbakulu kebhu nɛ ebui alɛ nɛidi shu.”

english_verse: Then he sent them out of the court.
translation_verse: Kliso akɔ bebu balɛ eyinɔ nɛ kebhuɔ okulukɔ.

english_verse: The crowd grabbed Sosthenes, the Jewish leader, and beat him up in front of the court. But none of this mattered to Gallio. Paul Returns to Antioch in Syria
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ balɛ petee etsyina atɔ̃ Sostene gɛenu ɔtsyɔakɔ abha eɖiɖieleteɛ kɛlɛ behuisi nɛ kebhu okulukɔ buvũɔ otugbà. Pɔ Galio xɔnɔ agɛ lɔkɔ kɛnanà nɛ ɛlɛ mɛ. Paulo Etsyina Avɛ Antioxia

english_verse: After Paul had stayed for a while with the Lord's followers in Corinth, he told them goodbye and sailed on to Syria with Aquila and Priscilla. But before he left, he had his head shaved at Cenchreae because he had made a promise to God.
translation_verse: Paulo azã Korinto ɔdɛ nanà kibè tili. Kɛlɛ ashɛ nɛ badzya ɔbha pɛ eku kelĩabhaohu avɛ Siria gɛ agbana Priskila nɛ̀ Akwila aplɛnɔ yɛishu. Flogo tɛ̀ ebeku kelĩabhaohuɔ, akɔ batsɛ kɛtakpuɔ akɔɛ nɛ Kenxrea nɛ bɔdɔ̃ tuli gɛ atanà shu so.

english_verse: The three of them arrived in Ephesus, where Paul left Priscilla and Aquila. He then went into the synagogue to talk with the people there.
translation_verse: Bediku Efeso, ɛkɛlɛ Paulo ashɛ ayɔlɔ Priskila nɛ̀ Akwila. Yɛ xɔnɔ avɛ ɔtsyɔakɔ pɛ adɛ bhɛtɛ ekpeɖekpeɖe mɛ aplɛnɔ Yudanɛ.

english_verse: They asked him to stay longer, but he refused.
translation_verse: Kibeɛ gɛ betseɖe yɛ ɔsɛ tɛ azã balɛ ɔbha kɛbɔɛ nɔ, agɛ dɛmɛ tɔɖɔ kɛlɛ abha.

english_verse: He told them goodbye and said, “If God lets me, I will come back.”
translation_verse: Pɔ gɛ ashɛɛ, atɔɖɔ elú asɛ, “Ɛba tsodzi ba gɛ Onupɛ kinu Owusoso Akaa tɛpɛpɛɛ.” Kɛlɛ eku kelĩabhaohuɔ odenɔ Efeso.

english_verse: Paul sailed to Caesarea, where he greeted the church. Then he went on to Antioch.
translation_verse: Kibeɛ gɛ ediku Kaisarea nɔ, ewu avɛ adɛ sɛnɔ ɔhaa, kɛlɛ ezhi avɛ Antioxia.

english_verse: After staying there for a while, he left and visited several places in Galatia and Phrygia. He helped the followers there to become stronger in their faith. Apollos in Ephesus
translation_verse: Gɛ azã Antioxia odiyinɔ kibè tiliɛ, Paulo azagã kidzo edenɔ ɛkɛlɛ pɛ azakanà nɛ Galatia nɛ̀ Frigia bɔdɔ̃ petee mɛ pɛ akpɛ badɔkpasɛanɛ petee kemi.

english_verse: A Jewish man named Apollos came to Ephesus. Apollos had been born in the city of Alexandria. He was a very good speaker and knew a lot about the Scriptures.
translation_verse: Nɛ kibe ɛlɛ Yudaeyi teli gɛ bezekulu asɛ Apolo, anɔ gɛede Aleksandro tsyɛ aba Efeso. Edzi anɔ gɛ akpasɛ bɔdɔ̃ avɛnɔ otugbà pɛ enyi Owusoso Akaa ebuiɛ piapii.

english_verse: He also knew much about the Lord's Way, and he spoke about it with great excitement. What he taught about Jesus was right, but all he knew was John's message about baptism.
translation_verse: Bakasɛ yɛɔdɔ nɛ Apaleteɛ edzo nabha, kɛlɛ azaɖɔaɖɔ nɛ bupù mɛ, kɛlɛ akasɛ bɔdɔ̃ nɛ Kristo shu pɛ bɔɔnà, pɔ Yohanes bulĩkpɛkpɛ ko batɛ kpɛ kɔɛ flogo.

english_verse: Apollos started speaking bravely in the synagogue. But when Priscilla and Aquila heard him, they took him to their home and helped him understand God's Way even better.
translation_verse: Abakpà kaɖɔ ɔɖɔ nɛ ɛtsyɔakɔ nɛ kifù masi masi mɛ. Kibeɛ gɛ Priskila nɛ̀ Akwila elũ yɛebuiɛ, bekulù yɛ amana balɛ bɔpã mɛ kɛlɛ bebu ebuiɛ mɛ kɔɛ piapii nɛ Owusoso Akaa yɛedzoɔ shu.

english_verse: Apollos decided to travel through Achaia. So the Lord's followers wrote letters, encouraging the followers there to welcome him. After Apollos arrived in Achaia, he was a great help to everyone who had put their faith in the Lord Jesus because of God's gift of undeserved grace.
translation_verse: Kibeɛ gɛ Apolo apɛ asɛ tɛ̀ avɛ Axaya, badzya akpɛ yiemi kɛlɛ bafɔnɔ keplukpa asɛsɛ badɔkpasɛnɛ nɛ ɛkɛlɛ asɛ tɛ̀ bayɛkii. Gɛ avɛ edikuɔ, edidzì apɛplɛnɔ akɔ bagɛ bebhui balɛ ebú pɛ bayɛkɛ aŋãa.

english_verse: He got into fierce arguments with the Jewish people, and in public he used the Scriptures to prove that Jesus is the Messiah.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ awzalɔ̃akɛ ebui nɛ̀ Yudanɛ nɛ ɔmaɛshi ebuimɛ pɔpɔɛ shu, pɛ azadalɛ ebuiɛ mɛ edenɔ Owusoso Akaa ebui fɔfɔnɔ mɛ ɔsɛ Yesu yɛenu Kristoɔ.

english_verse: While Apollos was in Corinth, Paul traveled across the hill country to Ephesus, where he met some of the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Kibeɛ gɛ Apolo azã Koritoɔ, Paulo agã kishitɛbha kidzoɔ avɛ ediyì nɛ Efeso. Ɛkɛlɛ yɛadɛ tɔnɔ badɔkpasɛanɛ teli

english_verse: He asked them, “When you put your faith in Jesus, were you given the Holy Spirit?” “No!” they answered. “We have never even heard of the Holy Spirit.”
translation_verse: pɛ ebisi alɛ asɛ, “Nɔyɛkɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã kibeɛ gɛ nɔyɛkɛ ɔŋã dza?” Bebuiliulũ asɛ, “Oo, bɔkalũ oɖi ɔsɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã ɔlɛ tsyɛ.”

english_verse: “Then why were you baptized?” Paul asked. They answered, “Because of what John taught.”
translation_verse: Kliso Paulo ebisi asɛ, “Kɛlɛ́ɛ bulĩ kpɛkpɛ ɖuwoe edi bakpɛ akɔ wɔnɔ? Bebuiliulũ asɛ, “Bɔyɛkɛ Yohanes yɛulĩkpɛkpɛ.”

english_verse: Paul replied, “John baptized people so they would turn to God. But he also told them someone else was coming, and they should put their faith in him. Jesus is the one that John was talking about.”
translation_verse: Paulo aɖɔ asɛ, “Yohanes bulĩkpɛkpɛ lɛkɔ etú mɛ titsyitsyina. Etseɖe banunɔ asɛ tɛ̀ bayɛkɛ anɔ nɔ̀ gɛ yɛabà nɛ yiede nabha ŋã, gɛenu Yesuɔ.

english_verse: After the people heard Paul say this, they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: Gɛ belũ kibui ɛlɛ, bakpɛ bulĩ akɔ alɛ nɛ Apalete Yesu yɛinyeɛ mɛ.

english_verse: Then Paul placed his hands on them. The Holy Spirit was given to them, and they spoke unknown languages and prophesied.
translation_verse: Gɛ Paulo atɔɖɔ yɛhɔɛ nɛ balɛ abha koɔ, Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã ɔba balɛ abha kɛlɛ baɖɔaɖɔ nɛ beluhũ vɔvɔ mɛ pɛ baɖɔ ebui elú.

english_verse: There were about twelve men in this group.
translation_verse: Bɔnɔ alɛ tɛkaklà mɛ bazã asɛ baanyɛ lɔfɔ nɛ̀ taabha ana.

english_verse: For three months Paul went to the synagogue and talked bravely with the people about God's kingdom. He tried to win them over,
translation_verse: Paulo etsoku ɔtsyɔakɔ pɛ aɖɔaɖɔ nɛ bupù mɛ nɛ ɛkɛlɛ bewoele tata mɛ pɛ ezebu ebui mɛ ɔkɔ alɛ nɛ Owusoso Akaa bukusiŋaŋã shu.

english_verse: but some of them were stubborn and refused to believe. In front of everyone they said terrible things about God's Way. Paul left and took the followers with him to the lecture hall of Tyrannus. He spoke there every day
translation_verse: Pɔ balɛ banɔ nɔ teli atɔmɛ agagalĩ, bagɛ pɛ asɛ ayɛkɛ aŋã kɛlɛ baɖɔaɖɔ kpa nɛ Yɛedzoɔ shu. Kliso Paulo ashɛ ayɔlɔ alɛ pɛ agbana badɔkpasɛanɛ aplɛnɔ yɛishu pɛ yɛnɛ alɛ bazapɔɛ ebui mɛ nɛ̀ bagɔlɔ mɛ kibe nyia be nɛ Tirano bɔdɔ̃kakasɛ buvũɔ mɛ.

english_verse: for two years, until every Jew and Gentile in Asia had heard the Lord's message. The Sons of Sceva
translation_verse: Kibui ɛlɛ vɛ ɛlɛ abha nɛ agɔ taabha mɛ, ɔsɛ tɛ Yudanɛ nɛ̀ Helanɛ petee gɛazã Asia bɔdɔ̃ mɛ elũ Apaleteɛ nɛibuiɛ.

english_verse: God gave Paul the power to work great miracles.
translation_verse: Owusoso Akaa abhɛtɛ atsyindzye ebui teli idi aganà Paulo abha,

english_verse: People even took handkerchiefs and aprons that had touched Paul's body, and they carried them to everyone who was sick. All of the sick people were healed, and the evil spirits went out.
translation_verse: gɛ kɛkɔ ɔsɛ apɔtɛ gulũ nɛ̀ agɛ bakpɛ̀ atɔɖɔ kitèmɛ ayɛ nabha gɛehunu yɛtsyɛ, balɔkɔ alɛ avɛnɔ banyanyã, kɛlɛ banyã ebelè pɛ ɛfɔfuĩa kpa tsyɛ koeyì nɛ balɛ mɛ.

english_verse: Some Jewish men started going around trying to force out evil spirits by using the name of the Lord Jesus. They said to the spirits, “Come out in the name of that same Jesus that Paul preaches about!”
translation_verse: Yudanɛ teli gɛ bazanyɔ akanà pɛ baakakà ɛfɔfuĩa kpa eyinɔ, bazapɛ asɛ tɛ̀ bekulu Apalete Yesu yɛinyèɛ atɔɖɔ bagɛ ɛfɔfuĩa zizĩ zã balɛ abhaa. Baaɖɔ asɛ, “Nɛ Yesu gɛ yɛinyè mɛ Paulo azaɖɔaɖɔ asɛ, ɛkpɛ ɔsha kɔwɔ ɔsɛ tɛ̀ okoeyì.”

english_verse: Seven sons of a high priest named Sceva were doing this,
translation_verse: Skeva gɛenu Yudanɛ banɔlũazazã gbagbla yɛeyìanyɛ tegegene teli balaza abhɛtɛ ebui alɛ.

english_verse: when an evil spirit said to them, “I know Jesus! And I have heard about Paul. But who are you?”
translation_verse: Kiwui tinikpo kɛlɛ ɔfɔfuĩa kpa lubuiliulũ ɔkɔ alɛ asɛ, “Inyi Yesu, kɛlɛ inyi Paulo tsyɛ, fakɔ anɛ wɔnɔ tsyɛ nonu?”

english_verse: Then the man with the evil spirit jumped on them and beat them up. They ran out of the house, naked and bruised.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ anyɛ gɛ ɔfɔfuĩa kpa ɔzã yɛmɛ alɔatɛɛ eku balɛ abha, pɛ aŋã balɛ petee abha. Ehuisi balɛ ɖɔtɔ ɖɔtɔ gɛ kɛkɔ ɔsɛ bebú tɛtsɛɖɛmɛ ekoeyì nɛ bɔpã mɛ kɛdɔ̃ makpɛ makpɛ pɛ dzyosu tsyɛ ezeyì nɛ balɛ shu.

english_verse: When the Jews and Gentiles in Ephesus heard about this, they were so frightened that they praised the name of the Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: Gɛ Yudanɛ nɛ̀ Helanɛ gɛ alɛ Efeso ebelũ kibui ɛlɛ, kifù yialɛ petee, kɛlɛ balɔkɔ kɛtakpu esi kpɛkpɛ hohoe akɔ Apalete Yesu yɛinyeɛ.

english_verse: Many who were followers now started telling everyone about the evil things they had been doing.
translation_verse: Otsi alɛ banɔ kpekpèɛ gɛ ayɛkɛ aŋã bege kpũ balɛishu kɛlɛ babadalɛ balɛ ishumɛ nɛ balɛ ebui kpa gɛ bahɛtɛ shu.

english_verse: Some who had been practicing witchcraft even brought their books and burned them in public. These books were worth about
translation_verse: Banɔ kasasà tieli gɛedzi behuinɛ tsyɛ alɔkɔ balɛ ihuiɛ amana pɛ bapɔɛ ɛlɛ nɛ ɔmaɛshi. Gɛ bakalà ehoeyiɛ gɛ bezĩ nɛ balɛ akpɛnɛ ebui bhɛbhɛtɛ shuɔ, labanà sikãfufu ihotu afɛeti, gɛenu ehoeyi gɛ lavɛ otugbà nɛ kibè ɛlɛ.

english_verse: So the Lord's message spread and became even more powerful. The Riot in Ephesus
translation_verse: Nɛ kidzo ɛlɛ nabha Apaleteɛ nɛibuiɛ yɛɛvɛ̀ pɛ kɛvɛ̀ kabha nɛ̀ ohumi.

english_verse: After all this had happened, Paul decided to visit Macedonia and Achaia on his way to Jerusalem. Paul had said, “From there I will go on to Rome.”
translation_verse: Gɛ alɛ nyɔ̃ɔ aba ɛlɛ mɛ ebekeɛ, Paulo abatɔɖɔ ɛlɛ ɔsɛ tɛ adɛgã Makedonia nɛ̀ Axaya tɛ avɛ Yerusalem. Aɖɔ asɛ, “Onupɛ ɛdɛ Yerusalem aba koɔ, ɛbadiɖi Roma tsyɛ.

english_verse: So he sent his two helpers, Timothy and Erastus, to Macedonia. But he stayed on in Asia for a while.
translation_verse: Asɛsɛ yɛaxɔ̃ɛdɛ taabha gɛenu Timoteo nɛ̀ Erasto nɛ Makedonia, gɛ yɛxɔnɔ azã Asia bɔdɔ̃ mɛ kɛbɔɛ. Kititì Lɔlɔ Ebuiɛ Gaba Efesoɔ

english_verse: At that time there was serious trouble because of the Lord's Way.
translation_verse: Nɛ kibè ɛlɛ kititì lɔlɔ tili babà nɛ Kidzoɔ shu.

english_verse: A silversmith named Demetrius had a business that made silver models of the temple of the goddess Artemis. Those who worked for him earned a lot of money.
translation_verse: Anyɛ teli gɛedzi sikãfufu axɔ̃ɛwɔa gɛ bezekulu yɛasɛ Demetrio, azabhɛtɛ sikãfufu ibudiuvũɔ ɔlɛ tɛzazã mɛ gɛ bekulu asɛ Artemis, pɛ amana axɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa akɔ banɔ gelee.

english_verse: Demetrius brought together everyone who was in the same business and said: Friends, you know we make a good living at this.
translation_verse: Ekulu balɛ eshogò aplɛnɔ baxɔ̃ɛwɔa bhlatɔ gɛ bawɔa axɔ̃ɛ alɛ nɛidi pɛ etseɖe balɛ asɛ, “Mɛadzya alɛ mɔ, nɔonyi ɔsɛ bɔɔmɔ ehoeyi piapii odenɔ kɛxɔ̃ɛ ɛlɛ wɔawɔa mɛ.

english_verse: But you have surely seen and heard how this man Paul is upsetting a lot of people, not only in Ephesus, but almost everywhere in Asia. He claims that the gods we humans make are not really gods at all.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ nɔmɔ̀ pɛ nɔolũ kana gɛ Paulo anyɛ alɛ etsyina banunɔ ekpeɖekpeɖeɛ mɛ, pɛ agbana banɔkpekpèɛ atsaɖakɛ idzo nɛ Efosoɔ nɛ̀ Asia bɔdɔ̃ mɛ petee tsyɛ. Aɖɔ asɛ ibudiɛ gɛ banɔdzyrameɛ abhɛtɛ tinu akaa nanà.

english_verse: Everyone will start saying terrible things about our business. They will stop respecting the temple of the goddess Artemis, who is worshiped in Asia and all over the world. Our great goddess will be forgotten!
translation_verse: Kibui ɛlɛ bazĩ bɔdɔ̃, gɛnɛ ɔsɛ bɔlɔ axɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa kinyèɛ ete kɛlɛ bayala, pɔ bazakà kpɛ kɛtakpu esi akɔ obidiuvũɔ Artemis gbagbla nɛ̀ yɛobudiukã tsyɛ, kɛlɛ adzɛobudiɛ tsyɛ gɛ bakpɛ kɛtakpu esi akɔ nɛ Asia bɔdɔ̃ mɛ nɛ̀ kanɔ nɔ̀ mɛ petee tsyɛ, yɛohuminɔ obokoeyi nɛ yɛahɔɛ mɛ.”

english_verse: When the workers heard this, they got angry and started shouting, “Great is Artemis, the goddess of the Ephesians!”
translation_verse: Gɛ belũ kibui ɛlɛ, ɔmɔpɔɛ oyialɛ kɛlɛ bakpà ozĩukpɛ̀ asɛ, “Obudi gbagbla ɔlɛonù Efesoanɛ Artemis.”

english_verse: Soon the whole city was in a riot, and some men grabbed Gaius and Aristarchus, who had come from Macedonia with Paul. Then everyone in the crowd rushed to the place where the town meetings were held.
translation_verse: Odunu ɔlɛ koɔ, ɔmamɛ oyukù nɛ̀ ozĩ. Banunɔ evũ Gayo nɛ̀ Aristaxo gɛ benu banɔ gɛ bagã kidzo ɔplɛnɔ Paulo odenɔ Makedonia, kɛlɛ bagbanà balɛ odunũ ɔlɛ avɛnɔ ɔma ɔlɛ bɔwufù ɔwɔkɔ.

english_verse: Paul wanted to go out and speak to the people, but the Lord's followers would not let him.
translation_verse: Paulo apɛ asɛ tɛ̀ avɛ banɔɛza namɛ pɔ yɛadɔkpasɛanɛ agɛ kɔɛ yɛidzo.

english_verse: A few of the local officials were friendly to Paul, and they sent someone to warn him not to go.
translation_verse: Banɔ hohoeɛ gɛenu Paulo yɛɔbhia alɛ nɛ kɛma ɛlɛ mɛ tsyɛ asɛsɛ yɛ ɔklasɔ̃ asɛ atɛpɛ kɛwɔawɔa asɛ avɛ balɛ buwufù ɔwɔkɔ.

english_verse: Some of the people in the meeting were shouting one thing, and others were shouting something else. Everyone was completely confused, and most of them did not even know why they were there.
translation_verse: Kazã egetì kɛnɔnɔ gɛ babà bhɛtɛɛ, beteli akpɛ ozĩ aɖɔaɖɔ nɛ kibui ɛlɛ̀ shu, beteli tsyɛ nɛ kɛbhlatɔ shu. Banunɔ teli tsyɛ etinyi kɛgɛ kɛlɛ sopɛ bavɛ ɛkɛlɛ.

english_verse: Several of the Jewish leaders pushed a man named Alexander to the front of the crowd and started telling him what to say. He motioned with his hand and tried to explain what was going on.
translation_verse: Yudanɛ etii Aleksandro avɛnɔ otugbà, kɛlɛ banunɔ gɛ ebeshogòɔ teli akpɛ ozĩ nɛ yɛabha pɛ bakasɛ yɛ kɛgɛ kɛlɛ ɔsɛ abhɛtɛɛ. Akasɛ balɛ ahɔɛ asɛ tɛ̀ bataatɔ̃ tɛ̀ amɔ kidzo ebú yɛishu ulũ nɛ banunɔ amɛ.

english_verse: But when the crowd saw that he was Jewish, they all shouted for two hours, “Great is Artemis, the goddess of the Ephesians!”
translation_verse: Pɔ gɛ bamɔ asɛ Yudaeyi yɛenuɔ, balɛ petee akpɛ ozĩ dzlolo kitsù asɛ ɔɖatatá tɛbha ana asɛ, “Obudi gbagbla ɔlɛonu Efesoanɛ Artemis!”

english_verse: Finally, a town official made the crowd be quiet. Then he said: People of Ephesus, who in the world doesn't know that our city is the center for worshiping the great goddess Artemis? Who doesn't know that her image which fell from heaven is right here?
translation_verse: Anɔ nɔ̀ gɛ yɛafɔnɔ bɔdɔ̃ akɔ kɛzaa akɔsɛ bataatɔ̃, pɛ etseɖe balɛ asɛ, “Efesoanɛ mɔ, banunɔ petee nɛ kanɔ nɔ̀ mɛ etinyi asɛ Efosoanɛ balepò Artemis obudiukã nɛ̀ ɔlɛ tɛzazã tɛfafatɛ gɛ wɔlɛ odenɔ kititiɛ.

english_verse: No one can deny this, and so you should calm down and not do anything foolish.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ gɛ ebui alɛ enu ebui gbãa, kɛlɛ ɔsɛ nɔtaatɔ̃, tɛ̀ nɔtɛ bɔbhɛtɛ kibui nanà odunũ ɔlɛ.

english_verse: You have brought men in here who have not robbed temples or spoken against our goddess.
translation_verse: Nɔgbanà banyɛ alɛ ɔmana ɛkɛlɛ gɛ begi dezuru ibudiukã mɛ kɛdɔ̃ nanà alo pɛ baɖɔ ebusoebui nɛ bɔlɔ adzɛobudiɛ shu.

english_verse: If Demetrius and his workers have a case against these men, we have courts and judges. Let them take their complaints there.
translation_verse: Onupɛ kibui tili kpasɛ Demetrio nɛ̀ yɛaxɔ̃ɛwɔa gɛ babhɔ bɔdɔ̃ aplɛnɔ yɛɛ nɛ anɔ teli shuɔ, kebhu okulukɔ ɔlɛ, kɛlɛ bagɛ beeɖi ɔma nabha tsyɛ alɛ, babàlà alɔkɔ kibui nɛ alɛ shu avɛnɔ ɛkɛlɛ.

english_verse: But if you want to do more than that, the matter will have to be brought before the city council.
translation_verse: Onupɛ kibui tili pɔ kalɛ gɛ nɔpɛ ɔsɛ tɛ̀ nofekeɛ, kɛlɛ ɔsɛ bavɛnɔ edibu kɛlɛ mɛ nɛ ɔsha mɛ.

english_verse: We could easily be accused of starting a riot today. There is no excuse for it! We cannot even give a reason for this uproar.
translation_verse: Asɛ kana gɛ kɛlɛ ana, kifu sisi lɛ kɛlɛishu ɔsɛ babà lɔkɔ kibui nɛ oboɔ ozĩ kpɛkpɛ ebui alɛ shu. Nɛ kibui ɛlɛ shu soɔ, bɔtɛ balà bu ishu ulũ nɛ ozĩ kpɛkpɛ ɔlɛ shu, ɔmɔsɛ kesilulũ nanà ama kɛlɛishu.”

english_verse: After saying this, he told the people to leave.
translation_verse: Gɛ aɖɔ kibui ɛlɛ ebekeɛ, akakà ɔzãkɔ.

english_verse: When the riot was over, Paul sent for the followers and encouraged them. He then told them goodbye and left for Macedonia.
translation_verse: Gɛ ozĩ kpɛkpɛ ebuiɛ abafala, Paulo akpɛ banɔ nɛ badɔkpasɛanɛ ɔkɔ, kɛlɛ gɛ akpɛ balɛ kemì ebekeɛ, ebisi kidzo pɛ ashɛ avɛ Makedonia.

english_verse: As he traveled from place to place, he encouraged the followers with many messages. Finally, he went to Greece
translation_verse: Anyɔ afala nɛ kɛma ɛlɛ mɛ, pɛ alɔkɔ kaɖɔ ɖɔɖɔ gelee akpɛ banɔ kemi nɔ, gɔgɔɛ nɔ, ediyì nɛ Hela bɔdɔ̃ mɛ,

english_verse: and stayed there for three months. Paul was about to sail to Syria. But some of the Jewish leaders plotted against him, so he decided to return by way of Macedonia.
translation_verse: ɛkɛlɛ yɛazã bewoele tatá. Ɔmɔsɛ Yudanɛ atɔɖɔ kibui kpa nɛishu kibeɛ gɛ apɛ asɛ tɛ̀ eku kelĩabhaohu avɛ Siria, klisoɔ atɔɖɔ asɛ etsodzi adɛgã Makedonia.

english_verse: With him were Sopater, son of Pyrrhus from Berea, and Aristarchus and Secundus from Thessalonica. Gaius from Derbe was also with him, and so were Timothy and the two Asians, Tychicus and Trophimus.
translation_verse: Soparte, Piro gɛede Beroa yɛeyianyɛ azã yiede, nɛ̀ Aristaxo nɛ̀ Sekundo gɛede Tesalonika, Gayo gɛede Derbe nɛ̀ Timoteo, nɛ̀ Tixiko nɛ̀ Trofimo gɛede Asia bɔdɔ̃ mɛ.

english_verse: They went on ahead to Troas and waited for us there.
translation_verse: Banyɛ alɛ eputɔ edeepo bɔlɔ nɛ Troa.

english_verse: After the Festival of Thin Bread, we sailed from Philippi. Five days later we met them in Troas and stayed there for a week. Paul's Last Visit to Troas
translation_verse: Pɔ buku kelĩabhaohuɔ odenɔ Filipi nɛ kibeɛ gɛ baŋã kifufulũ gɛ Kɛgɛ Nyanyã Iwuiɛ ebeke, kɛlɛ nɛ ewui tetì edeɛ bɔdɛ taplɛnɔ banɔ gɔgɔ nɛ Troa kɛlɛ bɔzã ɛkɛlɛ ewui tegegene. Banyɛmɛ Eutixo Denɔ Butsimɛ Nɛ Troa

english_verse: On the first day of the week we met to break bread together. Paul spoke to the people until midnight because he was leaving the next morning.
translation_verse: Nɛ ɔgɔnɛ ɔlɛ kiwuiɛ gɛiputɔ nabhaa, bɔba shogò ɔkɔ bɔlɔ ɔhakpɛkpɛ ɔdɔ̃ ŋaŋã. Paulo azaɖɔaɖɔ ɔkɔ banunɔ ɔmɔsɛ atɔɖɔ ɛlɛ asɛ abashɛ nɛ kelizaza mɛ, kliso azaɖɔaɖɔ idiyinɔ elishi tetì.

english_verse: In the upstairs room where we were meeting, there were a lot of lamps.
translation_verse: Badɛkɛ efù gelee elu kabha buvũɔ mɛ, nɛ ɛkɛlɛ gɛ bɔzã kɛtakpu tɔtɔ̃ mɛ.

english_verse: A young man by the name of Eutychus was sitting on a window sill. While Paul was speaking, the young man got very sleepy. Finally, he went to sleep and fell three floors all the way down to the ground. When they picked him up, he was dead.
translation_verse: Taɖa zibuku anyɛ ayisɔ̃ teli gɛ bekulu yɛasɛ Eutixoɔ, kibeɛ gɛ Paulo azã yɛaɖɔ ɖɔɖɔ nabha. Kibeɛ gɛ abavɛnɔ taɖa mɛɛ, abawɔlɛ edenɔ kabha buvũɔ gɛ babhɔ atɔɖɔ bɔtaɖamɛ nabha abadɔ kesiakpa kɛlɛ gɛ befeke yɛ etitsí.

english_verse: Paul went down and bent over Eutychus. He took him in his arms and said, “Don't worry! He's alive.”
translation_verse: Paulo ezhi avɛ kesiɛ, alɔkɔ yɛishu atɔɖɔ ayisɔ̃ nabha kɛlɛ alɔkɔ yɛ ɛhɔɛ akpɛ akana yɛishuɔ, pɛ aɖɔ asɛ, “Nɔotosì kifù, alɛ nɛkpã!”

english_verse: After Paul had gone back upstairs, he broke bread, and ate with us. He then spoke until dawn and left.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ ezewu avɛ kabha buvũɔ mɛ, pɛ adɛya kifufulũ nɔ atã. Gɛ aɖɔaɖɔ pɛ keliɛ azaa, abashɛ.

english_verse: Then the followers took the young man home alive and were very happy. The Voyage from Troas to Miletus
translation_verse: Banunɔ agbana anyɛ ayisɔ̃ avɛnɔ bɔpã mɛ enunù pɛ balɛ kitúɔ mɛ dɔ alɛ fɔ̃ɔ. Paulo Tɛshɛshɛ Nɛ Efeso

english_verse: Paul decided to travel by land to Assos. The rest of us went on ahead by ship, and we were to take him aboard there.
translation_verse: Bɔvɛ kɛlɛ abha ditsoku kelĩabhaohuɔ mɛ, pɛ bɔvɛ Aso tɛ̀ bɔdɛ tɔnɔ Paulo tɛ ekù kelĩabhaohuɔ aplɛnɔ ɔlɔ. Abhɛtɛ tɛtɔtɔɖɔ ɛlɛ ɔmɔsɛ azã bɔtsɛɖɛkpɔ kidzo abha avɛ ɛkɛlɛ.

english_verse: When he met us in Assos, he came aboard, and we sailed on to Mitylene.
translation_verse: Gɛ abatɔnɔ bɔlɔ nɛ Asoɔ, eku kelĩabhaohuɔ ɔplɛnɔ bɔlɔ, pɛ bɔvɛ Mitilene.

english_verse: The next day we came to a place near Chios, and the following day we reached Samos. The day after that we sailed to Miletus.
translation_verse: Gɛ keliɛ azaa, buku kelĩabhaohuɔ nɛ ɛkɛlɛ pɛ budiyì nɛ Xio. Gɛ keliɛ azazaa, bɔtsakɛ ɔvɛ Samo, kɛlɛ gɛ keliɛ azaa, budiyì nɛ Mileto.

english_verse: Paul had decided to sail on past Ephesus, because he did not want to spend too much time in Asia. He was in a hurry and wanted to be in Jerusalem in time for Pentecost. Paul Says Goodbye to the Church Leaders of Ephesus
translation_verse: Paulo atɔɖɔ ɛlɛ asɛ tɛ afala nɛ Efeso ɔbhà, tɛ̀ etibèzĩ kibè nɛ Asia bɔdɔ̃ mɛ, ɔmɔsɛ apɛ zia tɛ̀ ediku Yerusalem, onupɛ abapɛ tɛ̀ azã ɛkɛlɛ nɛ Pentekoste kiwui nabha.

english_verse: From Miletus, Paul sent a message for the church leaders at Ephesus to come and meet with him.
translation_verse: Denɔ Miletoɔ, Paulo akpɛ banɔ nɛ Efeso ɔhamɛ besheshèɛ ɔkɔ.

english_verse: When they got there, he said: You know everything I did during the time I was with you when I first came to Asia.
translation_verse: Gɛ bebekuɔ, etseɖe alɛ asɛ, “Noɔnyì kana gɛ ɛzanà mɛkpã, kibeɛ gɛ ɛzã wɔnɔ ɔbha, odenɔ kiwuiɛ gɛiputɔ gɛ ɛbaku Asia bɔdɔ̃ mɛ.

english_verse: Some of the Jews plotted against me and caused me a lot of sorrow and trouble. But I served the Lord and was humble.
translation_verse: Ɛlɛkɔ ishu esi zhizhinɔ hohoe nɛ̀ ɛwɔmɛ kpɛ kɛtakpu esi ɔkɔ Apaleteɛ nɔ, gɛ Yudanɛ alɔkɔ kɛtakpu mɛ kpa tɔtɔɖɔ efeke mɛɖi nɔ nyagaa flogo.

english_verse: When I preached in public or taught in your homes, I didn't hold back from telling anything that would help you.
translation_verse: Nɔonyi ɔsɛ igi yide nɛ ebui ɖɔɖɔ nɔnɔ gɛ laba aplɛnɔ wɔnɔ shu, pɔ ɛkasɛ wɔnɔ bɔdɔ̃ nɛ kɛtsalɛ mɛ, nɛ̀ denɔ bɔpã mɛ ɔvɛnɔ bɔpã mɛ.

english_verse: I told Jews and Gentiles to turn to God and have faith in our Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: Ɛdalɛ kɛlɛ mɛ ɔkɔ Yudanɛ nɛ̀ Helanɛ ɔsɛ tɛ betsyina atɔnɔ Owusoso Akaa nɛ̀ balɛ etúɔ tɛ̀ bayɛkɛ Apalete Yesu abha aŋã.

english_verse: I don't know what will happen to me in Jerusalem, but I must obey God's Spirit and go there.
translation_verse: “Kɛlɛ otsiɛ nɔ, gɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ ɔlɔɔ gbana mɛɛ, ɛɛtɔnɔ kidzo ɔvɛ Yerusalem kɛlɛ itinyi kɛgɛ babà mɛabha nɛ ɛkɛlɛ.

english_verse: In every city I visit, I am told by the Holy Spirit that I will be put in jail and will be in trouble in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Inyi sɛ nɛ ɔmahohoe ka nyia ka mɛɛ, Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã ɔkpɛ ɔsha kɔmɛ ɔsɛ ɔɖavũmɛ tɛzazã nɛ̀ ebui gagalĩ mɛbà dɛ mɔ.

english_verse: But I don't care what happens to me, as long as I finish the work the Lord Jesus gave me to do. And this work is to tell the good news about God's gift of undeserved grace.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ, ɛtɛka lɔkɔ mɛkpã asɛ lɛinu kɛdɔ nanà ɔkɔ mɛ, pɛ ɛtalà kɛxɔ̃ɛ gɛ Apalete Yesu akpɛ mɛahɔɛ wɔa bekeɛ, kɛxɔ̃ɛ gɛ kɛlɛinu ɔsɛ tɛ ɛdalɛ Owusoso Akaa ibuiɛ gɛ kibhui bɔlɔ buɔ shu.

english_verse: I have gone from place to place, preaching to you about God's kingdom, but now I know that none of you will ever see me again.
translation_verse: “Otsi alɛ inyi sɛ wɔnɔ mayɛmɛ nanà ma gɛ mɛnɛ yɛ zɛgã kidzo, alo pɛ bɔzɛɖɔ bukusiŋaŋã shu ebuiɛ azabamɔ mamɛ eɖi.

english_verse: I tell you today I am no longer responsible for any of you!
translation_verse: Klisoɔ, ɛdalɛ kɛlɛ mɛ kɔ wɔnɔ obuɔ ɔsɛ, onupɛ wɔnɔ mayɛmɛ teli ɛkpã bayalaa, ɛma ɛlɛ mɛ.

english_verse: I have told you everything God wants you to know.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ ɛgɛ yɔlɔ ebuiɛ petee gɛ Owusoso Akaa atɔɖɔ tɛɖɔɖɔ tseɖe wɔnɔ.

english_verse: Look after yourselves and everyone the Holy Spirit has placed in your care. Be like shepherds to God's church. It is the flock he bought with the blood of his own Son.
translation_verse: Nɔzanà awɔ̃ ɔkɔ wɔnɔ ishu tɛ nozoɖi ibhoshiɛ gɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã ɔkpɛ wɔnɔ ahɔɛ ɔsɛ nɔoɖi ɛlɛ abha. Nodzi ibhoshi agbagbana elete ɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa ɔhaa, gɛ edzi nɛ yɛ dzyosu nɔ.

english_verse: I know that after I am gone, others will come like fierce wolves to attack you.
translation_verse: Inyi sɛ onupɛ ɛbashɛɛ kidegbaa, igulu babà wɔnɔɛshi, kɛlɛ lɛti bàbi ibhoshiɛ ashagabui.

english_verse: Some of your own people will tell lies to win over the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Nɛ wɔnɔ mɛ tsyɛɛ, banɔ abalɔ́ pɛ babàsɛ asɛnɛ tɛ̀ badɔkpasɛanɛ etsyina tɔnɔ alɛ.

english_verse: Be on your guard! Remember how day and night for three years I kept warning you with tears in my eyes.
translation_verse: Kliso nɔzã nɛ̀ awɔ̃! Nɔbakà ɔsɛ̀ nɛ agɔ tatá mɛɛ, ɛgɛ yɔlɔ ɔsha kpɛkpɛ kɔ wɔnɔ nɛ̀ ɛwɔmɛ bibi mɛ elishi nɛ̀ kemuze.

english_verse: I now place you in God's care. Remember the message about his gift of undeserved grace! This message can help you and give you what belongs to you as God's people.
translation_verse: “Otsiɛ nɔ, ɛɛlɔkɔ̀ wɔnɔ ɔkpɛ ahɔɛ akɔ Owusoso Akaa nɛ̀ yɛibuiɛ gɛ alɔkɔ ebhui bɔlɔ ebuɔ nɔ̀. Kɛlɛ bàbhɔ wɔnɔ wunu kabha pɛ kɛbàkɔ wɔnɔ bagɛ badalɛ wɔnɔ shuɔ petee kaŋãsiɛ.

english_verse: I have never wanted anyone's money or clothes.
translation_verse: Ɛgɛ dzã kiwuhũ nɛ ananà sikãfufù, alo sikãɛkpã alo bɔdɔ̃ kpɛkpɛ shu.

english_verse: You know how I have worked with my own hands to make a living for myself and my friends.
translation_verse: Nɔonyi ɔsɛ mɛahɔɛ alɛ shu axɔ̃ɛ mɛwɔa lɔkɔ pɛ bɔdɔ̃ ɔkɔ mɛishu nɛ̀ mɛɔhagbo alɛ.

english_verse: By everything I did, I showed how you should work to help everyone who is weak. Remember that our Lord Jesus said, “More blessings come from giving than from receiving.”
translation_verse: Nɛ kɛka nyia ka gɛ ɛbhɛtɛ mɛɛ, ɛkasɛ wɔnɔ ɔsɛ̀ kɛxɔ̃ɛ gagalĩ ɛlɛ nɛidi wɔawɔa mɛ kɛlɛ ɔsɛ bɔplɛnɔ bagɛ ohumi ɔma balɛ ahɔɛ, ɛɛbakà ebui alɛ gɛ Apalete Yesu aɖɔ asɛ, ‘Tiyuyulà kpasɛ kɛdɔ̃ kɔkɔ mɛ ɔdɛ kɛdɔ̃ yɛyɛkɛ mɛ.’ ”

english_verse: After Paul had finished speaking, he knelt down with all of them and prayed.
translation_verse: Gɛ aɖɔ kibui ɛlɛ ebekeɛ, yɛnɛ̀ balɛ petee edzu ekluisi esi pɛ bekulu Owusoso Akaa.

english_verse: They were especially sad because Paul had told them, “You will never see me again.” Then they went with him to the ship.
translation_verse: Kɛgɛ kɔ bamɔ ɔwɔ̃nyɔnɔ ɔdɛ kɛlɛinu kibuiɛ gɛ aɖɔ asɛ bazakamɔ yiamɛ eɖiɛ. Kɛlɛ bagbana yɛ avɛnɔ kelĩabhaohuɔ shu.

english_verse: After saying goodbye, we sailed straight to Cos. The next day we reached Rhodes and from there sailed on to Patara.
translation_verse: Kibèɛ gɛ bɔɖɔ ɔsɛ basɛnɛ alɛ o pɛ bɔshɛɛ, bɔdɛgã kelĩabha kidzoɔ pɛ bɔvɛ tĩi diyì nɛ Kos. Gɛ keliɛ azaa, bɔvɛ Rodo, kɛlɛ odenɔ ɛkɛlɛɛ bɔvɛ Patara.

english_verse: We found a ship going to Phoenicia, so we got on board and sailed off.
translation_verse: Bɔmɔ Kelĩabhaohu toli gɛ lɔtsakɛ ɔvɛ Foenisia, kɛlɛ budiku ɔlɛ pɛ bɔtɔnɔ kelĩabha kidzoɔ.

english_verse: We came within sight of Cyprus and then sailed south of it on to the port of Tyre in Syria, where the ship was going to unload its cargo.
translation_verse: Kibeɛ gɛ bɔmɔ Kipro sɛsɛ, bɔgã fala nɛ bɔpialɛ ɔkɔ ebeɛ, pɛ budiyì nɛ Siria. Budiku Tiro, ɛkɛlɛ gɛ kɛlɛ ɔsɛ kelĩabhaohuɔ bu ɔlɛ etukuɔ.

english_verse: But when the week was over, we started on our way again. All the men, together with their wives and children, walked with us from the town to the seashore. We knelt on the beach and prayed.
translation_verse: Pɔ gɛ bɔlɔ kibeɛ naa, bɔshɛ kɛlɛ bɔvɛ bɔlɔ kidzo gagã nabha. Badɔkpasɛanɛ petee, balɛ edzinì nɛ̀ balɛ̀ eyi alɛ nyɔ̃ɔ azã bɔlɔ kede pɛ bagbana ɔlɔ koeyinɔ nɛ ɔmamɛ, kɛlɛ budzu ekluisi esi nɛ aƒu nɔtɔ̃ pɛ bukulu Owusoso Akaa.

english_verse: Then after saying goodbye to each other, we got into the ship, and they went back home.
translation_verse: Gɛ bɔkasɛ bagɔlɔ ahɔɛ ebekeɛ, budi tsoku kelĩabhaohuɔ mɛ, kɛlɛ balɛ tsyɛ etsyina avɛ bɔpã mɛ.

english_verse: We sailed from Tyre to Ptolemais, where we greeted the followers and stayed with them for a day.
translation_verse: Bɔzã bɔlɔ kelĩabha kidzogagã nabha odenɔ Tiro pɛ budiyì nɛ Ptolemai, nɛ ɛkɛlɛ bɔsɛnɔ badzyaa kɛlɛ bɔzã balɛ ɔbha kiwui tinikpo.

english_verse: The next day we went to Caesarea and stayed with Philip, the preacher. He was one of the seven men who helped the apostles,
translation_verse: Gɛ bɔshɛ kelizaza, budiyì nɛ Kaesarea kɛlɛ bɔdɛzã Filipo gɛ aɖɔ kibuiɛ akana pɛ enu banɔ Tegegeneɛ gɛ bebu nɛ yerusalemuɔ ɔmaɛmɛ tenikpoɔ bɔpã mɛ.

english_verse: and he had four unmarried daughters who prophesied.
translation_verse: Beyiadzɛ talɛ gɛ bakazana anyɛ azã yɛahɔɛ gɛ baɖɔ ebui elú.

english_verse: We had been in Caesarea for several days, when the prophet Agabus came to us from Judea.
translation_verse: Gɛ bɔzã ɛkɛlɛ ewui kɛbɔbhlui nɔ, ebuiɖɔluanɔ teli gɛ bekulu yɛasɛ Agabo ede Yudea aba.

english_verse: He took Paul's belt, and with it he tied up his own hands and feet. Then he told us, “The Holy Spirit says that some of the Jewish leaders in Jerusalem will tie up the man who owns this belt. They will also hand him over to the Gentiles.”
translation_verse: Gɛ aba bɔlɔ ɔbhaa, alɔkɔ Paulo yɛatsatsatɔɖɔ pɛ alɔkɔ yɛanyã yɛahɔɛ ɛtsɛɖɛ nɛ̀ ɛhɔɛ pɛ aɖɔ asɛ, “Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã ɔɖɔ ɔsɛ, ‘Kidzo ɛlɛ nabha Yudanɛ abanyã atsatsatɔɖɔ alɛ eleteɛ pɛ babà lɔkɔ yɛakpɛ ahɔɛ akɔ bagɛ betinu Yudanɛ.’ ”

english_verse: After Agabus said this, we and the followers living there begged Paul not to go to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Gɛ bulũ kibui ɛlɛ, bɔlɔ nɛ̀ banunɔ nɛ ɛkɛlɛ kɔ Paulo kɛtakpu asɛ etì wu avɛ Yerusalemuɔ.

english_verse: But Paul answered, “Why are you crying and breaking my heart? I am not only willing to be put in jail for the Lord Jesus, but I am even willing to die for him in Jerusalem!”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ Paulo ebuiliulũ asɛ, “Kiɖe sopɛ nɔobiyoyò pɛ nɔyɛɛ mɛ kitúɔ ana? Gɛnɛ mɛ tɛnyanyã ete shu mɛ balɛ ɔkɔ, pɔ ɛbalɛ tsyɛ sɛ tɛ̀ itsi nɛ Yerusalem nɛ Apalete Yesu yɛinyeɛ nɛtakpu.”

english_verse: Since we could not get Paul to change his mind, we gave up and prayed, “Lord, please make us willing to do what you want.”
translation_verse: Gɛ etielũ bɔlɔ bulũ tɛwɔɛwɔɛ nɔ soɔ, bɔshɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛishu pɛ bɔɖɔ ɔsɛ, “Apaleteɛ tɛpɛpɛ nɔ kɛlɛ ba ɛlɛ mɛ.”

english_verse: Then we got ready to go to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Nɛ kibui ɛlɛ niediɛ, bɔbalɛ pɛ buwu vɛ Yerusalem.

english_verse: Some of the followers from Caesarea went with us and took us to stay in the home of Mnason. He was from Cyprus and had been a follower from the beginning. Paul Visits James
translation_verse: Badɔkpasɛanɛ teli gɛede Kaisareanɔ agbana bɔlɔ avɛnɔ ediku Mnason gɛenu adɔkpasɛanɛ tenikpo yɛɔpã mɛ pɛbɔzã ɛkɛlɛ. Enu anɔ gɛede Kipro pɛ edzi adɔkpasɛnɛ gɛiputɔ̀ tenikpo. Paulo Yerusalem Tikuku

english_verse: When we arrived in Jerusalem, the Lord's followers gladly welcomed us.
translation_verse: Gɛ budiku Yerusalemuɔ, baadzya ayɛkɛ bɔlɔ nɛ̀ same.

english_verse: Paul went with us to see James the next day, and all the church leaders were present.
translation_verse: Gɛ keliɛ azaa, Paulo nɛ̀ bɔlɔ banɔ gɔgɔɛ nɔ vɛ diɖi Yakobo, kɛlɛ ɔhamɛ besheshèɛ petee azã ɛkɛlɛ.

english_verse: Paul greeted them and told how God had used him to help the Gentiles.
translation_verse: Paulo asɛnɔ alɛ kɛlɛ akɔ alɛ tititì piapi nɛ kɛgɛ Owusoso Akaa abhɛtɛ nɛ bagɛ etinu Yudanɛ nɛishi ɔganà yɛɔdɔ kakasɛ mɛ.

english_verse: Everyone who heard this praised God and said to Paul: My friend, you can see how many tens of thousands of our people have become followers! And all of them are eager to obey the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Gɛ belũ kibui ɛlɛ, bakpɛ Owusoso Akaa nɛinyè nɛitití. Kɛlɛ betseɖe Paulo asɛ, “Madzya, ɖi Yudanɛ ihotu tɛshɛ nɔnɔ gɛ bayɛkɛ aŋã pɛ banyana ɔsha mɛ evunɔɔ.

english_verse: But they have been told that you are teaching those who live among the Gentiles to disobey this Law. They claim that you are telling them not to circumcise their sons or to follow our customs.
translation_verse: Bakɔ betì asɛ wɔkasɛ Yudanɛ petee gɛ bakpasɛ bagɛ betinu Yudanɛ nɛishi asɛ betsodzi nɛ Mose ɔshaa niede, pɛ ootsèɖe balɛ ɔsɛ betee bhui kɛhɔ̃a akɔ balɛ eyi alɛ alo tɛ̀ bazanà ɛkpã nɛ bɔlɔ ɛkɔɔ nulũ.

english_verse: What should we do now that our people have heard you are here?
translation_verse: Kiɖe kɛlɛ ɔsɛ bɔbhɛtɛ? Bebèlũ kɛgbã asɛ ɔba xɔɔ,

english_verse: Please do what we ask, because four of our men have made special promises to God.
translation_verse: klisoɔ bhɛtɛ kɛgɛ butseɖe wɔ. Banyɛ talɛ akpasɛ bɔlɔ ishi gɛ batanà bɔdɔ̃ elú.

english_verse: Join with them and prepare yourself for the ceremony that goes with the promises. Pay the cost for their heads to be shaved. Then everyone will learn that the reports about you are not true. They will know you do obey the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Gbanà banyɛ alɛ ɔvɛnɔ, tɛ̀ wɔnɔ alɛ odiplu shu tɛ̀ ɔbuia kɛlɛ shu abhɛ, sɛ tɛ̀ bashɛ atakpuɔ ɔkɔ alɛ. Kɛlɛ aka nyia aka ebetì asɛ̀ ebui alɛ gɛ baɖɔ nɛ wɔshuɔ ama alɛ mɛ̀, tɛ̀ kɛkasɛ ɔsɛ̀ wɔtsyɛ oshù ɔshaa.

english_verse: Some while ago we told the Gentile followers what we think they should do. We instructed them not to eat anything offered to idols. They were told not to eat any meat with blood still in it or the meat of an animal that has been strangled. They were also told not to commit any terrible sexual sins.
translation_verse: Bagɛ betinu Yudanɛ pɛ bayɛkɛ aŋã xɔnɔɔ, bɔtɛ fɔnɔ keplukpa akɔ alɛ nɛ kɛgɛ bɔtɔɖɔ shu ɔsɛ, batɛ aŋã bɔdɔ̃ gɛ batɔ ɔkɔ ibudiɛ, dzyosu nɛ̀ kɛdzya gidè bagã gɛ bedzyinì balɛ iliɛ, nɛ̀ tɛ̀ batakaŋã kɛgɔlɔ.”

english_verse: The next day Paul took the four men with him and got himself ready at the same time they did. Then he went into the temple and told when the final ceremony would take place and when an offering would be made for each of them. Paul Is Arrested
translation_verse: Gɛ keliɛ azaa, Paulo agbana banyɛ avɛnɔ ediplu ishu nɛ̀ balɛ. Kɛlɛ avɛ Akaavũɔ pɛ adɛkɔ tititì nɛ kiwuiɛ gɛ kɛlɛ ɔsɛ ishu puplu kiwuiɛ belèɛ pɛ babàkɔ bɔdɔ̃ nɛ balɛ ɔmayɛmɛ aka nyia aka ulũɔ. Bevũ Paulo

english_verse: When the period of seven days for the ceremony was almost over, some of the Jewish people from Asia saw Paul in the temple. They got a large crowd together and started attacking him.
translation_verse: Gɛ ewui tegegeneɛ ebelè ebèkeɛ, Yudanɛ teli gɛede Asia bɔdɔ̃ mɛ adɛmɔ Paulo nɛ Akaavũɔ mɛ. Betsú kifu akpɛ banɔ nɔ petee mɛ kɛlɛ bedivũ yɛ,

english_verse: They were shouting, “Friends, help us! This man goes around everywhere, saying bad things about our nation and about the Law of Moses and about this temple. He has even brought shame to this holy temple by bringing in Gentiles.”
translation_verse: pɛ bakpɛ ozĩ asɛ, “Israelanɛ, nɔplɛnɔ ɔlɔ mɔ! Anyɛ gɛ akasɛ banɔ nɔ petee bɔdɔ̃ nɛ ɔkɔ nyia ɔkɔ afalanɔ pɛ alɔɛtití nɛ bɔlɔ banunɔ nɛ̀ ɔshaa shu nɛ̀ ɛkɛlɛ tsyɛ nɛ alɛ. Nɛ kɛlɛ̀ niedeɛ, adɛgbana Helanɛ amana Akaavũɔ shu pɛ akpɛ ɔdadalɛ ɔkɔlɛ̀ bɔtsɔ̃.”

english_verse: The whole city was in an uproar, and the people turned into a mob. They grabbed Paul and dragged him out of the temple. Then suddenly the doors were shut.
translation_verse: Ozĩ oyì nɛ ɔmàmɛ petee, kɛlɛ banɔ nɔ ebu tɛtsɛɖɛmɛ ekoeyì edenɔ kawɔ̃pɛ ka nyia ka mɛ aba. Gɛ bevũ Pauloɔ ebekeɛ, betsìi nɛ kesiakpa edenɔ Akaavũɔ shu, kɛlɛ badã budulũɔ odunũ ɔlɛ.

english_verse: The people were about to kill Paul when the Roman army commander heard that all Jerusalem was starting to riot.
translation_verse: Gɛ bapɛ asɛ tɛ̀ beyi yɛ, ebui alɛ ediku keyuadɛdɛ gɛ agbana Romanɛ keyuizaa natɔ̃ mɛ ɔsɛ kititì lɔlɔɛ ebui eku Yerusalem ɔmã mɛ.

english_verse: So he quickly took some soldiers and officers and ran to where the crowd had gathered. As soon as the mob saw the commander and soldiers, they stopped beating Paul.
translation_verse: Tsiɔ̃ ɔlɛ koɔ, kɛlɛ agbana ɛzãkɔ banunɔ nɛ̀ buyuadɛdɛ teli pɛ bebu tɛtsɛɖɛ mɛ avɛ banɔɛzaa mɛ. Kibeɛ gɛ banɔɛza amɔ keyuiza otugbà anɔ nɔ̀ nɛ̀ yɛ̀ buyuadɛdɛ, bayɔlɔ Paulo huisì.

english_verse: The army commander went over and arrested him and had him bound with two chains. Then he tried to find out who Paul was and what he had done.
translation_verse: Keyuiza otugbà anɔ nɔ̀ avɛ edivũi, kɛlɛ akɔ asɛ banyã yɛ nɛ̀ ɛɖahui tɛbha. Kɛlɛ ebisì yɛ anɔ nɔɛdi gɛ enuɔ nɛ̀ kɛgɛ yɛabhɛtɛɛ.

english_verse: Part of the crowd shouted one thing, and part of them shouted something else. But they were making so much noise that the commander could not find out a thing. Then he ordered Paul to be taken into the fortress.
translation_verse: Banunɔ teli nɛ banɔɛza mɛ akpɛ ozĩ aɖɔ kibui ɛlɛ̀, kɛlɛ beteli tsyɛ aɖɔ kɛbhlatɔ, kɛlɛ gɛ keyuiza otugbà anɔ nɔ̀ agɛmɔ kibuiɛ nɛtakpu nɛ ozĩɛ gɛ loyìɛ soɔ, akɔ ɔsha asɛ bagbana Paulo avɛnɔ buyuadɛdɛ ɔzãkɔɔ.

english_verse: As they reached the steps, the crowd became so wild that the soldiers had to lift Paul up and carry him.
translation_verse: Gɛ Paulo ediku gedulũɔ, kana gɛ banunɔ bamɛ atã kifuɔ ebewù kabha soɔ, kɛlɛ ɔsɛ buyuadɛdɛ abhasà yɛ ewunɔ kabha.

english_verse: The crowd followed and kept shouting, “Kill him! Kill him!” Paul Speaks to the Crowd
translation_verse: Banɔɛza gɛzã balɛ kedeɛ zã ozĩ kpɛkpɛ nabha asɛ, “Noyìi!” Paulo Aɖɔ Kaɖɔ Akɔ Banɔɛzaa

english_verse: When Paul was about to be taken into the fortress, he asked the commander, “Can I say something to you?” “How do you know Greek?” the commander asked.
translation_verse: Kibeɛ gɛ buyuadɛdɛ agbanà Paulo ebèku buyuadɛ ɔzãkɔɔ, ebisi keyuiza otugbà anɔ nɔ̀ asɛ, “Ɔtɔɔkɔ mɛidzo tɛ ɛɖɔ kibui tili tseɖe wɔ dza?” Ebisi yɛasɛ, “Olũ Helanɛ buluhũ dza?”

english_verse: “Aren't you that Egyptian who started a riot not long ago and led
translation_verse: Gɛnɛ wɔonu Egipte eyiɛ gɛ ɔmana kititì lɔlɔ ebui pɛ ɔgbana batɔmɛ gagalĩ banɔ ihotu tɛlɛ ɔvɛnɔ oxuiɛ kibeɛ tili gɛbafala dza?”

english_verse: “No!” Paul replied. “I am a Jew from Tarsus, an important city in Cilicia. Please let me speak to the crowd.”
translation_verse: Paulo ebuiliulũ asɛ, “Inu Yudaeyi gɛide Tarso nɛ Kilikia, ɔma gɛ batɛwɔ̀ nɛ ɔlɛ shu mɛ mɛide. Ɛkɔ wɔɛtakpu, kɔ mɛɖɔ kaɖɔ ɔkɔ banɔɛza.”

english_verse: The commander told him he could speak, so Paul stood on the steps and motioned to the people. When they were quiet, he spoke to them in Aramaic:
translation_verse: Gɛ keyuiza otugbà anɔ nɔ̀ akɔ yɛidzo ebekeɛ, Paulo alɔɛtití nɛ kedulũ okpó nabha pɛ akasɛ banunɔ ahɔɛ asɛ tɛ̀ bataatɔ̃. Gɛ balɛ nyɔ̃ɔ ataatɔ̃ ebekeɛ, aɖɔaɖɔ akɔ alɛ nɛ Aramaik buluhũ mɛ asɛ.

english_verse: “My friends and leaders of our nation, listen as I explain what happened!”
translation_verse: “Mɛadzya alɛ nɛ̀ mɛada alɛ mɔ, nɔtaatɔ̃ olũ mɛishu ulũ tibubuɔ.”

english_verse: When the crowd heard Paul speak to them in Aramaic, they became even quieter. Then Paul said:
translation_verse: Gɛ belũ yɛaɖɔaɖɔ ɔkɔ alɛ nɛ Aramaik buluhũɔ mɛ soɔ, badɔ kananá. Kɛlɛ Paulo aɖɔ asɛ,

english_verse: I am a Jew, born in the city of Tarsus in Cilicia. But I grew up here in Jerusalem where I was a student of Gamaliel and was taught to follow every single law of our ancestors. In fact, I was just as eager to obey God as any of you are today.
translation_verse: “Yudaeyi mɛinu, bamɔ́ mɛ nɛ Tarso nɛ Kilikia, pɔ Yerusalem ɔmalɛ mɛ banyɔnɔ mɛ. Gamaliel yɛadɔkpasɛnɛ mɛidzì pɛ ɛkpasɛ bɔlɔ akaalɛ ɔshaa piapii kɛlɛ ɛzɛwlɔakɛ ɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa asɛ kana gɛ wɔnɔ ɔmayɛmɛ aka nyia aka abhɛtɛ ɛlɛ obuɔ ana.

english_verse: I made trouble for everyone who followed the Lord's Way, and I even had some of them killed. I had others arrested and put in jail. I didn't care if they were men or women.
translation_verse: Ɛtɔnɔ bagɛ bazã Kidzo ɛlɛ nabha ɛkpã niede, kɛlɛ ɛkɔ beyí alɛ, gɛ kɛkɔ mɛivũ banyɛ nɛ̀ badzɛ ɔdɛkpɛ ɔɖavũ mɛ,

english_verse: The high priest and all the council members can tell you this is true. They even gave me letters to the Jewish leaders in Damascus, so that I could arrest people there and bring them to Jerusalem to be punished.
translation_verse: asɛ kana gɛ banɔlũazazã gbagbla nɛ̀ yɛ besheshèɛ petee abalà aɖɔ kaɖɔ nɛ kɛlɛishu ana. Ɛvɛ tsyɛ dɛyɛkɛ buplukpa odenɔ banɔlũazazã gbagbla ɔkɔ ɔsɛsɛ balɛ adzya alɛ nɛ Damasko, kɛlɛ ɛvɛ ɛkɛlɛ dɛgbana banɔ alɛ asɛ ɔɖavũmɛ azazã ana amana Yerusalem asɛ bapɔ̃ batɔ̃ akɔ alɛ.

english_verse: One day about noon I was getting close to Damascus, when a bright light from heaven suddenly flashed around me.
translation_verse: “Nɛ kemuze ebe ana gɛ iku Damaskoɔ ebekeɛ, nɛ mɛ matìmatì mɛɛ, kifú tili tsé odenɔ kititíɛ bakana mɛishuɔ.

english_verse: I fell to the ground and heard a voice asking, “Saul, Saul, why are you so cruel to me?”
translation_verse: Ɛwɔlɛ dɔ kesiakpa, kɛlɛ ilũ kaɖɔ tieli gɛetseɖe mɛ asɛ, ‘Saulo! Saulo! Kiɖeso pɛ ɔtɔnɔ miede ana?’

english_verse: “Who are you?” I answered. The Lord replied, “I am Jesus from Nazareth! I am the one you are so cruel to.”
translation_verse: Ibisi sɛ, “ ‘Apalete, wɔonu afana?’ Ebuiliulũ akɔ mɛ asɛ, “ ‘Mɛinu Apalete Yesu Nazaretieyiɛ gɛ yiede wɔtɔnɔɔ.’

english_verse: Those who were traveling with me saw the light, but did not hear the voice.
translation_verse: Mɛɔhayɛ amɔ kifúɔ giboeleɛ, pɔ begitì anunɔ gɛ yɛoliɛ mɛ ɔlɔ ɖɔaɖɔ tseɖe mɛ.

english_verse: I asked, “Lord, what do you want me to do?” Then he told me, “Get up and go to Damascus. When you get there, you will be told what to do.”
translation_verse: Ibisi sɛ, “ ‘Apalete, kiɖe kɛlɛ ɔsɛ mɛbhɛtɛ?’ Apaleteɛ etseɖe mɛasɛ, “ ‘Lɔɛtití tɛ̀ ɔvɛ Damasko ɔmamɛ. Nɛ ɛkɛlɛ bebètseɖe wɔ ebuiɛ gɛ bakpɛ wɔahɔɛ ɔsɛ ɔbhɛtɛɛ.’

english_verse: The light had been so bright that I couldn't see. And the others had to lead me by the hand to Damascus.
translation_verse: Mɛɔbhia alɛ anyanã mɛ ahɔɛ pɛ bagbana mɛ avɛnɔ Damasko ɔmamɛ, ɔmɔsɛ kana gɛ kifúɔ tséɛ akɔ mɛawuhũɔ epú.

english_verse: In that city there was a man named Ananias, who faithfully obeyed the Law of Moses and was well liked by all the Jewish people living there.
translation_verse: “Anyɛ teli gɛ bekulu asɛ Anania yɛaba ebeɖi mɛ. Edzi anɔ gɛ ezeshù ɔsha, kɛlɛ Yudanɛ petee gɛazã kɛma ɛlɛ mɛ adɛmɛ yɛibui.

english_verse: He came to me and said, “Saul, my friend, you can now see again!” At once I could see.
translation_verse: Anyɛ alɛ yɛaba lɔɛtití nɛ mɛɔbha pɛ etseɖe mɛasɛ, ‘Adzya Saulo, tsodzi ɔmɔ!’ Kɛlɛ nɛ kibè ɛlɛ koɔ, kɛlɛ mɛlà mɔyɛ.

english_verse: Then Ananias told me, “The God that our ancestors worshiped has chosen you to know what he wants done. He has chosen you to see the One Who Obeys God and to hear his voice.
translation_verse: “Kɛlɛ etseɖe mɛ asɛ, ‘Bɔlɔ akaalɛ Owusoso Akaa yɛebú wɔ ɔsɛ tɛ otì kɛgɛ yɛapɛ nɔ, ɔsɛ tɛ̀ ɔmɔ Anɔ Dadalɛ, nɛ̀ tɛ olũ ebui edenɔ yɛolugbũ mɛ̀.

english_verse: You must tell everyone what you have seen and heard.
translation_verse: Wɔbodzì anɔ gɛ ɔbɔɖɔaɖɔ ɔdalɛ yɛishu ɔkɔ banunɔ petee nɛ kɛgɛ ɔmɔ pɛ olũɔ shu.

english_verse: What are you waiting for? Get up! Be baptized, and wash away your sins by praying to the Lord.”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ otsiɛ nɔpɔ, kiɖe woko opoɔ dza? Lɔ, kɔ bakpɛ bulĩ akɔwɔ nɛ kɛtakpu tɛ̀ beplu wɔ ebui kpa atɛ́ɛ gana yɛinyeɛ tikukulu mɛ.’

english_verse: After this I returned to Jerusalem and went to the temple to pray. There I had a vision
translation_verse: “Gɛ itsodzi ba Yerusalem pɛ izikulu Owusoso Akaa nɛ Akaavũɔ mɛ, ɛbaku taɖa mɛ

english_verse: of the Lord who said to me, “Hurry and leave Jerusalem! The people won't listen to what you say about me.”
translation_verse: kɛlɛ ɛmɔ Apaleteɛ yɛaɖɔaɖɔ etseɖe mɛ. Aɖɔ asɛ ‘Hɔa! Shɛ nɛ Yerusalem otsiɛ tsiɔ, ɔmɔsɛ batɛ bayɛkɛ wɔɛbuiɛ gɛ ɔbɔɖɔ nɛ mɛishuɔ.’

english_verse: I replied, “Lord, they know that in many of our synagogues I arrested and beat people who had faith in you.
translation_verse: “Ibuiliulũ sɛ, ‘Apalete, banɔ alɛ enyi asɛ ɛɛlɔ̀de ɔtsyɔakɔ ɔlɛ ɔvɛnɔ ɔbhlatɔ, pɛ idi huisi bagɛ ayɛkɛ wɔabha aŋã pɛ ɛɛkpɛ alɛ nɛ ɔɖavũ mɛ.

english_verse: Stephen was killed because he spoke for you, and I stood there and cheered them on. I even guarded the clothes of the men who murdered him.”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ kibeɛ gɛ bazabalɛ̀ Stefano gɛetsi nɛ wɔɛtakpu dzyosu nɔ, ɛzã ɛkɛlɛ pɛ ɛzɛkpɛ balɛ kemi pɛ izipo bagɛ eyi yɛ akpɛkpɛ nɔ.’

english_verse: But the Lord told me to go, and he promised to send me far away to the Gentiles.
translation_verse: “Kɛlɛ Apaleteɛ etseɖe mɛasɛ, ‘Gavɛ, ɛbakpɛ wɔ sɛsɛ fãa nɛ bagɛ etinu Yudanɛ ɔkɔ.’ ” Paulo Gɛebedzì Romaeyiɛ

english_verse: The crowd listened until Paul said this. Then they started shouting, “Get rid of this man! He doesn't deserve to live.”
translation_verse: Banɔɛza zɛtaatɔ̃ nɛ̀ Paulo odiyinɔ kibeɛ gɛ azaɖɔ kibui ɛlɛ ɔsɛ ‘Gavɛ, ɛbakpɛ wɔ sɛsɛ fãa nɛ bagɛ etinu Yudanɛ ɔkɔ.’ ” Kɛlɛ bakpɛ ozĩ asɛ, “Nɔgbã yɛɔkpɛ nɛ kesiakpa nabha mɔ! Agɛ shɔnɔ asɛ azã nɛ̀ ɛkpã!”

english_verse: They kept shouting. They waved their clothes around and threw dust into the air. Paul and the Roman Army Commander
translation_verse: Kibeɛ gɛ bakpɛ ozĩɛ pɛ batɛ balɛ akpɛkpɛ elú, pɛ batɛ osubhududu nɔ kpɛ kɛlɛ́ɛ nɔ mɛɛ,

english_verse: The Roman commander ordered Paul to be taken into the fortress and beaten with a whip. He did this to find out why the people were screaming at Paul.
translation_verse: keyuiza anɔ gbagbla akɔ ɔsha asɛ bagbanà Paulo avɛnɔ buyuadɛdɛ ɔzãkɔɔ. Akasɛ alɛ asɛ behuisi yɛ tɛ̀ bebisi yɛ, tɛ betì kibui nɔnɔ shu sopɛ banunɔ akpɛ ozĩ ɔlɛ nɛ yɛabha ana.

english_verse: While the soldiers were tying Paul up to be beaten, he asked the officer standing there, “Is it legal to beat a Roman citizen before he has been tried in court?”
translation_verse: Kibèɛ gɛ banyã yɛ ɔsɛ tɛ behuisi yɛɛ, Paulo etseɖe keyuiza anɔ gbagbla gɛ eli ititì nɛ ɛkɛlɛ asɛ, “Ɔsha kɔ kidzo ɔsɛ behuisì Romaeyi gɛ begibu yɛpɔɔ dza?”

english_verse: When the officer heard this, he went to the commander and said, “What are you doing? This man is a Roman citizen!”
translation_verse: Kibeɛ gɛ keyuiza anɔ gbagbla elũ kibui ɛlɛ, avɛ keyuiza otugbànɔ ɔkɔ adɛ kɔyɛ tititì. Ebisi asɛ, “Kiɖuwoe wɔbɔ ɔbhɛtɛ? Anyɛ alɛ enu Romaeyi.”

english_verse: The commander went to Paul and asked, “Tell me, are you a Roman citizen?” “Yes,” Paul answered.
translation_verse: Keyuiza anɔ gbagbla avɛ Paulo ɔbhà pɛ edibisi yɛasɛ, “Tseɖe mɛ, Romaeyi wɔonu dza?” Ebuiliulũ asɛ, “Ee, yɛ mɛinu.”

english_verse: The commander then said, “I paid a lot of money to become a Roman citizen.” But Paul replied, “I was born a Roman citizen.”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ keyuiza anɔ gbagbla aɖɔ asɛ, “Kɛbhɛ hohoe mɛxɔnɔ buia flogo pɛ ɛbadzì Romaeyi.” Paulo ebuiliulũ asɛ, “Mɛxɔnɔ Romaeyi bamɔ mɛ.”

english_verse: The men who were about to beat and question Paul quickly backed off. And the commander himself was frightened when he realized that he had put a Roman citizen in chains. Paul Is Tried by the Council
translation_verse: Bagɛ apɛ asɛ ebisi yɛ ebuiɛ, beyidè odunu ɔlɛ. Kifù bayí keyuiza anɔ gbagbla tsyɛ gɛ ebetì asɛ anyã Paulo gɛenu Romaeyiɛ akpɛ ɛɖahuiɛ mɛ. Paulo Nɛ Ɔhamɛ Besheshèɛ Namɛ

english_verse: The next day the commander wanted to know the real reason why the Jewish leaders had brought charges against Paul. So he had Paul's chains removed, and he ordered the chief priests and the whole council to meet. Then he had Paul led in and made him stand in front of them.
translation_verse: Gɛ keliɛ azaa, gɛ keyuiza anɔ gbagbla apɛ asɛ tɛ̀ etì kibui nɔnɔ shu sopɛ Yudanɛ alɔkɔ kibui nɛ Paulo shu soɔ, akɔ bebui nɛ ɔɖavũɔ mɛ kɛlɛ akɔ ɔsha asɛ banɔlũazazã gbagbla nɛ̀ ɔhamɛ besheshèɛ petee ebeshogò. Kɛlɛ agbana Paulo amana pɛ akɔ yɛalɔɛtití nɛ balɛ amɛ.

english_verse: Paul looked straight at the council members and said, “My friends, to this day I have served God with a clear conscience!”
translation_verse: Paulo eɖi ɔhamɛ besheshèɛ namɛ pɛ aɖɔ asɛ, “Mɛadzya alɛ mɔ, ɛbhɛtɛ kɛgɛ kɛlɛ sɛ mɛbhɛtɛ petee ɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa nɛ ekpeɖekpeɖe gbangbã mɛ oboyinɔ obuoɔ.”

english_verse: Then Ananias the high priest ordered the men standing beside Paul to hit him on the mouth.
translation_verse: Nɛ kibui ɛlɛ gɛ aɖɔ soɔ, Anania gɛenu banɔlũazazã gbagbla etseɖe bagɛ beli kititì nɛ Paulo ɔbha asɛ behu olugbũ ɔlɛ ɛdɔ̃ akɔɛ.

english_verse: Paul turned to the high priest and said, “You whitewashed wall! God will hit you. You sit there to judge me by the Law of Moses. But at the same time you order men to break the Law by hitting me.”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ Paulo etseɖe yɛasɛ, “Owusoso Akaa ebehuwɔ ebho, wɔ kigilì gɛ badzã bɔdɔ̃ fufú akɔ! Ɔzã esì nɛ ɛkɛlɛ pɛ okulu kebhu nɛ mɛ nɛ ɔsha nulũ, pɔ wɔtsyɛ ɔgɛ ɔbhɛtɛ tɔɖɔ ɔsha nabha gɛ ɔɖɔ ɔsɛ behu mɛ kɛdɔ̃!”

english_verse: The men standing beside Paul asked, “Don't you know you are insulting God's high priest?”
translation_verse: Bagɛ balɔɛtití nɛ Paulo ɔbha aɖɔ asɛ, “Kifù tiyìwɔ gɛ ɔkwala Owusoso Akaa anɔlũazazã gbagbla dzã?

english_verse: Paul replied, “Oh! I didn't know he was the high priest. The Scriptures do tell us not to speak evil about a leader of our people.”
translation_verse: Paulo ebuiliulũ asɛ, “Mɛadzya alɛ mɔ, ɛgɛ mɔsɛ yɛenu banɔlũazazã gbagbla, ɔmɔsɛ bafɔnɔ elú asɛ, ‘Tɛɖɔ kaɖɔkpa ɔkpɛ agɛ yɛaŋã bukusi nɛ wɔnɔ banunɔ abha shu.’

english_verse: When Paul saw that some of the council members were Sadducees and others were Pharisees, he shouted, “My friends, I am a Pharisee and the son of a Pharisee. I am on trial simply because I believe that the dead will be raised to life.”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ Paulo, gɛenyi asɛ balɛ mɛ beteli enu Zadukianɛ pɛ bagɔgɔɛ nɔ enu Farisianɛ soɔ, aɖɔaɖɔ gagalĩ etseɖe ɔhamɛ besheshèɛ asɛ, “Mɛadzya alɛ mɔ, Farisieyi mɛinu, inu eyianyɛ ɔkɔ Farisieyi. Bekulu kebhu nɛ mɛ ɔmɔsɛ iɖi otugbà mɛ ɔkɔ betsitsíɛ tɛlɔlɔ.”

english_verse: As soon as Paul said this, the Pharisees and the Sadducees got into a big argument, and the council members started taking sides.
translation_verse: Gɛ aɖɔ kibui ɛlɛ koɔ, kawlɔ̃akɛ ebeku Farisianɛ nɛ̀ Zadukianɛ nɛishi, kɛlɛ kazã ebebu ɔtɔ nɛ bagɔlɔ shu.

english_verse: and so there was a lot of shouting. Some of the teachers of the Law of Moses were Pharisees. Finally, they became angry and said, “We don't find anything wrong with this man. Maybe a spirit or an angel really did speak to him.”
translation_verse: Ozĩ gbagbla ozoyi tɔɖɔ, kɛlɛ ɔshadɔkasɛ teli gɛenu Farisianɛ abalɔɛtití nɛ̀ kawlɔ̃akɛ ebui gagalĩi. Baɖɔ asɛ, bɔgɛmɔ kibui kpà nanà nɛ anyɛ alɛ shu, kiɖuwoe nɔbɔɖɔ gɛ onupɛ edzi asɛ ɔfɔfuĩa alo axɔ̃ɛdɛ teli yɛaɖɔaɖɔ alɛ etseɖe yɛ dza?”

english_verse: The argument became fierce, and the commander was afraid that Paul would be pulled apart. So he ordered the soldiers to go in and rescue Paul. Then they took him back into the fortress.
translation_verse: Kawlɔ̃akɛ nulũ bagagalĩ gɛ kɛkɔ ɔsɛ kifù bayí keyuiza anɔ gbagbla asɛ banunɔ abatsyɔ̃ɛ Paulo mɛ wluiwlui. Kliso akɔ ɔsha asɛ keyuiza ivolo nɔ ezhi avɛ adɛ yɛkɛ yɛ nɛ balɛ ahɔɛmɛ nɛ tɛgagalĩ mɛ tɛ bagbana yɛ amana buyuadɛdɛ ɔzãkɔɔ.

english_verse: That night the Lord stood beside Paul and said, “Don't worry! Just as you have told others about me in Jerusalem, you must also tell about me in Rome.” A Plot To Kill Paul
translation_verse: Kiwui ɛlɛ elishiɛ, Apaleteɛ abazã Paulo ɔbha pɛ etseɖe yɛasɛ, “Kpɛ kité! Kana gɛ ɔɖɔ kaɖɔ dalɛ mɛishu nɛ Yerusalemuɔ, kana kɛlɛ ɔsɛ wɔdɛɖɔ kaɖɔ nɛ mɛishu nɛ Roma tsyɛ.” Tɛtɔtɔɖɔ Kɔ Paulo Tìyiyi

english_verse: The next morning more than
translation_verse: Banunɔ gɛ badɛ tɔɖɔ kibui kpa ɛlɛ ekpè adɛ afɛtalɛ.

english_verse: Then some of them went to the chief priests and the nation's leaders and said, “We have promised God that we would not eat a thing until we have killed Paul.
translation_verse: Bavɛ banɔlũazazã gbagbla nɛ̀ besheshèɛ ɔkɔ editseɖe balɛ asɛ, “Bɔtanà bɔdɔ̃ ɔsɛ, bɔtɛ bàŋã bɔdɔ̃ nanà odiyinɔ ɔsɛ buyì Paulo.

english_verse: You and everyone in the council must go to the commander and pretend that you want to find out more about the charges against Paul. Ask for him to be brought before your court. Meanwhile, we will be waiting to kill him before he gets there.”
translation_verse: Kliso otsiɛnɔ, wɔnɔ nɛ̀ ɔhamɛ besheshèɛ ɔkɔ ɔsha ganà keyuiza anɔ gbagbla nabha tɛ̀ abhɛtɛ asɛ nɔkɔpɛ kibui mɛ dadalɛ nɛ yɛibuiɛ gɛ abhɛtɛ shu. Bɔlɔ xɔnɔ bɔbalɛ sɛ bɔ̀ba yii flogo pɛ ebekù ɛkɛlɛ.”

english_verse: When Paul's nephew heard about the plot, he went to the fortress and told Paul about it.
translation_verse: Pɔ kibeɛ gɛ Paulo yɛada yɛeyianyɛ elũ kibui kpa tɔtɔɖɔ ɛlɛ, avɛ buyuadɛdɛ ɔzãkɔ editseɖe Paulo.

english_verse: So Paul said to one of the army officers, “Take this young man to the commander. He has something to tell him.”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ Paulo ekulu keyuadɛ anɔ gbagbla tenikpo pɛ etseɖe yɛasɛ, “Gbana anyɛ ayisɔ̃ alɛ ɔvɛnɔ keyuiza anɔ gbagbla ɔkɔ, kibui tili lɛ yɛahɔɛ gɛ apɛ asɛ etseɖe yɛ.”

english_verse: The officer took him to the commander and said, “The prisoner named Paul asked me to bring this young man to you, because he has something to tell you.”
translation_verse: Kliso agbana yɛavɛnɔ keyuiza anɔ gbagbla ɔkɔ. Keyuadɛ anɔ gbagbla aɖɔ asɛ, “Paulo gɛenu ɔɖavũmɛ azazã yɛakpɛ banɔ nɛ mɛɔkɔ kɛlɛ etseɖe mɛ asɛ, ɛgbanà anyɛ ayisɔ̃ alɛ mana wɔkɔ ɔmɔsɛ kibui tili lɛ yɛahɔɛ gɛ apɛ asɛ aɖɔ etseɖe wɔ.”

english_verse: The commander took the young man aside and asked him in private, “What do you want to tell me?”
translation_verse: Keyuiza anɔ gbagbla anyanã anyɛ nuvɔɛ nɔ kahɔkpɔ, agbana yɛavɛnɔ kɛwɔa pɛ edibisi yɛasɛ, “Kibui ɖuwoe wɔpɛ ɔsɛ tɛ̀ otseɖe mɛ?”

english_verse: He answered, “Some men are planning to ask you to bring Paul down to the Jewish council tomorrow. They will claim they want to find out more about him.
translation_verse: Aɖɔ asɛ, “Yudanɛ adɛnyã ɛlɛ asɛ tɛ̀ bebisi edenɔ wɔ ahɔɛmɛ tɛ̀ ɔgbana Paulo mana ɔhamɛ besheshèɛ namɛ kivũ tɛ̀ abhɛtɛ asɛ gɛ onupɛ bakaapɛ kibui mɛ pɔpɔ̃ɛ edenɔ yɛɔkɔ ana.

english_verse: The commander sent the young man away after saying to him, “Don't let anyone know you told me this.” Paul Is Sent to Felix the Governor
translation_verse: Keyuiza anɔ gbagbla ashɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ ayisɔ̃ shu pɛ akpɛ ɔsha akɔɛ asɛ, “Titseɖe ananà asɛ ɔbɔkɔ mɛ kibui ɛlɛ tititì.” Balɔkɔ Paulo Asɛsɛ Kaesarea

english_verse: Get a horse ready for Paul and make sure he gets safely through to Felix the governor.”
translation_verse: Nɔpɛ ɛsɔ ɔkɔ Paulo tsyɛ tɛ̀ azã yɛabha asɛ tɛ̀ bagbana yɛavɛnɔ Felike gɛenu kɛmadi ekusiɛ nɛ ishu yɔyɔ mɛ.”

english_verse: The commander wrote a letter that said:
translation_verse: Afɔnɔ keplukpa alɛ asɛ,

english_verse: Greetings from Claudius Lysias to the Honorable Governor Felix:
translation_verse: Klauda Lisia, yɛafɔnɔ keplukpa alɛ, Ɛlɔkɔ sɛsɛ wɔ bɔlɔ kɛmadi ekusiɛ Felike anɔ gɛ adzã kiwuhũ. Ɛsɛnɛ wɔ.

english_verse: Some Jews grabbed this man and were about to kill him. But when I found out that he was a Roman citizen, I took some soldiers and rescued him.
translation_verse: Yudanɛ evũ anyɛ alɛ, kɛlɛ bapɛ asɛ tɛ̀ beyìi, pɔ mɛ nɛ̀ mɛ keyuiza bayɛkɛ yɛ nɛ balɛ ahɔɛ mɛ̀, ɔmɔsɛ ɛbatì ɔsɛ Romaeyi yɛenu.

english_verse: I wanted to find out what they had against him. So I brought him before their council
translation_verse: Ɛpɛ sɛ tɛ̀ mɛiti kibui nɔnɔ gɛ kɛlɛ sopɛ bebhui yɛulũɔ, kliso ɛgbana yɛ mana balɛ ɔhamɛ besheshèɛ ɔkɔ.

english_verse: and learned that the charges concern only their religious laws. This man isn't guilty of anything for which he should die or even be put in jail.
translation_verse: Ɛbamɔ ɔsɛ yɛulũɔ gɛ bebhuiɛ enu ebui bibisi yɛ nɛ balɛ ɔshaa shu, pɔ kibuiɛ gɛ balɔkɔ nanà ma gɛ kɛlɛ ɔsɛ beyii, alo tɛ̀ bakpɛ yɛ nɛ ɔɖavũ mɛ nɛ kɛlɛ ishu.

english_verse: As soon as I learned that there was a plot against him, I sent him to you and told their leaders to bring charges against him in your court.
translation_verse: Gɛ bakɔ mɛitì nɛ kibui kpa gɛ batɔɖɔ nɛ anyɛ alɛ shu soɔ, ɛɛlɔkɔ̀ yɛ sɛsɛ wɔ tsiɔ̃ ɔlɛ. Mɛtsyɛ kɔ bagɛ behui yɛulũ ɔsha asɛ, balɔkɔ kibuiɛ gɛ balɔkɔ ebhui yɛulũɔ mana wɔkɔ.

english_verse: The soldiers obeyed the commander's orders, and that same night they took Paul to the city of Antipatris.
translation_verse: Kliso gɛ buyuadɛ, abhɛtɛ tɔɖɔ kɛgɛ betseɖe alɛ nabhaa, bagbana Paulo aplɛnɔ balɛishu elishi alɛ kɛlɛ bavɛnɔ yɛ nɛ kitsí asɛ Antripatre ana.

english_verse: The next day the foot soldiers returned to the fortress and let the soldiers on horseback take him the rest of the way.
translation_verse: Gɛ keliɛ azaa, bakɔ bagɛ bedò ɛsɔ nɔ avɛ ɛplɛnɔ yɛ, kɛlɛ balɛ xɔnɔ etsodzì etsyina aba buyuadɛdɛ ɔzãkɔ.

english_verse: When they came to Caesarea, they gave the letter to the governor and handed Paul over to him.
translation_verse: Gɛ bagɛ bedo ɛsɔ nɔ ediku Kaesarea nɔ, balɔkɔ keplukpa akɔ kɛmadi ekusiɛ kɛlɛ balɔkɔ Paulo tsyɛ akpɛ ahɔɛ akɔɛ.

english_verse: The governor read the letter. Then he asked Paul and found out that he was from Cilicia.
translation_verse: Kɛmadi ekusiɛ abakalà keplukpa kɛlɛ ebisì yɛ kawɔ̃pɛ nɔnɔ gɛ edeɛ. Gɛ ebetì asɛ Kilikia yɛedeɛ,

english_verse: The governor said, “I will listen to your case as soon as the people come to bring their charges against you.” After saying this, he gave orders for Paul to be kept as a prisoner in Herod's palace.
translation_verse: aɖɔ asɛ, “Ɛbàlũ wuɛbuiɛ gɛ onupɛ bagɛ bebhui wɔlũɔ ebekù ɛkɛlɛ.” Kɛlɛ akɔ ɔsha sɛ̀ bezaepò Paulo nɛ Romanɛ ekusi Gbagbala ɔpã mɛ.

english_verse: Five days later Ananias the high priest, together with some of their leaders and a lawyer named Tertullus, went to the governor to present their case against Paul.
translation_verse: Ewui teti edeɛ, banɔlũazazã gbagbla Anania nɛ̀ Besheshèɛ teli abavɛ Kaisarea ɔplɛnɔ ɔshaetinɛ teli gɛ bezekulu yɛasɛ Tertulo, kɛlɛ balɔkɔ ebuiɛ gɛ bebhui Paulo ulũɔ nɔ amana kɛmadi ekusiɛ.
