english_verse: So Paul was called in, and Tertullus stated the case against him: Honorable Felix, you have brought our people a long period of peace, and because of your concern our nation is much better off.
translation_verse: Gɛ bedi kulu Paulo amanaa, Tertulo aɖɔ yɛulũ bhui bhui ebuiɛ etseɖe Felike asɛ, “Kibè dzololo nɛ ɛlɛ gɛ bɔkpasɛ wɔ esi nɛ ishu yɔyɔ mɛ, kɛlɛ gɛ ɔmɔ otugbàa mɛ sɛsɛ soɔ, ɔkɔsɛ bɔmɔ tìtsyitsyina gelee nɛ bɔlɔ kɛmadi ɛlɛ mɛ.

english_verse: I don't want to bother you, but please be patient with us and listen to me for just a few minutes.
translation_verse: Pɔ tɛ ɛtɛ kpɛ wɔluhũ vɛnɔ otugbàa, ɛba bisi odenɔ wɔkɔ ɔsɛ tɛ̀ ɔlɔkɔ ɛyamɛ gbangbã tɛ̀ ɔtaatɔ̃ nɛ̀ bɔlɔ kibui ɖɔɖɔ gulũi.

english_verse: This man has been found to be a real pest and troublemaker for our people all over the world. He is also a leader of a group called Nazarenes.
translation_verse: “Bɔbamɔ anyɛ alɛ asɛ enu anɔ gɛ akpɛ banɔ buluhũ, amana kititì lɔlɔ ebui nɛ Yudanɛ ishi nɛ kanɔ nɔ̀ mɛ petee. Enu Nazaretianɛ batɔ̃mɛ gagalĩ otugbà zazã anɔ teli

english_verse: When he tried to disgrace the temple, we arrested him. If you question him, you will find out for yourself that our charges are true.
translation_verse: pɛ akɔ ɔsha sɛ̀ bagɛ bebhui yɛulũɔ aba wɔkɔ. Onupɛ ɔnyɔ̃ yɛishuɔɔ, ɔbɔmɔ kibui gbã gɛ kpasɛ ebui alɛ nyɔ̃ɔ gɛ bɔlɔkɔ nɛishuɔ mɛ.”

english_verse: The Jewish crowd spoke up and agreed with what Tertullus had said. Paul Defends Himself
translation_verse: Yudanɛ akpɛ olugbũ nɛ ebuiɛ gɛ balɔkɔ ebhui yɛulũɔ mɛ, kɛlɛ beetuilɛ akpɛ asɛ ebuiɛ alɛ enu ebui gbã.

english_verse: The governor motioned for Paul to speak, and he began: I know that you have judged the people of our nation for many years, and I am glad to defend myself in your court.
translation_verse: Kibeɛ gɛ kɛmadi ekusiɛ akasɛ Paulo ahɔɛ asɛ aɖɔaɖɔɔ, ebuiliulũ asɛ, “Inyi ɔsɛ agɔ kpekpè nɛ alɛ gɛ okulu kebhu nɛ kɛmadi ɛlɛ mɛ, kliso pɛ ɛlɔkɔ same babu mɛishu ulũ.

english_verse: It was no more than twelve days ago that I went to worship in Jerusalem. You can find this out easily enough.
translation_verse: Asɛ gɛ olũ nɛ kibui ɛlɛ shuɔ, aka fala ewui lɔfɔ nɛ̀ taabha, gɛ ɛvɛ Yerusalem tɛ̀ mɛ dɛkpɛ kɛtakpu esi ɔkɔ Akaa.

english_verse: Never once did the Jews find me arguing with anyone in the temple. I didn't cause trouble in the synagogues or in the city itself.
translation_verse: Bagɛ ebhui mɛulũɔ agɛ bamɔ asɛ̀ ili wlɔãkɛ kibui nɛ̀ anana nɛ Akaavũɔ mɛ, alo sɛ ɛɛkpɛ ebelà nɛ banɔɛza nɛishi nɛ ɛtsyɔakɔ, alo ɔmamɛ ɔkɔ nanà tsyɛ.

english_verse: There is no way that they can prove these charges they are now bringing against me.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ batɛ balà ebù ebuiɛ gɛ alɛ balɔkɔ ebhui mɛulũ otsiɛ nɔ akɔwɔ.

english_verse: I admit that their leaders think the Lord's Way which I follow is based on wrong beliefs. But I still worship the same God that my ancestors worshiped. And I believe everything written in the Law of Moses and in the Prophets.
translation_verse: Pɔ, ɛdɛmɛ tɔɖɔ kɛlɛ abha asɛ ɛɛkpɛ kɛtakpu esi ɔkɔ bɔlɔ akaalɛ Owusoso Akaa asɛ anɔ gɛ etsiɖi Kidzo ɛlɛ gɛ bekulu otsiɛ sɛ̀ Nazaretianɛ batɔ̃mɛ gagalĩ otugbà zazã kidzoɔ. Ɛdɛmɛ tɔɖɔ kɛka nyia ka gɛ ɔsha ɖɔɔ nabha, gɛ kɛlɛ bafɔnɔ elú nɛ ebuiɖɔluanɛ keplukpa mɛ,

english_verse: I am just as sure as these people are that God will raise from death everyone, good or evil.
translation_verse: kɛlɛ kidzo tinikpo ɛlɛ mɛ mɛtsyɛ iɖi ɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa asɛ banɔ alɛ ana, asɛ tɛlɔlɔ odenɔ bekpùmɛ alɛ akɔ banɔ dadalɛ nɛ̀ atakpu mɛ gagalĩ eleteɛ.

english_verse: And because I am sure, I try my best to have a clear conscience in whatever I do for God or for people.
translation_verse: Kliso ɛwlɔãkɛ kibe nyia be ɔsɛ tɛ mɛ ekpeɖekpeɖe mɛ adalɛ nɛ Owusoso Akaa namɛ nɛ̀ anɔdzyrame shu.

english_verse: After being away for several years, I returned here to bring gifts for the poor people of my nation and to offer sacrifices.
translation_verse: “Gɛ agɔ kpekpè teli abafalaa, ɛba Yerusalem ɔsɛ tɛ ɛbakɔ betsyuitsyuiɛ bɔdɔ̃, nɛ̀ tɛ ɛlɔkɔ tuli tsyɛ kpɛ ahɔɛ asɛ bɔdɔ̃ kɔkɔ.

english_verse: This is what I was doing when I was found going through a ceremony in the temple. I wasn't with a crowd, and there was no uproar.
translation_verse: Mɛishu dalɛ nɛ ɔsha nulũ kibeɛ gɛ bamɔ mɛ nɛ Akaavũɔ shu pɛ ɛbhɛtɛ kibui ɛlɛ. Banɔɛza nanà agɛzã mɛɔbha, pɛ ebedzi asɛ ɛmana ebelà ebui tsyɛ.

english_verse: Some Jews from Asia were there at that time, and if they have anything to say against me, they should be here now.
translation_verse: Pɔ Yudanɛ teli ede Asia bɔdɔ̃ mɛ aba, gɛ kɛlɛ ɔsɛ bazã ɛkɛlɛ tɛ̀ bebhui mɛulũ nɛ wɔamɛ, gɛ onupɛ kibui tili lɛ balɛ ahɔɛ nɛ mɛishu flogo.

english_verse: Or ask the ones who are here. They can tell you that they didn't find me guilty of anything when I was tried by their own council.
translation_verse: Pɛnɔsɛ bagɛ balɛ alɛ ɛkɛlɛ, baɖɔ kibui kpa nɔnɔ gɛ bamɔ nɛ mɛishuɔ kibèɛ gɛ ɛlɔɛtití nɛ ɔhamɛ besheshèɛ amɛ-

english_verse: The only charge they can bring against me is what I shouted out in court, when I said, “I am on trial today because I believe that the dead will be raised to life!”
translation_verse: kibui nanà ma ɔdɛ kibui tinikpo ɛlɛ gɛ ɛzɛkpɛ ozĩ ɖɔ nɛ kibèɛ gɛ ɛlɔɛtití nɛ balɛ amɛ ɔsɛ, ‘Tɛlɔlɔ odenɔ bekpuɔ mɛ shu ibui sopɛ bekulù kebhu nimɛ nɛ wɔamɛ obuɔ.’ ”

english_verse: Felix knew a lot about the Lord's Way. But he brought the trial to an end and said, “I will make my decision after Lysias the commander arrives.”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ Felike anɔ gɛenyi Kidzo ɛlɛ piapii nɔ, alɔkɔ kebhuɔ atɔɖɔ kiwui bhlatɔ abha. Aɖɔ asɛ, “Onupɛ Lisia gɛenu agɛ yɛagbanà buyuadɛdɛ aba edenɔ Yerusalemuɔ tsyɛ, ɛbàɖi kibhuiɛ gɛ ɛbabhui wɔɛbuiɛ mɛ.”

english_verse: He then ordered the army officer to keep Paul under guard, but not to lock him up or to stop his friends from helping him. Paul Is Kept under Guard
translation_verse: Akpɛ keyuadɛdɛ anɔ gbagbla ɔsha asɛ tɛ̀ akɔ bazã epó Paulo piapii, pɔ akɔ yɛazã yɛishu ahɔɛ mɛ tɛ̀ akɔ yɛbhia alɛ ebeɖi yɛabha nɛ̀ kɛgɛ yɛapɛɛ nɔ.

english_verse: Several days later Felix and his wife Drusilla, who was Jewish, went to the place where Paul was kept under guard. They sent for Paul and listened while he spoke to them about having faith in Christ Jesus.
translation_verse: Ewui gelee edeɛ, Felike nɛ̀ yɛedzinì Drusila gɛenu Yudadziɛ aba. Akpɛ banɔ nɛ Paulo ɔkɔ kɛlɛ azataatɔ̃ niyɛ kibeɛ gɛ azaɖɔaɖɔ nɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã gɛlɛ Yesu Kristoɔ mɛ shuɔ.

english_verse: But Felix was frightened when Paul started talking to them about doing right, about self-control, and about the coming judgment. So he said to Paul, “That's enough for now. You may go. But when I have time I will send for you.”
translation_verse: Kibeɛ gɛ Paulo azaɖɔaɖɔ nɛ ishu dadalɛ, ishu abha ŋaŋã nɛ̀ kebhu kukuluɔ gɛ abaa, kifù yi Felike kɛlɛ abaɖɔ asɛ, “Abɔ otsiɛ gbɔ! Ɔbɔlà ɔshɛ. Onupɛ ɛbamɔ kibè tsyɛ ɛbà kpɛ banɔ nɛ wɔkɔ.”

english_verse: After this, Felix often sent for Paul and talked with him, because he hoped that Paul would offer him a bribe.
translation_verse: Kibè ɛlɛ tsyɛ ezeɖi kidzo mɛ asɛ Paulo abakɔɛ kɛdɔ̃ tili nɛ tikpukpũ mɛ asɛ tɛ ashɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛishu, kliso azakpɛ banɔ nɛ yɛɔkɔ ziazia pɛ azaɖɔaɖɔ niyɛ.

english_verse: Two years later Porcius Festus became governor in place of Felix. But since Felix wanted to do the Jewish leaders a favor, he kept Paul in jail.
translation_verse: Gɛ agɔ taabha abafalaa, Porkio Festo ebedzì kɛmadì ekusi nɛ Felike ede, pɔ gɛ Felike apɛ asɛ tɛ̀ ekù Yudanɛ awɔ̃ soɔ, ashɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ Paulo shu asɛ azã ɔɖavũɔ mɛ.

english_verse: Three days after Festus had become governor, he went from Caesarea to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Ewui tata ede gɛ ediku kesiakpa nabha, Festo alɔ ede kaisarea ewu avɛ Yerusalem,

english_verse: There the chief priests and some Jewish leaders told him about their charges against Paul. They also asked Festus
translation_verse: ɛkɛlɛ banɔlũazazã gbagbla nɛ̀ Yudanɛ otugbà banunɔ aba yɛɔkɔ nɛ̀ ebuiɛ gɛ balɔkɔ ebhui Paulo ulũ nɔ.

english_verse: if he would be willing to bring Paul to Jerusalem. They begged him to do this because they were planning to attack and kill Paul on the way.
translation_verse: Odunu ɔlɛ bebisi Festo kɛtɔ ɔsɛ tɛ̀ bekù yɛawɔ̃ tɛ̀ akɔ bebú Paulo asɛsɛ Yerusalem, ɔmɔsɛ bazã tikpukpũ mɛ pɛ bezepo yɛ, tɛ̀ beyii nɛ kidzoɔ mɛ.

english_verse: But Festus told them, “Paul will be kept in Caesarea, and I am soon going there myself.
translation_verse: Festo ebuiliulũ asɛ, “Paulo akpasɛ ɔɖavũ mɛ nɛ Kaisarea, kɛlɛ mɛtsyɛ ɛvɛ ɛkɛlɛ otsiɛ tsiɔ̃.

english_verse: If he has done anything wrong, let your leaders go with me and bring charges against him there.”
translation_verse: Nɔkɔ wɔnɔ otugbànɛ teli aba aplɛnɔ mɛ, tɛ̀ bɔlɔkɔ ebuiɛ nɛishu nɛ ɛkɛlɛ, tɛ̀ buɖì ɔsɛ̀ kibui kpa tili lɛ yɛishu dza.”

english_verse: Festus stayed in Jerusalem for eight or ten more days before going to Caesarea. Then the next day he took his place as judge and had Paul brought into court.
translation_verse: Gɛ aŋã ewui asɛ tasɛ alo kɛfɔ̀ nɛ balɛ ɔbhaa, ezhi avɛ Kaisarea nɛ̀ besheshèɛ, kɛlɛ gɛ keliɛ azaa, ɛkulu kebhu kazãa, kɛlɛ akɔ ɔsha asɛ bagbana Paulo amana yɛamɛ.

english_verse: As soon as Paul came in, the leaders from Jerusalem crowded around him and said he was guilty of many serious crimes. But they could not prove anything.
translation_verse: Kibeɛ gɛ Paulo ebekuɔ, Yudanɛ gɛede Yerusalem abaa ebeshogò nɛishu, pɛ balɔkɔ ebui gagalĩ nɛishu, ebuiɛ gɛ batɛ alà alɛ mɛ ebú tsyɛ.

english_verse: Then Paul spoke in his own defense, “I have not broken the Law of my people. And I have not done anything against either the temple or the Emperor.”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ Paulo ebu yɛishu ulũ asɛ, “Ɛgɛ bhɛtɛ kibui kpa nanà nɛ Yudanɛ ɔsha, alo Akaavũɔ alo Kaisaro shu.”

english_verse: Festus wanted to please the leaders. So he asked Paul, “Are you willing to go to Jerusalem and be tried by me on these charges?”
translation_verse: Festo gɛapɛ asɛ tɛ̀ ekù Yudanɛ awɔ̃ soɔ, etseɖe Paulo asɛ, “Ɔpɛ ɔsɛ tɛ̀ owù ɔvɛ Yerusalemuɔ tɛ mɛidì kulu kebhu nɛ̀wɔ nɛ ebui alɛ gɛ balɔkɔ nɛ wɔshuɔ nɛ ɛkɛlɛ dza?”

english_verse: Paul replied, “I am on trial in the Emperor's court, and that's where I should be tried. You know very well I have not done anything to harm the Jewish nation.
translation_verse: Paulo ebuiliulũ asɛ, “Kaisaro yɛ kebhu okulukɔ mɛ lɛ otsiɛ, ɛkɛlɛ kɛlɛ ɔsɛ bekulu kebhu nɛ mɛ. Ɛgɛ bhɛtɛ kibui kpa nanà nɛ̀ Yudanɛ shu, asɛ gɛ nɔonyi piapii nɔ.

english_verse: If I had done something deserving death, I would not ask to escape the death penalty. But I am not guilty of any of these crimes, and no one has the right to hand me over to these people. I now ask to be tried by the Emperor himself.”
translation_verse: Onupɛ edzi asɛ kibui kpa tili mɛbhɛtɛ pɛ kɛshɔnɔ ɔkɔ butsì tsyɛ elo, ɛgɛɖɔ ɔsɛ ititsì. Pɔ onupɛ edzì asɛ ebuiɛ gɛ Yudanɛ alɔkɔ nɛ mɛishu etinu ebui gbãa, anɔ nanà agɛmɔ kidzo tɛ̀ alɔkɔ mɛ akpɛ balɛ ahɔɛ mɛ. Ɛkɔ wɔ Kaisaro kɛtakpu ɔsɛ tɛ olũ mɛ kibui ɛlɛ!”

english_verse: After Festus had talked this over with members of his council, he told Paul, “You have asked to be tried by the Emperor, and to the Emperor you will go!” Paul Speaks to Agrippa and Bernice
translation_verse: Kibeɛ gɛ Festo adɛtɔ̃ kɛtakpu nɛ̀ yɛ ɔmaesheshèɛ ebekeɛ, adalɛ ɛlɛ mɛ asɛ, “ɔkɔ kɛtakpu ɔsɛ kebhuɔ avɛ Kaisaro ɔkɔ kliso, Kaisaro ɔkɔ wɔbɔ̀ vɛ!” Festo Adɛtɔ̃ Kɛtakpu Nɛ̀ Ekusi Agripa

english_verse: A few days later King Agrippa and Bernice came to Caesarea to visit Festus.
translation_verse: Ewui gulũi tieli edeɛ, Ekusi Agripa nɛ̀ Bernike ebekù Kaisarea tɛ̀ badɛ sɛnɔ Festo.

english_verse: They had been there for several days, when Festus told the king about the charges against Paul. He said: Felix left a man here in jail,
translation_verse: Gɛ babàzã ɛkɛlɛ ewui kpekpè mɛ soɔ, Festo adɛ tsaɖakɛ Paulo ebuiɛ gɛ balɔkɔ nɛishuɔ nɛ̀ Ekusiɛ. Aɖɔ asɛ, “Anyɛ teli alɛ ɛkɛlɛ gɛ Felike ashɛnɛ ahɔɛ elù asɛ ɔɖavũmɛ azazã.

english_verse: and when I went to Jerusalem, the chief priests and the Jewish leaders came and asked me to find him guilty.
translation_verse: Kibeɛ gɛ ɛbawù ɔvɛ Yerusalemuɔ, banɔlũazazã gbagbla nɛ̀ Yudanɛ besheshèɛ alɔkɔ ebui nɛ yɛishu, kɛlɛ bezebisi kidzo asɛ tɛ̀ beyìi.

english_verse: I told them it isn't the Roman custom to hand a man over to people who are bringing charges against him. He must first have the chance to meet them face to face and to defend himself against their charges.
translation_verse: “Itseɖe balɛ ɔsɛ, kitinu Romanɛ ɔkɔ̀ ɔsɛ tɛ̀ balɔkɔ anɔ nanà akpɛ ahɔɛ tɛ̀ atsyɔa kitemɛ bamɛ nɛ̀ bamɛ, nɛ̀ bagɛ bebhui yɛuluɔ flogo tɛ̀ amɔ kidzò edibu yɛishu ulũ, nɛ̀ ebuiɛ gɛ balɔkɔ nɛishuɔ.

english_verse: So when they came here with me, I wasted no time. On the very next day I took my place on the judge's bench and ordered him to be brought in.
translation_verse: Gɛ mɛ nɛ alɛ ba ɛkɛlɛ̀, ɛgɛ kɔ kebhuɔ awɔ̀ kidè, pɔ ikulu kebhu kazãa nɛ kelizaza mɛ pɛ ɛkɔ ɔsha sɛ̀ bagbana anyɛ amana.

english_verse: But when the men stood up to make their charges against him, they did not accuse him of any of the crimes that I thought they would.
translation_verse: Kibeɛ gɛ bagɛ bebhui yɛulũ alɔɛtití asɛ tɛ baɖɔaɖɔɔ, bagɛ ìbhui yɛulũ nɛ̀ kibui kpa nanà shu asɛ kana gɛ ɖiɛ ana.

english_verse: Instead, they argued with him about some of their beliefs and about a dead man named Jesus, who Paul said was alive.
translation_verse: Pɔ, ebuiɛ teli gɛ lakpà aplɛnɔ balɛ ɛkɔɔ baza wlɔãkɛ niyɛ, nɛ̀ anyɛ teli gɛetitsí pɛ beekulu yɛasɛ Yesuɔ shu, anɔ gɛ Paulo xɔnɔ aɖɔ asɛ akalɛ nɛkpã.

english_verse: Since I did not know how to find out the truth about all this, I asked Paul if he would be willing to go to Jerusalem and be put on trial there.
translation_verse: Itinyi kɛgɛ babhɛtɛ nɛ ebui alɛ nɛidi mɛ nyɔnyɔ̃ shu, kliso ibisi yɛ ɔsɛ, adɛmɛ asɛ abavɛ Yerusalemuɔ tɛ̀ bedi kulu kebhu niyɛ, nɛ̀ ebui alɛ gɛ balɔkɔ nɛ yɛishuɔ dza?

english_verse: But Paul asked to be kept in jail until the Emperor could decide his case. So I ordered him to be kept here until I could send him to the Emperor.
translation_verse: Kibeɛ gɛ Paulo ebisi asɛ tɛ̀ bashɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛishu ɔkɔ kɛmadi ekusi gbagbla tɛ̀ ebhui kibui mɛ kɔyɛɛ, akɔ ɔsha asɛ, batɛ̀ shɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛishu odiyinɔ ɔsɛ̀ ɛlɔkɔ yɛ sɛsɛ Kaisaro.”

english_verse: Then Agrippa said to Festus, “I would also like to hear what this man has to say.” Festus answered, “You can hear him tomorrow.”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ Agripa etseɖe Festo asɛ, “Mɛtsyɛ bapɛ sɛ tɛ̀ ilũ anyɛ alɛ ebuiɛ.” Ebuiliulũ asɛ, “Kivũ tsyɛ òbolũ yɛebuiɛ.” Paulo Avɛ Agripa Amɛ

english_verse: The next day Agrippa and Bernice made a big show as they came into the meeting room. High ranking army officers and leading citizens of the town were also there. Festus then ordered Paul to be brought in
translation_verse: Gɛ keliɛ azaa, Agripa nɛ̀ Bernike aba, nɛ bɔdɔ̃ kpɛkpɛ gbangbã mɛ̀, kɛlɛ yɛ nɛ̀ ɛzãkɔ ɖaɖama eleteɛ nɛ̀ bagɛ baagbanà ɔmaa etsoku buvũɔ mɛ gɛ bebèlũ kibuiɛ. Nɛ Festo ɔshakɔkɔ mɛ̀ koɔ, kɛlɛ badɛgbanà Paulo amana.

english_verse: and said: King Agrippa and other guests, look at this man! Every Jew from Jerusalem and Caesarea has come to me, demanding for him to be put to death.
translation_verse: Festo aɖɔ asɛ, “Ekusi Agripa, nɛ̀ bagɛ alɛ bɔlɔ ɔbha nɛ ɛkɛlɛ, nɔɔmɔ anyɛ alɛ dza! Yudanɛ petee, ebhui yɛulu nɛ Yerusalem nɛ̀ Kaisarea ɛkɛlɛ, nɛ ozĩ kpɛkpɛ shu gɛ kɛma ɔsɛ tɛ̀ ewlui azã nɛkpã.

english_verse: I have not found him guilty of any crime deserving death. But because he has asked to be judged by the Emperor, I have decided to send him to Rome.
translation_verse: Ɛbamɔ ɔsɛ̀ agɛ bhɛtɛ kibui kpa nanà, gɛ kɛshɔnɔ ɔkɔ butsí, pɔ gɛ ebisi asɛ kɛmadi Ekusi Gbagbla elũ yɛibuiɛ soɔ, ɛbhɛtɛ ekpeɖekpeɖe mɛ ɔsɛ ɛbalɔkɔ yɛ sɛsɛ Roma.

english_verse: I have to write some facts about this man to the Emperor. So I have brought him before all of you, but especially before you, King Agrippa. After we have talked about his case, I will then have something to write.
translation_verse: Pɔ kibui nanà pɔtìi ma mahɔɛ gɛ ɛba fɔnɔ nɛishu sɛsɛ Ekusi Ohumi eleteɛ. Kliso ɛgbana yɛ mana wɔnɔ petee amɛ, asɛ wɔ Ekusi Agripa amɛ ana, sɛ tɛ̀ odenɔ kibui ɛlɛ mɛ nyɔnyɔ̃ niyɛɛ, ɛbà mɔ kibui tili fɔnɔ.

english_verse: It makes no sense to send a prisoner to the Emperor without stating the charges against him.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ gɛnɛ bubuti ibui kinù ɔsɛ tɛ̀ ɛlɔkɔ ɔɖavũmɛ azazã ɔsɛsɛ gɛ onupɛ ɛgɛ mɔ kibui nɔnɔ gɛ balɔkɔ ebhui yɛulũ nɔ.”

english_verse: Agrippa told Paul, “You may now speak for yourself.” Paul stretched out his hand and said:
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ Agripa etseɖe Paulo asɛ, “Kidzo lɛ kɔwɔ ɔsɛ tɛ̀ ɔɖɔ kaɖɔ obù wɔshu ulũ nɔ̀.” Kliso Paulo ebú yɛɔhɔɛ akasɛ pɛ akpà yɛishu ulũ bú asɛ,

english_verse: King Agrippa, I am glad for this chance to defend myself before you today on all these charges my own people have brought against me.
translation_verse: Ekusi Agripa, ɛmɔsɛ mɛtakpu pɛ̃, gɛ ɛlɔɛtití nɛ wɔamɛ oboɔ, pɛ ibù mɛishu ulũ nɛ ebuiɛ petee gɛ Yudanɛ alɔkɔ ebhui mɛulũɔ nɔ shu,

english_verse: You know a lot about our religious customs and the beliefs that divide us. So I ask you to listen patiently to me.
translation_verse: ɛɖɔ anà ɔmɔsɛ, onyi Yudanɛ ɛkɔɔ piapii nɛ̀ ebui nɔnɔ gɛ alɔɛtití nɛ ɛlɛ shu. Klisoɔ, ɛkɔwɔ kɛtakpu ɔsɛ tɛ̀ olù kitu esi tɛ̀ olũ mɛ kibuiɛ.

english_verse: All the Jews have known me since I was a child. They know what kind of life I have lived in my own country and in Jerusalem. And if they were willing, they could tell you I was a Pharisee, a member of a group that is stricter than any other.
translation_verse: Benyi mɛ kibè dzlolo nɛ ɛlɛ, kɛlɛ babalà aɖɔaɖɔ adalɛ kɛlɛ shu, onupɛ badɛmɛ, sɛ idzì Farisieyi, banɔ gɛ bagagalĩ nɛ ɔshaa gɛ̀ ɛlɛ kpà plɛnɔ bɔlɔ ɛkɔɔ shuɔ mɛ.

english_verse: Now I am on trial because I believe the promise God made to our people long ago.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ otsiɛ nɔ, kidzoɔ mɛ gɛiɖi ɔkɔ kɛgɛ Owusoso Akaa atɔɖɔ elú akɔ bɔlɔ akaalɛ sopɛ beekulu kebhu nɛ̀ mɛ oboɔ.

english_verse: Day and night our twelve tribes have earnestly served God, waiting for his promised blessings. King Agrippa, because of this hope, some of our leaders have brought charges against me.
translation_verse: Kibui tɔtɔɖɔlu ɛlɛ shù, bɔlɔ egbogbo lɔfɔ nɛ̀ taabha eɖì kidzo mɛ ɔsɛ kɛbà ba ɛlɛ mɛ sopɛ baakpɛ kɛtakpu esi akɔ Owusoso Akaa kemuze nɛ̀ elishi. O Ekusi, kibui ɛlɛ gɛ buɖì kidzo mɛ kɔ sopɛ Yudanɛ alɔkɔ kibui nɛ mɛishu.

english_verse: Why should any of you doubt that God raises the dead to life?
translation_verse: Kiɖeso pɛ kɛlɛ ɔsɛ wɔnɔ ɔmayɛmɛ teli, akɔ ɛlɛ kɛsɛnɛ ibui ɔsɛ Owusoso Akaa efeke bekpuɔ?

english_verse: I once thought that I should do everything I could to oppose Jesus from Nazareth.
translation_verse: “Mɛtsyɛ kibui ɛlɛ gɛikù mawɔ flogo, kliso ɛbhɛtɛ kɛka nyia gɛ ɛbalà tɛ ɛlɔɛtití nɛ Yesu Nazaretieyiɛ shu.

english_verse: I did this first in Jerusalem, and with the authority of the chief priests I put many of God's people in jail. I even voted for them to be killed.
translation_verse: Kibui ɛlɛ mɛzɛ bhɛtɛ nɛ Yerusalemuɔ. Ɔganà banɔlũazazã gbagbla nabhaa, ɛkpɛ banɔ dadalɛ gelee nɛ ɔɖavũ mɛ, kɛlɛ gɛ bezeyi alɛ, ɛzɛ tɔɖɔ ahɔɛ nɛ kɛlɛ abha.

english_verse: I often had them punished in our synagogues, and I tried to make them give up their faith. In fact, I was so angry with them, that I went looking for them in foreign cities.
translation_verse: Ewuimɛ ewuimɛɛ, ɛlɔ odenɔ ɛtsyɔakɔ ɔvɛnɔ ɛbhlatɔ ɔsɛ tɛ bapɔ̃ batɔ̃ akɔ alɛ, kɛlɛ izitũilɛ kpɛ balɛulũ asɛ tɛ̀ baɖɔ ebusoebui. Mɛ ɔmɔpɔɛ nɔ nɛ balɛishu soɔ, ɛtɔnɔ baliede vɛ amadi bhlatɔ tsyɛ mɛ.

english_verse: King Agrippa, one day I was on my way to Damascus with the authority and permission of the chief priests.
translation_verse: “Nɛ kidzo ɛlɛ tinikpo gagã mɛɛ, ɛzã kidzo mɛ ɛvɛ Damaskoɔ nɛ banɔlũazazã gbagbla ɔsha kɔkɔ ulũ.

english_verse: About noon I saw a light brighter than the sun. It flashed from heaven on me and on everyone traveling with me.
translation_verse: Oo, ekusi asɛ ɛɖàtata lɔfɔ nɛ̀ tɛbha mɛ anaa, gɛ itsiɖi kidzo nabha vɛɛ, ɔmɔ kifù odenɔ kititiɛ gɛ kitsè dɛ kiwuiɛ, pɛ kɛba kanà mɛ nɛ̀ mɛɔbhia alɛ.

english_verse: We all fell to the ground. Then I heard a voice say to me in Aramaic, “Saul, Saul, why are you so cruel to me? It's foolish to fight against me!”
translation_verse: Bɔlɔ nyɔ̃ɔ wɔlɛdɔ kesiakpa, kɛlɛ ilũ kaɖɔ tieli gɛ ketseɖe mɛ ebuiɛ nɛ Aramaik buluhũɔ mɛ asɛ, ‘Saulo, Saulo, kiɖe sopɛ ɔtɔnɔ miede ana? Abagagalĩ akɔwɔ tɛ ɔlɔkɔ wɔtsɛɖɛ tá ɔɖà tsɔtsɔ̃.’

english_verse: “Who are you?” I asked. Then the Lord answered, “I am Jesus! I am the one you are so cruel to.
translation_verse: “Kɛlɛ mɛibisi ɔsɛ Apalete, wɔonu afana?’ Apaleteɛ ebuiliulũ asɛ, ‘Mɛinu Yesu, anɔ gɛ yiede wɔtɔnɔɔ.’

english_verse: Now stand up. I have appeared to you, because I have chosen you to be my servant. You are to tell others what you have learned about me and what I will show you later.”
translation_verse: ‘Otsi alɛ lɔzã wɔɛtsɛɖɛ abha. Ibu mɛishu kasɛ wɔ, ɔsɛ tɛ̀ ibu wɔ asɛ axɔ̃ɛdɛ, nɛ̀ asɛ anɔ gɛ abaɖɔaɖɔ, nɛ kɛgɛ wɔmɔ nɛ mɛishuɔ, nɛ̀ kɛgɛ ɛzɛbà bùkasɛ wɔ shu.

english_verse: The Lord also said, “I will protect you from the Jews and from the Gentiles that I am sending you to.
translation_verse: Ɛbà yɛkɛ akɔwɔ odenɔ wɔanunɔ, nɛ̀ bagɛ betinu Yudanɛ ahɔɛ mɛ. Ɛɛkpɛ̀ wɔ nɛ balɛ ɔkɔ

english_verse: I want you to open their eyes, so they will turn from darkness to light and from the power of Satan to God. Then their sins will be forgiven, and by faith in me they will become part of God's holy people.”
translation_verse: tɛ̀ ɔkpà balɛ awuhũɔ, tɛ̀ otsyinà alɛ odenɔ otumũ mɛ̀, ɔmana kanɔ dadalɛ mɛ̀, nɛ̀ odenɔ Satana yɛohumi ahɔɛ mɛ̀, ɔmana Owusoso Akaa ɔkɔ. Sɛ tɛ̀ balɔkɔ ebui kpa etsye alɛ, tɛ̀ bamɔ ɔzãkɔ nɛ bagɛ badalɛ balɛ ishu ɔganà mɛabha tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛ.’

english_verse: King Agrippa, I obeyed this vision from heaven.
translation_verse: “Kliso nɔ̀, Ekusi Agripa, ɛgɛ gagalĩ batɔ̃mɛ nɛ kibuiɛ gɛ bebu akasɛ mɛ odenɔ kititíɛ shu.

english_verse: First I preached to the people in Damascus, and then I went to Jerusalem and all over Judea. Finally, I went to the Gentiles and said, “Stop sinning and turn to God! Then prove what you have done by the way you live.”
translation_verse: Iputɔ dɛ bagɛ alɛ Damasko ɔkɔ, kɛlɛ ɛbavɛ Yerusalem nɛ̀ Yudea petee, nɛ̀ bagɛ betinu Yudanɛ ɔkɔ tsyɛ, ɛɖɔ kibuiɛ tseɖe balɛ ɔsɛ̀ tɛ betsyina avɛ Owusoso Akaa ɔkɔ, tɛ̀ bakasɛ balɛ tìtsyitsyina ɔganà ebui bhɛbhɛtɛ mɛ.

english_verse: That is why some men grabbed me in the temple and tried to kill me.
translation_verse: Kibui ɛlɛ shu sopɛ Yudanɛ evũ mɛ̀, nɛ Akaavũɔ shu, pɛ bapɛ asɛ tɛ̀ beyì mɛ.

english_verse: But all this time God has helped me, and I have preached both to the rich and to the poor. I have told them only what the prophets and Moses said would happen.
translation_verse: Pɔ Owusoso Akaa aplɛnɔ mɛ, oboyinɔ obo kiwui ɛlɛ abha, kliso ɛlɔɛtití nɛ ɛkɛlɛ pɛ ɛdalɛ ebui mɛ ɔkɔ bunɔ bhlui bhlui nɛ̀ behohoeɛ petee shɔshɔɛ. Ɛtɛɛɖɔ kibui nanà falanɔ kɛgɛ ebuiɖɔluanɛ nɛ̀ Mose aɖɔ elúɔ asɛ kɛbàba ɛlɛ mɛ shu,

english_verse: I told them how the Messiah would suffer and be the first to be raised from death, so he could bring light to his own people and to the Gentiles.
translation_verse: sɛ Kristoɔ abamɔ bɔtɔ, kɛlɛ asɛ anɔ gɛiputɔ gɛ abàlɔ edenɔ bekpuɔ nɛishi, abaɖɔ kanɔ mɛ dadalɛ ɛbui etseɖe yɛanɔ nɔ̀ nɛ̀ bagɛ betinu Yudanɛ.”

english_verse: Before Paul finished defending himself, Festus shouted, “Paul, you're crazy! Too much learning has driven you out of your mind.”
translation_verse: Gɛ aɖɔaɖɔ eku ɛkɛlɛ, Festo abaɖɔaɖɔ ebhui Paulo kibuiɛ nulũ. Akpɛ ozĩ asɛ, “Paulo, ɔma piapii, wɔ bɔdɔ̃ gbagbla tɛkpakpasɛ pɛ sɛ kɔ wɔamɛ ezĩ.”

english_verse: But Paul replied, “Honorable Festus, I am not crazy. What I am saying is true, and it makes sense.
translation_verse: Paulo ebuiliulũ asɛ, “Mamɛ etiezĩ, bɔlɔ Ekusi Gbagbla Festo, kibuiɛ gɛ ɛɖɔɔ, nu kibui gbã kɛlɛ kesilulũ alɛ kɛlɛ mɛ.

english_verse: None of these things happened off in a corner somewhere. I am sure that King Agrippa knows what I am talking about. That's why I can speak so plainly to him.”
translation_verse: Ekusiɛ enyi ebui alɛ, kɛlɛ ɛbalà ɖɔ kaɖɔ nɛ alɛishu kɔɛ fãa. Inyi sɛ ebui alɛ ɔmayɛmɛ nanà ama matì mati mɛ ɔkɔɛ, ɔmɔsɛ batɛ abhɛtɛ alɛ nɛ okpukpũ ɔkɔ.

english_verse: Then Paul said to Agrippa, “Do you believe what the prophets said? I know you do.”
translation_verse: Ekusi Agripa, ɔyɛkɛ ebuiɖɔluanɛ nabha aŋã dza? Inyi sɛ ɔyɛkɛ ɔŋã.”

english_verse: Agrippa asked Paul, “In such a short time do you think you can talk me into being a Christian?”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ Agripa ebisi Paulo asɛ, “Ɔyɛkɛ ɔŋã asɛ nɛ kibè gulũi ɛlɛ mɛɛ, ɔbɔlàwɔɛ mɛulũ pɛ ɛbàdzi Kristoeyi dza?”

english_verse: Paul answered, “Whether it takes a short time or a long time, I wish you and everyone else who hears me today would become just like me! Except, of course, for these chains.”
translation_verse: Paulo ebuiliulũ asɛ, “Kibeɛ dzi gulũio kizidze tsyɛ elo, itikulù Owusoso Akaa ɔkɔ wɔ ete, pɔ ikului ɔkɔ bagɛ ataatɔ̃ nɛ̀ mɛ oboɔ petee ɔsɛ tɛ bazã asɛ mɛ ana, pɔ batɛ̀kpɛ̀ ɛɖahui ɛlɛ nɛ balɛ xɔnɔ shu.”

english_verse: Then King Agrippa, Governor Festus, Bernice, and everyone who was with them got up.
translation_verse: Ekusiɛ alɔ, yɛ nɛ̀ kɛmadì ekusiɛ nɛ̀ Bernike ɔplɛnɔ bagɛ bazã ɛkɛlɛ aplɛnɔ alɛ.

english_verse: But before they left, they said, “This man isn't guilty of anything. He doesn't deserve to die or to be put in jail.”
translation_verse: Bekoeyì nɛ buvũɔ mɛ, kɛlɛ gɛ bazaɖɔaɖɔ nɛ̀ bagɔlɔ mɛɛ, baɖɔ asɛ, “Anyɛ alɛ atɛ abhɛtɛ kibui nanà gɛ shɔnɔ ɔkɔ butsí alo ɔɖavũmɛ zazã.”

english_verse: Agrippa told Festus, “Paul could have been set free, if he had not asked to be tried by the Roman Emperor.”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ Agripa etseɖe Festo asɛ, “Pɛ otɛɔsɛ anyɛ alɛ agɛ vɛnɔ kibuiɛ nɛ Romanɛ Ekusi Gbagbla ɔkɔɔ, tsyɛalo bɔtà shɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ yɛishu.

english_verse: When it was time for us to sail to Rome, Captain Julius from the Emperor's special troops was put in charge of Paul and the other prisoners.
translation_verse: Kibeɛ gɛ bebhui kibui mɛ ɔsɛ bɔbàgã kelĩabha kidzo ɔvɛ Italia nɔ̀, balɔkɔ Paulo nɛ̀ ɔɖavũmɛ azazã anɔ teli kpɛ ahɔɛ akɔ keyuiza anɔ gbagbla gɛ alɛ buyuadɛdɛ akpã ulũ pɛ bezekulu yɛasɛ Yulio ahɔɛ mɛ, anɔ gɛ ewuli akà kpasɛ keyuiza ovolo toli mɛ.

english_verse: We went aboard a ship from Adramyttium that was about to sail to some ports along the coast of Asia. Aristarchus from Thessalonica in Macedonia sailed on the ship with us.
translation_verse: Buku kelĩabhaohu odenɔ Adramiteo, ɔsɛ tɛ̀ bɔgã aƒuɔ ogbogboɔ nɛ kelĩabhahuɔ ɔtɔkɔ gɛlɛ Asia bɔdɔ̃ mɛ, kɛlɛ bɔbàlɔ tɔnɔ aƒuabha idzoɔ. Aristaxo gɛenu Makedonia eyì gɛede Tesalonika tsyɛ azã aplɛnɔ ɔlɔ.

english_verse: The next day we came to shore at Sidon. Captain Julius was very kind to Paul. He even let him visit his friends, so they could give him whatever he needed.
translation_verse: Gɛ keliɛ azaa, budizhi nɛ Sidon, kɛlɛ Yulio abhɛtɛ ɛyamɛ gbangbã akɔ Paulo, kɛlɛ akɔ yɛidzo asɛ avɛ yɛɔbhia alɛ ɔkɔ tɛ̀ bakɔyɛ kɛdɔ̃ nɔnɔ gɛ apɛɛ nɔ̀.

english_verse: When we left Sidon, the winds were blowing against us, and we sailed close to the island of Cyprus to be safe from the wind.
translation_verse: Denɔ ɛkɛlɛ, bɔzɛlɔ tɔnɔ kelĩabha kidzoɔ kɛlɛ aƒuilɛ nɔ lɔkɔ ɔlɔ ɔvɛnɔ Kipro ogbogbòɔ ɔmɔsɛ aƒuilɛ nɔ̀ yalɛ̀ bɔlɔ.

english_verse: Then we sailed south of Cilicia and Pamphylia until we came to the port of Myra in Lycia.
translation_verse: Gɛ bɔgã tsakɛ aƒuɔ gɛ akpasɛ Kilikia ogbogbò ibeɛ nɛ̀ Pamfilia nɔ̀, budizhi nɛ Mira gɛ akpasɛ Likia mɛ.

english_verse: There the army captain found a ship from Alexandria that was going to Italy. So he ordered us to board that ship.
translation_verse: Ɛkɛlɛ keyuiza anɔ gbagbla amɔ Aleksandro kelĩabhaohu gɛ lɔvɛ Italia, kɛlɛ akɔ bukù ɔlɛ.

english_verse: We sailed along slowly for several days and had a hard time reaching Cnidus. The wind would not let us go any farther in that direction, so we sailed past Cape Salmone, where the island of Crete would protect us from the wind.
translation_verse: Bɔgã kidzoɔ kɛbɔɛ ɛbɔɛ nɛ ewui gelee mɛ kɛlɛ bɔmɔ bɔtɔ flogo pɛ̀ budiku Knido. Gɛ kɛlɛɛ fufùɔ gɛkɔ bɔlɔ kidzò ɔsɛ tɛ̀ bɔvɛ̀ otugbàa, bɔgã Kreta aƒuɔ ogbogbòɔ gɛ lotsyinà kede ɔkpɛ Salmoniɛ.

english_verse: We went slowly along the coast and finally reached a place called Fair Havens, not far from the town of Lasea.
translation_verse: Bɔgã ogbogbò ɔlɛ nɛ bɔtɔ̀ mɛ, kɛlɛ budiku ɔkɔ toli gɛ bekulu asɛ, Ɔtsɛkɔ Gbangbã gɛ lotsò ɔplɛnɔ Lasea ɔmãa.

english_verse: By now we had already lost a lot of time, and sailing was no longer safe. In fact, even the Great Day of Forgiveness was past.
translation_verse: Butizĩ kibè bafala nɔ, kɛlɛ kelĩabha idzo gagã tsyɛ zikuifù, ɔmɔsɛ olugbũ nyanyã gɛonu Tɛdɔdɔfala Kiwuiɛ tɛfala. Kliso Paulo akpɛ ɔshà akɔ alɛ asɛ,

english_verse: Then Paul spoke to the crew of the ship, “Men, listen to me! If we sail now, our ship and its cargo will be badly damaged, and many lives will be lost.”
translation_verse: “Baanyɛ mɔ, ɛmɔsɛ bɔlɔ kelĩabha kidzogagã ɛlɛ bamanà buluhũ, kɛlɛ bɔlɔ kelĩabhaohuɔ, etukuɔ, nɛ̀ bɔlɔ ɛkpã tsyɛ bazĩ.”

english_verse: But Julius listened to the captain of the ship and its owner, rather than to Paul.
translation_verse: Pɔ keyuiza anɔ gbagbla, gɛ agɛ pɛ asɛ tɛ̀ elũ Paulo ibuiɛ soɔ, avɛ edilũ agɛ yɛegè kelĩabhaohuɔ nɛ̀ ɔlɛ elete nɛibuiɛ.

english_verse: The harbor at Fair Havens wasn't a good place to spend the winter. Because of this, almost everyone agreed that we should at least try to sail along the coast of Crete as far as Phoenix. It had a harbor that opened toward the southwest and northwest, and we could spend the winter there. The Storm at Sea
translation_verse: Gɛ kelĩabhaohuɔ ɔtsɛkɔ ɔtɛpɛ̃ nɛ ekukù mɛ soɔ, banɔ kpekpèɛ abadɛmɛ atɔɖɔ ɛlɛ abha sɛ bɔvɛ kidzoɔ tɛgagã nabha, kɛlɛ buziɖi kidzo mɛ ɔsɛ tɛ̀ budiku Foenike tɛ̀ bɔzã ɛkɛlɛ nɛ ekukùɔ mɛ. Ɛkɛlɛ onù kelĩabhaohu ɔtɔkɔ nɛ Kreta gɛ obu bamɛ ɔkpɛ kanɔ nɔ̀ bɔpialɛ gɛɔvɛ kiwuiɛ ɔdɔkɔ ɛbé nɛ̀ kanɔ nɔ̀ bɔpiabha ɔkɔ gɛɔvɛ̀ kiwuiɛ ɔdɔkɔ ɛbeɛ. Kɛlɛɛ Gagalĩ Tifufù

english_verse: When a gentle wind from the south started blowing, the men thought it was a good time to do what they had planned. So they pulled up the anchor, and we sailed along the coast of Crete.
translation_verse: Gɛ kɛlɛɛ gidè kanɔ nɔ̀ bɔpialɛ ibeɛ kpɛ tifufù esi kɛbɔɛ ɛbɔɛ nɔ, abhɛtɛ balɛ xɔnɔ asɛ bamɔ kɛgɛ bapɛ̀ɛ nɔ̀, klisoɔ bebú ɔɖaa gɛ lɔnyanà kelĩabhaohuɔ kɛlɛ bɔgã kelĩabha kidzoɔ ɔvɛ̀ Kreta ogbogbòɔ.

english_verse: The wind struck the ship, and we could not sail against it. So we let the wind carry the ship.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛɛ nɔ vũ kelĩabhaohuɔ kɛlɛ lɔzɔkɔ̀ làvɛ otugbà kliso bashɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ ɔlɛ shu pɛ kɛlɛɛ nɔ̀ gbana ɔlɛ̀ vɛnɔ.

english_verse: We went along the island of Cauda on the side that was protected from the wind. We had a hard time holding the lifeboat in place,
translation_verse: Gɛ bɔdɛ falà nɛ ogbogbò tolì nɛ aƒu kesiakpa gɛ bekulu asɛ Klauda shuɔ, abagagalĩ akɔ bɔlɔ flogo pɛ bɔbà kpakà ɛkpã ohu ɔlɛ.

english_verse: but finally we got it where it belonged. Then the sailors wrapped ropes around the ship to hold it together. They lowered the sail and let the ship drift along, because they were afraid it might hit the sandbanks in the gulf of Syrtis.
translation_verse: Gɛ banyɛ akpakà ɔlɛ elu ebekeɛ, baganà ihuiɛ nɛ kelĩabhaohuɔ niesi pɛ banyana ɔlɛ evunɔ. Gɛ kifù sisi zã ɔsɛ ayisɛ ebevũ ɔlɛ nɛ Libia soɔ, beboele ɛɖaa gɛ kpakà kelĩabhaohuɔ, pɛ kɛlɛɛ nɔ̀ gbanà ɔlɛ vɛnɔ.

english_verse: The storm was so fierce that the next day they threw some of the ship's cargo overboard.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛɛ fufùɔ hɔhɔlɛ bɔlɔ gɛ akɔ asɛ gɛ keliɛ abazaa, bakpà etukuɔ ebú atɛɛ akpɛ aƒuɔ mɛ.

english_verse: Then on the third day, with their bare hands they threw overboard some of the ship's gear.
translation_verse: Nɛ kiwui tramɛ nabhaa, bebu ɛɖaa nɛ̀ ihuiɛ gɛ nyanà kelĩabhaohuɔ ovunɔɔ atɛɛ akpɛ aƒuɔ mɛ nɛ̀ balɛ ahɔɛ.

english_verse: For several days we could not see either the sun or the stars. A strong wind kept blowing, and we finally gave up all hope of being saved.
translation_verse: Gɛ kiwuiɛ nɛ̀ awɔɛɛ etikekoeyì nɛ ewui gelee mɛ pɛ kɛlɛɛ nɔ̀ pɔ kafùɔ, bɔgaɖi idzomɛ ɔsɛ bɔbà zã nɛ̀ ɛkpã.

english_verse: Since none of us had eaten anything for a long time, Paul stood up and told the men: You should have listened to me! If you had stayed on in Crete, you would not have had this damage and loss.
translation_verse: Gɛ banyɛ agɛŋã bɔdɔ̃ nɛ ewui gelee mɛ nɛ kɛlɛɛ nɔ gifùɔ shu soɔ, Paulo abalɔɛtití nɛ balɛishi pɛ aɖɔ asɛ, “Banyɛ mɔ, nɔotolũ mɛibuiɛ gɛ ɛɖɔ ɔsɛ tɛ butì gè kelĩabhaohuɔ nɛ Kreta nɔ̀, tsyɛ alo bɔtɔ ɔlɛ̀ nɛ̀ bɔdɔ̃ gizĩ kɔ ɔlɔ gɛtà ba ɛlɛ mɛ ana.

english_verse: But now I beg you to cheer up, because you will be safe. Only the ship will be lost.
translation_verse: Pɔ otsiɛ nɔ ɛɛkpɛ wɔnɔ kemì ɔsɛ tɛ̀ nɔkpɛ kité, ɔmɔsɛ wɔnɔ ɔmaɛyɛmɛ nanà ɛkpã tɛbà yala, kelĩabhaohuɔ ete ɔlɛ obozĩ.

english_verse: I belong to God, and I worship him. Last night he sent an angel
translation_verse: Kivũɔ elishiɛ, Owusoso Akaa axɔ̃ɛdɛ teli gɛ ɛkpɛ kɛtakpu esì ɔkɔɔ ebu yɛishu akasɛ mɛ

english_verse: to tell me, “Paul, don't be afraid! You will stand trial before the Emperor. And because of you, God will save the lives of everyone on the ship.”
translation_verse: pɛ aɖɔ asɛ, ‘Paulo, tɛosi kifù. Kɛlɛ ɔsɛ ɔbà kebhu mɛ nɛ Romanɛ Ekusi Gbagbla namɛ, kɛlɛ Owusoso Akaa etibhui wɔnɛ bagɛ nɔgã kelĩabha kidzoɔ ɛkpã ebú.’

english_verse: Cheer up! I am sure that God will do exactly what he promised.
translation_verse: Kliso banyɛ mɔ, nɔzã kitè kpɛkpɛ nabha, ɔmɔsɛ ɛyɛkɛ Owusoso Akaa nabha ɔŋã ɔsɛ kɛbà ba ɛlɛ mɛ asɛ kana gɛ etseɖe mɛ ana.

english_verse: But we will first be shipwrecked on some island.
translation_verse: Pɔ, kɛlɛɛ nɔ̀ bafù bɔlɔ ɔvɛnɔ aƒu kesiakpa tieli abha pɛ aƒumɛ ayisɛ ebevũ bɔlɔ.” Kelĩabhaohuɔ Tɛyɛyɛ̀ɛ

english_verse: They cut the anchors loose and let them sink into the sea. At the same time they untied the ropes that were holding the rudders. Next, they raised the sail at the front of the ship and let the wind carry the ship toward the beach.
translation_verse: Gɛ bebhui ihuiɛ gɛ nyana kelĩabhaohuɔ bekeɛ, bashɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ ɔlɛ shu kpɛ aƒuɔ mɛ, kidzo ɛlɛ abha bebhui ihuiɛ gɛ nyanà kɛpɔtɛ tsyɛ vunɔ nɛ adamɛ ɔkɔɔ. Kɛlɛ basɔ̃ kɛpɔtɛɛ gɛ kɛkɔ pɛ lɔgãa nɛ ɔlɛ shù kɛlɛ bekoeyì nɛ ɔlɛ mɛ aba ogbogbòɔ.

english_verse: The sailors wanted to escape from the ship. So they lowered the lifeboat into the water, pretending that they were letting down some anchors from the front of the ship.
translation_verse: Gɛ bazabhɛtɛ asɛ tɛ̀ ebù tɛtsɛɖɛ mɛ ekoeyì nɛ kelĩabhaohuɔ mɛ soɔ, kelĩabha adɛdɛ akɔ kelĩabhaohuɔ ozhiku aƒuɔ mɛ kesiɛ, babhɛtɛ kɛlɛ asɛ gɛ onupɛ babàta ihuiɛ gɛ lɛnyanà kelĩabhaohuɔ vunɔɔ ɔganà kelĩabhaohuɔ otugbà ɔkɔ ibeɛ akpɛ̀ kelĩɛ mɛ ana.

english_verse: The sailors were afraid that we might hit some rocks, and they let down four anchors from the back of the ship. Then they prayed for daylight.
translation_verse: Gɛ kifùsisi zã ɔsɛ bɔbàdɛ yalɛ̀ ɛxɔa soɔ, batá ihui tɛlɛ̃ɛ gɛ kpakà kelĩabhaohuɔ vunɔɔ akpɛ keliɛ mɛ kɛlɛ buzikulu kititiayɛ asɛ akɔ tɛ̀ kelìɛ aza nɛ̀ ɔlɔ.

english_verse: But Paul said to Captain Julius and the soldiers, “If the sailors don't stay on the ship, you won't have any chance to save your lives.”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ Paulo etseɖe keyuiza anɔ gbagbla nɛ̀ buyuadɛdɛ asɛ, “Odiyinɔ̀ ɔsɛ banyɛ alɛ azã kelĩabhaohuɔ mɛɛ, bɔtɛ bazã nɛkpã.”

english_verse: The soldiers then cut the ropes that held the lifeboat and let it fall into the sea.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ, buyuadɛdɛ ebhuĩ ihuiɛ gɛ nyanã ɛkpã ohuɔ vunɔ kɛlɛ lozhioku keliɛ mɛ.

english_verse: I beg you to eat something. Your lives depend on it. Do this and not one of you will be hurt.”
translation_verse: Otsi alɛ ɛkpalà wɔnɔ ɔsɛ nɔŋã bɔdɔ̃ tɛ̀ nɔzã nɛ̀ ɛkpã. Wɔnɔ maɛyɛmɛ nanà kɛtakpu tɛkã tinikpo tsyɛ tɛbà yalà odenɔ yɛtakpuɔ.”

english_verse: After Paul had said this, he took a piece of bread and gave thanks to God. Then in front of everyone, he broke the bread and ate some.
translation_verse: Gɛ aɖɔ kibui ɛlɛ ebekeɛ, alɔkɔ kifufulũ tili kɛlɛ ehu Owusoso Akaa ahɔɛ nɛ ɛlɛ ɛtakpù nɛ balɛ nyɔ̃ɔ amɛ. Kɛlɛ aya ɛlɛ mɛ̀ pɛ akpaatã.

english_verse: They all felt encouraged, and each of them ate something.
translation_verse: Kemì kpɛkpɛ ebeku balɛ nyɔ̃ɔ mɛ kɛlɛ baŋã bɔdɔ̃

english_verse: and after everyone had eaten, they threw the cargo of wheat into the sea to make the ship lighter. The Shipwreck
translation_verse: Gɛ baŋã bɔdɔ̃ asɛ gɛ bapɛ nɔ̀, bebù nikpèe etukuɔ atɛɛ akpɛ bulĩɛ mɛ asɛ tɛ̀ lɔzã foelee.

english_verse: Morning came, and the ship's crew saw a coast they did not recognize. But they did see a cove with a beach. So they decided to try to run the ship aground on the beach.
translation_verse: Gɛ kiwiɛ bakoeyiɛ, betikènyi kesiakpa gɛ kalɛ abha bekuɔ tsyɛ, pɔ bamɔ ɔkɔ toli gɛ ayisɛ eyukù, ɛkɛlɛ bapɛ asɛ tɛ̀ begè kelĩabhaohuɔ adɛ vɔbɔ akpɛ ayisɛ mɛ̀ onupɛ babalàa.

english_verse: But it ran aground on a sandbank. The front of the ship stuck firmly in the sand, and the rear was being smashed by the force of the waves.
translation_verse: Pɔ kelĩabhaohuɔ ɔdɛ yalɛ̀ ayisɛ kɛlɛ lɔwɔ́ ɛkɛlɛ. Kelĩabhaohuɔ otugbà ɔkɔ ɔdɛ yalɛ̀ ayisɛ kɛlɛ lɔzɔ lagã, kɛlɛ ɔlɛ kede ɔkɔɔ ibeɛ ɔyɛ̀ɛ wluiwlui nɛ aƒulĩɛ gɛitsùɔ shu so.

english_verse: The soldiers decided to kill the prisoners to keep them from swimming away and escaping.
translation_verse: Buyuadɛdɛ anɔ nɔ̀ atɔɖɔ ɛlɛ asɛ tɛ̀ beyì ɔɖavũmɛ azazã, tɛ balɛ ɔmayɛmɛ anɔ nanà etibè de bulĩɛ tɛ ebù tɛtsɛɖɛmɛ ashɛ.

english_verse: But Captain Julius wanted to save Paul's life, and he did not let the soldiers do what they had planned. Instead, he ordered everyone who could swim to jump into the water and head for shore.
translation_verse: Pɔ gɛ keyuiza anɔ gbagbla apɛ asɛ tɛ̀ ebù Paulo xɔnɔ eyìnɔ nɛ ɛlɛ mɛ soɔ, agɛkɔ balɛ kidzò. Etseɖe bagɛ babalà bulĩɛ edoɔ asɛ beputɔ̀ atɛɛ ekoeyì nɛ kelĩabhaohuɔ mɛ tɛ̀ bavɛ kesiakpa.

english_verse: Then he told the others to hold on to planks of wood or parts of the ship. At last, everyone safely reached shore.
translation_verse: Bagɔgɔɛ nɔ xɔnɔ azã isikpã nɛ̀ kelĩabhaohuɔ gɛɔyɛɛ nɔ̀ busipɛ̃ nabha, kɛlɛ nɛ kidzo ɛlɛ abhaa, aka nyia aka ezhi eku kesiakpa nabha nɛ ishu yɔyɔ mɛ.

english_verse: When we came ashore, we learned that the island was called Malta.
translation_verse: Gɛ budiku ogbogbòɔ nɛ ishu yɔyɔ mɛ koɔ, bɔba mɔsɛ bekulu aƒumɛ ketukpɔ alɛ asɛ̀ Malta.

english_verse: The local people were very friendly, and they welcomed us by building a fire, because it was rainy and cold.
translation_verse: Banunɔ gɛ alɛ aƒumɛ ketukpɔ alɛ nabha abhɛtɛ ɛyamɛ gbangbã akɔ ɔlɔ. Bakpɛ kifú kɛlɛ bekulu bɔlɔ ɔsɛ bɔbaŋãlɛ ɛlɛ ɔmɔsɛ subha adɔ kɛlɛ bɔbɔlɔ zã.

english_verse: After Paul had gathered some wood and had put it on the fire, the heat caused a snake to crawl out, and it bit him on the hand.
translation_verse: Paulo eshogò bɔdzrɔbiyì, kɛlɛ gɛ alɔkɔ bɔlɛ aakpɛ kifuɔ mɛ koɔ, eblidza gulũ teli gɛ kifúɔ hunu yɛ pɛ ekoeyì nɛ bɔdzyɛ nɔ mɛɛ, aba akpɛ akanà yɛahɔkpɔ.

english_verse: When the local people saw the snake hanging from Paul's hand, they said to each other, “This man must be a murderer! He didn't drown in the sea, but the goddess of justice will kill him anyway.”
translation_verse: Gɛ banunɔ gɛ betsiɖi aƒumɛ ketukpɔ nabha amɔ eblidza gɛ akpɛ asɔ̃ nɛ yɛahɔkpɔɔ, betseɖe bagɔlɔ asɛ, “Anyɛ alɛ ebedzì anɔ gɛ eyianɔ, ɔmɔsɛ gɛ ebu yɛkpã eyinɔ nɛ aƒuɔ mɛ ebeke tsyɛɛ, beke kulu kebhu nɛ yɛ asɛ tɛ̀ atɛzã nɛkpã.”

english_verse: Paul shook the snake off into the fire and wasn't harmed.
translation_verse: Pɔ, Paulo eshuku eblidza akpɛ kifúɔ mɛ kɛlɛ kɛnanà gɛ bhɛtɛ yɛ.

english_verse: The people kept thinking that Paul would either swell up or suddenly drop dead. They watched him for a long time, and when nothing happened to him, they changed their minds and said, “This man is a god.”
translation_verse: Banunɔ ezeɖi asɛ ebèyeke alo ebeglebe adɔ etsì odunu ɔlɛ, pɔ gɛ bazã epo kibè dzloloɔ pɛ bagɛ mɔ asɛ kibui nanà bhɛtɛ yɛɛ, betsyina balɛ ekpeɖekpeɖe mɛ pɛ baɖɔ asɛ obudì anɔ yɛenu.

english_verse: The governor of the island was named Publius, and he owned some of the land around there. Publius was very friendly and welcomed us into his home for three days.
translation_verse: Bevũ teli azã kibe ɛlɛ gɛenu Publio, anɔ gɛ yɛaŋã bukusi nɛ aƒumɛ kesiakpa nabha ayɛ. Ayɛkɛ bɔlɔ akpɛ yɛɔpã mɛ kɛlɛ nɛ ewui tata mɛɛ, akpɛ bɔlɔ samɛ kɛlɛ awɔa bufu akɔ ɔlɔ.

english_verse: His father was in bed, sick with fever and stomach trouble, and Paul went to visit him. Paul healed the man by praying and placing his hands on him.
translation_verse: Yɛakaa adɔ bɔnyã eli bɔdɔ̃ abha, gɛ buwui vũi pɛ azadɛ dzyosu. Paulo etsoku edì ɖii, kɛlɛ gɛ ekulu Owusoso Akaa ebekeɛ, atɔɖɔ yɛahɔɛ nɛ yɛabha kɛlɛ yɛishumɛ gagalĩ.

english_verse: After this happened, everyone on the island brought their sick people to Paul, and they were all healed.
translation_verse: Gɛ kibui ɛlɛ ba ɛlɛ mɛɛ, banyanyã gɔgɔɛ nɔ̀ nɛ aƒumɛ ketukpɔ alɛ nabha aba, kɛlɛ ebu banyã akɔ alɛ.

english_verse: The people were very respectful to us, and when we sailed, they gave us everything we needed. From Malta to Rome
translation_verse: Badɛmɛ bɔlɔ ibui nɛ edzo gelee abha, kɛlɛ nɛ kibeɛ gɛ bɔbà tɔnɔ̀ kelĩabha kidzoɔ, bakɔ ɔlɔ bɔdɔ̃ gɛ bɔɔpɛ nɔ̀. Roma Tikuku

english_verse: Three months later we sailed in a ship that had been docked at Malta for the winter. The ship was from Alexandria in Egypt and was known as “The Twin Gods.”
translation_verse: Nɛ bewoele tata edeɛ gɛ bɔzã ekukùɔ mɛ nɛ aƒumɛ ketukpɔ alɛ nabha, bɔzɛ tɔnɔ kelĩabha kidzo. Kelĩabhaohuɔ gɛ bukuɔ ɔlɛ onu Aleksandro ohu gɛ bekulù ɔlɛ sɛ taabha eyi obudì, Kasto nɛ̀ Pulox.

english_verse: We arrived in Syracuse and stayed for three days.
translation_verse: Budizhi nɛ Sirakuse ogbogbòɔ, kɛlɛ bɔzã ɛkɛlɛ ewui tata.

english_verse: From there we sailed to Rhegium. The next day a south wind began to blow, and two days later we arrived in Puteoli.
translation_verse: Denɔ ɛkɛlɛ bɔzɛ tɔnɔ kelĩabha kidzoɔ kɛlɛ budiku Regio. Gɛ keliɛ azaa, kanɔ nɔ̀ bɔpialɛ ɔkɔ kɛlɛɛ nɔ zɛkpɛ tifufù esi, kɛlɛ gɛ keliɛ azazaa, budiyì nɛ Puteoli

english_verse: There we found some of the Lord's followers, who begged us to stay with them. A week later we left for the city of Rome.
translation_verse: Ɛkɛlɛ bɔdɛ tɔnɔ badzya teli gɛ bekulu ɔlɔ asɛ bɔzã balɛ ɔbha ɔgɔnɛ zɛzɛ. Ɛkɛlɛ bɔlɔde pɛ budiku Roma.

english_verse: Some of the followers in Rome heard about us and came to meet us at the Market of Appius and at the Three Inns. When Paul saw them, he thanked God and was encouraged. Paul in Rome
translation_verse: Badzya nɛ ɛkɛlɛ etilũ asɛ bɔɔbà, kliso baba tsyana ɔlɔ avɛnɔ ɔkɔ dzlolo gɛonu Apio kedziɛ mɛ nɛ̀ beefù ɔtsɛkɔ. Gɛ Paulo anyamɔ banyɛ alɛ koɔ kɛlɛ ehu Owusoso Akaa nɛahɔɛ pɛ kemikpɛkpɛ eku yɛ mɛ.

english_verse: We arrived in Rome, and Paul was allowed to live in a house by himself with a soldier to guard him.
translation_verse: Gɛ budiku Roma nɔ̀, bashɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ Paulo shu asɛ tɛ̀ azã yɛishu ahɔɛ mɛ tɛ̀ keyuadɛdɛ tenikpo azãpoe. Bezepo Paulo Pɛ Aɖɔ Kibuiɛ Nɛ Roma

english_verse: Three days after we got there, Paul called together some of the Jewish leaders and said: My friends, I have never done anything to hurt our people, and I have never gone against the customs of our ancestors. But in Jerusalem I was handed over as a prisoner to the Romans.
translation_verse: Ewui tata edeɛ, Paulo ɛkulu Yudanɛ besheshèɛ eshogò. Gɛ beshogò ebekeɛ, etseɖe balɛ asɛ, “Mɛadzya alɛ Israelanɛ mɔ, ɛgɛ bhɛtɛ kibui kpa nanà nɛ̀ bɔlɔ banunɔ alo bɔlɔ ashuĩ alɛ ɛkɔɔ shu flogo, bevũ mɛ nɛ Yerusalem pɛ balɔkɔ mɛ akpɛ ahɔɛ akɔ Romanɛ.

english_verse: They looked into the charges against me and wanted to release me. They found that I had not done anything deserving death.
translation_verse: Bapɔɛ ebui mɛ nimɛ kɛlɛ bapɛ asɛ tɛ̀ bashɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ mɛishu, ɔmɔsɛ ɛgɛ bhɛtɛ kibui kpa nanà gɛ bashɔnɔ ɔkɔ butsí.

english_verse: The Jewish leaders disagreed, so I asked to be tried by the Emperor. But I don't have anything to say against my own nation.
translation_verse: Pɔ gɛ Yudanɛ agɛ dɛmɛ ana soɔ, bebètũilɛ akpɛ mɛulũ asɛ ɛvɛ Romanɛ Ekusi Gbagbla ɔkɔ, gɛnɛ gɛ kibui nanà gɛlɛ mahɔɛ gɛ ɛpɛ sɛ tɛ̀ ɛlɔkɔ nɛ mɛ banunɔ shu flogo.

english_verse: I am bound by these chains because of what we people of Israel hope for. This is why I have called you here to talk about this hope of ours.
translation_verse: Nɛ kibui ɛlɛ shu soɔ, ɛpɛ sɛ tɛ ɛmɔ wɔ tɛ̀ bɔɖɔaɖɔ. Mɛ awɔ̃ lulu ɔkɔ Israelanɛ so pɛ banyã mɛ nɛ̀ ɛɖahui ɛlɛ.”

english_verse: The leaders replied, “No one from Judea has written us a letter about you. And not one of them has come here to report on you or to say anything against you.
translation_verse: Bebuiliulũ asɛ, “Bɔkà yɛkɛ buplukpa nanà odenɔ Yudea nɛ wɔshu, kɛlɛ badzya ɔmayɛmɛ nanà gɛede ɛkɛlɛ aba, aka kɔ bɔlɔ tititì nanà alo pɛ abaɖɔ kibui kpa nanà nɛ wɔshu.

english_verse: But we would like to hear what you have to say. We understand that people everywhere are against this new group.”
translation_verse: Pɔ bɔpɛ sɛ tɛ̀ bulũ kɛgɛ ɔmɔ nɛ kɛlɛ shuɔ, ɔmɔsɛ bunyi sɛ banunɔ nɛ ɔkɔ nyia ɔkɔ aɖɔaɖɔ nɛ ɔhakpa alɛ shu.”

english_verse: They agreed on a time to meet with Paul, and many of them came to his house. From early morning until late in the afternoon, Paul talked to them about God's kingdom. He used the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets to try to win them over to Jesus.
translation_verse: Batɔɖɔ kiwui asɛ tɛ babamɔ Paulo, kɛlɛ balɛ anɔ nɔ̀ gɛaba ebekpe atɔɖɔ nɛ ɛkɛlɛ gɛ Paulo azãa. Odenɔ kɛvɔlɔ vɛnɔ owlagoɔ, ezebu ebui mɛ pɛ adalɛ Owusoso Akaa bukusi ŋaŋã shu kɔ alɛ, kɛlɛ apɛ asɛ tɛ̀ etsyina balɛ ekpeɖekpeɖeɛ mɛ odenɔ Mose ɔshaa nɛ̀ ebuiɖɔluanɛ buplukpa mɛ avɛnɔ Yesu ɔkɔ.

english_verse: Some of the leaders agreed with what Paul said, but others did not.
translation_verse: Beteli ekpeɖekpeɖe mɛ ebetsyina nɛ ebuiɛ gɛ azaɖɔ shu, pɔ bagɔgɔɛ nɔ xɔnɔ agɛ yɛkɛ aŋã.

english_verse: Since they could not agree among themselves, they started leaving. But Paul said, “The Holy Spirit said the right thing when he sent Isaiah the prophet
translation_verse: Bazawlɔãkɛ ebuiɛ nɛ bagɔlɔ mɛ kɛlɛ bakpa ashɛ kibeɛ gɛ Paulo aɖɔ kibui gɔgɔɛ ɛlɛ asɛ, “Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã ɔɖɔ kibui gbã otseɖe wɔnɔ ashuĩ alɛ kibeɛ gɛ aɖɔ gana ebuiɖɔluanɔ Yesaya abha asɛ,

english_verse: to tell our ancestors, ‘Go to these people and tell them: You will listen and listen, but never understand. You will look and look, but never see.
translation_verse: “ ‘Vɛ banɔ alɛ ɔkɔ tɛ̀ oditseɖe alɛ ɔsɛ, “Nobozolũ ebuiɛ, pɔ noti bozolũ alɛ esi, Nɔbɔzɔmɔ bɔdɔ̃ pɔ noti bozolũ bɔlɛ esi.”

english_verse: All of you have stubborn hearts. Your ears are stopped up, and your eyes are covered. You cannot see or hear or understand. If you could, you would turn to me, and I would heal you.’ ”
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ banɔ alɛ ekpeɖekpeɖeɛ mɛ etibu tɛtsɛɖɛ, agagalĩ flogo pɛ belũ kibui akpɛ batɔ̃ mɛ, kɛlɛ badã balɛ awuhũɔ. Tsyɛ alo balɛ awuhũɔ atamɔ bɔdɔ̃ flogo, kɛlɛ betelũ nɛ̀ balɛ atɔ̃ɔ, betelũ ebui esi nɛ̀ balɛ etúɔ kɛlɛ betetsyinà pɛ ɛtabu banyã ɔkɔ alɛ.’

english_verse: Paul said, “You may be sure that God wants to save the Gentiles! And they will listen.”
translation_verse: Gɛ aɖɔ kibui ɛlɛ ebekeɛ, Yudanɛ ashɛ pɛ baza wlɔãkɛ kibui gagalĩ nɛ bagɔlɔ mɛ.

english_verse: For two years Paul stayed in a rented house and welcomed everyone who came to see him.
translation_verse: Paulo azã ɛkɛlɛ agɔ taabha nɛ bɔpã tuli gɛ abuia kɛbhɛ asɛ tɛ̀ azã bɔlɛ mɛ, kɛlɛ ayɛkɛ banunɔ nyɔ̃ɔ gɛ bazaba yɛɔkɔ asɛ tɛ bamɔɛ nɔ̀.

english_verse: He bravely preached about God's kingdom and taught about the Lord Jesus Christ, and no one tried to stop him.
translation_verse: Gɛ ananà egidzu ahɔɛ nɛ yɛabha soɔ, aɖɔ Owusoso Akaa bukusiŋaŋã kibuiɛ nɛ bupù mɛ, kɛlɛ akasɛ bɔdɔ̃ nɛ Apalete Yesu Kristo shu.

english_verse: From Paul, a servant of Christ Jesus. God chose me to be an apostle, and he appointed me to preach the good news
translation_verse: Mɛ Paulo, Kristo Yesu yɛaxɔ̃ɛdɛ gɛ bekulu asɛ tɛ edzi anɔkpɛkpɛ, pɛ bebu elu akɔ Owusoso Akaa Ibui Gbangbã.

english_verse: that he promised long ago by what his prophets said in the holy Scriptures.
translation_verse: Kibui Gbangbã gɛ atɔɖɔ elu akpɛ otugbà ɔgana yɛebuiɖɔluanɛ nabha, nɛ Kibui Dadalɛ mɛ

english_verse: This good news is about his Son, our Lord Jesus Christ! As a human, he was from the family of David. But the Holy Spirit proved that Jesus is the powerful Son of God, because he was raised from death.
translation_verse: kɛlɛ, gana Ɔfɔfuĩa dadalɛ mɛɛ, bebui akasɛ nɛ ohumi nɔ mɛ ɔsɛ, yɛenu Owusoso Akaa Yɛeyi, gana yɛtɛlɔlɔ odenɔ bekpuɔ mɛɛ, yɛenu Yesu Kristo, bɔlɔ Apaleteɛ.

english_verse: Jesus was kind to me and chose me to be an apostle, so that people of all nations would obey and have faith.
translation_verse: Gana yɛabha, kɛlɛ nɛ yɛinye mɛɛ, bɔmɔ tibhui bhuibu kɛlɛ budzi banɔkpɛkpɛ sɛ tɛ bukulu banɔ odenɔ bagɛ betinu Yudanɛ pete ishi mana batɔ̃ lulũ gɛ bede tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛ.

english_verse: You are some of those people chosen by Jesus Christ.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ wɔnɔ Romanɛ tsyɛ nɔkpasɛ bagɛ bekulu asɛ bedzi Yesu Kristoanɛ nɛishi.

english_verse: This letter is to all of you in Rome. God loves you and has chosen you to be his very own people. I pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! A Prayer of Thanks
translation_verse: Wɔnɔ banɔ dadalɛ pete nɛ Roma gɛ bekulu, pɛ Owusoso Akaa adɛmɛ nɔɔ, Tibhui bhuibu nɛ̀ ishuyɔyɔ odenɔ bɔlɔ Owusoso Akaa nɛ̀ Apalete Yesu Kristo ɔkɔ kɔ wɔnɔ. Paulo Yɛtɛpɛpɛ Ɔsɛ Tɛ Avɛ Roma

english_verse: First, I thank God in the name of Jesus Christ for all of you. I do this because people everywhere in the world are talking about your faith.
translation_verse: Iputɔ, ihu mɛ Owusoso Akaa ahɔɛ nɛ wɔnɔ ɛtakpu ɔgana Yesu Kristo abha, ɔmɔsɛ baɖɔ wɔnɔ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã ibui afala nɔ nɛ kanɔ nɔ̀ mɛ nyɔ̃ɔ.

english_verse: God has seen how I never stop praying for you, while I serve him with all my heart and tell the good news about his Son.
translation_verse: Owusoso Akaa, anunɔ gɛ yɛaxɔ̃ɛ mɛdɛ nɛ̀ mɛitúɔ nyɔ̃ɔ nɛ Yɛeyi Nɛibui Gbangbã tɛɖɔɖɔ mɛɛ, yɛabà dalɛ mɛishu nɛ kɛbakà gɛ ɛbakà wɔnɔɛbui ibe nyiɛ beɛ

english_verse: In all my prayers, I ask God to make it possible for me to visit you.
translation_verse: nɛ mɛ Owusoso Akaa tikukulu kiwui nyiɛ wui mɛ, kɛlɛ mɛ tɛpɛpɛ otsi alɛ kɛlɛ nu ɔsɛ tɛ Owusoso Akaa adalɛ idzo akɔ mɛ tɛ mɛba wɔnɔ ɔbha.

english_verse: I want to see you and share with you the same blessings that God's Spirit has given me. Then you will grow stronger in your faith.
translation_verse: Mɛ tɛpɛpɛ kɛlɛinu ɔsɛ tɛ mɛmɔ ɔnɔ, sɛ tɛ mɛlɔkɔ ɔfɔfuĩa mɛ bɔdɔ̃ kɔkɔ tuli kɔ ɔnɔ tɛ nɔmɔ ohumi.

english_verse: What I am saying is that we can encourage each other by the faith that is ours.
translation_verse: Kɛgɛ ɛɖɔ, pɛ ɛpɛ nɔ, kɛlɛ inu sɛ tɛ bɔlɔkɔ bɔlɔ Owusoso Akaa abha tɛyɛyɛkɛŋãa kpɛ bagɔlɔ ohumi nɔ.

english_verse: My friends, I want you to know that I have often planned to come for a visit. But something has always kept me from doing it. I want to win followers to Christ in Rome, as I have done in many other places.
translation_verse: Mɛelɛ alɛ mɔ, ɛpɛ sɛ tɛ kɛzã tititì mɛ kɔ ɔnɔ ɔsɛ anɛ obo mɛtɔɖɔ wɔnɔ ɔkɔ tɛbaba ibui flogo tɛ mɛmɔ ɛdzo tɛ mɛibu atɔwui nɛ ɔnɔ mɛ, asɛ ibu gɛ itibu atɔwui teli gɛ letinu Yudanɛ nɛ ɛkɔ tili ana.

english_verse: I am proud of the good news! It is God's powerful way of saving all people who have faith, whether they are Jews or Gentiles.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ Kibui Gbangbã tinu kiwuĩbui ɔkɔ mɛ, ɔmɔsɛ kinu Owusoso Akaa yɛohumi ɔkɔ, anɔ nyia anɔ gɛ ayɛkɛ aŋãa yɛtibubu. Putɔ nɔɔ, ɔkɔ Yudanɛ flogo pɛ bagɛ betinu Yudanɛ.

english_verse: The good news tells how God accepts everyone who has faith, but only those who have faith. It is just as the Scriptures say, “The people God accepts because of their faith will live.” Everyone Is Guilty
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ denɔ Kibui Gbangbã mɛɛ bebu kɛlɔkɔ gɛ Owusoso Akaa alɔkɔ anɔ teli asɛ enu anɔ dadalɛ, gɛ kinu tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã ibui odenɔ kesi kpɛkpɛ mɛ ɔvɛnɔ ɔtɔ̃kɔ, asɛ kɛfɔ̀nɔ gɛ bafɔnɔ ɛlɛ elu asɛ, “Anɔ dadalɛ abazã nɛ̀ ɛkpã ɔgana tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛ.” Owusoso Akaa Yɛɔmɔpɔɛ Nɛ Banɔdzyrame Shu

english_verse: From heaven God shows how angry he is with all the wicked and evil things that sinful people do to crush the truth.
translation_verse: Bebu Owusoso Akaa yɛɔmɔpɔɛ nɛ banunɔ nyɔ̃ɔ gɛ betisì yɛifuɔ akasɛ odenɔ kititíɛ ke, kɛlɛ kɛtakpu mɛ gagalĩeleteɛ gɛ ezhinɔ kibuiɛ gɛlɛ ɛlɛ ɔzãkɔ akpɛ esi ɔgana balɛ ɛtakpu mɛ gagalĩ ebui bhɛbhɛtɛ mɛɛ,

english_verse: They know everything that can be known about God, because God has shown it all to them.
translation_verse: kɛgɛ ɛlɛ sɛ beti odenɔ Owusoso Akaa shu pɔ lɛ tititì mɛ ɔkɔ balɛ kukuku flogo, ɔmɔsɛ Owusoso Akaa atɛ dalɛ kɛlɛ mɛ akasɛ alɛ.

english_verse: God's eternal power and character cannot be seen. But from the beginning of creation, God has shown what these are like by all he has made. That's why those people don't have any excuse.
translation_verse: Denɔ kanɔ mɛ esi kpɛkpɛ mɛ keɛ, Owusoso Akaa tɛzazã gbangbã gɛlɛ tikpukpũ mɛ, nɛ̀ yɛohumi tihɔɔ nɛ̀ yɛ ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ tɛzazã mɛɛ, bɔmɔ alɛ mɛ tɛdadalɛ, kɛlɛ esi lulũ alɛ akɔ ebuiɛ odenɔ kɛgɛ babhɛtɛ mɛ, kliso kɛtakpu kɔkɔ ma ɔkɔ anɔ nanà sɛ etinyi ebuiɛ.

english_verse: They know about God, but they don't honor him or even thank him. Their thoughts are useless, and their stupid minds are in the dark.
translation_verse: Gɛ benyii ɔmɔ asɛ Owusoso Akaa tsyɛ elo, batɛkpɛ yɛinye nɛ itití alo pɛ behu yɛahɔɛ, balɛ ekpeɖekpeɖe mɛ bhɛbhɛtɛ edzi kɛlɛ́, kɛlɛ balɛ bɔga dɔdɔ etúɔ mɛ eku otumũ.

english_verse: They claim to be wise, but they are fools.
translation_verse: Beɖi asɛ bebuitinɛ benu, pɔ bebedzi baga,

english_verse: They don't worship the glorious and eternal God. Instead, they worship idols that are made to look like humans who cannot live forever, and like birds, animals, and reptiles.
translation_verse: sɛ tɛ bakpɛ Owusoso Akaa gɛ alɛ nɛ ɛkpã tihɔɔ nɔ̀ inye nɛititíɛ, betsyina ɛlɛ akɔ bɔdɔ̃ gɛlɛ asɛ banɔ tsitsí, bɔzhɔɛ, bagã nɛ̀ bagã gɛ betsì nɛ esiakpa boŋ.

english_verse: So God let these people go their own way. They did what they wanted to do, and their filthy thoughts made them do shameful things with their bodies.
translation_verse: Kliso nɛ balɛ etúɔ tɛdɛdɛmɛ ɔkɔ ebui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ ulũ soɔ, Owusoso Akaa ashɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ balɛishu akɔ kɛgɔlɔ ŋaŋã ebui nɛ̀ atsɔ̃ ebui bhɛbhɛtɛ nɛ̀ bagɔlɔ.

english_verse: They gave up the truth about God for a lie, and they worshiped God's creation instead of God, who will be praised forever. Amen.
translation_verse: Batsrakɛ Owusoso Akaa ibuiɛ gbangbã nɛ̀ asɛnɛ sɛsɛ, kɛlɛ baakpɛ ɛtakpu esi pɛ baadɛ axɔ̃ɛ akɔ banɔdzyrame ɔdɔ̃ bhɛbhɛtɛ, ɔdɛ ɔsɛ tɛ bakpɛ ɛtakpu esi akɔ Kititíayɛ gɛ ebu bɔdɔ̃ɔ, anunɔ gɛ kɛlɛ bakpɛ yɛinye nɛitití tihɔɔ nɔ. Amen.

english_verse: God let them follow their own evil desires. Women no longer wanted to have sex in a natural way, and they did things with each other that were not natural.
translation_verse: Nɛ alɛ shu soɔ Owusoso Akaa ashɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ balɛishu akɔ awuĩ ebui gɛ badɛmɛ nɔ. Balɛ adzɛ etsyina awɔsɔ̃ nɛ bagɔlɔ, gɛ kitinu agɛ ebu alɛ tɛtɔtɔɖɔ flogo.

english_verse: Men behaved in the same way. They stopped wanting to have sex with women and had strong desires for sex with other men. They did shameful things with each other, and what has happened to them is punishment for their foolish deeds.
translation_verse: Nɛidzo ɛlɛ nabhaa banyɛ tsyɛ ashɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ balɛ adzɛ gbagbana shu, pɛ bawɔsɔ̃ nɛ̀ balɛ banyɛ ɔhagbo alɛ. Banyɛ abhɛtɛ atsɔ̃ ebui nɛ̀ balɛ banyɛ ɔhagbo alɛ kɛlɛ bayɛkɛ balɛ atɔ̃pɔpɔ̃ nɛ ebuiɛ gɛ kɛma sɛ babhɛtɛ pɛ babhɛtɛɛ shu.

english_verse: Since these people refused even to think about God, he let their useless minds rule over them. That's why they do all sorts of indecent things.
translation_verse: Otsi nɔ, gɛ bagɛmɔ asɛ kinu ibui gɛ kɛyɛkɛ edzi sɛ tɛ bayɛkɛ Owusoso Akaa bubuti akpɛ ishu mɛ soɔ, etsyina balɛ ekpeɖekpeɖeɛ mɛ etũ, sɛ tɛ baabhɛtɛ ebuiɛ gɛ lama ɔbhɛtɛ kɔɔ.

english_verse: They are evil, wicked, and greedy, as well as mean in every possible way. They want what others have, and they murder, argue, cheat, and are hard to get along with. They gossip,
translation_verse: Banunɔ mɛ eyuku nɛ̀ atakpu mɛ gagalĩ, ebui zizĩ, mɛete ɛyɛ tɛpɛpɛ̃ nɛ̀ banɔ tidzyidzyini ɔkpɛ, alɛ ayɛkɛ alɛ mɛ. Ebui kpa bhlatɔ enu awuhũ dzadzã nɛ banɔ shu, banɔ yiyí, ebla pɛpɛ, banɔ wɔɛwɔ̀ɛ nɛ̀ ɔhagbo shu tizizĩ. Benu olugbũedzi agagã anɔ,

english_verse: say cruel things about others, and hate God. They are proud, conceited, and boastful, always thinking up new ways to do evil. These people don't respect their parents.
translation_verse: kaɖɔ kpa ɖɔɖɔ nɛ banɔ shu, banɔ gɛ anyana Owusoso Akaa akpɛ ɛya mɛ, banɔ gɛ bakɔala anɔ, befeke ishu pɛ beku ishu awɔ̃, bapɛɛ edzo nɔnɔ abha gɛ babagã abhɛtɛ ibui zizĩ, batɛkpɛ ɛtakpu esi akɔ balɛ akaa nɛ̀ balɛ elĩ alɛ,

english_verse: They are stupid, unreliable, and don't have any love or pity for others.
translation_verse: bubuti nɛ̀ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã ma alɛ, betiɖi anɔ ashagabui, pɔɔ bagagalĩ atakpu mɛ.

english_verse: They know God has said that anyone who acts this way deserves to die. But they keep on doing evil things, and they even encourage others to do them.
translation_verse: Gɛ benyi piapii ɔsɛ Owusoso Akaa ɔsha gɛ akɔ ɔɖɔ ɔsɛ bagɛ bazã nɛ ɛkpã ɛlɛ nɛidiɛ, babapɔ̃ balɛ atɔ̃ nɛ̀ butsí tsyɛ elo, bavɛ ɛlɛ abha azã nɛ̀ ɛkpã kpa ɛlɛ, kɛlɛ bakɔ asɛ tɛ banɔ bhlatɔ tsyɛ azã nɛ̀ ɛkpã ɛlɛ.

english_verse: Some of you accuse others of doing wrong. But there is no excuse for what you do. When you judge others, you condemn yourselves, because you are guilty of doing the very same things.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ wɔɛtakpu kɔkɔ nanà ma, wɔ anunɔ gɛ okulu ebhu ɔkɔ anɔ teliɛ, ɔmɔsɛ kibui nɔnɔ́ idi gɛ ɔlɔkɔ okulu ebhu ɔkɔ anɔ bhlatɔ nɔɔ, kɛlɛ wɔ tsyɛ ɔlɔkɔ obu ishui ipɔ nɔ, ɔmɔsɛ wɔanɔ gɛ obhui ibui mɛ tsyɛɛ, ɔbhɛtɛ kibui ɛlɛ nɛidi.

english_verse: We know that God is right to judge everyone who behaves in this way.
translation_verse: Bunyi otsi alɛ ɔsɛ, Owusoso Akaa yɛebhu kukulu nɛ̀ bagɛ babhɛtɛ ebui alɛ nɛidiɛ nu kibui gɛlɛ ɛlɛ ɔzãkɔ nulũ.

english_verse: Do you really think God won't punish you, when you behave exactly like the people you accuse?
translation_verse: Kliso onupɛ wɔ anɔ wɔ̃a obu banɔɛ ɛpɔ, pɛ wɔ tsyɛ ɔbhɛtɛ ebui alɛ nɛidi keɛ pɔ, buɖi woɖi ɔsɛ ɔbɔlà obu tɛtsɛɖɛ mɛ nɛ Owusoso Akaa ebhu kukulu nulũɔ dza?

english_verse: You surely don't think much of God's wonderful goodness or of his patience and willingness to put up with you. Don't you know that the reason God is good to you is because he wants you to turn to him?
translation_verse: Pɛnɔsɛ mabomabo nɔlɔkɔ ɔkɔɛ nɛ yɛyamɛ gbangbã tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ, yɛemi kpɛkpɛ nɛ̀ yɛtɛdɔdɔ fɔ̃ɔ ɔkɔ ɔnɔɔ shu, pɔ nɔtɛ ɔmɔsɛ yɛyamɛ gbangbã gɛ abhɛtɛɛ kɛkɔ ɔsɛ notsyina etú mɛɛ dza?

english_verse: God will reward each of us for what we have done.
translation_verse: Owusoso Akaa aɖɔ asɛ, “ebebhui ibui mɛ akɔ anɔ nyia anɔ nɛ kɛgɛ abhɛtɛ nulũ.” (Psalomo

english_verse: He will give eternal life to everyone who has patiently done what is good in the hope of receiving glory, honor, and life that lasts forever.
translation_verse: Bagɛ gana balɛ emi kpɛkpɛ mɛ pɛ baabhɛtɛ ebui gbangbã, pɛ bazapɛɛ inye gbangbã nɛ̀ bobo akɔ Owusoso Akaa, pɛ bazapɛ asɛ tɛ bazã nɛ̀ ɛkpã ɖaa nɔ, abakɔ alɛ ɛkpã tihɔɔ.

english_verse: But he will show how angry and furious he can be with every selfish person who rejects the truth and wants to do evil.
translation_verse: Pɔ bagɛ bapɛ balɛyɛ ete tɛpɛpɛ, pɛ bashɛ nɛ ahɔɛ nɛ kibui gɛlɛ ɛlɛ ɔzãkɔ shu pɛ balɛ ebui kpa edeɛ, batɔ̃ pɔpɔ̃ gagalĩi nɛ̀ ɔmɔpɔɛ ɔlɛ ɔkɔ alɛ.

english_verse: All who are wicked will be punished with trouble and suffering. It doesn't matter if they are Jews or Gentiles.
translation_verse: Bɔtɔ nɛ̀ ɔwɔ̃nyɔnɔ ɔbɔba anɔ nyia anɔ gɛ azã nɛ ɛkpã zizĩ nabha, banɔ gɛ eputɔɔ enu Yudanɛ, flogo pɛ ɔkɔ bagɛ betinu Yudanɛ tsyɛ,

english_verse: But all who do right will be rewarded with glory, honor, and peace, whether they are Jews or Gentiles.
translation_verse: pɔ ishu dadalɛ, bobo nɛ̀ ishu yɔyɔ ɔkɔ anɔ nyia anɔ gɛ abhɛtɛ ibui gbangbã. Banɔ gɛ eputɔɔ enu Yudanɛ, flogo pɛ ɔkɔ bagɛ betinu Yudanɛ tsyɛ.

english_verse: God doesn't have any favorites!
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ Owusoso Akaa xɔnɔ etiɖi ananà amɛ.

english_verse: Those people who don't know about God's Law will still be punished for what they do wrong. And the Law will be used to judge everyone who knows what it says.
translation_verse: Banunɔ nyɔ̃ɔ gɛ Mose yɛɔsha gɛnyã alɛ pɛ bazã nɛ̀ ɛkpã kpaa, batɛ balɔkɔ ɔsha ɔlɛ àpɔ batɔ̃ akɔ alɛ nɔ, pɔ banunɔ nyɔ̃ɔ gɛ alɛ Mose ɔsha ɔlɛ nìesi pɛ babhɛtɛ ebui kpaa, babalɔkɔ ɔsha ɔlɛ ekulu ebhu akɔ alɛ nɔ.

english_verse: God accepts those who obey his Law, but not those who simply hear it.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ anɛ bagɛ belũ ɔshaa balɛ enu banɔ dadalɛ nɛ Owusoso Akaa yɛamɛ, pɔ bagɛ aabhɛtɛ ɔsha nabhaa, balɛ Owusoso Akaa ebebu akasɛ asɛ benu anɔ dadalɛ.

english_verse: This proves that the conscience is like a law written in the human heart. And it will show whether we are forgiven or condemned,
translation_verse: pɔ nɛ balɛ tɛgagã nɛ ɔsha lũɔɔ, kɛkasɛ ɔsɛ bafɔnɔ ɔsha nɛ balɛ etúɔ mɛ, kɛlɛ balɛ etúɔ mɛ ebui tsyɛ adalɛ alɛ shu, kɛlɛ balɛ ekpeɖekpeɖeɛ mɛ ebhui alɛ ulũ, kɛlɛ kidzo ɛlɛ nabhaa yɛebu balɛulũ tsyɛ.)

english_verse: when God appoints Jesus Christ to judge everyone's secret thoughts, just as my message says. The Jews and the Law
translation_verse: Kɛbàba kɛlɛ mɛ nɛ kiwui nabha gɛ Owusoso Akaa ebekulu ebhu nɛ banɔdzyrame ebui kpukpũɔ, ɔgana Yesu Kristo abha asɛ ɛɖɔɔ gɛ mɛibui gbangbã ɖɔ luɔ ana. Yudanɛ Nɛ̀ Ɔshaa

english_verse: Some of you call yourselves Jews. You trust in the Law and take pride in God.
translation_verse: Wɔ, onupɛ okulu ishu ɔsɛ Yudaeyi wonu, pɛ olu awɔ̃ nɛ ɔsha nabha, pɛ ofeke ishu nɛ wɔ Owusoso Akaa yɛeyi dzidzì shu,

english_verse: By reading the Scriptures you learn how God wants you to behave, and you discover what is right.
translation_verse: onupɛ onyi Owusoso Akaa tɛtɔtɔɖɔ lu, pɛ ɔdɛmɛ ɔtɔɖɔ ɛlɛ abha, ɔmɔsɛ ɔkpasɛ kɛlɛ odenɔ ɔshaa mɛ,

english_verse: You are sure that you are a guide for the blind and a light for all who are in the dark.
translation_verse: ɔmɔ ishu ɔsɛ onu awuhũpu agbagbana nɛ̀ kifu ɔkɔ bagɛ balɛ otumũ mɛ,

english_verse: And since there is knowledge and truth in God's Law, you think you can instruct fools and teach young people.
translation_verse: gɛ onu adɔkasɛ ɔkɔ bawaga, nɛ̀ adɔkasɛ ɔkɔ bunuvɔɛ, ɔmɔsɛ bùbuti nɛ̀ kibui gɛlɛ ɛlɛ ɔzãkɔɔ, kɛlɛ kpasɛ wɔ mɛɛ.

english_verse: But how can you teach others when you refuse to learn? You preach it is wrong to steal. But do you steal?
translation_verse: kɛlɛ wɔ gɛ ɔkasɛ banɔ bhlatɔ ɔdɔ̃ɔ, ɔtɛ ɔlà kasɛ wɔshu udɔ̃ dza? Wɔ gɛ ɔsɛ beti zuruɔ, wɔ ozuruɔ dza?

english_verse: You say people should be faithful in marriage. But are you faithful? You hate idols, yet you rob their temples.
translation_verse: Wɔ gɛ ɔɖɔ ɔsɛ banɔ batɛ dɛ ɔmamɛɛ, wɔ ɔdɛ ɔmamɛɛ dza? Wɔ gɛ ɔtɛ ɔdɛmɛ obudi ibuiɛ, wɔ ozuru nɛ obudi ukã mɛɛ dza?

english_verse: You take pride in the Law, but you disobey the Law and bring shame to God.
translation_verse: Wɔ gɛ ofeke ishu nɛ ɔsha shuɔ, wɔtɛɛ Owusoso Akaa olu pɛ ɔta esi nɛ ɔsha shuɔ dza?

english_verse: It is just as the Scriptures tell us, “You have made foreigners say insulting things about God.”
translation_verse: Asɛ kɛfɔnɔ gɛ bafɔnɔ elu asɛ, “Wɔnɔ Yudanɛ wɔnɔ ɔkɔ ɔsɛ bagɛ etinu Yudanɛ baɖɔ ebusoebui nɛ Owusoso Akaa shu.” (Yesaya

english_verse: Being circumcised is worthwhile, if you obey the Law. But if you don't obey the Law, you are no better off than people who are not circumcised.
translation_verse: Kɛhɔ̃a bhui bhui pɛ popopo onupɛ ɔbhɛtɛ ɛlɛ tibhui bhui ɔsha nabha, pɔ onupɛ ɔta esi nɛ ɔshaa shuɔ, kɛlɛ ɔshɔnɔ anɔ gɛ egibhui kɛhɔ̃a.

english_verse: In fact, if they obey the Law, they are as good as anyone who is circumcised.
translation_verse: Onupɛ bagɛ begibhui ahɔ̃a akɔ, pɔ balɛ nɛ̀ ɛkpã nɛ ɔsha nulũɔ, kiɖeso pɛ batɛ balɔkɔ alɛ asɛ anɔ gɛ bebhui ahɔ̃a akɔ anaa dza?

english_verse: So everyone who obeys the Law, but has never been circumcised, will condemn you. Even though you are circumcised and have the Law, you still don't obey its teachings.
translation_verse: Anunɔ gɛ begibhui kɛhɔ̃a akɔ anii, pɔ aagã nɛ ɔsha nabha piapii nɔ, ebebu wɔ gɛ ɔshaa tɛtɔtɔɖɔ lɛ wɔahɔɛ pɛ onu kɛhɔ̃a bhui bhui elete, pɔ onu anunɔ gɛ ɔta esiɛ.

english_verse: Just because you live like a Jew and are circumcised doesn't make you a real Jew.
translation_verse: Anɔ etinu Yudaeyi onupɛ ishupi mɛ ete ko yɛalɛ, pɛnɔsɛ kɛhɔ̃a bhui bhuiɛ nu ishudzya mɛ ibui ete ko.

english_verse: To be a real Jew you must obey the Law. True circumcision is something that happens deep in your heart, not something done to your body. And besides, you should want praise from God and not from humans.
translation_verse: Ao, anunɔ gɛ enu Yudaeyi tututuɔ yɛenu anɔ gɛ alɛ yɛishu mɛ, kɛlɛ yɛhɔ̃a bhui bhuiɛ nu kitúɔ ɛhɔ̃a bhui bhui odenɔ ɔfɔfuĩa mɛ, kɛlɛ anɛ nɛ ɔsha gɛ bafɔnɔ eluɔ nulũ. Anɔ alɛ nɛidi inye kpɛkpɛ nɛititíɛ, anɛ banɔ ɔkɔ kide pɔ Owusoso Akaa ɔkɔ.

english_verse: What good is it to be a Jew? What good is it to be circumcised?
translation_verse: Otsiɛ nɔ pɔ, kinye yɛyɛkɛ dɛ ɖuwoe gɛ lɛ kɛlɛ̀ mɛ ɔsɛ anɔ teli enu Yudaeyi, alo tɛpɛpɛ̀ dɛ ɖuwoe gɛ lɛ kɛhɔ̃a bhui bhui mɛ?

english_verse: It is good in a lot of ways! First of all, God's messages were spoken to the Jews.
translation_verse: Ebuiɛ gɛ bamɔɔ, lekpè! Kɛgi putɔ ɔkɔ alɛ onu ɔsɛ, Yudanɛ ahɔɛ mɛ Owusoso Akaa alɔkɔ yɛebuiɛ akpɛ.

english_verse: It is true that some of them did not believe the message. But does this mean that God cannot be trusted, just because they did not have faith?
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ kɛbazã onupɛ balɛ mɛ eteli agɛyɛkɛ aŋã? Balɛ ibui abha mayɛkɛ ŋãa bakɔ ɔsɛ Owusoso Akaa tsyɛ abayɔlɔ ɛbui gɛlɛ ɛlɛ ɔzãkɔɔ tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ dza?

english_verse: No, indeed! God tells the truth, even if everyone else is a liar. The Scriptures say about God, “Your words will be proven true, and in court you will win your case.”
translation_verse: Kɛma ɛlɛ mɛ ana! Banunɔ pete edzi asɛnɛ elete tsyɛ elo, Owusoso Akaa xɔnɔ atɛba yɔlɔ kɛgɛ lɛ ɛlɛ ɔzãkɔɔ tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ. Asɛ kɛfɔ̀nɔ gɛ bafɔnɔ ɛlɛ elu asɛ, “Sɛ tɛ bayɛkɛ wɔebui nabha aŋã, onupɛ ɔɖɔaɖɔɔ kɛlɛ tɛ bebhui bɔtsyɔa akɔ wɔ.”

english_verse: If our evil deeds show how right God is, then what can we say? Is it wrong for God to become angry and punish us? What a foolish thing to ask.
translation_verse: Onupɛ bɔlɔ ebui abha mabhɛtɛ mabhɛtɛ lakɔ asɛ Owusoso Akaa ebui abha tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ xɔnɔ awɔ̃ɛ piapii nɔ, kiɖuwoe bɔbaɖɔo? Bɔbaɖɔ ɔsɛ Owusoso Akaa agɛna bhɛtɛ onupɛ akpɛ ɔmɔpɔɛ nɛ ɔlɔ shuɔ dza?

english_verse: But the answer is, “No.” Otherwise, how could God judge the world?
translation_verse: Kɛma ɛlɛ mɛ ana! Onupɛ kɛlɛ ɛlɛ̀ mɛ ana pɔɔ, kɛlɛ Owusoso Akaa abalà kebhu ekulu nɛ̀ kanɔ nɔ̀ɔ dza?

english_verse: Since your lies bring great honor to God by showing how truthful he is, you may ask why God still says you are a sinner.
translation_verse: Anɔ teli abapɔ̃ɛ ɛlɛ mɛasɛ, “Onupɛ mɛasɛnɛ sɛsɛ yɛakɔ asɛ tɛ Owusoso Akaa ibui gbangbã bhɛbhɛtɛ dalɛ yɛishu gelee nɔ, kiɖeso pɛ beze bebu mɛ ɛpɔ asɛ ebui kpa abhɛbhɛtɛelete mɛinu?”

english_verse: You might as well say, “Let's do something evil, so that something good will come of it!” Some people even claim that we are saying this. But God is fair and will judge them as well. No One Is Good
translation_verse: Kiɖe pɛ nɔtɛ ɔɖɔ ɔsɛ, nɛ kana gɛ bezĩ bɔlɔ shu afalanɔ pɛ baaɖɔ ebui kpa nɛ ɔlɔ shu soɔ, “Nɔkɔ bɔbhɛtɛ ibui kpa tɛ bɔmɔ kɛgbangbã boŋ edenɔ ɛlɛ mɛɛ dza?” Nɛ kɛlɛ nulũɔ, balɛ kɛpɔ bubúɔ lɛ kɛlɛ ɔzãkɔ. Anɔ Nanà Etinu Anɔ Gbangbã

english_verse: What does all this mean? Does it mean that we Jews are better off than the Gentiles? No, it doesn't! Jews, as well as Gentiles, are ruled by sin, just as I have said.
translation_verse: Otsiɛ nɔ pɔ, kiɖuwoe lu bɔbaɖɔ? Bɔbaɖɔ ɔsɛ bɔpɛ̀ dɛ anɔ teli dza? Ao, kɛma ɛlɛ mɛ ana kura! Bɔtɛ ibu kɛlɛ mɛ kpɛ otugbà ɔsɛ, bagɛ enu Yudanɛ nɛ̀ bagɛ etinu Yudanɛ pete alɛ ebui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ esi.

english_verse: The Scriptures tell us, “No one is acceptable to God!
translation_verse: Asɛ kɛfɔ̀nɔ gɛ bafɔnɔ elu asɛ, “Anɔ nanà ama gɛ enu anɔ gbangbã, tenikpo ete tsyɛ ama,

english_verse: Not one of them understands or even searches for God.
translation_verse: Anɔ nanà ama gɛ elũ ebui esi, kɛlɛ tenikpo ete ama gɛ ebisi Owusoso Akaa nɛtɔ.

english_verse: They have all turned away and are worthless. There isn't one person who does right.
translation_verse: Balɛ pete betsyina ɛdzo, balɛ ɔmayɛmɛ nanà abhama ɛnanà mɛ, balɛ anɔ tenikpo tsyɛ atɛ abhɛtɛ kibui gbangbã tenikpo pɛ tsyɛ ama.”

english_verse: Their words are like an open pit, and their tongues are good only for telling lies. Each word is as deadly as the fangs of a snake,
translation_verse: “Balɛ ahalukoe nɔ enu idzeɖe gɛ lɛyaka dzi, balɛ enikeɛ, asɛnɛ ebui laɖɔ.” “Beblidzya gulũ abɔɛ kalɛ alɛ balɛ olũgbuɔ shu.”

english_verse: and they say nothing but bitter curses.
translation_verse: “Ebui ɖɔɖɔ kpɛ anɔ nɛ̀ agɛ apɔɛ nɔ, alɛ eyuku balɛ ilugbũɔ mɛ.”

english_verse: These people quickly become violent.
translation_verse: “Bebu tɛtsɛɖɛ mɛ akɔ dzyosu esi tɛ babàlɛ,

english_verse: Wherever they go, they leave ruin and destruction.
translation_verse: bɔdɔ̃ zizĩ nɛ̀ ɔwɔ̃nyɔnɔ ɔlɛ obu alɛ edzoɔ akasɛ nɛ balɛ mɛ,

english_verse: They don't know how to live in peace.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ ishu yɔyɔ ɛdzo xɔnɔɔ, betinyi.”

english_verse: They don't even fear God.”
translation_verse: “Betisì Owusoso Akaa nɛifu nɛ kɛnanà mɛ.”

english_verse: We know that everything in the Law was written for those who are under its power. The Law says these things to stop anyone from making excuses and to let God show that the whole world is guilty.
translation_verse: Otsi alɛ bunyi sɛ kɛka nyia ka gɛ ɔsha ɔɖɔɔ, kinu bagɛ balɛ ɔsha niesi ibui, tɛ kɛkɔ ɔsɛ anɔ nyia anɔ ataatɔ̃, tɛ Owusoso Akaa ekulu ebhu nɛ̀ kanɔ nɔ̀ petee.

english_verse: God doesn't accept people simply because they obey the Law. No, indeed! All the Law does is to point out our sin. God's Way of Accepting People
translation_verse: Kliso batɛ balɔkɔ anɔ nanà asɛ enu anɔ gbangbã nɛ Owusoso Akaa namɛ, sɛ gɛ abhɛtɛ ɔsha nabha so, pɔ gana ɔsha mɛ boŋuɔ, buuti sɛ ebui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ alɛ. Gana Yesu Abhaa Balɔkɔ Anɔ Asɛ Enu Anɔ Gbangbã

english_verse: Now we see how God does make us acceptable to him. The Law and the Prophets tell how we become acceptable, and it isn't by obeying the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Owusoso Akaa ebu akasɛ ɔlɔ otsi alɛ nɛ kɛlɔkɔ gɛ balɔkɔ anɔ teli asɛ enu anɔ gbangbã, gɛ anɛ ɔsha mɛ kide ba. Ɔsha nɛ̀ Ebuiɖɔluanɛ adalɛ kɛlɛishu.

english_verse: God treats everyone alike. He accepts people only because they have faith in Jesus Christ.
translation_verse: Anɔ gbangbã dzidzi alɛ, Owusoso Akaa ɔkɔ kide ɔgana Yesu Kristo abha tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛ ɔkɔ bagɛ ayɛkɛ aŋã petee. Egibu ananà ɔtɔ,

english_verse: All of us have sinned and fallen short of God's glory.
translation_verse: ɔmɔsɛ anɔ ka nyia ka abhɛtɛ ibui kpa, kɛlɛ Owusoso Akaa yɛishu dadalɛ yala nɛ bɔlɔ nyɔ̃ɔ shu,

english_verse: But God treats us much better than we deserve, and because of Christ Jesus, he freely accepts us and sets us free from our sins.
translation_verse: kɛlɛ bebhui utsyɔa gbangbã akɔ ɔlɔ ɔgana Yesu Kristo gɛ abalɛ dzyosu nɛ ɔlɔ ɛtakpu nabha.

english_verse: God sent Christ to be our sacrifice. Christ offered his life's blood, so by faith in him we could come to God. And God did this to show that in the past he was right to be patient and forgive sinners. This also shows that God is right when he accepts people who have faith in Jesus.
translation_verse: Abhɛtɛ ana nɛ kibe ɛlɛ sɛ tɛ alɔkɔ akasɛ asɛ, enu anunɔ gɛ abhɛtɛ ibui nɛ ɛlɛ ɔzãkɔ. Kidzo ɛlɛ nabhaa, yɛalɔkɔ bagɛ ayɛkɛ Yesu abha aŋã tsyɛ asɛ benu banɔ gbangbã.

english_verse: What is left for us to brag about? Not a thing! Is it because we obeyed some law? No! It is because of faith.
translation_verse: Fakɔ kura ishu fefeke lɛ? Kɛma ɛlɛ mɛ. Nɛ idzo ɖuwoe lu abha? Kɛlinu sɛ ɔbhɛtɛ nɛ ɔsha nabha loo? Ao, nɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛ boŋ.

english_verse: We see that people are acceptable to God because they have faith, and not because they obey the Law.
translation_verse: Kɛgɛ bɔnyana vunɔ kɛli nu ɔsɛ balɔkɔ anɔ teli asɛ enu anɔ gbangbã onupɛ ayɛkɛ Yesu abha aŋã, etinu asɛ ɔsha nabha tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ.

english_verse: Does God belong only to the Jews? Isn't he also the God of the Gentiles? Yes, he is!
translation_verse: Owusoso Akaa pɔ, Yudanɛ ayɛ ete ko yɛenu dza? Anɛ bagɛ betinu Yudanɛ tsyɛ ayɛ yɛenuɔ dza? Ee, bagɛ betinu Yudanɛ tsyɛ ayɛ yɛenu.

english_verse: There is only one God, and he accepts Gentiles as well as Jews, simply because of their faith.
translation_verse: Gɛ Owusoso Akaa alɛ tenikpo ko yɛ alɛ gɛ abalɔkɔ bagɛ benu Yudanɛ asɛ benu banɔ gbangbã nɛ balɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã lũɔ, kidzo ɛlɛ nabha bagɛ betinu Yudanɛ tsyɛ denɔ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã tinikpo ɛlɛ mɛ.

english_verse: Do we destroy the Law by our faith? Not at all! We make it even more powerful.
translation_verse: Kliso otsi alɛɛ, nɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã lɛ nulũ soɔ bɔba shɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ ɔsha shu dza? Ao, kɛma ɛlɛ mɛ ana kura! Pɔ, kɛlɛ sɛ tɛ bɔnyana ɔlɛ mɛ vunɔ gagalĩi.

english_verse: Well then, what can we say about our ancestor Abraham?
translation_verse: Otsiɛ nɔ pɔ, kiɖuwoe lu bɔbaɖɔ nɛ bɔlɔ ashuĩ Abraham shu ɔsɛ amɔ nɛ kibui ɛlɛ mɛ?

english_verse: If he became acceptable to God because of what he did, then he would have something to brag about. But he would never be able to brag about it to God.
translation_verse: Onupɛ kɛgbã, balɔkɔ Abraham asɛ enu anɔ gbangbã nɛ yɛaxɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa lũ soɔ, kɛlɛ kɛdɔ̃ tili zã yɛahɔɛ gɛ kɛlɛishu yɛalɔkɔ etũ kitelũ nɔ, pɔ anɛ nɛ Owusoso Akaa yɛamɛ.

english_verse: The Scriptures say, “God accepted Abraham because Abraham had faith in him.”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ́ Kibuiɛ ɖɔ? “Abraham ayɛkɛ Owusoso Akaa nabha aŋã kɛlɛ kɔ ɔsɛ balɔkɔ ɛlɛ nɛishu asɛ kinu ibui gbangbã.”

english_verse: Money paid to workers isn't a gift. It is something they earn by working.
translation_verse: Onupɛ anɔ teli awɔɛ ɛxɔ̃ɛɛ, yɛbhɛ babuia nɔ tinu asɛ kɛdɔ̃ gɛ balɔkɔ akɔɛ ete, pɔ kinu kibui tɔtɔɖɔ olu nɛ kɛxɔ̃ɛ shu.

english_verse: But you cannot make God accept you because of something you do. God accepts sinners only because they have faith in him.
translation_verse: Anɔ teliɛ anɛ kɛxɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa mɛ yɛagã, pɔ ayɛkɛ Owusoso Akaa gɛ abhɛtɛ ɛtakpu mɛ gagalĩelete yɛedzi anɔ gbangbã nabha aŋãa, yɛtɛyɛyɛkɛŋãa tɛkɔ ɔsɛ tɛ edzi anɔ gbangbã.

english_verse: In the Scriptures David talks about the blessings that come to people who are acceptable to God, even though they don't do anything to deserve these blessings. David says,
translation_verse: David aɖɔ kɛlɛ idi asɛ, anɔ gɛ Owusoso Akaa alɔkɔ asɛ enu anɔ gbangbã, pɔ anɛ nɛ yɛxɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa lũɔ, enu anɔ yuyula. Aɖɔ asɛ,

english_verse: Are these blessings meant for circumcised people or for those who are not circumcised? Well, the Scriptures say that God accepted Abraham because Abraham had faith in him.
translation_verse: Tiyuyula lɛɛ pɔ, tɛ lɛ ɔkɔ bagɛ enu Yudanɛ ete ko loo, pɛnɔsɛ bagɛ betinu Yudanɛ tsyɛɛ dza? Bɔzɛɖɔ ɔsɛ Abraham enu anɔ gbangbã nɛ yɛtɛyɛyɛkɛŋãa ulũ so.

english_verse: But when did this happen? Was it before or after Abraham was circumcised? Of course, it was before.
translation_verse: Ɛkpã ɖuwoe mɛ yɛazã flogo pɛ balɔkɔɛ asɛ edzi anɔ gbangbã? Kɛhɔ̃a bebhui akɔɛ flogo loo, pɛnɔsɛ gɛ bekebhui ɛlɛ akɔɛ nɛibeɛ dza? Anɛ kidegba ibe, pɔ kɛba kpɛ otugbà.

english_verse: Abraham let himself be circumcised to show he had been accepted because of his faith even before he was circumcised. This makes Abraham the father of all who are acceptable to God because of their faith, even though they are not circumcised.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ Abraham ayɛkɛ kɛhɔ̃a bhui bhui ibui, gɛ kidzi kɛklagbu tɔtɔɖɔ nɛ yɛanɔ gbangbã gɛ balɔkɔɛ odenɔ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛ, gɛ ekebhui kɛhɔ̃a flogo. Kɛlɛ yɛedzi Akaa akɔ bagɛ bayɛkɛ aŋã, pɔ begibhui kɛhɔ̃a flogo, denɔ kɛlɛ mɛɛ sɛ tɛ balɔkɔ alɛ asɛ benu banɔ gbangbã.

english_verse: This also makes Abraham the father of everyone who is circumcised and has faith in God, as Abraham did before he was circumcised. The Promise Is for All Who Have Faith
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ yɛenu Akaa akɔ bagɛ bebhui kɛhɔ̃a, gɛ anɛ kɛhɔ̃a ete ko bebhui, pɔ bagã nɛ Abraham yɛtɛyɛyɛkɛŋã idzo ɛlɛ nabha flogo pɛ ebebhui kɛhɔ̃a.

english_verse: God promised Abraham and his descendants that he would give them the world. This promise wasn't made because Abraham had obeyed a law, but because his faith in God made him acceptable.
translation_verse: Anɛ gana ɔsha mɛ flogo pɛ Abraham nɛ̀ yɛeyi alɛ bayɛkɛ tɛtɔtɔɖɔ asɛ kesiakpa ebedzi balɛ aŋãsi, pɔ gana anɔ gbangbã gɛ bedzi odenɔ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã ulũ mɛ.

english_verse: If Abraham and his descendants were given this promise because they had obeyed a law, then faith would mean nothing, and the promise would be worthless.
translation_verse: Onupɛ bagɛ eshu ɔsha balɛ ebedzi kaŋãsiɛeleteɛ, kɛlɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã dzi kibui ete ko, kɛlɛ tɛtɔtɔɖɔ oluɔ tsyɛ bha ma kɛnanà mɛ,

english_verse: God becomes angry when his Law is broken. But where there isn't a law, it cannot be broken.
translation_verse: ɔmɔsɛ ɔmɔpɔɛ ibui ɔsha lɔmana. Kɛlɛ ɛkɛlɛ gɛ ɔsha ɔmaa, kibui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ tsyɛ tɛzã.

english_verse: Everything depends on having faith in God, so that God's promise is assured by his gift of undeserved grace. This promise isn't only for Abraham's descendants who have the Law. It is for all who are Abraham's descendants because they have faith, just as he did. Abraham is the ancestor of us all.
translation_verse: Kliso tɛtɔtɔɖɔ ɛlɛ de tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛ, sɛ tɛ kidzi asɛ tibhui bhuibu gɛ badɛ mɛ alɔkɔ akɔ bagɛ benu Abraham yɛeyi alɛ pete, anɛ ɔkɔ banunɔ gɛ beshù ɔsha ete ko, pɔ ɔkɔ bagɛ bayɛkɛ aŋã asɛ Abraham ana. Gɛ yɛenu akaa akɔ bɔlɔ petee nɔ.

english_verse: The Scriptures say that Abraham would become the ancestor of many nations. This promise was made to Abraham because he had faith in God, who raises the dead to life and creates new things.
translation_verse: Asɛ kɛfɔ̀nɔ gɛ bafɔnɔ ɛlɛ elu asɛ, “Ɛbhɛtɛ wɔ sɛ tɛ odzi Akaa ɔkɔ ɛmadi gelee.” Yɛenu bɔlɔ Akaa nɛ Owusoso Akaa namɛ, anunɔ abha gɛ ayɛkɛ aŋã. Owusoso Akaa gɛ akɔ bekpu bamɛ ɛkpã, kɛlɛ ekulu bɔdɔ̃ gɛgɛ̀zã kuku ibeɛ amana otsiɛ.

english_verse: God promised Abraham a lot of descendants. And when it all seemed hopeless, Abraham still had faith in God and became the ancestor of many nations.
translation_verse: Gɛ tɛtɔtɔɖɔ kɔɛ ɛlɛ gɛzɛba kɛlɛ mɛ tsyɛ elo, Abraham akpɛemi ayɛkɛ aŋã, kɛlɛ kɔ ɔsɛ ebedzi Akaa ɔkɔ amadi gelee, asɛ kitseɖeɛ gɛ betseɖe yɛasɛ, “Kana wɔeyi mɔmɔ alɛ abazãa.”

english_verse: But Abraham never doubted or questioned God's promise. His faith made him strong, and he gave all the credit to God.
translation_verse: Gɛ kɛzã ana tsyɛ elo, ayɛkɛ aŋã asɛ kɛgɛ Owusoso Akaa atɔɖɔ akɔɛ bàba ɛlɛ mɛ, azamɔ ohumi atɔɖɔ nɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛ kɛlɛ aakpɛ Owusoso Akaa nɛinye nɛitití,

english_verse: Abraham was certain that God could do what he had promised.
translation_verse: gɛ ayɛkɛ aŋã piapii asɛ ohumi ɔlɛ Owusoso Akaa ahɔɛ sɛ abalà abhɛtɛ kɛgɛ atɔɖɔ asɛ ababhɛtɛɛ.

english_verse: just as we read in the Scriptures. But these words were not written only for Abraham.
translation_verse: Ebui alɛ asɛ “balɔkɔɛ ɛlɛ akɔɛɛ” gɛ anɛ yɛ ete bafɔnɔ alɛ akɔ,

english_verse: They were written for us, since we will also be accepted because of our faith in God, who raised our Lord Jesus to life.
