translation_verse: pɔ ɔkɔ ɔlɔ tsyɛ, gɛ Owusoso Akaa balɔkɔ asɛ bunu banɔ gbangbã, bɔlɔ agɛ yɛkɛ yɛ gɛ efeke Yesu bɔlɔ Apaleteɛ edenɔ bekpu mɛ abha aŋãa.

english_verse: God gave Jesus to die for our sins, and he raised him to life, so that we would be made acceptable to God.
translation_verse: Balɔkɔɛ akpɛ ahɔɛ akɔ butsi nɛ bɔlɔ ebui kpa ɛtakpu, pɔ Kitití ayɛ akɔ yɛalɔ nɛ bekpuɔ mɛ, sɛ tɛ balɔkɔ bɔlɔ asɛ bunu banɔ gbangbã.

english_verse: By faith we have been made acceptable to God. And now, thanks to our Lord Jesus Christ, we have peace with God.
translation_verse: Kliso gɛ balɔkɔ ɔlɔ asɛ bunu banɔ gbangbã ɔgana tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛɛ, ishu yɔyɔ lɛ ɔlɔ nɛ̀ Owusoso Akaa nɛishi, ɔgana bɔlɔ Apalete Yesu Kristo abha,

english_verse: Christ has also introduced us to God's gift of undeserved grace on which we now take our stand. So we are happy, as we look forward to sharing in the glory of God.
translation_verse: anɔ gɛ yɛabha bɔgã pɛ bɔmɔ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã nɛ̀ tibhui bhuibu ulɛ mɛ gɛ bɔlɛ otsiɛ. Kɛlɛ samɛ eku ulɔ gɛ buulu awɔ̃ nɛ Owusoso Akaa yɛishu dadalɛ nabha.

english_verse: But that's not all! We gladly suffer, because we know that suffering helps us to endure.
translation_verse: Anɛ kɛlɛ ete ko, pɔ bɔmɔ same tsyɛ nɛ bɔlɔ ɔtɔ mɛ, ɔmɔsɛ bunyi sɛ bɔtɔ kɔ sɛ anɔ akpɛemi,

english_verse: that will never disappoint us. All of this happens because God has given us the Holy Spirit, who fills our hearts with his love.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ awɔ̃luluɔ atɛwɔɛ anɔ, ɔmɔsɛ Owusoso Akaa yɛtɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ̀ɔ yɛalɔkɔ eyuku bɔlɔ etúɔ mɛ nɔ, ɔgana Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã gɛ akɔ ɔlɔ mɛ.

english_verse: Christ died for us at a time when we were helpless and sinful.
translation_verse: Nɛ kibe gbangbã mɛ gɛ kibui bhɛbhɛtɛ nanà gazã bɔlɔ ahɔɛɛ, Kristo ebetsí nɛ bagɛ betinyi Owusoso Akaa nɛ̀ ɛtakpu.

english_verse: No one is really willing to die for an honest person, though someone might be willing to die for a truly good person.
translation_verse: Abagagalĩ ɔsɛ tɛ anɔ teli etsí nɛ anɔ gbangbã ɛtakpu, pɔ nɛ eyamɛ gbangbãelete teli itakpu xɔnɔɔ, anɔ teli abadɛmɛ asɛ tɛ etsí.

english_verse: But God showed how much he loved us by having Christ die for us, even though we were sinful.
translation_verse: Pɔ Owusoso Akaa ebu kɛdɛmɛ gɛ adɛmɛ nɛ̀ ɔlɔ akasɛ nɛidzo ɛlɛ nabha asɛ, gɛ buwlui zã ɔlɔ ebui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ abhaa, Kristo etsí nɛ ɔlɔ ɛtakpu.

english_verse: But there is more! Now that God has accepted us because Christ sacrificed his life's blood, we will also be kept safe from God's anger.
translation_verse: Otsi alɛ gɛ bebhui utsyɔa akɔ ɔlɔ ɔgana yɛdzyosu nɔ mɛɛ, kidzo ɛlɛ nabha ke yɛebebu ɔlɔ, odenɔ Owusoso Akaa yɛɔmɔpɔɛ nɔ mɛ.

english_verse: Even when we were God's enemies, he made peace with us, because his Son died for us. Yet something even greater than friendship is ours. Now that we are at peace with God, we will be saved by the life of his Son.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ nɛ kibeɛ gɛ bɔgɛ dɛmɛ nɛ Owusoso Akaa, Yɛeyiɛ ebevũ iboe nɛ bɔlɔɛishi, ɔgana yɛutsíɛ mɛ. Gɛ evũ iboe ɛlɛ anaa, kɛlɛ́ pɛ atɛ balà ɔlɔ ebu ɔgana yɛkpã gɛ alɔkɔ eluɔ mɛɛ dza?

english_verse: And in addition to everything else, we are happy because God sent our Lord Jesus Christ to make peace with us. Adam and Christ
translation_verse: Anɛ kɛlɛ ete ko, pɔ bɔmɔ same tsyɛ nɛ Owusoso Akaa mɛ ɔgana bɔlɔ Apalete Yesu Kristo abha, anunɔ gɛ yɛabhaa bɔgã, pɛ bɔmɔ tenikpo bhɛbhɛtɛ. Kɛgɛ Kɔ Ɔsɛ Adam Nɛ̀ Kristo Bagɛ Shɔ̃

english_verse: Adam sinned, and that sin brought death into the world. Now everyone has sinned, and so everyone must die.
translation_verse: Kliso asɛ gana anɔ tenikpo abha pɛ kibui kpa ku kanɔ nɔ̀ nyɔ̃ɔ mɛ, kɛlɛ gana kibui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ mɛ butsí ba banɔ nyɔ̃ɔ abha, ɔmɔsɛ balɛ nyɔ̃ɔ abhɛtɛ ibui kpa-

english_verse: Sin was in the world before the Law came. But no record of sin was kept, because there was no Law.
translation_verse: ɔmɔsɛ flogo pɛ bakɔ ɔshaa, kibui kpa zã kanɔ nɔ̀ mɛ kukuku. Pɔ gɛ ɔsha gɛzã soɔ bagɛ lɔkɔ kɛlɛ akɔ ananà asɛ abhɛtɛ ibui kpa.

english_verse: Yet death still had power over all who lived from the time of Adam to the time of Moses. This happened, though not everyone disobeyed a direct command from God, as Adam did. In some ways Adam is like Christ who came later.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ butsí zɛŋã ukusi odenɔ Adam ibe ɔmana Mose abha, gɛ bagɛta esi asɛ Adam yɛibui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ ana tsyɛ flogo. Anunɔ gɛ yɛtɛzazã mɛ lɛ asɛ anunɔ gɛ ababaa.

english_verse: But the gift of God's undeserved grace was very different from Adam's sin. That one sin brought death to many others. Yet in an even greater way, Jesus Christ alone brought God's gift of undeserved grace to many people.
translation_verse: Pɔ kɛdɔ̃ kɔkɔ ɛlɛ gã ɔtɔ nɛ Adam esitata shu. Onupɛ banɔ gelee betsí aganà anɔ tenikpo alɛ gɛ ata esi soɔ, kiɖeso pɛ Owusoso Akaa yɛtibhui bhuibu nɛ̀ kɛdɔ̃ kɔkɔ gikoeyi odenɔ tibhui bhuibu odenɔ anyɛ tenikpo alɛ, gɛ enu Yesu Kristo, gɛ atsakɛ afala nɛ banunɔ nyɔ̃ɔ mɛ.

english_verse: There is a lot of difference between Adam's sin and God's gift. That one sin led to punishment. But God's gift made it possible for us to be acceptable to him, even though we have sinned many times.
translation_verse: Kɛdɔ̃ gɛ Owusoso Akaa akɔɔ, tinu anɔ tenikpo alɛ ete esi tatá shu ibui. Kebhu kukulu ɔkɔ kibui kpa tinikpo nɔ mana kɛpɔ bubu, pɔ kɛdɔ̃ kɔkɔ xɔnɔ kɔ ɔsɛ banɔ gelee gɛ atá esiɛ, bebhui bɔtsyɔa akɔ alɛ.

english_verse: Death ruled like a king because Adam had sinned. But that cannot compare with what Jesus Christ has done. God has treated us with undeserved grace, and he has accepted us because of Jesus. And so we will live and rule like kings.
translation_verse: Onupɛ gana anɔ tenikpo alɛ esi tatá mɛ butsí ŋã bukusi ɔgana yɛtakpuɔ, kɛlɛ́ kɛbazã ɔkɔ bagɛ ayɛkɛ Owusoso Akaa tibhui bhuibu gelee nɔ nɛ̀ ɛlɛ ɔdɔ̃ kɔkɔ ginu ishu dadalɛ ɛkpã ukusi ŋaŋã ɔgana anɔ tenikpo alɛ gɛenu Yesu Kristoɔ abha dza?

english_verse: Everyone was going to be punished because Adam sinned. But because of the good thing that Christ has done, God accepts us and gives us the gift of life.
translation_verse: Gɛ gana anɔ tenikpo yɛesi tatá ɛtakpu so bebu banunɔ petee ɛpɔɔ, kidzo ɛlɛ nabhaa, gana anɔ tenikpo yɛibui gbangbã bhɛbhɛtɛ ɛtakpu soɔ, bebu bɔtsyɔa akɔ banunɔ nyɔ̃ɔ.

english_verse: Adam disobeyed God and caused many others to be sinners. But Jesus obeyed him and will make many people acceptable to God.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔ asɛ gana anɔ tenikpo alɛ batɔ̃ malũ malu mɛɛ, banɔ petee edzi ebui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛeleteɛ, kidzo ɛlɛ nabha ke gana anɔ tenikpo alɛ batɔ̃ lulũ mɛɛ, babàbhɛtɛ banɔ gele tɛ bedzi banɔ gbangbã.

english_verse: The Law came, so that the full power of sin could be seen. Yet where sin was powerful, God's gift of undeserved grace was even more powerful.
translation_verse: Bakɔ ɔsha aplɛnɔ, gɛ kɛkɔ ɔsɛ ɔshaa esi tatá tsyɛ iziwu abha. Pɔ ɛkɛlɛ gɛ ebui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ ekpè atɔɖɔɔ, tibhui bhuibuɔ tsyɛ ikpè tɔɖɔ gelee ɔdɛ.

english_verse: Sin ruled by means of death. But God's gift of grace now rules, and God has accepted us because of Jesus Christ our Lord. This means that we will have eternal life.
translation_verse: Asɛ kɛŋã gɛ kibui kpa ŋã bukusi nɛ bekpu mɛɛ, kidzo ɛlɛ nabha tibhui bhuibu tsyɛ baŋã bukusi ɔgana kɛgbangbã mɛ, sɛ tɛ banɔ amɔ ɛkpã tihɔɔ ɔgana Yesu Kristo bɔlɔ Apalete nabha.

english_verse: What should we say? Should we keep on sinning, so that God's gift of undeserved grace will show up even better?
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ bɔbaɖɔ otsiɛ? Bɔbaavɛ ɛlɛ abha zɛbhɛtɛ ebui kpaa ko, tɛ kɛkɔ ɔsɛ ɔlɔ tibhui bhuibuɔ zikpè tɔɖɔ koɔ dza?

english_verse: No, we should not! If we are dead to sin, how can we go on sinning?
translation_verse: Kɛma ɛlɛ mɛ ana! Gɛ butitsí kɔ kibui kpaa, kiɖeso pɛ bɔbazã kɛlɛ tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ abha vɛnɔ otugbà?

english_verse: Don't you know that all who share in Christ Jesus by being baptized also share in his death?
translation_verse: Pɔɔ, notinyi ɔsɛ bɔlɔ pete gɛ bakpɛ ulĩ akɔ nɛ Kristo Yesu yɛinye mɛɛ, yɛutsíɛ mɛ tsyɛ bakpɛ ulĩɛ akɔ ɔlɔ dza?

english_verse: When we were baptized, we died and were buried with Christ. We were baptized, so we would live a new life, as Christ was raised to life by the glory of God the Father.
translation_verse: Kliso bavɔbɔ ɔlɔ nii ɔgana bulĩ kpɛkpɛ mɛ ɔvɛnɔ butsí mɛ, sɛ tɛ kɛzã ɔmɔ asɛ kifeke gɛ befeke Kristo edenɔ bekpuɔ mɛ ɔgana Yɛakaa ishu dadalɛ mɛɛ, sɛ tɛ bɔlɔ tsyɛ zã nɛ ɛkpã vɔvɔ.

english_verse: If we shared in Jesus' death by being baptized, we will be raised to life with him.
translation_verse: Onupɛ bɔbhɛtɛ tenikpo nii ana nɛ yɛutsíɛ mɛɛ, kidzo ɛlɛ nabha ɛbeɛ, bɔbabhɛtɛ tenikpo nii nɛ yɛtɛlɔlɔ odenɔ bekpuɔ mɛ tsyɛ.

english_verse: We know that the persons we used to be were nailed to the cross with Jesus. This was done, so our sinful bodies would no longer be the slaves of sin.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ bunyi sɛ bakpala bɔlɔ ɛkpã kukuɔ nɛ̀ Kristo tɛ kɛkɔ ɔsɛ bɔshɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ ishupiɛ gɛ dɛmɛ ebui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ shu lu, sɛ tɛ bɔtadzi banɔ dzidzi ɔkɔ ebui kpa-

english_verse: We know sin doesn't have power over dead people.
translation_verse: ɔmɔsɛ anɔ nyia anɔ gɛ etsíɛ, bebui eyinɔ nɛ ebui kpa ahɔɛ mɛ.

english_verse: As surely as we died with Christ, we believe we will also live with him.
translation_verse: Kliso onupɛ butsí nɛ̀ Kristoɔ, bɔyɛkɛŋã sɛ bɔbazã nɛ̀ ɛkpã tsyɛ nii.

english_verse: We know that death no longer has any power over Christ. He died and was raised to life, never again to die.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ bunyi sɛ gɛ bakɔ Kristo alɔ edenɔ bekpu mɛɛ, ezeke etsí, kɛlɛ butsí tɛka nu apalete nɛ yɛabha.

english_verse: When Christ died, he died for sin once and for all. But now he is alive, and he lives only for God.
translation_verse: Butsíɛ gɛ etsíɛ, ebui kpa yɛetsí bɔlɛ akɔ, nɛ kibe nyiɛ be ulũ, pɔ ɛkpã gɛ edzinɔɔ, alɛ ɛlɛ akɔ Owusoso Akaa.

english_verse: In the same way, you must think of yourselves as dead to the power of sin. But Christ Jesus has given life to you, and you live for God.
translation_verse: Kidzo ɛlɛ nabha lũɔ nɔlɔkɔ wɔnɔshu ɔmɔ asɛ banɔ tsitsí ɔkɔ ebui kpa, pɔ nonu anɔ gɛ ɔlɛ nɛ ɛkpã ɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa nɛ Kristo Yesu mɛ.

english_verse: Don't let sin rule your body. After all, your body is bound to die, so don't obey its desires
translation_verse: Kliso nɔtɛkɔ ɔsɛ ibui kpa aŋã ukusi nɛ wɔnɔ abha tɛ nɔzɛ ɔbhɛtɛ ɔtɔɖɔ kibui kpa tɛpɛpɛ ulũ.

english_verse: or let any part of it become a slave of evil. Give yourselves to God, as people who have been raised from death to life. Make every part of your body a slave that pleases God.
translation_verse: Nɔtɛ shɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ wɔnɔ ishupiɛ shu ɔkɔ nanà ɔkɔ kibui kpa, asɛ kɛxɔ̃ɛwadɔ̃ ɔkɔ kɛtakpu mɛ gagalĩ ebui bhɛbhɛtɛ, pɔ nɔshɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ wɔnɔshu ɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa ɔmɔ asɛ banɔ gɛ bebu edenɔ butsí mɛ ɔmana ɛkpã mɛ ana, tɛ nɔshɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ wɔnɔ ishupiɛ shu ɔkɔ nyia ɔkɔ ɔkɔɛ, asɛ bɔxɔ̃ɛwɔadɔ̃ ɔkɔ kibui gbangbã tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ.

english_verse: Don't let sin keep ruling your lives. You are ruled by God's undeserved grace and not by the Law. Slaves Who Do What Pleases God
translation_verse: Tɛkɔ ɔsɛ kibui kpa dzi wɔapalete, ɔmɔsɛ ɔma ɔsha niesi, pɔ tibhui bhuibu esi wɔlɛ. Banɔ Dzidzi Bunu Ɔkɔ Kibui Gbangbã Bhɛbhɛtɛ

english_verse: What does all this mean? Does it mean we are free to sin, because we are ruled by God's gift of undeserved grace and not by the Law? Certainly not!
translation_verse: Kiɖuwoe lu otsiɛ mɔ? Bɔbaa bhɛtɛ ibui kpa ɔmɔsɛ bɔma ɔsha niesi, pɔ tibhui bhuibu esi bɔlɛ soɔ dza? Kɛma ɛlɛ mɛ ana!

english_verse: Don't you know that you are slaves of anyone you obey? You can be slaves of sin and die, or you can be obedient slaves of God and be acceptable to him.
translation_verse: Notinyi ɔsɛ nɔlɔkɔ batɔ̃ lulũ ɔkɔ anɔ teli asɛ banɔ dzidzi anaa, nonu banɔ dzidzi ɔkɔ anunɔ gɛ nolũ atɔ̃ ɔkɔɔ, nonu banɔ dzidzi ɔkɔ kibui kpa gɛ gbanà anɔ ɔvɛnɔ butsí mɛ, alo ɔkɔ batɔ̃ lulũ gɛ gbanà anɔ ɔkpɛ kibui gbangbã bhɛbhɛtɛ mɛɛ dza?

english_verse: You used to be slaves of sin. But I thank God that with all your heart you followed the example set forth in the teaching you received.
translation_verse: Pɔ nohu Owusoso Akaa yɛahɔɛ ɔsɛ, wɔnɔ gɛ nodzi banɔ dzidzi ɔkɔ ebui kpa flogo nɔɔ, nɔbɔlɔkɔ wɔnɔ etúɔ nyɔ̃ɔ nɛ̀ batɔ̃ lulũ ɔyɛkɛ bɔdɔ̃ kakasɛ ɛlɛ, gɛ balɔkɔ akpɛ ahɔɛ akɔ wɔnɔ.

english_verse: Now you are set free from sin and are slaves who please God.
translation_verse: Bebu ɔnɔ eyinɔ nɛ ebui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ mɛ, kɛlɛ otsi alɛ nodzi banɔ dzidzi ɔkɔ kibui gbangbã tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ.

english_verse: I am using these everyday examples, because in some ways you are still weak. You used to let the different parts of your body be slaves of your evil thoughts. But now you must make every part of your body serve God, so that you will belong completely to him.
translation_verse: Ɛɖɔ kɛlɛ nɛ anɔdzyrame yɛekpeɖekpeɖe ulũ, ɔmɔsɛ nobotsò nɛ wɔnɔ tɛzazã mɛ. Asɛ kɛlɔkɔ gɛ nɔzɔlɔkɔ ɔnɔ ishuɔ ɛkɔ toli ɔkɔ asɛ banɔ dzidzi ɔkɔ atsɔ̃ ebui bhɛbhɛtɛ, nɛ̀ ɛtakpu mɛ gagalĩ ebui tɛ lekpè atɔɖɔɔ, kliso otsi alɛ nɔlɔkɔ ishu ɔkɔ asɛ banɔ dzidzi ɔkɔ kibui gbangbã gɛ gbanà anɔ ɔvɛnɔ tɛdadalɛ mɛ.

english_verse: When you were slaves of sin, you didn't have to please God.
translation_verse: Gɛ nodzì banɔ dzidzi ɔkɔ kibui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛɛ, bebu wɔnɔ eyinɔ nɛ kibui gbangbã bhɛbhɛtɛ gɛ ɛgbana anunɔ esi.

english_verse: But what good did you receive from the things you did? All you have to show for them is your shame, and they lead to death.
translation_verse: Kiɖe nɔmɔ odenɔ kibe ɛlɛ ebui bhɛbhɛtɛ gɛ lenu awuĩ ebui ɔkɔ ɔnɔ otsiɛ nɔ mɛɛ dza? Ebui alɛ agbana anɔ avɛnɔ butsí mɛ.

english_verse: Now you have been set free from sin, and you are God's slaves. This will make you holy and will lead you to eternal life.
translation_verse: Otsi alɛ gɛ bebu ɔnɔ eyinɔ nɛ ebui kpa mɛ, pɛ nodzi Owusoso Akaa yɛanɔ dzidziɛ, kɛgɛ nɔbɔ mɔ odenɔ kɛlɛ mɛɛ, kɛba gbana ɔnɔ ɔvɛnɔ tɛdadalɛ mɛ, kɛlɛ kɛgɛ badekoeyì nɛ kɛlɛ mɛ badzi ɛkpã tihɔɔ.

english_verse: Sin pays off with death. But God's gift is eternal life given by Jesus Christ our Lord.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ kɛbhɛ gɛ babuia ɔkɔ ebui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ, kɛli nu butsí, pɔ Owusoso Akaa yɛdɔ̃ kɔkɔ kɛli nu ɛkpã tihɔɔ nɛ Kristo Yesu bɔlɔ Apaleteɛ mɛ.

english_verse: My friends, you surely understand enough about law to know that laws only have power over people who are alive.
translation_verse: Bunyi notinyiɛ mɔ dza, mɛelɛ alɛ, bagɛ benyi ɔshaa mɛɖɔaɖɔ ɔkɔ ɔsɛ nɛ kibeɛ gɛ anɔ alɛ nɛ̀ ɛkpã koɔ, alɛ ɔsha esiɛ mɔɔ dza?

english_verse: For example, the Law says that a man's wife must remain his wife as long as he lives. But once her husband is dead, she is free
translation_verse: Nɛ kibui nulũɔ, ɔsha ɔnyã anyɛedzini nɛ yɛenyine shu nɛ kibeɛ gɛ anyɛ alɛ nɛ ɛkpã, pɔ onupɛ yɛenyineɛ etsíɛ, kɛlɛ ekoeyì nɛ tɛgbagbana ɔsha ɔlɛ niesi.

english_verse: to marry someone else. However, if she goes off with another man while her husband is still alive, she is said to be unfaithful.
translation_verse: Kliso onupɛ alɔ pɛ adɛzãna anyɛ bhlatɔ nɛ kibeɛ gɛ yɛenyineɛ akalɛ nɛ ɛkpãa, kɛlɛ edzi ɔmamɛ adɛdɛ anɔ. Pɔ onupɛ yɛenyineɛ etsí xɔnɔ, ekoeyì nɛ ɔsha mɛ, gɛ etikenu ɔmamɛ adɛdɛ anɔ, onupɛ avɛ adɛzãna anyɛ bhlatɔ tsyɛ.

english_verse: That is how it is with you, my friends. You are now part of the body of Christ and are dead to the power of the Law. You are free to belong to Christ, who was raised to life so we could serve God.
translation_verse: Kliso mɛelɛ alɛ mɔ, wɔnɔ tsyɛ notsí ɔkɔ ɔshaa ɔgana Kristo abha, tɛ nodzi tenikpo ɔkɔ anunɔ gɛ befeke edenɔ bekpuɔ mɛɛ, tɛ buyu busi eyuyu gbangbã ɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa.

english_verse: When we thought only of ourselves, the Law made us have sinful desires. It made every part of our bodies into slaves who are doomed to die.
translation_verse: Nɛ kibe ɛlɛ gɛ kanɔ mɛ ebuiɛ azagbana ɔlɔɔ, ebui nɔnɔ gɛ agbanà anɔ akpɛ ebui kpa mɛ nɛ ɔsha nulũɔ azã ɔlɔ mɛ, kɛlɛ agɛ bɔbhɛtɛ pete agbana ɔlɔ ɔkpɛ butsí mɛ.

english_verse: But the Law no longer rules over us. We are like dead people, and it cannot have any power over us. Now we can serve God in a new way by obeying his Spirit, and not in the old way by obeying the written Law. The Battle with Sin
translation_verse: Pɔ otsi alɛ gɛ buzitsí nɛ ɔsha gɛ ɔtɛ nya ɔlɔ oɖi shu oluɔ, bebu ɔlɔ edenɔ ɔshaa mɛ, tɛ kɛkɔ ɔsɛ bɔkpɛ ɛtakpu esi nɛ idzo vɔvɔ abha gana Ɔfɔfuĩa mɛ, kɛlɛ kitinu asɛ nɛ kidzo kukuɔ gɛ bafɔnɔ eluɔ nulũ. Kawlɔ̃akɛ Nɛ̀ Kibui Kpa Bhɛbhɛtɛ

english_verse: Does this mean that the Law is sinful? Certainly not! But if it had not been for the Law, I would not have known what sin is really like. For example, I would not have known what it means to want something that belongs to someone else, unless the Law had told me not to do this.
translation_verse: Otsiɛ nɔ pɔ, kiɖuwoe lu bɔbaɖɔ? Kibui kpa ɔsha onu dza? Ao, kɛma ɛlɛ mɛ ana! Kibui gbã kinu ɔsɛ, ɛgɛ tatì kɛdɔ̃ nɔnɔ gɛ kibui kpa nu, pɔ gana ɔshaa mɛɛ, iti ɛlɛ ana. Tsyɛ alo ɛgɛ tatì kɛgɛ inu kiwuhũ dzadzã nɛ anɔ teli idɔ̃ shu, onupɛ ɔsha ɔma ɔsɛ, “Tɛɔdzã kiwuhũ nɛ ɔhagbo ɛdɔ̃ shuɔ.”

english_verse: It was sin that used this command as a way of making me have all kinds of desires. But without the Law, sin is dead.
translation_verse: Pɔ gɛ kibui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ ŋã kibuiɛ gɛ ɔsha ɔɖɔ nabha soɔ, kɛkɔ ɔsɛ kawɔ̃kpɛ nɛ anɔ ɛdɔ̃ shu kpasɛ mɛ mɛ̀. Onupɛ ɔsha ɔmaa, kɛlɛ kibui kpa tsyɛ ma.

english_verse: Before I knew about the Law, I was alive. But as soon as I heard that command, sin came to life,
translation_verse: Ɛtɛzã nɛ̀ ɛkpã nɛ ɔshaa matimati mɛ iɖi. Pɔ kibeɛ gɛ ɔsha ɔba koɔ, kibui kpa lɔ nɛ ɛkpã, kɛlɛ mɛitsí.

english_verse: and I died. The very command that was supposed to bring life to me, instead brought death.
translation_verse: Ɛmɔ ɔsɛ ɔshaa gɛ kɛlɛ sɛ tɛ lɔkɔ mɛ ɛkpãa, ɔlɛ ke ɔlɛ otsyina ɔmana mɛ utsí.

english_verse: Sin used this command to trick me, and because of it I died.
translation_verse: Pɔ gɛ kibui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ mɔɛ idzo nɛ ɔsha nabha soɔ, kibui kpa wɔɛ mɛ, kɛlɛ nɛ ɔsha nulũɔ butsí ba mabha.

english_verse: Still, the Law and its commands are holy and correct and good.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ ɔshaa nɛ̀ ɔsha kpɛkpɛɛ shu dalɛ, lɔna kɛlɛ lɔpɛ̃.

english_verse: Am I saying something good caused my death? Certainly not! It was sin that killed me by using something good. Now we can see how terrible and evil sin really is.
translation_verse: Otsiɛ pɔ, kibuiɛ gɛinu kɛgbangbãa kɛli tsyina dzi butsí kɔ mɛɛ dza? Ao kɛma ɛlɛ mɛ ana! Pɔ tɛ kibui kpa bu ishu kasɛ asɛ kibui zizĩɛ, kɛgã kibui gbangbã nabha pɛ kibu butsí bakpɛ mɛ, so gana ɔsha kpɛkpɛ mɛɛ, sɛ tɛ kibui kpa wlui zĩ tɔɖɔ dɛ.

english_verse: We know that the Law is spiritual. But I am merely a human, and I have been sold as a slave to sin.
translation_verse: Bunyi ɔsha ɔsɛ ɔfɔfuĩa mɛibui lonu, kɛlɛ mɛ pɔɔ anɛ ɔfɔfuĩa mɛdɔ̃ mɛinu, gɛ ɛlɛ ɔmɔ asɛ anɔ dzidzì ɔkɔ kibui kpa ana.

english_verse: In fact, I don't understand why I act the way I do. I don't do what I know is right. I do the things I hate.
translation_verse: Itilũ kɛgɛ ɛbhɛtɛɛ, niesi. Ɔmɔsɛ kɛgɛ ɛpɛ sɛ tɛ ɛbhɛtɛɛ, ɛtɛlà kɛlɛ bhɛtɛ, pɔ kɛgɛ ɛgɛdɛ mɛɛ, mɛbhɛtɛ.

english_verse: Although I don't do what I know is right, I agree that the Law is good.
translation_verse: Onupɛ kɛgɛ ɛtɛdɛmɛ sɛ tɛ mɛbhɛtɛ pɛ kɛlɛ mɛbhɛtɛɛ, kɛlɛ ɛdɛmɛ tɔɖɔ ɔsɛ ɔsha ɔpɛ.

english_verse: So I am not the one doing these evil things. The sin that lives in me is what does them.
translation_verse: So kɛgɛ ɛbhɛtɛɛ otsiɛ nɔ anɛ mɛ alɛ mɛbhɛtɛ ɛlɛ, pɔ kibui kpaa gɛlɛ mɛ mɛɛ kɛlɛ bhɛtɛ.

english_verse: I know that my selfish desires won't let me do anything that is good. Even when I want to do right, I cannot.
translation_verse: Gɛ anɔdzyrame mɛinu soɔ, inyi sɛ kɛgbangbã nanà ma mɛmɛ̀. Ɔmɔsɛ mɛ tɛpɛpɛ kinu ɔsɛ tɛ ɛbhɛtɛ kibui gbangbã flogo, pɔ ɛtɛlà kɛlɛ bhɛtɛ.

english_verse: And so, if I don't do what I know is right, I am no longer the one doing these evil things. The sin that lives in me is what does them.
translation_verse: Onupɛ kɛgɛ ɛgɛpɛ ɔsɛ tɛ mɛbhɛtɛ pɛ kɛlɛ mɛbhɛtɛɛ otsiɛ nɔ, kɛlɛ anɛ mɛbhɛtɛ ɛlɛ, pɔ kibui kpa gɛ kpasɛ mɛ mɛɛ, kɛlɛ bhɛtɛ ɛlɛ.

english_verse: The Law has shown me that something in me keeps me from doing what I know is right.
translation_verse: Kliso ilũ ɔsha axɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa niesi ɔsɛ, onupɛ ɛpɛ sɛ tɛ mɛbhɛtɛ kibui gbangbãa, kibui zizĩɛ lɛ mɛɔbha nɛ ɛkɛlɛ.

english_verse: With my whole heart I agree with the Law of God.
translation_verse: Nɛ mɛishu mɛ pɔɔ, ɛmɔ same nɛ Owusoso Akaa ɔshaa shu flogo,

english_verse: But in every part of me I discover something fighting against my mind, and it makes me a prisoner of sin that controls everything I do.
translation_verse: pɔ iwlui mɔ ɔsha bhlatɔ gɛ lɔwɔa axɔ̃ɛ nɛ mɛishu udzyaa mɛ, gɛ lɔlɔkɔ keyu nɛ mɛekpeɖekpeɖe mɛ bhɛbhɛtɛ shu, kɛlɛ lɔkpɛ mɛ ɔɖavũ anɔ ɔkɔ ɔsha kpa gɛ ɔwɔa axɔ̃ɛ nɛ mɛishu udzya mɛ.

english_verse: What a miserable person I am. Who will rescue me from this body that is doomed to die?
translation_verse: Ashagabui elete ɖuwoe lu idi nɛ alɛ mɛinuɔ mɔ! Afana gɛ ebebu mɛishupi ɛlɛ eyinɔ nɛ butsi lɛ mɛ?

english_verse: Thank God! Jesus Christ will rescue me. So with my mind I serve the Law of God, although my selfish desires make me serve the law of sin.
translation_verse: Buhu Owusoso Akaa ahɔɛ-ɔgana bɔlɔ Apalete Yesu Kristo abha! Kliso mɛ xɔnɔ nɛ mɛekpeɖekpeɖe nulũɔɔ, inu anɔ dzidzì ɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa yɛɔsha, pɔ mɛ asɛ anɔdzyrame anaa, inu anɔ dzidzì ɔkɔ ebui kpa ɔshaa.

english_verse: If you belong to Christ Jesus, you won't be punished.
translation_verse: Otsiɛ alɛ, kɛpɔ bubu nanà ma ɔkɔ bagɛ alɛ Kristo Yesu mɛ,

english_verse: The Holy Spirit will give you life that comes from Christ Jesus and will set you free from sin and death.
translation_verse: ɔmɔsɛ gana Kristo Yesu abha, ɔfɔfuĩa mɛ ɛkpã ɔsha oboele mɛ odenɔ kibui kpa ɔsha nɛ̀ butsí mɛ.

english_verse: The Law of Moses cannot do this, because our selfish desires make the Law weak. But God set you free when he sent his own Son to be like us sinners and to be a sacrifice for our sin. God used Christ's body to condemn sin.
translation_verse: Kɛgɛ ɔsha ɔgɛmɔ ohumi ɔbhɛtɛ, ɔmɔsɛ kanɔ alɛ mɛ ɛkpã kpa ŋã ɔlɛ abha soɔ, kɛlɛ Owusoso Akaa abhɛtɛ aganà yɛahɔɛ Eyiɛ gɛ asɛsɛ nɛ ebui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ anɔ tɛzazã mɛ, tɛ edzi asɛ kɛdɔ̃ kɔkɔ nɛ ebui kpa ɛtakpu ana. Kliso ebu kibui kpa ɛpɔ nɛ kibui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ anɔ mɛ,

english_verse: He did this, so that we would do what the Law commands by obeying the Spirit instead of our own desires.
translation_verse: tɛ kɛkɔ ɔsɛ kɛpiapi nɔ gɛ bapɛ asɛ tɛ kɛna nɛ ɔsha nulũɔ, tɛ bamɔ kɛlɛ nɛ bɔlɔ mɛ. Bɔlɔ agɛ tɛzã nɛ̀ ebui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ mɛ, pɔ pɛ bɔzã nɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ tɛpɛpɛ ulũ.

english_verse: People who are ruled by their desires think only of themselves. Everyone who is ruled by the Holy Spirit thinks about spiritual things.
translation_verse: Bagɛ bazã nɛ ɛkpã asɛ kanɔ alɛ mɛanɔ anaa, balɛ ekpeɖekpeɖeɛ alɛ kanɔ nɔ̀ mɛ ebui tɛdɛdɛ mɛ nɔ shu, pɔ bagɛ balɛ nɛ ɛkpã nɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ tɛpɛpɛ ulũɔ, balɛ ekpeɖekpeɖeɛ alɛ kɛgɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ ɔdɛ mɛ nɔ shu.

english_verse: If our minds are ruled by our desires, we will die. But if our minds are ruled by the Spirit, we will have life and peace.
translation_verse: Kɛgɛinu ebui kpaelete yɛtɛtɔtɔɖɔɔ, kɛmana butsí, pɔ ekpeɖekpeɖeɛ gɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ lɔgbana xɔnɔ lonu ɛkpã nɛ̀ ishu yɔyɔ,

english_verse: Our desires fight against God, because they do not and cannot obey God's laws.
translation_verse: ebui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ ekpeɖekpeɖe enu kitití lɔlɔ nɛ Owusoso Akaa shu. Atɛ bhɛtɛ Owusoso Akaa ɔsha nabha, kɛlɛ atɛ balà abhɛtɛ tsyɛ.

english_verse: If we follow our desires, we cannot please God.
translation_verse: Bagɛ kanɔ alɛ mɛ ɛkpã gbana, batɛ balà eku Owusoso Akaa awɔ̃.

english_verse: You are no longer ruled by your desires, but by God's Spirit, who lives in you. People who don't have the Spirit of Christ in them don't belong to him.
translation_verse: Wɔnɔ xɔnɔ anɛ kanɔ alɛ mɛ ɛkpã kpa ɛlɛ gbana ɔnɔ, pɔ Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ, onupɛ Owusoso Akaa Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ ɔlɛ ɔnɔ mɛ. Kɛlɛ onupɛ Kristo yɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ ɔma anɔ teli yɛahɔɛ, etinu Kristo yɛayɛ.

english_verse: But Christ lives in you. So you are alive because God has accepted you, even though your bodies must die because of your sins.
translation_verse: Onupɛ Kristo alɛ ɔnɔ mɛ, pɛ wɔnɔ ishupi lɛ tsí nɛ kibui kpa shu tsyɛ elo, wɔnɔ ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ xɔnɔ ɔlɛ nɛ ɛkpã, ɔmɔsɛ balɔkɔ wɔnɔ asɛ banɔ gɛ alɛ piapii ana so.

english_verse: Yet God raised Jesus to life! God's Spirit now lives in you, and he will raise you to life by his Spirit.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ onupɛ anunɔ gɛ yɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa ɔlɛ ofeke Yesu edenɔ bekpuɔ mɛ ɔlɛ wɔnɔ mɛɛ, kɛlɛ yɛ gɛ efeke Kristo edenɔ bekpuɔ mɛɛ, yɛ ke abakɔ wɔnɔ ishupi tsitsí ɛlɛ ɛkpã ɔgana yɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ, gɛ ɔlɛ wɔnɔ mɛɛ.

english_verse: If you do, you will die. But you will live, if by the help of God's Spirit you say “No” to your desires.
translation_verse: Onupɛ nɔzã nɛ̀ ɛkpã nɛ kanɔ alɛ ɛkpã kpa ulũɔ, nobotsí, pɔ onupɛ nɔlɔkɔ Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ oyí kanɔ mɛ ebui kpa gɛ nɔdɛmɛ nɔ nɔ̀ɔ, nɔbɔzã nɛ ɛkpã,

english_verse: Only those people who are led by God's Spirit are his children.
translation_verse: ɔmɔsɛ bagɛ Owusoso Akaa Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ ɔgbanaa, Owusoso Akaa yɛeyi alɛ benu,

english_verse: God's Spirit doesn't make us slaves who are afraid of him. Instead, we become his children and call him our Father.
translation_verse: ɔmɔsɛ anɛ ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ gɛ lɔkɔ ɔsɛ tɛ anɔ dzidzi esi ifuɔ ɔlɛ lɛ wɔnɔ mɛ, pɔ Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ gɛ lɔzã beyi mɛɛ, nɔyɛkɛ. Kliso bɔkpɛ ozĩ ɔsɛ, “Ao, lɔɛitití.”

english_verse: God's Spirit makes us sure that we are his children.
translation_verse: Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ ɔbhɛtɛ tenikpo nɛ̀ bɔlɔ ɔyɛ, pɛ lɔdalɛ bɔlɔ ishu ɔsɛ Owusoso Akaa yɛeyi alɛ bunu.

english_verse: His Spirit lets us know that together with Christ we will be given what God has promised. We will also share in the glory of Christ, because we have suffered with him. A Wonderful Future for God's People
translation_verse: Kliso onupɛ yɛeyi alɛ bunuɔ, kɛlɛ budzi kaŋãsiɛelete-Owusoso Akaa kaŋãsiɛelete ɔplɛnɔ Kristo. Kɛmɔ̀ gɛ bɔmɔ̀ ɔtɔ ɔplɛnɔ Kristoɔ, kidzo ɛlɛ nabha babakpɛ bɔlɔ tsyɛ nɛ kitití ɔmɔ asɛ yɛ ana. Tɛdadalɛ Gɛbaba Kidegbaa

english_verse: I am sure what we are suffering now cannot compare with the glory that will be shown to us.
translation_verse: Nɛ mɛɔdɔ̃ mɔmɔ lũɔ, kibe ɛlɛ ɔtɔ tinu kɛdɔ̃ gɛ babalà alɔkɔ ashɔnɔ ishu dadalɛ gɛ bebebu akasɛ nɛ ɔlɔ mɛɛ.

english_verse: In fact, all creation is eagerly waiting for God to show who his children are.
translation_verse: Kanɔ mɛ bɔdɔ̃ bhɛbhɛtɛ pete epo nɛ̀ awuhũ dzadzã ɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa yɛeyi alɛ tibubu kasɛ.

english_verse: Meanwhile, creation is confused, but not because it wants to be confused. God made it this way in the hope
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ bebu Owusoso Akaa kanɔ mɛɔdɔ̃ gɛ ebuɔ nɛ pɔ asɛ, bɔma kɛnanà mɛ, gɛ anɛ yɛtɛtɔtɔɖɔ kinu, Owusoso Akaa yɛapɛ asɛ kɛzã ana, nɛ awɔ̃ lulu asɛ kɛbàba ɛlɛ mɛ,

english_verse: that creation would be set free from decay and would share in the glorious freedom of his children.
translation_verse: sɛ kiwui tiliɛ kanɔ mɛ bɔdɔ̃ pete baboele ishu odenɔ nɛ banɔ esi, kɛlɛ beti betsí atsɔmɔ, kɛlɛ babà mana alɛ nɛ ishu ahɔɛ mɛ zazã dadalɛ gbangbã mɛ, gɛ babàkɔ Owusoso Akaa yɛeyi alɛ.

english_verse: We know that all creation is still groaning and is in pain, like a woman about to give birth.
translation_verse: Nɛ kibe ɛlɛ mɛɛ, bunyi sɛ kanɔ mɛ bɔdɔ̃ petee nimi asɛ kɛtã gɛ ɛya tã ayamunɔ ana ɔlɔkɔ mana oboewui nabha.

english_verse: The Spirit makes us sure about what we will be in the future. But now we groan silently, while we wait for God to show that we are his children. This means that our bodies will also be set free.
translation_verse: Anɛ kanɔ mɛ bɔdɔ̃ ete, pɔ bɔlɔ bagɛ buputɔ nimi nɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa mɛ ɛdɔ̃ kɔkɔ shu tsyɛ, bunimi nɛ ɔlɔ ishu mɛ, nɛ kibeɛ gɛ bupo bɔlɔ tɛyɛyɛkɛ ɔmɔ asɛ beyi anà, tɛ bɔmɔ ɔlɔ ishu ahɔɛmɛ zazã.

english_verse: And this hope is what saves us. But if we already have what we hope for, there is no need to keep on hoping.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ nɛ awɔ̃ lulu alɛ mɛɛ, bayɛkɛ ɔlɔ elu nɛ̀ ɛkpã. Pɔ awɔ̃ lulu nɔnɔ́ gɛ alɛ tɛmɔmɔ mɛɛ, letinu awɔ̃ lulu kura. Afana gɛ ezebelu awɔ̃ nɛ kɛdɔ̃ gɛ kɛlɛ yɛahɔɛ kukukuɔ nabhaa dza?

english_verse: However, we hope for something we have not yet seen, and we patiently wait for it.
translation_verse: Pɔ onupɛ bulu awɔ̃ nɛ kɛdɔ̃ gɛ kaku ɔlɔ ahɔɛ mɛɛ, kɛlɛ bulú kitú esi ipo ɛlɛ.

english_verse: In certain ways we are weak, but the Spirit is here to help us. For example, when we don't know what to pray for, the Spirit prays for us in ways that cannot be put into words.
translation_verse: Kidzo ɛlɛ nabha ke Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ ɔplɛnɔ ɔlɔ nɛ ɔlɔ tibobotsò mɛ. Butinyi kɛgɛ bɔbaɖɔ nɛ Owusoso Akaa tikukulu mɛ, pɔ Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ ɔyɛkɛ ebuiɛ nɛ ɔlɔ lu nɛ tininimi gele gɛ olugbũ ɔtɛ bɔlà ɔɖɔɔ mɛ.

english_verse: All of our thoughts are known to God. He can understand what is in the mind of the Spirit, as the Spirit prays for God's people.
translation_verse: Owusoso Akaa gɛ amɔ pɛ enyi bɔlɔ etúɔ mɛɛ, enyi Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ ekpeɖekpeɖe mɛ, ɔmɔsɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ ɔyɛkɛ ebui nɛ banɔ dadalɛ nulũ, nɛ Owusoso Akaa tɛpɛpɛ ulũ. Bɔŋã Kabha Ɔdɛ Kɛɖɔ Gɛ Bɔbaɖɔ

english_verse: We know that God is always at work for the good of everyone who loves him. They are the ones God has chosen for his purpose,
translation_verse: Bunyi nɛ kɛka nyia ka mɛɛ ɔsɛ, Owusoso Akaa abhɛtɛ ɛlɛ kidzi kɛgbangbã ɔkɔ bagɛ adɛmɛ nɛ yɛ, bagɛ alɛ yɛekulu nɛ yɛtɛtɔtɔɖɔ ɔluɔ.

english_verse: and he has always known who his chosen ones would be. He had decided to let them become like his own Son, so his Son would be the first of many children.
translation_verse: Bagɛ Owusoso Akaa enyi elu kukukuɔ, balɛ ke yɛebu asɛ bazã nɛ ɛkpã asɛ Yɛeyiɛ, sɛ tɛ ebedzi Yɛeyi gɛ eputɔ nɛ beyianyɛ gelee ishi.

english_verse: God then accepted the people he had already decided to choose, and he has shared his glory with them. God's Love
translation_verse: Bagɛ balɛishu yɛabhɛtɛ tɛtɔtɔɖɔ ɛlɛ elu akɔɔ, balɛ tsyɛ yɛekulu, bagɛ ekuluɔ balɛ tsyɛ yɛebhui utsyɔa akɔ, kɛlɛ bagɛ balɛ yɛebhui utsyɔa akɔɔ, balɛ tsyɛ shu yɛadalɛ.

english_verse: What can we say about all this? If God is on our side, can anyone be against us?
translation_verse: Kiɖuwoe bɔbaɖɔ otsiɛ tɛ bɔlɔkɔ bu kibui ɛlɛ nulũ nɔ? Onupɛ Owusoso Akaa enu ɔlɔ ayɛɛ, afana gɛ abalà alɔɛtití nɛ ɔlɔ shu?

english_verse: God did not keep back his own Son, but he gave him for us. If God did this, won't he freely give us everything else?
translation_verse: Owusoso Akaa alɛ gɛ agɛkɔ ɛtakpu asɛ atɛ ashɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ Yɛeyiɛ shu, pɔ ashɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ yɛishu akɔ ɔlɔ petee nɔ, kiɖeso pɛ atɛ alɔkɔ bɔdɔ̃ pete tsyɛ aplɛnɔ akɔ ɔlɔ nɛ̀ sameɛ dza?

english_verse: If God says his chosen ones are acceptable to him, can anyone bring charges against them?
translation_verse: Afana gɛ abalà alɔkɔ kibui nɛ Owusoso Akaa anunɔ gɛ ebu eluɔ shu? Owusoso Akaa yɛebu utsyɔa akɔ banɔ.

english_verse: Or can anyone condemn them? No indeed! Christ died and was raised to life, and now he is at God's right side, speaking to him for us.
translation_verse: Afana gɛ yɛebu kɛpɔ? Kristo Yesu, anunɔ gɛ yɛetsíɛ, kɛgɛ plɛnɔ ɔdɛɛ yɛenu anunɔ gɛ befeke edenɔ bekpu mɛ pɛ alɛ nɛ ɛkpã, adɛzã Owusoso Akaa nɛ̀ ɛŋã mɛ, kɛlɛ akɔ yɛtakpu tsyɛ nɛ bɔlɔ lũ.

english_verse: Can anything separate us from the love of Christ? Can trouble, suffering, and hard times, or hunger and nakedness, or danger and death?
translation_verse: Afana gɛ ebebhui tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ gɛlɛ bɔlɔ nɛ̀ Kristo ishi? Kɛba dzi bɔtɔ alo banɔ buluhũ kpɛkpɛ alo banɔ edetɔtɔ nɔ alo bɔbhɛ alo kɛdɔ̃ makpɛ makpɛ alo kifu ibui alo bɛtɛ̀ɛ dza?

english_verse: It is exactly as the Scriptures say, “For you we face death all day long. We are like sheep on their way to be butchered.”
translation_verse: Asɛ kɛfɔ̀nɔ gɛ bafɔnɔ kɛlɛ eluɔ, “Nɛ wɔɛtakpuɔ, bɔmɔ butsí ɛlɛ nɛ kiwui mɛ pete, balɔkɔ bɔlɔ asɛ ibhoshi gɛ bedi yí.”

english_verse: In everything we have won more than a victory because of Christ who loves us.
translation_verse: Ao, nɛ alɛ pete mɛɛ, bɔŋã abha ɔdɛ tɛɖɔɖɔ ɔgana Kristo gɛ adɛmɛ nɛ bɔlɔɔ nabha.

english_verse: I am sure that nothing can separate us from God's love—not life or death, not angels or spirits, not the present or the future,
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ ɛyɛkɛŋã ɔsɛ, onu butsí tsyɛo alo ɛkpã, akaa axɔ̃ɛdɛ alo ɛfɔfuĩa zizĩ, kibe ɛlɛ alo kɛgɛ babaa, alo ihumi,

english_verse: and not powers above or powers below. Nothing in all creation can separate us from God's love for us in Christ Jesus our Lord!
translation_verse: ɔɖaɖama ɔkɔ alo odzudzukɔ alo kɛka nyia ka idi nɛ kanɔ alɛ pete mɛɛ, tɛ balà ebhui ulɔ ɔtɛ nɛ Owusoso Akaa tɛdɛdɛ mɛ nɔ gɛ akɔ bɔmɔ odenɔ bɔlɔ Apalete Kristo Yesu mɛ shu.

english_verse: I am a follower of Christ, and the Holy Spirit is a witness to my conscience. So I tell the truth and I am not lying when I say
translation_verse: Ɛɖɔ kɛgɛ lɛ kɛlɛ ɔzãkɔ nɛ Kristo mɛgɛ ɛtɛsɛ asɛnɛ, mɛitú mɛ ebuiɛ atɔɖɔ ahɔɛ nɛ kɛlɛ abha nɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã mɛ.

english_verse: my heart is broken and I am in great sorrow.
translation_verse: Ɛmɔ ɔwɔ̃nyɔnɔ toli idi kɛlɛ bɔtɔ tii bele mɛitúɔ mɛ.

english_verse: I would gladly be placed under God's curse and be separated from Christ for the good of my own people.
translation_verse: Pɔ kɛta dzi mɛ tɛpɛpɛ ɔsɛ tɛ baɖɔ kibui akpɛ mɛ, kɛlɛ tɛ bebhui mɛ atɛɛ nɛ Kristo shu nɛ mɛelɛ alɛ, nɛ̀ mɛepi anɛ,

english_verse: They are the descendants of Israel, and they are also God's chosen people. God showed them his glory. He made agreements with them and gave them his Law. The temple is theirs and so are the promises that God made to them.
translation_verse: gɛenu Israelanɛ, nɛ ɛtakpu. Balɛ Owusoso Akaa etsyina ayɛkɛ asɛ beyi pɛ ebu yɛishu dadalɛ akasɛ alɛ, anya olugbũ nɛ̀ balɛ pɛ bayɛkɛ yɛɔshaa, akasɛ alɛ kɛtakpu esi ɔkpɛkɔ, nɛ̀ yɛebui tɔtɔɖɔ olu ɔkɔ alɛ.

english_verse: They have those famous ancestors, who were also the ancestors of the Christ. I pray that God, who rules over all, will be praised forever! Amen.
translation_verse: Bashuĩ gbagbla eyimɔmɔ alɛ benu, kɛlɛ balɛ mɛ Kristo edekoeyì asɛ anɔdzyrame ana, bɔbakpɛ Owusoso Akaa gɛ aŋã kɛka nyia ka abha nɛinye nɛitití ɔvɛnɔ tihɔ tihɔ! Amen.

english_verse: It cannot be said that God broke his promise. After all, not all of the people of Israel are the true people of God.
translation_verse: Anɛ gɛ Owusoso Akaa yɛibuiɛ gɛ wɔa ɛxɔ̃ɛ. Ɔmɔsɛ anɛ anɔ ka nyia ka gɛ ede Israelanɛ ishopoɔ mɛ balɛ enu Israelanɛ.

english_verse: In fact, when God made the promise to Abraham, he meant only Abraham's descendants by his son Isaac. God was talking only about Isaac when he promised
translation_verse: Nɛibui mɛ dadalɛ ulũɔ, anɛ beshui ka nyia ka balɛ enu Owusoso Akaa yɛeyi alɛ, pɔ beyiɛ gɛ Owusoso Akaa atɔɖɔ elu asɛ bedzi Abraham yɛeyi alɛ, balɛ enu yɛtsyɛ yɛeyimɔmɔ alɛ.

english_verse: Sarah, “At this time next year I will return, and you will already have a son.”
translation_verse: Kibuiɛ gɛ atɔɖɔ eluɔ kɛli inu ɔsɛ, “Nɛibe tɔtɔɖɔ lu nabhaa, ebatsyina ba kɛlɛ eyianyɛinuvɔɛ abazã Sara ahɔɛ mɛ.”

english_verse: Don't forget what happened to the twin sons of Isaac and Rebekah.
translation_verse: Kɛgɛ zɛplɛnɔ tsyɛ nu ɔsɛ, akaa tenikpo yɛalɛ akɔ Rebeka yɛeyi alɛ, kɛlɛ akaa tenikpo alɛɛ, yɛenu bɔlɔ akaa Isak.

english_verse: That's why the Scriptures say that the Lord liked Jacob more than Esau.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔ asɛ kɛfɔ̀nɔ gɛ bafɔnɔ elu asɛ, “Yakob yɛenu anunɔ gɛ ɛdɛmɛ nɔ, Esau pɔ ɛgɛ dɛmɛ yɛibui.”

english_verse: The Lord told Moses that he has pity and mercy on anyone he wants to.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ aɖɔ etseɖe Mose asɛ, “Ɛbaɖi ashagabui ɔkɔ anunɔ gɛ ɛsɛ iɖi ashagabui ɔkɔ, kɛlɛ ɛbabhɛtɛ ɛyamɛ gbangbã ɔkɔ anunɔ gɛ ɛpɛ sɛ tɛ ɛbhɛtɛ ɛyama gbangbã ɔkɔ.”

english_verse: Everything then depends on God's mercy and not on what people want or do.
translation_verse: Kitinu anɔ teli yɛtɛdɛdɛmɛ ulũ alo yɛemikpɛkpɛ ulũ, pɔ kinu Owusoso Akaa yɛashagabui tiɖiɖi ulũ.

english_verse: In the Scriptures the Lord says to the king of Egypt, “I let you become king, so that I could show you my power and be praised by all people on earth.”
translation_verse: Owusoso Akaa etseɖe Farao asɛ, “Kɛgɛ ɛliso pɛ idzu wɔ bukusi kɛli nu ɔsɛ, tɛ mɛgã wɔabha tɛ mɛibu mɛohumi nɔ kasɛ, kɛlɛ sɛ tɛ mɛinye yi fala nɛ kesiakpa nyɔ̃ɔ abha.”

english_verse: Everything depends on what God decides to do, and he can either have pity on people or make them stubborn. God's Anger and Mercy
translation_verse: Kliso Owusoso Akaa eɖi ashagabui akɔ anunɔ gɛ apɛ asɛ tɛ eɖi ashagabui akɔ, kɛlɛ anunɔ gɛ apɛ asɛ ɛtakpu mɛ gagalĩ zã yɛ, akɔ ɛlɛ mɛ gagalĩ.

english_verse: Someone may ask, “How can God blame us, if he makes us behave in the way he wants us to?”
translation_verse: Wɔnɔmɛ anɔ teli ebebisi mɛasɛ, “Kɛlɛ kiɖeso pɛ Owusoso Akaa ekebu ulɔ ɛpɔ? Afana gɛ abalà lɔ itití nɛ kɛgɛ atɔɖɔ asɛ abhɛtɛ shu?”

english_verse: But, my friend, I ask, “Who do you think you are to question God? Does the clay have the right to ask the potter why he shaped it the way he did?
translation_verse: Pɔ, anɔdzyrame, afana wonu sɛ tɛ obu kibui ulũ ɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa? Pɔ kɛdɔ̃ tili gɛ anɔ teli abhɔ nɛ̀ tɛbhɔɔ balà bisi anunɔ gɛ abhɔ ɛlɛ asɛ, “Kiɖeso pɛ abhɔ mɛ asɛ kɛlɛ gɛ ɛlɛɛ dza?”

english_verse: Doesn't a potter have the right to make a fancy bowl and a plain bowl out of the same lump of clay?”
translation_verse: Bɔdɔ̃ abhɔbhɔɔ pɔ, agɛmɔ idzo asɛ tɛ alɔkɔ tisiɛ gɛ etuɔ tili abhɔ kɛdɔ̃ gbangbã tili gɛ kɛpɔ ɖi alo sɛ tɛ alɔkɔ abhɔ kɛka nyia ka idi ɔkɔ kɛlɛ ishu axɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔaa dza?

english_verse: God wanted to show his anger and reveal his power against everyone who deserved to be destroyed. But instead, he patiently put up with them.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ onupɛ Owusoso Akaa apɛ asɛ tɛ ebu yɛɔmɔpɔɛ nɛ̀ ohumi akasɛɛ, adɔ fɔ̃ɔ akɔ bagɛ alɛ shu yɛakpɛ ɔmɔpɔɛ nɔ, bagɛ abálɛ elu akɔ titsitsíɛ.

english_verse: He did this by showing how glorious he is when he has pity on the people he has chosen to share in his glory.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ kɛbazã onupɛ Owusoso Akaa apɛ asɛ tɛ ebu yɛehoeyiudɔ̃ gɛlɛ yɛishu dadalɛ mɛ akasɛ bagɛ eɖi ashagabui pɛ etiputɔ abalɛ elu akɔ yɛishu dadalɛ,

english_verse: just as the Lord says in the book of Hosea, “Although they are not my people, I will make them my people. I will treat with love those nations that have never been loved.
translation_verse: Asɛ kɛɖɔ gɛ aɖɔ elu ɔgana Ebuiɖɔluanɔ Hosea abha asɛ, “Ɛbaklu balɛ ɔsɛ ‘mɛanɔ benu’ gɛ betinu mɛanɔ flogo, kɛlɛ ɛbakulu adzɛ sɛ ‘adzɛ gɛ ɛdɛmɛ nɔ’ anunɔ gɛ etinu anɔ gɛ ɛdɛmɛ nɔ flogo,”

english_verse: “Once they were told, ‘You are not my people.’ But in that very place they will be called children of the living God.”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ, “Kɛbàba ɛlɛ mɛ ɔsɛ ɛkɛlɛ tututu gɛ baɖɔ etseɖe balɛ asɛ, ‘Gɛnɛ mɛanɔ nonu,’ bebekulu balɛ asɛ, ‘Owusoso Akaa enunuɔ yɛeyi alɛ.’ ”

english_verse: And this is what the prophet Isaiah said about the people of Israel, “The people of Israel are as many as the grains of sand along the beach. But only a few who are left will be saved.
translation_verse: Yesaya azakpɛ ozĩ nɛ Israel itakpu asɛ, “Gɛ Israelanɛ tɛkaklà kpè asɛ ayisɛ nɛ aƒu nɔtɔ̃ tsyɛ elo, banɔ gɛ bebu eluɔ ete ko.

english_verse: The Lord will be quick and sure to do on earth what he has warned he will do.”
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ Apaleteɛ abalɔkɔ yɛebui gɔgɔɛ mɛ tibhui bhui akɔ esiakpaa nɛ̀ tɛhɔahlɔa kɛlɛ kɛka nyia ka ba bele.”

english_verse: Isaiah also said, “If the Lord All-Powerful had not spared some of our descendants, we would have been destroyed like the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah.” Israel and the Good News
translation_verse: Ɔmɔ asɛ kɛɖɔ gɛ Yesaya atɛɖɔ afala nɔ asɛ, “Pɛ otɛ ɔsɛ anɛ Apalete Ohumieleteɛ gɛ yɛakɔ beteli agɔ̃ akɔ ɔlɔ tsyɛ elo, alo buti tsyina edzi asɛ Sodom ana bɔtazã asɛ Gomora ana.” Israelanɛ Mayɛkɛ Mayɛkɛŋã

english_verse: What does all of this mean? It means that the Gentiles were not trying to be acceptable to God, but they found that he would accept them if they had faith.
translation_verse: Kiɖuwoe lu bɔbaɖɔ? Gɛ bagɛ betinu Yudanɛ agɛ tɔnɔ tɛzazã piapii ede tsyɛ elo, bamɔ tɛzazã piapii ɔgana tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛ,

english_verse: It also means that the people of Israel were not acceptable to God. And why not? It was because they were trying to be acceptable by obeying the Law instead of by having faith in God. The people of Israel fell over the stone that makes people stumble,
translation_verse: Kiɖuwoe lu so? Ɔmɔsɛ bagɛ lɔkɔ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã atɔnɔ ɔlɛ abha tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ edeɛ, pɔ beɖi asɛ gana balɛ ahɔɛ axɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa mɛ. Kɛkɔ ɔsɛ bakpatà “kɛklagbu gɛ bakpatàa.”

english_verse: just as God says in the Scriptures, “Look! I am placing in Zion a stone to make people stumble and fall. But those who have faith in that one will never be disappointed.”
translation_verse: Asɛ kibuiɛ gɛ bafɔnɔ elu asɛ, “Dio, ilu kɛklagbu gɛkɔ sɛ anɔ akpata pɛ adɔ oboeɛ nɛ Zion ɔxɔa gɛ lɔkɔ ɔsɛ banɔ bakpata tɛ badɔ oboe, anɔ gɛ elu awɔ̃ nɛ Owusoso Akaa nabhaa, batɛ bakpɛ yɛalɛ awuĩ.”

english_verse: Dear friends, my greatest wish and my prayer to God is for the people of Israel to be saved.
translation_verse: Mɛelɛ alɛ mɔ, mɛitú mɛibui tɛpɛpɛ nɛ mɛ Owusoso Akaa tikukulu nɛ Israelanɛ ɛtakpu kɛlinu sɛ tɛ bebu alɛ.

english_verse: what makes people acceptable to him. So they refuse to trust God, and they try to be acceptable by obeying the Law.
translation_verse: Gɛ betinyi tɛzazã gbangbã gɛide Owusoso Akaa ɔkɔ soɔ, babhɛtɛ balɛ ahɔɛ tɛtɔtɔɖɔ ebui, gɛitinu itakpu esi kpɛkpɛ ɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa tɛzazã gbangbã.

english_verse: But Christ makes the Law no longer necessary for those who become acceptable to God by faith. Anyone Can Be Saved
translation_verse: Kristo yɛtɛbaba ɔkɔ ɔsɛ ohumi obele nɛ ɔsha ahɔɛ, sɛ tɛ anɔ ka nyia ka gɛ ayɛkɛ aŋãa, tɛ amɔ tɛzazã gbangbã.

english_verse: Moses said a person could become acceptable to God by obeying the Law. He did this when he wrote, “If you want to live, you must do all the Law commands.”
translation_verse: Mose ebu tɛzazã gbangbã gɛ lɛ ɔsha nulũ asɛ, “Anunɔ gɛ akpɛ emi pɛ abhɛtɛ nɛ ɔsha nulũɔ abamɔ ɛkpã edenɔ kɛlɛ mɛ.”

english_verse: But people whose faith makes them acceptable to God will never ask, “Who will go up to heaven to bring Christ down?”
translation_verse: Pɔ tɛzazã gbangbã ɔgana tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛɛ, aɖɔ asɛ, “Tɛɔɖɔ nɛ wɔitúɔ mɛ ɔsɛ, ‘Afana gɛ ebewu avɛ ititíɛ?’ ”

english_verse: Neither will they ask, “Who will go down into the world of the dead to raise him to life?”
translation_verse: “pɛnɔsɛ, ‘Afana gɛ ebezhi avɛ ozĩ dzudzuɔ mɛ̀?’ ” .

english_verse: All who are acceptable because of their faith simply say, “The message is as near as your mouth or your heart.” And this is the same message we preach about faith.
translation_verse: Pɔ kiɖuwoe koŋ kibuiɛ ɖɔ? Kɛɖɔ ɔsɛ, “Kibuiɛ lɛ wɔbha, kɛlɛ wɔolugbũɔ mɛ, nɛ̀ wɔɛtúɔ mɛ,” kɛlɛinu tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã ibuiɛ gɛ baɖɔɔ.

english_verse: So you will be saved, if you honestly say, “Jesus is Lord,” and if you believe with all your heart that God raised him from death.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛinu ɔsɛ, tɛɔdalɛ kɛlɛ mɛ nɛ wɔolũgbuɔ ɔsɛ, “Yesu enu Apalete,” kɛlɛ ɔyɛkɛ ɔŋã nɛ wɔɛtúɔ mɛ ɔsɛ, Owusoso Akaa efeke edenɔ bekpuɔ mɛɛ, bebè bu wɔ.

english_verse: God will accept you and save you, if you truly believe this and tell it to others.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ wɔɛtúɔ wɔlɔkɔ ɔyɛkɛ ɔŋã nɔ, pɛ bamɔ ɔsɛ onu anɔ gbangbã, kɛlɛ olugbũ wɔlɔkɔ ɔdalɛ ebui mɛ nɔ pɛ bayɛkɛ wɔ elu nɛ ɛkpã.

english_verse: The Scriptures say no one who has faith will be disappointed,
translation_verse: Owusoso Akaa nɛibuiɛ ɖɔ ɔsɛ, “Anɔ ka nyia ka gɛ ayɛkɛ yɛabha aŋãa, kiwuĩ tɛbabà yɛabha oɖi ɖa.”

english_verse: no matter if that person is a Jew or a Gentile. There is only one Lord, and he is generous to everyone who asks for his help.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ Yudanɛ nɛ̀ bagɛ betinu Yudanɛ pete nɔ, tenikpo balɛ. Apalete tenikpo alɛ yɛenu Apaleteɛ akɔ balɛ petee, kɛlɛ yɛeyula tutu esi akɔ anɔ nyia anɔ gɛ akpɛ ɛtakpu esi akɔɛ,

english_verse: All who call out to the Lord will be saved.
translation_verse: ɔmɔsɛ, “Anɔ nyia anɔ gɛ ekulu Apaleteɛ nɛinyeɛ, aba mɛ ɛkpã.”

english_verse: How can people have faith in the Lord and ask him to save them, if they have never heard about him? And how can they hear, unless someone tells them?
translation_verse: So, kɛlɛ́ anɔ abalà ekulu kinye nɔnɔ́ abha gɛ agɛyɛkɛ aŋãa dza? Kɛlɛ́ babàlà yɛkɛ anunɔ gɛ bekelũ yɛɔdɔ̃ eɖiɛ abha aŋãa dza? Kɛlɛ kɛlɛ́ babalà elũ Owusoso Akaa ibuiɛ onupɛ anɔ nanà atɛ aɖɔ kɛlɛ etseɖe alɛɛ dza?

english_verse: And how can anyone tell them without being sent by the Lord? The Scriptures say it is a beautiful sight to see even the feet of someone coming to preach the good news.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ́ babalà ibuiɛ aɖɔ onupɛ bagɛ kpɛ ananà nɔɔ dza? Asɛ kɛfɔ̀nɔ gɛ bafɔnɔ elu asɛ, “Bagɛ bamana Owusoso Akaa ibui gbangbãa, bagbã ɔtsɛɖɛ gbangbã!”

english_verse: Yet not everyone has believed the message. For example, the prophet Isaiah asked, “Lord, has anyone believed what we said?”
translation_verse: Pɔ anɛ Israelanɛ petee balɛ ayɛkɛ ibui gbangbã nabha aŋã, ɔmɔsɛ Yesaya afɔnɔ elu asɛ, “Apalete, afana gɛ ayɛkɛ kibuiɛ gɛ bɔɖɔɔ tseɖe alɛ nabha aŋã?”

english_verse: No one can have faith without hearing the message about Christ.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ, odenɔ kibuiɛ tilulũ mɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã koeyi, kɛlɛ kibui ɛlɛ bulũ odenɔ Kristo yɛibuiɛ mɛ.

english_verse: But am I saying that the people of Israel did not hear? No, I am not! The Scriptures say, “The message was told everywhere on earth. It was announced all over the world.”
translation_verse: Pɔ ibisi sɛ. Begilũ dza? Baa nɔ belũ alɛ, “Balɛ kibuiɛ ozĩ kpɛkpɛ oyi gɛ lɔtsyɔa esiakpa nabha petee, balɛ ebuiɛ avɛ kanɔ nɔ̀ ɔtɔ̃ kɔ.”

english_verse: Did the people of Israel understand or not? Moses answered this question when he told that the Lord had said, “I will make Israel jealous of people who are a nation of nobodies. I will make them angry with people who don't understand a thing.”
translation_verse: Iwlui ka bisi sɛ. Kliso Israel egilũ ebuiɛ alɛ mɛɛ dza? Putɔ nɔɔ, Mose aɖɔ asɛ, “Ɛbakɔ nɔdzã kiwuhũ ɔkɔ bagɛ etinu kɛmadiɛ, Ɛbakɔ nɔkpɛ̀ ɔmɔpɔɛ nɛ̀ kɛmadiɛ gɛitilũ ebui esiɛ.”

english_verse: Isaiah was fearless enough to tell that the Lord had said, “I was found by people who were not looking for me. I appeared to the ones who were not asking about me.”
translation_verse: Yesaya akpɛ ité aɖɔ asɛ, “Bagɛ bagɛzapɛ mɛɛ, balɛ abamɔ mɛ, kɛlɛ bagɛ begibisì mɛtɔɔ, balɛ mɛibu mɛishu kasɛ.”

english_verse: And Isaiah said about the people of Israel, “All day long the Lord has reached out to people who are stubborn and refuse to obey.”
translation_verse: Israelanɛ pɔɔ, aɖɔ nɛ balɛishu asɛ, “Nɛ kiwuìɛ mɛ pete nɔ, ɛdalɛ mɛahɔɛ mɛ ilu ɔkɔ bagɛ beti elũ atɔ̃ɔ, nɛ̀ bagɛ balɔ kitití nɛ mɛishuɔ.”

english_verse: Am I saying that God has turned his back on his people? Certainly not! I am one of the people of Israel, and I myself am a descendant of Abraham from the tribe of Benjamin.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ ibisi sɛ, Owusoso Akaa ashɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ yɛmadiɛ shu dza? Kɛma kɛlɛ mɛ ana! Israelaeyi mɛinu, kɛlɛ Abraham yɛeshui mɛinu gɛ ide Benyamin yɛigbogboɔ mɛ.

english_verse: God did not turn his back on his chosen people. Don't you remember reading in the Scriptures how Elijah complained to God about the people of Israel?
translation_verse: Owusoso Akaa agɛ shɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ yɛmadi Israel shu, banunɔ gɛenyi akpɛ otugbà kukukuɔ. Pɛnɔsɛ notinyi kɛgɛ yɛibuiɛ ɖɔ nɛ Eliya shu-nɛ kibisi gɛ ebisi denɔ Owusoso Akaa ɔkɔ nɛ̀ Israel ɛtakpuɔ shu dza? Aɖɔ asɛ,

english_verse: He said, “Lord, they killed your prophets and destroyed your altars. I am the only one left, and now they want to kill me.”
translation_verse: “Apalete, beyí wɔebuiɖɔluanɛ kɛlɛ bàyɛɛ bɔdɔ̃ pɔpɔɛ osubha afalanɔ. Mɛ tenikpo ko mɛgɔ̃, kɛlɛ̀ baapɛ asɛ tɛ̀ beyì mɛ.”

english_verse: This means that only a chosen few of the people of Israel found what all of them were searching for. And the rest of them were stubborn,
translation_verse: Kiɖuwoe otsiɛ? Kɛgɛ kɛmadi Israel azapɛ nɛ awuhũ dzãdzaa, bagɛ mɔ̃ kɛlɛ, banɔ teli gɛ Owusoso Akaa ebu elúɔ xɔnɔ bamɔ ɛlɛ. Akɔ banɔ gɔgɔ̃ɛ nɔ xɔnɔ bagagalĩ batɔ̃,

english_verse: It is the same way now. God treated the people of Israel with undeserved grace, and so a few of them are still his followers.
translation_verse: Kliso nɛ kibe ɛlɛ tsyɛɛ, banɔ teli bagɔ̃ gɛ bebu balɛ elú ɔgana balɛ tibhui bhuibu mɛ.

english_verse: This happened because of God's undeserved kindness and not because of anything they have done. It could not have happened except for God's gift of undeserved grace.
translation_verse: Kliso onupɛ ɔgana tibhui bhuibu mɛɛ, kɛlɛ́ɛ, kɛtɛ kanu ɔsɛ kide balɛ axɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa mɛ̀. Onupɛ balɛ axɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa mɛ kide pɔɔ, kɛlɛ kɛtɛ kanu tibhui bhuibu.

english_verse: just as the Scriptures say, “God made them so stupid that their eyes are blind, and their ears are still deaf.”
translation_verse: asɛ kɛfɔ̀nɔ gɛ bafɔnɔ kibuiɛ elu asɛ, “Owusoso Akaa akɔ bepu awuhũ nɛ ɔfɔfuĩa mɛ, sɛ tɛ batɛlà amɔ̃ bɔdɔ̃ batɔ̃ alɛ balɛ ahɔɛ pɔ sɛ tɛ batɛ elũ kibui, ɔmana obo kiwui lɛ nabha.”

english_verse: Then David said, “Turn their meals into bait for a trap, so that they will stumble and be given what they deserve.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ David aɖɔ asɛ, “Balɛ bɔdɔ̃ ŋaŋã ɔkpɔlɔ̃ obotsyina odzì bɔnyɔ̃ nɛ̀ kɛgbã, tɛ kidzì kɛdɔ̃ kpakpata nɛ̀ batɔ̃ pɔpɔ̃ ɔkɔ balɛ.

english_verse: Blindfold their eyes! Don't let them see. Bend their backs beneath a burden that will never be lifted.” Gentiles Will Be Saved
translation_verse: Otumũ ɔbɔdã balɛ awuhũ nabha sɛ tɛ̀ batɛ̀là amɔ tɛ balɛ budeshiɛ dzyini ku tihɔɔ. Busi Gɛ Bebhui Atɔɖɔ Busi Abha

english_verse: Do I mean that the people of Israel fell, never to get up again? Certainly not! Their failure made it possible for the Gentiles to be saved, and this will make the people of Israel jealous.
translation_verse: Ezebisì asɛ, Israel akpata bɔdɔ̃ adɔ oboè pɛ azalà alɔ odenɔ nɛ ɛkɛlɛ dza? Ao, kɛma kɛlɛ mɛ ana! Nɛ Yudanɛ ebui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ soɔ, tibubu tsyina vɛ bagɛ betinu Yudanɛ ɔkɔ sɛ tɛ kidzi awuhũdzadzã ibui ɔkɔ Israelanɛ.

english_verse: But if the rest of the world's people were helped so much by their sin and loss, they will be helped even more by their full return.
translation_verse: Onupɛ Israelanɛ ebui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ etsyina edzì tiyuyulà gelee ɔkɔ kanɔ nɔ mɛ, kɛlɛ balɛ kide vɛvɛ nɛ Owusoso Akaa shu tsyina dzì tiyuyulà ɔkɔ bagɛ betinu Yudanɛɛ, kɛlɛ tiyuyulà ɖuwoe kɛbadzì onupɛ Israelanɛ pete etsyina edzì Owusoso Akaa ayɛɛ dza?

english_verse: I am now speaking to you Gentiles, and as long as I am an apostle to you, I will take pride in my work.
translation_verse: Ɛɖɔ kaɖɔ ɔkɔ wɔnɔ bagɛ notinu Yudanɛ. Mɛ asɛ anɔkpɛkpɛ ɔkɔ wɔnɔ bagɛ notinu Yudanɛ ana, ɛwɔa mɛaxɔ̃ɛ

english_verse: I hope in this way to make some of my own people jealous enough to be saved.
translation_verse: nɛ awɔ̃lulu sɛ ɛbalà nyɛmɛ mɛ kɛmadiɛ ginu Yudanɛ sɛ tɛ mɛlɔkɔ awuhũ dzadzã tɛ mɛibu balɛ anɔ teli.

english_verse: When Israel rejected God, the rest of the people in the world were able to turn to him. So when God makes friends with Israel, it will be like bringing the dead back to life.
translation_verse: Onupɛ tɛshɛshɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ balɛishu mɛ pɛ babhɛtɛ tenikpo nɛ̀ kanɔ nɔ̀, kɛlɛ bedzini balɛ ayɛkɛ edenɔ butsí mɛ amana ɛkpã mɛ.

english_verse: If part of a batch of dough is made holy by being offered to God, then all of the dough is holy. If the roots of a tree are holy, the rest of the tree is holy too.
translation_verse: Onupɛ nikpèe nɔ gɛ bebhoso edzyini abakɔ Akaa pɛ yɛishu dalɛɛ, kɛlɛɛ nikpèe nɔ̀ gɛ bebuɔ pete tsyɛ shu dalɛ. Kidzo ɛlɛ nabha onupɛ busiɛ ɔlɛ iguɔ shu dalɛɛ, kɛlɛ bɔshɛ nɔ shu tsyɛ dalɛ alɛ.

english_verse: You Gentiles are like branches of a wild olive tree made to be part of a cultivated olive tree. You have taken the place of some branches that were cut away from it. And because of this, you enjoy the blessings that come from being part of that cultivated tree.
translation_verse: Onupɛ bebhui bɔshɛ nɔ tuli pɛ balɔkɔ wɔnɔ bagɛ notinu Yudanɛ, pɛ nɔlɛ asɛ kifutù mɛ besi ana, pɛ bebhui wɔnɔ bɔshɛ nɔ atɔɖɔ besidzudzu nulũɔ, kɛlɛ wɔnɔ tsyɛ nɔmɔ̃ bɔŋaŋã gɛide besidzudzuɔ balɛ iguɔ mɛ̀.

english_verse: But don't think you are better than the branches that were cut away. Just remember you are not supporting the roots of this tree. Its roots are supporting you.
translation_verse: Nɔtɛ ofeke ishu ɔsɛ nɔpɛ ɔdɛ bɔshɛ nɔ abha gɛ notsiɖiɛ. Nɔbhɛtɛ anaa, nɔbaka ɔsɛ anɛ wɔnɔ nyana igu ovunɔ, pɔ iguɔ ɛlɛ nyana wɔnɔ ovunɔ.

english_verse: Maybe you think those branches were cut away, so you could be put in their place.
translation_verse: Ogilu awɔ̃ɔ, ɔbɔɖɔ ɔsɛ, “Bebhui busiushɛ nɔ atsɛ sɛ tɛ balɔkɔ mɛ atɔɖɔ bɔlɛulũ.”

english_verse: That's true enough. But they were cut away because they did not have faith, and you are where you are because you do have faith. So don't be proud, but be afraid.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ kɛlɛ́ mɛ. Pɔ bebhui bɔshɛ nɔ atsɛ ɔmɔsɛ bagɛ yɛkɛ aŋã, wɔ xɔnɔ wɔ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã so pɛ bayɛkɛ wɔedzu. Tɛofeke ishu, kɔ kifu pɔ zã wɔ mɛ.

english_verse: If God cut away those natural branches, couldn't he do the same to you?
translation_verse: Onupɛ Owusoso Akaa egiɖi Yudanɛ gɛ balɛ asɛ besidzudzu bɔshɛ ana ashagabuiɛ, etibeɖi wɔnɔ tsyɛ ashagabui.

english_verse: Now you see both how kind and how hard God can be. He was hard on those who fell, but he was kind to you. And he will keep on being kind to you, if you keep on trusting in his kindness. Otherwise, you will be cut away too.
translation_verse: Kliso nɔbaka Owusoso Akaa yɛyamɛ gbangbã bhɛbhɛtɛ nɛ̀ yɛahɔɛ gagalĩ nɛ wɔnɔ abha shu. Agagalĩ ahɔɛ nɛ bagɛ betsodzi nɛ yɛedeɛ shu, kɛlɛ wɔnɔ xɔnɔ aabhɛtɛ ɛyamɛ gbangbã akɔ wɔnɔ onupɛ nogitsodzi nɛ yɛedeɛ. Onu gɛna naa, abashɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ wɔnɔ tsyɛ shu atɛ elu.

english_verse: If those other branches will start having faith, they will be made a part of that tree again. God has the power to put them back.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ onupɛ Israeleyiɛ etsodzi ayɛkɛ aŋãa, bebè dzini balɛ edzu nɛ balɛ ɔzãkɔ, ɔmɔsɛ ohumi ɔlɛ Owusoso Akaa yɛahɔɛ gɛ abalà edzini balɛ edzu nɛ balɛ ɔzãkɔ.

english_verse: After all, it wasn't natural for branches to be cut from a wild olive tree and to be made part of a cultivated olive tree. So it is much more likely that God will join the natural branches back to the cultivated olive tree. The People of Israel Will Be Brought Back
translation_verse: Wɔnɔ bagɛ notinu Yudanɛ, gɛ nɔlɛ asɛ kifutu mɛ busiushɛ gɛ bebhui edzu atɔɖɔ besidzudzu ushɛ abha ana, gɛ balɛ enu Israel ɛmadiɛ. Kliso kitinu kibui gagalĩ ɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa asɛ tɛ edzini besidzudzu alɛ nɔshɛ bhui bhuiɛ edzu atɔɖɔ besidzudzu alɛ ke abha. Owusoso Akaa Eɖi Anɔ Ka nyia Ka Ashagabui

english_verse: My friends, I don't want you Gentiles to be too proud of yourselves. So I will explain the mystery of what has happened to the people of Israel. Some of them have become stubborn, and they will stay like that until the complete number of you Gentiles has come in.
translation_verse: Ɛpɛ sɛ tɛ mɛkɔ noti kibui nɛ kibui kpukpũ ɛlɛ shu, tɛ notɛ ofeke ishu ɔsɛ nonyi ibui popopo. Kibuiɛ kɛlɛinu ɔsɛ, nɛ Israelanɛ teli atɔ̃ gagalĩ ibeɛ, kinu tɛtɔtɔɖɔ ɔsɛ onupɛ bamɔ̃ bagɛ betinu Yudanɛ tɛkaklàa gɛ aapɛ nɔɔ anaa, babàba Owusoso Akaa ɔkɔ.

english_verse: In this way all of Israel will be saved, as the Scriptures say, “From Zion someone will come to rescue us. Then Jacob's descendants will stop being evil.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ, babà yɛkɛ akɔ Israel pete, ɔmɔ asɛ kɛfɔ̀nɔ gɛ bafɔnɔ kibuiɛ elu asɛ, “Anunɔ gɛebebu banunɔ ebede Zion, ebebu kɛtakpu makpɛ makpɛ ɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa eyinɔ nɛ Yakob yɛɔpã mɛ.

english_verse: This is what the Lord has promised to do when he forgives their sins.”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ mɛolugbũ nyanyã nɛ alɛ onu ɔsɛ, gɛ ɛlɔkɔ balɛ ebui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ tsye alɛ.”

english_verse: The people of Israel are treated as God's enemies, so the good news can come to you Gentiles. But they are still the chosen ones, and God loves them because of their famous ancestors.
translation_verse: Nɛ Kibui Gbangbã nulũɔ, Israelanɛ benu banɔ gɛ bagɛ dɛmɛ nɔ nɛ̀ Owusoso Akaa. Pɔ nɛ balɛ ashuĩ alɛ ɛtakpu soɔ, Owusoso Akaa adɛmɛ balɛ ibui pɛ ebu alɛ,

english_verse: God doesn't take back the gifts he has given or disown the people he has chosen.
translation_verse: ɔmɔsɛ Owusoso Akaa yɛbɔdɔ̃ kɔkɔ anɔ nɛ̀ yɛ banɔ tikukuluɔ titsyina ɖa.

english_verse: At one time you Gentiles rejected God. But now Israel has rejected God, and you have been shown mercy.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔ asɛ nɛ kuku kibeɛ gɛ wɔnɔ bagɛ notinu Yudanɛ nodzi batɔ̃ malũ maluanɔ ɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa, pɔ otsiɛ nɔ eɖi wɔnɔ ashagabui nɛ Yudanɛ atɔ̃ malũmalũɔ so.

english_verse: And because of the mercy shown to you, they will also be shown mercy.
translation_verse: Balɛ tsyɛ bedzi batɔ̃ malũ malũanɔ otsiɛ, sɛ tɛ Owusoso Akaa eɖi alɛ ashagabui nɛ ashagabuiɛ gɛeɖi wɔnɔ nɛ takpu.

english_verse: All people have disobeyed God, and that's why he treats them as prisoners. But he does this, so that he can have mercy on all of them.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ Owusoso Akaa abhɛtɛ banunɔ pete asɛ tɛ bagagalĩ batɔ sɛ tɛ alà eɖi alɛ ashagabui. Kpɛ Owusoso Akaa Nɛinyè Nɛitití

english_verse: Who can measure the wealth and wisdom and knowledge of God? Who can understand his decisions or explain what he does?
translation_verse: Owusoso Akaa yɛehoeyiudɔ̃ mɔmɔ̃ɔ kpè mɔ lo! Kɛlɛ yɛubutiɛ nɛ̀ ebuesi lulũɔ mɛ edzu mɔɔ dza! Kiɖuwoe gɛlɛ anɔ teli ahɔɛ akɔɛ onupɛ aɖɔaɖɔ pɛ ebhui ibui mɛ? Kɛlɛ afana gɛ abalà elũ yɛɔgã mɛ niesi?

english_verse: “Has anyone known the thoughts of the Lord or given him advice?
translation_verse: Afana gɛelũ Apaleteɛ yɛekpeɖekpeɖe mɛ niesi? Pɛnɔsɛ afana gɛenu anunɔ geuhu buluhũ akɔɛ?

english_verse: Has anyone loaned something to the Lord that must be repaid?”
translation_verse: Afana gɛ atɛkɔ Owusoso Akaa kɛdɔ̃ tili eɖi, sɛ tɛ yɛtsyɛ edzini kɛlɛ kɛbhɛ abuia akpɛ kɛliulũ akɔ anunɔ?

english_verse: Everything comes from the Lord. All things were made because of him and will return to him. Praise the Lord forever! Amen.
translation_verse: Owusoso Akaa yɛabhɛtɛ kɛka nyia ka, kɛlɛ yɛahɔɛ mɛ lalɛ, kɛlɛ yɛahɔɛ mɛ lagã. Kɛtakpu esi kpɛkpɛ inu yɛyɛ́ tihɔɔ! Amen.

english_verse: Dear friends, God is good. So I beg you to offer your bodies to him as a living sacrifice, pure and pleasing. That's the most sensible way to serve God.
translation_verse: Mɛelɛ alɛ mɔ, nɛ Owusoso Akaa yɛashagabui nɛidi gɛeɖi ɔlɔ nɔ soɔ, ɛkpalà ɔnɔ ɔsɛ tɛ nɔlɔkɔ ishu ɔbhɛtɛ asɛ bɔdɔ̃ pɔpɔɛ gɛlɛ nɛkpã ana, gɛ ɛlɛishu dalɛ pɛ Owusoso Akaa adɛmɛ nɔɔ, kɛlɛinu wɔnɔ ɔfɔfuĩa mɛ ɛtakpu esi kpɛkpɛ.

english_verse: Don't be like the people of this world, but let God change the way you think. Then you will know how to do everything that is good and pleasing to him.
translation_verse: Nɔtɛ ɔzã nɛkpã asɛ kanɔ alɛ mɛanɛ, pɔ nɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa yɛetsyina wɔnɔ ekpeɖekpeɖe mɛ tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ yɛedzi avɔvɔ. Kɛlɛ̀ bakɔ ɔsɛ nɔpɔ̃ɛ Owusoso Akaa ebui tɔtɔɖɔɔ mɛ oɖi, kɛgɛ ɛpɛ nɛ̀ kɛgɛ yɛadɛmɛ nɔ, pɛ kɛlɛ ná piapii nɔ.

english_verse: I realize God has treated me with undeserved grace, and so I tell each of you not to think you are better than you really are. Use good sense and measure yourself by the amount of faith that God has given you.
translation_verse: Nɛ tibhui bhuibuɔ gɛ bakɔ mɛ nulũɔ, itseɖe wɔnɔ ɔmɛ anɔ ka nyia ka asɛ atɛ alɔkɔ yɛishu asɛ yɛadɛ. Nɛ kɛlɛ inye mɛɛ, nɔbhɛtɛ ekpeɖekpeɖe mɛ nɛ ishu esi zhizhinɔ ɛkpã zazãna shu boŋ, tɛ anɔ nyia anɔ ebhui ibui mɛ nɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã nɛidi gɛ nɔyɛkɛ odenɔ Owusoso Akaa akɔ nɔɔ shu.

english_verse: A body is made up of many parts, and each of them has its own use.
translation_verse: Asɛ kɛlɛ́ɛ gɛ ishupi bɔwɔ̃pɛ lɛ bɔlɔ mɛ anɔ aka nyia aka ahɔɛɛ, pɔ anɛ kɛxɔ̃ɛ idi tinikpo bɔwɔa,

english_verse: That's how it is with us. There are many of us, but we each are part of the body of Christ, as well as part of one another.
translation_verse: kana ɛbe gana Kristo mɛɛ, bɔlɔ banɔ kpekpè alɛ pete bɔbhɛtɛ tenikpo, kɛlɛ bɔlɔ ishupi ɔwɔ̃pɛ nɔ kpɛ olũgbu nɛ bagɔlɔ mɛ.

english_verse: God has also given each of us different gifts to use. If we can prophesy, we should do it according to the amount of faith we have.
translation_verse: Bɔdɔ̃ kɔkɔ ediedi alɛ bɔlɔ ahɔɛ nɛ tibhui bhuibuɔ gɛ bakɔ ɔlɛ nulũ. Anunɔ gɛ yɛdɔ̃ kɔkɔ kɛlɛinu ɔsɛ tɛ aɖɔ ebui eluɔ, aɖɔ alɛ elu nɛ kɛkɔ gɛ bakɔɛ nɛ yɛibui tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã nulũ.

english_verse: If we can serve others, we should serve. If we can teach, we should teach.
translation_verse: Onupɛ kɛxɔ̃ɛ dɛdɛ anunɔ ayɛkɛɛ, nɔkɔ yɛadɛ ɛlɛ, kana ɛbe onupɛ kinu bɔdɔ̃ kakasɛ gɛ anɔ teli tsyɛ ayɛkɛ, nɔkɔ yɛakasɛ bɔdɔ̃ɔ,

english_verse: If we can encourage others, we should encourage them. If we can give, we should be generous. If we are leaders, we should do our best. If we are good to others, we should do it cheerfully. Rules for Christian Living
translation_verse: onupɛ anɔ teli yɛyɛ́ kɛlinu sɛ tɛ akpɛ banɔ emiɛ, nɔkɔ yɛakpɛ banɔ emi, anunɔ gɛ àkɔ bagɛ kɛdɔ̃ nanà ma balɛ ahɔɛɛ, azakɔ balɛ nɛ̀ ɛyamɛ gbangbã, onupɛ banɔ gbagbana ɛxɔ̃ɛ anunɔ ayɛkɛ, agbana banunɔ piapii, kɛlɛ onupɛ ashagabui ɖiɖi anunɔ ayɛkɛ tsyɛɛ, eɖi ashagabuiɛ nɛ same mɔmɔ mɛ. Tɛdɛdɛmɛ Nɛ Banɔ

english_verse: Be sincere in your love for others. Hate everything that is evil and hold tight to everything that is good.
translation_verse: Nɔkɔ wɔnɔ tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ nɛ̀ banɔ nɔ̀ ode ɛya gbangbã mɛ. Nɔtɛ dɛmɛ nɔ nɛ kibui zizĩ, nɔnyana kibui gbangbã bhɛbhɛtɛ mɛ ovunɔ.

english_verse: Love each other as brothers and sisters and honor others more than you do yourself.
translation_verse: Nɔlɔkɔ tɛpɛplɛnɔ ɔkɔ bagɔlɔ nɛ tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ̀ nɛ̀ bagɔlɔ mɛ, ɔmɔ asɛ bélɛɛ nɛ Kristo mɛ ana. Nɔkpɛ ɛtakpu esi ɔkɔ bagɔlɔ ɔdɛ wɔnɔ ahɔɛ ishu.

english_verse: Never give up. Eagerly follow the Holy Spirit and serve the Lord.
translation_verse: Nɔtɛ shrɛrɛ nɛ wɔnɔ axɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa mɛ, pɔ nɔkɔ kifu zã wɔnɔ mɛ nɛ ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ mɛ, ɔkɔ axɔ̃ɛ dɛdɛ ɔkɔ Apaleteɛ.

english_verse: Let your hope make you glad. Be patient in time of trouble and never stop praying.
translation_verse: Awɔ̃ lulu azã wɔnɔ same mɔmɔ mɛ, tɛdɔdɔ fɔ̃ɔ nɛ banɔ luhũ ikpɛ ibe, nɔlɔkɔ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã okulu Akaa nɔ.

english_verse: Take care of God's needy people and welcome strangers into your home.
translation_verse: Nɔplɛnɔ ɔhamɛeyi nɔnɔ́ gɛ kɛdɔ̃ nanà ma yɛahɔɛ. Nɔzã ɛyamɛ gbangbã tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ abha ɔkɔ befu.

english_verse: Ask God to bless everyone who mistreats you. Ask him to bless them and not to curse them.
translation_verse: Noyula tutu esi ɔkɔ bagɛ atɔ̃nɔ wɔnɔ edeɛ, noyula ɔkɔ alɛ, pɔ nɔtɛɖɔ kibui ɔkpɛ alɛ.

english_verse: When others are happy, be happy with them, and when they are sad, be sad.
translation_verse: Nɔmɔ same nɛ̀ bagɛ amɔ same, kɛlɛ nobi yoyo nɛ̀ bagɛ ebieyoɔ.

english_verse: Be friendly with everyone. Don't be proud and feel that you know more than others. Make friends with ordinary people.
translation_verse: Nɔzã nɛ tenikpo bhɛbhɛtɛ mɛ nɛ̀ bagɔlɔ. Nɔtɛ ozofeke ishu, pɔ nɔdɛmɛ tɛ nɔzã tenikpo nɛ̀ bagɛ alɛ wɔnɔ esiɛ. Nɔtɛ ɔlɔkɔ ishu asɛ bebutianɛ nonu.

english_verse: Don't mistreat someone who has mistreated you. But try to earn the respect of others,
translation_verse: Nɔtɛ bhɛtɛ ibui kpa ɔkpɛ ibui kpa lũ ɔkɔ anɔ na nà. Nolu awɔ tɛ nɔbhɛtɛ ibui gɛinu kɛgbangbã nɛ anɔ ka nyia ka amɛ.

english_verse: and do your best to live at peace with everyone.
translation_verse: Onupɛ kɛbalà de wɔnɔ kawɔ̃pɛ mɛɛ, tɛnɔzã nɛ̀ anɔ ka nyia ka nɛ ishu yɔyɔ mɛ.

english_verse: Dear friends, don't try to get even. Let God take revenge. In the Scriptures the Lord says, “I am the one to take revenge and pay them back.”
translation_verse: Mɛɔbhia alɛ mɔ, nɔtɛ obisi otití ɔkɔ ishu, pɔ nobu kidzo ɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa yɛɔmɔpɔɛ nɔ, ɔmɔsɛ bafɔnɔ kibuiɛ elu asɛ, “Mɛxɔ̃ɛ kinu ɔsɛ tɛ mɛibisi otití nɛ wɔnɔ ulũ, tɛ mɛpɔ̃ batɔ̃,” Apaleteɛ yɛaɖɔ ɛlɛ.

english_verse: The Scriptures also say, “If your enemies are hungry, give them something to eat. And if they are thirsty, give them something to drink. This will be the same as piling burning coals on their heads.”
translation_verse: Nɛ kɛlɛ nɛinye mɛɛ, “Onupɛ bɔbhɛ iyi anunɔ gɛ atɛ dɛmɛ nɛ wɔɔ, wɔ kɔɛ ɛŋaŋã yɛaŋã, onupɛ oluwɔ̃ɛ oyi yɛɛ, kɔɛ kɛdɔ̃ tili yɛawɔ̃ɛ. Nɛ kidzo ɛlɛ nabhaa, kɛbazã asɛ gɛ ɔlɔkɔ afɔɛifu ɔtɔɖɔ yɛada mɛ.”

english_verse: Don't let evil defeat you, but defeat evil with good.
translation_verse: Nɔtɛkɔ kibui kpa ŋã wɔnɔ abha, pɔ nɔkɔ kibui gbangbã bhɛbhɛtɛ ŋã kibui kpa nabha boŋ.

english_verse: Obey the rulers who have authority over you. Only God can give authority to anyone, and he puts these rulers in their places of power.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ sɛ anɔ nyia anɔ elũ batɔ̃ akɔ bekusiɛ gɛ aŋã bɔlɔ abhaa, ɔmɔsɛ kabha ŋaŋã na nà ma gɛ anɛ Owusoso Akaa yɛedzu alɛ. Kabha ŋaŋã gɛ alɛɛ, Owusoso Akaa yɛedzu alɛ.

english_verse: People who oppose the authorities are opposing what God has done, and they will be punished.
translation_verse: Kliso onupɛ anɔ gɛ alɔɛtití nɛ agaŋã abha shuɔ, kɛlɛ anɔ alɛ alɔɛtití nɛ Owusoso Akaa ɛbui tɔtɔɖɔ luɔ shu, kɛlɛ banunɔ gɛ abhɛtɛ anaa, bebèkulu ebhu nɛ alɛ.

english_verse: Rulers are a threat to evil people, not to good people. There is no need to be afraid of the authorities. Just do right, and they will praise you for it.
translation_verse: Banunɔ gɛ baŋã abhaa, betiku ebui gbangbã bhɛbhɛtɛelete nɛifu, pɔ ebui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛeleteɛ ko. Ɔpɛ ɔsɛ tɛ ɔzã tɛ anunɔ gɛ aŋã abha etiku wɔɛifu dza? Kɛlɛ bhɛtɛ kibui gɛ ɛpɛɛ tɛ akpɛ wɔɛinye nɛitití.

english_verse: After all, they are God's servants, and it is their duty to help you. If you do something wrong, you ought to be afraid, because these rulers have the right to punish you. They are God's servants who punish criminals to show how angry God is.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ Owusoso Akaa axɔ̃ɛdɛ yɛenu sɛ tɛ abhɛtɛ kibuiɛ gɛ ɛpɛɛ akɔ ɔnɔ. Pɔ ɔbhɛtɛ kibui kpaa, kɛlɛ sɛ tɛ kifu yi wɔ ɔmɔsɛ bɛtɛ̀ alɛ yɛahɔɛ akɔ yɛishu axɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa. Owusoso Akaa axɔ̃ɛdɛ yɛenu, gɛ alɛ yɛinye mɛ sɛ tɛ apɔ batɔ̃ akɔ ebui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛelete.

english_verse: But you should obey the rulers because you know it is the right thing to do, and not just because of God's anger.
translation_verse: Kliso kɛlɛ sɛ tɛ nɔkpɛ ɛtakpu esi ɔkɔ bagaŋã abha, anɛ sɛ nɛ batɔ̃ pɔpɔ̃ ɛtakpu ete so, pɔ kinu ɛkpã gbangbã gɛ lɛshɔnɔ ɔkɔ tɛzazã.

english_verse: You must also pay your taxes. The authorities are God's servants, and it is their duty to take care of these matters.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ́ nɛtakpu tsyɛ so pɛ nɔbuia ɔmɛadzɔ, ɔmɔsɛ bagaŋã abha enu Owusoso Akaa yɛaxɔ̃ɛdɛ, gɛ balɔkɔ balɛ kibe akɔ nɛ esiakpa abha axɔ̃ɛ abha tiɖiɖi mɛ.

english_verse: Let love be your only debt! If you love others, you have done all that the Law demands.
translation_verse: Nɔtɛkɔ ɛbhɛ na nà zã ɔnɔ shu, ɔdɛ tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ nɛ bagɔlɔ ete ko, ɔmɔsɛ anunɔ gɛ adɛmɛ nɔ nɛ yɛɔhagbo nɔɔ, kɛlɛ abhɛtɛ nɛ ɔsha pete abha.

english_verse: In the Law there are many commands, such as, “Be faithful in marriage. Do not murder. Do not steal. Do not want what belongs to others.” But all of these are summed up in the command that says, “Love others as much as you love yourself.”
translation_verse: Ɔsha ɔɖɔ ɔsɛ, “Tɛɔdɛ ɔma mɛ,” “tɛoyi anɔ,” “tɛozuru,” “Tɛɔdzã awuhũ nɛ wɔhagbo ɛdɔ̃ shu,” nɛ̀ ɔsha bhlatɔ pete nɛ ɛtakpu ibuiɛ gɛ kihu alɛ ɔtɔ̃ nu ɔsɛ, “Dɛmɛ nɛ wɔhagbo asɛ wɔshu ana.”

english_verse: No one who loves others will harm them. So love is all that the Law demands. The Day When Christ Returns
translation_verse: Tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ nɛ anɔ tɛmana kibui kpa nɛ ɔhagbo ishi. Kliso ɔsha nabha tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ ibui nu tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ nɛ anɔ.

english_verse: You know what sort of times we live in, and so you should live properly. It is time to wake up. You know that the day when we will be saved is nearer now than when we first put our faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Nɔbhɛtɛ kɛlɛ tɛ nolũ kibeɛ mɛ gɛ bɔkpasɛ otsiɛ nɔ. Ɔmɔsɛ kibeɛ na ɔkɔ ɔnɔ ɔsɛ tɛ nɔnyɛmɛ odenɔ wɔnɔ taɖa dɔdɔ mɛ, ɔmɔsɛ bɔlɔ tibubu ibeɛ itsu plɛnɔ ɔlɔ ɔdɛ kibeɛ gɛ bulũ kibuiɛ pɛ bɔyɛkɛŋãa.

english_verse: Night is almost over, and day will soon appear. We must stop behaving as people do in the dark and be ready to live in the light.
translation_verse: Elishibeɛ fala, kɛlɛ keli aza nɛ ɔlɔ ebeke, kliso nɔkɔ bɔshɛ nɛ ahɔɛ nɛ otumũ mɛ ebui bhɛbhɛtɛ shu, tɛ bɔlɔkɔ keyudɔ̃ dadalɛ kpàta.

english_verse: So behave properly, as people do in the day. Don't go to wild parties or get drunk or be vulgar or indecent. Don't quarrel or be jealous.
translation_verse: Nɔkɔ bɔzã nɛ ɛkpã gbangbã asɛ kiwui dadalɛ mɛ ana, tɛ bɔta bhɛtɛ agbagbà ebui asɛ bɔmɔ same, tɛ bɔzɛwɔ̃ɛ bunũ tizì buku, tɛ bɔɔbhɛtɛ banyɛ nɛ̀ badzɛ ebui pɛ bɔbaatɛ ishu lu esi. Kɛgɛ pɛ ɔsɛ tɛ bɔɔkpɛ ɔmɔpɔɛ tɛ bɔzɛpɛ ebalá alo tɛ bɔzɛdzã awuhũ nɛ bagɔlɔ shu.

english_verse: Let the Lord Jesus Christ be as near to you as the clothes you wear. Then you won't try to satisfy your selfish desires.
translation_verse: Nɛ alɛ nɛinye mɛɛ, nɔkɔ bɔlɔkɔ Apalete Yesu Kristo kpɛ ishu asɛ akpɛkpɛ ana, tɛ bɔta zɛbhɛtɛ ekpeɖekpeɖe mɛ nɛ kɛmɔ gɛ bɔmɔ same nɛ kanɔ alɛ mɛebui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ shu.

english_verse: Welcome all the Lord's followers, even those whose faith is weak. Don't criticize them for having beliefs that are different from yours.
translation_verse: Nɔyɛkɛ anunɔ gɛ yɛtɛyɛyɛkɛŋãa kakpɛ oguɔ tsyɛ ɔkpɛ wɔnɔishi, tɛ nɔtɛ ozokulu ebhu nɛ ebui wawlãkɛ shu.

english_verse: Some think it is all right to eat anything, while those whose faith is weak will eat only vegetables.
translation_verse: Anɔ teli nɛ yɛtɛyɛyɛkɛŋã lũɔ, adɛmɛ aŋã kɛka nyia ka, pɔ anɔ teli gɛ aka kpɛ ogù nɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛɛ, yɛ xɔnɔ tɛgã ete ko yɛaŋã.

english_verse: What right do you have to criticize someone else's servants? Only their Lord can decide if they are doing right, and the Lord will make sure that they do right.
translation_verse: Afana wonu pɛ kɛlɛ sɛ tɛ okulu ebhu nɛ̀ Owusoso Akaa yɛaxɔ̃ɛdɛ? Apaleteɛ ete ko yɛenyi asɛ alɛ piapii alo agɛpɛ. Abalà azã piapii, ɔmɔsɛ Apaleteɛ abalà bhɛtɛ asɛ tɛ akpɛ ogù.

english_verse: Some of the Lord's followers think one day is more important than another. Others think all days are the same. But each of you should make up your own mind.
translation_verse: Kana ɛbe anɔ teli tsyɛ alɔkɔ kiwui tili asɛ kɛlɛ shu dalɛ dɛ tili. Anɔ bhlatɔ pɔ alɔkɔ ewuiɛ nyɔ̃ɔ asɛ lashɔ̃. Kɛlɛ sɛ tɛ anɔ ka nyia ka abhɛtɛ yɛekpeɖekpeɖe mɛ nɛ kɛgɛ aɖɔɔ shu.

english_verse: Any followers who count one day more important than another day do it to honor their Lord. And any followers who eat meat give thanks to God, just like the ones who don't eat meat.
translation_verse: Anunɔ gɛ alɔkɔ kiwui tili asɛ kɛlinu kɛgɛ dzã kiwuhũ dɛɛ, abhɛtɛ kɛlɛ sɛ tɛ alɔkɔ akpɛ Apalete nɛinye nɛitití nɔ. Kana ɛbe anunɔ gɛ atã kɛdzyaa, abhɛtɛ ana sɛ tɛ akpɛ Apalete nɛinye nɛitití, ɔmɔsɛ ehu Owusoso Akaa ahɔɛ nɛ kɛŋaŋã nɛ ɛtakpu, kɛlɛ anunɔ gɛ atɛ aŋãa tsyɛɛ, abhɛtɛ ana akɔ Apaleteɛ tɛ alɔkɔ ehu Owusoso Akaa ahɔɛ nɔ.

english_verse: Whether we live or die, it must be for God, rather than for ourselves.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ bɔlɔ ɔmayɛmɛ na nà atɛ azã nɛ ɛkpã ɔkɔ yɛahɔɛ ishu ete, kana ɛbe bɔlɔ ɔmayɛmɛ anɔ na nà atɛ etsí akɔ yɛahɔɛ ishu ete.

english_verse: Whether we live or die, it must be for the Lord. Alive or dead, we still belong to the Lord.
translation_verse: Onupɛ bɔlɛ nɛ ɛkpãa, bɔlɛ ɛlɛ ɔkɔ Apaleteɛ, kɛlɛ onupɛ butsí tsyɛɛ, butsí ɔkɔ Apaleteɛ, kliso bɔlɛ nɛ ɛkpã alo butsí tsyɛɛ elo, Apaleteɛ ayɛ ko bunu.

english_verse: This is because Christ died and rose to life, so that he would be the Lord of the dead and of the living.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ̀ nɛ̀ ɛtakpu so ko pɛ gɛ Kristo etsíɛ, alɔ pɛ alɛ nɛ ɛkpã sɛ tɛ edzi Apalete akɔ bavɛvɛ nɛ̀ bagɛ alɛ nɛ ɛkpãa.

english_verse: Why do you criticize other followers of the Lord? Why do you look down on them? The day is coming when God will judge all of us.
translation_verse: Wɔ pɔ, kiɖeso pɛ okulu ebhu nɛ̀ wɔelee? Alo kiɖeso pɛ oɖi wɔele nɔ sɛ etinu kɛdɔ̃ na nà nɔ dza? Ɔmɔsɛ bɔlɔ pete bɔbazã bebèkulu ebhu nɛ ɔlɔ nɛ Owusoso Akaa na mɛ.

english_verse: In the Scriptures God says, “I swear by my very life that everyone will kneel down and praise my name!”
translation_verse: Bafɔnɔ kibuiɛ elu asɛ, “Apalete aɖɔ asɛ, ‘Gɛ ɛnya lɛ nɛ ɛkpã koɔ,’ anɔ nyia anɔ ebedzu ekluisi esi akɔ mɛ, kɛlɛ olugbũ ka nyia ka ɔbɔdalɛ ɛlɛ mɛ ɔsɛ mɛinu Owusoso Akaa.’ ”

english_verse: And so, each of us must give an account to God for what we do. Don't Cause Problems for Others
translation_verse: Kliso bɔlɔ mɛ aka nyia aka ebebu yɛishu lũ akɔ Owusoso Akaa.

english_verse: We must stop judging others. We must also make up our minds not to upset anyone's faith.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ̀ soɔ, nɔkɔ bɔyɔlɔ bubhu tikuklu nɛ̀ bagɔlɔ. Nɛ kɛlɛ́ nɛinye mɛɛ, anɔ na nà atɛ lɔkɔ kɛdɔ̃ gɛ bakpata alo tɛ abhɛtɛ kibui alɔkɔ ebhui idzo mɛ akɔ yɛadzya nɔ.

english_verse: The Lord Jesus has made it clear to me that God considers all foods fit to eat. But if you think some foods are unfit to eat, then for you they are not fit.
translation_verse: Mɛ, asɛ anɔ gɛ alɛ Apalete Yesu mɛɛ anaa, kɛlɛ mɛ dalɛ ɔkɔ mɛ piapii ɔsɛ, kɛŋaŋã kpa na nà ma nɛ kɛlɛishu ahɔɛ mɛ. Pɔ anunɔ gɛ amɔ kɛŋaŋã tili asɛ kɛlɛishu gɛ dalɛɛ, kɛlɛ anɔ alɛ kɛgɛ dalɛ ɔkɔ.

english_verse: If you are hurting others by the foods you eat, you are not guided by love. Don't let your appetite destroy someone Christ died for.
translation_verse: Onupɛ kɛgɛ ɔŋãa, kɛkɔ ɔsɛ tɛ wɔele akpɛ ɔmɔpɔɛ nɔ, kɛlɛ ɔtsakɛ nɛ tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ nɛ̀ elé idzo abha. Tɛkɔ ɔsɛ wɔɛŋaŋã kizĩ anɔ teli yɛkpã, anunɔ gɛ yɛtakpu so pɛ Kristo ebetsíɛ.

english_verse: Don't let your right to eat bring shame to Christ.
translation_verse: Tɛkɔ kaɖɔ zizĩ na nà eyi nɛ kibui tili gɛ wɔ oɖi ɔsɛ kɛpɛ pɛ ɔbhɛtɛ shu.

english_verse: God's kingdom isn't about eating and drinking. It is about pleasing God, about living in peace, and about true happiness. All this comes from the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ Owusoso Akaa bukusi ŋaŋãa, itinu bɔdɔ̃ ŋaŋã nɛ̀ bɔdɔ̃ wɔɛwɔɛ ibui, pɔ kinu tɛzazã piapii, ishu yɔyɔ nɛ̀ same mɔmɔ nɛ̀ Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã mɛ ibui.

english_verse: If you serve Christ in this way, you will please God and be respected by people.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ anunɔ gɛ akpɛ ɛtakpu esi akɔ Kristo nɛ idzo ɛlɛ nɛidi abhaa yɛibui Owusoso Akaa adɛmɛ nɔ, kɛlɛ banɔ tsyɛ adɛmɛ atɔɖɔ kɛlɛ abha.

english_verse: We should try to live at peace and help each other have a strong faith.
translation_verse: Kliso nɔkɔ bɔkpɛ emi tɛ bɔbhɛtɛ kɛgɛ bamana ishu yɔyɔɔ, tɛ bɔbhɛtɛ ebuiɛ gɛ bɔba lɔkɔ kpɛ bagɔlɔ ohumi nɔɔ nɔ̀.

english_verse: Don't let your appetite destroy what God has done. All foods are fit to eat, but it is wrong to cause problems for others by what you eat.
translation_verse: Tɛkɔ ɔsɛ kɛŋaŋã so tɛ Owusoso Akaa yɛxɔ̃ɛ izĩ. Kɛŋaŋã ka nyia ka shu dalɛ, pɔ kɛgɛpɛ ɔsɛ tɛ anɔ aŋã kɛdɔ̃ nɔnɔ́ gɛ bakɔ ɔsɛ tɛ anɔ teli etso dzi nɛ yɛtɛyɛyɛkɛŋã niede.

english_verse: It is best not to eat meat or drink wine or do anything else that causes problems for other followers of the Lord.
translation_verse: Kɛpɛ dɛ ɔsɛ tɛ ɔtɛtã kɛdzya alo ɔwɔɛ bunu alo ɔbhɛtɛ kɛgɛ bakɔ ɔsɛ tɛ wɔɛle nɔ etso dzi nɛ yɛtɛyɛyɛkɛŋã niede.

english_verse: What you believe about these things should be kept between you and God. You are fortunate, if your actions don't make you have doubts.
translation_verse: Kliso kɔ kɛgɛ ɔyɛkɛ ɔŋãa nɛ ebui alɛ shuɔ azã wɔ nɛ̀ Owusoso Akaa ete ko ishi. Tiyuyula kinu ɔkɔ anunɔ gɛ atɛ ebu yɛibuiɛ gɛ atɔɖɔ ahɔɛ nɛ kɛlɛ abhaa, nɛpɔ.

english_verse: But if you do have doubts about what you eat, you are going against your beliefs. And you know that is wrong, because anything you do against your beliefs is sin.
translation_verse: Pɔ anunɔ gɛ yɛitúɔ mɛ kpɛ yɛuluhũ flogo pɛ aŋã kɛŋaŋã tiliɛɛ, aŋã ɛpɔ, ɔmɔsɛ anɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛ yɛalɔkɔ aŋã kɛŋaŋãa nɔ, kɛlɛ kɛka nyia ka gɛ gide koeyi nɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛɛ, kinu kibui kpa.

english_verse: If our faith is strong, we should be patient with the Lord's followers whose faith is weak. We should try to please them instead of ourselves.
translation_verse: Bɔlɔ agɛ kpɛ ogù nɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛɛ, kɛlɛ sɛ tɛ bɔyɛkɛ ɔkɔ bagɛ lɔma alɛ ahɔɛ, kɛlɛ kɛma sɛ tɛ bɔɔ bhɛtɛ kɛgɛ iku ɔlɔ awɔ̃ɔ.

english_verse: We should think of their good and try to help them by doing what pleases them.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ sɛ tɛ bɔlɔ ɔmayɛmɛ aka nyia aka abhɛtɛ kɛgɛ baku yɛɔhagbo awɔ̃, pɛ ebeshe atɔɖɔ nɛ yɛtɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛ.

english_verse: Even Christ did not try to please himself. But as the Scriptures say, “The people who insulted you also insulted me.”
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ Kristo tsyɛ elo, agɛ babhɛtɛ kɛgɛ yɛadɛmɛ nɔ akɔ yɛishu, pɔ asɛ kɛfɔ̀nɔ gɛ bafɔnɔ kibuiɛ elu asɛ, “Akwalatuɔ gɛ banunɔ alɔkɔ akwala wɔ nɔɔ, aba yalɛ̀ mɛ.”

english_verse: God is the one who makes us patient and cheerful. I pray that he will help you live at peace with each other, as you follow Christ.
translation_verse: Owusoso Akaa gɛ akɔ kemikpɛkpɛ nɛ̀ kité kpɛkpɛɛ, akɔ ɔnɔ tenikpo bhɛbhɛtɛ ɔfɔfuĩa asɛ banɔ gɛ alɛ Yesu ede anaa,

english_verse: Then all of you together will praise God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ. The Good News Is for Jews and Gentiles
translation_verse: sɛ tɛ nɔlɔkɔ kitú tinikpo nɛ̀ olugbũ tonikpo ɔkpɛ Owusoso Akaa, gɛenu bɔlɔ Apalete Yesu Kristo Yɛakaa nɛinye nɛitití.

english_verse: Honor God by accepting each other, as Christ has accepted you.
translation_verse: Kliso nɔyɛkɛ bagɔlɔ asɛ kɛyɛkɛ gɛ Kristo tsyɛ ayɛkɛ ɔnɔɔ, tɛ kɛkpɛ Owusoso Akaa yɛinye nɛitití.

english_verse: I tell you that Christ came as a servant of the Jews to show that God has kept the promises he made to their famous ancestors. Christ also came,
translation_verse: Ɛkɔ noti ɔsɛ Kristo ebedzi axɔ̃ɛdɛ akɔ Yudanɛ nɛ̀ Owusoso Akaa yɛibui gbangbã nɛinye mɛ, sɛ tɛ atɔɖɔ ɛklagbu nɛ kɛgɛ atɔɖɔ elu akɔ balɛ ashuĩ alɛ nabha,

english_verse: so that the Gentiles would praise God for being kind to them. It is just as the Scriptures say, “I will tell the nations about you, and I will sing praises to your name.”
translation_verse: sɛ tɛ bagɛ betinu Yudanɛ tsyɛ tɛ bakpɛ Owusoso Akaa nɛinye nɛitití nɛ yɛashagabui ɖiɖiɛ shu, asɛ kɛfɔ̀nɔ gɛ bafɔnɔ kibuiɛ elu asɛ, “Kliso ɛbakpɛ wɔɛnye nɛitití nɛ bagɛ betinu Yudanɛ nɛishi, kɛlɛ ɛbazhã ɛdzɛ nɛ wɔɛnye nɛtakpu.”

english_verse: The Scriptures also say to the Gentiles, “Come and celebrate with God's people.”
translation_verse: Tɛfɔfɔnɔ bhlatɔ tsyɛ ɖɔ ɔsɛ, “O! Wɔnɔ bagɛ notinu Yudanɛ, nɔkpɛ same nɛ̀ yɛanunɔ.”

english_verse: Again the Scriptures say, “Praise the Lord, all you Gentiles. All you nations, come and worship him.”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ bazafɔnɔ kibuiɛ elu asɛ, “Nɔkpɛ Apaleteɛ nɛinye nɛitití, wɔnɔ bagɛ otinu Yudanɛ petee, kɛlɛ wɔnɔ pete nɔzhã ɛdzɛ tɛ nɔkpɛ yɛinye nɛitití.”

english_verse: Isaiah says, “Someone from David's family will come to power. He will rule the nations, and they will put their hope in him.”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ Yesaya tsyɛ azaɖɔ asɛ, “Isai yɛyamɛeyi teli ebekoeyì aba, gɛ yɛenu anunɔ gɛ abá baŋã ukusi nɛ ɛmadi nabha, bagɛ betinu Yudanɛ, bebèlu awɔ̃ nɛ yɛabha.”

english_verse: I pray that God, who gives hope, will bless you with complete happiness and peace because of your faith. And may the power of the Holy Spirit fill you with hope. Paul's Work as a Missionary
translation_verse: Owusoso Akaa nabha gɛ nolu awɔ̃ akɔ asɛ tɛ same nɛ̀ ishu yɔyɔ yuku unɔ ɔgana Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã ohumi nɔ mɛ. Paulo Avɛnɔ Kibui Gbangbã Nɛ Bagɛ Betinu Yudanɛ Ɔkɔ

english_verse: My friends, I am sure that you are very good and that you have all the knowledge you need to teach each other.
translation_verse: Mɛelɛ alɛ mɔ, ɛmɔ kɛlɛ idenɔ mɛishu mɛ ɔsɛ, nodzi banɔ gbangbã nɛ bubuti mɛ, kɛlɛ nɔna gɛ nɔbɔlà kasɛ bagɔlɔ ɔdɔ̃.

english_verse: But I have spoken to you plainly and have tried to remind you of some things. God treated me with undeserved grace!
translation_verse: Ɛfɔnɔ ebui gelee kpɛ eplukpa alɛ mɛ nɛ̀ kitèkpɛkpɛ sɛ tɛ iwlui báka ɔnɔ, nɛ tibhui bhuibuɔ gɛ Owusoso Akaa ebhui mɛebuɔ shu,

english_verse: He chose me to be a servant of Christ Jesus for the Gentiles and to do the work of a priest in the service of his good news. God did this so that the Holy Spirit could make the Gentiles into a holy offering, pleasing to him.
