translation_verse: ebu mɛ asɛ tɛ mɛidzi Kristo Yesu yɛibui gbangbã axɔ̃ɛdɛ ɔkɔ bagɛ betinu Yudanɛ, kɛlɛ asɛ banɔlũazazã anaa, tɛ mɛɖɔ Owusoso Akaa Ibui Gbangbã tseɖe bagɛ betinu Yudanɛ, sɛ tɛ bedzi asɛ bɔdɔ̃ kɔkɔ gbangbã ɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa, gɛ bɔlɛishu Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã ɔdalɛ ana.

english_verse: Because of Christ Jesus, I can take pride in my service for God.
translation_verse: Kliso mɛinye wui abha nɛ Yesu Kristo mɛ nɛ̀ mɛxɔ̃ɛ dɛdɛ ɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa nɛtakpu.

english_verse: In fact, all I will talk about is how Christ let me speak and work, so that the Gentiles would obey him.
translation_verse: Ɛtɛbapɛ ɛwɔawɔa sɛ ibu olugbũ mɛ tɛɖɔ kibui na nà ɔdɛ kɛgɛ Kristo abhɛtɛ aganà mɛabha, sɛ tɛ bagɛ betinu Yudanɛ tsyɛ tɛ belũ atɔ̃ akɔ Owusoso Akaa, ɔgana kɛgɛ ɛɖɔ pɛ ɛbhɛtɛ mɛ-

english_verse: Indeed, I will tell how Christ worked miracles and wonders by the power of the Holy Spirit. I have preached the good news about him all the way from Jerusalem to Illyricum.
translation_verse: ɔgana ohumi udɔ̃ bubu kasɛ nɛ̀ atsyindzye ebui, ɔgana Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ ohumi mɛ. Kliso denɔ Yerusalemuɔ, ɛgã kana Iliriko pete batsyɔa, pɛ ɛzɛɖɔ Kristo Ibui Gbangbã, gɛ kɛna piapii.

english_verse: But I have always tried to preach where people have never heard about Christ. I am like a builder who doesn't build on someone else's foundation.
translation_verse: Kidzi mɛtakpu tɔtɔɖɔ ɔsɛ tɛ mɛɖɔ Kibui Gbangbã nɛ ɛklɛ gɛ betinyi Kristoɔ, sɛ tɛ mɛtɛ kpɛ ɛtakpu nɛ ɛkɛlɛ gɛ anɔ bhlatɔ teli atɛkpɛ ibuiɛ tɛɖɔɖɔ esi akɔ banunɔ.

english_verse: It is just as the Scriptures say, “All who haven't been told about him will see him, and those who haven't heard about him will understand.” Paul's Plan To Visit Rome
translation_verse: Asɛ kɛfɔ̀nɔ gɛ bafɔnɔ kibuiɛ elu asɛ, “Banunɔ gɛ bakaɖɔ yɛibuiɛ etseɖe eɖiɛ, babàmɔɛ, kɛlɛ banunɔ gɛ bekelũɔ, bebèlũ kɛlɛ esi.”

english_verse: My work has always kept me from coming to see you.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ so pɛ ɛtɔɖɔ wɔnɔ ɔkɔ tɛbaba ibui flogo pɛ kɛtɛ shɔ̃. Paulo Yɛtɛtɔtɔɖɔ Sɛ Tɛ Avɛ Roma

english_verse: Now there is nothing left for me to do in this part of the world, and for years I have wanted to visit you.
translation_verse: Otsi alɛ gɛ mɛaxɔ̃ɛ ebelè pɛ ɛtɛkadɛ ɔkɔ na nà nɛ kɛma ɛlɛ mɛɛ, kɛlɛ agɔ gelee nɛ alɛ gɛ kinu mɛ tɛpɛpɛ sɛ tɛ mɛbaɖi unɔɔ,

english_verse: So I plan to stop off on my way to Spain. Then after a short, but refreshing, visit with you, I hope you will quickly send me on.
translation_verse: ɛtɔɖɔ sɛ tɛ mɛba wɔnɔ ɔkɔ nɛ kibeɛ gɛ ɛvɛ Spania. Kibui tɔtɔɖɔ kinu ɔsɛ tɛ mɛiku wɔnɔ ɔkɔ nɛ kibeɛ gɛ ɛfala pɛ ɛvɛɛ, kɛlɛ tɛ mɛbabisi tɛpɛplɛnɔ odenɔ wɔnɔ ɔkɔ nɛ kidzoɔ tɛgagã mɛ, onupɛ ɛzã wɔnɔ ɔbha kɛbɔɛ nɔ.

english_verse: I am now on my way to Jerusalem to deliver the money that the Lord's followers in Macedonia and Achaia collected for God's needy people.
translation_verse: Makedonianɛ nɛ̀ Axayanɛ beshogò bɔdɔ̃ nɛ̀ same sɛ tɛ balɔkɔ aplɛnɔ betsyuitsyuiɛ galɛ banɔ dadalɛ nɛishi nɛ Yerusalemuɔ.

english_verse: This is something they really wanted to do. But sharing their money with the Jews was also like paying back a debt, because the Jews had already shared their spiritual blessings with the Gentiles.
translation_verse: Batɔɖɔ akɔ bɔdɔ̃ nɛ̀ same, kɛlɛ ɔmɔ asɛ kɛbhɛ gɛ badɔ nɛ balɛishu ana. Onupɛ bagɛ betinu Yudanɛ amɔ bɔdɔ̃ gbangbã odenɔ ɔfɔfuĩa tiyuyula gɛinu Yudanɛ ɔyɛ mɛɛ, kɛlɛ badɔ ɛbhɛ nɛ Yudanɛ shu gɛ kɛlɛ kɔ alɛ sɛ tɛ balɔkɔ bɔdɔ̃ɔ gɛ bamɔ̃ edenɔ tiyuyula mɛɛ, aplɛnɔ Yudanɛ nɔ.

english_verse: After I have safely delivered this money, I will visit you and then go on to Spain.
translation_verse: Kliso onupɛ ɛdɛ kɛxɔ̃ɛ ɛlɛ, pɛ iɖi kɛlɛ ɔkɔ ɔsɛ bɔdɔ̃ ɔlɛ ku balɛ ahɔɛ mɛ ebekeɛ, ɛba bàgã wɔnɔ ɔkɔ fala vɛ Spania.

english_verse: And when I do arrive in Rome, I know it will be with the full blessings of Christ.
translation_verse: Inyi sɛ ɛbaku wɔnɔ ɔkɔɔ, ɛbalɔkɔ Kristo yɛtiyuyula gɛ lɛ Yɛibui Gbangbã mɛ mana wɔnɔ nɛ tikpekpè mɛ.

english_verse: My friends, by the power of the Lord Jesus Christ and by the love that comes from the Holy Spirit, I beg you to pray sincerely with me and for me.
translation_verse: Mɛelɛ alɛ mɔ, ɛkpalà ɔnɔ ɔgana Apalete Yesu Kristo abha, nɛ̀ Yɛɔfɔfuĩa nɔ tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ nɛ̀ banɔ mɛɛ, tɛ nɔplɛnɔ mɛ nɛ ɛxɔ̃ɛ gagalĩ lɛ mɛ, tɛ nokulu Owusoso Akaa nɛ mɛtakpu.

english_verse: Pray that God will protect me from the unbelievers in Judea, and that his people in Jerusalem will be pleased with what I am doing.
translation_verse: Nokulu Owusoso Akaa sɛ tɛ ebu mɛ eyinɔ nɛ bagɛ agɛ yɛkɛ Yesu abha aŋã nɛ Yudea nɔ ahɔɛ mɛ, kɛlɛ bɔdɔ̃ gɛ ɛlɔkɔ vɛnɔ nɛ̀ mɛaxɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa nɛ Yerusalemuɔ leku bagɛ balɛishu badalɛ nɛ ɛkɛlɛ awɔ̃,

english_verse: Ask God to let me come to you and have a pleasant and refreshing visit.
translation_verse: sɛ tɛ nɛ Owusoso Akaa tɛpɛplɛnɔ mɛɛ, ɛbaku wɔnɔ ɔkɔ nɛ same kɛlɛ mɛ nɛ̀ wɔnɔ pete nɔ, tɛbɔkpɛ same nɛ bagɔlɔ shu.

english_verse: I pray that God, who gives peace, will be with all of you. Amen.
translation_verse: Owusoso Akaa gɛ yɛakɔ ishu yɔyɔ azãplɛnɔ wɔnɔ petee. Amen.

english_verse: I have good things to say about Phoebe, who is a leader in the church at Cenchreae.
translation_verse: Ɛbaka bɔlɔ ada Foebe ibui kɔ ɔnɔ, gɛenu ɔha axɔ̃ɛdɛ gbangbã teli idi nɛ Kɛnxren ɔha mɛ.

english_verse: Welcome her in a way that is proper for someone who has faith in the Lord and is one of God's own people. Help her in any way you can. After all, she has proved to be a respected leader for many others, including me.
translation_verse: Ibisi denɔ wɔnɔ ahɔɛ sɛ tɛ nɔyɛkɛ yɛ nɛ Apalete nɛinye mɛ. Nɔyɛkɛ yɛ asɛ kɛyɛkɛ gɛ nɔyɛkɛ banɔ dadalɛɛ ana, kɛlɛ nɔkɔɛ tɛpɛplɛnɔ ka nyia ka gɛ apɛ edenɔ wɔnɔ ɔkɔ, ɔmɔsɛ axɔ̃ɛdɛ yɛenu akɔ banɔ gelee, gɛ mɛ tsyɛ inu balɛmɛ anɔ tenikpo.

english_verse: Give my greetings to Priscilla and Aquila. They have not only served Christ Jesus together with me,
translation_verse: Nɔsɛnɔ Priskila nɛ̀ Akwila, mɛaxɔ̃ɛdɛ ɔhagbo alɛ nɛ Yesu Kristo mɛ.

english_verse: but they have even risked their lives for me. I am grateful for them and so are all the Gentile churches.
translation_verse: Bálɔkɔ balɛ ɛkpã akɔ nɛ mɛtakpu. Anɛ mɛ ete, pɔ bagɛ etinu Yudanɛ ɔhamɛanɛ pete tsyɛ ezehu alɛ ahɔɛ.

english_verse: Greet the church that meets in their home. Greet my dear friend Epaenetus, who was the first person in Asia to have faith in Christ.
translation_verse: Sɛnɔ bahá gɛ atsyɔa nɛ balɛ ɔpã mɛɛ tsyɛ. Ɛsɛnɔ Epaineto, anɔ gɛ ɛdɛmɛ nɔ. Yɛedzi anɔ nɛ Asia gɛ eputɔ etsyina edzi Kristoanɔ.

english_verse: Greet Mary, who has worked so hard for you.
translation_verse: Sɛnɔ Maria, gɛ edzi anunɔ gɛ azawɔa axɔ̃ɛ nɛ̀ kemikpɛkpɛ akɔ ɔnɔ.

english_verse: Greet my relatives Andronicus and Junia, who were in jail with me. They are highly respected by the apostles and were followers of Christ before I was.
translation_verse: Sɛnɔ mɛeshopo alɛ Androniko nɛ̀ Yunio, balɛ nɛ̀ mɛ bɔzã ɔɖavũ. Banɔkpɛkpɛ enyi alɛ piapii gɛ bedzi Kristoelete akpɛ otugbà akɔ mɛ.

english_verse: Greet Ampliatus, my dear friend whose faith is in the Lord.
translation_verse: Sɛnɔ Ampliatus, anunɔ gɛ ɛdɛmɛ nɔ nɛ Apaleteɛ mɛ.

english_verse: Greet Urbanus, who serves Christ along with us. Greet my dear friend Stachys.
translation_verse: Sɛnɔ Urbano, anunɔ gɛ azadɛ Kristo axɔ̃ɛ nɛ̀ mɛ, nɛ̀ Staxis anunɔ gɛ ɛdɛmɛ nɔɔ.

english_verse: Greet Apelles, a faithful servant of Christ. Greet Aristobulus and his family.
translation_verse: Sɛnɔ Apeles, anɔ gɛ eshè nɛ̀ ɔhakpɛkpɛ nɛ Kristo mɛ. Sɛnɔ Aristobolo nɛ̀ yɛɔpãmɛanɛ tsyɛ piapii.

english_verse: Greet Herodion, who is a relative of mine. Greet Narcissus and the others in his family, who have faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Sɛnɔ mɛeshopo Herodion. Sɛnɔ Narkiso nɛ̀ yɛɔpãmɛanɛ gɛ balɛ ɔha kpɛkpɛ mɛ nɛ̀ Apalete Kristoɔ.

english_verse: Greet Tryphaena and Tryphosa, who work hard for the Lord. Greet my dear friend Persis. She also works hard for the Lord.
translation_verse: Sɛnɔ Trifaena nɛ̀ Trisofa gɛ benu banunɔ gɛ bazadɛ axɔ̃ɛ akɔ Apaleteɛ. Sɛnɔ mɛɔbhia Persis, anɔ gɛ akpɛ emi nɛ Apalete nɛxɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa mɛ.

english_verse: Greet Rufus, that special servant of the Lord, and greet his mother, who has been like a mother to me.
translation_verse: Sɛnɔ Rufo, anɔ gɛ bebu elu akɔ Apaleteɛ, nɛ̀ yɛelĩ gɛenu mɛ tsyɛ mɛelĩɛ.

english_verse: Greet Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, and Hermas, as well as our friends who are with them.
translation_verse: Sɛnɔ Asinkrito, Flego, Herme, Patroba, Herma nɛ̀ baadzya gɛ balɛ wɔbha nɛ ɛkɛlɛ.

english_verse: Greet Philologus, Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas, and all of God's people who are with them.
translation_verse: Sɛnɔ Filologo nɛ̀ Yulia, Nerea, yɛada nɛ̀ Olimpa ɔlɔkɔ plɛnɔ ɔhamɛeyi bhlatɔ pete gɛ balɛ alɛ ɔbha nɛ ɛkɛlɛ.

english_verse: Be sure to give each other a warm greeting. All of Christ's churches greet you.
translation_verse: Nɔsɛnɔ bagɔlɔ nɛ̀ same, nɛ ahɔɛ kpɛkpɛ nɛ bagɔlɔ mɛ. Kristo yɛɔhamɛeyiɛ pete basɛnɛ wɔnɔ piapii.

english_verse: My friends, I beg you to watch out for anyone who causes trouble and divides the church by refusing to do what all of you were taught. Stay away from them!
translation_verse: Mɛelɛ alɛ mɔ, ihu buluhũ kɔ ɔnɔ sɛ tɛ nolú awɔ̃ nɛ̀ bagɛ bamana kishi zizĩ nɛ banɔ ishi, pɛ babhɛtɛ ebui gɛ etsu awuhũ, pɛ lagã ɔtɔ nɛ ebuiɛ gɛ bɔkasɛ pɛ nɔkpasɛ shu. Nɔtɛkpɛ alɛ ɔha.

english_verse: They want to serve themselves and not Christ the Lord. Their flattery and fancy talk fool people who don't know any better.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ banɔ alɛ nɛidiɛ, anɛ bɔlɔ Apalete Kristo anɔkpɛkpɛ benu, balɛ evũ shu axɔ̃ɛ baadɛ. Balɔkɔ ebui dzyɛdzyɛ nɛ̀ banɔ balɛ enye kpɛkpɛ nɛitití, pɛ balɔkɔ awɔɛ banɔ gɛ batɛ abhɛtɛ ekpeɖekpeɖe mɛ nɛ kibuiɛ shuɔ nɔ.

english_verse: I am glad that everyone knows how well you obey the Lord. But still, I want you to understand what is good and not have anything to do with evil.
translation_verse: Anɔ nyia anɔ elũ kibui nɛ wɔnɔ batɔ̃ lulũ shu, kɛlɛ kinu kibui gɛ iku mɛ same nɛ wɔnɔ shu, kɛlɛ kɛgɛ̀ ɛpɛ kɔ wɔnɔ kɛlɛinu ɔsɛ tɛ nodzi bebutinɛ nɛ kibui gbangbã shu, kɛlɛ nɛ ebui kpa shu pɔɔ, nɔkɔ wɔnɔ ahɔɛ ladalɛ nɛ kɛka nyia ka shu.

english_verse: Then God, who gives peace, will soon crush Satan under your feet. I pray that our Lord Jesus will be kind to you.
translation_verse: Ishu yɔyɔ Owusoso Akaa abayɛ Satana ohumi akpɛ wɔnɔ ɔtsɛɖɛkpɔ esi tsaitsai. Apalete Yesu yɛtibhui bhuibuɔ zã ɔplɛnɔ wɔnɔ.

english_verse: Timothy, who works with me, sends his greetings, and so do my relatives, Lucius, Jason, and Sosipater.
translation_verse: Timoteo gɛ adɛ ɛxɔ̃ɛ ɛlɛ nɛ̀ mɛɛ, Lukio, Yawson nɛ̀ Sesipatro gɛ benu mɛeshopo alɛ tsyɛɛ, basɛnɛ wɔnɔ piapii.

english_verse: I, Tertius, also send my greetings. I am a follower of the Lord, and I wrote this letter.
translation_verse: Mɛ, Tertio gɛfɔnɔ keplukpa alɛ tsyɛɛ, ɛsɛnɛ wɔnɔ nɛ Apaleteɛ nɛinye mɛ.

english_verse: Praise God! He can make you strong by means of my good news, which is the message about Jesus Christ. For ages and ages this message was kept secret,
translation_verse: Anunɔ gɛ yɛalà ayɛkɛ wɔnɔ edzu nɛ ɛkpã ɔgana Kibui Gbangbã gɛ ɛkasɛ ɔnɔ mɛ nɛ Yesu Kristo shu, nɛ̀ kibu gɛ bebu ebui kpukpũɔ gɛ lalɛ alɛ ɔzãkɔ odenɔ kuku ibeɛ,

english_verse: but now at last it has been told. The eternal God commanded his prophets to write about the good news, so that all nations would obey and have faith.
translation_verse: pɔ otsi alɛ bebu alɛ akasɛ, kɛlɛ buti alɛ ɔgana ebuiɖɔluanɛ ebui fɔfɔnɔ luɔ mɛ, nɛ̀ ɔgana ɔsha gɛ Owusoso Akaa akɔɔ mɛ, sɛ tɛ ɛmadiɛ pete bayɛkɛ aŋã, kɛlɛ tɛ belũ batɔ̃ɔ akɔɛ,

english_verse: And now, because of Jesus Christ, we can praise the only wise God forever! Amen.
translation_verse: sɛ tɛ bɔkpɛ Owusoso Akaa gɛenu ebuitinɛ tenikpo nɔ nɛitití tihɔɔ nɛ Yesu Kristo yɛinye mɛ! Amen.

english_verse: From Paul, chosen by God to be an apostle of Christ Jesus, and from Sosthenes, who is also a follower.
translation_verse: Mɛ Paulo gɛ bekulú asɛ tɛ idzì anɔkpɛkpɛ ɔkɔ Yesu Kristo nɛ Owusoso Akaa yɛ tɛpɛpɛ ulũɔ, nɛ̀ bɔlɔ adzya Sostenese yɛafɔnɔ keplukpa alɛ.

english_verse: To God's church in Corinth. Christ Jesus chose you to be his very own people, and you worship in his name, as we and all others do who call him Lord.
translation_verse: Ɛfɔnɔ kalɛ sɛsɛ Owusoso Akaa ɔha gɛalɛ Korinto, nɛ̀ bagɛ badalɛ balɛishu nɛ Yesu Kristo mɛ pɛ balɛishu dalɛ, ɔlɔkɔ plɛnɔ bagɛ balɛ ɔkɔ nyia ɔkɔ pɛ beekulu Apalete Yesu Kristoɔ inyeɛ, anɔ gɛenu balɛ Apaleteɛ nɛ̀ bɔlɔ ayɛ.

english_verse: My prayer is that God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace!
translation_verse: Tibhui bhuibu nɛ̀ ishu yɔyɔ mɛpɛɛ kɔ wɔnɔ odenɔ Kititiayɛ gɛenu bɔlɔ Akaa nɛ̀ bɔlɔ Apalete Yesu Kristo ɔkɔ. Ahɔɛhuhu

english_verse: I never stop thanking my God for treating you with undeserved grace by giving you Christ Jesus,
translation_verse: Iihu Owusoso Akaa yɛahɔɛ kibe nyia be nɛ wɔnɔ kɛtakpu, nɛ tibhui bhuibuɔ gɛ akɔ wɔnɔ nɛ Yesu Kristo mɛɛ shu.

english_verse: Now you are certain that everything we told you about our Lord Christ Jesus is true.
translation_verse: ɔmɔsɛ ebuiɛ gɛ bɔɖɔ nɛ Kristo shuɔ awɔɛ nɛ wɔnɔ ɛkpã mɛ.

english_verse: You are not missing out on any blessings, as you wait for him to return.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ, nɔtɛ ɔpɛɛ ɔfɔfuĩa mɛ kɛdɔ̃ kɔkɔ nanà gɛ noɖì kidzo mɛ gagalĩi ɔkɔ bɔlɔ Apalete Yesu Kristo yɛ tibubu kasɛɛ.

english_verse: And until the day Christ does return, he will keep you completely innocent.
translation_verse: Abakɔ nɔzã gagalĩ ɔvɛnɔ kiwui gɔgɔ nabha, kɛba kɔsɛ nɔtɛ bɔmɔ bamɛ tsitsí nɛ bɔlɔ Apalete Yesu Kristo yɛiwui nabha.

english_verse: God can be trusted, and he chose you to be partners with his Son, our Lord Jesus Christ. Taking Sides
translation_verse: Owusoso Akaa gɛ yɛekulu wɔnɔ akɔ ɔhakpɛkpɛ nɛ̀ Yɛeyianyɛ Yesu Kristo gɛenu bɔlɔ Apaleteɛ, enu kibui gɛlɛ kɛlɛ ɔzãkɔ abhɛbhɛtɛ elete. Ɔtɔbubu Nɛ Ɔha Mɛ

english_verse: My dear friends, as a follower of our Lord Jesus Christ, I beg you to get along with each other. Don't take sides. Always try to agree in what you think.
translation_verse: Mɛelé alɛ mɔ, ɛkɔɔ wɔnɔ kɛtakpu nɛ bɔlɔ Apalete Yesu Kristo yɛinye mɛ sɛ tɛ̀ wɔnɔ nyɔ̃ɔ nɔ ozolũ bagɔlɔ esi, tɛ̀ ɔtɔbubu nanà ɔtɔzã wɔnɔɛshi, tɛ̀ nɔbhɛtɛ tenikpo piapii nɛ ekpeɖekpeɖe nɛ̀ ebui tɔtɔɖɔ mɛ.

english_verse: Several people from Chloe's family have already reported to me that you keep arguing with each other.
translation_verse: Mɛelé alɛ mɔ, banɔ teli nɛ Kloe bɔpã mɛ akɔ mɛitì sɛ ebla ebui akpasɛ wɔnɔɛshi.

english_verse: They have said that some of you claim to follow me, while others claim to follow Apollos or Peter or Christ.
translation_verse: Kɛgɛ mɛɖɔ kɛlɛinu ɔsɛ, wɔnɔ mɛ anɔ tenikpo aaɖɔ asɛ, “Paulo ede mɛlɛ.” Anɔ bhlatɔ tsyɛ asɛ, “Apolo ede mɛlɛ.” Teli tsyɛ asɛ, “Kefa ede mɛlɛ” kɛlɛ anɔbhlatɔ tsyɛ aɖɔ asɛ “Kristo ede mɛlɛ.”

english_verse: Has Christ been divided up? Was I nailed to a cross for you? Were you baptized in my name?
translation_verse: Pɔ, bayɛɛ Kristoɔ mɛ akpɛ bɔwɔ̃pɛ mɛɛ dza? Paulo bakpalà nɛ butsi osiɛ shu akɔ wɔnɔ dza?, pɛnɔsɛ Paulo inye mɛ bakpɛ bulĩ akɔ wɔnɔ dza?

english_verse: I thank God that I didn't baptize any of you except Crispus and Gaius.
translation_verse: Ihú ahɔɛ ɔsɛ, ɛgɛ kpɛ bulĩ kɔ wɔnɔ mɛ anɔ nanà ɔdɛ Krispo nɛ̀ Gayo,

english_verse: Not one of you can say that you were baptized in my name.
translation_verse: kliso anɔ nanà atɛbà làɖɔ asɛ mɛinye mɛ bakpɛ bulĩ akɔ wɔnɔ.

english_verse: Christ did not send me to baptize. He sent me to tell the good news without using words that sound wise and would make the cross of Christ lose its power. Christ Is God's Power and Wisdom
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ Kristo agɛkpɛ mɛasɛ tɛ̀ ɛdɛkpɛ bulĩ, pɔ tɛ̀ mɛdɛɖɔ kibui gbangbãa, gɛnɛ nɛ anɔdzyrame yɛ bubuti mɛ, onu gɛnanaa, Yesu butsi osiɛ ohumi nɔma kɛnanà mɛ. Kristo Yɛenu Owusoso Akaa Bubuti Nɛ̀ Yɛohumi

english_verse: The message about the cross doesn't make any sense to lost people. But for those of us who are being saved, it is God's power at work.
translation_verse: Butsi osiɛ shu ebuiɛ enu bɔga ebui ɔkɔ bagɛ bayalàa, pɔ bɔlɔ bagɛ baayɛkɛ elu nɛkpã xɔnɔɔ, lɛenu Owusoso Akaa ohumi ɔkɔ bɔlɔ.

english_verse: As God says in the Scriptures, “I will destroy the wisdom of all who claim to be wise. I will confuse those who think they know so much.”
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ bafɔnɔ elú asɛ, Ɛbazĩ bebuti eleteɛ balɛ bubutiɛ, ɛba futsù bagɛ eɖi asɛ bubutì lɛ balɛ ahɔɛ mɛ balɛ bubutiɛ mɛ kpɛ.”

english_verse: What happened to those wise people? What happened to those experts in the Scriptures? What happened to the ones who think they have all the answers? Didn't God show that the wisdom of this world is foolish?
translation_verse: Fakɔ ebutinɛ alɛ? Fakɔ buplukpaeleteɛ alɛ. Fakɔ bagɛ apɛɛ obo kanɔ nɔ̀ bubutì idzoɔ alɛ dza? Owusoso Akaa egitsyina kanɔ alɛ mɛ bubutiɛ budzi bɔga dɔdɔ dza?

english_verse: God was wise and decided not to let the people of this world use their wisdom to learn about him. Instead, God chose to save only those who believe the foolish message we preach.
translation_verse: Nɛ Owusoso Akaa bubuti nulũ soɔ, kanɔ alɛ mɛ gana kalɛ bubutiɛ mɛ betinyi yɛ, bɔwɔaga kibuiɛ gɛ baɖɔ tɛ bebù bagɛ ayɛkɛ aŋã elu nɛkpã ku Owusoso Akaa nɔ awɔ ɔdɛ.

english_verse: Jews ask for miracles, and Greeks want something that sounds wise.
translation_verse: Yudanɛ ebisi atsyindzyeɛ ebui tɛ̀ bamɔ, kɛlɛ Helanɛ xɔnɔ atɔnɔ bubuti ede,

english_verse: But we preach that Christ was nailed to a cross. Most Jews have problems with this, and most Gentiles think it is foolish.
translation_verse: pɔ bɔlɔ xɔnɔɔ, Kristoɔ gɛ bakpalà nɛ butsi osiɛ shu nɛibui bɔɖɔ, kibui gɛ kinu kɛdɔ̃ kpakpatà ɔkɔ Yudanɛ pɛ bɔga kibui kinu ɔkɔ bagɛ betinu Yudanɛ xɔnɔ,

english_verse: Our message is God's power and wisdom for the Jews and the Greeks that he has chosen.
translation_verse: pɔ bagɛ Owusoso Akaa ekulu xɔnɔ, Yudanɛ nɛ̀ Helanɛ petee, Yesu yɛenu Owusoso Akaa yɛohumi nɛ̀ bubutì.

english_verse: Even when God is foolish, he is wiser than everyone else, and even when God is weak, he is stronger than everyone else.
translation_verse: Owusoso Akaa yɛ bɔgadɔdɔ nu bubutì ɔdɛ anɔdzyrame yɛubutì, kɛlɛ Owusoso Akaa tibobotsòɔ gagalĩ ɔdɛ banɔdzyrame ohumi.

english_verse: My dear friends, remember what you were when God chose you. The people of this world didn't think that many of you were wise. Only a few of you were in places of power, and not many of you came from important families.
translation_verse: Mɛele alɛ mɔ, nɔbakà kɛzã gɛ nɔzã flogo pɛ bekulu wɔnɔ. Gɛnɛ wɔnɔ banɔ kpekpèɛ balɛ edzi bebutinɛ nɛ banɔdzyrame bɔdɔ̃ mɔmɔ ulũ, gɛnɛ banɔ gelee gɛazã wɔnɔɛshiɛ balɛ edzì bagɛ eku banɔ awɔ, gɛnɛ wɔnɔ banɔ gelee balɛ ede baapã gbangbã mɛ.

english_verse: But God chose the foolish things of this world to put the wise to shame. He chose the weak things of this world to put the powerful to shame.
translation_verse: Pɔɔ Owusoso Akaa alɔkɔ bɔga dɔdɔ ebuiɛ nɛ kanɔ nɔ̀ mɛ sɛ tɛ̀ alɔkɔ akpɛ bebutinɛ nawuĩ nɔ. Owusoso Akaa alɔkɔ bɔdɔ̃ gɛ bubotsoɔ akpɛ bɔgagalĩ nawuĩ nɔ.

english_verse: What the world thinks is worthless, useless, and nothing at all is what God has used to destroy what the world considers important.
translation_verse: Alɔkɔ bɔdɔ̃ gɛmã kɛnanà mɛ nɛ kanɔ alɛ mɛ pɛ banɔ etie ɖi akpɛ kɛnanà mɛ, nɛ̀ ebuiɛ gɛ legi yi kɛdɔ̃ nanàa, ebù ebuiɛ gɛalɛ eyinɔ,

english_verse: God did all this to keep anyone from bragging to him.
translation_verse: sɛ tɛ̀ ananà etibe feke yɛishu nɛ yɛamɛ.

english_verse: You are God's children. He sent Christ Jesus to save us and to make us wise, acceptable, and holy.
translation_verse: Yɛ sopɛ nɔkpasɛ Yesu Kristo mɛ, anɔ gɛ yɛakɔ ɔlɔ bubuti odenɔ Owusoso Akaa ɔkɔ, gɛ kɛlɛinù bɔlɔ tɛzazã piapii, ishudadalɛ nɛ̀ bɔlɔ tibubuyinɔ odenɔ ebui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ mɛ.

english_verse: So if you want to brag, do what the Scriptures say and brag about the Lord.
translation_verse: Kliso asɛ gɛ bafɔnɔ eluɔ, “Anunɔ gɛ asɛ efeke yɛishuɔ, tɛ efeke ɛlɛ nɛ Apaleteɛ mɛ.”

english_verse: Friends, when I came and told you the mystery that God had shared with us, I didn't use big words or try to sound wise.
translation_verse: Mɛelé alɛ mɔ, gɛba wɔnɔ ɔbha, ɛgɛba asɛ anɔ gɛenyi kaɖɔ ɖɔɖɔ piapii alo asɛ anɔ gɛ yɛ bubutì vɛ otugbà ana, nɛ kibeɛ gɛ itseɖe wɔnɔ ebuiɛ gɛ ɛzɛ mɔ nɛ Owusoso Akaa shu.

english_verse: In fact, while I was with you, I made up my mind to speak only about Jesus Christ, who had been nailed to a cross.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ gɛzã wɔnɔ ɛshiɛ, ɛbhɛtɛ mɛishu asɛ itinyí kɛnanà ɔdɛ Yesu Kristo, nɛ̀ kana gɛ bakpalà yɛ nɛ butsi osiɛ shu.

english_verse: At first, I was weak and trembling with fear.
translation_verse: Ɛba wɔnɔ ɔkɔ nɛ tibobotsò nɛ̀ kifusisi mɛ, nɛ̀ tishushukù gbagbagbà mɛ.

english_verse: When I talked with you or preached, I didn't try to prove anything by sounding wise. I simply let God's Spirit show his power.
translation_verse: Mɛ ebuiɛ gɛ ɛɖɔ nɛ Owusoso Akaa shuɔ, etinu bubutì nɛ̀ ebui gɛ batɛ elũ alɛ esi, pɔ lenu ebui gɛ Owusoso Akaa Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ ohumi nɔ obu ɔkasɛ,

english_verse: That way you would have faith because of God's power and not because of human wisdom.
translation_verse: sɛ tɛ̀ wɔnɔ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã tɛbà zã banɔdzyrame bubuti abha, pɔ tɛ lɔzã Owusoso Aka yɛohumi nabha boŋ. Bubutiɛ Gide Ɔfɔfuĩa Nɔ Mɛ

english_verse: We do use wisdom when speaking to people who are mature in their faith. But it isn't the wisdom of this world or of its rulers, who will soon disappear.
translation_verse: Bɔkaaɖɔ bubuti ebui alɛ nɛ bagɛ batɛ kpɛ iguɔ nɛishi, pɔ gɛnɛ obo kanɔ alɛ mɛ bubuti alo bagɛ baaŋã bukusì nɛ kalɛ mɛ, gɛ beti bedzi kɛdɔ̃ nanà.

english_verse: We speak of God's hidden and mysterious wisdom that God decided to use for our glory long before the world began.
translation_verse: Oo, Owusoso Akaa bubutiɛ gɛ bukpũɔ shu bɔɖɔ kaɖɔ, bubutìɛ gɛ bekpũ bɔlɛ pɛ Owusoso Akaa abalɛ elú akɔ bɔlɔ shu dadalɛ flogo pɛ kanɔ nɔ̀ abakpɛ kesi.

english_verse: The rulers of this world didn't know anything about this wisdom. If they had known about it, they would not have nailed the glorious Lord to a cross.
translation_verse: Kanɔ alɛ mɛ beekusi nanà egilũ bɔlɛ esi, ɔmɔsɛ pɛ otɛ ɔsɛ belũ bɔlɛ kesiɛ tsyɛ alo, bagɛ takpalà Apaleteɛ gɛ yɛishu dalɛ nɛ butsi osiɛ shu.

english_verse: But it is just as the Scriptures say, “What God has planned for people who love him is more than eyes have seen or ears have heard. It has never even entered our minds!”
translation_verse: Asɛ kana gɛ bafɔnɔ elu asɛ, “Ebuiɛ gɛ awuhũ nanà akamɔ eɖiɛ, pɛ batɔ̃ eke elũ eɖiɛ, pɛ kɛka ba ananà ekpeɖekpeɖe mɛ tsyɛ eɖiɛ, alɛ Owusoso Akaa abalɛ elu akɔ bagɛ badɛmɛ yɛibuiɛ”

english_verse: God's Spirit has shown you everything. His Spirit finds out everything, even what is deep in the mind of God.
translation_verse: pɔ Owusoso Akaa ebu alɛ akasɛ bɔlɔ xɔnɔ ganà yɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ mɛ. Ɔfɔfuĩa ɔpɔ̃ɛ ebuiɛ petee mɛ, Owusoso Akaa yɛebui dzudzuɔ mɛ tsyɛ.

english_verse: You are the only one who knows what is in your own mind, and God's Spirit is the only one who knows what is in God's mind.
translation_verse: Afana gɛ akpasɛ banɔishi pɛ ebeti kɛnɔnɔ gɛ kɛkpasɛ anɔ bhlatɔ yɛ ekpeɖekpeɖe mɛ ɔdɛ ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ gɛ ɔlɔ kpasɛ yɛ mɛɛ dza? Kidzo ɛlɛ nabha ananà etinyi kɛnɔnɔ gɛ kpasɛ Owusoso Akaa yɛekpeɖekpeɖe mɛ ɔdɛ Owusoso Akaa Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ ete.

english_verse: But God has given us his Spirit. This is why we don't think the same way that the people of this world think. This is also why we can recognize the blessings God has given us.
translation_verse: Gɛnɛ ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ gɛode kanɔ nɔ̀ mɛ bɔyɛkɛ, pɔ ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ gɛode Owusoso Akaa ɔkɔ bɔyɛkɛ, sɛ tɛ̀ bulũ kɛgɛ Owusoso Akaa akɔ bɔlɔ kɛbhɛ mabuia mabuia niesi.

english_verse: Every word we speak was taught to us by God's Spirit, not by human wisdom. And this same Spirit helps us teach spiritual things to spiritual people.
translation_verse: Ebui alɛ bɔɖɔ, gɛnɛ banɔdzyrame ebui nɛ̀ bubutì balɔkɔ akasɛ bɔlɔ ɔdɔ̃ nɔ, pɔ gana ebuiɛ gɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ kasɛ bɔlɔ abha, gɛ ladalɛ ɔfɔfuĩa mɛ ebuiɛ gɛ lakpasɛ alɛ mɛ ɔganà ɔfɔfuĩa ebuiɖɔɖɔ mɛ.

english_verse: This is why only someone who has God's Spirit can understand spiritual blessings. Anyone who doesn't have God's Spirit thinks these blessings are foolish.
translation_verse: Anunɔ gɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ ma yɛmɛɛ, atɛyɛkɛ ebuiɛ gɛ alɛ ede Owusoso Akaa Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ mɛ, ɔmɔsɛ lenu kɛwɔaga ibui ɔkɔɛ, kɛlɛ atɛ balà elũ alɛ esi ɔmɔsɛ ɔfɔfuĩa balɔkɔ elũ alɛ esi nɔ.

english_verse: People who are guided by the Spirit can make all kinds of judgments, but they cannot be judged by others.
translation_verse: Anunɔ gɛ akpasɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ mɛɛ, ekulu kebhu nɛ ebuiɛ petee shu, pɔ yɛxɔnɔ ananà atɛlà kebhu ekulu niyɛ.

english_verse: The Scriptures ask, “Has anyone ever known the thoughts of the Lord or given him advice?” But we understand what Christ is thinking.
translation_verse: “Ɔmɔsɛ afana gɛenyi kɛgɛ Apaleteɛ atɔɖɔ pɛ abalà kasɛ yɛɔdɔ̃ɔ dza?” Bɔlɔ xɔnɔ, Kristo yɛibui tɔtɔɖɔ kɛlɛ kpasɛ bɔlɔ mɛ.

english_verse: My friends, you are acting like the people of this world. That's why I could not speak to you as spiritual people. You are like babies as far as your faith in Christ is concerned.
translation_verse: Mɛelé alɛ mɔ, ɛtɛɖɔ kaɖɔ ɔkɔ wɔnɔ asɛ ɔfɔfuĩa mɛ anɔ, pɔ asɛ anɔdzyrame ana, asɛ beyì vɔvɔ gɛ akpasɛ Kristo mɛ ana.

english_verse: So I had to treat you like babies and feed you milk. You could not take solid food, and you still cannot,
translation_verse: Ɛkɔ wɔnɔ kɛmɔlĩ, gɛnɛ bɔŋaŋã, ɔmɔsɛ nɛɔko oshè pɛ nɔbɔ ŋã bɔlɛ, kibui gbã nɛɔkɔ balɛ otsiɛ ɔkɔ bɔlɛ tɛŋaŋã.

english_verse: because you are not yet spiritual. You are jealous and argue with each other. This proves you are not spiritual and you are acting like the people of this world.
translation_verse: Kanɔ mɛ banɔ ete nonu. Gɛ kiwuhũ dzadzã nɛ̀ ebela ebui akpasɛ wɔnɔɛishiɛɛ, kɛlɛ kanɔ mɛ anɔ nonuɔ dza? Nɔtɛ ɔbhɛtɛ ebui ɔmɔ asɛ kanɔ nɔ̀ mɛ banɔ nɔ ete dza?

english_verse: Some of you say you follow me, and others claim to follow Apollos. Isn't this how ordinary people behave?
translation_verse: Kliso onupɛ anɔ teli aɖɔ asɛ, “Paulo ayɛ mɛinu,” pɛ anɔ bhlatɔ tsyɛ aɖɔ asɛ, “Apolo ayɛ mɛinuɔ pɔ,” kɛlɛ anɛ kanɔ mɛ banɔ ete nonuɔ dza?

english_verse: Apollos and I are merely servants who helped you to have faith. It was the Lord who made it all happen.
translation_verse: Alɛ petee niedeɛ pɔ, afana gɛenu Apolo? Kɛlɛ afana tsyɛ gɛenu Paulo? Baxɔ̃ɛdɛ ete gɛ balɛ akɔ asɛ nɔyɛkɛ ɔŋã asɛ kɛkpɛ gɛ Apaleteɛ akpɛ kɛxɔ̃ɛ nɛ balɛ ɔmayɛmɛ aka nyia aka ahɔɛ ana.

english_verse: I planted the seeds, Apollos watered them, but God made them sprout and grow.
translation_verse: Mɛɖa bɔdɔ̃eyiɛ, Apolo yɛakpɛ bulĩ nɛ bɔlɛ shu, pɔ Owusoso Akaa yɛakɔ bushè.

english_verse: What matters isn't those who planted or watered, but God who made the plants grow.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ gɛnɛ agɛ yɛaɖa bɔdɔ̃eyiɛ pɛnɔsɛ agɛ yɛakpɛ bulĩɛ nɛ bɔlɛ shuɔ yɛabhɛtɛ̀ kɛnanà, pɔ Owusoso Akaa yɛakɔ bɔdɔ̃eyiɛ eshè.

english_verse: The one who plants is just as important as the one who waters. And each one will be paid for what they do.
translation_verse: Tɛtɔtɔɖɔ tinikpo kɛlɛ ɛlɛ anunɔ gɛ yɛaɖaa bɔdɔ̃ nɛ̀ agɛ yɛakpɛ bulĩɛ nɛ bɔlɛ shuɔ ahɔɛ, kɛlɛ babà buia kɛbhɛ kɔ aka nyia aka nɛ yɛ kɛxɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa shu.

english_verse: Apollos and I work together for God, and you are God's garden and God's building. Only One Foundation
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ bɔlɔ nyɔ̃ɔ nu Owusoso Akaa kɛxɔ̃ɛ tinikpo awɔawɔa, kɛlɛ wɔnɔ onu Owusoso Akaa yɛɔyà, nɛ yɛ̀ buvũɔ.

english_verse: God treated me with undeserved grace and let me become an expert builder. I laid a foundation on which others have built. But we must each be careful how we build,
translation_verse: Nɛ kibhuiɛ gɛ Owusoso Akaa ebhui mɛebuɔ, mɛkpɛ buvũ niesi asɛ buvũdzudzu gbangbã elete ana, kɛlɛ anɔbhlatɔ yɛaba bhɔ bɔlɛ atɔɖɔ. Pɔ anɔ nyia anɔ eɖi piapii nɛ̀ kɛbhɔ gɛ abhɔɔ yɛuvũɔ.

english_verse: Whatever we build on this foundation will be tested by fire on the day of judgment. Then everyone will find out if we have used gold, silver, and precious stones, or wood, hay, and straw.
translation_verse: yɛxɔ̃ɛ bakasɛ kɛgɛ yɛalɔkɔ abhɔ bɔlɛ nɔ, ɔmɔsɛ Kiwuiɛ babu ɛlɛ kasɛ. Bebebù ɛlɛ akasɛ nɛ̀ kifú, kɛlɛ kifúɔ badalɛ aka nyia aka yɛ ɛxɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa shu.

english_verse: We will be rewarded if our building is left standing.
translation_verse: Onupɛ kɛgɛ yɛabhɔ eluɔ zã nɛ̀ ɛkpãa, aba yɛkɛ yɛbhɛ babuia.

english_verse: But if it is destroyed by the fire, we will lose everything. Yet we ourselves will be saved, like someone escaping from flames.
translation_verse: Onupɛ kɛtãa, yɛbhɛ tã, yɛxɔnɔ babà yɛkɛ yɛ elu nɛ̀ ɛkpã, pɔ kɛbazã asɛ anɔ gɛebu tɛtsɛɖɛmɛ ekoeyì odenɔ kifù mɛ ana.

english_verse: All of you surely know you are God's temple and his Spirit lives in you.
translation_verse: Nɔotinyi ɔsɛ Owusoso Akaavũɔ nɔonu pɛ Owusoso Akaa Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ kpasɛ wɔnɔ mɛ dza?

english_verse: Together you are God's holy temple, and God will destroy anyone who destroys his temple.
translation_verse: Onupɛ anɔ teli ayɛɛ Owusoso Akaavũɔ, Owusoso Akaa ebezĩ yɛ tsyɛ, ɔmɔsɛ Owusoso Akaavũɔ shu dalɛ, kɛlɛ wɔnɔ onu Owusoso Akaavũ lɛ.

english_verse: Don't fool yourselves! If any of you think you are wise in the things of this world, you will have to become foolish before you can be truly wise.
translation_verse: Nɔtɛ̀ wɔɛ wɔnɔshu. Onupɛ abhɛtɛ wɔnɔ mɛ anɔ teli asɛ enyi kibui nɛ obo kanɔ alɛ mɛ bubutì ulũɔ, etsyina edzì “awɔaga” sɛ tɛ̀ ebedzi ebutinɛ.

english_verse: This is because God considers the wisdom of this world to be foolish. It is just as the Scriptures say, “God catches the wise when they try to outsmart him.”
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ kanɔ mɛ bubuti ɛlɛ nu bɔga dɔdɔ nɛ Owusoso Akaa namɛ. Asɛ gɛ bafɔnɔ elú asɛ, “Eevũ bagɛ eɖi asɛ benyi kibuiɛ nɛ balɛ bɔshɛ̃ ebui bhɛbhɛtɛ mɛ”

english_verse: The Scriptures also say, “The Lord knows that the plans made by wise people are useless.”
translation_verse: kana bewlui aɖɔ asɛ, “Apaleteɛ enyi asɛ bagɛ eɖi asɛ benyi kibuiɛ bubutiɛ ma kɛnanà mɛ.”

english_verse: So stop bragging about what anyone has done. Paul and Apollos and Peter all belong to you. In fact, everything is yours, including the world, life, death, the present, and the future. Everything belongs to you,
translation_verse: onupɛ edzi asɛ Paulo, Apolo pɛnɔsɛ Kefa tsyɛɛ, kanɔ mɛ alo ɛkpã alo butsí alo obo kanɔ nɔ̀ pɛnɔsɛ kagɛ kabàa, bɔlɛ petee wɔnɔ ɔyɛ bunù,

english_verse: and you belong to Christ, and Christ belongs to God.
translation_verse: kɛlɛ Kristo ayɛ nonu, kɛlɛ Kristoɔ tsyɛ enu Owusoso Akaa ayɛ.

english_verse: Think of us as servants of Christ who have been given the work of explaining God's mysterious ways.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ, kɛlɛ sɛ aka nyia aka alɔkɔ bɔlɔ asɛ Kristo yɛanɔkpɛkpɛ ana, nɛ̀ asɛ bagɛ balɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa yɛebui kpũkpũɔ akpɛ ahɔɛ akɔ alɛ ana.

english_verse: And since our first duty is to be faithful to the one we work for,
translation_verse: Kliso bebisì edenɔ bagɛ balɛ ahɔɛ balɔkɔ, ebuiɛ akpɛ asɛ tɛ bamɔ alɛ sɛ babhɛtɛ kibui gɛlɛ kɛlɛ ɔzãkɔ.

english_verse: it doesn't matter to me if I am judged by you or even by a court of law. In fact, I don't judge myself.
translation_verse: Ɛgɛ lɔkɔ nɛ kɛlɛ mɛ gɛ onupɛ wɔnɔ, alo anɔdzyrame kebhu okulukɔ toli okulu kebhu nɛ̀ mɛ, kibui gbã itikulù kebhu nɛ mɛishu.

english_verse: I don't know of anything against me, but this doesn't prove I am right. The Lord is my judge.
translation_verse: Mɛ kitúɔ mɛ dalɛ, pɔ kɛgɛ kasɛ ɔsɛ anɔ gbangbã mɛinu. Apaleteɛ yɛebe kulu kebhu nɛ̀ mɛ.

english_verse: So don't judge anyone until the Lord returns. He will show what is hidden in the dark and what is in everyone's heart. Then God will be the one who praises each of us.
translation_verse: Kliso nɔoti kulu kebhu kpɛ otugbà flogo tɛ̀ kibeɛ ba na, nɔzã opo odiyinɔ ɔsɛ Apaleteɛ aba. Yɛaba ebu ebui kpukpũ nɔnɔ gɛ akpasɛ otumũɔ mɛ oyinɔ amana ɔtɔ̃, kɛlɛ ebebu ebuiɛ gɛ lakpasɛ anɔdzyrame yɛitùɔ mɛ akasɛ. Nɛ kibe ɛlɛ Owusoso Akaa aba kpɛ bagɛ kɛlɛ sɛ akpɛ nititíɛ.

english_verse: Friends, I have used Apollos and myself as examples to teach you the meaning of the saying, “Follow the rules.” I want you to stop saying one of us is better than the other.
translation_verse: Mɛdzya alɛ mɔ, mɛ nɛ̀ Apolo lɔkɔ ebui alɛ kɔ wɔnɔ nɛ wɔnɔ tɛpɛpɛ̃ shu, ɔsɛ tɛ nɔkpasɛ bɔdɔ̃ odenɔ bɔlɔkɔ tɛ nolũ ebui alɛ niesi ɔsɛ “Nɔtɛ bhɛtɛ ɔfalanɔ kɛgɛ bafɔnɔ eluɔ shu.” Sɛ tɛ notibo feke anana owunɔ kabha ɔdɛ yɛɔhagbo.

english_verse: What is so special about you? What do you have that you were not given? And if it was given to you, how can you brag?
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ afana gɛ abhɛtɛ wɔ pɛ ɔgã ɔtɔ nɛ banɔ bhlatɔ shu? Kiɖe gɛlɛ wɔ ahɔɛ pɛ gɛnɛ bɔyɛkɛ wɔ yɛkɛ ɛlɛɛ dza? Kɛlɛ onu pɛ ɔyɛkɛ ɛlɛɛ, kiɖe so pɛ ofeke ishu ɔmɔ asɛ gɛnɛ bɔkɔ bakɔ wɔɔ dza?

english_verse: Are you already satisfied? Are you now rich? Have you become kings while we are still nobodies? I wish you were kings. Then we could have a share in your kingdom.
translation_verse: Putɔnɔ kɛka nyia ka gɛ nɔpɛɛ nɔ lɛ wɔnɔ ahɔɛ xɔɔ! Kɛlɛ nɔotsyina odzi ehoeyielete! Nɔodzi bekusi, gɛ bɔlɔ ahɔɛ tsyɛ akpasɛ ɛlɛ mɛ. Ɛtapɛ ɔsɛ tɛ̀ nodzi bekusi kɛgbã tɛ bɔŋã bukusiɛ plɛnɔ wɔnɔ!

english_verse: It seems to me that God has put us apostles in the worst possible place. We are like prisoners on their way to death. Angels and the people of this world just laugh at us.
translation_verse: Kidzì kɔmɛ xɔnɔ asɛ Owusoso Akaa agbana bɔlɔ banɔkpɛkpɛ amana ɔmɔ asɛ banɔ gɔgɔɛ nɛ banɔdzyrame ishi ana, asɛ banɔ gɛ bebu alɛ ɛpɔ asɛ tɛ̀ beyi alɛ nɛ bɔdɔ̃ oɖikɔ ana. Babhɛtɛ bɔlɔ budzì bɔdɔ̃ gɛ kanɔ nɔ̀ mɛ nyɔ̃ɔ eɖi, nɛ Owusoso Akaa axɔ̃ɛdɛ nɛ̀ banɔdzyrameɛ amɛ.

english_verse: Because of Christ we are thought of as fools, but Christ has made you wise. We are weak and hated, but you are powerful and respected.
translation_verse: Budzi baaga nɛ Kristo ɛtakpu, pɔ bebutinɛ nɔonu nɛ Kristo mɛ! Bubotsò, pɔ wɔnɔ xɔnɔ ɔgagalĩ. Baakpɛ wɔnɔ kinyè nɛitití, pɔ bɔlɔ xɔnɔ batɛ̀ɛ ɔlɔ lu esi!

english_verse: Even today we go hungry and thirsty and don't have anything to wear except rags. We are mistreated and don't have a place to live.
translation_verse: Boyinɔ otsiɛ nɔ, bɔbhɛ nɛ olũwɔɛ oyi ɔlɔ xɔnɔ, bɔdɔ̃ tsatsã mɛ bɔkpasɛ, beehuisi ɔlɔ, kɛlɛ bɔpã tsyɛ mã ɔlɔ ahɔɛ.

english_verse: We work hard with our own hands, and when people abuse us, we wish them well. When we suffer, we are patient.
translation_verse: Bɔwɔa axɔ̃ɛ gagalĩ nɛ̀ bɔlɔ ahɔɛ. Onu baɖɔ kibui akpɛ bɔlɔɔ, bɔlɔ xɔnɔ yula tutu esi kɔ alɛ, onupɛ batɔnɔ bɔlɔ ɛkpã niedeɛ, bɔkpɛ kemi nɛ kɛlɛishu,

english_verse: When someone curses us, we answer with kind words. Until now we are thought of as nothing more than the trash and garbage of this world.
translation_verse: onupɛ banɔ aɖɔaɖɔ kpa nɛ ɔlɔ shuɔ, bubù ebui mɛ kɔ alɛ nɛ̀ eyamɛ gbangbã. Boyinɔ oboɔ, budzì kɛtɔ abha ɔdɔ̃ nɛ̀ kanɔ mɛ tɛwɔnɔ̃.

english_verse: I am not writing to embarrass you. I want to help you, just as parents help their own dear children.
translation_verse: Ɛtɛɛ́ fɔnɔ ebuiɛ alɛ kɔ ɔnɔ ɔsɛ tɛ ɛlɔkɔ kpɛ wɔnɔ awuĩ nɔ, pɔ tɛ̀ ɛlɔkɔ kpalà wɔnɔ, ɔmɔ asɛ mɛeyi alɛ gɛ ɛdɛmɛ nɔ ana.

english_verse: Ten thousand people may teach you about Christ, but I am your only father. You became my children when I told you about Christ Jesus,
translation_verse: Gɛ banɔ asɛ ihotu lɔfɔ lɛ wɔnɔ ahɔɛ nɛ Kristo mɛ tsyɛ elo, wɔnɔ akaa alɛ egikpè, ɔmɔsɛ nɛ Yesu Kristoɔ mɛ, ɛbadzì akaa ɔkɔ wɔnɔ ɔgana kibui gbangbã mɛ.

english_verse: and I want you to be like me.
translation_verse: Kliso ɛkpalà wɔnɔ ɔsɛ tɛ nɔkpasɛ mɛ.

english_verse: This is why I sent Timothy to you. I love him like a son, and he is a faithful servant of the Lord. Timothy will tell you what I do to follow Christ and how it agrees with what I always teach about Christ in every church.
translation_verse: Nɛ kibui ɛlɛ shuɔ ɛsɛsɛ Timoteo nɛ wɔnɔ ɔkɔ, mɛ eyianyɛ gɛ ɛdɛmɛ nɔ, anɔ gɛ abhɛtɛ kibui gɛlɛ ɛlɛ ɔzãkɔ nɛ Apaleteɛ mɛ. Yɛaba abaka wɔnɔ nɛ kɛzã gɛ ɛzã nɛkpã nɛ Kristo Yesu mɛ, gɛ kɛshɔnɔ kɛgɛ ɛkasɛ wɔnɔ nɛ ɔkɔ nyia ɔkɔ nɛ ɔhaa ka nyia ka mɛ.

english_verse: Some of you think I am not coming for a visit, and so you are bragging.
translation_verse: Wɔnɔ mɛ beteli ezefeke balɛishu, asɛ gɛ onupɛ ɛka bà wɔnɔ ɔbha oɖi ana.

english_verse: But if the Lord lets me come, I will soon be there. Then I will find out if the ones who are doing all this bragging really have any power.
translation_verse: Ɛbaba wɔnɔ ɔbha nɛ kibè gulũi mɛ gɛ onupɛ Apaleteɛ adɛmɛ, kɛlɛ ɛbatì kaɖɔ nɔnɔ ɛdi gɛ banɔ alɛ gɛ befeke balɛishuɔ aɖɔ, nɛ̀ ohumi nɔnɔ gɛ ɔlɛ balɛ ahɔɛ.

english_verse: God's kingdom isn't just a lot of words. It is power.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ Owusoso Akaa bukusi ŋaŋã ma kaɖɔ ɖɔɖɔ mɛ, pɔ ohumi mɛ bɔlɛ.

english_verse: What do you want me to do when I arrive? Do you want me to be hard on you or to be kind and gentle?
translation_verse: Kiɖuwoe nɔpɛ ɔdɛ? Ɛba wɔnɔ ɔkɔ nɛ̀ kishìi, tɛ ɛba ehuisi wɔnɔ, pɛnɔsɛ ɛba nɛ tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ nɛ̀ ɔfɔfuĩa gɛ ɔlɛ ishumɛ yɔɔ dza?

english_verse: I have heard terrible things about some of you. In fact, you are behaving worse than Gentiles. A man is even sleeping with his own stepmother.
translation_verse: Bakɔ bɔlɔ tititì nɛ wɔnɔ shu sɛ kɛgɔlɔ ŋaŋã ebui akpasɛ wɔnɔ mɛ, kibui gɛ ibudi elete tsyɛ atɛ abhɛtɛ. Anyɛ teli ayɛkɛ yɛakaa edzini.

english_verse: You are proud, when you ought to feel bad enough to chase away anyone who acts like this.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ nofeke wɔnɔ ishu! Gɛnɛ ɔwɔ̃nyɔnɔ boŋ ɔlɔtɔzã wɔnɔ mɛ pɛ notobu anyɛ gɛ abhɛtɛ kibui ɛlɛ oyinɔ nɛ wɔnɔ ɛshiɛ dza?

english_verse: I am with you only in my thoughts. But in the name of our Lord Jesus I have already judged this man, as though I were with you in person. So when you meet together and the power of the Lord Jesus is with you, I will be there too.
translation_verse: Onupɛ noshogò nɛ bɔlɔ Apalete Yesu kinyeɛ mɛ, pɛ ɛkpasɛ wɔnɔɛ ishi nɛ ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ mɛɛ, pɛ bɔlɔ apalete Yesu ohumi nɔ tsyɛ ɔlɛ wɔnɔɛ shiɛ,

english_verse: You must then hand that man over to Satan. His body will be destroyed, but his spirit will be saved when the Lord Jesus returns.
translation_verse: nɔlɔkɔ anyɛ alɛ ɔkpɛ ahɔɛ ɔkɔ Satana, sɛ tɛ̀ ezĩ yɛ ushudzya tɛ̀ bayɛkɛ yɛ ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ olu nɛkpã nɛ bɔlɔ Apalete Yesu kiwui nabha.

english_verse: Stop being proud! Don't you know how a little yeast can spread through the whole batch of dough?
translation_verse: Wɔnɔ ishuɔ gɛ nofekeɛ gɛ pɛ. Nɔoti nyi ɔsɛ amɔwɔ tsɔtsɔ̃mɔ bhlui teli yɛakɔ avɔvɔ gɛ ekpéɛ tsyɛ atsɔmɔ̃ɔ dza?

english_verse: Get rid of the old yeast! Then you will be like fresh bread made without yeast, and this is what you are. Our Passover lamb is Christ, who has already been sacrificed.
translation_verse: Nɔlɔkɔ amawɔ kuku tsɔtsɔ̃mɔ ɔtɛɛ sɛ tɛ nɔodzi amɔwɔ vɔvɔ gɛ kɛdɔ̃ tsɔtsɔmɔ̃ ma yɛmɛ, asɛ kidziɛ gɛ nodziɛ. Ɔmɔsɛ balɔkɔ Kristoɔ gɛenu Tɛdɔdɔ fala obhoshi eyiɛ abhɛtɛ bɔdɔ̃ pɔpɔɛ nɔ.

english_verse: So don't celebrate the festival by being evil and sinful, which is like serving bread made with yeast. Be pure and truthful and celebrate by using bread made without yeast.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ nɔkɔ bɔbhɛtɛ tɔɖɔ kiwui ŋaŋã nabha, gɛnɛ kɛdɔ̃ tsɔtsɔmɔ̃ kiyi kukuɔ, kiyiɛ gɛ manà ebui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ nɛ̀ kɛtakpu mɛ gagalĩ bhɛbhɛtɛ ebui, pɔ nɔzã asɛ kifufulũ gɛ bagɛ kpɛ kɛdɔ̃ tsɔtsɔmɔ̃ kiyi nɛ kɛlɛ mɛ, kifufulũ gɛ bagɛ kpɛ kɛdɔ̃ tsɔtsɔmɔ̃ nɛ yɛ mɛ pɛ kinu kibui gɛ kɛlɛ ɛlɛ ɔzãkɔ tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ.

english_verse: In my other letter I told you not to have anything to do with immoral people.
translation_verse: Ɛfɔnɔ ɔsɛsɛ wɔnɔ nɛ mieplukpa mɛ ɔsɛ, nɔtɛ kpɛ ɔha nɛ banɔ nɔ̀ gɛ baŋã agɔlɔ,

english_verse: But I wasn't talking about the people of this world. You would have to leave this world to get away from everyone who is immoral or greedy or who cheats or worships idols.
translation_verse: gɛnɛ asɛ kanɔ alɛ mɛ anunɔ gɛ balɔkɔ ishu akɔ kɛgɔlɔ ŋaŋã, mɛ ete tɛpɛpɛ elete, bagɛ edzyinì banɔ akpɛ alo ibudi elete ana. Nɛ kibui ɛlɛ nɛidi shuɔ, kɛlɛ sɛ tɛ nɔshɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ kanɔ alɛ shu.

english_verse: I was talking about your own people who are immoral or greedy or worship idols or curse others or get drunk or cheat. Don't even eat with them!
translation_verse: Pɔ, otsiɛnɔ ɛfɔnɔ kɔ wɔnɔ sɛ, kɛma sɛ tɛ nɔkpɛ ɔha nɛ anunɔ gɛ ekulu yɛishu sɛ adzya yɛenu flogo pɛ aka aŋã kɛgɔlɔ apɛ yɛ ete tɛpɛpɛ, anɔ gɛ eshu ibudi ɔkɔ̀ alo anɔ gɛ ezĩ banɔ shu, enuwɔ̃ɛ alo anɔ gɛ edzyinì banɔ akpɛ. Nɔtɛŋã bɔdɔ̃ ete tsyɛ nɛ banɔ alɛ nɛidi.

english_verse: Why should I judge outsiders? Aren't we supposed to judge only church members?
translation_verse: Kitinu mɛ kɛxɔ̃ɛ sɛ tɛ̀ ikulu kebhu nɛ bagɛ bama ɔha mɛɛ. Gɛnɛ bagɛ alɛ ɔhaa mɛɛ kɛlɛ sɛ tɛ ikulu kebhuɔ nɔ dza?

english_verse: God judges everyone else. The Scriptures say, “Chase away any of your own people who are evil.”
translation_verse: Owusoso Akaa yɛebe kulu kebhu nɛ bagɛ ama ɔha mɛ. “Nɔkakà atakpu mɛ gagalĩ eleteɛ nɔ wɔnɔ ɛshi.”

english_verse: When one of you has a complaint against another, do you take your complaint to a court of sinners? Or do you take it to God's people?
translation_verse: Onupɛ kibui ku wɔnɔ mɛ banɔ teli ishiɛ, atɛ lɔkɔ yɛavɛnɔ bagɛ etinyi Owusoso Akaa balɛ kebhu okulukɔ, pɔ alɔkɔ yɛavɛnɔ bayɛyɛkɛŋã ɔkɔ.

english_verse: Don't you know that God's people will judge the world? And if you are going to judge the world, can't you settle small problems?
translation_verse: Nɔotinyi ɔsɛ bagɛ enu bayɛyɛkɛŋã balɛ ebekulu kebhu nɛ kanɔ mɛ banɔ nɔɔ dza? Kɛlɛ onupɛ wɔnɔ obokulu kebhu nɛ̀ kanɔ nɔ̀ mɛ, nɔgɛ ná sɛ tɛ nokulu bubhu bhlui bhlui lɛ nɛidiɛ dza?

english_verse: Don't you know we will judge angels? And if this is so, we can surely judge everyday matters.
translation_verse: Nɔotinyi ɔsɛ bɔlɔ ba kulu kebhu nɛ̀ Akaa axɔ̃ɛdɛ dza? Kɛlɛ so pɛ kanɔ mɛ ebui alɛ bɔtalà okulu kebhu nɔ dza?

english_verse: Why do you take everyday complaints to judges who are not respected by the church?
translation_verse: Klisoɔ, onupɛ ebela aba nɛ ebui alɛ nɛidi shuɔ, nɔlɔkɔ banɔ gɛ ɔzãkɔ ma balɛ ahɔɛ nɛ ɔhaa mɛ asɛ bebhukulu ana!

english_verse: I say this to your shame. Aren't any of you wise enough to act as a judge between one follower and another?
translation_verse: Ɛɖɔ ebui alɛ sɛ tɛ ɛlɔkɔ kpɛ wɔnɔ awuĩ nɔ. Kɛpɛ otsiɛ sɛ ebutinɛ nanà atɛzã wɔnɔ ɛshi gɛ abalà kebhu ekulu nɛ bayɛyɛkɛŋã nɛishi dza?

english_verse: Why should one of you take another to be tried by unbelievers?
translation_verse: Pɔ, nɔlɔkɔ bagɔlɔ ɔvɛnɔ kebhu mɛ, kɛlɛ nɔbhɛtɛ alɛ nɛ bagɛ agɛ yɛkɛ aŋã amɛ!

english_verse: When one of you takes another to court, all of you lose. It would be better to let yourselves be cheated and robbed.
translation_verse: Gɛ kɛgbã sɛ̀ nɔlɔkɔ bagɔlɔ ɔvɛnɔ kebhu soɔ, kɛtɛ kasɛ ɔsɛ baŋã wɔnɔ abha xɔɔ. Kiɖe so pɛ nɔtɔkɔ yɛabhɛtɛ kibui kpa nɛ wɔnɔ boŋ shu? Kiɖe so pɛ nɔtɔkɔ balɛ boŋ ete dzyini wɔnɔ akpɛɛ dza?

english_verse: Don't you know that evil people won't have a share in the blessings of God's kingdom? Don't fool yourselves! No one who is immoral or worships idols or is unfaithful in marriage or is a pervert or behaves like a homosexual
translation_verse: Nɔotinyi ɔsɛ atakpu mɛ gagalĩ eleteɛ atɛba vɛ Owusoso Akaa bukusi ɔŋãkɔɔ dza? Nɔtɛkɔ bawɔɛ wɔnɔ, bagɛ baŋã kɛgɔlɔ tsyɛ, ibudi eleteɛ, bagɛ badɛ ɔmamɛ, banyɛ gɛ bawɔsɔ̃ nɛ̀ balɛ banyɛ ɔhagbo alɛ,

english_verse: will share in God's kingdom. Neither will any thief or greedy person or drunkard or anyone who curses and cheats others.
translation_verse: nɛ bezĩɛ nɛ̀ bagɛ basɛ balɛ ete ko abaŋã nɛ beenuwɔɛ nɛ̀ bagɛ aɖɔ banɔ ishu tibuiɛ nɛ̀ bagɛ edzyinì banɔ akpɛɛ batɛ bavɛ Owusoso Akaa bukusi ɔŋãkɔ.

english_verse: Some of you used to be like that. But now the name of our Lord Jesus Christ and the power of God's Spirit have washed you and made you holy and acceptable to God. Honor God with Your Body
translation_verse: Ebui alɛ wɔnɔ mɛ banɔ teli aza abhɛtɛ. Pɔ beplu wɔnɔ ishu, badalɛ wɔnɔ shu, kɛlɛ bebhui bɔtyɔa akɔ wɔnɔ nɛ Apalete Yesu Kristoɔ nɛ Owusoso Akaa Ɔfɔfuĩa nɛinyeɛ mɛ. Kɛgɔlɔ Tɛŋaŋã

english_verse: Some of you say, “We can do anything we want to.” But I tell you not everything is good for us. So I refuse to let anything have power over me.
translation_verse: “Bakɔ mɛ kidzo sɛ tɛ̀ ɛbhɛtɛ kɛka nyia ka,” pɔ gɛnɛ kɛka nyia ka kɛlɛ pɛ ɔkɔ anɔ. Bakɔ mɛ kidzo sɛ tɛ̀ ɛbhɛtɛ kɛka nyia ka,” pɔ ɛtɛbakɔ sɛ tɛ kɛka nyia ka dzi apalete nɛ mɛabha.

english_verse: You also say, “Food is meant for our bodies, and our bodies are meant for food.” But I tell you that God will destroy them both. We are not supposed to do indecent things with our bodies. We are to use them for the Lord who is in charge of our bodies.
translation_verse: “Bɔŋaŋã lɛ kɔ kevũɔ, kɛlɛ kevũɔ tsyɛ alɛ kɔ́ bɔŋaŋã,” pɔ Owusoso Akaa ebezĩ balɛ taabha. Bagɛ bhɛtɛ ishudzyaa kɔ́ kɛgɔlɔ ŋaŋã ebui, pɔ Apaleteɛ babhɛtɛ ɛlɛ akɔ, kɛlɛ babhɛtɛ Apaleteɛ tsyɛ akɔ ishudzya.

english_verse: God will raise us from death by the same power he used when he raised our Lord to life.
translation_verse: Nɛ yɛ Ohumi nɔ mɛɛ, Owusoso Akaa efeke Apaleteɛ denɔ bekpuɔ mɛ, kana yɛebe feke bɔlɔ tsyɛ.

english_verse: Don't you know that your bodies are part of the body of Christ? Is it right for me to join part of the body of Christ to a prostitute? No, it isn't!
translation_verse: Nɔotinyi ɔsɛ wɔnɔ shudzyaa nu Kristoɔ ɔyɛ dza? Pɔ kɛpɛ̃ sɛ ɛlɔkɔ mɛ ishudzyaa gɛinu Kristoɔ ɔyɛ bhɛtɛ tenikpo nɛ kedulũ azazã eleteɛ dza? Kɛma ɛlɛ mɛ ana!

english_verse: Don't you know that a man who does that becomes part of her body? The Scriptures say, “The two of them will be like one person.”
translation_verse: Nɔotinyi ɔsɛ anyɛ nɔnɔ gɛ abhɛtɛ tenikpo nɛ adzɛ gɛ azã kedulũɔ, abhɛtɛ tenikpo nɛ yɛ nɛ ishudzyaa mɛɛ dza? Ɔmɔsɛ baɖɔ ɛlɛ lú asɛ, “Balɛ taabha ebedzi ishudzya tenikpo.”

english_verse: But anyone who is joined to the Lord is one in spirit with him.
translation_verse: Pɔ anunɔ gɛ abhɛtɛ tenikpo nɛ Apaleteɛ, alɛ tenikpo nɛ yɛ nɛ ɔfɔfuĩa mɛ.

english_verse: Don't be immoral in matters of sex. That is a sin against your own body in a way no other sin is.
translation_verse: Nɔobu tɛtsɛɖɛ mɛ ɔshɛ nɛ kɛgɔlɔ ŋaŋã ebui shu. Ebui kpa ka nyia ka gɛ anɔ abhɛtɛ, abhɛtɛ alɛ nɛ ishupi niede, pɔ anunɔ gɛ abhɛtɛ kibui kpa odenɔ kɛgɔlɔ ŋaŋã mɛɛ xɔnɔɔ, abhɛtɛ kibui kpa nɛ yɛahɔɛ ishudzya shu.

english_verse: You surely know that your body is a temple where the Holy Spirit lives. The Spirit is in you and is a gift from God. You are no longer your own.
translation_verse: Nɔotinyi ɔsɛ wɔnɔ ishupiɛ nu Owusoso Akaavũɔ ɔkɔ Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã, gɛ lɔkpasɛ wɔnɔ mɛ, anunɔ gɛ nɔyɛkɛ odenɔ Owusoso Akaa ɔkɔɔ dza? Nɔma ɔkɔ wɔnɔ ishu,

english_verse: God paid a great price for you. So use your body to honor God.
translation_verse: budzi bedzi wɔnɔ nɛ kedzi tieli. Klisoɔ, nɔkpɛ Owusoso Akaa nɛinye nɛ kititi nɛ wɔnɔ ishupiɛ.

english_verse: Now I will answer the questions you asked in your letter. You asked, “Is it best for people not to marry?”
translation_verse: Otsiɛ nɛ ebuiɛ gɛ alɛ shu nɔfɔ̃nɔ bɔdɔ̃ɔ, apɛ akɔ anyɛ sɛ tɛ̀ atɛ gbana adzɛ.

english_verse: Well, having your own husband or wife should keep you from doing something immoral.
translation_verse: Pɔ gɛ kɛgɔlɔ ŋaŋã ebuiɛ bekpe soɔ, adzɛ azã anyɛ ka nyia ka ahɔɛ, kɛlɛ anyɛ tsyɛ azã adzɛ ka nyia ka ahɔɛ.

english_verse: Husbands and wives should be fair with each other about having sex.
translation_verse: Adzɛ enyineɛ abhɛtɛ tɛgbagbana ɛbuiɛ ɔkɔ yɛedziniɛ, adzɛ tsyɛ abhɛtɛ ana akɔ yɛ enyineɛ.

english_verse: A wife belongs to her husband instead of to herself, and a husband belongs to his wife instead of to himself.
translation_verse: Adzɛ yɛishupiɛ tinu yɛ tenikpo ɔyɛ, pɔ bɔlɛ ɔkɔ yɛenyineɛ tsyɛ. Nɛ kidzo ɛlɛ nabhaa, adzɛ enyineɛ ishupiɛ tinu yɛ tenikpo ɔyɛ, pɔ bɔlɛ ɔkɔ yɛedziniɛ tsyɛ.

english_verse: So don't refuse sex to each other, unless you agree not to have sex for a little while, in order to spend time in prayer. Then Satan won't be able to tempt you because of your lack of self-control.
translation_verse: Nɔtɛ lɔkɔ wɔnɔ ishu kanà bagɔlɔ, ɔdɛ sɛ nɔ tɔɖɔ kɛlɛ elú ana ɔkɔ kibè tili, sɛ tɛ nɔlɔkɔ wɔnɔshu ɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa tikukulu. Kɛlɛ nɔ bhɛtɛ tenikpo sɛ tɛ Satana atɛ ba efeke wɔnɔ eɖi sɛ gɛ nɔtɛɔ là wɔnɔ shu abha ŋã so.

english_verse: In my opinion that is what should be done, though I don't know of anything the Lord said about this matter.
translation_verse: Ɛɖɔ ebui alɛ sɛ tɛ ɛkɔ wɔnɔ kidzo nɛ alɛ shu, gɛnɛ ɔsha mɛ ɛkpɛ ɔkɔ wɔnɔ.

english_verse: My advice for the unmarried and for widows is that it is acceptable for them to remain single, just as I am.
translation_verse: Otsiɛ nɔ itseɖe bagbalugba nɛ̀ bavɛvɛ edziniɛ asɛ, kɛpɛ kɔ alɛ ɔsɛ tɛ bazã nɛ̀ kɛgbà, asɛ mɛ ana.

english_verse: But if you don't have enough self-control, then go ahead and get married. After all, it is better to marry than to burn with desire.
translation_verse: Pɔ onupɛ batɛ balà balɛ ishu abhaa aŋãa, kɛlɛ sɛ bazana anyɛ, ɔmɔsɛ apɛ̃ asɛ tɛ̀ ɔzanà anyɛ ɔdɛ ɔsɛ tɛ̀ wɔshumɛ zɛlɔ nɛ anyɛ shu.

english_verse: I instruct married couples to stay together, and this is exactly what the Lord himself taught. A wife who leaves her husband
translation_verse: Nɛ bagɛ agbana adzɛ shuɔ mɛ ɛkɔ ɔsha lɛ asɛ, Adzɛ agɛ mɔ kidzo sɛ tɛ ashɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ yɛenyine shù.

english_verse: should either stay single or go back to her husband. And a husband should not leave his wife.
translation_verse: Pɔ onupɛ abhɛtɛ ana, azã nɛ̀ kɛgbà alo tɛ etsodzi abhɛtɛ tenikpo nɛ yɛenyineɛ. Anyɛ tsyɛ atɛ shɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ yɛedzini shù.

english_verse: I don't know of anything else the Lord said about marriage. All I can do is to give you my own advice. If your wife isn't a follower of the Lord, but is willing to stay with you, don't divorce her.
translation_verse: Wɔnɔ bagɔgɔ mɛitseɖe kibui ɛlɛ ɔsɛ, Onupɛ ɔhamɛ anɔ teli agbana adzɛ gɛetinu anɔ gɛ ayɛkɛ aŋã pɛ adɛmɛ sɛ abazã nɛ yɛɛ, atɛ shɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛishu.

english_verse: If your husband isn't a follower, but is willing to stay with you, don't divorce him.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ onupɛ adzɛ edzini anyɛ gɛetinu anɔ gɛ ayɛkɛ aŋã pɛ adɛmɛ asɛ abazã nɛ yɛ, atɛ shɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛishu.

english_verse: Your husband or wife who isn't a follower is made holy by having you as a partner. This also makes your children holy and keeps them from being unclean in God's sight.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ yɛenyineɛ gɛ agɛ ayɛkɛ ŋãa, atɛmɔ ishu dadalɛ ɔgana yɛedzinì nabha, kana adzɛ tsyɛ gɛ agɛ yɛkɛ ŋã atɛmɔ ishu dadalɛ ɔgana yɛenyineɛ gɛ ayɛkɛ aŋã nabha. Onu gɛnɛ ana wɔnɔ eyi alɛ shu tɛba dalɛ, pɔ asɛ gɛ kɛlɛ ana, balɛ shu dalɛ.

english_verse: If your husband or wife isn't a follower of the Lord and decides to divorce you, then you should agree to it. You are no longer bound to that person. After all, God chose you and wants you to live at peace.
translation_verse: Pɔ onupɛ anunɔ gɛ agɛ yɛkɛ aŋã pɛ asɛ ashɛɛ, nɔkɔ yɛagavɛ. Ɔsha gɛ nyã anyɛ nɛ adzɛ nɔnɔ gɛ ayɛkɛ aŋã nɛ ebui alɛ nɛidi mɛ, Owusoso Akaa ekulu lɔ́ sɛ tɛ bɔzã nɛishu yɔyɔ mɛ.

english_verse: And besides, how do you know if you will be able to save your husband or wife who isn't a follower? Obeying the Lord at All Times
translation_verse: Anyɛ edzini, otinyi, ɔsɛ wɔ ɔbɔlà obu wɔenyineɛ dza? Kidzo ɛlɛ nabha wɔ adzɛ enyine tsyɛ otinyi ɔsɛ ɔbɔlà yɛkɛ woedziniɛ elu nɛ ɛkpãa dza?

english_verse: In every church I tell the people to stay as they were when the Lord Jesus chose them and God called them to be his own. Now I say the same thing to you.
translation_verse: Kɛkpasɛ kɛlɛ mɛ pɔ, anɔ ka nyia ka anyana ɔzãkɔ gɛ Apaleteɛ akpɛ ahɔɛ akɔɛ nɛ̀ kɛgɛ Owusoso Akaa ekulu yɛakɔ evunɔ. Ɔsha lɛ nabha mɛidzu ɔhaa petee tɔɖɔ.

english_verse: If you are already circumcised, don't try to change it. If you are not circumcised, don't get circumcised.
translation_verse: Bebhui kɛhɔ̃a ɔkɔ anyɛ teli flogo pɛ bekulú yɛ dza? Eti dzí anɔ gɛ begi bhui kɛhɔ̃a akɔ. Anyɛ teli alɛ gɛ begi bhui kɛhɔ̃a akɔ yɛ flogo pɛ etsyina dza? Beteke bhui kɛhɔ̃a akɔɛ.

english_verse: Being circumcised or uncircumcised isn't really what matters. The important thing is to obey God's commands.
translation_verse: Kɛhɔ̃a bhui bhui tinu kɛdɔ̃ nanà, kɛhɔ̃a gɛ begi bhui tsyɛ itinu kɛdɔ̃ nanà. Owusoso Akaa ɔshaa nabha tɛbhɛ bhɛtɛ kɛlɛinu kɛgɛ pɛ ɔdɛ.

english_verse: So don't try to change what you were when God chose you.
translation_verse: Anɔ ka nyia ka azã kɛzãa gɛ azã flogo pɛ Owusoso Akaa ebekulú yɛ.

english_verse: Are you a slave? Don't let that bother you. But if you can win your freedom, you should.
translation_verse: Anɔ dzidzi wɔonu flogo pɛ bekulú wɔ̀ dza? Tɛkɔ kɛkpɛ wɔ buluhũ, onupɛ edzi asɛ ɔbɔlá yɛkɛ wɔ ishu ahɔɛ mɛ zazãa, bhɛtɛ ana.

english_verse: When the Lord chooses slaves, they become his free people. And when he chooses free people, they become slaves of Christ.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ anɔ gɛedzi anɔ dzidzì flogo pɛ Apaleteɛ ebekulú yɛ, enu Apaleteɛ yɛanɔ ishu ahɔɛmɛ azazã, kidzo ɛlɛ nabha anunɔ gɛ azã yɛishu ahɔɛmɛ flogo pɛ bekulu yɛ, edzì Kristo yɛanɔ dzidzi.

english_verse: God paid a great price for you. So don't become slaves of anyone else.
translation_verse: Bedzi wɔnɔ nɛ̀ kedzì tieli, nɔoti tsyina odzi banɔ dzidzi ɔkɔ banɔdzyrame.

english_verse: Stay what you were when God chose you. Unmarried People
translation_verse: Mɛ adzya alɛ, anɔka nyia ka, gɛ akpasɛ Owusoso Akaa mɛɛ, azã kɛgɛ kɛlɛ mɛ Owusoso Aka ekulu yɛakpɛ.

english_verse: I don't know of anything the Lord said about people who have never been married. But I will tell you what I think. And you can trust me, because the Lord has treated me with kindness.
translation_verse: Otsiɛ nɛ bubiteɛ gɛ betinyi anyɛ shuɔ, ɛgɛ yɛkɛ ɔsha nanà odenɔ Apaleteɛ ɔkɔ, pɔ ikulu kebhu asɛ anɔ gɛ denɔ Apaleteɛ ashagabui ɖiɖi mɛɛ bayɛkɛ yɛabha aŋã.

english_verse: We are now going through hard times, and I think it is best for you to stay as you are.
translation_verse: Nɛ otsiɛ bɔtɔ lɛ shu soɔ, kɛba pɛ ɔkɔ wɔnɔ sɛ nɔzã asɛ gɛ nɔ ɔlɛ ana.

english_verse: If you are married, stay married. If you are not married, don't try to get married.
translation_verse: Ɔgbana adzɛ dza? Nɔtɛ shɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ bagɔlɔ shu. Odzi nɛ kɛgbàa dza? Tɛ pɛɛ adzɛ sɛ tɛ̀ ɔgbanà.

english_verse: It isn't wrong to marry, even if you have never been married before. But those who marry will have a lot of trouble, and I want to protect you from this.
translation_verse: Pɔ edzi asɛ onupɛ ɔtɛ gbana adzɛɛ, ɔgɛ bhɛtɛ kibui kpa, kɛlɛ onupɛ kebiteɛ gɛ etinyi anyɛ pɛ azana anyɛɛ, agɛ bhɛtɛ kibui kpa. Pɔ ba agɛ bagbana bagɔlɔ babamɔ bɔtɔ nɛ ɛkpã ɛlɛ mɛ, kɛlɛ ɛtɛpɛɛ sɛ ebui alɛ aba wɔnɔ abha.

english_verse: My friends, what I mean is that the Lord will soon come, and it won't matter if you are married or not.
translation_verse: Mɛ adzyaa alɛ, kɛgɛ mɛɖɔ kɛlɛinu ɔsɛ, kibeɛ dzi gulũi. Denɔ otsiɛ ɔvɛnɔ, bagɛ bagbana adzɛ bazã asɛ gɛ adzɛ ama balɛ ahɔɛ ana,

english_verse: It will be all the same if you are crying or laughing, or if you are buying or are completely broke.
translation_verse: bagɛ ebieyio, asɛ betie bi, bagɛ amɔ sameɛ, asɛ batiamɔ same, bagɛ edzi kɛdɔ̃ tiliɛ, asɛ kitinu balɛ kɛdɔ̃ babalɛ́,

english_verse: It won't make any difference how much good you are getting from this world or how much you like it. This world as we know it is now passing away.
translation_verse: bagɛ balawɔa kanɔ alɛ mɛ bɔdɔ̃ shu axɔ̃ɛ, asɛ gɛ onupɛ butinu kɛdɔ̃ nanà akɔ alɛ. Ɔmɔsɛ kanɔ alɛ mɛ asɛ gɛ kalɛ otsiɛ nɔ ulũ fala.

english_verse: I want all of you to be free from worry. An unmarried man worries about how to please the Lord.
translation_verse: Ɛpɛ sɛ tɛ wɔnɔ etúɔ mɛ adɔ fɔ̃ɔ nɛ kɛka nyia ka shu. Anyɛ nɔnɔ gɛ agɛ gbana adzɛɛ alɔkɔ̀ tili nɛ Apaleteɛ ebui mɛ, nɛ kɛdɛmɛ gɛ abadɛmɛ nɔ nɛ̀ Apaleteɛ.

english_verse: But a married man has more worries. He must worry about the things of this world, because he wants to please his wife.
translation_verse: Pɔ anyɛ gɛ agbana adzɛ xɔnɔ yɛebui tɔtɔɖɔ bazã kanɔ nɔ̀ mɛ ebuiɛ shu, nɛ kana gɛ ebekù yɛedzini awɔ̃

english_verse: So he is pulled in two directions. Unmarried women and women who have never been married worry only about pleasing the Lord, and they keep their bodies and minds pure. But a married woman worries about the things of this world, because she wants to please her husband.
translation_verse: kɛgɛ kɔ yɛamɔ sameɛ bagã ɔtɔ. Kebite nɔnɔ gɛ aka zana anyɛ, alɔkɔ tili nɛ Apaleteɛ ebui mɛ, Yɛ tɛtɔtɔɖɔ kɛlɛinu ɔsɛ tɛ̀ alɔkɔ yɛishu ashɔ akpɛ Apaleteɛ mɛ nɛ ishupi nɛ̀ ɔfɔfuĩa mɛ. Pɔ anyɛedzini xɔnɔ yɛebuitɔtɔɖɔ bazã kanɔ alɛ mɛ ebuiɛ shu-kana gɛ ebekù yɛinyine awɔ.

english_verse: What I am saying is for your own good—it isn't to limit your freedom. I want to help you to live right and to love the Lord above all else.
translation_verse: Ɛɖɔ ebui alɛ akɔ wɔnɔ tɛpɛpɛ̃, anɛ sɛ tɛ̀ itsodzi wɔnɔ, pɔ sɛ tɛ̀ nɔzã nɛ kidzo gbangbãa gɛ kɛgɛ gã ɔtɔ nɛ Apaleteɛ mɛ zazã shu.

english_verse: But suppose you are engaged to someone old enough to be married, and you want her so much that all you can think about is getting married. Then go ahead and marry. There is nothing wrong with that.
translation_verse: Onupɛ abhɛtɛ anyɛ teli ɔsɛ atɛ alà agã nɛ kebiteɛ gɛ akpɛ bulũwɔa akɔ asɛ tɛ̀ agbanà shuɔ, pɛ adzɛ agɔ pɔ eekpè atɔɖɔ gɛ apɛasɛ tɛ agbanà adzɛɛ, abhɛtɛ asɛ kana gɛ apɛ nɔ. Agɛ abhɛtɛ kibui kpá. Bagbana bagɔlɔ.

english_verse: It is perfectly all right to marry, but it is better not to get married at all.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ, anunɔ gɛ yɛagbana kebiteɛ abhɛtɛ piapii, pɔ anunɔ gɛ agɛ gbana kebiteɛ abhɛtɛ kɛgɛ pɛ̃ ɔdɛ.

english_verse: A wife should stay married to her husband until he dies. Then she is free to marry again, but only to a man who is a follower of the Lord.
translation_verse: Ɔsha ɔnyã adzɛ nɛ yɛenyine shu nɛ kibeɛ gɛ anyɛ alɛ nɛ ɛkpã. Pɔ onupɛ yɛenyine etsiɛ xɔnɔ, ɔsha ɔkɔ kidzo sɛ tɛ̀ azãna anyɛ nɔnɔ gɛ yɛibui zã yɛuliɛ, pɔ kɛlɛ sɛ anyɛ azã Apaleteɛ mɛ.

english_verse: However, I think I am obeying God's Spirit when I say she would be happier to stay single.
translation_verse: Nɛ mɛ bɔdɔ̃ mɔmɔ ulũɔ, same ebekù yɛ ɔdɛ gɛ onupɛ awɔ kɛgba, kɛlɛ ɛmɔ ɔsɛ Owusoso Akaa Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ ɔlɛ otsiɖi mɛ tsyɛ abha.

english_verse: In your letter you asked me about food offered to idols. All of us know something about this subject. But knowledge makes us proud of ourselves, while love makes us helpful to others.
translation_verse: Otsiɛ nɛ bɔŋaŋãa gɛ batɔ akɔ ibudiɛ shuɔ, bunyi ɔsɛ kɛlɛ tititì mɛ ɔkɔ ɔlɔ petee. Bubutì kɔ befeke ishu, pɔ tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ xɔnɔ bhɔ banɔ ewunɔ kabha.

english_verse: In fact, people who think they know so much don't know anything at all.
translation_verse: Anunɔ gɛ abhɛtɛ yɛ asɛ enyi kibui tiliɛ, eketì kɛlɛ asɛ kana gɛ kɛlɛ sɛ etiɛ.

english_verse: But God has no doubts about who loves him.
translation_verse: Pɔ Owusoso Akaa enyi anunɔ gɛ adɛmɛ nɔ nɛ yɛ.

english_verse: Even though food is offered to idols, we know that none of the idols in this world are alive. After all, there is only one God.
translation_verse: Kliso nɛ bɔŋaŋã gɛ batɔ akɔ ibudi shuɔ, bunyi ɔsɛ obudì otinu kɛdɔ̃ nanà nɛ kanɔ nɔ̀ mɛ, kɛlɛ Owusoso Akaa nanà abha ma ɔdɛ tenikpo alɛ ete.

english_verse: Many things in heaven and on earth are called gods and lords, but none of them really are gods or lords.
translation_verse: Onupɛ edzì asɛ-baaka teli alɛ gɛ bekulu balɛ nɛ kititiɛ alo nɛ kesiakpa nabha ,

english_verse: We have only one God, and he is the Father. He created everything, and we live for him. Jesus Christ is our only Lord. Everything was made by him, and by him life was given to us.
translation_verse: pɔ nɛ bɔlɔkɔ xɔnɔ, Owusoso Akaa tenikpo yɛalɛ, Akaa, gɛ denɔ yɛabha kɛka nyia ka de koeyì, pɛ yɛ bɔlɛ nɛ ɛkpã ɔkɔɔ, pɛ kana Apalete tenikpo tsyɛ yɛalɛ gɛenu Yesu Kristo, gɛ gana yɛabha kɛka nyia ka de koeyì pɛ ganà yɛabha bɔlɛ nɛ ɛkpã.

english_verse: Not everyone knows these things. In fact, many people have grown up with the belief that idols have life in them. So when they eat meat offered to idols, they are bothered by a weak conscience.
translation_verse: Pɔ gɛnɛ aka nyia aka yɛenyi kibui ɛlɛ ana. Banɔ teli gɛ ibudi ebui akakpasɛ balɛ mɛ, gɛ onupɛ baŋã bɔdɔ̃ lɛ nɛidi gɛ batɔ akɔ obudiɛ, pɔ gɛ ebuiɛ gɛ akpasɛ balɛ etúɔ mɛ ebotso soɔ, bakpɛ bɔŋaŋãa nɔtsɔ̃.

english_verse: But food doesn't bring us any closer to God. We are no worse off if we don't eat, and we are no better off if we do.
translation_verse: Pɔ bɔŋaŋã tɛ gbana bɔlɔ mana Owusoso Akaa bha, bɔgɛ ŋã bɔdɔ̃ɔ, kigizĩ kɛnana nɛ bɔlɔ shu, kana onupɛ bɔŋã tsyɛɛ, bɔgɛ pɛ dɛ ananà.

english_verse: Don't cause problems for someone with a weak conscience, just because you have the right to eat anything.
translation_verse: Nɔoɖi piapii, sɛ tɛ̀ wɔnɔ ishu ahɔɛ mɛ zazã gɛ nɔmɔɔ tidzì kɛdɔ̃ kpakpatà ɔkɔ bagɛ bakakpɛ iguɔ.

english_verse: You know all this, and so it doesn't bother you to eat in the temple of an idol. But suppose a person with a weak conscience sees you and decides to eat food that has been offered to idols.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ onupɛ anɔ teli gɛ akakpɛ igu nɛ yɛitúɔ mɛ abamɔ wɔanɔ gɛ bubuti lɛ wɔahɔɛ pɛ ɔŋã bɔdɔ̃ nɛ ibudi bɔkã mɛɛ, kɛtɛ bakɔ sɛ yɛ tsyɛ avɛ adɛ ŋã bɔdɔ̃ lɛ gɛ batɔ akɔ ibudi nɛ ishu fefekeɛ mɛɛ dza?

english_verse: Then what you know has destroyed someone Christ died for.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ ozĩ adzya alɛ gɛ ebotsoɔ yɛkpã nɛ̀ wɔbutiɛ, gɛ yɛtakpu sopɛ Kristoɔ ebetsíɛ.

english_verse: When you sin by hurting a follower with a weak conscience, you sin against Christ.
translation_verse: Onupɛ ɔbhɛtɛ kibui kpa nɛ wɔadzya alɛ shu nɛ kidzo ɛlɛ nabhaa, pɛ ɔkpɛ kɛpɔ nɛ balɛ kitúɔ gɛ kibotsoɔ shuɔ, ɔbhɛtɛ kibui kpa nɛ Kristo shu.

english_verse: So if I hurt one of the Lord's followers by what I eat, I will never eat meat as long as I live.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ, onupɛ kɛgɛ ɛŋãa kɔ sɛ tɛ̀ mɛadzya ezhi eku kibui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ mɛɛ, ɛzakatã kɛdzya ɖi, sɛ tɛ̀ ɛtɛ bakɔ yɛadɔ oboe.

english_verse: I am free. I am an apostle. I have seen the Lord Jesus and have led you to have faith in him.
translation_verse: Itinu anɔ gɛ alɛ mɛishu ahɔɛ mɛɛ dza? Itinu anɔkpɛkpɛ dza? Ɛgɛ mɔ Yesu gɛenu bɔlɔ Apaleteɛ tsyɛ dza? Gɛnɛ wɔnɔ onu kɛdɔ̃ gidekoeyì nɛ mɛ axɔ̃ɛ wɔàwɔa nɛ Apaleteɛ mɛ dza?

english_verse: Others may think that I am not an apostle, but you are proof that I am an apostle to you.
translation_verse: Onupɛ gɛnɛ anɔkpɛkpɛ mɛinu ɔkɔ babhlatɔ ete tsyɛɛ, inu anɔkpɛkpɛ kɔ wɔnɔ xɔnɔ! Ɔmɔsɛ wɔnɔ onu kɛdɔ̃ gɛ balɔkɔ adã nɛ anɔkpɛkpɛ kɛxɔ̃ɛ gidzì nɛ Apaleteɛ mɛ nulũ.

english_verse: When people question me, I tell them
translation_verse: Bulũbubuɔ gɛ lɛ mɛahɔɛ ɔkɔ bagɛ azã kesi nɛ kebhu mɛ nimɛ nɛ alɛ.

english_verse: that Barnabas and I have the right to our food and drink.
translation_verse: Bɔgɛ mɔ kidzo sɛ tɛ̀ bɔŋã bɔdɔ̃ alo tɛ bɔwɔɛ bɔdɔ̃ dza?

english_verse: We each have the right to marry one of the Lord's followers and to take her along with us, just as the other apostles and the Lord's brothers and Peter do.
translation_verse: Bɔgɛ mɔɛdzo sɛ tɛ̀ bɔgbanà bɔlɔ edzini nɔnɔ gɛ ayɛkɛ aŋã ovunɔ ɔplɛnɔ bɔlɔ asɛ kana gɛ banɔkpɛkpɛ gɔgɔɔ nɛ̀ Apaleteɛ yɛadza alɛ nɛ Kefa abhɛtɛɛ ɛlɛ dza?

english_verse: Are we the only ones who have to support ourselves by working at another job?
translation_verse: Pɛnɔsɛ mɛ nɛ Barnaba ete kɛlɛ sɛ tɛ̀ bɔwɔa axɔ̃ɛ tɛ̀ bɔpɛ kɛŋaŋã ɔkɔ ishuɔ dza?

english_verse: Do soldiers pay their own salaries? Don't people who raise grapes eat some of what they grow? Don't shepherds get milk from their own goats?
translation_verse: Keyuadɛ ɖuwoelu gɛ awɔa kɛxɔ̃ɛ pɛ azabuia kɛbhɛ akɔ ishu dza? Afana gɛ adɛ wain bɔyɛ pɛ atɛ aŋã yɛ eyuyuɔ dza? Afana gɛ elú ibhoshì pɛ atɛ awɔ̃ɛ ɛlɛ kɛmɔlĩɛ dza?

english_verse: I am not saying this on my own authority. The Law of Moses tells us not to muzzle an ox when it is grinding grain. But was God concerned only about an ox?
translation_verse: Bafɔnɔ ɛlɛ elú nɛ Mose ɔshaa mɛ, “Tɛnyã olugbũ ɔkɔ nɛntsyɔɛ enyè nɔnɔ gɛ ehuisi amɔ.” Nɛntsyɔɛ nyè bui kɛlɛ dzã kiwuhũ ɔdɛ Owusoso Akaa?

english_verse: No, he wasn't! He was talking about us. This was written in the Scriptures so that all who plow and all who grind the grain will look forward to sharing in the harvest.
translation_verse: Kibui gbã bɔlɔ yɛaɖɔ ebui alɛ etseɖe? Kibui gbã, bɔlɔ bafɔnɔ ebui alɛ elú akɔ, ɔmɔsɛ onupɛ ayadɛ nɛ̀ agɛ yɛetù amɔ ebetu amɔ́ɔ, kɛlɛ ɔsɛ babhɛtɛ ana sɛ tɛ aka nyia aka amɔ ɔŋãkɔ nɛ kɛxɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa mɛ.

english_verse: When we told the message to you, it was like planting spiritual seed. So we have the right to accept material things as our harvest from you.
translation_verse: Onupɛ bɔɖa ɔfɔfuĩa mɛ eyí kpɛ wɔnɔ ɛshiɛ, gɛpɛ falanɔ kifu gɛ onupɛ bɔmɔ bɔdɔ̃ denɔ wɔnɔkɔ dza?

english_verse: If others have the right to do this, we have an even greater right. But we haven't used this right of ours. We are willing to put up with anything to keep from causing trouble for the message about Christ.
translation_verse: Onupɛ bɔkɔ banɔ bhlatɔ amɔ tɛpɛplɛnɔ kidzo ɛlɛ nɛidi denɔ wɔnɔkɔ, kɛlɛ pɛ bɔtaamɔ kɛlɛ dɛ ana dza? Pɔ bɔgɛ gã kidzo ɛlɛ nɛidi abha. Nɛ kɛlɛ shuɔ, bɔyɛkɛ kɛka nyia ka ɔkɔ ishu sɛ tɛ̀ kidzo tɛdã ɔkɔ Kristo yɛibui gbangbã.

english_verse: Don't you know that people who work in the temple make their living from what is brought to the temple? Don't you know that a person who serves at the altar is given part of what is offered?
translation_verse: Nɔotinyi ɔsɛ bagɛ awɔa axɔ̃ɛ nɛ Owusoso Akaavũɔ mɛ bamɔ bɔŋaŋã edenɔ ɛkɛlɛ dza, kɛlɛ osubha gɛ ɔlɛ abha banɔ nɔ apɔɛ bɔdɔ̃, gɛnɛ ɛkɛlɛ baŋã bɔdɔ̃ edenɔɔ dza?

english_verse: In the same way, the Lord wants everyone who preaches the good news to make a living from preaching this message.
translation_verse: Nɛ kidzo ɛlɛ nabha, Apaleteɛ akɔ alɛ ɔsha sɛ bagɛ baɖɔ kibui gbangbã bamɔ balɛ ɔŋãkɔ odenɔ kibui gbangbã mɛ.

english_verse: But I have never used these privileges of mine, and I am not writing this because I want to start now. I would rather die than have someone rob me of the right to take pride in this.
translation_verse: Pɔ mɛxɔnɔ gɛ wɔa ohumi ɔlɛ nanà shu kɛxɔ̃ɛ. Kɛlɛ ɛtɛ fɔnɔ kibui lɛ sɛ tɛ̀ nɔbhɛtɛ ebui alɛ nɛidi ɔkɔ mɛ. Kɛbapɛ ɔkɔ mɛ sɛ tɛ̀ mɛitsí ɔdɛ sɛ tɛ anɔ teli ayɛkɛ kɛdɔ̃ gɛ kɛlɛishu mɛifeke ishuɔ nɛ̀ mɛahɔɛ.

english_verse: I don't have any reason to brag about preaching the good news. Preaching is something God told me to do, and if I don't do it, I am doomed.
translation_verse: Pɔ onupɛ ɛɖɔ kibui gbangbã, ɛtɛ balà feke mɛishu, ɔmɔsɛ batɔɖɔ ɛlɛ akɔ mɛ ɔsɛ tɛ ɛɖɔ Owusoso Akaa ibuiɛ. Abagagalĩ akɔ mɛ gɛ onupɛ ɛgɛ ɖɔ kibui gbangbã.

english_verse: If I preach because I want to, I will be paid. But even if I don't want to, it is still something God has sent me to do.
translation_verse: Onupɛ ɛlɛkɔ mɛishu kɔ kibuiɛ tɛɖɔɖɔ, kɛbhɛ babuia bazã kɛlɛ mɛ ɔkɔ mɛ, onupɛ gɛnɛ mɛishu mɛlɔkɔ ɔkɔ kibuiɛ tɛɖɔɖɔ, kibuiɛ gɛ bakpɛ mɛ ahɔɛ sɛ ɛbhɛtɛ ete mɛbhɛtɛ.

english_verse: What pay am I given? It is the chance to preach the good news free of charge and not to use the privileges that are mine because I am a preacher.
translation_verse: Kiɖuwoe gɛinu mɛ kɛbhɛ babuia nɔɔ dza? Kɛlɛ̀ ete ɔsɛ, tɛ̀ onupɛ ɛɖɔ kibui gbangbã, ɛtɛ bayɛkɛ kɛbhɛ babuia nanà, kɛlɛ ɛtɛba yɛkɛ kɛnanà nɛ kɛlɛ tɛɖɔɖɔ mɛ, kliso ɛgɛ lɔkɔ mɛ ohumi nɔ gɛɔlɛ mɛahɔɛ kpɛ kibuiɛ tɛɖɔɖɔ mɛ.

english_verse: I am not anyone's slave. But I have become a slave to everyone, so I can win as many people as possible.
translation_verse: Ɛlɛ mɛishu ahɔɛ mɛ, kɛlɛ gɛ ɛma ananà esi tsyɛ elo, ɛlɔkɔ mɛishu asɛ anɔ dzidzi ɔkɔ aka nyia aka, sɛ tɛ̀ ɛkɔ banɔ kpekpèɛ amɔ ɛkpã.

english_verse: When I am with the Jews, I live like a Jew to win Jews. They are ruled by the Law of Moses, and I am not. But I live by the Law to win them.
translation_verse: Onupɛ ɛlɛ Yudanɛ nɛishiɛ, ɛbhɛtɛ mɛishu asɛ Yudaeyi sɛ tɛ̀ ibu alɛ. Nɛ bagɛ akpasɛ Mose ɔsha niesi soɔ, ɛbhɛtɛ mɛishu asɛ anɔ gɛ akpasɛ ɔsha niesi, sɛ tɛ̀ ibu bagɛ akpasɛ ɔsha niesi so.

english_verse: And when I am with people who are not ruled by the Law, I forget about the Law to win them. Of course, I never really forget about the law of God. In fact, I am ruled by the law of Christ.
translation_verse: Nɛ bagɛ ama ɔsha niesi nɛishiɛ, ɛbhɛtɛ ishu asɛ balɛ mɛ anɔ teli, , sɛ tɛ mɛibu bagɛ akpasɛ ɔsha niesi.

english_verse: When I am with people whose faith is weak, I live as they do to win them. I do everything I can to win everyone I possibly can.
translation_verse: Onupɛ ɛkpasɛ bagɛ ebotso nishiɛ, ɛzã asɛ balɛ sɛ tɛ ibu alɛ. Idzi kɛdɔ̃ ka nyia ka ɛdi kɔ anɔ ka nyia ka sɛ tɛ gana kidzo ka nyia ka abha tɛ mɛibu balɛ banɔ teli.

english_verse: I do all this for the good news, because I want to share in its blessings. A Race and a Fight
translation_verse: Nɛ kibui gbangbã nɛtakpu so ɛbhɛtɛ alɛ nyɔ̃ɔ, sɛ tɛ̀ mɛtsyɛ ɛmɔ tiyuyula nɛ kɛlɛ mɛ.

english_verse: You know that many runners enter a race, and only one of them wins the prize. So run to win!
translation_verse: Nɔ otinyi ɔsɛ nɛ tɛtsɛɖɛmɛ tibubu mɛɛ, banɔ nɔ nyɔ̃ɔ ebu tɛtsɛɖɛmɛ, pɔ anɔ tenikpo ko yɛaŋã kabha dza? Nɔobu tɛtsɛɖɛmɛ nɛ kidzoɔ gɛ kɛlɛ abha nɔbɔmɔ kabha ŋaŋã.

english_verse: Athletes work hard to win a crown that cannot last, but we do it for a crown that will last forever.
translation_verse: Anɔ ka nyia ka gɛ apɛ asɛ tɛ̀ awlɔ̃akɛ nɛ bɔwufu lɛ nɛidi mɛɛ, akpasɛ bɔlɛ piapii. Babhɛtɛ alɛ sɛ tɛ̀ bayɛkɛ kikutuɔ gɛtɛ bazã tihɔɔ nɔ, pɔ bɔbhɛtɛ alɛ nyɔ̃ɔ sɛ tɛ̀ bɔyɛkɛ kikutuɔ gɛ bazã tihɔɔ nɔ.

english_verse: I don't run without a goal. And I don't box by beating my fists in the air.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ, itibu tɛtsɛɖɛmɛ asɛ anɔ gɛ tɛtɔtɔɖɔ nanà ma yɛahɔɛ, kɛlɛ ɛtɛta ekpili tsyɛ asɛ anɔ gɛ ehuisi kɛlɛɛ nɔ ana.

english_verse: I keep my body under control and make it my slave, so I won't lose out after telling the good news to others.
translation_verse: Kɛmaɛlɛ mɛ ana, ɛkpɛ mɛ ishupiɛ bɔtɔ pɛ ɛbhɛtɛ ishu asɛ anɔ dzidzì ana, sɛ tɛ̀ onupɛ ɛɖɔ kibui gbangbã ɔkɔ banɔ nɔ̀ bekeɛ, betibè bumɛ eyinɔ ɔsɛ ɛgɛ na ɔsɛ tɛ̀ ɛyɛkɛ kikutuɔ.

english_verse: Friends, I want to remind you that all our ancestors walked under the cloud and went through the sea.
translation_verse: Mɛ adzya alɛ mɔ, pɔ ɛgɛpɛ sɛ tɛ̀ kɛzã tikpukpũ mɛ ɔkɔ wɔnɔ sɛ, bɔlɔ ashuĩ alɛ petee azã ɔmɔlɔ niesi, kɛlɛ balɛ petee agã aƒuɔ mɛ ekoeyì.

english_verse: This was like being baptized and becoming followers of Moses.
translation_verse: Bakpɛ bulĩ akɔ alɛ asɛ Mose ede azazã ana nɛ ɔmɔlɔ̃ nɔ nɛ̀ aƒuɔ mɛ.

english_verse: All of them also ate the same spiritual food
translation_verse: Balɛ petee aŋã ɔfɔfuĩa mɛ bɔŋaŋã lɛ tunikpo

english_verse: and drank the same spiritual drink, which flowed from the spiritual rock that followed them. That rock was Christ.
translation_verse: kɛlɛ bawɔɛ ɔfɔfuĩa mɛ bɔdɔ̃ tunikpo ɔlɛ, ɔmɔsɛ bawɔɛ bɔlɛ edenɔ ɔfɔfuĩa mɛ kɛklagbuɔ gɛgbanà alɛ vunɔɔ mɛ, kɛklagbu ɛlɛ kɛlɛinu Kristoɔ.

english_verse: But most of them did not please God. So they died, and their bodies were scattered all over the desert.
translation_verse: Kɛkpasɛ ɛlɛ mɛ ana pɔ, Owusoso Akaa agɛmɔ samɛ nɛ balɛ mɛ anɔ kpekpèɛ teli shu, balɛ ishudzya petee pɔɛ kanà nɛ oxuiɛ mɛ.

english_verse: What happened to them is a warning to keep us from wanting to do the same evil things.
translation_verse: Ebui alɛ aba ɛlɛ mɛ ana ɔsɛ tɛ̀ kidzì kibui mɔmɔlu, tɛ kɛkɔ ɔsɛ bɔtakà lɔkɔ bɔlɔ etúɔ tɔɖɔ ebui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ abha asɛ gɛ babhɛtɛ ana.

english_verse: They worshiped idols, just as the Scriptures say, “The people sat down to eat and drink. Then they got up to dance around.” So don't worship idols.
translation_verse: Nɔotidzì ibudielete, asɛ gɛ balɛ mɛ banɔ teli dzi ana, asɛ kana gɛ bafɔnɔ elu asɛ, “Banunɔ azã kesi sɛ tɛ̀ baŋã tɛ̀ bawɔɛ kɛlɛ babhɛtɛ ibudielete ebui.

english_verse: Some of those people did shameful things, and in a single day about
translation_verse: Nɔtɛ bhɛtɛ kɛgɔlɔ ŋaŋã ebui, asɛ kana gɛ balɛ mɛanɔ teli abhɛtɛɛ ana, pɛ balɛ mɛ anɔ ihotu afɛtaabha nɛ̀ tata etsi nɛ kiwui tinikpo abha.

english_verse: Some of you say, “We can do whatever we want to!” But I tell you not everything may be good or helpful.
translation_verse: “Bakɔ kibui ka nyia ka shu kidzo,” pɔ gɛnɛ kɛka nyia ka kɛlɛ vɛnɔ anɔ nɛ otugbà. “Bakɔ kibui ka nyia ka shu kidzo-pɔ gɛnɛ kɛka nyia ka kɛlɛ pɛ̃kɔ anɔ.

english_verse: And don't try to test Christ, as some of them did and were later bitten by poisonous snakes.
translation_verse: Kɛma ɔsɛ tɛ̀ bufeke Apaleteɛ eɖi, asɛ gɛ balɛ mɛ anɔ teli abhɛtɛ pɛ beblidzya atá alɛ betsiɛ.

english_verse: Don't even grumble, as some of them did and were killed by the destroying angel.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ nɔtɛzɔ kpamɛ ishu, asɛ kana gɛ balɛ mɛanɔ teli abhɛtɛ pɛ Owusoso Akaa axɔ̃ɛdɛ gɛezĩ bɔdɔ̃ ebeyi alɛ ana.

english_verse: These things happened to them as a warning to us. All this was written in the Scriptures to teach us who live in these last days.
translation_verse: Ebui alɛ aba balɛ abha sɛ tɛ balɔkɔ amɔ elu, kɛlɛ bafɔnɔ alɛ elú sɛ tɛ balɔkɔ etseɖe nɔ nɛ bɔlɔ, bɔlɔ bagɛ ebuiɛ gɛ bafɔnɔ eluɔ aba bɔlɔ abha.

english_verse: Even if you think you can stand up to temptation, be careful not to fall.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ, onupɛ abhɛtɛ wɔ asɛ ɔyɛkɛ ɔŋã piapii nɔ, ɖi piapii ɔsɛ tɛ ɔtɛdɔ oboe.

english_verse: You are tempted in the same way that everyone else is tempted. But God can be trusted not to let you be tempted too much, and he will show you how to escape from your temptations.
translation_verse: Kibui nanà tifeke wɔɖi ɔdɛ kibui nɔnɔ gɛŋã anɔ ka nyia ka abha. Pɔ Owusoso Akaa xɔnɔ abhɛtɛ kibui gbã, atɛ bakɔ tifefekeɖi nɔnɔ gɛ kɛbà dɛwɔ ba wɔabha. Pɔ onupɛ befeke wɔ eɖiɛ, abapɛ kidzo nɔnɔ abha gɛ ɔbɔgã pɛ obokoeyì nɛ kɛlɛ mɛ. Obudi Bɔdɔ̃ Nɛ̀ Apaleteɛ Bɔdɔ̃ ŋaŋã

english_verse: My friends, you must keep away from idols.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ mɛɔbhia alɛ, nɔobu tɛtsɛɖɛmɛ nɛ ibudi ebui shu.

english_verse: I am speaking to you as people who have enough sense to know what I am talking about.
translation_verse: Ɛɖɔ kaɖɔ ɔkɔ wɔnɔ asɛ bebutinɛ ana, nɔokulu kebhu ɔkɔ ishu nɛ̀ kɛgɛ mɛɖɔɔ.

english_verse: When we drink from the cup we ask God to bless, isn't that sharing in the blood of Christ? When we eat the bread we break, isn't that sharing in the body of Christ?
translation_verse: Ahɔɛhuhu kayintse nɔ gɛ bɔlɔkɔ huahɔɛ nɔ pɔ, gɛnɛ gɛ bɔlɔkɔ tili nɛ Yesu dzyosu nɔ mɛ soɔ dza? Bɔŋaŋã gɛ bɔya tsyɛ pɔ, butinu tili gɛ bɔlɔkɔ nɛ Kristo ishupiɛ shuɔ so dza?

english_verse: By sharing in the same loaf of bread, we become one body, even though there are many of us.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ gɛ bùkpèe, pɛ kifufulũ tenikpo pɔ kɛlɛ lɛɛ, so budzi ishupi tunikpo, pɛ bɔlɔ petee tã kifufulũ tinikpo alɛ.

english_verse: Aren't the people of Israel sharing in the worship when they gather around the altar and eat the sacrifices offered there?
translation_verse: Nɔolu awɔɖi Israelanɛ. Gɛnɛ bagɛ bawɔa axɔ̃ɛ nɛ bɔdɔ̃pɔpɔɛ osubha nabha balɛ aŋã bɔdɔ̃ pɔpɔɛ nɔ nɛ ɔlɛ abha dza?

english_verse: Am I saying that either the idols or the food sacrificed to them is anything at all?
translation_verse: Bɔɖɔ mɛɖɔ sɛ bɔdɔ̃ gɛ bapɔɛ akɔ ibudiɛ nu kɛdɔ̃ tili, pɛnɔsɛ kɛdɔ̃ tili obudiɛ onuɔ dza?

english_verse: No, I am not! This food is really sacrificed to demons and not to God. I don't want you to have anything to do with demons.
translation_verse: Gɛnɛ ana, pɔ ibudi eleteɛ bɔdɔ̃ pɔpɔɛ nɔ ɛfɔfuĩa zizĩ balɔkɔ ɛlɛ akɔ, gɛnɛ Owusoso Akaa balɔkɔ ɔlɛ akɔ, kɛlɛ ɛgɛ pɛ sɛ tɛ nɔ ɔkpɛ ahɔɛ nɛ ɛfɔfuĩa zizĩ ebui mɛ.

english_verse: You cannot drink from the cup of demons and still drink from the Lord's cup. You cannot eat at the table of demons and still eat at the Lord's table.
translation_verse: Otibɔ la ɔwɔɛ nɛ Apaleteɛ kayintse nulũ nɛ ɔfɔfuĩa zizĩ tsyɛ kayintse nulũ, kana otibɔ là ɔzã Apaleteɛ ɔkpɔlɔ̃ nɔ shu pɛ ɔzɔbɔ zã ɛfɔfuĩa zizĩ ɛlɛ ɔkpɔlɔ̃ nɔ tsyɛ ishu.

english_verse: We would make the Lord jealous if we did this. And we are not stronger than the Lord. Always Honor God
translation_verse: Bɔpɛ ɔsɛ tɛ Apaleteɛ alɔkɔ kabhã wawlãkɛ dza? Bɔgagalĩ ɔdɛ yɛ dza? Bayɛyɛkɛŋã Ishu Ahɔɛmɛ Zazã

english_verse: We should think about others and not about ourselves.
translation_verse: Ananà atɛpɛ yɛ tenikpo tɛpɛpɛ̃, pɔ apɛ kɛgbangbã akɔ babhlatɔ tsyɛ.

english_verse: However, when you buy meat in the market, go ahead and eat it. Keep your conscience clear by not asking where the meat came from.
translation_verse: Nɔŋã kɛdɔ̃ ka nyia ka gɛ baadɛ nɛ kedziɛ mɛ, pɔ nɔtɛ kɔ kɛkpɛ wɔnɔ bulũhũ tɛ nodi bisi kɛliesi olũ,

english_verse: The Scriptures say, “The earth and everything in it belong to the Lord.”
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ kesiakpa nɛ kɛka nyia ka gɛ kpasɛ kalɛ mɛ nu Apaleteɛ ɛyɛ.

english_verse: If an unbeliever invites you to dinner, and you want to go, then go. Eat whatever you are served. Don't cause a problem for someone's conscience by asking where the food came from.
translation_verse: Onupɛ anɔ teli gɛetinu anɔ gɛ ayɛkɛ aŋã ekulu wɔnɔ sɛ nɔbɔ ŋã bɔdɔ̃ pɛ nɔpɛ ɔsɛ ɔvɛɛ, nɔŋã kɛka nyia ka gɛ bedzu wɔnɔ otugbà tɛ̀ nɔtɛ kɔ kɛkpɛ wɔnɔ bulũhũ tɛ nodi bisi kɛlɛ esi olũ.

english_verse: But if you are told it has been sacrificed to idols, don't cause a problem by eating it. I don't mean a problem for yourself, but for the one who told you. Why should my freedom be limited by someone else's conscience?
translation_verse: tɛ kɛtɛ bakpɛ anɔ bhalà ekpeɖekpeɖe mɛ uluhũ gɛnɛ wɔ ɔyɛ. Pɔ kiɖeso pɛ kɛlɛ ɔsɛ tɛ anɔ bhlatɔ alɔkɔ yɛekpeɖekpeɖeɛ ekulu kebhu nɛ̀ mɛishu ahɔɛmɛ zazãa dza?

english_verse: If I give thanks for what I eat, why should anyone accuse me of doing wrong?
translation_verse: Onupɛ ɛŋã bɔdɔ̃ nɛ̀ ahɔɛhuhuɔ pɔ, kɛlɛ so pɛ bebumɛ kɛpɔ nɛ kɛdɔ̃ gɛ kɛlɛ takpu mɛihu Owusoso Akaa ahɔɛ nɛ kɛlɛ takpuɔ shuɔ dza?

english_verse: When you eat or drink or do anything else, always do it to honor God.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ, onupɛ nɔŋã bɔdɔ̃ alo bɔwɔɛ alo kɛka nyia ka gɛ nɔbhɛtɛ, nɔbhɛtɛ kɛlɛ ɔkɔ sɛ tɛ̀ bakpɛ Owusoso Akaa nɛinyè nɛitití.

english_verse: Don't cause problems for Jews or Greeks or anyone else who belongs to God's church.
translation_verse: Nɔtɛ kɔ sɛ anɔ nanà akpatà bɔdɔ̃, edzi asɛ Yudanɛ o, Helanɛ, pɛnɔsɛ Owusoso Akaa yɔhaa

english_verse: I always try to please others instead of myself, in the hope that many of them will be saved.
translation_verse: asɛ gɛ ɛpɛ sɛ tɛ mɛiku aka nyia aka awɔ̃ nɛ kidzo ka nyia ka abhaa. Gɛnɛ mɛ ete tɛpɛpɛ̃ mɛpɛɛ, pɔ banɔ kpekpèɛ tɛpɛpɛ̃, ɔsɛ tɛ bayɛkɛ alɛ elù nɛkpã.

english_verse: You must follow my example, as I follow the example of Christ. Rules for Worship
translation_verse: Noɖi mɛ ɛkpã tɛ̀ nɔzã nɛ ɛlɛ ulũ, asɛ gɛ mɛtsyɛ lɔkɔ Kristo ɛkpã dzinɔ ana. Bɔdɔ̃ Dadã Nɛ Kɛtakpu Nɛ Kɛtakpu Esi Kpɛkpɛ Mɛ

english_verse: I am proud of you, because you always remember me and obey the teachings I gave you.
translation_verse: Ɛkpɛ wɔnɔ kinyè nɛitití nɛ kɛbakà gɛ nɔbaka mɛ nɛ kɛka nyia ka mɛ, nɛ̀ kana gɛ nɔnyanã bɔdɔ̃ kakasɛ mɛ ovunɔ asɛ kɛkasɛ gɛ ɛkasɛ wɔnɔ ana.

english_verse: Now I want you to know that Christ is the head of all men, and a man is the head of a woman. But God is the head of Christ.
translation_verse: Otsiɛnɔ, ɛpɛ sɛ nɔoti ɔsɛ̀ anɔnyɛ yɛ kɛtakpuɔ kɛlɛinu Kristo, kɛlɛ anɔdzɛ yɛ kɛtakpuɔ kɛlɛinu anɔnyɛ, kɛlɛ Kristoɔ tsyɛ yɛtakpúɔ kɛlɛinu Owusoso Akaa.

english_verse: This means that any man who prays or prophesies with something on his head brings shame to his head.
translation_verse: Anyɛ ka nyia ka gɛ adã bɔdɔ̃ nɛ kɛtakpu flogo pɛ ekulu Owusoso Akaa alo aɖɔ ebui elúɔ, atɛ yɛ kɛtakpuɔ gɛinu Kristoɔ elu esi.

english_verse: But any woman who prays or prophesies without something on her head brings shame to her head. In fact, she may as well shave her head.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ anɔdzɛ ka nyia ka tsyɛ gɛ agɛnyã yɛtakpúɔ flogo pɛ ekulu Owusoso Akaa alo pɛ aɖɔ ebui eluɔ, atɛ yɛenyinè gɛenu yɛtakpúɔ elu esi, gɛ edzi asɛ onupɛ bashɛ yɛtakpúɔ ana.

english_verse: A woman should wear something on her head. It is a disgrace for a woman to shave her head or cut her hair. But if she refuses to wear something on her head, let her cut off her hair.
