translation_verse: Onupɛ anɔdzɛ agɛdã bɔdɔ̃ nɛ yɛtakpúɔ, akɔ batsɛ yɛ kɛtakpu tɛkãa, kɛlɛ onupɛ kinu kiwuĩ ɔkɔ anɔdzɛ sɛ tɛ batsɛ yɛ kɛtakpu tɛkãa, alo tɛ bashɛ kɛlɛ, adã bɔdɔ̃ nɛ kɛlɛ abha.

english_verse: Men were created to be like God and to bring honor to God. This means a man should not wear anything on his head. Women were created to bring honor to men.
translation_verse: Kɛma sɛ tɛ anɔnyɛ xɔnɔ adã bɔdɔ̃ nɛ yɛtakpuɔ, ɔmɔsɛ akpasɛ Owusoso Akaa tɛzazã nɛ̀ yɛishu dadalɛ mɛ, pɔ anɔdzɛ xɔnɔ enu anyɛ yɛishu dadalɛ.

english_verse: It was the woman who was made from a man, and not the man who was made from a woman.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ anɔnyɛ egide koeyi nɛ anɔdzɛ mɛ, pɔ anɔdzɛ yɛ edekoeyì nɛ anɔnyɛ mɛ,

english_verse: He wasn't created for her. She was created for him.
translation_verse: kana bagɛ bhɛtɛ anyɛ tsyɛ akɔ adzɛ, pɔ adzɛ bahɛtɛ akɔ anɔnyɛ.

english_verse: And so, because of this, and also because of the angels, a woman ought to wear something on her head, as a sign of her authority.
translation_verse: Nɛ kibui ɛlɛshu, nɛ̀ baxɔ̃ɛdɛ shu soɔ, kɛlɛ sɛ tɛ̀ anɔdzɛ amɔ ohumi nɛ yɛ kɛtakpu tɛkã nabha.

english_verse: As far as the Lord is concerned, men and women need each other.
translation_verse: Nɛ Apaleteɛ mɛ tsyɛ, adzɛ ama yɛishu ahɔɛ mɛ nɛ anyɛ shu, kana anyɛ tsyɛ ama yɛishu ahɔɛ mɛ nɛ adzɛ shu.

english_verse: It is true that the first woman came from a man, but all other men have been given birth by women. Yet God is the one who created everything.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ kana gɛ anɔdzɛ edekoeyì nɛ anɔnyɛ mɛɛ, kidzo ɛlɛ nabha adzɛ tsyɛ yɛamɔ́ anyɛ. Pɔ kɛka nyia ka de Owusoso Akaa ɔkɔ.

english_verse: Ask yourselves if it is proper for a woman to pray without something on her head.
translation_verse: Nɔobhui kibui mɛ ɔkɔ ishu ɔsɛ, kɛpɛ̃ ɔkɔ anɔdzɛ sɛ tɛ̀ eze kulu Owusoso Akaa gɛ agɛdã bɔdɔ̃ nɛ kɛtakpuɔ dza?

english_verse: But long hair is a beautiful way for a woman to cover her head.
translation_verse: kɛlɛ onupɛ anɔdzɛ kɛtakpu tɛkã dzeɛ, kinu ishu dadalɛ kɔ yɛ dza? Ɔmɔsɛ bakɔ yɛ kɛtakpu tɛkã dzlolo sɛ tɛ kɛdã yɛ kɛtakpuɔ.

english_verse: This is how things are done in all of God's churches, and this is why none of you should argue about what I have said. Rules for the Lord's Supper
translation_verse: Onupɛ anɔ teli apɛ asɛ tɛ̀ awlɔ̃akɛ kibui ɛlɛ, kibui nanà bha má bɔlɔ nɛ̀ Owusoso Akaa ɔhaa xɔnɔ ahɔɛ bɔɔ bhɛtɛ̀. Apaleteɛ Ɔwlago bɔdɔ̃ ŋaŋã

english_verse: Your worship services do you more harm than good. I am certainly not going to praise you for this.
translation_verse: Nɛ ebui gɛ ɛbabà tseɖe wɔnɔ otsiɛ nɔɔ, ɛtɛba kpɛ wɔnɔ kinyè nɛitití, ɔmɔsɛ wɔnɔ tɛtsyɔatsyɔa manà ebui kpa ɔdɛ agbangbã.

english_verse: I am told you can't get along with each other when you worship, and I am sure that some of what I have heard is true.
translation_verse: Putɔ nɔɔ, ilũ ɔsɛ onupɛ nɔba oshogò asɛ ɔhaa ana, ɔtɔbubú ɔkpasɛ wɔnɔɛishi, kɛlɛ nɛ ebuiteli shu soɔ, ɛyɛkɛ kɛlɛ abha ŋã.

english_verse: You are bound to argue with each other, but it is easy to see which of you have God's approval.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ kɛlɛ sɛ tɛ̀ ɔtɔbubu ɔzã wɔnɔɛishi, tɛ̀ kɛkasɛ wɔnɔ mɛ anunɔ abha gɛ Owusoso Akaa atɔɖɔ ahɔɛ.

english_verse: You even start eating before everyone gets to the meeting, and some of you go hungry, while others get drunk.
translation_verse: ɔmɔsɛ onupɛ nɔŋãa bɔdɔ̃ɔ, wɔnɔ mɛ aka nyia aka aŋã bɔlɛ eputɔnɔ gɛ etie po ananà. Anɔ teli gɛ bɔbhɛ yiyɛɛ, teli xɔnɔ atɛ wɔɛ ebuku.

english_verse: The Lord meant that when you eat this bread and drink from this cup, you tell about his death until he comes.
translation_verse: Kibe nyia be gɛ nɔtã kifufulũ alɛ pɛ nɔwɔɛ nɛ kayintse alɛ nulũɔ, nɔ ɔɖɔɛ ɛlɛ ɔkanà nɛ Apaleteɛ yɛutsiɛ shu odiyinɔ sɛ etsyina aba.

english_verse: If you fail to understand that you are the body of the Lord, you will condemn yourselves by the way you eat and drink.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ anɔka nyia ka gɛ aŋã pɛ awɔɛ pɛ atɛ abakà Apaleteɛ yɛishudzya mɛeɖi piapii nɔ, atã kɛlɛ awɔɛ kebhu kukulu elu akɔ yɛishu.

english_verse: After the meal, Jesus took a cup of wine in his hands and said, “This is my blood, and with it God makes his new agreement with you. Drink this and remember me.”
translation_verse: Nɛ kidzo ɛlɛ nabha gɛ baŋã bɔdɔ̃ ebekeɛ, aza alɔkɔ kayintse nɔ, kɛlɛ aɖɔ asɛ, “Kaiyintse alɛ kɛlɛ enu olugbũ nyanyã vɔvɔ gɛ ɛnyã nɛ wɔnɔ nɛ mɛ dzyosu nɔ mɛ, nɔbhɛtɛ kɛlɛ, kibe nyia be gɛ nɔwɔɛ nɛ kalɛ ulũɔ, tɛ nɔlɔkɔ ɔbaka mɛ nɔ.”

english_verse: I have already told you what the Lord Jesus did on the night he was betrayed. And it came from the Lord himself. He took some bread in his hands.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ kɛgɛ mɛ yɛkɛ odenɔ Apaleteɛ ɔkɔ mɛ ɛtsyɛ lɔkɔ kpɛ ahɔɛ ɛkɔ wɔnɔ. Elishi alɛ, gɛ bakpɛ Apalete Yesu ahɔɛ, alɔkɔ kifufulũ,

english_verse: Then after he had given thanks, he broke it and said, “This is my body, which is given for you. Eat this and remember me.”
translation_verse: kɛlɛ gɛ ehu ahɔɛ nɛ ɛlɛ ɛtakpuɔ ebekeɛ, ayá kifufulũɔ mɛ kɛlɛ aɖɔ asɛ, “Mɛishu dzya nɔ ɔlɛ, gɛ ɛlɔkɔ kɔ wɔnɔ, nɔbhɛtɛ kɛlɛ tɛ nɔlɔkɔ ɔbaka mɛ nɔ.”

english_verse: But if you eat the bread and drink the wine in a way that isn't worthy of the Lord, you sin against his body and blood.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ anɔ ka nyia ka gɛ aatã kifufulũ ilɛ, alo pɛ aawɔ̃ɛ nɛ Apaleteɛ kayintse alɛ nulũɔ nɛ kidzoɔ gɛ kɛma ɛlɛ ɔzãkɔ abhaa, aatà esi nɛ Apaleteɛ yɛishudzya nɛ̀ dzyosu nɔ shu.

english_verse: This is why you must examine the way you eat and drink.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ sɛ anɔ avɛ yɛishu mɛ oɖì piapii flogo tɛ atã kifufulũ ilɛ, alo tɛ̀ awɔɛ nɛ kayintse alɛ ulũ.

english_verse: This is why many of you are sick and weak and why a lot of others have died.
translation_verse: Kliso so pɛ wɔnɔ mɛ banɔ teli ebotsò pɛ baanyã, kɛlɛ wɔnɔ mɛ banɔ teli tsyɛ etsí.

english_verse: If we carefully judge ourselves, we won't be punished.
translation_verse: Pɔ onupɛ bukulu kebhu nɛishuɔ, beze eke kulu kebhu nɛ bɔlɔ.

english_verse: But when the Lord judges and punishes us, he does it to keep us from being condemned with the rest of the world.
translation_verse: Onupɛ Apaleteɛ ekulu kebhu nɛ bɔlɔ, apɔ́ batɔ̃ akɔ bɔlɔ sɛ tɛ̀ beti bebu bɔlɔ ɛpɔ ɔplɛnɔ kanɔ nɔ̀.

english_verse: My dear friends, you should wait until everyone gets there before you start eating.
translation_verse: Mɛ adzya alɛ, kliso, onupɛ nobo oshogò pɛ nɔŋã bɔdɔ̃ɔ, nɔzãopo bagɔlɔ.

english_verse: If you really are hungry, you can eat at home. Then you won't condemn yourselves when you meet together. After I arrive, I will instruct you about the other matters.
translation_verse: Onupɛ bɔbhɛ yì wɔnɔ mɛ anɔ teliɛ, baŋã bɔdɔ̃ nɛ bɔpã mɛ, sɛ tɛ̀ onupɛ nɔbɔ tsyɔaa, kɛtɛbà tsyina dzì kebhu kukulu. Kɛlɛ onupɛ ɛzɛ ba wɔnɔ ɔbhaa tsyɛɛ, ɛba tseɖe wɔnɔ kɛgɛ nɔbɔ bhɛtɛɛ.

english_verse: I want you to remember that before you became followers of the Lord, you were led in all the wrong ways by idols that cannot even talk.
translation_verse: Nɔonyi ɔsɛ gɛ nodzì ibudi eleteɛ, bawɔɛ wɔnɔ agbanà avɛnɔ ibudiɛ gɛ lɛtɛ ɖɔ kaɖɔɔ ɔkɔ.

english_verse: Now I want you to know that if you are led by God's Spirit, you will say that Jesus is Lord, and you will never curse Jesus.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ, itseɖe wɔnɔ sɛ anɔ nanà gɛ aɖɔaɖɔ nɛ̀ Owusoso Akaa ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ atɛ baɖɔ asɛ, “Baɖɔ kibui akpɛ Yesu,” kɛlɛ ananà tsyɛ atɛbàla aɖɔ asɛ, “Yesu yɛenu Apaleteɛ,” ɔdɛ sɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã ɔkpasɛ yɛ mɛ.

english_verse: There are different kinds of spiritual gifts, but they all come from the same Spirit.
translation_verse: Bɔdɔ̃ kɔkɔ lɛ ediedi, pɔ Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ xɔnɔ tonikpo lonu.

english_verse: There are different ways to serve the same Lord,
translation_verse: Axɔ̃ɛdɛdɛ ediedi alɛ, pɔ Apaleteɛ xɔnɔ tenikpo.

english_verse: and we can each do different things. Yet the same God works in all of us and helps us in everything we do.
translation_verse: Axɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa ediedi tsyɛ alɛ, pɔ Owusoso Akaa xɔnɔ tenikpo yɛ awɔa alɛ nɛ banɔ nɔ petee mɛ.

english_verse: The Spirit has given each of us a special way of serving others.
translation_verse: Otsiɛnɔ anɔka nyia ka bakɔɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ axɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa ɔkɔ kɛgbangbã tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ.

english_verse: Some of us can speak with wisdom, while others can speak with knowledge, but these gifts come from the same Spirit.
translation_verse: Anɔ teli bakɔɛ bubutì kaɖɔɖɔɖɔ ɔganà Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ mɛ, anɔbhlatɔ tsyɛ bakɔɛ kibuititì ɔganà Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ mɛ,

english_verse: To others the Spirit has given great faith or the power to heal the sick
translation_verse: anɔbhlatɔ tsyɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã ɔganà Ɔfɔfuĩa ɔlɛ mɛ, anɔ teli tsyɛ bɔnyã bubu kɔanɔ nɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa tonikpo ɔlɛ mɛ,

english_verse: or the power to work mighty miracles. Some of us are prophets, and some of us recognize when God's Spirit is present. Others can speak different kinds of languages, and still others can tell what these languages mean.
translation_verse: anɔ teli tsyɛ bakɔɛ ohumi gɛ lɔbhɛtɛ ebui gɛ labhɛtɛ kibì, teli tsyɛ kibui ɖɔɖɔlu, teli tsyɛ tɛ̀ amɔ Ɛfɔfuĩa nɔ gɛinu ɛgbangbã nɛ̀ ɛkpa, teli tsyɛ kaɖɔɖɔɖɔ nɛ beluhũ bhlatɔ mɛ, kɛlɛ teliɛ tsyɛ tɛ ebu beluhũ vɔvɔ huhuɔ niesi.

english_verse: But it is the Spirit who does all this and decides which gifts to give to each of us. One Body with Many Parts
translation_verse: Alɛ nyɔ̃ɔ enu Ɔfɔfuĩa ɔlɛ tonikpo ɔlɛ axɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa, kɛlɛ akɔ anɔka nyia ka nɛ kana gɛ apɛ nulũ. Ishupi Tunikpo, Bɔwɔ̃pɛ Xɔnɔ Kpè

english_verse: The body of Christ has many different parts, just as any other body does.
translation_verse: Ishupiɛ ɛlɛ tinikpo, pɔ babhɛtɛ ɔlɛ akpɛ bɔwɔ̃pɛ ediedi mɛ, kɛlɛ gɛ bɔlɛ bɔwɔ̃pɛ gelee mɛ tsyɛ elo, bunu ishupi tunikpo. Kana kɛlɛ nɛ Kristo mɛ.

english_verse: Some of us are Jews, and others are Gentiles. Some of us are slaves, and others are free. But God's Spirit baptized each of us and made us part of the body of Christ. Now we each drink from that same Spirit.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa tonikpo balɔkɔ akpɛ bulĩ akɔ bɔlɔ nɔ, edzì asɛ Yudaeyi alo Helaeyi, anɔ dzidzì alo anɔ gɛ alɛ yɛishu ahɔɛ mɛ tsyɛ elo, Ɔfɔfuĩa tonikpo ɔlɛ bakɔ bɔlɔ nyɔ̃ɔ ɔsɛ bɔwɔ̃ɛ.

english_verse: Our bodies don't have just one part. They have many parts.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ ishupiɛ ma tenikpo, pɔ bɔwɔ̃pɛ gelee balɔkɔ abhɛtɛ ɔlɛ nɔ.

english_verse: Suppose a foot says, “I'm not a hand, and so I'm not part of the body.” Wouldn't the foot still belong to the body?
translation_verse: Onupɛ ɔtsɛɖɛ ɔɖɔ sɛ, “Gɛ itinu ɔhɔɛ soɔ, ɛma ɔkɔ ishupiɛ,” gɛnɛ kibui ɛlɛ tɛɖɔɖɔ shu so pɛ, ɔhɔɛ obobu ɔtɔ nɛ ishupiɛ shu.

english_verse: Or suppose an ear says, “I'm not an eye, and so I'm not part of the body.” Wouldn't the ear still belong to the body?
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ onupɛ batɔ̃ aɖɔ asɛ, “Gɛ itinu kiwuhũ soɔ, ɛma ɔkɔ ishupiɛ,” gɛnɛ kibui ɛlɛ tɛɖɔɖɔ shu so pɛ batɔ̃ ebebu ɔtɔ nɛ ishupiɛ shu.

english_verse: If our bodies were only an eye, we couldn't hear a thing. And if they were only an ear, we couldn't smell a thing.
translation_verse: Onupɛ ishupiɛ petee tsyina dzi asɛ kiwuhũ ana pɔ, fakɔ kɛgɛ kɔ belũ bɔdɔ̃ bazã dza? Kɛlɛ onupɛ ishudzya petee lɛ asɛ batɔ̃ ana pɔ, fakɔ kɛgɛ ɛkɔ belũ bɔdɔ̃ bazã mɔɔ dza?

english_verse: But God has put all parts of our body together in the way that he decided is best.
translation_verse: Pɔ nɛ kibui gbã mɛ, Owusoso Akaa alɔkɔ bɔwɔ̃pɛ ɔwɔ̃pɛ abhɔ ishupiɛ nɔ, bɔlɛ mɛ ɔkɔ nyia ɔkɔ, nɛ kana gɛ apɛ asɛ tɛ̀ bɔzã nulũ.

english_verse: A body isn't really a body, unless there is more than one part.
translation_verse: Onupɛ bɔlɛ nyɔ̃ɔ nu kawɔ̃pɛ tienikpo nɔ pɔ, fakɔ ishupiɛ bazãa dza?

english_verse: It takes many parts to make a single body.
translation_verse: Asɛ kana gɛ kɛlɛ, bɔwɔ̃pɛɛ kpè, pɔ ishupi tunikpo.

english_verse: That's why the eyes cannot say they don't need the hands. That's also why the head cannot say it doesn't need the feet.
translation_verse: Kiwuhũɔ tɛbalà tseɖe ɔhɔɛ ɔsɛ, “Ɛtɛ pɛɛ wɔ!” Kɛlɛ kɛtakpuɔ tsyɛ tɛbalà tseɖe ɛtsɛɖɛ ɔsɛ, “Ɛtɛ pɛɛ wɔ!”

english_verse: In fact, we cannot get along without the parts of the body that seem to be the weakest.
translation_verse: Kana nɛ kidzo ɛlɛ nabha, batɛ balà shɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ ishupi bɔwɔ̃pɛ ɛlɛ gɛibotsoɔ shu,

english_verse: We take special care to dress up some parts of our bodies. We are modest about our personal parts,
translation_verse: kɛlɛ sɛ bɔbhɛtɛ bɔwɔ̃pɛ ɛlɛ gɛ abhɛtɛ̀ bɔlɔ asɛ bɔma kɛnanà mɛɛ tɛ bɔlɛ tsyɛ zã piapii. Kɛlɛ tɛ bɔlɔkɔ nɛ tilì mɛ ɔkɔ bɔwɔ̃pɛ ɛlɛ tsyɛ gɛibotsoɔ,

english_verse: but we don't have to be modest about other parts. God put our bodies together in such a way that even the parts that seem the least important are valuable.
translation_verse: gɛ bɔgɛ lɔkɔ kabha ɖiɖi idi nanà ɔkɔ ishupiɛ ɛkɔ tili tsyɛ elo, Owusoso Akaa xɔnɔ eshogo ishup ɛlɛ gɛibotsoɔ pɛ akpɛ ɛlɛ nɛitití,

english_verse: He did this to make all parts of the body work together smoothly, with each part caring about the others.
translation_verse: sɛ tɛ̀ ɔtɔbubu ɔtɛbɔzã ishupiɛ mɛ, pɔ sɛ tɛ̀ bɔwɔ̃pɛ petee lɔkɔ tili nɛ kɛlɛ mɛ ɔkɔ bagɔlɔ.

english_verse: If one part of our body hurts, we hurt all over. If one part of our body is honored, the whole body will be happy.
translation_verse: Onupɛ kawɔ̃pɛ tienikpo tieli amɔ bɔtɔɔ, bɔwɔ̃pɛ petee mɔ bɔtɔ ɔplɛnɔ kalɛ, onupɛ bakpɛ kawɔ̃pɛ tienikpo nɛititíɛ, bɔwɔ̃pɛ petee mɔ samè ɔplɛnɔ kalɛ.

english_verse: Together you are the body of Christ. Each one of you is part of his body.
translation_verse: Otsiɛnɔ wɔnɔ onu Ishupiɛ ɔkɔ Kristoɔ, kɛlɛ wɔnɔ petee onu kawɔ̃pɛ tieli ɔkɔ ɛlɛ.

english_verse: First, God chose some people to be apostles and prophets and teachers for the church. But he also chose some to work miracles or heal the sick or help others or be leaders or speak different kinds of languages.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ nɛ ɔhaa mɛ tsyɛ, Owusoso Akaa eputɔ ebu banɔkpɛkpɛ, babhalà balɛenu ebuiɖɔluanɔ, batramɛ balɛenu badɔkasɛ, flogo pɛ bagɛ abhɛtɛ̀ ebui gɛ abhɛtɛ̀ ibì, ɔplɛnɔ bagɛ bɔnyã bubu nɛ̀, baagɛ aplɛnɔ banɔ, bagɛ bazã banɔ ulũ nɛ̀ bagɛ behu beluhũ vɔvɔ.

english_verse: Not everyone is an apostle. Not everyone is a prophet. Not everyone is a teacher. Not everyone can work miracles.
translation_verse: Pɔ banɔkpɛkpɛ balɛ petee enuɔ dza? Ebuiɖɔluanɔ balɛ petee enuɔ dza? Ebui gɛ labhɛtɛ kibì balɛ petee enuɔ dza?

english_verse: Not everyone can heal the sick. Not everyone can speak different kinds of languages. Not everyone can tell what these languages mean.
translation_verse: Balɛ nyɔ̃ɔ bakɔ bɔnyã tibubu ɛdɔ̃ kɔkɔɔ dza? Balɛ nyɔ̃ɔ ehu beluhũ vɔvɔ dza? Balɛ nyɔ̃ɔ balɛ ebu beluhũ vɔvɔ niesiɛ dza?

english_verse: I want you to desire the best gifts. So I will show you a much better way.
translation_verse: Pɔ nɔpɛ kɛdɔ̃ kɔkɔ gigboglo ɔdɛ. Kɛlɛ otsiɛnɔ ɛbakasɛ wɔnɔ kidzoɔ gɛ pɛ ɔdɛ.

english_verse: What if I could speak all languages of humans and even of angels? If I did not love others, I would be nothing more than a noisy gong or a clanging cymbal.
translation_verse: Onupɛ ihu beluhũ vɔvɔ petee gɛ banɔdzyrameɛ nɛ̀ baxɔ̃ɛdɛ tsyɛ ehuɔ, pɛ tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ nɛ̀ anɔ ama mɛahɔɛ, kɛlɛ mɛ kaɖɔ ɖɔɖɔ edzi asɛ ɔgã alo kesipɛ̃ kpá gɛ kalɛ tiyiyi pɔɛ banɔ atɔ̃ mɛ ana.

english_verse: What if I could prophesy and understand all mysteries and all knowledge? And what if I had faith that moved mountains? I would be nothing, unless I loved others.
translation_verse: Onupɛ kibuiɖɔlu kɛdɔ̃ kɔkɔ gɛbalà ebui kpukpũ nɛ bubutì ebui bukasɛ lɛ mɛahɔɛ, kɛlɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã gɛbalà betu akpa ebu lɛ mɛahɔɛ, pɛ tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ ma mɛahɔɛ elo, itinu kɛdɔ̃ nanà.

english_verse: What if I gave away all that I owned and let myself be burned alive? I would gain nothing, unless I loved others.
translation_verse: Onupɛ ɛlɔkɔ mɛ bɔdɔ̃ petee gɛlɛ mɛahɔɛ kɔ́ bahɔɛkpà eleteɛ pɛ ɛlɔkɔ mɛishu kɔ bapɔɛ awɔwɔɔ tsyɛ, pɛ tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ ɔmá mɛahɔɛ, ɛtɛba mɔ kɛdɔ̃ nanà.

english_verse: rude. Love isn't selfish or quick tempered. It doesn't keep a record of wrongs that others do.
translation_verse: Tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ tɛbhɛtɛ̀ awuĩ ebui, tɛtɛ pɛ mɛ ete yɛ tɛpɛpɛ, tɛtɛ kpɛ ɔmɔpɔɛ nɛ̀ anɔ, tɛtɛ balɛ ebui kpa lu ɔkɔ anɔ.

english_verse: Love rejoices in the truth, but not in evil.
translation_verse: Tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ tɛkpɛ same nɛ kibui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ shu, pɔ tɛ kpɛ samɛ nɛ kibui gbã shu.

english_verse: Love is always supportive, loyal, hopeful, and trusting.
translation_verse: Tɛplɛnɔ anɔ, tɛkɔ bayɛkɛ anɔ abha aŋã, tiɖi kidzo mɛ ɔkɔ kibui gbangbã, tɛkpɛ kemi.

english_verse: Love never fails! Everyone who prophesies will stop, and unknown languages will no longer be spoken. All that we know will be forgotten.
translation_verse: Tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ tɛ wɔɛ anɔ. Pɔ ɛkɛlɛ gɛ ebuiɖɔlu ebuiɛ alɛ, alɛ ulũ bafala, ɛkɛlɛ gɛ beluhũ vɔvɔ huhu alɛɛ, alɛ ulũ bazɔkɔ yɔlɔ, ɛkɛlɛ gɛ kibuititì tsyɛ lɛɛ, kɛlɛ ulũ bafala.

english_verse: We don't know everything, and our prophecies are not complete.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ ebui bhlui ete bunyi, kɛlɛ ebui kawɔ̃pɛ ete bɔɖɔ lu,

english_verse: But what is perfect will someday appear, and what isn't perfect will then disappear.
translation_verse: pɔ onupɛ ebuiɛ gɛaná ebekoeyìɛ, agɛ agɛna abayalà.

english_verse: When we were children, we thought and reasoned as children do. But when we grew up, we quit our childish ways.
translation_verse: Kibeɛ gɛ idzi anuvɔ, ɛzɛɖɔ kaɖɔ asɛ anuvɔ ana, ɛzɛ bhɛtɛ mɛebui tɔtɔɖɔ asɛ anuvɔ ana, ɛzɛ bhɛtɛ mɛ ekpeɖekpeɖe mɛ tsyɛ asɛ anuvɔ ana. Kibeɛ gɛ ɛbadzì anɔ sheshèɛ, ɛshɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ bunuvɔme ebuiɛ shu lu.

english_verse: Now all we can see of God is like a cloudy picture in a mirror. Later we will see him face to face. We don't know everything, but then we will, just as God completely understands us.
translation_verse: Otsi buɖi ebuikukuɔ asɛ ahuhɔ̃ɛ gɛeɖì ɔlɔ pɛ bɔlɔ tsyɛ iɖì yɛanà, kɛlɛ bɔbàɖi bamɛ nɛ̀ bamɛ. Otsiɛ ebuiɛ kawɔ̃pɛ ete mɛinyi, kɛlɛ ɛbàtì alɛ petee, asɛ kana gɛ benyi mɛtsyɛ ana.

english_verse: For now there are faith, hope, and love. But of these three, the greatest is love.
translation_verse: Kliso otsiɛ nɔ ebui tatá alɛ alalɛ, tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã, kidzo mɛɖiɖi nɛ̀ tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ. Pɔ kɛgɛ gbogbolo ɔdɛ nɛ alɛ nɛishiɛ kɛlɛinu tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ.

english_verse: Love should be your guide. Be eager to have the gifts that come from the Holy Spirit, especially the gift of prophecy.
translation_verse: Nɔtɔnɔ tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ kidzoɔ ede gagalĩ, tɛ̀ nɔpɛ ɔfɔfuĩa mɛ bɔdɔ̃ kɔkɔ, asɛ kibuiɖɔlu bɔdɔ̃ kɔkɔ ana.

english_verse: If you speak languages that others don't know, God will understand what you are saying, though no one else will know what you mean. You will be talking about mysteries that only the Spirit understands.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ anɔka nyia ka gɛ ehu beluhũ vɔvɔ, gɛnɛ banɔ yɛehu bɔlɛ akɔ pɔ Owusoso Akaa. Kibui gbã, ananà etielũ kɛgɛ yɛaɖɔ niesi, aɖɔ ebui gɛ labhɛtɛ kibì nɛ̀ yɛ ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ ohumi.

english_verse: But when you prophesy, you will be understood, and others will be helped. They will be encouraged and made to feel better.
translation_verse: Pɔ anɔka nyia ka gɛ aɖɔ kibui elu xɔnɔ, aɖɔaɖɔ akɔ banɔ sɛ tɛ̀ akpɛ balɛ ohumi, tɛ̀ akpɛ balɛ kemi nɛ̀ tɛ ayɔ balɛ ishumɛ.

english_verse: By speaking languages that others don't know, you help only yourself. But by prophesying you help everyone in the church.
translation_verse: Anunɔ gɛ ehu beluhũvɔvɔ xɔnɔ, yɛishu ete shu yɛabhɔ ewunɔ kabha, pɔ anunɔ gɛ aɖɔ kibui elu xɔnɔɔ, ɔhaa yɛabhɔ ewunɔ kabha.

english_verse: I am glad for you to speak unknown languages, although I prefer that you would prophesy. In fact, prophesying does much more good than speaking unknown languages, unless someone can help the church by explaining what you mean.
translation_verse: Ɛbapɛ sɛ tɛ̀ wɔnɔ mɛ aka nyia aka ezehu beluhũ vɔvɔ, pɔ kɛbapɛ ɔkɔ mɛ ɔdɛ gɛ onupɛ nɔɖɔ kibui olu boŋ. Anunɔ gɛ aɖɔ kibui elùɔ egbogblo adɛ anunɔ gɛ eehu beluhũ vɔvɔ, ɔdɛ ɔsɛ ebu kɛgɛ yɛaɖɔ niesi sɛ tɛ̀ alɔkɔ abhɔ ɔhaa ewunɔ kabha nɔ.

english_verse: My friends, what good would it do, if I came and spoke unknown languages to you and didn't explain what I meant? How would I help you, unless I told you what God had shown me or gave you some knowledge or prophecy or teaching?
translation_verse: Mɛadzya alɛ, otsiɛnɔ onupɛ ɛba wɔnɔ ɔbhà pɛ ihu beluhũ vɔvɔɔ, kɛgbangbã ɖuwoe mɛbadzì ɔkɔ wɔnɔ, ɔdɛ ɔsɛ ɛmanà wɔnɔ kibui tili gɛ bebu akasɛ mɛ alo kibuititì alo kibui gɛ baɖɔ eluɔ alo kibui gɛ baɖɔ alɔkɔ akpalà banɔ nɔ dza?

english_verse: If all musical instruments sounded alike, how would you know the difference between a flute and a harp?
translation_verse: Nɛ bɔdɔ̃ gɛ ɛkpã ma bɔlɛ mɛ, pɛ bɔɔkpɛ ozĩ asɛ baabe nɛ̀ sangũ tsyɛ, afana gɛbetì ɔdzɛ nɔnɔ gɛ beebhoɔ onupɛ iliɛmɛ ediedi gibu ɔdzɛ kasɛ gikoeyiɛ dza?

english_verse: If a bugle call isn't clear, how would you know to get ready for battle?
translation_verse: Kidzo ɛlɛ nabha, onupɛ kexoele nɔ tsyɛ oliɛmɛ ɔgɛ dalɛ, afana gɛ aba balɛ asɛ avɛ keyuɔ dza?

english_verse: This is how it is when you speak unknown languages. If no one can understand what you are talking about, you will only be talking to the wind.
translation_verse: Kana kibui ɛlɛ ɛlɛ wɔnɔ kawɔ̃pɛ mɛ. Odiyinɔ sɛ nɔlɔkɔ kinekeɛ ɖɔ bubutì ebui nɔ, afana gɛ ebelũ kɛgɛ nɔɖɔ niesiɛ dza? Kɛlɛɛ́ nɔ mɛ ete wɔbɔzɔɖɔ kaɖɔ kpɛ xɔɔ.

english_verse: But if I don't understand the language that someone is using, we will be like foreigners to each other.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ onupɛ igilũ kibui tili esi gɛ anɔ teli aɖɔɔ, efu ete mɛinu ɔkɔ agɛ yɛaɖɔaɖɔ, kɛlɛ yɛtsyɛ enu efu akɔ mɛ ana.

english_verse: If you really want spiritual gifts, choose the ones that will be most helpful to the church.
translation_verse: Kana kibui ɛlɛ ɛlɛ wɔnɔ kawɔ̃pɛ mɛ. Gɛ nɔpɛ ɔsɛ tɛ̀ nɔyɛkɛ ɔfɔfuĩa mɛ bɔdɔ̃ kɔkɔ soɔ, nɔkpɛ kemi tɛ̀ wu kabha nɛ bɔdɔ̃kɔkɔ nɔnɔ gɛ bɔbà bhɔ ɔhaa wunɔ kabha mɛ.

english_verse: When we speak languages that others don't know, we should pray for the power to explain what we mean.
translation_verse: Nɛ kibui ɛlɛshu soɔ, anunɔ gɛ yɛehu beluhũvɔvɔ, ekulu Owusoso Akaa sɛ tɛ̀ alabù kɛgɛ yɛaɖɔ niesi.

english_verse: For example, if I use an unknown language in my prayers, my spirit prays but my mind is useless.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ onupɛ ɛɖɔ kaɖɔ nɛ beluhũvɔvɔ mɛ, mɛ ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ ɔlɔ kulu Owusoso Akaa, pɔ mɛ ekpeɖekpeɖeɛ xɔnɔ atɛamɔ eyuyù nanà denɔ kɛlɛ mɛ.

english_verse: Then what should I do? There are times when I should pray with my spirit, and times when I should pray with my mind. Sometimes I should sing with my spirit, and at other times I should sing with my mind.
translation_verse: Kliso kiɖe mɛbabhɛtɛ dza? Ɛbalɔkɔ mɛ ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ kulu Owusoso Akaa nɔ, kɛlɛ iwlui bakulù Owusoso Akaa nɛ̀ mɛ ekpeɖekpeɖeɛ tsyɛ, ɛbazhã ɔdzɛ nɛ̀ mɛ ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ, kɛlɛ iwlui bazhã ɔdzɛ nɛ̀ mɛ ekpeɖekpeɖeɛ tsyɛ.

english_verse: Suppose some strangers are in your worship service, when you are praising God with your spirit. If they don't understand you, how will they know to say, “Amen”?
translation_verse: Onupɛ ɔɔkpɛ̀ Owusoso Akaa nɛinye nɛitití nɛ wɔ ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ, kiɖe anunɔ gɛ akpasɛ bagɛ begilũ kɛliesi mɛ abaɖɔ sɛ “Amen” ɔkɔ ahɔɛ gɛ ohuɔ, nɛ kibeɛ gɛ egilũ kibui nɔnɔ gɛ kɛlɛ wɔɖɔ niesiɛ dza?

english_verse: You may be worshiping God in a wonderful way, but no one else will be helped.
translation_verse: Obozohu ahɔɛ piapii xɔnɔ, pɔ ɔgɛbhɔ anɔbhla xɔnɔ ewunɔ kabha.

english_verse: I thank God that I speak unknown languages more than any of you.
translation_verse: Ihu Owusoso Akaa ahɔɛ ɔsɛ iihu beluhũ vɔvɔ ɔdɛ wɔnɔ nyɔ̃ɔ.

english_verse: But words that make sense can help the church. This is why in church I would rather speak five words that make sense than to speak
translation_verse: Pɔ nɛ ɔhaa xɔnɔ mɛɛ, ɛbapɛ sɛ tɛ̀ mɛɖɔ bubutì ebui teti tɛ̀ ɛlɔkɔ kpalà bagɔgɔɛ nɔ nɔ ɔdɛ ebui ihotù lɔfɔ ɖɔɖɔ nɛ beluhũ vɔvɔ mɛ.

english_verse: My friends, stop thinking like children. Think like mature people and be as innocent as tiny babies.
translation_verse: Mɛadzya alɛ, nɔyɔlɔ ekpeɖekpeɖe mɛ tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ asɛ bunuvɔɛ ana. Nɛ ebui kpa tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ shu xɔnɔ, nɔzã asɛ bunuvɔɛ ana, pɔ nɛ wɔnɔ ekpeɖekpeɖe bhɛbhɛtɛ mɛ xɔnɔ nɔzã asɛ banɔ sheshè ana.

english_verse: In the Scriptures the Lord says, “I will use strangers who speak unknown languages to talk to my people. They will speak to them in foreign languages, but still my people won't listen to me.”
translation_verse: Nɛ ɔshamɛ, bafɔnɔ elú asɛ, “Ɔgana beefu gɛ beehu beluhũ vɔvɔ abhaa, kɛlɛ odenɔ beefu olugbũɔ mɛ ɛbaɖɔaɖɔ ɔkɔ banɔ alɛ, pɔ nɛ kɛlɛ mɛ tsyɛ, batɛbà taatɔ̃ nɛ mɛ,” kana Apaleteɛ aɖɔ.

english_verse: Languages others don't know may mean something to unbelievers, but not to the Lord's followers. Prophecy, on the other hand, is for followers, not for unbelievers.
translation_verse: Beluhũvɔvɔ xɔnɔ, kibui bubukasɛ kinu, gɛnɛ kɔ bagɛ ayɛkɛ aŋã, pɔ ɔkɔ bagɛ agɛ yɛkɛ aŋã, kibuiɖɔlu xɔnɔ, kɛlɛ ɔkɔ bagɛ ayɛkɛ aŋãa, gɛnɛ bagɛ agɛ yɛkɛŋãa.

english_verse: Suppose everyone in your worship service started speaking unknown languages, and some outsiders or some unbelievers come in. Won't they think you are crazy?
translation_verse: Kliso onupɛ ɔhaa petee ebeshogòɔ pɛ aka nyia aka ehu beluhũvɔvɔ, pɛ bagɛ betielũ kɛlɛ esiɛ, alo pɛ bagɛ agɛ yɛkɛŋãa teli ebekoeyìɛ pɔ, batɛ baɖɔ sɛ nɔmà piapii dza?

english_verse: But suppose all of you are prophesying when those unbelievers and outsiders come in. They will realize that they are sinners, and they will want to change their ways because of what you are saying.
translation_verse: Pɔ onupɛ anɔ gɛ agɛ yɛkɛŋã, alo anunɔ gɛ etielũ buluhũ niesi ebekoeyì nɛ kibeɛ gɛ anɔ ka nyia ka aaɖɔ kibui elu xɔnɔɔ, ebelũ ɛlɛ esi ɔsɛ ebui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ elete yɛenu kɛlɛ babalɔkɔ ebui alɛ petee ekulu kebhu nii nɔ̀,

english_verse: They will tell what is hidden in their hearts. Then they will kneel down and say to God, “We are certain that you are with these people.” Worship Must Be Orderly
translation_verse: kɛlɛ ebui kpukpũɔ gɛ akpasɛ yɛitúɔ mɛ ebebu alɛishu kasɛ. Klisoɔ ebezhi nì ekluisi esi pɛ abakpɛ Owusoso Akaa nɛitití nɛ̀ ozĩ asɛ, “Owusoso Akaa akpasɛ wɔnɔɛishi kɛgbã!” Kidzo Nabha Gɛ Bakpɛ̀ Kɛtakpu esi

english_verse: My friends, when you meet to worship, you must do everything for the good of everyone there. That's how it should be when someone sings or teaches or tells what God has said or speaks an unknown language or explains what the language means.
translation_verse: Mɛadzya alɛ mɔ, kiɖe buwlui zɛbaɖɔ dza? Onupɛ nobo shogòɔ, ɔdzɛ zhazhã ɔlɛ aka nyia aka ahɔɛ alo kibui gɛ balɔkɔ akpalà anɔ, kibui bubu kasɛ, beluhũ vɔvɔ tihuhu nɛ̀ bɔlɛ esi bubu lɛ aka nyia aka ahɔɛ. Kɛlɛ sɛ babhɛtɛ alɛ nyɔ̃ɔ tɛ ledzi ohumi kpɛkpɛ ɔkɔ ɔhaa.

english_verse: No more than two or three of you should speak unknown languages during the meeting. You must take turns, and someone should always be there to explain what you mean.
translation_verse: Onupɛ anɔ teli aɖɔaɖɔ nɛ beluhũ vɔvɔ mɛɛ, banɔ taabha, alo pɛ bekpè ana tataa-betihu buluhũɔ nɛ kibè tinikpo mɛ, kɛlɛ tɛ̀ anɔ bhlatɔ ebu kɛlɛ esi.

english_verse: If no one can explain, you must keep silent in church and speak only to yourself and to God.
translation_verse: Onupɛ agɛ yɛebebu kɛlɛ esi ama, anunɔ gɛ yɛaɖɔaɖɔ ataatɔ̃ nɛ ɔhaa mɛ tɛ̀ aɖɔaɖɔ ɔkɔ yɛishu nɛ̀ yɛ Owusoso Akaa ete.

english_verse: Two or three persons may prophesy, and everyone else must listen carefully.
translation_verse: Ebuiɖɔluanɛ asɛ taabha alo tata baɖɔaɖɔ, kɛlɛ tɛ̀ bagɔgɔ banyɔ kibuiɛ gɛ kɛlɛ yɛaɖɔ shu piapii.

english_verse: If someone sitting there receives a message from God, the speaker must stop and let the other person speak.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ onupɛ bebu kibui tili kasɛ anunɔ gɛ edzi kesiɛ, anɔ gɛiputɔ gɛ yɛaɖɔaɖɔ azɔkɔ yɔlɔ.

english_verse: Let only one person speak at a time, then all of you will learn something and be encouraged.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ wɔnɔ nyɔ̃ɔ ɔbɔlà ɖɔ kibui olu, sɛ tɛ̀ aka nyia aka amɔ kibui denɔ kɛlɛ mɛ plɛnɔ kemikpɛkpɛ.

english_verse: A prophet should be willing to stop and let someone else speak.
translation_verse: Ebuiɖɔluanɛ ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ ɔkpasɛ ebuiɖɔluanɛ niesi.

english_verse: God wants everything to be done peacefully and in order. When God's people meet in church,
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ Owusoso Akaa etinu Akaa gɛ efutsù pɔ ishu yɔyɔ anɔ yɛenu. Asɛ gɛ kɛlɛ banɔ dadalɛ ɔha petee mɛ ana,

english_verse: the women must not be allowed to speak. They must keep quiet and listen, as the Law of Moses teaches.
translation_verse: baadzɛ ataatɔ̃ nɛ ɔhaa mɛ. Bagɛ kɔ balɛidzo asɛ tɛ̀ baɖɔaɖɔ, pɔ bezhinì balɛ ishu esi, asɛ gɛ Ɔsha ɔɖɔ ana.

english_verse: If there is something they want to know, they can ask their husbands when they get home. It is disgraceful for women to speak in church.
translation_verse: Onupɛ bapɛ asɛ tɛ̀ bebisi kibui tili mɛelũɔ, bebisi balɛ enyine alɛ nɛ bɔpã mɛ, ɔmɔsɛ kinu kiwũi ɔkɔ anɔdzɛ asɛ tɛ̀ aɖɔaɖɔ nɛ ɔha mɛ.

english_verse: God's message did not start with you people, and you are not the only ones it has reached.
translation_verse: Wɔnɔ ɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa kibuiɛ de baa dza? Pɛnɔsɛ wɔnɔ ete ɔkɔ kɛtɛba kuɔ dza?

english_verse: If you think of yourself as a prophet or a spiritual person, you will know I am writing only what the Lord has commanded.
translation_verse: Onupɛ abhɛtɛ anɔ teli asɛ enu ebuiɖɔluanɔ alo ayɛkɛ ɔfɔfuĩa mɛ kɛdɔ̃ kɔkɔɔ, nɔkɔ yɛetì asɛ kibuiɛ gɛ ɛfɔnɔ kɔ wɔnɔ nu Apaleteɛ ɔsha kpɛkpɛ.

english_verse: So don't pay attention to anyone who ignores what I am writing.
translation_verse: Onupɛ agɛ yɛkɛ kibui ɛlɛ, batɛbà yɛkɛ yɛtsyɛ.

english_verse: My friends, be eager to prophesy and don't stop anyone from speaking languages that others don't know.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ, mɛadzya alɛ mɔ, nɔkpɛ kemi tɛ̀ nɔɖɔ kibui elú, kɛlɛ nɔtɛ zɔkɔ kaɖɔɖɔ ɔyɔlɔ nɛ beluhũ vɔvɔ mɛ tsyɛ.

english_verse: My friends, I want you to remember the message I preached and that you believed and trusted.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ otsiɛnɔ mɛadzya alɛ, ɛpɛsɛ bakà wɔnɔ nɛ kibui gbangbã gɛ ɛɖɔ tseɖe wɔnɔ shu, kibuiɛ gɛ nɔyɛkɛ pɛ nɔotsiɖi kɛlɛ abha.

english_verse: You will be saved by this message, if you hold firmly to it. But if you don't, your faith was all for nothing.
translation_verse: Ɔgana kibui gbangbã ɛlɛ mɛ bebú wɔnɔ ɛkpã, gɛ onupɛ nɔnyanã kibuiɛ gɛ itseɖe wɔnɔ mɛ vunɔ gagalĩ. Onu gɛnanaa, wɔnɔ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã ma kɛnanà mɛ.

english_verse: I told you the most important part of the message exactly as it was told to me. This part is: Christ died for our sins, as the Scriptures say.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ kɛgɛ mɛyɛkɛ kɛlɛ mɛtsyɛ etseɖe wɔnɔ asɛ kibui gɛiputɔ kɛdzã kiwuhũ ana, sɛ Kristoɔ ebetsí nɛ bɔlɔ ebui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ nɛtakpu nɛ ebuiɛ gɛ bafɔnɔ nulũ,

english_verse: He was buried, and three days later he was raised to life, as the Scriptures say.
translation_verse: kɛlɛ bavɔbɔ yɛ, kɛlɛ befeke yɛ nɛ kiwui tramɛ nabha nɛ ebuiɛ gɛ bafɔnɔ nulũ,

english_verse: Christ appeared to Peter, then to the twelve.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ ebu yɛishu akasɛ Petro nɛ̀ banɔ lɔfɔ nɛ̀ taabha.

english_verse: After this, he appeared to more than five hundred other followers. Most of them are still alive, but some have died.
translation_verse: Nɛ kɛlɛ niedeɛ, ebu yɛishu akasɛ badzya gɛ bekpè adɛ akpã teti nɛ kibè tinikpo mɛ, beeteli nɛ balɛ mɛ akalɛ nɛkpã, pɔ beteli xɔnɔ etitsí.

english_verse: He also appeared to James, then to all of the apostles.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ ebu yɛishu akasɛ Yakobo, nɛ̀ banɔkpɛkpɛ petee,

english_verse: Finally, he appeared to me, even though I am like someone who was born at the wrong time.
translation_verse: kɛlɛ gɔgɔɛ nɔ ebebú yɛishu akasɛ mɛ gɛ bamɔ asɛ anɔ gɛ agɛzã piapii ana.

english_verse: I am the least important of all the apostles. In fact, I caused so much trouble for God's church that I don't even deserve to be called an apostle.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ mɛinù banɔkpɛkpɛ balɛ agɔgɔɛ nɔ gɛ ɛgɛ shɔnɔ ɔsɛ bekulu mɛ asɛ anɔkpɛkpɛ flogo, ɔmɔsɛ ɛzɛtɔnɔ Owusoso Akaa yɛɔhaa niede.

english_verse: But God treated me with undeserved grace! He made me what I am, and his grace wasn't wasted. I worked much harder than any of the other apostles, although it was really God's grace at work and not me.
translation_verse: Pɔ nɛ kana gɛ Owusoso Akaa ebhui mɛ ebuɔ shu soɔ, idzi kɛdɔ̃ nɔnɔ gɛ kɛlɛ mɛinu oboɔ, kɛlɛ yɛ tibhui bhuibuɔ wɔa axɔ̃ɛ. Oo, ɛwɔa axɔ̃ɛ gagalĩ ɔdɛ balɛ petee, pɔ gɛnɛ mɛ, Owusoso Akaa tibhui bhuibuɔ kɛlɛ zã ɔplɛnɔ mɛ so.

english_verse: But it doesn't matter if I preached or if they preached. All of you believed the message just the same. God's People Will Be Raised to Life
translation_verse: Kidzi asɛ mɛ alo balɛ tsyɛ, kibui ɛlɛ bɔɔɖɔ, kɛlɛ kibui ɛlɛ abha nɔyɛkɛ ɔŋã. Bɔnɔ tsitsíɛ Tɛlɔlɔ

english_verse: If we preach that Christ was raised from death, how can some of you say the dead will not be raised to life?
translation_verse: Pɔ onupɛ baɖɔ kibuiɛ sɛ befeke Kristoɔ edenɔ bekpuɔ mɛ, kiɖuwoe so pɛ wɔnɔ beteli ɔbɔɖɔ sɛ tɛlɔlɔ denɔ bekpumɛ ma dza?

english_verse: If they won't be raised to life, Christ himself wasn't raised to life.
translation_verse: Onupɛ banɔ tsitsiɛ tɛlɔlɔ ma, kɛlɛ begi feke kristoɔ tsyɛ edenɔ bekpuɔ mɛ.

english_verse: And if Christ wasn't raised to life, our message is worthless, and so is your faith.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ onupɛ begi feke Kristoɔ tsyɛ edenɔ bekpuɔ mɛ, kɛlɛ bɔlɔ kibui ɖɔɖɔ nɛ̀ wɔnɔ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã ma kɛnanà mɛ.

english_verse: If the dead won't be raised to life, we have told lies about God by saying he raised Christ to life, when he really did not.
translation_verse: Ɔlɔkɔ ɔplɛnɔ ɔdɛ ana, budzì banɔ gɛ bɔɖɔ kɛsɛnɛ ibui nɛ Owusoso Akaa shu. Ɔmɔsɛ bɔɖɔ kaɖɔ nɛ Owusoso Akaa shu sɛ efeke Kristo edenɔ bekpuɔ mɛ. Pɔ egifeke yɛ odenɔ bekpuɔ mɛ, gɛgbã bekpuɔ azalɔ.

english_verse: So if the dead won't be raised to life, Christ wasn't raised to life.
translation_verse: Pɔ onupɛ bagɛ kɔ bekpuɔ alɔɔ, kɛlɛ begi feke Kristoɔ tsyɛ edenɔ bekpuɔ mɛ.

english_verse: Unless Christ was raised to life, your faith is useless, and you are still living in your sins.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ onupɛ begi feke Kristoɔ denɔ bekpuɔ mɛɛ, wɔ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã ma kɛnanà mɛ, nɔkɔ kpasɛ wɔnɔ ebui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ mɛ.

english_verse: And those people who died after putting their faith in him are completely lost.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ banɔ nɔ nyɔ̃ɔ gɛ betsí nɛ Kristoɔ mɛ tsyɛ ayala.

english_verse: If our hope in Christ is good only for this life, we are worse off than anyone else.
translation_verse: Onupɛ kanɔ alɛ mɛ ete shu so pɛ bulũ awɔ nɛ Kristo abhaa, kɛlɛ bunù ashagabui elete ɔdɛ anɔnyianɔ.

english_verse: But Christ has been raised to life! And he makes us certain that others will also be raised to life.
translation_verse: Pɔ kibui gbã kinu ɔsɛ befeke Kristoɔ edenɔ bekpuɔ mɛ, yɛedzì anɔ gɛiputɔ gɛ alɔ akɔ bagɛ betsiɛ petee.

english_verse: Just as we will die because of Adam, we will be raised to life because of Christ.
translation_verse: Pɔ asɛ kana gɛ butsì gã anɔ abha baa, tɛlɔlɔ denɔ bekpuɔ mɛ tsyɛ gã anɔ abha ba.

english_verse: Adam brought death to all of us, and Christ will bring life to all of us.
translation_verse: Asɛ kana gɛ denɔ Adam mɛ bɔlɔ nyɔ̃ɔ tsiɛɛ, kidzo ɛlɛ nabha gana Kristo abha banɔ nɔ nyɔ̃ɔ tsyɛ bamɔ ɛkpã.

english_verse: But we must each wait our turn. Christ was the first to be raised to life, and his people will be raised to life when he returns.
translation_verse: Pɔ anɔ nyia anɔ nɛ yɛibè gɛ batɔɖɔ akɔɛ. Kristo gɛenu anɔ gɛiputɔ, gɛ onupɛ etsodzi aba ebefeke bagɛ benu yɛayɛ.

english_verse: Then after Christ has destroyed all powers and forces, the end will come, and he will give the kingdom to God the Father.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛɛ́ kibè gɔgɔɔ babà, gɛ onupɛ ashɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ bukusi ŋaŋã shu akɔ Owusoso Akaa gɛenu Yɛakaa nɛ kibeɛ gɛ ayɛɛ bukusiɛ petee, ɔshaeleteɛ nɛ̀ ohumi balɛ bukusi ɔŋãkɔ.

english_verse: Christ will rule until he puts all his enemies under his power,
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ kɛlɛ sɛ tɛ̀ aŋã bukusi ɔvɛnɔ kibeɛ gɛ edzu ɔtsɛɖɛ nɛ bagɛ batɛ adɛmɛ yɛibuiɛ petee abha.

english_verse: and the last enemy he destroys will be death.
translation_verse: Kɛdɔ̃ kpa gɔgɔɛ nɔ gɛ ebezĩɛ kɛlɛinu butsí.

english_verse: When the Scriptures say he will put everything under his power, they don't include God. It was God who put everything under the power of Christ.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ “atɛkpɛ kɛka nyia ka nɛ yɛtsɛɖɛ esi.” Otsiɛnɔ onupɛ kibuiɛ ɖɔ sɛ bakpɛ “kɛka nyia ka” nɛ yɛesiɛ, kɛlɛ, kɛlɛ mɛ dalɛ ɔsɛ Owusoso Akaa gɛ akpɛ kɛka nyia ka nɛ Kristo esiɛ ama kɛlɛ mɛ.

english_verse: After everything is under the power of God's Son, he will put himself under the power of God, who put everything under his Son's power. Then God will mean everything to everyone.
translation_verse: Onupɛ abhɛtɛ ɛlɛ ana, kɛlɛ Yɛeyianyɛ akpɛ kɛka nyia ka nɛ yɛesiɛ abazã yɛesi, sɛ tɛ̀ Owusoso Akaa edzì kɛtakpu akɔ kɛka nyia ka.

english_verse: If the dead are not going to be raised to life, what will people do who are being baptized for them? Why are they being baptized for those dead people?
translation_verse: Kliso otsiɛ onupɛ tɛlɔlɔ denɔ bekpumɛ kɛma, kɛlɛ so pɛ bakpɛ bulĩ akɔ alɛ nɛ banɔ tsitsíɛ nɛtakpu? Onupɛ begi feke bekpuɔ, kɛlɛ so pɛ bakpɛ bulĩ akɔ alɛ?

english_verse: and face death every day? The pride that I have in you because of Christ Jesus our Lord is what makes me say this.
translation_verse: Ɛtɔnɔ butsí kibe nyia be-mɛadzya alɛ kibui gbã mɛɖɔ-asɛ kana gɛ ɛmɔ same nɛ wɔnɔ kɛtakpu nɛ bɔlɔ Apalete Yesu Kristo mɛ.

english_verse: What do you think I gained by fighting wild animals in Ephesus? If the dead are not raised to life, “Let's eat and drink. Tomorrow we die.”
translation_verse: Onupɛ ɛta keyu nɛ̀ bagãhoe nɛ Efeso nɛ anɔdzyrame ebui shu tsyɛ elo, kiɖe mɛmɔ odenɔ ɛlɛ mɛ? Gɛ onupɛ begifeke bekpuɔ, “Nɔkɔ bɔŋã tɛ̀ bɔwɔɛ, ɔmɔsɛ kivũ ko butsi.”

english_verse: Don't fool yourselves. Bad friends will destroy you.
translation_verse: Nɔtɛkɔ bawɔɛ wɔnɔ, “Ɔhakpa tɛkpɛkpɛ zĩ ɛkpã zazãna gbangbã.”

english_verse: Be sensible and stop sinning. You should be embarrassed that some people still don't know about God. What Our Bodies Will Be Like
translation_verse: Nɔotsodzi ɔba wɔnɔ ekpeɖekpeɖe mɛ asɛ gɛ kɛlɛ sɛ nɔbhɛtɛ, tɛ̀ nɔyɔlɔ ebui kpa tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ, ɔmɔsɛ banɔ teli etinyi asɛ Owusoso Akaa alɛ-ɛɖɔ kibui ɛlɛ kpɛ wɔnɔ kiwũi nɔ. Tɛlɔlɔ Denɔ Bekpuɔ Mɛ

english_verse: Some of you have asked, “How will the dead be raised to life? What kind of bodies will they have?”
translation_verse: Pɔ anɔ teli xɔnɔ ebebisì asɛ, “Kidzo ɖuwoe abha befeke bekpuɔ dza? Ishupi ɖuwoe edì babà lɔkɔ amana dza?”

english_verse: Don't be foolish. A seed must die before it can sprout from the ground.
translation_verse: Bɔga kibui ɖuwoe di nɛ ɔlɛ! Kɛnanà ma gɛ ɔɖa pɛ kɛgi tsiɛ pɛ kɛbà zã nɛkpã.

english_verse: Wheat seeds and all other seeds look different from the sprouts that come up.
translation_verse: Onupɛ nɔɖa kɛdɔ̃, nɔtɛɔ ɖa ishupiɛ gɛ bazã, pɔ kɛdɔ̃yi ete kinu, asɛ agɔ̃lɛ alo kɛdɔ̃ɛyi ka nyia ka ɛdi ana.

english_verse: This is because God gives everything the kind of body he wants it to have.
translation_verse: Pɔ Owusoso Akaa ɔkɔ ɛlɛ ishupi asɛ gɛ apɛ ana, kɛlɛ akɔ kɛdɔ̃ɛyi ka nyia ka nɛ̀ kɛlɛ ishupi.

english_verse: People, animals, birds, and fish are each made of flesh, but none of them are alike.
translation_verse: Ishupiɛ petee gɛshɔ̃, ishupi tinikpo ɛdi lɛ banɔdzyrame ahɔɛ, bagã tsyɛ ayɛkɛ bɔbhlatɔ ɛdi, bɔzhɔɛ nɔ tsyɛ yɛkɛ tuli idi kɛlɛ akpa nɔ tsyɛ ayɛkɛ bɔbhlatɔ.

english_verse: Everything in the heavens has a body, and so does everything on earth. But each one is very different from all the others.
translation_verse: Kititì ishupi lɛ, kɛlɛ kesiakpa abha ishupi tsyɛ lɛ, pɔ tɛpɔpɔɖiɛ gɛlɛ kititì ishupiɛ shuɔ nu tinikpo ɛdi, kɛlɛ tɛpɔpɔɖiɛ gɛlɛ kesiakpa abha ishupiɛ tsyɛ shuɔ nu bɔbhlatɔ.

english_verse: The sun isn't like the moon, the moon isn't like the stars, and each star is different.
translation_verse: Tɛpɔpɔɖi tili idi lɛ kiwuiɛ ahɔɛ, kɛlɛ tɛpɔpɔɖi bhlatɔ lɛ woele nɔ tsyɛ ahɔɛ nɛ̀ awɔɛ, kɛlɛ kɛwɔɛ gã ɔtɔ ɔkɔ kɛwɔɛ gɛ tɛpɔpɔɖi lɛ ɛlɛ ahɔɛ.

english_verse: That's how it will be when our bodies are raised to life. These bodies will die, but the bodies that are raised will live forever.
translation_verse: Kana kɛbazã nɛ tɛlɔlɔ denɔ bekpuɔ mɛ shu. Ishudzya gɛ baɖa nu kɛdɔ̃ tsɔtsɔmɔ̃, pɔ bebe feke ɛlɛ gɛ kɛzakà tsɔmɔ̃,

english_verse: As surely as there are physical bodies, there are spiritual bodies. And our physical bodies will be changed into spiritual bodies.
translation_verse: baɖa ɛlɛ asɛ ishudzya ana, pɔ befeke ɛlɛ nɛ ɔfɔfuĩa mɛ. Onupɛ ishudzya lɛɛ, kɛlɛ ɔfɔfuĩa tsyɛ ɔlɛ.

english_verse: The first man was named Adam, and the Scriptures tell us that he was a living person. But Jesus, who may be called the last Adam, is a life-giving spirit.
translation_verse: Asɛ gɛ bafɔnɔ elu asɛ, “anyɛ gɛiputɔ gɛedzì adam abazã nɛkpã”, Adam gɔgɔɛ nɔ yɛedzì-ɔfɔfuĩa gɛ lɔkɔ ɛkpã.

english_verse: We see that the one with a spiritual body did not come first. He came after the one who had a physical body.
translation_verse: Ɔfɔfuĩa mɛ anunɔ egiputɔ ba, pɔ ishupiɛ ɛlɛ putɔ flogo pɛ ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ ba.

english_verse: The first man was made from the dust of the earth, but the second man came from heaven.
translation_verse: Anyɛ gɛiputɔ edzì osubhududu odenɔ kesiakpa mɛ, kɛlɛ anyɛ abhalà ede kititì aba.

english_verse: Everyone on earth has a body like the body of the one who was made from the dust of the earth. And everyone in heaven has a body like the body of the one who came from heaven.
translation_verse: Kana gɛ kesiakpa abha anyɛ azã, kana bagɛ betsiɖì kesiakpa nabha tsyɛ alɛ, kɛlɛ asɛ kana gɛ kititì anyɛ edziɛ, kana bagɛ balɛ kititiɛ tsyɛ alɛ.

english_verse: Just as we are like the one who was made out of earth, we will be like the one who came from heaven.
translation_verse: Asɛ kana gɛ bamɔ bɔlɔ budzì asɛ kesiakpa abha anɔ nɔ̀ɔ, kana bɔbà zã asɛ anunɔ gɛ ede kititiɛ.

english_verse: My friends, I want you to know that our bodies of flesh and blood will decay. This means they cannot share in God's kingdom, which lasts forever.
translation_verse: Mɛadzya alɛ, ɛkɔ notì ɔsɛ ishupi ɛlɛ nɛ̀ dzyosu atɛba mɔ Owusoso Akaa bukusi bɔpã, pɛnɔsɛ kɛdɔ̃ gizĩɛ bamɔ ɔŋãkɔ odenɔ kɛdɔ̃ gitiziɛ ɔkɔ.

english_verse: I will explain a mystery to you. Not every one of us will die, but we will all be changed.
translation_verse: Nɔtaatɔ̃ tɛ̀ mɛitseɖe wɔnɔ kibui kpukpũ ɛlɛ. Bɔlɔ nyɔ̃ɔ tɛbàtsí, pɔ bebètsyina bɔlɔ nyɔ̃ɔ,

english_verse: It will happen suddenly, quicker than the blink of an eye. At the sound of the last trumpet the dead will be raised. We will all be changed, so we will never die again.
translation_verse: nɛ odunu mɛ, nɛ tɛwɔsɛ tɛdadã mɛ, gɛ babɔ̀ kexoele gɔgɔɛ nɔ. Ɔmɔsɛ kexoele nɔ abàbɔ, babà nyɛmɛ bekpuɔ gɛ bezeke tsì eɖi, kɛlɛ bebètsyina bɔlɔ.

english_verse: Our dead and decaying bodies will be changed into bodies that won't die or decay.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ kɛlɛ sɛ kɛdɔ̃ gɛbaziɛ lɔkɔ kɛgɛ tɛbàziɛ akpɛkpɛ akpɛ, kɛlɛ tɛ̀ ishupiɛ gɛbàtsɔmɔ lɔkɔ ɛgɛ tɛbatsɔmɔ akpɛkpɛ ɔkpɛ.

english_verse: The bodies we now have are weak and can die. But they will be changed into bodies that are eternal. Then the Scriptures will come true, “Death has lost the battle!
translation_verse: Onupɛ balɔkɔ ishupiɛ gɛtɛ bàtsɔmɔ akpɛkpɛ akpɛ akɔ ishupiɛ gɛbà tsɔmɔ̃ɔ, pɛ balɔkɔ ishupiɛ gɛbàzã tihɔɔ akpɛkpɛ akpɛ kɔ ishupiɛ gɛtɛ bàzã tihɔɔ nɔ, kɛlɛ kibuiɛ gɛ bafɔnɔ elúɔ babà kɛlɛ mɛ ɔsɛ, “Bamɛnɔ butsi edenɔ kabhaŋaŋã mɛ.”

english_verse: Where is its victory? Where is its sting?”
translation_verse: Oo butsí, fakɔ wɔ kabhaŋaŋã alɛɛ dza? Oo butsí, fakɔ kɛgɛ ɔlɔkɔ ɔtá banunɔ lɛ dza?

english_verse: Sin is what gives death its sting, and the Law is the power behind sin.
translation_verse: Kɛgɛ butsì lɔkɔ ɔtà banɔ nɔ kɛlɛinu kibui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ, kɛlɛ kibui kpa kɛlɛ ohumi nɔ ɔlɛ onù ɔsha.

english_verse: But thank God for letting our Lord Jesus Christ give us the victory!
translation_verse: Pɔ ahɔɛ huhu yɛenu Owusoso Akaa ayɛ! Yɛakɔ bɔlɔ kabhaŋaŋã ɔganà Apalete Yesu Kristo abha.

english_verse: My dear friends, stand firm and don't be shaken. Always keep busy working for the Lord. You know that everything you do for him is worthwhile.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ, mɛadzya alɛ mɔ, nɔzã gagalĩ. Nɔtɛkɔ kɛnanà tii wɔnɔ. Nɔlɔkɔ wɔnɔ ishu petee ɔkɔ Apalete nɛxɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa, ɔmɔsɛ nɔonyi ɔsɛ wɔnɔ kɛxɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa ɔkɔ Apaleteɛ tɛbàdzì kɛlɛɛ etee.

english_verse: When you collect money for God's people, I want you to do exactly what I told the churches in Galatia to do.
translation_verse: Otsiɛ nɛ bɔdɔ̃yɛyɛkɛ ɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa nɔ shuɔ. Nɔbhɛtɛ kɛgɛ itseɖe Galiatia ɔhaa asɛ babhɛtɛ̀.

english_verse: That is, each Sunday each of you must put aside part of what you have earned. If you do this, you won't have to take up a collection when I come.
translation_verse: Nɛ ɔgɔnɛ ka nyia ka ɔlɛ kiwui gɛiputɔ mɛ abhaa, anɔka nyia ka abalɛ ehoeyi teli elu edenɔ yɛɔdɔ mɔmɔ mɛ, azabalɛ alɛ elú, sɛ tɛ̀ onupɛ ɛbakuɔ bazaka yɛkɛ ehoeyi nɛ ananà ahɔɛ.

english_verse: Choose some followers to take the money to Jerusalem. I will send them on with the money and with letters which show that you approve of them.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ onupɛ ɛbakuɔ, ɛbakɔ bagɛ nɔobuɔ olúɔ bɔdɔ̃ kakasɛ buplukpa, kɛlɛ ɛbakpɛ balɛ tɛ balɔkɔ bɔdɔ̃ kɔkɔ avɛnɔ Yerusalem.

english_verse: If you think I should go along, they can go with me. Paul's Travel Plans
translation_verse: Onupɛ kɛpɛ ɔkɔ mɛ tsyɛ sɛ tɛ̀ ɛvɛ nɛ alɛ, babà gbana mɛ avɛnɔ. Kɛdɔ Bibisi Ɔkɔ Ishu

english_verse: After I have gone through Macedonia, I hope to see you
translation_verse: Onupɛ ɛgã koeyì nɛ Makedonia nɔ, ɛbaba wɔnɔ ɔkɔ, ɔmɔsɛ ɛdɛgã Makedonia mɛ koeyì.

english_verse: and visit with you for a while. I may even stay all winter, so that you can help me on my way to wherever I will be going next.
translation_verse: Ogilu awɔ̃ɔ ɛbazã wɔnɔ ɔbha ɔvɛnɔ kibè tili, alo ɛbazã wɔnɔ ɔbha nɛ subhà ɛdɔbe, sɛ tɛ̀ nɔlà plɛnɔ mɛ nɛ mɛidzo gagã mɛ nɛ ɔkɔ nyia ɔkɔ gɛ ɛvɛɛ.

english_verse: If the Lord lets me, I would rather come later for a longer visit than to stop off now for only a short visit.
translation_verse: Ɛgɛ pɛ sɛ tɛ̀ ɛmɔ wɔnɔ otsiɛ, tɛ̀ iɖi wɔnɔ ete tɛ̀ ɛfala, ɛbapɛ sɛ tɛ̀ ɛbazã wɔnɔ ɔbha kɛbɔɛ gɛ onupɛ Apaleteɛ adɛmɛ.

english_verse: I will stay in Ephesus until Pentecost,
translation_verse: Pɔ ɛbazã Efeso odiyinɔ Pentekoste kiwui nabha,

english_verse: because there is a wonderful opportunity for me to do some work here. But there are also many people who are against me.
translation_verse: ɔmɔsɛ kidzò gbagbla tili dalɛ ɔkɔ mɛxɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa, kɛlɛ banɔ kpekpè alɛ gɛ balɔɛtití nɛ mɛishu.

english_verse: When Timothy arrives, give him a friendly welcome. He is doing the Lord's work, just as I am.
translation_verse: Onupɛ Timoteo abaa, nɔoɖi ɛlɛ kɔ ɔsɛ etisì kifu nɛ kibui nanà shu, nɛ kibeɛ gɛ alɛ wɔnɔ ɔbha, ɔmɔsɛ awɔa Apalete nɛxɔ̃ɛ asɛ mɛ ana.

english_verse: Don't let anyone mistreat him. I am looking for him to return to me together with the other followers. So when he leaves, send him off with your blessings.
translation_verse: Ananà atɛɖɔ asɛ atɛa yɛkɛ yɛ. Nɔkpɛ yɛidzo sɛsɛ nɛ ishu yɔyɔ mɛ, sɛ tɛ̀ etsodzi aba mɛɔkɔ. Iɖì yɛ nɛ badzya idzomɛ.

english_verse: I have tried hard to get our friend Apollos to visit you with the other followers. He doesn't want to come just now, but he will come when he can. Personal Concerns and Greetings
translation_verse: Otsiɛ nɛ bɔlɔ adzya Apolo shuɔ, ɛkpalà yɛ gagalĩi sɛ avɛ nɛ̀ wɔ nɛ̀ badzyaa. Tsyɛ alo agɛ pɛ asɛ tɛ̀ avɛ otsiɛ flogo, pɔ abavɛ gɛ onupɛ kibeɛ kɔ kidzoɔ.

english_verse: Keep alert. Be firm in your faith. Stay brave and strong.
translation_verse: Nɔoɖi wɔnɔ shu abha piapii, nɔzã gagalĩ nɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛ, nɔodzi banyɛ gɛ bakpɛ̀ kemi, nɔzã gagalĩ.

english_verse: Show love in everything you do.
translation_verse: Nɔbhɛtɛ kɛka nyia ka nɛ tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ mɛ.

english_verse: You know that Stephanas and his family were the first in Achaia to have faith in the Lord. They have done all they can for God's people. My friends, I ask you
translation_verse: Nɔonyi ɔsɛ Stefana yɛɔpã mɛ anɛ balɛenu banɔ gɛ beputɔ pɛ etsyinà nɛ Axaya, kɛlɛ balɔkɔ balɛishu akpɛ banɔ dadalɛ axɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa mɛ. Ɛkpalà wɔnɔ, badzya,

english_verse: to obey leaders like them and to do the same for all others who work hard with you.
translation_verse: ɔsɛ tɛ̀ nɔkpɛ kɛtakpu esi asɛ banɔ alɛ nɛidi nɛ̀ anɔ aka nyia aka tsyɛ gɛ afala eku kɛxɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa ɛlɛ mɛ pɛ aatɔ nɛ ɛlɛ shuɔ.

english_verse: I was glad to see Stephanas and Fortunatus and Achaicus. Having them here was like having you.
translation_verse: Same eku mɛ nɛ kibeɛ gɛ Stefana, Fortunato nɛ̀ Axaiko ebekuɔ, ɔmɔsɛ bakɔ wɔnɔ kɛgɛ nɔpɛ nɔ.

english_verse: They made me feel much better, just as they made you feel better. You should appreciate people like them.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ bakɔ mɛ nɛ̀ wɔnɔ ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ ɔnyɛmɛ. Kɛlɛ sɛ bɔbakà banyɛ alɛ nɛidi. Bɔdɔ̃ Sɛsɛnɔ Gɔgɔɛ Nɔ

english_verse: Greetings from the churches in Asia. Aquila and Priscilla, together with the church that meets in their house, send greetings in the name of the Lord.
translation_verse: Ɔhaa gɛ ɔlɛ Asia bɔdɔ̃ mɛ asɛnɛ wɔnɔ asɛsɛ. Akwila nɛ̀ Priskila basɛnɔ wɔnɔ piapii nɛ Apaleteɛ inyeɛ mɛ, kana ɔhaa gɛ baatsyɔa nɛ balɛ bɔpã mɛ tsyɛ asɛnɛ wɔnɔ.

english_verse: All the Lord's followers send their greetings. Give each other a warm greeting.
translation_verse: Badzya nɛ ɛkɛlɛ petee asɛnɛ wɔnɔ. Nɔsɛnɛ bagɔlɔ nɛ̀ ɔhɔɛ dadalɛ kpɛkpɛ nɛ bagɔlɔ oli.

english_verse: I am signing this letter myself: PAUL.
translation_verse: Mɛ, Paulo mɛfɔnɔ bɔdɔ̃ sɛsɛnɔ ɔlɛ nɛ̀ mɛahɔɛ.

english_verse: I pray that God will put a curse on everyone who doesn't love the Lord. And may the Lord come soon.
translation_verse: Onupɛ anɔ teli agɛ dɛmɛ nɔ nɛ Apaleteɛ, kibui ɖɔɖɔ ɔkpɛ anɔ zã yiede. O, Apalete, ba!

english_verse: I pray the Lord Jesus will be kind to you.
translation_verse: Apalete Yesu yɛ tibhui bhuibuɔ zã ɔplɛnɔ wɔnɔ.

english_verse: I love everyone who belongs to Christ Jesus.
translation_verse: Mɛ tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ zãplɛnɔ wɔnɔ petee nɛ Yesu Kristo mɛ. Amen.

english_verse: From Paul, chosen by God to be an apostle of Jesus Christ, and from Timothy, who is also a follower. To God's church in Corinth and to all of God's people in Achaia.
translation_verse: Paulo gɛedzi anɔkpɛkpɛ akɔ Yesu Kristo nɛ Owusoso Akaa tɛpɛpɛ ulũɔ, nɛ̀ Timoteo gɛenu bɔlɔ adzyaa, Mɛ sɛsɛ Korintoanɛ ɔhaa keplukpa alɛ ɔlɔkɔ plɛnɔ bagɛ bayɛkɛ aŋã nɛ Axaya petee asɛ,

english_verse: I pray that God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! Paul Gives Thanks
translation_verse: Tibhui bhuibu nɛ̀ ishu yɔyɔ ode Owusoso Akaa nɛ̀ Apalete Yesu Kristo ɔkɔ kɔ wɔnɔ. Owusoso Akaa gɛ Ayɔ Banɔ Nyɔɔ Ishumɛ

english_verse: Praise God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ! The Father is a merciful God, who always gives us comfort.
translation_verse: Nɔkpɛ Owusoso Akaa gɛenu Apalete Yesu Yɛakaa nɛinye nɛitití, Akaa gɛ ebi ashagabui nɛ̀ Owusoso Akaa gɛ ayɔ banɔ nyɔ̃ɔ ishumɛ,

english_verse: He comforts us when we are in trouble, so that we can share this same comfort with others in trouble.
translation_verse: anɔ gɛ yɛayɔ bɔlɔ ishumɛ odenɔ bɔlɔ bɔtɔ petee gɛ bɔmɔ mɛ, sɛ tɛ bɔlɔ tsyɛ lɔkɔ ishumɛ yɔyɔ gɛ bɔmɔ denɔ Owusoso Akaa ɔkɔ yɔ ishumɛ ɔkɔ bagɛ bamɔ bɔtɔ.

english_verse: We share in the terrible sufferings of Christ, but also in the wonderful comfort he gives.
translation_verse: Kɛba gɛ Kristo yɛ bɔtɔ ba bɔlɔ ɛkpã mɛɛ, kidzo ɛlɛ nabha gana Kristo abhaa, ishumɛ yɔyɔ ɔkɔ bɔlɔ yuku balɛ.

english_verse: We suffer in the hope that you will be comforted and saved. And because we are comforted, you will also be comforted, as you patiently endure suffering like ours.
translation_verse: Onupɛ kibui tɔɖɔ bɔlɔ abhaa, kinu wɔnɔ ishumɛ yɔyɔ nɛ̀ tibubú, onupɛ bayɔ bɔlɔ kitúɔ mɛɛ, wɔnɔ bayɔ kitúɔ mɛ akɔ, gɛ kɛmana tɛdɔdɔ fɔ̃ɔ, kemikpɛkpɛ odenɔ bɔtɔ tunikpo ɔlɛ gɛ bɔtɔ mɛ.

english_verse: You never disappoint us. You suffered as much as we did, and we know that you will be comforted as we were.
translation_verse: Bɔlɔ kidzo mɛ ɖiɖi ɔkɔ wɔnɔ ɔlɛ gagalĩ, ɔmɔsɛ bunyi sɛ kana gɛ nɔmɔ bɔtɔ ɔplɛnɔ bɔlɔɔ, kidzo ɛlɛ nabha nɔmɔ ishumɛ yɔyɔ tsyɛ ɔplɛnɔ bɔlɔ.

english_verse: My friends, I want you to know what a hard time we had in Asia. Our sufferings were so horrible and so unbearable that death seemed certain.
translation_verse: Mɛadzya alɛ mɔ, bɔpɛ sɛ nɔotì ebui alɛ, nɛ bɔtɔ gɛ bɔmɔ nɛ Asia bɔdɔ̃ mɛ shu. Bɔgã bɔtɔ gbagbla tuli mɛ gɛ fala bɔlɔ kemikpɛkpɛ, gɛ kɛkɔ ɔsɛ ɛkpã zazãna batɔ̀ bɔlɔ.

english_verse: In fact, we felt sure we were going to die. But this made us stop trusting in ourselves and start trusting God, who raises the dead to life.
translation_verse: Kibui gbã, nɛ bɔlɔ etúɔ mɛɛ, bɔmɔ ɛlɛ asɛ butsí bɔtsyɔa bebhui akɔ ɔlɔ. Pɔ kɛba ɛlɛ mɛ ana ɔsɛ tɛ̀ butì sɛ bɔtɛ balà ishu ahɔɛ mɛ zã ɔdɛ Owusoso Akaa ahɔɛ mɛ, agɛ yɛakɔ bekpuɔ alɔ edenɔ bekpù mɛ.

english_verse: God saved us from the threat of death, and we are sure that he will do it again and again.
translation_verse: Ebú bɔlɔ odenɔ butsí ɛlɛ nɛidì mɛ̀, kɛlɛ ezebè bu ɔlɔ. Yɛabha bulu awɔ̃ sɛ abàzã kɛlɛ abha pɛ ebebu bɔlɔ,

english_verse: Please help us by praying for us. Then many people will give thanks for the blessings we receive in answer to all these prayers. Paul's Change of Plans
translation_verse: asɛ gɛ nɔplɛnɔ bɔlɔ nɛ wɔnɔ Owusoso Akaa tikukulu mɛ̀ ana. Kɛlɛ́ banɔ kpekpè ebehu ahɔɛ nɛ bɔlɔ kɛtakpu nɛ tibhui bhuibu gbangbã gɛ ebhui bɔlɔ ebuɔ nɛ banɔ kpekpèɛ gɛ bekulu Owusoso Akaa nɛ bɔlɔ kɛtakpu so. Paulo Etsyina Yɛibui Tɔtɔɖɔ

english_verse: We can be proud of our clear conscience. We have always lived honestly and sincerely, especially when we were with you. And we were guided by God's gift of undeserved grace instead of by the wisdom of this world.
translation_verse: Pɔ otsiɛ nɔ butũ kitélũ ɔsɛ, bɔlɔ kitú mɛ ebui etseɖe bɔlɔ ɔsɛ bɔnyãna bɔlɔ shu piapii nɛ kanɔ nɔ̀ mɛ, asɛ kana gɛ bɔkpɛ ɔha nɛ̀ wɔnɔ, nɛ tɛdadalɛ kibui gɛlɛ kɛlɛ ɔzãkɔ tɛɖɔɖɔ kide Owusoso Akaa ɔkɔ. Gɛnɛ kesiakpa abha bubutì bɔlɔkɔ bhɛtɛ ebui alɛ nɔ, pɔ nɛ Owusoso Akaa tibhui bhuibu mɛ boŋ.

english_verse: I am not writing anything you cannot read and understand. I hope you will understand it completely,
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ bɔtɛ fɔnɔ kibui nɔnɔ gɛ nɔtɛ bɔlà ɔkalàa, alo pɛ nɔtɛ bɔlà kɛlɛ esi ulũɔ ɔsɛsɛ ɔnɔ. Kɛlɛ ɛyɛkɛŋã ɔsɛ,

english_verse: just as you already partly understand us. Then when our Lord Jesus returns, you can be as proud of us as we are of you.
translation_verse: asɛ kana gɛ nɔolũ ɔlɔ esi nɛ ebuiɛ teli mɛɛ, nobolũ alɛ petee esi pɛ nobotũ etelũ nɛ bɔlɔ ɛtakpu asɛ kanà gɛ bɔlɔ tsyɛ tũ etelũ nɛ Apalete Yesu kiwuiɛ nabha.

english_verse: I was so sure of your pride in us that I had planned to visit you first of all. In this way you would have the blessing of two visits from me—
translation_verse: Kliso ɛyɛkɛ kɛlɛ abha ɔŋã, ɛtɔɖɔ ɛlɛ sɛ tɛ ɛbaɖí wɔnɔ putɔnɔ sɛ tɛ nɔmɔ ebui gbangbã taabha odenɔ kɛlɛ mɛ.

english_verse: once on my way to Macedonia and again on my return from there. Then you could send me on to Judea.
translation_verse: Ɛtɔɖɔ kɛlɛ sɛ tɛ ɛbaɖí wɔnɔ nɛ kibeɛ gɛ ɛvɛ Makedonia nɔ, tɛ mitsodzi bà wɔnɔ ɔbha gɛ ide Makedonia nɔ baa, tɛ nɔkpɛ mɛidzo mɛvɛ Yudea.

english_verse: Do you think I couldn't make up my mind about what to do? Or do I seem like someone who says “Yes” or “No” simply to please others?
translation_verse: Kibeɛ gɛ ɛtɔɖɔ kibui ɛlɛ, kibui ete mɛtɔɖɔ dza? Pɛnɔsɛ ɛbhɛtɛ tɛtɔtɔɖɔ ɛlɛ asɛ kanɔ mɛ anɔ ana gɛ aɖɔ asɛ “Ee, ee,” pɛnɔsɛ “Oo, oo nɔɔ dza”?

english_verse: God can be trusted, and so can I, when I say our answer to you has always been “Yes” and never “No.”
translation_verse: Pɔ asɛ kana gɛ Owusoso Akaa aɖɔ kibui gbãa, kana bɔlɔ kibui ɖɔɖɔ ɔkɔ wɔnɔ tsyɛ itinu “Ee nɛ̀ Oo.”

english_verse: This is because Jesus Christ the Son of God is always “Yes” and never “No.” And he is the one Silas, Timothy, and I told you about.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ Owusoso Akaa Yɛeyi Yesu Kristoɔ gɛ yɛibui mɛ nɛ̀ Silvano nɛ̀ Timoteo bɔɖɔɔ tinu ɔsɛ “Ee” alo “Oo” pɔ odenɔ yɛmɛ, kibe nyia beɛ kɛka nyia ka lɛ ɔsɛ “Ee.”

english_verse: Christ says “Yes” to all God's promises. This is why we have Christ to say “Amen” for us to the glory of God.
translation_verse: Kikpè gɛ Owusoso Akaa ebui ɖɔɖɔ luɔ zikpè kɔ ɔlɔ tsyɛ elo, lɛenu “Ee” nɛ Kristo mɛ. Kliso ɔgana yɛabhaa, bɔɖɔ “Amen” ɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa nɛinye shu tɛdadalɛ.

english_verse: And so God makes it possible for you and us to stand firmly together with Christ. God is also the one who chose us
translation_verse: Otsi nɔ, Owusoso Akaa yɛabhɛtɛ ɔlɔ pɛ budzi gagalĩ nɛ Kristo mɛ. Asra bɔbɔ akɔ ɔlɔ,

english_verse: and put his Spirit in our hearts to show that we belong only to him.
translation_verse: alɔkɔ adadã gɛ akasɛ asɛ yayɛ bunuɔ adã bɔlɔ ulũ nɔ, kɛlɛ alɔkɔ yɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ akpɛ bɔlɔ etúɔ mɛ̀ asɛ kɛdɔ̃ gɛ alɔkɔ atɔɖɔ kɛklagbu nɛ kɛgɛ baba nabha.

english_verse: God is my witness that I stayed away from Corinth, just to keep from being hard on you.
translation_verse: Ikulu Owusoso Akaa asɛ anɔ gɛ akpɛ abhalà akɔ mɛ, sɛ kitinu mɛ tɛpɛpɛ ɔsɛ ibhui wɔnɔ bho nɛ kibeɛ gɛ itsyina ba Korinto nɔ.

english_verse: We are not bosses who tell you what to believe. We are working with you to make you glad, because your faith is strong.
translation_verse: Gɛnɛ sɛ buutũ kɛlɛ kpɛ wɔnɔ ulũ ɔsɛ nɔyɛkɛ ɔŋã, pɔ bɔwɔa axɔ̃ɛ plɛnɔ wɔnɔ ɔkɔ wɔnɔ samè mɔmɔ, ɔmɔsɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã kɛlɛ bakɔ ɔsɛ tɛ nɔzã gagalĩ.

english_verse: I have decided not to make my next visit with you so painful.
translation_verse: Kliso ɛbhɛtɛ mɛ ekpeɖekpeɖe mɛ sɛ ɛzaka ba wɔnɔ ɔbha nɛ ishumɛ pɔpɔɛ mɛ.

english_verse: If I make you feel bad, who would be left to cheer me up, except the people I had made to feel bad?
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ onupɛ ɛkpɛ wɔnɔ ɔmɔpɔɛ nɔ, afana gɛ alɛ pɛ abakɔ mɛmɔ same ɔdɛ wɔnɔ bagɛ ɛkpɛ ɔmɔpɔɛ nɛ wɔnɔ shuɔ dza?

english_verse: The reason I want to be happy is to make you happy. I wrote as I did because I didn't want to visit you and be made to feel bad, when you should make me feel happy.
translation_verse: Ɛfɔnɔ keplukpa asɛ kana gɛ ɛbhɛtɛ sɛ onupɛ ɛbaa, ɛtɛba nyɔnɔ awɔ nɛ bagɛ kɛlɛ sɛ bakpɛ mɛ sameɛ shu. Ɛyɛkɛ wɔnɔ petee abha ŋã, sɛ wɔnɔ nyɔ̃ɔ wɔnɔ ɔbɔmɔ samè ɔplɛnɛ mɛ.

english_verse: At the time I wrote, I was suffering terribly. My eyes were full of tears, and my heart was broken. But I didn't want to make you feel bad. I only wanted to let you know how much I cared for you. Forgiveness
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ ɔwɔ̃nyɔnɔ gbagbla, kibui tɔtɔɖɔ anɔ abha nɛ̀ ɛwɔmɛ mɛ̀ mɛlɔkɔ fɔnɔ ebui alɛ kɔ wɔnɔ nɔ, gɛnɛ sɛ tɛ ɛkpɛ wɔnɔ ɔmɔpɔɛ nɔ, pɔ tɛ nɔmɔ mɛ tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ gbagbla ɔkɔ wɔnɔ. Tɛlɔlɔkɔ Tsye Kibui kpa Bhɛbhɛtɛ Elete

english_verse: I don't want to be hard on you. But if one of you has made someone feel bad, I am not really the one who has been made to feel bad. Some of you are the ones.
translation_verse: Onupɛ anɔ teli amana ɔmɔpɔɛ nɔ, agɛ kpɛ mɛ ɔmɔpɔɛ asɛ kana gɛ akpɛ wɔnɔ petee ɔmɔpɔɛ nɔ, nɛ kidzo tili abhaa, ɛgɛ pɛ sɛ tɛ mɛvɛ kɛlɛ abha faanɔ.

english_verse: When people sin, you should forgive and comfort them, so they won't give up in despair.
translation_verse: Pɔ otsiɛnɔ, kɛlɛ sɛ tɛ nɔlɔkɔ otsye yɛ tɛ nɔyɔ yɛishu mɛ, sɛ tɛ ɔwɔ̃nyɔnɔ gbagbla ɔtɛbɔ ŋã yɛabha.

english_verse: You should make them sure of your love for them.
translation_verse: Kliso ɛkpalà wɔnɔ sɛ tɛ nɔkɔ wɔnɔ tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ ɔkɔɛ kpè tɔɖɔ.

english_verse: I also wrote because I wanted to test you and find out if you would follow my instructions.
translation_verse: Kibui nɔnɔ shu so pɛ ɛfɔnɔ kibui ɛlɛ kɔ wɔnɔ kɛlɛinu ɔsɛ tɛ̀ mɛiɖi sɛ nɔbɔ làzã tifefekeɖiɛ ulũ pɛ nobolũ batɔ̃ nɛ kɛka nyia ka mɛ dza.

english_verse: I will forgive anyone you forgive. Yes, for your sake and with Christ as my witness, I have forgiven whatever needed to be forgiven.
translation_verse: Onupɛ nɔlɔkɔ tsye anɔ teliɛ, kɛlɛ mɛtsyɛ lɔkɔ tsye yɛ. Kɛlɛ kibui nɔnɔ gɛ ɛlɔkɔ tsyeɛ, onupɛ edzi asɛ kibui tili lɛ sɛ balɔkɔ etsye tsyɛ elo, ɛlɔkɔ kɛlɛ itsye nɛ Kristo amɛ nɛ wɔnɔ kɛtakpu so,

english_verse: I have done this to keep Satan from getting the better of us. We all know what goes on in his mind.
translation_verse: sɛ tɛ Satana atɛ bàwɔɛ wɔnɔ. Ɔmɔsɛ butinyi kɛgɛ yɛatɔɖɔɔ. Bagɛ Bawɔaxɔ̃ɛ Kɔ Olugbũnyanyã Vɔvɔɔ

english_verse: When I went to Troas to preach the good news about Christ, I found that the Lord had already prepared the way.
translation_verse: Kibeɛ gɛ ɛvɛ Troa tɛ mɛdɛɖɔ kibuiɛ nɛ Yesu shu pɛ ɛmɔ sɛ Apaleteɛ adalɛ kedulũ tieli akɔ mɛɛ,

english_verse: But I was worried when I didn't find my friend Titus there. So I left the other followers and went on to Macedonia.
translation_verse: mɛ ekpeɖekpeɖe mɛ agɛzã dɔ akɔ mɛ, ɔmɔsɛ ɛgɛ dɛ mɔ mɛadzya Tito nɛ ɛkɛlɛ. Kliso ibisi kidzo nɛ balɛ ɔkɔ pɛ ɛshɛ vɛ Makedonia.

english_verse: I am grateful that God always makes it possible for Christ to lead us to victory. God also helps us spread the knowledge about Christ everywhere, and this knowledge is like the smell of perfume.
translation_verse: Pɔ ahɔɛhuhu yɛenu Owusoso Akaa ayɛ, anɔ gɛ agbana bɔlɔ kibe nyia be sɛ tɛ bɔŋã kabha nɛ Kristo mɛ, kɛlɛ gana bɔlɔ abhaa yɛ bubutì kɛlɛɛ huhuhuɔ fu vɛ ɔkɔ nyia ɔkɔ.

english_verse: A lot of people try to get rich from preaching God's message. But we are God's sincere messengers, and by the power of Christ we speak our message with God as our witness.
translation_verse: Asɛ gɛ beteli abhɛtɛɛ, bɔlɔ xɔnɔɔ, bɔtɛwɔa Owusoso Akaa nɛxɔ̃ɛ tɛ̀ bɔlɔkɔ pɛ bɔdɔ̃ nɔ. Nɛ kidzo bhlatɔ abhaa, bɔɖɔ kibui gbã nɛ Kristo mɛ nɛ̀ Owusoso Akaa namɛ, asɛ banɔ gɛ bakpɛ odenɔ Owusoso Akaa ɔkɔ ana.

english_verse: Are we once again bragging about ourselves? Do we need letters to you or from you to tell others about us? Some people do need letters telling about them.
translation_verse: Bɔzɛba wlui kpɛ bɔlɔ ishu nɛitití mɔɔ dza? Buplukpa bade banɔ teli ɔkɔ kɔ wɔnɔ tububu kasɛ alo bɔlɔkɔ, ɔkɔ wɔnɔ inyeɛ tɛkpɛkpɛ nɛititiɛ dza?

english_verse: But you are our letter, and you are in our hearts for everyone to read and understand.
translation_verse: Wɔnɔ onu bɔlɔ keplukpa gɛ bafɔnɔ nɛ bɔlɔ etúɔ nabha, gɛ aka nyia aka enyi pɛ akalàa.

english_verse: You are like a letter written by Christ and delivered by us. But you are not written with pen and ink or on tablets made of stone. You are written in our hearts by the Spirit of the living God.
translation_verse: Nɔkasɛ ɔsɛ Kristo keplukpa nɔonu, kɛgɛ kɔ sɛ bɔlɔ axɔ̃ɛ avɛ kabhaa, gɛ bagɛ fɔnɔ nɛ̀ ahɔɛ pɔ Owusoso Akaa Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ balɔkɔ afɔnɔ kalɛ nɔ. Bagɛ fɔnɔ alɛ nɛ ɛklagbu abha, pɔ banɔdzyrame etú aklagbu nabha bafɔnɔ alɛ atɔɖɔ.

english_verse: We are sure about all this. Christ makes us sure in the very presence of God.
translation_verse: Banɔ abha yɛyɛkɛŋã alɛ nɛidiɛ nu bɔlɔ ayɛ ɔgana Kristo abha vɛnɔ Owusoso Akaa amɛ.

english_verse: We don't have the right to claim that we have done anything on our own. God gives us what it takes to do all we do.
translation_verse: Etinu asɛ gɛ bɔna nɛ kɛxɔ̃ɛ dɛdɛ mɛ ɔsɛ tɛ bɔyɛkɛ kɛdɔ̃ nanà kɔ bɔlɔshu, pɔ Owusoso Akaa yɛakɔ bɔlɔ kɛxɔ̃ɛ gbangbã tɛwɔawɔa ɛlɛ nɛidi.

english_verse: He makes us worthy to be the servants of his new agreement that comes from the Holy Spirit and not from a written Law. After all, the Law brings death, but the Spirit brings life.
translation_verse: Yɛakɔ bɔlà kɛxɔ̃ɛ gbangbã wɔawɔa nɛ̀ olugbũ nyanyã vɔvɔ ɔlɛ, anɛ ɔsha keplukpa ɔkɔ kide, pɔ Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ, ɔmɔsɛ keplukpa xɔnɔ eyì banɔ, pɔ Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ xɔnɔ lɔkɔ ɛkpã. Tɛdadalɛ Gɛlɛ Olugbũ Nyanyã Vɔvɔ Shu

english_verse: The Law of Moses brought only the promise of death, even though it was carved on stones and given in a wonderful way. Still the Law made Moses' face shine so brightly the people of Israel could not look at it, even though it was a fading glory.
translation_verse: Otsiɛ nɔ, onupɛ kɛxɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa gɛ mana butsí, gɛ bafɔnɔ atɔɖɔ ɔsha buplukpa nɛ̀ kɛklagbu abhaa, ba nɛ̀ tɛdadalɛ sɛ tɛ Israelanɛ atɛlà eɖi Mose amɛ nɛ gɛ kitséɛ shu, gɛ kalɛ ulũ pɔ bafala flogo,

english_verse: So won't the agreement the Spirit brings to us be even more wonderful?
translation_verse: kidzi ana pɔ, bɔlɔ axɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa gɛede Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔmɛ ishu taadalɛ ɔdɛ ana dza?

english_verse: If something that brings the death sentence is glorious, won't something that makes us acceptable to God be even more glorious?
translation_verse: Onupɛ axɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa gɛebu banɔ kɛpɔ shu dalɛɛ ana pɔ, kɛlɛ kɛxɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa gɛ mana ishu dadalɛ pɔ ba zã dza?

english_verse: In fact, the new agreement is so wonderful that the Law is no longer glorious at all.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ kɛgɛ buɖi sɛ kɛlɛishu dalɛ, tɛka lɛ ana, gɛ onupɛ bɔlɔkɔ shɔnɔ tɛdadalɛ gɛ bazã tihɔɔ nulũ.

english_verse: The Law was given with a glory that faded away. But the glory of the new agreement is much greater, because it will never fade away.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ onupɛ kɛgɛ kɛlɛ ulũ bafalàa ba nɛ̀ tɛdadalɛ ɛlɛ nɛidiɛ pɔ, kɛlɛ tɛdãdalɛ gɛba zã tihɔɔ nɔ bazãa mɔɔ dza?

english_verse: This wonderful hope makes us feel like speaking freely.
translation_verse: Kliso gɛ buɖi kidzo mɛ ɔkɔ ebui alɛ, kifù tiyi bɔlɔ.

english_verse: We are not like Moses. His face was shining, but he covered it to keep the people of Israel from seeing the brightness fade away.
translation_verse: Bɔlɔ butidzi asɛ Mose ana, gɛ abalɔkɔ kɛpɔtɛ adã yɛamɛ asɛ tɛ̀ Israelanɛ atɛmɔ tɛdadalɛ gɛ ɛlɛ ulũ falàa nɛ yɛamɛ.

english_verse: The people were stubborn, and something still keeps them from seeing the truth when the Law is read. Only Christ can take away the covering that keeps them from seeing.
translation_verse: Pɔ babhɛtɛ balɛ ekpeɖekpeɖeɛ yɛazã yɔɔ, ɔmɔsɛ oboyinɔ otsiɛnɔ kɛpɔtɛ wɔ balɛ amɛ nɛ kibeɛ gɛ bakà akalà olugbũ nyanyã kukuɔ. Bagɛ tsɛ ɛlɛ nɛ balɛ amɛ, ɔmɔsɛ kristo mɛ ete babà tsɛ ɛlɛ ashɛnɔ.

english_verse: When the Law of Moses is read, they have their minds covered over
translation_verse: Boyinɔ obuɔ onupɛ bakalà Mose ɔsha keplukpa, baka adã kɛpɔtɛ ɛlɛ nɛ alɛ etúɔ nabha.

english_verse: with a covering that is removed only for those who turn to the Lord.
translation_verse: Pɔ onupɛ anɔ teli tsyina avɛ Apaleteɛ ɔkɔɔ, kɛlɛ́ batsɛ kɛpɔtɛ ɛlɛ nɛ yɛamɛ.

english_verse: The Lord and the Spirit are one and the same, and the Lord's Spirit sets us free.
translation_verse: Otsiɛ Apaleteɛ yɛenu Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ, kɛlɛ ɛkɛlɛ gɛ Apaleteɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ lɛɛ, ɛkɛlɛ ishu ahɔɛmɛ zazã tsyɛ azã.

english_verse: So our faces are not covered. They show the bright glory of the Lord, as the Lord's Spirit makes us more and more like our glorious Lord.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ bɔlɔ gɛ bagɛ tɔɖɔ kɛpɔtɛ nɛ bɔlɔ amɛ butsé nɛ̀ Apaleteɛ tɛdadalɛ, betsyina bɔlɔ akpɛ yɛ tɛzazã mɛ, nɛ̀ tɛdadalɛ gɛikpè tɔɖɔ, gɛide Apaleteɛ ɔkɔ, anɔ gɛenu Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ ɔkɔɔ.

english_verse: God has been kind enough to trust us with this work. This is why we never give up.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ, gɛ gana Owusoso Akaa ashagabui ɖiɖi mɛ bɔwɔa axɔ̃ɛ soɔ, bɔlɔ etúɔ ohuiɛ otio bhui.

english_verse: We don't do shameful things that must be kept secret. And we don't try to fool anyone or twist God's message around. God is our witness that we speak only the truth, so others will be sure we can be trusted.
translation_verse: Pɔ, bɔtɛ badɛmɛ tɔɖɔ ebui kpukpũ nanà tɛɖɔɖɔ nɛ̀ awũi edzo abha, bɔgɛ lɔkɔ tɛwɔɛwɔɛ ebui, alo pɛ butsyina Owusoso Akaa yɛibuiɛ tũ. Pɔ nɛ kidzo ɛlɛ nabha, bɔlɔkɔ kibui gɛlɛ ɛlɛ ɔzãkɔ bɔlɔkɔ ɖɔ kitú mɛ kibui, nɛ Owusoso Akaa namɛ.

english_verse: If there is anything hidden about our message, it is hidden only to someone who is lost.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ onupɛ kidzi asɛ badã kibui gbangbã nɔdɔ̃ tsyɛ elo, badã kɛlɛ bɔdɔ̃ ɔkɔ bagɛ balɛ ɛkpã yalaa.

english_verse: The god who rules this world has blinded the minds of unbelievers. They cannot see the light, which is the good news about our glorious Christ, who shows what God is like.
translation_verse: Obo kanɔ nɔ̀ ibudiɛ pú awuhũ akɔ bagɛ agɛ yɛkɛ aŋã, sɛ tɛ batɛmɔ tɛdadalɛ gɛlɛ Kristo yɛibuiɛ shu, anɔ gɛ alɛ asɛ Owusoso Akaa ana.

english_verse: We are not preaching about ourselves. Our message is that Jesus Christ is Lord. He also sent us to be your servants.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ anɛ bɔlɔ shu kibui bɔɖɔ, pɔ Yesu Kristoɔ bɔlɔ Apaleteɛ shu bɔɖɔ kaɖɔɔ, kɛlɛ budzi asɛ baxɔ̃ɛdɛ akɔ Yesu ana.

english_verse: The Scriptures say, “God commanded light to shine in the dark.” Now God is shining in our hearts to let you know that his glory is seen in Jesus Christ.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ Owusoso Akaa aɖɔ asɛ, “Nɔkɔ kifu dɛkɛ nɛ otumũɔ mɛ,” nɔkɔ yɛifúɔ dɛkɛ nɛ bɔlɔ etúɔ mɛ, tɛ kɛdɛkɛ kɔ bɔlɔ asɛ bubuti kifù ana tɛ kɛkɔ Owusoso Akaa shu dalɛ nɛ Kristo amɛ.

english_verse: We are like clay jars in which this treasure is stored. The real power comes from God and not from us.
translation_verse: Pɔ ehoeyi bɔdɔ̃ bɔlɛ lɛ ɔlɔ ahɔɛ gɛkpasɛ butsukpu mɛ tɛ kɛkasɛ sɛ ohumi ɔlɛ gɛ ɔdɛ ɔka nyia ka ogide bɔlɔkɔ, pɔ Owusoso Akaa ɔkɔ lode.

english_verse: We often suffer, but we are never crushed. Even when we don't know what to do, we never give up.
translation_verse: Bamɔnɔ bɔlɔ akpɛ gagalĩ nɛ kawɔ̃pɛ ka nyia ka mɛ, pɔ begihu bɔlɔ esi, kɛkpɛ ɔlɔ buluhũ pɔ bɔlɔ shu mɛ gɛgbã,

english_verse: In times of trouble, God is with us, and when we are knocked down, we get up again.
translation_verse: batɔnɔ ɔlɔ ede, pɔ bagɛ shɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ bɔlɔ shu, behu ɔlɔ esi pɔ bugitsí.

english_verse: We face death every day because of Jesus. Our bodies show what his death was like, so his life can also be seen in us.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ bɔlɔ bagɛ lɛ nɛkpã, butsi kpɛ bɔlɔ ɛkpã nɛifu nɛ Yesu kɛtakpu sɛ tɛ bebu yɛkpã zazãna mɛ akasɛ nɛ bɔlɔ ishupiɛ gɛbatsiɛ mɛ.

english_verse: This means that death is working in us, but life is working in you.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ, butsí wɔa axɔ̃ɛ nɛ bɔlɔ mɛ, pɔ ɛkpã ɛlɛ wɔa axɔ̃ɛ nɛ wɔnɔ xɔnɔ mɛ.

english_verse: In the Scriptures it says, “I spoke because I had faith.” We have this same kind of faith. So we speak
translation_verse: Bafɔnɔ kɛlɛ elú asɛ, “Ɛyɛkɛŋã, kliso pɛ ɛɖɔ kaɖɔɔ.” Nɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã ɔfɔfuĩa tonikpo ɔlɛ mɛɛ, bɔlɔ tsyɛ yɛkɛ ŋã kliso pɛ bɔɖɔ kaɖɔ,

english_verse: because we know that God raised the Lord Jesus to life. And just as God raised Jesus, he will also raise us to life. Then he will bring us into his presence together with you.
translation_verse: ɔmɔsɛ bunyi sɛ agɛ yɛanyɛmɛ Yesu denɔ bekpuɔ mɛ yɛaba nyɛmɛ bɔlɔ aplɛnɔ Yesu kɛlɛ abagbana bɔlɔ nɛ̀ wɔnɔ avɛnɔ yɛamɛ.

english_verse: All of this has been done for you, so more and more people will know how kind God is and will praise and honor him. Faith in the Lord
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ petee lɛ ɔkɔ wɔnɔ tɛpɛpɛ̃ so, sɛ tɛ tibhui bhuibuɔ bavɛ banɔ kpekpèɛ ɔkɔ gɛ bamana ahɔɛhuhu gɛbà yuku gbangbã Owusoso Akaa tɛdadalɛ.

english_verse: We never give up. Our bodies are gradually dying, but we ourselves are being made stronger each day.
translation_verse: Kliso bɔlɔ kitúɔ tibhui. Nɛ ɔtɔ̃ɔ, bɔwɔɛ asɛ bɔlɔ ishudzya botso, pɔ nɛ bɔlɔ kɛmɛ xɔnɔɔ, bɔmɔ ɛkpã tsyitsyina vɔvɔ kiwui nyia wui.

english_verse: These little troubles are getting us ready for an eternal glory that will make all our troubles seem like nothing.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ bɔlɔ bɔtɔ lɛ gidzi foele pɛ bɔfalà gulũi nɔ, bakɔ bɔlɔ̀ tɛdadalɛ gɛbazã tihɔɔ gɛ dɛ kɛka nyia ka.

english_verse: Things that are seen don't last forever, but things that are not seen are eternal. This is why we keep our minds on the things that cannot be seen.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ bɔtɛ ilu awɔ̃ nɛ kɛgɛ bamɔ nɛ̀ awuhũɔ nabha, pɔ buulu awɔ̃ nɛ bɔdɔ̃ gɛ batɛ amɔ nabha. Ɔmɔsɛ kɛgɛ bɔmɔ nɛ̀ anwuhũɔ tɛzã kibe dzlolo, pɔ kɛgɛ batɛmɔ nɛ̀ awuhũɔ kɛlɛ zã tihɔɔ.

english_verse: Our bodies are like tents that we live in here on earth. But when these tents are destroyed, we know that God will give each of us a place to live. These homes will not be buildings someone has made, but they are in heaven and will last forever.
translation_verse: Otsi alɛ bunyi sɛ onupɛ bezĩ kesiakpa abha kagbaa gɛ kalɛ mɛ bɔkpasɛ, ɔzãkɔ ɔlɛ bɔlɔ ahɔɛ nɛ Owusoso Akaa ɔkɔ, bɔpã tihɔɔ, gɛ anɛ anɔdzyrame ahɔɛ balɔkɔ edzu bɔlɛ nɔ.

english_verse: While we are here on earth, we sigh because we want to live in that heavenly home.
translation_verse: Pɔ otsiɛ nɔ atɛatsɔ akɔ bɔlɔ tɛ bakɔ bɔlɔ kititì ɔzãkɔ

english_verse: We want to put it on like clothes and not be naked.
translation_verse: ɔmɔsɛ onupɛ balɔkɔ bɔlɛ akɔ ɔlɔ, bɔtɛ bazã kɛdɔ̃ makpɛ makpɛ.

english_verse: These tents we now live in are like a heavy burden, and we groan. But we don't do this just because we want to leave these bodies that will die. It is because we want to change them for bodies that will never die.
translation_verse: Pɔ otsiɛ xɔnɔ gɛ bɔkpasɛ kagba alɛ mɛɛ, atɛatsɔ akɔ bɔlɔ, kɛlɛ kɛtɔɖɔ bɔlɔ abha, ɔmɔsɛ bɔtɛpɛ sɛ tɛ bebu kɛdɔ̃ kpɛkpɛ ginu bɔlɔ ɔzãkɔ nɛ bɔlɔ shu, pɔ tɛ bakpɛ kɛpɔtɛ ginu kititì ɔzãkɔ nɛ bɔlɔ shu sɛ tɛ̀ ɛkpã ŋã kɛdɔ̃ gitsíɛ nabha.

english_verse: God is the one who makes all this possible. He has given us his Spirit to make us certain he will do it.
translation_verse: Otsiɛ nɔ, Owusoso Akaa yɛabhɛtɛ bɔlɔ akɔ kibui tɔtɔɖɔ ɛlɛ kɛlɛ akɔ bɔlɔ Ɔfɔfuĩa alɔkɔ elú tɛ̀ kɛkasɛ kidzo ɔkɔ kɛgɛ babaá.

english_verse: So always be cheerful! As long as we are in these bodies, we are away from the Lord.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ buɖi kidzo mɛ kibe nyia be, kɛlɛ bunyi tsyɛ sɛ gɛ bɔkpasɛ ishudzya bɔpãlɛ mɛɛ soɔ, bɔlɛ odzlolo ɔkɔ nɛ Apaleteɛ shu.

english_verse: But we live by faith, not by what we see.
translation_verse: Tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛ bɔlɔkɔ dzi nɛ ɛkpã nɔ, anɛ nɛ kɛdɔ̃ ɖiɖi ulũ.

english_verse: We should be cheerful, because we would rather leave these bodies and be at home with the Lord.
translation_verse: Ɛɖɔ ɛlɛ sɛ bɔyɛkɛ bɔlɔ shu abha ŋã, kɛlɛ bɔba pɛ sɛ tɛ bukoeyi nɛ ishudzya ɛlɛ mɛ tɛ bɔdɛzã Apaleteɛ bɔpã mɛ.

english_verse: But whether we are at home with the Lord or away from him, we still try our best to please him.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ, kinu kibui gɛ bɔtɔɖɔ sɛ bɔpɛ sɛ tɛ buku yɛawɔ, gɛ onupɛ edzi asɛ bɔkpasɛ bɔpãlɛ ginu ishudzya alo onupɛ bɔma bɔlɛ mɛ tsyɛ.

english_verse: After all, Christ will judge each of us for the good or the bad that we do while living in these bodies. Bringing People to God
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ kɛlɛ sɛ bɔlɔ petee ba Kristo kebhu kuklu kegbu namɛ, sɛ tɛ aka nyia aka ayɛkɛ kɛgɛ kɛlɛ sɛ bakɔɛ, nɛ̀ kɛgɛ yɛabhɛtɛ kibeɛ gɛ azã ishudzya mɛ, onupɛ kinu kibui gbangbã pɛnɔsɛ kibui kpa tsyɛ. Kɛxɔ̃ɛ Wɔawɔa Ɔkɔ Tenikpo Bhɛbhɛtɛ

english_verse: We know what it means to respect the Lord, and we encourage everyone to turn to him. God himself knows what we are like, and I hope you also know what kind of people we are.
translation_verse: Denɔ kibe ɛlɛ gɛ bɔbatì sɛ kɛlɛ sɛ busi Apaleteɛ nɛifu soɔ, bɔpɛ sɛ tɛ bubu ebui mɛ kɔanɔ. Owusoso Akaa enyi kɛgɛ bunùɔ, kɛlɛ inyi tsyɛ sɛ kɛlɛ mɛ dalɛ ɔkɔ wɔnɔ ebuesilulũ.

english_verse: We are not trying once more to brag about ourselves. But we want you to be proud of us, when you are with those who are not sincere and brag about what others think of them.
translation_verse: Bɔtɛ pɛ sɛ tɛ̀ bɔɖɔ kaɖɔ nɛ bɔlɔ shu kɔ wɔnɔ, pɔ bɔpɛ sɛ nɔmɔ kidzo tɛ nofeke ishu nɛ bɔlɔ kɛtakpu. Sɛ tɛ nɔlà bu kibui ulũ ɔkɔ bagɛ befeke ishu nɛ kɛgɛ bamɔ shu ɔdɛ kɛgɛ kpasɛ kitúɔ mɛ.

english_verse: If we seem out of our minds, it is between God and us. But if we are in our right minds, it is for your good.
translation_verse: Onupɛ bɔlɔ ekpeɖekpeɖe mɛ ama piapii tsyɛ elo, Owusoso Akaa shu so pɛ kɛlɛ ana, onupɛ bɔlɔ ekpeɖekpeɖeɛ mɛ alɛ piapii nɔ, bɔlɛ ana kɔ wɔnɔ.

english_verse: We are ruled by Christ's love for us. We are certain that if one person died for everyone else, then all of us have died.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ Kristoɔ tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ tĩi bɔlɔ, ɔmɔsɛ kɛlɛ mɛ dalɛ kɔ bɔlɔ sɛ anɔ tenikpo etsí kɔ bɔlɔ petee, klisoɔ bɔlɔ petee tsí.

english_verse: And Christ did die for all of us. He died so we would no longer live for ourselves, but for the one who died and was raised to life for us.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ etsí akɔ bɔlɔ petee, sɛ tɛ bagɛ alɛ nɛkpã atakazã nɛkpã akɔ balɛishu pɔ tɛ̀ bàzã nɛkpã akɔ agɛ yɛetsí nɛ balɛ kɛtakpuɔ pɛ beze feke yɛ edenɔ bekpuɔ mɛ.

english_verse: We are careful not to judge people by what they seem to be, though we once judged Christ in this way.
translation_verse: Kliso denɔ otsiɛ ɔvɛnɔɔ, bɔtɛ kaɖì ananà nɛ kanɔ mɛ ɔdɔ̃ ɖiɖi ulũ. Kɛkpasɛ ɛlɛ mɛ ɔsɛ, butiɖi Kristo ana oɖi, pɔ bɔtɛka bhɛtɛ ɛlɛ ana.

english_verse: Anyone who belongs to Christ is a new person. The past is forgotten, and everything is new.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ, onupɛ anɔ teli akpasɛ Kristo mɛɛ, enu kɛdɔ̃ bhɛbhɛtɛ vɔvɔ, kɛdɔ̃ kukuɔ tɛba fala, kɛvɔvɔ ba otsi alɛ!

english_verse: God has done it all! He sent Christ to make peace between himself and us, and he has given us the work of making peace between himself and others.
translation_verse: Alɛ petee ede Owusoso Akaa ɔkɔ, anɔ gɛ yɛabhɛtɛ tenikpo nɛ̀ bɔlɔ ɔgana Kristo abha kɛlɛ akɔ bɔlɔ kɛxɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa, gɛ mana tenikpo bhɛbhɛtɛ,

english_verse: What we mean is that God was in Christ, offering peace and forgiveness to the people of this world. And he has given us the work of sharing his message about peace.
translation_verse: sɛ Owusoso Akaa abhɛtɛ tenikpo nɛ kanɔ nɔ̀ mɛ ɔgana Kristo abha, gɛ atɛa kalà banɔdzyrame ebui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ kɔ alɛ. Kɛlɛ asɛsɛ bɔlɔ kibuiɛ gɛ bamana tenikpo bhɛbhɛtɛ.

english_verse: We were sent to speak for Christ, and God is begging you to listen to our message. We speak for Christ and sincerely ask you to make peace with God.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ bunu Kristo yɛanɔkpɛkpɛ, gɛ Owusoso Akaa agã bɔlɔ abha pɛ aɖɔaɖɔ etseɖe wɔnɔ. Kliso bubisi denɔ wɔnɔkɔ nɛ Kristo kinye mɛ sɛ tɛ̀ nɔbhɛtɛ tenikpo nɛ̀ Owusoso Akaa.

english_verse: Christ never sinned! But God treated him as a sinner, so Christ could make us acceptable to God.
translation_verse: Owusoso Akaa bhɛtɛ anɔ nɔ gɛ yɛishu kibui kpa ma sɛ tɛ edzi kibui kpa nɛ bɔlɔ kɛtakpu, sɛ tɛ gana yɛabhaa, budzi Owusoso Akaa anɔ dadalɛ.

english_verse: We work together with God, and we beg you to make good use of God's gift of undeserved grace.
translation_verse: Asɛ Owusoso Akaa ɔhaxɔ̃ɛwɔa anaa, bɔkpalà wɔnɔ sɛ nɔtɛ yɛkɛ Owusoso Akaa tibhui bhuibuɔ asɛ kɛlɛ́ɛ mɛdɔ̃ ana.

english_verse: In the Scriptures God says, “When the time came, I listened to you, and when you needed help, I came to save you.” That time has come. This is the day for you to be saved.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ aɖɔ asɛ, “Nɛ kibeɛ gɛ kɛlɛ sɛ ɛbhɛtɛ ɛyamɛ gbangbã kɔ wɔnɔ, ilũ wɔnɔ atɔ̃, kɛlɛ nɛ anɔ tibubu iwui nabhaa, ɛplɛnɔ wɔnɔ.” Itseɖe wɔnɔ sɛ kibe ɛlɛ kɛlɛinu Owusoso Akaa ɛyamɛ gbangbã bhɛbhɛtɛ kiwuiɛ, otsiɛ ɔlɛ onu tibubú kiwuiɛ. Bɔtɔɔ Gɛ Paulo Amɔɔ

english_verse: We don't want anyone to find fault with our work, and so we try hard not to cause problems.
translation_verse: Bugilu kɛdɔ̃ kpakpata nɛ anɔ nanà kidzo mɛ, ɔsɛ tɛ bɔlɔ axɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa etibedzi kɛlɛɛ ete.

english_verse: But in everything and in every way we show we truly are God's servants. We have always been patient, though we have had a lot of trouble, suffering, and hard times.
translation_verse: Pɔ asɛ Owusoso Akaa axɔ̃ɛdɛ ana, bɔlɔkɔ bɔlɔshu kɔ nɛ kidzo ka nyia ka abha, nɛ̀ kemi kpɛkpɛ gbagbla, bɔtɔ nɛ̀ ɔwɔ̃nyɔnɔ mɛ,

english_verse: We have been beaten, put in jail, and hurt in riots. We have worked hard and have gone without sleep or food.
translation_verse: nɛ tihuhuisi mɛ, ɔɖavũmɛ zazã nɛ̀ ebelà ebui mɛ, nɛ kɛxɔ̃ɛ gagalĩ tɛwɔawɔa mɛ̀, elishi taɖa madɔ madɔ nɛ bɔbhɛ mɛ,

english_verse: But we have kept ourselves pure and have been understanding, patient, and kind. The Holy Spirit has been with us, and our love has been real.
translation_verse: nɛ ekpeɖekpeɖe gbangbã mɛ, ebui esi lulũ, kitú esi lulu nɛ̀ ɛyamɛ gbangbã tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ mɛ, nɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã mɛ tɛzazã nɛ̀ tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ gidzuɔ mɛ̀,

english_verse: We have spoken the truth, and God's power has worked in us. In all our struggles we have said and done only what is right.
translation_verse: nɛ kibui gbã tɛɖɔɖɔ mɛ nɛ̀ Owusoso Akaa yɛohumi nɔ mɛ, tɛ bɔlɔkɔ kibui gbangbã bhɛbhɛtɛ keyutadɔ̃ nɛ kɛŋã nɛ̀ kɛblɛkpɛ mɛ̀,

english_verse: Whether we were honored or dishonored or praised or cursed, we always told the truth about ourselves. But some people said we did not.
