translation_verse: ɔgana ishu dadalɛ nɛ̀ awũi ebui mɛɛ, kibui kpa tɛɖɔɖɔ nɛ̀ kibui gbangbã tɛɖɔɖɔ nɛ anɔ shu mɛ, bɔna pɔ balɔkɔ bɔlɔ asɛ butinu anunɔ gɛ yɛ bɔɖɔ sɛ yɛ bunuɔ,

english_verse: We are unknown to others, but well known to you. We seem to be dying, and yet we are still alive. We have been punished, but never killed,
translation_verse: benyi bɔlɔ pɔ babhɛtɛ asɛ betinyi bɔlɔ ana, abhɛtɛ asɛ butsi pɔ bɔlɛ nɛkpã, behuisi bɔlɔ pɔ begiyì bɔlɔ,

english_verse: and we are always happy, even in times of suffering. Although we are poor, we have made many people rich. And though we own nothing, everything is ours.
translation_verse: ɔwɔ̃nyɔnɔ kpasɛ bɔlɔ mɛ, pɔ bɔmɔ same kibe nyia be, bɔdɔ̃ kitsyui, pɔ bɔkɔ banɔ kpekpèɛ amɔ ehoeyi bɔdɔ̃, kɛdɔ̃ nanà ma bɔlɔ ahɔɛ, pɔ kɛka nyia ka lɛ bɔlɔ ahɔɛ.

english_verse: Friends in Corinth, we are telling the truth when we say there is room in our hearts for you.
translation_verse: Bɔdalɛ ishumɛ ɖɔ kaɖɔ nɛ̀ wɔnɔ Korintoanɛ, kɛlɛ bɔdalɛ bɔlɔ etúɔ mɛ petee kɔ wɔnɔ.

english_verse: We are not holding back on our love for you, but you are holding back on your love for us.
translation_verse: Bɔgɛ nyanã bɔlɔ tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ ɔkɔ wɔnɔ ovunɔ, pɔ wɔnɔ xɔnɔ ɔnyana wɔnɔ tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ ovunɔ nɛ bɔlɔ shu.

english_verse: I speak to you as I would speak to my own children. Please make room in your hearts for us. The Temple of the Living God
translation_verse: Tɛ itiɖì banɔ amɛɛ, ɛɖɔ kaɖɔ kɔ wɔnɔ asɛ mɛeyi alɛ ana, kliso wɔnɔ tsyɛ nɔdalɛ wɔnɔ etúɔ mɛ. Nɔtɛkɔ Bakpɛ Kakɔ Nɛ Wɔnɔ Oli Ɔplɛnɔ Bagɛ Agɛ Yɛkɛ Aŋã

english_verse: Stay away from people who are not followers of the Lord! Can someone who is good get along with someone who is evil? Are light and darkness the same?
translation_verse: Nɔtɛkɔ bakpɛ kakɔ nɛ wɔnɔ oli ɔplɛnɔ bagɛ agɛ yɛkɛ aŋã. Ɔmɔsɛ tenikpo bhɛbhɛtɛ ɖuwoe gɛ akpasɛ kibui gbangbã nɛ̀ kibui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ ishi? Pɛnɔsɛ tenikpo bhɛbhɛtɛ ɖuwoe gɛ akpasɛ kifu nɛ̀ otumũ ishi?

english_verse: Is Christ a friend of Satan? Can people who follow the Lord have anything in common with those who don't?
translation_verse: Tenikpo bhɛbhɛtɛ ɖuwoe gɛ akpasɛ Kristo nɛ̀ anɔzizĩ ishi? Tenikpo bhɛbhɛtɛ ɖuwoe gɛ akpasɛ agɛ ayɛkɛ aŋã nɛ̀ anɔ gɛ agɛ yɛkɛ aŋã ishi dza?

english_verse: Do idols belong in the temple of God? We are the temple of the living God, as God himself says, “I will live with these people and walk among them. I will be their God, and they will be my people.”
translation_verse: Kibui nyanyã ɖuwoe gɛ kpasɛ Owusoso Akaavũɔ nɛ̀ ibudi ishi? Bɔlɛinu buvũɔ ɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa enunuɔ. Asɛ kana gɛ Owusoso Akaa aɖɔ asɛ, “Ɛbazã plɛnɔ balɛ kɛlɛ ɛbagã nɛ balɛishi, kɛlɛ ɛbadzi balɛ Owusoso Akaa, kɛlɛ bebèdzi mɛ banunɔ.”

english_verse: The Lord also says, “Leave them and stay away! Don't touch anything that isn't clean. Then I will welcome you
translation_verse: Klisoɔ nokoeyi nɛ balɛishi tɛ nobu ɔtɔ, kana Apaleteɛ aɖɔ. Nɔoti hunu kɛnanà gɛ kɛlɛishu gɛdalɛ, kɛlɛ ɛbayɛkɛ wɔnɔ.”

english_verse: and be your Father. You will be my sons and my daughters, as surely as I am God, the All-Powerful.”
translation_verse: “Ɛbadzi Akaa ɔkɔ wɔnɔ kɛlɛ nɔ obodzi mɛeyianyɛ nɛ̀ beyiadzɛ alɛ, kana Apaleteɛ gɛenu Ohumi petee eleteɛ aɖɔ.”

english_verse: My friends, God has made us these promises. So we should stay away from everything that keeps our bodies and spirits from being clean. We should honor God and try to be completely like him. The Church Makes Paul Happy
translation_verse: Mɛ ɔbhia alɛ gɛ ɛdɛmɛnɔ, gɛ batɔɖɔ ebui alɛ elú akɔ bɔlɔ soɔ, nɔkɔ bɔdalɛ bɔlɔ shu odenɔ kɛka nyia ka gɛ kɛkpɛ̀ kibò nɛ bɔlɔ ishudzya nɛ̀ ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ shu, tɛ bɔlɔ shu dalɛ tɔɖɔ nɛ kɛtakpu esi kpɛkpɛ ɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa mɛ. Paulo Yɛ Same Mɔmɔ

english_verse: Make a place for us in your hearts! We haven't mistreated or hurt anyone. We haven't cheated anyone.
translation_verse: Nɔbhɛtɛ ɔzãkɔ ɔkɔ bɔlɔ nɛ wɔnɔ etúɔ mɛ. Bɔgɛ bhɛtɛ kibui kpa nɛ anɔ nanà shu, bugi tuturu ananà, bugi dzyini ananà tsyɛ ɔkpɛ.

english_verse: I am not saying this to be hard on you. But, as I have said before, you will always be in our thoughts, whether we live or die.
translation_verse: Ɛtɛɖɔ kibui ɛlɛ tɛ̀ ɛlɔkɔ bù wɔnɔ kɛpɔ nɔ, ɛtɛɖɔ ɛlɛ ɖi sɛ ɔzãkɔ ɔlɛ nɛidi ɔlɛ wɔnɔ ahɔɛ nɛ bɔlɔ etúɔ mɛ gɛ bakɔ sɛ bɔzã nɛkpã alo tɛ̀ butsì plɛnɔ wɔnɔ.

english_verse: I trust you completely. I am always proud of you, and I am greatly encouraged. In all my trouble I am still very happy.
translation_verse: Ɛyɛkɛ wɔnɔ abha aŋã, kɛlɛ itũ mɛitelũ nɛ wɔnɔ kɛtakpu. Kemi kpɛkpɛ gbagbla alɛ mɛahɔɛ sɛ nɛ bɔlɔ bɔtɔ gelee mɛ tsyɛ, ɔtɔ̃kɔ nanà ma ɔkɔ mɛ samè mɔmɔ.

english_verse: After we came to Macedonia, we didn't have any chance to rest. We were faced with all kinds of problems. We were troubled by enemies and troubled by fears.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ gɛ bɔba Makedonia nɔ, bɔlɔ ishudzya ɛlɛ gimoele ɖi, pɔ bazã kpɛ bɔlɔ buluhũ nɛ kɛka nyia ka bhɛbhɛtɛ shu, ebela pɛpɛ ebui nɛ ɔtɔ̃ɔ, kɛmana kifu sisi nɛ bɔlɔ etúɔ mɛ.

english_verse: But God cheers up people in need, and this is what he did when he sent Titus to us.
translation_verse: Pɔ Owusoso Akaa gɛ ayɔ ishumɛ akɔ agɛ yɛ bezhini akpɛ kesiɛ, alɔkɔ Tito tɛbaba ayɔ bɔlɔ ishumɛ nɔ,

english_verse: Of course, we were glad to see Titus, but what really made us glad is the way you cheered him up. He told how sorry you were and how concerned you were about me. And this made me even happier.
translation_verse: kɛlɛ gɛnɛ yɛtɛbaba ete, pɔ nɛ ishumɛ yɔyɔ gɛ nɔlɔkɔ ɔkɔɛ shu so. Etseɖe bɔlɔ kana gɛ nɔpɛ ɔsɛ tɛ nɔmɔ mɛɛ, wɔnɔ ɔwɔ̃nyɔnɔ gbagblaa, kanà gɛ nɔbhɛtɛ ekpeɖekpeɖe mɛ nɛ mɛishuɔ, sɛ tɛ̀ mɛ samɛ mɔmɔ ekpè adɛ kana gɛ azã.

english_verse: I don't feel bad anymore, even though my letter hurt your feelings. I did feel bad at first, but I don't now. I know that the letter hurt you for a while.
translation_verse: Onupɛ nɔmɔ ɔwɔ̃nyɔnɔ ebui nɛ keplukpa gɛ ɛfɔnɔ sɛsɛ wɔnɔ mɛ tsyɛ elo, kɛgɛ tɔɖɔ mɛabha. Gɛ kɛtɔɖɔ mɛabha flogo tsyɛ elo-ɛmɔsɛ mɛ keplukpa mɛ ebuiɛ apɔɛ wɔnɔ, pɔ kɛba zãna ɔkɔ kibè gulũi ete-

english_verse: Now I am happy, but not because I hurt your feelings. It is because God used your hurt feelings to make you turn back to him, and none of you were harmed by us.
translation_verse: pɔ otsi alɛ xɔnɔ same eeku mɛ, gɛnɛ sɛ gɛ kɛtɔɖɔ wɔnɔ abha so, pɔ gɛ wɔnɔ ɔwɔ̃nyɔnɔ gbana wɔnɔ ɔkpɛ etú titsyitsyina ebuiɛ mɛ so. Ɔmɔsɛ nɔbɔ mɔ ɔwɔ̃nyɔnɔ asɛ kana gɛ Owusoso Akaa atɔɖɔ ɛlɛ, klisoɔ gɛnɛ bɔlɔ bhɛtɛ kibui gɛ kɛkpɛ kibì nanà nɛ wɔnɔ shu.

english_verse: When God makes you feel sorry enough to turn to him and be saved, you don't have anything to feel bad about. But when this world makes you feel sorry, it can cause your death.
translation_verse: Ɔwɔ̃nyɔnɔ mɔmɔ gɛode Owusoso Akaa mɛɛ, lɔmana kitú mɛ tsyitsyina gɛ gbana anɔ ɔkpɛ kɛtɔwui bubu mɛ gɛ kitinu kibui gɛ dɛ mɛulũ, pɔ kanɔ mɛ ɔwɔ̃nyɔnɔ xɔnɔ lɔmana butsí.

english_verse: Just look what God has done by making you feel sorry! You sincerely want to prove you are innocent. You are angry. You are shocked. You are eager to see that justice is done. You have proved that you were completely right in this matter.
translation_verse: Noɖi kɛgɛ ɔwɔ̃nyɔnɔ nɛ Owusoso Akaa mɛ ɔbhɛtɛ nɛ wɔnɔ ɛkpã mɛ, akɔ asɛ nɔkpɛ kemi nɛ kibui shu ɔdɛ, akɔ asɛ nɔpɛ ɔsɛ tɛ wɔnɔ shu dalɛ, akɔ asɛ nɔkpɛ ɔmɔpɔɛ nɛ kɛgɛ gɛpɛ̃ɛ shu, akɔ asɛ notisi kifù, akɔ asɛ noɖi kidzo mɛ ɔkɔ ebui teli, akɔ asɛ nɔlɔkɔ tili nɛebui mɛ, akɔ asɛ nɔpɛ ɔsɛ tɛ bapɔ̃ batɔ̃ akɔ anɔ gɛ atɛa bhɛtɛ kibui gbangbã. Nɛ ebuiɛ alɛ petee mɛɛ, nɔkasɛ ɔsɛ wɔnɔ shu dalɛ nɛ alɛ shu.

english_verse: When I wrote you, it wasn't to accuse the one who was wrong or to take up for the one who was hurt. I wrote, so God would show you how much you do care for us.
translation_verse: Kliso gɛ ɛfɔnɔ keplukpa kɔ wɔnɔ tsyɛ elo, gɛnɛ anɔ gɛ abhɛtɛ kibui kpa shu so alo anunɔ gɛ babhɛtɛ kibui kpa nɛishuɔ so, pɔ sɛ tɛ nɛ Owusoso Akaa namɛ, tɛ nɔlà mɔ ebuiɛ ɔkɔ ishu nɛ kana gɛ nɔmɔ kibè ɔkɔ bɔlɔɔ.

english_verse: And we were greatly encouraged. Although we were encouraged, we felt even better when we saw how happy Titus was, because you had shown he had nothing to worry about.
translation_verse: Nɛ ebui alɛ nyɔ̃ɔ mɛ, bɔmɔ kemi kpɛkpɛ. Ɔlɔkɔ ɔplɛnɔ bɔlɔ kemikpɛkpɛ, kɛzã bɔlɔ uli nɛ kɛmɔ gɛ Tito tsyɛ amɔ sameɛ shu, ɔmɔsɛ wɔnɔ petee nɔkɔ yɛ ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ lodzi ɔvɔvɔ.

english_verse: We had told him how much we thought of you, and you did not disappoint us. Just as we have always told you the truth, so everything we told him about you has also proved to be true.
translation_verse: Itũ mɛishu kitelũ nɛ wɔnɔ kɛtakpu kɔɛ, kɛlɛ nɔgɛ kpɛ mɛ buluhũ. Pɔ asɛ kana gɛ kɛka nyia ka gɛ butseɖe wɔnɔ lɛ kɛlɛ ɔzãkɔɔ, kidzo ɛlɛ nabha kitelũɔ gɛ butũ nɛ wɔnɔ shu ɔkɔ Tito nɔ, kasɛ sɛ kinu kibui gbã.

english_verse: Titus loves all of you very much, especially when he remembers how you obeyed him and how you trembled with fear when you welcomed him.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ yɛ tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔɔ ɔkɔ wɔnɔ zɛbà gbogblo dɛ gɛ onupɛ abakà ɔsɛ wɔnɔ nyɔ̃ɔ olũ batɔ̃, pɛ nɔyɛkɛ yɛ nɛ̀ kifusisi nɛ̀ tishushukù mɛ.

english_verse: It makes me really glad to know I can depend on you.
translation_verse: Same eku mɛ, kɛlɛ ɛbalà yɛkɛ wɔnɔ abha ŋã.

english_verse: My friends, we want you to know that the churches in Macedonia have shown others God's gift of undeserved grace.
translation_verse: Mɛ adzya alɛ, otsiɛ nɔ bɔpɛ sɛ tɛ̀ notì gibhuiɛ gɛ Owusoso Akaa ebhui Makedonia ɔhaa ebuɔ.

english_verse: Although they were going through hard times and were very poor, they were glad to give generously.
translation_verse: Ɔganà tifefekeɖi gagalĩ gɛ dɛ mɛɛ, balɛ sameɛ gɛ bamɔ nɛ balɛ ahɔɛ kɛdɔ̃ mazamazã mɛ tsyɛ elo, badalɛ ɛyamɛ pɛ bakɔ bɔdɔ̃.

english_verse: They gave as much as they could afford and even more, simply because they wanted to.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ ɛdalɛ kɛlɛ mɛ sɛ bakɔ bɔdɔ̃ nɛ kɛlà gɛ babalà akɔ nulũ, kɛlɛ bazakɔ adɛ kana gɛ babalàa. Bɔdɔ̃ kɔkɔ ɔlɛ petee de balɛ ahɔɛ mɛ,

english_verse: They even asked and begged us to let them have the joy of giving their money for God's people.
translation_verse: bakɔ bɔlɔ kɛtakpu odunu ɔlɛ sɛ bɔkɔ balɛ tsyɛ kidzo tɛ̀ balɛ tsyɛ amɔ tili edenɔ bɔlɔ bɔdɔ̃ kɔkɔ ɔkɔ banɔ nɔ gɛ balɛishu dalɛ mɛ̀

english_verse: And they did more than we had hoped. They gave themselves first to the Lord and then to us, just as God wanted them to do.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ bagɛ bhɛtɛ asɛ gɛ bɔpɛ nɔ, pɔ beputɔ alɔkɔ balɛishu akɔ Apaleteɛ nɛ bɔlɔ nɛ Owusoso Akaa tɛpɛpɛ ulũ.

english_verse: Titus was the one who got you started doing this good thing, so we begged him to help you finish what you had begun.
translation_verse: Kliso bɔkpɛ Tito Kemi ɔmɔsɛ yɛatɛkpɛ kɛxɔ̃ɛ niesi sɛ tɛ̀ alɔkɔ kɛlɛ amana kɛlɛ ɔtɔ̃kɔ soɔ avɛ kɛlɛ abha tɛ awɔa kɛlɛ ebeke.

english_verse: You do everything better than anyone else. You have stronger faith. You speak better and know more. You are eager to give, and you love us better. Now you must give more generously than anyone else.
translation_verse: Pɔ asɛ kana gɛ nɔmɔ otugbà vɛvɛ nɛ kɛka nyia ka mɛɛ-nɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛ̀, kaɖɔ ɖɔɖɔ mɛ̀, bubutì mɛ̀, nɛ̀ tɛpɛplɛnɔ ɔkɔ kɛxɔ̃ɛ nɛ kɛdɛmɛɛ gɛ nɔdɛmɛ bɔlɔ kibuiɛ-noɖi kɛlɛ ɔkɔ ɔsɛ nɔbhɛtɛ ɔdɛ nɛ tibhui bhuibu bɔdɔ̃ kɔkɔ kɛxɔ̃ɛ ɛlɛ mɛ.

english_verse: I am not ordering you to do this. I am simply testing how real your love is by comparing it with the concern that others have shown.
translation_verse: Gɛnɛ ɔsha mɛkɔ wɔnɔ, pɔ ɛpɛsɛ tɛ̀ mifeke wɔnɔ tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ ɖi sɛ kɛlɛ ɛlɛ ɔzãkɔ dza, kliso pɛ ɛlɔkɔ banɔ bhlatɔ kibui gbangbã tɛbhɛbhɛ shɔnɔ bagɔgɔ ɛyɛ nɔ.

english_verse: You know our Lord Jesus Christ treated us with undeserved grace by giving up all his riches, so you could become rich.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ nonyi kibhuiɛ gɛ Apalete Yesu ebhui bɔlɔ ebuɔ, pɔ gɛ edzi ehoeyi elete tsyɛ elo wɔnɔ shu so pɛ ebedzi etsyuitsyui, sɛ tɛ nɔmɔ ehoeyi bɔdɔ̃ ɔgana yɛitsyui dɔdɔ mɛ.

english_verse: A year ago you were the first ones to give, and you gave because you wanted to. So listen to my advice.
translation_verse: Mɛuluhũɔ gɛ ɛbà hu nɛ kɛgɛ pɛ̃ɔdɛ ɔkɔ wɔnɔ nɛ kibui ɛlɛ mɛ nɛ alɛ. Kɛgɔanàa wɔnɔ odzì banɔ gɛiputɔ, anɛ sɛ tɛ nɔkɔ kɛdɔ̃ ete, pɔ kide wɔnɔ kitú mɛ ɔsɛ tɛ nɔkɔ bɔdɔ̃ɔ.

english_verse: I think you should finish what you started. If you give according to what you have, you will prove you are as eager to give as you were to think about giving.
translation_verse: Otsiɛ nɔwɔa kɛxɔ̃ɛ obekè, tɛ kanà gɛ nɔpɛ ɔsɛ ɔwɔa ɛlɛ kɔ ɔsɛ nɔwɔa ɛlɛ obekè, odenɔ kɛgɛ lɛ wɔnɔ ahɔɛ mɛ.

english_verse: It doesn't matter how much you have. What matters is how much you are willing to give from what you have.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ onupɛ tɛpɛpɛ ɔkɔ kɛdɔ̃ kɔkɔ lɛɛ, baba yɛkɛ kɛgɛ ɔlàkɔ nɛ kɛgɛ lɛ wɔahɔɛ mɛ, gɛnɛ kɛgɛ ma wɔahɔɛ pɛ ɔtɛbɔlà kɔ shu.

english_verse: I am not trying to make life easier for others by making life harder for you. But it is only fair
translation_verse: Bɔlɔ tɛpɛpɛɛ kɛinu ɔsɛ tɛ bagɔgɔ atɛ̀ lɔkɔ kɛnanà nɛ kɛlɛ mɛ, pɛ wɔnɔ xɔnɔ ebui atɔɖɔ wɔnɔ abha, pɔ bɔpɛ ɔsɛ tɛ̀ kɛka nyia ka shɔ̃ piapii.

english_verse: for you to share with them when you have so much, and they have so little. Later, when they have more than enough, and you are in need, they can share with you. Then everyone will have a fair share,
translation_verse: Nɛ kibe ɛlɛ mɛ, wɔnɔ bɔdɔ̃ kɔkɔ kpekpèɛ banà kɔ alɛ, sɛ tɛ balɛ tsyɛ akɔ wɔnɔ kɛgɛ nɔpɛ nɔ. Kɛlɛ ebuiɛ petee abashɔ̃,

english_verse: just as the Scriptures say, “Those who gathered too much had nothing left. Those who gathered only a little had all they needed.” Titus and His Friends
translation_verse: asɛ gɛ bafɔnɔ elú ana asɛ, “Anunɔ gɛ yɛeshogo bɔdɔ̃ gelee nɔ bɔdɔ̃ gelee tɛzã yɛahɔɛ, kɛlɛ anunɔ gɛ yɛeshogo bɔdɔ̃ ɛbɔɛ nɔ, anɛ bɔdɔ̃ ɛbɔɛ bɔlɛ zã yɛahɔɛ.” Bakpɛ Tito Nɛ Korinto

english_verse: I am grateful that God made Titus care as much about you as we do.
translation_verse: Ihu Owusoso Akaa ahɔɛ, anɔ gɛ yɛakpɛ kɛgɛ mɛpɛ kɔ wɔnɔ nɛ̀ Tito tsyɛ yɛituɔ mɛ.

english_verse: When we begged Titus to visit you, he said he would. He wanted to because he cared so much for you.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ gɛnɛ bɔlɔ kibuiɛ ete Tito amana wɔnɔ, pɔ aaba wɔnɔ ɔkɔ nɛ̀ kemikpɛkpɛ gbagbla nɛ yɛahɔɛ mɛ ebui teli tsyɛ shu.

english_verse: With Titus we are also sending one of the Lord's followers who is well known in every church for spreading the good news.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ bɔɔkpɛ adzya teli plɛnii gɛ ɔhaa petee adɛmɛ nɔ nɛ yɛ kibui gbangbã axɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa shu so.

english_verse: The churches chose this follower to travel with us while we carry this gift that will bring praise to the Lord and show how much we hope to help.
translation_verse: Kɛgɛ zɛdɛɛ, ɔhaa yɛebú yɛ asɛ tɛ atɔnɔ bɔlɔ nɛ kibeɛ gɛ bɔlɔkɔ bɔdɔ̃kɔkɔ vɛnɔɔ, bɔdɔ̃ gɛ bɔdɛkɔ tɛ̀ bɔkpɛ̀ Apaleteɛ kinyè nɛitití, nɛ̀ tɛ bɔkasɛ ɔsɛ bɔpɛ sɛ tɛ̀ bɔplɛnɔ.

english_verse: We don't want anyone to find fault with the way we handle your generous gift.
translation_verse: Bɔgɛ pɛ sɛ tɛ baɖɔ kibui kpa nanà nɛ kana gɛ bɔkɔ ɛyamɛ gbangbã bɔdɔ̃ ɔlɛ shu.

english_verse: But we want to do what pleases the Lord and what people think is right.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ bɔkpɛ kemi pɛ bɔbhɛtɛ̀ kɛgɛ pɛ nɔ, gɛnɛ nɛ Apaleteɛ namɛ ete, pɔ nɛ banɔdzyrame tsyɛ amɛ.

english_verse: We are also sending someone else with Titus and the other follower. We approve of this man. In fact, he has already shown us many times that he wants to help. And now he wants to help even more than ever, because he trusts you so much.
translation_verse: Ɔlɔkɔ ɔplɛnɔɔ, bɔɔkpɛ̀ bɔlɔ adzya gɛ abhɛtɛ akɔ bɔmɔ sɛ apɛ asɛ tɛ̀ awɔa kɛxɔ̃ɛ asɛsɛ, kɛlɛ otsiɛ nɔ akapɛ asɛ tɛ̀ awɔa kɛlɛ atɔɖɔ ɔmɔsɛ ayɛkɛ wɔnɔ abha aŋã.

english_verse: Titus is my partner, who works with me to serve you. The other two followers are sent by the churches, and they bring honor to Christ.
translation_verse: Tito xɔnɔɔ, enu mɛɔbhia gɛenu mɛ axɔ̃ɛwɔa ɔhagbo nɛ wɔnɔɛishi, bɔlɔ badzya alɛ xɔnɔɔ, benu banɔ gɛ ɔhaa akpɛ asɛ tɛ bazã balɛ inye mɛ tɛ bakpɛ̀ Kristo kinyeɛ nɛitití.

english_verse: Treat them in such a way that the churches will see your love and will know why we bragged about you.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ, nɔkasɛ banɔ alɛ ɔsɛ nɔdɛmɛnɔ nɛ alɛ, nɛ kɛgɛ kɛlɛishu so pɛ butũ ahɔɛ nɛ kitelũ nɛ wɔnɔ kɛtakpuɔ tɛ̀ ɔhaa balà amɔ ɛlɛ.

english_verse: I don't need to write you about the money you plan to give for God's people.
translation_verse: Kɛbha ma ɔsɛ tɛ ɛfɔnɔ keplukpa kɔ wɔnɔ nɛ axɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa ebui alɛ nɛ banɔ dadalɛ shu.

english_verse: I know how eager you are to give. And I have proudly told the Lord's followers in Macedonia that you people in Achaia have been ready for a whole year. Now your desire to give has made them want to give.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ inyi kana gɛ nɔpɛ ɔsɛ tɛ nɔplɛnɔ, kɛlɛ itũ ahɔɛ kitelũ nɛ kɛlɛishu ɔkɔ Makedonianɛ, pɛ itseɖe balɛ̀ sɛ̀, denɔ kɛgɔanàa wɔnɔ bagɛ nɔlɛ̀ Axaya ɔpɛ ɔsɛ tɛ̀ nɔkɔ banɔ bɔdɔ̃, kɛlɛ kana gɛ nɔpɛ ɔsɛ tɛ nɔkɔ bɔdɔ̃ɔ abhɛtɛ akɔ asɛ banɔ kpekpèɛ tsyɛ apɛ asɛ tɛ bakɔ kɛdɔ̃.

english_verse: This is why I am sending Titus and the two others to you. I want you to be ready, just as I promised. This will prove we were not wrong to brag about you.
translation_verse: Pɔ ɛɛkpɛ badzyaa sɛsɛ tɛ bɔlɔ ahɔɛ gɛ butũ kitelũ nɛ wɔnɔ shu nɛ kibui ɛlɛ mɛɛ tìdzi kɛlɛɛ, pɔ nɔbalɛ olu asɛ kana gɛ itsèɖe balɛ nɛ wɔnɔ shuɔ, itseɖe alɛ sɛ nɔbɔ balɛ olu.

english_verse: Some followers from Macedonia may come with me, and I want them to find that you have the money ready. If you don't, I would be embarrassed for trusting you to do this. But you would be embarrassed even more.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ onupɛ Makedonia eyì teli atɔnɔ mɛ aba ɛkɛlɛ pɛ abamɔ wɔnɔ ɔsɛ nɔgɛ balɛ̀ oluɔ, bɔlɔɔ xɔnɔ bɔtɛ baɖɔ kibui nanà nɛ wɔnɔ shu, ɔmɔsɛ kiwũi bayi bɔlɔ nɛ kana gɛ bɔyɛkɛ wɔnɔ abha ŋãa shu.

english_verse: So I have decided to ask Titus and the others to spend some time with you before I arrive. This way they can arrange to collect the money you have promised. Then you will have the chance to give because you want to, and not because you feel forced to.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ aabhɛtɛ mɛasɛ kɛpɛ sɛ bɔkpalà badzya tɛ bebèɖi wɔnɔ kpɛ otugbà tɛ̀ bakpɛ kɛka nyia ka nɛ kɛlɛ ɔzãkɔ ɔkɔ bɔdɔ̃ kɔkɔ gɛ nɔtɔɖɔ olúɔ. Kɛlɛ bɔdɔ̃ kɔkɔ kibeɛ bana, batɛbà akɔ ɔlɛ nɛ amumu mɛ. Bɔdɔ̃ Ɖaɖa Nɛ̀ Ɛyamɛ Gbangbã

english_verse: Remember this saying, “A few seeds make a small harvest, but a lot of seeds make a big harvest.”
translation_verse: Nɔbakà kibui ɛlɛ ɔsɛ, anɔ gɛ aɖá bɔdɔ̃ bhlui nɔ, kɛdɔ̃ bhlui yɛaba apɔ̃. Kɛlɛ anunɔ gɛ aɖa bɔdɔ̃ nɛ ɛyamɛ gbangbã abapɔ bɔdɔ̃ nɛ̀ ɛyamɛ gbangbã

english_verse: Each of you must make up your own mind about how much to give. But don't feel sorry that you must give and don't feel you are forced to give. God loves people who love to give.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ sɛ anɔ ka nyia ka akɔ kɛgɛ atɔɖɔ asɛ akɔ edenɔ yitúɔ mɛ̀, gɛnɛ gana kibui tɔtɔɖɔ ɔkɔ banɔ abha, ɔmɔsɛ Owusoso Akaa adɛmɛ nɛ̀ anɔ gɛ akɔ kɛdɔ̃ nɛ̀ same.

english_verse: God can bless you with everything you need, and you will always have more than enough to do all kinds of good things for others.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ Owusoso Akaa abalà kɔ wɔnɔ tibhui bhuibuɔ kpè tɔɖɔ, sɛ tɛ̀ nɛ ebuiɛ pete mɛ nɛ kibè ka nyia ka mɛɛ, gɛ kɛka nyia ka gɛ nɔpɛ nɔ lɛ wɔnɔ ahɔɛ soɔ, nɔbɔ lɔkɔ wɔnɔshu ɔkpɛ kɛxɔ̃ɛ gbangbã ka nyia ka wɔawɔa mɛ.

english_verse: The Scriptures say, “God freely gives his gifts to the poor, and always does right.”
translation_verse: Asɛ gɛ bafɔnɔ elú asɛ, “Apɔ̃ɛ yɛɔdɔ̃ kɔkɔ mɛ akanà asɛsɛ bètsyuitsyuiɛ, yɛ kibui gbangbã tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ lɛ tihɔɔ.”

english_verse: God gives seed to farmers and provides everyone with food. He will increase what you have, so you can give even more to those in need.
translation_verse: Otsi nɔ, anɔ nɔ̀ gɛ yɛalɔkɔ bɔdɔ̃eyi akɔ anɔ gɛ yɛahɔɛ bɔdɔ̃eyi nɛ̀ kɛŋaŋã gɛ baŋã, yɛabakɔ asɛ wɔnɔ kibui gbangbã bhɛbhɛtɛ bɔdɔ̃eyi ɔbalɛ ɔkɔ ɔnɛ̀ɛ ɔtɔɖɔ, kɛlɛ abakɔ asɛ wɔnɔ tɛzazã piapi nɔ tsyɛ ba gbogblo ɔtɔɖɔ.

english_verse: You will be blessed in every way, and you will be able to keep on being generous. Then many people will thank God when we deliver your gift.
translation_verse: Babà bhɛtɛ wɔnɔ nobodzì ehoeyielete nɛ kidzo ka nyia ka abha sɛ tɛ̀ wɔnɔ tsyɛ wɔnɔ ɛyamɛ pɛ̃ kibè nyia bè, kɛlɛ gana bɔlɔ abhaa, wɔnɔ bɔdɔ̃ kɔkɔ badzi ahɔɛhuhu akɔ Owusoso Akaa.

english_verse: What you are doing is much more than a service that supplies God's people with what they need. It is something that will make many others thank God.
translation_verse: Axɔ̃ɛ alɛ gɛ nɔɔdɛ atɛakɔ bagɛ balɛenu Owusoso Akaa anɔ nɔ̀ ete bɔdɔ̃, pɔ buyuku balɛ gɛ balɔkɔ ehu Owusoso Akaa ahɔɛ nɔ.

english_verse: The way in which you have proved yourselves by this service will bring honor and praise to God. You believed the message about Christ, and you obeyed it by sharing generously with God's people and with everyone else.
translation_verse: Nɛ axɔ̃ɛ gɛ alɛishu nɔkpɛ kemiɛ pɛ nobu ishu kasɛ, banɔ abakpɛ̀ Owusoso Akaa nɛinye nɛitití nɛ wɔnɔ batɔ̃ lulũɔ gɛ akpà plɛnɔ wɔnɔ ebuimɛ dadalɛ nɛ Kristo shu, nɛ̀ wɔnɔ ɛyamɛ gbangbã gɛ nɔlɔkɔ ɔkɔ alɛ nɛ anɔ ka nyia ka.

english_verse: Now they are praying for you and want to see you, because God used you to bless them so very much.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ nɛ balɛ Owusoso Akaa tikukulu nɛ wɔnɔɛtakpu soɔ, balɛ etúɔ mɛ abazã wɔnɔ shu, nɛ tibhui bhuibuɔ gɛdɛ kɛka nyia ka gɛ Owusoso Akaa alɔkɔ akɔ wɔnɔ shu so.

english_verse: Thank God for his gift that is too wonderful for words!
translation_verse: Ahɔɛhuhu yɛenu Owusoso Akaa ayɛ nɛ yɛɔdɔ kɔkɔ gɛ bɔtɛ balà ɖɔ kaɖɔ nɛ bɔlɛ shuɔ.

english_verse: Do you think I am a coward when I am with you and brave when I am far away? Well, I ask you to listen, because Christ himself was humble and gentle.
translation_verse: Gana mɛishu esi zhizhinɔ nɛ Kristo mɛɛ, ibisi ɛlɛ denɔ wɔnɔ ahɔɛ mɛ, mɛ Paulo ɛbhɛtɛ ebui asɛ “anɔ gɛ esi kifú” nɛ wɔnɔ bamɛ pɔ ɛkpɛ kité gɛ onupɛ ɛlɛ kitsí nɛ wɔnɔ ɔbha.

english_verse: Some people have said we act like the people of this world. So when I arrive, I expect I will have to be firm and forceful in what I say to them. Please don't make me treat you that way.
translation_verse: Ɛkɔ wɔnɔ kɛtakpu sɛ onupɛ ɛbaa, ɛtɛ pɛ sɛ tɛ̀ ibú mɛishu kasɛ wɔnɔ asɛ anɔ gɛ etisì kifù tɛ ɛyɛkɛ̀ mɛishu abha ŋã. Pɔ abhɛtɛ wɔnɔ mɛayɛmɛ teli asɛ kanɔ alɛ mɛ bubùti bɔlɔkɔ bhɛtɛ̀ ebuiɛ nɔ.

english_verse: We live in this world, but we don't act like its people
translation_verse: Gɛ kanɔ nɔ̀ mɛ bɔlɛ tsyɛ elo, bɔtɛ lɔkɔ keyù asɛ kana gɛ kanɔ nɔ̀ mɛ anɛ abhɛtɛ̀ɛ.

english_verse: or fight our battles with the weapons of this world. Instead, we use God's power that can destroy fortresses. We destroy arguments
translation_verse: Keyuta ɔdɔ̃ gɛ bɔɔtà keyuɔ nɔɔ butinu kanɔ mɛ keyuta ɔdɔ̃. Nɛ kidzo bhlatɔ abhaa, ɔfɔfuĩa mɛ ohumi ɔlɛ balɛ ahɔɛ ɔsɛ tɛ balɔkɔ ayɛɛ ɔfɔfuĩa mɛ ebui gagalĩ nɔ.

english_verse: and every bit of pride that keeps anyone from knowing God. We capture people's thoughts and make them obey Christ.
translation_verse: Butũ ɔtsɛɖɛ kpɛ kawlɔ̃akɛ nɛ̀ asɛnɛ ebuiɛ gɛ alɔɛtití nɛ Owusoso Akaa yɛubutiɛ shuɔ mɛ. Kɛlɛ bɔŋã ekpeɖekpeɖe nɔnɔ gɛ atɛakɔ pɛ bɔbàlũ atɔ̃ ɔkɔ Kristoɔ nabha.

english_verse: And when you completely obey him, we will punish anyone who refuses to obey.
translation_verse: Kliso bɔbazã nɛ̀ awɔ sɛ tɛ bɔpɔ̃ batɔ̃ ɔkɔ batɔ̃mɛ gagalĩ ebui bhɛbhɛtɛ ka nyia ka ɛdi, gɛ onupɛ wɔnɔ batɔ̃lulũ aba anaa.

english_verse: You judge by appearances. If any of you think you are the only ones who belong to Christ, then think again. We belong to Christ as much as you do.
translation_verse: Wɔnɔ xɔnɔ kabha ebuiɛ ete nooɖì. Onupɛ anɔ teli ayɛkɛ yɛishu abha aŋã sɛ enu Kristoɔ ayɛ, ewlui eɖi kɛlɛ mɛ tɛ̀ amɔ asɛ bɔlɔ tsyɛ lɛ Kristo mɛ asɛ kɛlɛ gɛ alɛ ana.

english_verse: Maybe I brag a little too much about the authority that the Lord gave me to help you and not to hurt you. Yet I am not embarrassed to brag.
translation_verse: Onupɛ itũ ahɔɛ itelũ nɛ ohumi nɔ gɛ Apaleteɛ akɔ bɔlɔ gɛ bɔlɔkɔ bhɔ wɔnɔ wunɔ kabha ɔdɛ sɛ tɛ buzhini wɔnɔ esiɛ, kiwũi tɛba yimɛ nɛ ɛlɛ shu.

english_verse: And I am not trying to scare you with my letters.
translation_verse: Ɛgɛ pɛ sɛ tɛ abhɛtɛ asɛ ɛkpɛ wɔnɔ kifù nɛ̀ mɛ buplukpaa.

english_verse: Some of you are saying, “Paul's letters are harsh and powerful. But in person, he is a weakling and has nothing worth saying.”
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ beteli aɖɔ asɛ, “Bupù kpasɛ yɛ buplukpa mɛ kɛlɛ butũ kibui ɔkpɛ banɔ ulũ, pɔ nɛ yɛɔkɔɔ xɔnɔ atɛ akpɛ̀ banɔ kemi kɛlɛ yɛebuiɖɔɖɔ tsyɛ atɛ avɛ ɔkɔ nanà.”

english_verse: Those people had better understand that when I am with you, I will do exactly what I say in my letters.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ sɛ banɔ alɛ nɛidi etì asɛ ebui nɔnɔ gɛ bɔɖɔ nɛ bɔlɔ buplukpa mɛ gɛ onupɛ bɔmaa ɛkɛlɛ, bɔbà bhɛtɛ̀ alɛ abha gɛ onupɛ bɔlɛ wɔnɔ ɔbhaa.

english_verse: We won't dare compare ourselves with those who think so much of themselves. But they are foolish to compare themselves with themselves.
translation_verse: Bɔtɛ pɛ sɛ tɛ̀ lɔkɔ bɔlɔshu shɔnɔ beeteli gɛ bakpɛ balɛishu nɛitití ana. Onupɛ balɔkɔ balɛishu asɛ banɔ gbagbla pɛ balɔkɔ balɛishu ashɔnɔ ishuɔ, betinu bebutinɛ.

english_verse: We won't brag about something we don't have a right to brag about. We will only brag about the work God has sent us to do, and you are part of that work.
translation_verse: Bɔlɔ xɔnɔɔ, tɛbàtũ ahɔɛ nɛ etelũ falanɔ kifú, bɔbà lɔkɔ etelũɔ gɛ butũɔ kpɛ kɛxɔ̃ɛ nɔnɔ gɛ Owusoso Akaa akpɛ ahɔɛ akɔ bɔlɔ mɛ, axɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa gɛ leku wɔnɔ kɔɔ tsyɛ.

english_verse: We are not bragging more than we should. After all, we did bring the message about Christ to you.
translation_verse: Bɔtɛbà tũ etelũ falanɔ kifú faanɔ, asɛ gɛ kɛbà ba ɛlɛ mɛ gɛ onupɛ bɔgɛ ba wɔnɔ ɔbha ana, ɔmɔsɛ bɔlɔ lɔkɔ Kristo kibui gbangbã mana wɔnɔ.

english_verse: We don't brag about what others have done, as if we had done those things ourselves. But I hope as you become stronger in your faith, we will be able to reach many more of the people around you. That has always been our goal.
translation_verse: Buti tũ ahɔɛ bɔlɔ etelũ falanɔ kifù faanɔ nɛ kɛxɔ̃ɛ gɛ banɔ bhlatɔ awɔa shu. Kɛgɛ buɖi kidzo mɛ kɔ kɛlɛinu ɔsɛ, kibeɛ gɛ wɔnɔ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã bakpe tɔɖɔɔ, bɔlɔ axɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa nɛ wɔnɔɛishiɛ tsyɛ banɛɛ tɔɖɔ.

english_verse: Then we will be able to preach the good news in other lands where we cannot take credit for work someone else has already done.
translation_verse: Sɛ tɛ bɔla ɖɔ Owusoso Akaa nɛibuiɛ nɛ busiakpa gɛ lɛ wɔnɔ kede ɔkɔ ɔlɛ. Ɔmɔsɛ bɔtɛpɛ sɛ tɛ butũ ahɔɛ etelũ nɛ axɔ̃ɛ nɔnɔ gɛ banɔ bhlatɔ awɔa nɛ busiakpa bhlatɔ abha.

english_verse: The Scriptures say, “If you want to brag, then brag about the Lord.”
translation_verse: Pɔ “Anunɔ gɛ apɛ asɛ tɛ etũ ahɔɛ nɛ etelũɔ, etũ ahɔɛ etelũ nɛ Apaleteɛ mɛ.”

english_verse: You may brag about yourself, but the only approval that counts is the Lord's approval.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ gɛnɛ anunɔ gɛ aɖɔaɖɔ akpɛ yɛishu nɛititíɛ yɛabha batɔɖɔ ahɔɛ, pɔ anunɔ gɛ Apaleteɛ aɖɔaɖɔ nɛishuɔ.

english_verse: Please put up with a little of my foolishness.
translation_verse: Iɖi kidzo mɛ sɛ nɔbɔ kpɛ kemì nɛ mɛ bɔgadɔdɔ ɔlɛ shu kɛbɔɛ, pɔ nɔvɛlɔ bhɛtɛ ɛlɛ.

english_verse: I am as concerned about you as God is. You were like a virgin bride I had chosen only for Christ.
translation_verse: Ɛlɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa mɛ tɛzazã wɔlãkɛ kabhã nɛ wɔnɔ shu. Ɛtɔɖɔ ɛlɛ lu kɔ wɔnɔ sɛ nɔzanà anyɛ tenikpo gɛenu Kristoɔ, sɛ tɛ ɛlɔkɔ wɔnɔ kpɛ ahɔɛ kɔɛ asɛ adzɛ gɛ etinyi anyɛ ana.

english_verse: But now I fear that you will be tricked, just as Eve was tricked by that lying snake. I am afraid that you might stop thinking about Christ in an honest and sincere way.
translation_verse: Pɔ kifu yimɛ sɛ asɛ kana gɛ eblidzya alɔkɔ bɔshɛ edzo awɔɛ Eva nɔ, babà tsyina wɔnɔ tsyɛ ekpeɖekpeɖe mɛ ashɛnɛ nɛ wɔnɔ kibui gbangbã tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ nɛ̀ wɔnɔ ishu tɛlɔlɔkɔ ɔkɔ Kristoɔ shu.

english_verse: We told you about Jesus, and you received the Holy Spirit and accepted our message. But you let some people tell you about another Jesus. Now you are ready to receive another spirit and accept a different message.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ onupɛ anɔ teli aba wɔnɔ ɔkɔ pɛ abaɖɔ kibui nɛ Yesu teli shu ɔdɛ agɛ yɛ shu bɔɖɔ kaɖɔɔ, alo onupɛ nɔyɛkɛ ɔfɔfuĩa bhlatɔ ɔdɛ ɔgɛ ɔlɛ nɔtɛyɛkɛ shuɔ, alo kibui gbangbã bhlatɔ gɛ kɛgã ɔtɔ denɔ kɛgɛ kɛlɛ nɔtɛ yɛkɛ shuɔ, kɛlɛ notsyina tsiɔ̃ faanɔ.

english_verse: I think I am as good as any of those super apostles.
translation_verse: Pɔ batɛbà lɔkɔ mɔzãkɔ ɔlɔ ashɔnɔ bagɛ nokulù ɔsɛ “Banɔkpɛkpɛ alɛ gɛ balɛ ɔzãkɔ ɔɖama.”

english_verse: I may not speak as well as they do, but I know as much. And this has already been made perfectly clear to you.
translation_verse: Itinu anɔ gɛ kpasɛ kaɖɔ ɖɔɖɔ piapii, pɔ kibuititì xɔnɔ lɛ mɛahɔɛ. Bɔkɔ sɛ kibui ɛlɛmɛ dalɛ̃ kɔ wɔnɔ piapii nɛ kidzo ka nyia ka abha.

english_verse: Was it wrong for me to lower myself and honor you by preaching God's message free of charge?
translation_verse: Kibui kpa kinu ɔkɔ mɛ ɔsɛ tɛ̀ izhinì mɛishu esi tɛ ɛbhɛtɛ wɔnɔ nowu kabha ɔganà Owusoso Akaa kibuiɛ gbangbã tɛɖɔɖɔ otseɖe wɔnɔ kɛbhɛ mabuia mabuia mɛ dza?

english_verse: I robbed other churches by taking money from them to serve you.
translation_verse: Gana tɛpɛplɛnɔ gɛ ɛyɛkɛ denɔ ɔhaa bhlatɔ mɛɛ ɛlɔkɔ ba plɛnɔ wɔnɔ nɔ.

english_verse: Even when I was in need, I still didn't bother you. In fact, some of the Lord's followers from Macedonia brought me what I needed. I have not been a burden to you in the past, and I will never be a burden.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ kibeɛ gɛ ɛzã wɔnɔ ɔbha pɛ ɛzɛpɛ kɛdɔ̃ tiliɛ, igidzi bɔtɔ ɔkɔ ananà, ɔmɔsɛ badzya gɛede Makedonia nɔ bakɔ mɛ mɛɔdɔ̃ pɛpɛ nɔ. Ɛgɛpɛ sɛ tɛ mɛidzì bɔtɔ ɔkɔ wɔnɔ nɛ kidzo nanà abha, kɛlɛ ɛbazã kɛlɛ abha zɛbhɛtɛ ana.

english_verse: As surely as I speak the truth about Christ, no one in Achaia can stop me from bragging about this.
translation_verse: Gɛ kibui gbã Kristo kibui gɛlɛ ɛlɛ ɔzãkɔ kpasɛ mɛmɛ soɔ, anɔ ka nyia ka gɛede Axaya bɔdɔ̃ mɛ atɛ balà aɖɔ asɛ kibuiɛ gɛ itũ ahɔɛ nɛ mɛitelũɔ ɖɔ ma ɛlɛ mɛ.

english_verse: And it isn't because I don't love you. God himself knows how much I do love you.
translation_verse: Kiɖe so? Pɛnɔsɛ ɛgɛ dɛmɛ nɛ wɔnɔ ibui soɔ dza? Owusoso Akaa xɔnɔ enyi asɛ ɛdɛmɛ wɔnɔɛ ibui!

english_verse: I plan to go on doing just what I have always done. Then those people won't be able to brag about doing the same things we are doing.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ ɛbazã ɛlɛ abha zɛbhɛtɛ kɛgɛ ɛlɛ mɛbhɛtɛɛ, ɔsɛ tɛ̀ mɛibu bagɛ bapɛ kidzo asɛ tɛ̀ balɛ nɛ bɔlɔ shɔ nɛ ebuiɛ gɛ alɛ shu butũ ahɔɛ nɛ ɔlɔ ɛtelũɔ yinɔ.

english_verse: Anyway, they are no more than false apostles and dishonest workers. They only pretend to be apostles of Christ.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ banyɛ alɛ nɛidi enu basɛnɛ Kristo banɔkpɛkpɛ, baxɔ̃ɛwɔa gɛ bawɔɛ banɔ, gɛ babhɛtɛ balɛishu asɛ Kristo yɛanɔkpɛkpɛ benu ana.

english_verse: And it is no wonder. Even Satan tries to make himself look like an angel of light.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ kɛgɛ bhɛtɛ kibi, ɔmɔsɛ Satana tsyɛ abhɛtɛ̀ yɛishu asɛ axɔ̃ɛdɛ dadalɛ ana.

english_verse: So why does it seem strange for Satan's servants to pretend to do what is right? Someday they will get exactly what they deserve. Paul's Sufferings for Christ
translation_verse: Klisoɔ kɛgɛ bhɛtɛ ibì gɛ onupɛ yɛaxɔ̃ɛdɛ abhɛtɛ balɛishu asɛ baxɔ̃ɛdɛ gbangbã ana. Babà yɛkɛ kɛgɛ shɔnɔ asɛ bayɛkɛ nɛ balɛ axɔ̃ɛdɛdɛ alɛ niede. Paulo Etũ Ahɔɛ Yɛetelũ Nɛ Yɛɔtɔ Shu

english_verse: I don't want any of you to think I am a fool. But if you do, then let me be a fool and brag a little.
translation_verse: Ɛkaɖɔ sɛ, Ananà atɛ lɔkɔ mɛasɛ awɔaga mɛinu. Pɔ onupɛ nɔbhɛtɛ ɛlɛ ana, kɛlɛ nɔyɛkɛ mɛ asɛ gɛ nɔbɔbhɛtɛ ɔkɔ awɔaga ana, sɛ tɛ ɛlà tũ ahɔɛ mɛitelũɔ kɛbɔɛ.

english_verse: When I do all this bragging, I do it as a fool and not for the Lord.
translation_verse: Nɛ ishu abha ɛlɛ gɛ ɛyɛkɛŋã nɛ mɛitelũɔ gɛ itũ shuɔ, ɛtɛɖɔ kaɖɔ asɛ gɛ Apaleteɛ abaɖɔ, pɔ asɛ awɔaga ana.

english_verse: Yet if others want to brag about what they have done, so will I.
translation_verse: Gɛ banɔ kpekpèɛ etũ ahɔɛ nɛetelũ asɛ kanɔ mɛ elete ana soɔ, mɛtsyɛ batũ ahɔɛ nɛ mɛitelũ.

english_verse: And since you are so smart, you will gladly put up with a fool.
translation_verse: Nɔlɔkɔ same ɔzã plɛnɔ bawɔaga ɔmɔsɛ bebutinɛ nonu so!

english_verse: In fact, you let people make slaves of you and cheat you and steal from you. Why, you even let them strut around and slap you in the face.
translation_verse: Nɛ kibui gbã mɛɛ, nɔzã ɔplɛnɔ bagɛ abhɛtɛ wɔnɔ asɛ banɔ dzidziɛ kura tsyɛ, pɛ bedzyinì wɔnɔkpɛ alo pɛ bamɔ̃ kidzo nɛ wɔnɔ shu so, alo pɛ bafala eputɔ pɛ amanà wɔnɔ kɛdɔ nɛ bamɛ.

english_verse: I am ashamed to say we are too weak to behave in such a way. If they can brag, so can I, but it is a foolish thing to do.
translation_verse: Kiwũi kinu ɔkɔ mɛ tɛ ɛdɛmɛ tɔɖɔ ɛlɛ abha sɛ buzhi esi ana popopo! Kibui ka nyia ka gɛ anɔ teli alà etũ ahɔɛ etelũ akɔɔ, mɛxɔnɔ ɖɔ ɛlɛ asɛ awɔaga ana, mɛtsyɛ balà tũ kitelũ nɛlɛ shu.

english_verse: Are they Hebrews? So am I. Are they Jews? So am I. Are they from the family of Abraham? Well, so am I.
translation_verse: Anɛ Yudaeyi benuɔ dza? Yudaeyi mɛtsyɛ inu. Anɛ Israelanɛ benuɔ dza? Israeleyi mɛtsyɛ inu. Anɛ Abraham yɛeshui alɛ benuɔ dza? Abraham Yɛeshui mɛtsyɛ inu.

english_verse: Are they servants of Christ? I am a fool to talk this way, but I serve him better than they do. I have worked harder and have been put in jail more times. I have been beaten with whips more and have been in danger of death more often.
translation_verse: Kristo yɛaxɔ̃ɛdɛ benuɔ dza? pɔ igbogblo dɛ alɛ. Ɛwɔa axɔ̃ɛ gagalĩ ɔdɛ balɛ, ɛzã ɛɖa buvũ mɛ gelee ɔdɛ balɛ, behuisi mɛ ɖɔtɔɖɔtɔ dɛ balɛ, kɛlɛ ɛzakɔ butsi kibe nyia be.

english_verse: Five times my own people gave me
translation_verse: Buhuisi titì behuisi mɛ gɛ ɛyɛkɛ bushi afɛata nɛ̀ tuzhita odenɔ Yudanɛ ahɔɛ mɛ.

english_verse: Three times the Romans beat me with a big stick, and once my enemies stoned me. I have been shipwrecked three times, and I even had to spend a night and a day in the sea.
translation_verse: Buhuisi tɛtà behuisi mɛ nɛ̀ ɔɖaɛshi, bututinikpo behu mɛ aklagbu, bɔyɛɛ tɛtà kelĩabha ohu ɔyɛɛ nɛ mɛ, ɛzã aƒu nabha kiwui tinikpo nɛ̀ elishi tenikpo,

english_verse: During my many travels, I have been in danger from rivers, robbers, my own people, and foreigners. My life has been in danger in cities, in deserts, at sea, and with people who only pretended to be the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: ɛzã kidzo gagã abha kibe nyia be. Ɛzã butsi nɛ ɛkpã ishi, odenɔ bulĩukekpù mɛ, banɔ kpa, ɛzã butsi nɛ̀ ɛkpã ishi odenɔ mɛahɔɛ banɔ nɔ tɛhɔɛ mɛ, nɛ̀ bagɛ betinu Yudanɛ tsyɛ tɛhɔɛ mɛ, ɛzã butsi nɛ̀ ɛkpã ishi odenɔ ɔma gbagbla mɛ, nɛ̀ bayàa mɛ, nɛ aƒu nabha, nɛ̀ badzya gɛenu basɛnɛ elete mɛ.

english_verse: I have worked and struggled and spent many sleepless nights. I have gone hungry and thirsty and often had nothing to eat. I have been cold from not having enough clothes to keep me warm.
translation_verse: Ɛwɔa axɔ̃ɛ gagalĩ, kɛlɛ ɛmɔ bɔtɔ, kɛlɛ ɛzɛ gbã tɛwɔnɔ̃, ɛzanà bɔbhɛ nɛ̀ oluwɔɛ, kɛlɛ ɛzɛ fala kɛdɔ̃ maŋamaŋã, bɔbɔlɔ ziyi mɛ kɛlɛ ɛzã kɛdɔ̃ makpɛ makpɛ.

english_verse: Besides everything else, each day I am burdened down, worrying about all the churches.
translation_verse: Nɛ alɛ petee edeɛ, ɔhaa ebuiɛ akɔ ɛgɛzɛ mɔ ɔhɔɛ nanà.

english_verse: When others are weak, I am weak too. When others are tricked into sin, I get angry.
translation_verse: Afana gɛ ebotsò pɛ mɛ xɔnɔ gɛibotsoɔ dza? Afana bagbana akpɛ kibui kpa mɛ pɛ kɛtɛ pɔɛ mɛishuɔ mɛɛ dza?

english_verse: If I have to brag, I will brag about how weak I am.
translation_verse: Onupɛ kɛlɛ ɔsɛ itũ ahɔɛ nɛ mɛitelũɔ, ɛbatũ ahɔɛ nɛitélũ nɛ ebuiɛ gɛ akasɛ ɔsɛ ibotsoɔ shu.

english_verse: God, the Father of our Lord Jesus, knows I am not lying. And God is to be praised forever!
translation_verse: Owusoso Akaa gɛenu Akaa ɔkɔ Apalete Yesuɔ, agɛ yɛ kɛlɛ ɔsɛ bakpɛ yɛinye nɛitití tihɔɔ nɔ enyi ɔsɛ ɛtɛ sɛ asɛnɛ.

english_verse: The governor of Damascus at the time of King Aretas had the city gates guarded, so he could capture me.
translation_verse: Nɛ Damasko, kɛmadi ekusiɛ gɛ akpasɛ ekusi gbagbla Aretas esiɛ akɔ sɛ bazãepo Damasene ɔmagbagbla sɛ tɛ bevũ mɛ so.

english_verse: But I escaped by being let down in a basket through a window in the city wall.
translation_verse: Pɔ balɔkɔ mɛ akpɛ kashɔ mɛ pɛ bezhinì mɛ aganà kedulũ bhlui nɔ gɛ akpasɛ kigiliɛ mɛ.

english_verse: I have to brag. There is nothing to be gained by it, but I must brag about the visions and other things that the Lord has shown me.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ sɛ tɛ ɛzã kɛlɛ abha tɛ itũ ahɔɛ nɛ mitelũ. Kɛnanà ma gɛ ɛbamɔ nɛlɛ mɛ pɔ, ɛbazã kɛlɛ abha bhɛtɛ tɔɖɔ mɛɔdɔ̃ mɔmɔ nɛ tibubukasɛ gɛide Apaleteɛ ɔkɔ nabha.

english_verse: I am planning to visit you for the third time. But I still won't make a burden of myself. What I really want is you, and not what you have. Children are not supposed to save up for their parents, but parents are supposed to take care of their children.
translation_verse: Otsiɛ nɔ ɛbalɛ beke sɛ tɛ̀ ɛbaɖi wɔnɔ bɔtramɛ, kɛlɛ ɛtɛ badzi bɔtɔ kɔ wɔnɔ, ɔmɔsɛ gɛnɛ wɔnɔ bɔdɔ̃ gɛ nɔmɔ mɛpɛ, pɔ wɔnɔ mɛpɛ. Nɛ alɛ nyɔɔ edeɛ, gɛnɛ bunuvɔɛ kɛlɛ sɛ tɛ beɖi balɛ elĩ nɛ̀ balɛ akaa alɛ nabha, pɔ beyi akaa nɛ̀ beyielĩ kɛlɛ sɛ tɛ beɖi balɛ eyi alɛ abha.

english_verse: As I said, only God really knows if this man was in his body at the time.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ inyi sɛ anyɛ alɛ, onupɛ ishupi mɛ alo gɛnɛ ishupi mɛ o, mɛxɔnɔ tinyi, Owusoso Akaa ete yɛenyi ɔsɛ

english_verse: But he was taken up into paradise, where he heard things too wonderful to tell.
translation_verse: bavɛnɔ yɛ nɛ paradiso mɛ. Elũ ebui gɛ atɛ balà alɛ esi bu, ebui gɛ bagɛ kɔ anɔ kidzo sɛ tɛ alà aɖɔ.

english_verse: I will brag about that man, but not about myself, except to say how weak I am.
translation_verse: Ɛbatũ mɛitelũ nɛ anyɛ alɛ nɛidi shu, pɔ ɛtɛ batũ kitelũ ɔkɔ mɛishu, ɔdɛ mɛ bɔtɔ shu ete.

english_verse: Yet even if I did brag, I would not be foolish. I would simply be speaking the truth. But I will try not to say too much. That way, none of you will think more highly of me than you should because of what you have seen me do and say.
translation_verse: Onupɛ edzi asɛ ɛpɛ sɛ tɛ mɛitũ ahɔ nɛitũ tsyɛ elo, ɛtɛ badzi awɔaga ɔmɔsɛ ɛbazã ɛlɛ abha zɛɖɔ kibuiɛ gɛlɛ ɛlɛ ɔzãkɔ. Pɔ ɛzakà tũ mɛitelũ, sɛ tɛ anɔ nanà etì beɖi mɛ asɛ mɛinu anɔ gbagbla ɔdɛ ɔganà kɛgɛ ɛbhɛtɛ pɛ ɛɖɔ shu.

english_verse: Of course, I am now referring to the wonderful things I saw. One of Satan's angels was sent to make me suffer terribly, so that I would not feel too proud.
translation_verse: Tɛ kibu mɛ oyinɔ denɔ ishu fefeke mɛ gɛ, nɛ ebui gɛ labhɛtɛ kibi alɛ gɛ bebu akasɛ mɛ so, balɔkɔ kiwú akpɛ mɛishuɔ mɛ, gɛenu Satana yɛanɔkpɛkpɛ sɛ tɛ̀, ahɔhɔlɛ mɛ.

english_verse: Three times I begged the Lord to make this suffering go away.
translation_verse: Bɔtɛ tɛtà mɛkɔ Apalete nɛtakpu sɛ tɛ ebu mɛ eyinɔ edenɔ kɛlɛ mɛ.

english_verse: But he replied, “My gift of undeserved grace is all you need. My power is strongest when you are weak.” So if Christ keeps giving me his power, I will gladly brag about how weak I am.
translation_verse: Pɔ etseɖe mɛasɛ, “Mɛ tibhui bhuibuɔ kpè kɔ wɔnɔ, ɔmɔsɛ mɛ ohuminɔ ɔna denɔ tibobotsòɔ mɛ.” Klisoɔ ɛbatũ mɛitelũ tɔɖɔ nɛ̀ same nɛ mɛ tibobotsò nulũ, sɛ tɛ Kristo yɛohumi nɔ zã mɛabha.

english_verse: Yes, I am glad to be weak or insulted or mistreated or to have troubles and sufferings, if it is for Christ. Because when I am weak, I am strong. Paul's Concern for the Lord's Followers at Corinth
translation_verse: Kliso pɛ nɛ Kristo yɛtakpuɔ, ɛmɔ same nɛ tibobotsò mɛ, nɛ akwalatu mɛ, nɛ ebui gagalĩ mɛ, nɛ banɔede tɛtɔtɔnɔ mɛ nɛ̀ bɔtɔ mɛ. Ɔmɔsɛ onupɛ ibotsoɔ, ɛkɛlɛ mɛmɔ tɛgagalĩ. Kɛgɛ Paulo Apɛ Akɔ Korintoanɛ

english_verse: I have been making a fool of myself. But you forced me to do it, when you should have been speaking up for me. I may be nothing at all, but I am as good as those super apostles.
translation_verse: Ɛdɔ bɔwɔaga, pɔ wɔnɔ gbana mɛɔkpɛ bɔlɛ tɛdɔdɔ mɛ. Tsyɛ alo kɛlɛ sɛ nɔɖɔ kibui gbangbã nɛ mɛishu flogo ɔmɔsɛ itinu agɔgɔɛ ɔkɔ “banɔkpɛkpɛ gɛ balɛ ɔzakɔ gbagbla” pɔ mɛxɔnɔ tinu kɛdɔ̃ nanà kɔ alɛ flogo.

english_verse: When I was with you, I was patient and worked all the powerful miracles and signs and wonders of a true apostle.
translation_verse: Kɛgɛ kɔ bamɔ anɔ ɔsɛ enu anɔ kpɛkpɛ yɛenu ebui bubukasɛ, ebui gɛ abhɛtɛ ibi nɛ̀ atsyindzye ebui alɛ babhɛtɛ nɛ wɔnɔɛshi nɛ kemikpɛkpɛ.

english_verse: You missed out on only one blessing the other churches received. That is, you didn't have to support me. Forgive me for doing you wrong.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ nɔzã asɛ banɔ bhlui ɔkɔ ɔhaa bhlatɔ, gɛ idzi bɔtɔ kɔ wɔnɔ flogo nɔɔ dza? Nɔlɔkɔ kesitata alɛ tsye mɛ!

english_verse: So I will gladly give all I have and all I am. Will you love me less for loving you too much?
translation_verse: Klisoɔ ɛbalɔkɔ same zĩ kɛka nyia ka gɛlɛ mɛahɔɛ ɔlɔkɔ plɛnɔ mɛishu nɛ wɔnɔɛtakpu. Onupɛ ɛdɛmɛ wɔnɔɛbui tɔɖɔ, wɔnɔ xɔnɔ ɔbɔ dɛmɛ mɛibui bhlui dza?

english_verse: You agree that I wasn't a burden to you. Maybe that's because I was trying to catch you off guard and trick you.
translation_verse: Asɛ kana gɛ kɛlɛ ana, itinu bɔtɔ kɔ wɔnɔ. Pɔ bɔshɛ mɛ anɔ mɛinu, kliso ivũ wɔnɔ nɛ mɛ bɔshɛ wɔawɔa mɛ!

english_verse: Were you cheated by any of those I sent to you?
translation_verse: Idzyini wɔnɔkpɛ gana banyɛ teli gɛ ɛkpɛ nɛ wɔnɔkɔ mɛ dza?

english_verse: I urged Titus to visit you, and I sent another follower with him. But Titus didn't cheat you, and we felt and behaved the same way he did.
translation_verse: Ɛkpalà Tito sɛ tɛ aba wɔnɔkɔ, kɛlɛ ɛkpɛ ɔlɔ adzya plɛnɔ yɛ. Tito egidzyini wɔnɔ akpɛ, alo edzyini wɔnɔ akpɛɛ dza? Bɔgɛ bhɛtɛ ebui gana ɔfɔfuĩa tonikpo abha pɛ bɔzã kibui tinikpo bhɛbhɛtɛ abha dza?

english_verse: Have you been thinking all along that we have been defending ourselves to you? Actually, we have been speaking to God as followers of Christ. But, my friends, we did it all for your good.
translation_verse: Aabhɛtɛ wɔnɔ sɛ bɔpɛ asɛ tɛ bubu bɔlɔshu ulũ nɛ wɔnɔ amɛ dza? Bɔɔɖɔ kaɖɔ nɛ Owusoso Akaa amɛ asɛ banɔ gɛ akpasɛ Kristo mɛ ana, mɛadzya alɛ gɛ ɛdɛmɛ nɔ, kɛka nyia ka gɛ bɔbhɛtɛ, bɔbhɛtɛ ɛlɛ ɔkɔ wɔnɔ sɛ tɛ nɔzã gagalĩ.

english_verse: I am afraid when I come, we won't be pleased with each other. I fear that some of you may be arguing or jealous or angry or selfish or gossiping or insulting each other. I even fear that you may be proud and acting like a mob.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ kifù yimɛ sɛ onupɛ ɛba wɔnɔ ɔkɔ, ɛtɛbà mɔ wɔnɔ asɛ kana gɛ ɛpɛ sɛ nɔzã, kɛlɛ wɔnɔ tsyɛ ɔtɛbɔ mɔ mɛ asɛ kana gɛ nɔpɛ ɔsɛ tɛ mɛzã. Kifù yimɛ ɔsɛ ebelà pɛpɛ ebui, kabhãwawlakɛ ebui, ɔmɔpɔɛ kpɛkpɛ, ɔhakpa yaya, kaɖɔ kpa tɛɖɔɖɔ, banɔ shu tizizĩ, ishu fefeke nɛ̀ kawlɔ̃akɛ ebui ebeku wɔnɔ mɛ.

english_verse: I am afraid God will make me ashamed when I visit you again. I will feel like crying because many of you have never given up your old sins. You are still doing things that are immoral, indecent, and shameful.
translation_verse: Kifù yimɛ sɛ onupɛ ɛzɛba, mɛ Owusoso Akaa abakɔ mɛizhini mɛishu esi nɛ wɔnɔ amɛ, kɛlɛ ɛbàmɔ ɔwɔ̃nyɔnɔ denɔ banɔ gelee gɛ batɛ bhɛtɛ ebui kpa akpɛ otugbà pɛ begitsyina nɛ balɛ etúɔ mɛ odenɔ atsɔ̃bui bhɛbhɛtɛ mɛ, ebui asɛ ɔmamɛ dɛdɛ nɛ̀ bunu tibubuku ebui gɛ balɔkɔ balɛishu ashɔkpɛ mɛ.

english_verse: I am on my way to visit you for the third time. And as the Scriptures say, “Any charges must be proved true by at least two or three witnesses.”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ badzi bɔtramɛ gɛ ɛba wɔnɔ ɔbha. “Nɛ kibui ka nyia ka tɛyɛyɛkɛ mɛɛ, kɛlɛ sɛ banɔ taabha alo tata aɖɔaɖɔ adalɛ kɛlɛ shu flogo.”

english_verse: During my second visit I warned you that I would punish you and anyone else who doesn't stop sinning. I am far away from you now, but I give you the same warning.
translation_verse: Ɛti tseɖe nɛ̀ wɔnɔ bafala nɔ nɛ kibeɛ gɛ ɛba wɔnɔ ɔkɔ bɔbhalà. Ɛka wlui ɖɔ ɛlɛ nɛ kibeɛ gɛ ɛma wɔnɔ ɔbha. Onupɛ itsyina ba, ɛtɛba bi bagɛ abhɛtɛ ebui kpa bafala nɔ nɛ̀ bagɔgɔɛ tsyɛ ashagabui,

english_verse: This should prove to you that I am speaking for Christ. When he corrects you, he won't be weak. He will be powerful!
translation_verse: gɛ nɔpɛ ɔsɛ oti kɛgbã sɛ Kristo aɖɔaɖɔ ɔgana mɛabha. Kristo egi botso nɛ kɛnanà bhɛbhɛtɛ nɛ wɔnɔ, pɔ ohumi ɔlɛ yɛahɔɛ nɛ wɔnɔɛshi.

english_verse: Although he was weak when he was nailed to the cross, he now lives by the power of God. We are weak, just as Christ was. But you will see that we will live by the power of God, just as Christ does.
translation_verse: Tɛ kɛzã tititì mɛ, bakpalɛ yɛ nɛ butsi osi shu nɛ tibobotsò mɛ, pɔ alɛ nɛkpã nɛ Owusoso Akaa ohumi mɛ. Kidzo ɛlɛ nabha bɔlɔ tsyɛ bubotso nɛ yɛ mɛ, pɔ nɛ Owusoso Akaa ohumi mɛ bɔbàza plɛni tɛ bɔdɛ axɔ̃ɛ kɔ wɔnɔ.

english_verse: Test yourselves and find out if you really are true to your faith. If you pass the test, you will discover that Christ is living in you. But if Christ isn't living in you, you have failed.
translation_verse: Nɔvɛ wɔnɔ ishumɛ oɖi sɛ nɔlɛ wɔnɔ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛ dza, nɔ ofeke wɔnɔ ishu oɖi. Nɔtɛɔ mɔ ɛlɛ ɔsɛ Yesu Kristo akpasɛ wɔnɔ mɛ, pɛnɔsɛ nofeke wɔnɔ shu oɖi pɛ nɔmɔ ɔsɛ nɔgɛ naa dza?

english_verse: I hope you will discover we have not failed.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ ɛyɛkɛ ŋã sɛ nɔmɔ ɛlɛ ɔsɛ bɔŋã kabha nɛ tifefekeɖiɛ mɛ.

english_verse: We pray you will stop doing evil things. We don't pray like this to make ourselves look good, but to get you to do right, even if we are failures.
translation_verse: Otsi nɔ bukulu Owusoso Akaa sɛ nɔtɛ bɔbhɛtɛ kibui kpa nanà. Gɛnɛ gɛ banɔ abamɔ̃ asɛ bɔŋã kabha nɛ tifefekeɖiɛ mɛ so, pɔ sɛ tɛ nɔbhɛtɛ kibui nɔnɔ gɛpɛ̃ onupɛ edzi asɛ bɔgɛ ŋã kabha tsyɛ.

english_verse: All we can do is to follow the truth and not fight against it.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ bɔtɛ balà bhɛtɛ kibui nanà gɛlɔ kititi nɛ kibuiɛ gɛlɛ ɛlɛ ɔzãkɔ shu, pɔ kɛgɛ lɛ ɛlɛ ɔzãkɔ ete.

english_verse: Even though we are weak, we are glad that you are strong, and we pray you will do even better.
translation_verse: Same eku ɔlɔ gɛ onupɛ bubotsoɔ, pɔ wɔnɔ xɔnɔ ɔgagalĩ, kɛlɛ bɔlɔ Owusoso Akaa tikukulu lɛ ɔkɔ wɔnɔ tɛpɛpɛ̃.

english_verse: I am writing these things to you before I arrive. This way I won't have to be hard on you when I use the authority the Lord has given me. I was given this authority, so I could help you and not destroy you.
translation_verse: Kibui ɛlɛ shu so pɛ ɛfɔnɔ ebui alɛ kɔ wɔnɔ nɛ kibeɛ gɛ ɛma wɔnɔ ɔbha, sɛ tɛ onupɛ ɛbaa, ɛtɛba lɔkɔ ɔsha tũ kibui kpɛ kɔ wɔnɔ nɔ, ɔsha gɛ Apaleteɛ akɔ mɛ asɛ tɛ ɛlɔkɔ bhɔ wɔnɔ wunɔ kabha nɔ, gɛnɛ tɛ ɛlɔkɔ ɔlɛ zhini wɔnɔ kpɛ kesi nɔ. Bɔdɔ̃ Sɛsɛnɔ Gɔgɔɛ Nɔ

english_verse: Goodbye, my friends. Do better and pay attention to what I have said. Try to get along and live peacefully with each other. Now I pray that God, who gives love and peace, will be with you.
translation_verse: Otsi alɛ mɛ adzya alɛ, ɛshɛ mɔ̃ o. Nɔoɖi kɛgɛ bakɔ nɔbɔ pɛ ɔtɔɖɔ ɔkɔ, nɔolũ mɛibuiɛ gɛ ibisi denɔ wɔnɔ ɔkɔ, nɔzã ekpeɖekpeɖe tenikpo mɛ, nɔzã ishu yɔyɔ mɛ. Kɛlɛ Owusoso Akaa gɛ alɛ ɔkɔ tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ nɛ̀ ishu yɔyɔ mɛɛ abazã plɛnɔ wɔnɔ.

english_verse: Give each other a warm greeting. All God's people send their greetings.
translation_verse: Nɔsɛnɔ bagɔlɔ nɛ ahɔɛ kpɛkpɛ nɛ bagɔlɔ oli gɛ adalɛ.

english_verse: I pray that God the Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace!
translation_verse: Tibhui bhuibu nɛ̀ ishu yɔyɔ zã plɛnɔ wɔnɔ odenɔ Owusoso Akaa nɛ̀ Apalete Yesu Kristo ɔkɔ,

english_verse: Christ obeyed God our Father and gave himself as a sacrifice for our sins to rescue us from this evil world.
translation_verse: anɔ gɛ alɔkɔ yɛishu akɔ nɛ bɔlɔ ibui kpa nɛtakpu, ɔsɛ tɛ ebu ɔlɔ odenɔ obo kanɔ alɛ ebui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ mɛ, nɛ tɛtɔtɔɖɔ gɛide Owusoso Akaa gɛenu bɔlɔ Akaa ɔkɔ,

english_verse: God will be given glory forever and ever. Amen. The Only True Message
translation_verse: yɛ kɛlɛ ɔsɛ badalɛ yɛishu odenɔ tihɔɔ ɔvɛnɔ tihɔɔ mɛ. Amen. Kibui Gbangbã Nanà Bha Ma

english_verse: I am shocked that you have so quickly turned from God, who chose you with his gift of undeserved grace. You have believed another message,
translation_verse: Kɛbhɛtɛ mɛibi ɔsɛ tsiɔ̃ ɔlɛ koɔ, nɔ vɛlɛ shɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ anunɔ gɛ yɛekulu ɔnɔ amana Kristo yɛ tibhui bhuibuɔ mɛ shu olú, pɛ nɔ otsyina ɔvɛ ebui bhlatɔ gɛ bafɔnɔ eluɔ shu,

english_verse: when there is really only one true message. But some people are causing you trouble and want to make you turn away from the good news about Christ.
translation_verse: gɛ letinu kibui gbangbã kuraa. Kɛba wɔ̃ɛ ɔsɛ, banɔ teli balɛ eetsyina wɔnɔ ekpeɖekpeɖeɛ mɛ, kɛlɛ baapɛ asɛ tɛ bezĩ Kristo yɛibui gbangbã.

english_verse: I pray that God will punish anyone who preaches anything different from our message to you! It doesn't matter if that person is one of us or an angel from heaven.
translation_verse: Pɔ onupɛ bɔlɔ, alo axɔ̃ɛdɛ teli gɛede owusoso nɔ mɛ abaɖɔ kibui gbangbã tili gɛ kɛgã ɔtɔ nɛ kɛgɛ bɔɖɔ tseɖe wɔnɔ shuɔ, kɛlɛ bebu anɔ alɛ ɛpɔ tihɔɔ!

english_verse: I have said it before, and I will say it again. I hope God will punish anyone who preaches anything different from what you have already believed.
translation_verse: Asɛ gɛ bɔtɛ ɖɔ bafala nɔ anaa, ɛkaaɖɔ ɛlɛ otsiɛ tsyɛ ɔsɛ, Onupɛ anɔ teli aɖɔ kibui gbangbã bhlatɔ tili nɛ kɛgɛ nɔtɛ yɛkɛ niedeɛ, kɛlɛ bebu anɔ alɛ ɛpɔ tihɔɔ!

english_verse: I am not trying to please people. I want to please God. Do you think I am trying to please people? If I were doing that, I would not be a servant of Christ. How Paul Became an Apostle
translation_verse: Banɔ mɛpɛ otsiɛ ɔsɛ baɖɔ atɔɖɔ mɛibuiɛ nabha pɛnɔsɛ Owusoso Akaa dza? Pɛnɔsɛ banɔ mɛpɛ ɔsɛ tɛ mɛiku alɛ awɔ̃ dza? Onupɛ ɛka pɛ ɔsɛ tɛ mɛiku banɔ awɔ̃ɔ, kɛlɛ ɛtɛ balà dzi axɔ̃ɛdɛ ɔkɔ Kristo. Owusoso Akaa Yɛekulu Paulo

english_verse: My friends, I want you to know that no one made up the message I preach.
translation_verse: Mɛadzya alɛ mɔ, ɛpɛ ɔsɛ tɛ nɔotì ɔsɛ, kibui gbangbã ɛlɛ gɛ ɛɖɔɔ kitinu banɔdzyrame ebui fafatɛ.

english_verse: It wasn't given or taught to me by some mere human. My message came directly from Jesus Christ when he appeared to me.
translation_verse: Ɛgɛ yɛkɛ ɛlɛ odenɔ ananà ɔkɔ, pɛ ebedzi asɛ bɔkasɛ bakasɛ mɛ ɛlɛ, pɔ ɛyɛkɛ ɛlɛ odenɔ kibui bubu kasɛ gɛide Yesu Kristo ɔkɔ mɛ.

english_verse: You know how I used to live as a Jew. I was cruel to God's church and even tried to destroy it.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ nɔotilũ mɛ ɛkpãa gɛ ɛzã bafala nɔ nɛ Yudanɛ ɛkɔɔ tishushuɔ shu, kɛtɔnɔ gɛ ɛzɛ tɔnɔ ɔha niede gagalĩi pɛ ɛpɛ ɔsɛ tɛ̀ mɛyɛ yɛɛ.

english_verse: I was a much better Jew than anyone else my own age, and I obeyed every law our ancestors had given us.
translation_verse: Ɛzɛmɔ otugbà vɛvɛ nɛ Yudanɛ ɛkɔ mɛ ɔdɛ Yudanɛ gelee gɛ bɔnɛ alɛ kpasɛ agɔ mɛ, kɛlɛ ɛzã mɛakaa alɛ ɛkɔɔ tishushu ebuiɛ nabha gagalĩi.

english_verse: to show me his Son, so I would announce his message to the Gentiles. I didn't talk this over with anyone.
translation_verse: ɔsɛ tɛ ebu Yɛeyi Anyɛɛ akasɛ ɔgana mɛabha, tɛ mɛdɛ ɖɔ yɛibuiɛ nɛ bagɛ etinu Yudanɛ nɛishi, ɛgɛ yɛkɛ kibuititì odenɔ ananà ɔkɔ,

english_verse: I didn't say a word, not even to the men in Jerusalem who were apostles before I was. Instead, I went at once to Arabia, and afterwards I returned to Damascus.
translation_verse: kɛlɛ igi wu ɔvɛ Yerusalem tsyɛ ɔsɛ tɛ mɛdɛ mɔ bagɛ eputɔ edzi banɔkpɛkpɛ ɔkɔ mɛ, pɔ tsiɔ̃ ɔlɛ ɛshɛ vɛ Arabia kɛlɛ kidegbaa izitsyina ba Damasko.

english_verse: Three years later I went to visit Peter in Jerusalem and stayed with him for
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ nɛ agɔ tata edeɛ, ɛbawù ɔvɛ Yerusalem ɔsɛ tɛ mɛ nɛ Petro butì bagɔlɔ, kɛlɛ ɛzã yɛɔbha ewui lɔfɔ nɛ̀ teti.

english_verse: The only other apostle I saw was James, the Lord's brother.
translation_verse: Ɛgɛ mɔ banɔkpɛkpɛ alɛ mɛ anɔ nanà—ɔdɛ Yakobo ete, gɛenu Apaleteɛ yɛadzya.

english_verse: And in the presence of God I swear I am telling the truth.
translation_verse: Ɛtɔɖɔ kɛklagbu nɛ̀ ɛlɛ abha kɔ ɔnɔ nɛ Owusoso Akaa namɛ ɔsɛ, kibuiɛ gɛ ɛfɔnɔ kɔ ɔnɔ, tinu kɛsɛnɛ ibui.

english_verse: Later, I went to the regions of Syria and Cilicia.
translation_verse: Kidegbaa, ɛfala ɛdɛ Siria nɛ̀ Kilikia tsyɛ.

english_verse: But no one who belonged to Christ's churches in Judea had ever seen me in person.
translation_verse: Ɔhaa gɛ ɔzã Kristo mɛ nɛ Yudea nɔ egitì mɛ.

english_verse: They had only heard that the one who had been cruel to them was now preaching the message that he had once tried to destroy.
translation_verse: Bulũ bhui bhui ɛlɛ ete belũ nɛ mɛishu asɛ, “Anyɛ gɛ aza atɔnɔ bɔlɔ ede abafala nɔɔ, otsi alɛ xɔnɔ yɛtsyɛ aɖɔ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã ibuiɛ gɛ apɛ asɛ ayɛɛ afalanɔ flogo.”

english_verse: And because of me, they praised God.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ bakpɛ Owusoso Akaa nɛinye nɛitití nɛ mɛtakpu.

english_verse: Fourteen years later I went to Jerusalem with Barnabas. I also took along Titus.
translation_verse: Nɛ agɔ lɔfɔ nɛ̀ talɛ edeɛ, iziwu ɔvɛ Yerusalem, otsi alɛ xɔnɔ mɛ nɛ Barnaba. Ɛgbana Tito tsyɛ plɛnɔ mɛishu ɔvɛnɔ.

english_verse: But I went there because God had told me to go, and I explained the good news I had been preaching to the Gentiles. Then I met privately with the ones who seemed to be the most important leaders. I wanted to make sure my work in the past and my future work would not be for nothing.
translation_verse: Ɛdɛ ɛkɛlɛ nɛ kibui bubu kasɛ tili shu, kɛlɛ ɛɖɔ kibui gbangbã gɛitseɖe bagɛ etinu Yudanɛ tsyɛ tseɖe alɛ. Pɔ ɛbhɛtɛ ɛlɛ̀ ete ɔkɔ bagɛ alɛ otugbà ɔkɔ banunɔ, nɛ kifu sisí mɛ ɔsɛ ɛzɛdɛ kɛxɔ̃ɛ alo ɛkaadɛ ɛxɔ̃ɛ kpɛ ɛlɛɛ mɛ.

english_verse: Titus went to Jerusalem with me. He was a Greek, but still he wasn't forced to be circumcised.
translation_verse: Pɔ Tito gɛedzi Helanɔ pɛ azã mɛɔbha tsyɛ elo, ananà agɛ tɔɖɔ ɛlɛ asɛ bebhui kɛhɔ̃a akɔɛ.

english_verse: We went there because of those who pretended to be followers and had sneaked in among us as spies. They had come to take away the freedom Christ Jesus had given us, and they were trying to make us their slaves.
translation_verse: Kibui ɛlɛ bayì ɔmɔsɛ asɛnɛ badzya teli aba afala eku bɔlɔ ɛzãkɔɔ tɛ belu awɔ̃ eɖi ɔlɔ ishuahɔɛmɛ zazã gɛ lɛ ɔlɔ ahɔɛ nɛ Kristo mɛ, tɛ̀ betsyina ɔlɔ budzì banɔ dzidzi.

english_verse: But we wanted you to have the true message. This is why we didn't give in to them, not even for a second.
translation_verse: Bɔgɛ shɛnɛ ahɔɛ bɔlɔ shu kɔ alɛ nɛ kibe nanà mɛ, ɔsɛ tɛ kibui gbangbã gɛlɛ kibui gbã shuɔ zã ɔnɔ ɔkɔ.

english_verse: Some of them were supposed to be important leaders, but I didn't care who they were. God doesn't have any favorites! None of these so-called special leaders added anything to my message.
translation_verse: Bagɛ awɔ̃ɛ anɔ asɛ ɛzãkɔ lɛ balɛ ahɔɛ xɔnɔɔ, kɛka nyia ka ɛdi gɛ benu tsyɛ elo, kitinu kibui gɛ gã ɔtɔ ɔkɔ mɛ, Owusoso Akaa etiekulù kebhu nɛ kana gɛ bɔmɔ anɔ shu, banɔ alɛ agɛ lɔkɔ kɛnanà ɔplɛnɔ mɛ ibuiɛ.

english_verse: They realized God had sent me with the good news for Gentiles, and he had sent Peter with the same message for Jews.
translation_verse: Nɛ kidzo bhlatɔ abhaa tsyɛ, bamɔ asɛ balɔkɔ ɛlɛ akpɛ ahɔɛ akɔ mɛ ɔsɛ tɛ mɛdɛ ɖɔ kibui gbangbã tseɖe bagɛ etinu Yudanɛ, asɛ kana gɛ Petro tsyɛ adɛ Yudanɛ ɔkɔ ana.

english_verse: God, who had sent Peter on a mission to the Jews, was now using me to preach to the Gentiles.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ Owusoso Akaa gɛ awɔa axɔ̃ɛ ɔplɛnɔ Petro asɛ anɔkpɛkpɛ ɔkɔ Yudanɛ anaa, yɛawɔa axɔ̃ɛ nɛ mɛ tsyɛ asɛ anɔkpɛkpɛ ɔkɔ bagɛ etinu Yudanɛ.

english_verse: James, Peter, and John realized that God had given me the message about his gift of undeserved grace. And these men are supposed to be the backbone of the church. They even gave Barnabas and me a friendly handshake. This was to show that we would work with Gentiles and that they would work with Jews.
translation_verse: Yakobo, Petro nɛ̀ Yohanes gɛenu kibuiɛ kɛlɛ bubhi kashàa, bakɔ mɛ nɛ̀ Barnaba ahɔɛ ɔkɔ kɛdzyà bhɛbhɛtɛ nɛ kibeɛ gɛ bamɔ tibhui bhuibuɔ gɛ bakɔ mɛ. Badɛmɛ atɔɖɔ ɛlɛ abha asɛ tɛ̀ bɔvɛ bagɛ etinu Yudanɛ ɔkɔ, tɛ balɛ tsyɛ avɛ Yudanɛ ɔkɔ.

english_verse: They only asked us to remember the poor, and this was something I had always been eager to do. Paul Corrects Peter at Antioch
translation_verse: Kɛgɛ bebisi odenɔ bɔlɔkɔ kɛlɛinu ɔsɛ tɛ bɔzã ɛlɛ abha tɛ bɔzɛ bakà betsyuitsyuiɛ tsyɛ, kibuiɛ gɛ ɛlɛ mɛpɛ ɔsɛ tɛ ɛbhɛtɛɛ. Paulo Agɛ Dɛmɛ Atɔɖɔ Petro Ibui Bhɛbhɛtɛ Abha

english_verse: When Peter came to Antioch, I told him face to face that he was wrong.
translation_verse: Kibeɛ gɛ Petro aba Antioxia nɔ, mɛ Paulo ɛgɛ dɛmɛ tɔɖɔ yɛebui bhɛbhɛtɛ abha nɛ yamɛ, ɔmɔsɛ ata esi.

english_verse: He used to eat with Gentile followers of the Lord, until James sent some Jewish followers. Peter was afraid of the Jews and soon stopped eating with Gentiles.
translation_verse: Flogo tɛ banyɛ teli ebede Yakobo ɔkɔ abaa, azaŋã bɔdɔ̃ nɛ̀ bagɛ etinu Yudanɛ. Pɔ gɛ bebekuɔ, akpà kide yi pɛ ebu yɛishu ɔtɔ nɛ bagɛ etinu Yudanɛ shu, ɔmɔsɛ kifu ziyi yɛ nɛ̀ bagɛ azã kɛhɔ̃a bhui bhui kɛzaa mɛ.

english_verse: He and the others hid their true feelings so well that even Barnabas was fooled.
translation_verse: Yudanɛ bhlatɔ tsyɛ azã yiede nɛ kibui ɛlɛ shu, kɛlɛ nɛ kibui ɛlɛ shuɔ bagbana Barnaba tsyɛ atsrakɛ idzo.

english_verse: But when I saw they were not really obeying the truth that is in the good news, I corrected Peter in front of everyone and said: Peter, you are a Jew, but you live like a Gentile. So how can you force Gentiles to live like Jews?
translation_verse: Kibeɛ gɛ ɛmɔsɛ batɛ agã nɛ kibui gbangbã gɛlɛ ɛlɛ ɔzãkɔ nabhaa, itseɖe Petro nɛ balɛ nyɔ̃ɔ amɛ ɔsɛ, “Yudaeyi wɔonu, pɔ odzi nɛ̀ ɛkpã asɛ anɔ gɛetinu Yudaeyi, wɔɛkpã ma asɛ Yudaeyi ana. Kiɖe so pɛ otũ ɛlɛ ɔkpɛ bagɛ etinu Yudanɛ ulũ xɔnɔ ɔsɛ beze shu Yudanɛ ɛkɔ dza?

english_verse: We are Jews by birth and are not sinners like Gentiles.
translation_verse: “Bɔlɔ gɛinù Yudaeyi mɔmɔ, gɛ butidzi asɛ ‘Bagɛ etinu Yudanɛ gɛ balɔkɔ alɛ asɛ ebui kpa babhɛtɛ eleteɛ’

english_verse: But we know that God accepts only those who have faith in Jesus Christ. No one can please God by simply obeying the Law. So we put our faith in Christ Jesus, and God accepted us because of our faith.
translation_verse: bunyi ɔsɛ beti bhui bɔtsyɔa akɔ anɔ nɛ yɛ ɔsha abha tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ shu, pɔ ɔganà tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã nɛ Yesu Kristo abha mɛ. Klisoɔ, bɔlɔ tsyɛ lɔkɔ bɔlɔ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã kpɛ Kristo Yesu mɛ ɔsɛ tɛ bebhui bɔtsyɔa akɔ ɔlɔ nɛ Kristo abha tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛ, gɛnɛ nɛ ɔsha nabha tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ shu so, ɔmɔsɛ ganà ɔsha nabha tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ mɛɛ, betibe bhui bɔtsyɔa akɔ anɔ nanà.

english_verse: When we Jews started looking for a way to please God, we discovered that we are sinners too. Does this mean that Christ is the one who makes us sinners? No, it doesn't!
translation_verse: “Onupɛ nɛ kibeɛ gɛ bɔpɛ ɔsɛ tɛ bebhui bɔtsyɔa akɔ ɔlɔ nɛ Kristoɔ mɛɛ, kɛlɛ mɛ dalɛ ɔsɛ bɔlɔ tsyɛ ebui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ elete bunu. Pɔ kɛlɛ esi nu ɔsɛ Kristo atɔɖɔ ahɔɛ nɛ ebui kpa tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ abha dza? Gɛnɛ ana!

english_verse: But if I tear down something and then build it again, I prove that I was wrong at first.
translation_verse: Onupɛ idzini kɛgɛ izĩɛ woeseɛ, kɛlɛ ɛkasɛ ishu ɔsɛ anɔ gɛ atɛ bhɛtɛ tɔɖɔ ɔsha abha mɛinu.

english_verse: It was the Law itself that killed me and freed me from its power, so I could live for God. I have been nailed to the cross with Christ.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ gana ɔsha nabha, itsí nɛ ɔsha shu, ɔsɛ tɛ mɛ zanà ɛkpã ɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa.

english_verse: I have died, but Christ lives in me. And I now live by faith in the Son of God, who loved me and gave his life for me.
translation_verse: Bakpalà mɛ ɔplɛnɔ Kristo nɛ butsi osiɛ shu kɛlɛ gɛnɛ mɛ kalɛ nɛkpã, pɔ Kristoɔ yɛakpasɛ mɛ mɛ̀. Ɛkpã gɛ ɛlɛ mɛidzinɔ nɛ ishudzya mɛɛ, idzi nɛ ɛlɛ nɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã nɛ Owusoso Akaa Yɛeyianyɛ mɛ, anɔ gɛ adɛmɛ mɛibui pɛ alɔkɔ yɛishu akɔ nɛ mɛtakpu.

english_verse: I don't turn my back on God's gift of undeserved grace. If we can be acceptable to God by obeying the Law, it was useless for Christ to die.
translation_verse: Ɛgɛ lɔkɔ mɛ Owusoso Akaa tibhui bhuibuɔ lu ɛwɔ̃a, ɔmɔsɛ onupɛ babàmɔ kibui gbangbã tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ ɔgana ɔshaa mɛɛ, kɛlɛ Kristoɔ etsí bɔwɔ̃a kua!”

english_verse: You stupid Galatians! I told you exactly how Jesus Christ was nailed to a cross. Has someone now put an evil spell on you?
translation_verse: Wɔnɔ Galatianɛ awɔaga! Afana gɛ atã ɔnɔ nɛ̀ ohui ana dza? Nɛ wɔnɔ amɛɛ, nɔmɔ piapii ɔsɛ bakpalà Yesu Kristo nɛ butsi osiɛ shu.

english_verse: I want to know only one thing. How were you given God's Spirit? Was it by obeying the Law of Moses or by hearing about Christ and having faith in him?
translation_verse: Ɛba pɛ ɔsɛ tɛ mɛ ɛkpasɛ kibui tinikpo odenɔ wɔnɔ ɔkɔ, ɔsha nabha tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ mɛ nɔyɛkɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ, pɛnɔsɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã nɛ kɛgɛ nɔolũ nabha soɔ dza?

english_verse: How can you be so stupid? Do you think that by yourself you can complete what God's Spirit started in you?
translation_verse: Bawɔaga nɔonu ana dza? Gɛ nɔkpɛ esi nɛ̀ Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ bekeɛ pɔ, otsi nɔpɛ ɔsɛ tɛ nɔmɔ kɛgɛ nɔpɛ nɔ ɔgana anɔdzyrame ebui bhɛbhɛtɛ mɛ dza?

english_verse: Have you gone through all of this for nothing? Is it all really for nothing?
translation_verse: Bɔwɔã tɔ nɔtɔ̀ ɔkɔ kɛdɔ̃ nanà mamɔ mamɔ dza, gɛ onupɛ kɛgbã kinu ɔsɛ bɔtɔ ɔlɛ ma kɔ kɛdɔ̃ nanà dza?

english_verse: God gives you his Spirit and works miracles in you. But does he do this because you obey the Law of Moses or because you have heard about Christ and have faith in him?
translation_verse: Pɔ bɔlɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa alɔkɔ yɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ akɔ ɔnɔ pɛ abhɛtɛ yɛ atsyindzye ebui nɛ ɔnɔ ɛshi nɛ gɛ nɔbhɛtɛ ɔtɔɖɔ ɔshaa nabha so, pɛnɔsɛ gɛ nɔyɛkɛ kɛgɛ nolũ nabha ɔŋã soɔ dza?

english_verse: The Scriptures say that God accepted Abraham because Abraham had faith.
translation_verse: Nɔolu awɔ̃ oɖi Abraham, “Ayɛkɛ Owusoso Akaa nabha aŋã, kɛlɛ balɔkɔ ɛlɛ nɛishu asɛ kibui gbangbã bhɛbhɛtɛ kinu.”

english_verse: And so, you should understand that everyone who has faith is a child of Abraham.
translation_verse: Nɔolũ ɛlɛ esi ɔsɛ, bagɛ ayɛkɛ aŋã enu Abraham yɛeyi alɛ.

english_verse: Long ago the Scriptures said God would accept the Gentiles because of their faith. This is why God told Abraham the good news that all nations would be blessed because of him.
translation_verse: Kibuiɛ gɛ bafɔnɔ eluɔ tɛmɔ putɔ nɔ ɔsɛ Owusoso Akaa ebe bhui bɔtsyɔa akɔ bagɛ etinu Yudanɛ odenɔ balɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛ, kɛlɛ eputɔ akɔ Abraham etì kibui gbangbã ɛlɛ elu ɔsɛ, “Gana wɔabha, bebe yulà akɔ amadiɛ petee.”

english_verse: This means everyone who has faith will share in the blessings given to Abraham because of his faith.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ beyulà akɔ bagɛ ayɛkɛ aŋã aplɛnɔ Abrahamuɔ, anyɛ gɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã lɛ yɛahɔɛ.

english_verse: Anyone who tries to please God by obeying the Law is under a curse. The Scriptures say, “Everyone who doesn't obey everything in the Law is under a curse.”
translation_verse: Bagɛ elu awɔ̃ nɛ ɔsha nabha tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ xɔnɔ, baɖɔ kibui akpɛ alɛ, ɔmɔsɛ bafɔnɔ elu asɛ, “Baɖɔ kibui akpɛ anɔ ka nyia ka gɛ agɛ zã ɛlɛ abha abhɛtɛ atɔɖɔ kɛka nyia ka gɛ bafɔnɔ akpɛ ɔsha keplukpa mɛ.”

english_verse: No one can please God by obeying the Law. The Scriptures also say, “The people God accepts because of their faith will live.”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ mɛ dalɛ ɔsɛ bagɛ lɔkɔ ɔsha ebhui bɔtsyɔa akɔ ananà nɔ nɛ Owusoso Akaa namɛ, ɔmɔsɛ, “Bagɛ enu ebui gbangbã bhɛbhɛtɛ eleteɛ abazã nɛ̀ ɛkpã ɔgana tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛ.”

english_verse: The Law isn't based on faith. It promises life only to people who obey its commands.
translation_verse: Ɔshaa ɔgɛ kpà plɛnɔ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋãa, nɛidzo bhlatɔ tsyɛ abhaa, “Anunɔ gɛ abhɛtɛ ebui alɛ nɛidiɛ abazã nɛ̀ ɛkpã nɛ ɛlɛ shu.”

english_verse: But Christ rescued us from the Law's curse, when he became a curse in our place. This is because the Scriptures say that anyone who is nailed to a tree is under a curse.
translation_verse: Kristo ebu ɔlɔ odenɔ kibuiɛ gɛ baɖɔ akpɛ ɔlɔ ɔsha niesi, kɛlɛ yɛebedzi kibuiɛ gɛ baɖɔ akpɛ ɔlɔ, ɔmɔsɛ bafɔnɔ elu asɛ, “Baɖɔ kibui akpɛ anɔ ka nyia ka gɛ akpɛ ohui oli nɛ busi mɛ.”

english_verse: And because of what Jesus Christ has done, the blessing promised to Abraham was taken to the Gentiles. This happened so that by faith we would be given the promised Holy Spirit. The Law and the Promise
translation_verse: Ebu ɔlɔ ɔsɛ tɛ tiyuyula gɛ balɔkɔ akɔ Abrahamuɔ ba bagɛ etinu Yudanɛ tsyɛ ɔkɔ ɔgana Kristo Yesu abha, ɔsɛ tɛ ɔgana tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛɛ, bɔba yɛkɛ kɛgɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ tɔɖɔ lu kɔ ɔlɔ. Ɔshaa Nɛ̀ Kɛgɛ Batɔɖɔ Eluɔ

english_verse: My friends, I will use an everyday example to explain what I mean. Once someone agrees to something, no one else can change or cancel the agreement.
translation_verse: Mɛadzya alɛ mɔ, nɔkɔ mɛɖɔ kibui ɛlɛ lɔkɔ shɔnɔ kiwui nyia wui ɛkpã zazãna shu. Asɛ kana gɛ ananà atɛ balà ebu, alo pɛ abalà lɔkɔ kɛdɔ̃ nanà aplɛnɔ anɔdzyrame olugbũ nyanyã nɔnɔ gɛ batɔɖɔ eluɔ akɔɛ nɔ, kana ibe kɛlɛ nɛ kibui ɛlɛ tsyɛ mɛ.

english_verse: That is how it is with the promises God made to Abraham and his descendant. The promises were not made to many descendants, but only to one, and that one is Christ.
translation_verse: Baɖɔ ebuiɛ gɛ batɔɖɔ eluɔ etseɖe Abraham nɛ̀ yɛeshui alɛ. Kibuiɛ gɛ bafɔnɔ eluɔ gɛɖɔ ɔsɛ “ɔkɔ wɔ beshui alɛ” gɛ kɛlɛ esi kɛlɛinu ɔsɛ banɔ kpekpèɛ, pɔ kɛɖɔsɛ, “nɛ̀ wɔ eshui nɔ,” gɛ kɛlɛ esi ɔsɛ enu anɔ tenikpo, gɛenu Kristoɔ.

english_verse: What I am saying is that the Law cannot change or cancel God's promise made
translation_verse: Kibuiɛ gɛ ɛɖɔɔ niesi nɛ ɛlɛ ɔsɛ, Ɔshaa, gɛ bakpɛ elu asɛ agɔ akpã talɛ nɛ̀ afɛata nɛ alɛ (

english_verse: If we have to obey the Law in order to receive God's blessings, those blessings don't really come to us because of God's promise. But God was kind to Abraham and made him a promise.
translation_verse: Onupɛ ganà ɔshaa mɛ babà yɛkɛ kaŋãsiɛ alɛ, kɛlɛ kɛtɛ kanu ɔsɛ kɛgã tɛtɔtɔɖɔ lu mɛ, pɔ nɛ Owusoso Akaa tibhui bhuibuɔ ulũɔ, alɔkɔ ɔsha akɔ Abraham ganà tɛtɔtɔɖɔ luɔ mɛ.

english_verse: What is the use of the Law? It was given later to show that we sin. But it was only supposed to last until the coming of that descendant who was given the promise. In fact, angels gave the Law to Moses, and he gave it to the people.
translation_verse: Kiɖuwoe shu so pɛ ɔshaa ɔlɛ otsiɛ dza? Bakɔ ɔsha ɔlɛ aplɛnɔ nɛ kibui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ shu so, odiyinɔ sɛ Eshui nɔ gɛ batɔɖɔ ebui alɛ elu akɔ abekoeyì. Agɛ yɛevũ iboe alɔkɔ ɔsha kpɛ axɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa mɛ ɔgana baxɔ̃ɛdɛ nabha.

english_verse: There is only one God, and the Law did not come directly from him. Slaves and Children
translation_verse: Anɔ gɛevũ iboe, atɛzã akɔ kawɔ̃pɛ tienikpo, pɔ Owusoso Akaa alɛ tenikpo.

english_verse: Does the Law disagree with God's promises? No, it doesn't! If any law could give life to us, we could become acceptable to God by obeying that law.
translation_verse: Ɔshaa pɔ, bafɔnɔ ɔlɛ alɔkɔ alɔɛtití nɛ Owusoso Akaa yɛibuiɛ gɛ atɔɖɔ eluɔ shu dza? Gɛnɛ kana kɛlɛ! Ɔmɔsɛ onupɛ bakpɛ ɔsha toli olu gɛ lobo tsyina banɔ ɛkpãa, kɛlɛ kibui gbangbã bhɛbhɛtɛ tade koeyi nɛ ɔshaa mɛ xɔ̃ɔ.

english_verse: But the Scriptures say that sin controls everyone, so that God's promises will be for anyone who has faith in Jesus Christ.
translation_verse: Kibuiɛ gɛ bafɔnɔ eluɔ mɛ dalɛ ɔsɛ, kanɔ nɔ̀ petee alɛ kibui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ ohumi esi, ɔsɛ tɛ tɛtɔtɔɖɔ luɔ gɛ kpa plɛnɔ Yesu Kristo abha tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛ, dzì bagɛ ayɛkɛ yɛabha ŋã nɛdɔ̃.

english_verse: The Law controlled us and kept us under its power until the time came when we would have faith.
translation_verse: Flogo pɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã ɛlɛ babaa, ɔshaa nyanà ɔlɔ ovunɔ asɛ ɔɖavũmɛ azazã ana, lɔnya bɔlɔ olú ɔvɛnɔ kibeɛ gɛ bebu tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã akasɛ.

english_verse: In fact, the Law was to be our teacher until Christ came. Then we could have faith and be acceptable to God.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ bakpɛ ɔshaa elu asɛ tɛ lɔgbana ɔlɔ ɔvɛnɔ Kristo ɔkɔ, ɔsɛ tɛ balɔkɔ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã ebhui bɔtsyɔa akɔ ɔlɔ nɔ.

english_verse: But once a person has learned to have faith, there is no more need to have the Law as a teacher.
translation_verse: Otsiɛ alɛ gɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã bakoeyiɛ, bɔbha ma ɔshaa ɔlɛ anɔ abha ɖiɖi esi. Bagɛ Enu Owusoso Akaa Yɛeyi Alɛ

english_verse: All of you are God's children because of your faith in Christ Jesus.
translation_verse: Beyì wɔnɔ petee onu ɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa ɔgana tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã gɛ ɛlɛ Kristo Yesu mɛ,

english_verse: And when you were baptized, it was as though you had put on Christ in the same way you put on new clothes.
translation_verse: ɔmɔsɛ wɔnɔ agɛ bakpɛ ulĩ akɔ petee nɛ Kristoɔ mɛ, nɔkpɛ akpɛkpɛ akɔ ishu ɔplɛnɔ Kristo.

english_verse: Faith in Christ Jesus is what makes each of you equal with each other, whether you are a Jew or a Greek, a slave or a free person, a man or a woman.
translation_verse: Yudaeyi alo Helaeyi nanà abha ma, anɔ dzidzi alo anɔ gɛ alɛ yɛishu ahɔɛ mɛ, anyɛ alo adzɛ abha ma, ɔmɔsɛ wɔnɔ petee odzi tenikpo nɛ Kristo Yesu mɛ.

english_verse: So if you belong to Christ, you are now part of Abraham's family, and you will be given what God has promised.
translation_verse: Onupɛ onu Kristo ayɛ, kɛlɛ onu Abraham yɛeshui, nɛ̀ kaŋãsi elete nɛ kɛgɛ batɔɖɔ elu nulũ.

english_verse: Children who are under age are no better off than slaves, even though everything their parents own will someday be theirs.
translation_verse: Kibuiɛ gɛ ɛɖɔ kɛlɛinu ɔsɛ, gɛ agɛ abaŋã kaŋãsiɛ ekenu anuvɔɔ soɔ, agɛ gã ɔtɔ nɛ anɔ dzidzi shu, gɛ bevũɔ petee pɔ enu yiayɛ flogo.

english_verse: This is because children are placed in the care of guardians and teachers until the time their parents have set.
translation_verse: Akpasɛ bagɛ anyɔ̃nìi nɛ̀ bagɛ yɛɔdɔ̃ kpasɛ alɛ ahɔɛ mɛ niesi, odiyinɔ kibeɛ gɛ yɛakaa atɔɖɔ eluɔ kɔɛ.

english_verse: This is how it was with us. We were like children ruled by the powers of this world.
translation_verse: Kidzo ɛlɛ nabha, nɛ kibeɛ gɛ budzi bunuvɔɛ nɔ, budzi banɔ dzidzi gɛ bɔzã ɛfɔfuĩa nɔ ebui tɔtɔɖɔ gɛazã kanɔ nɔ̀ mɛ niesi.

english_verse: But when the time was right, God sent his Son, and a woman gave birth to him. His Son obeyed the Law,
translation_verse: Pɔ gɛ kibeɛ petee bana nɔ, Owusoso Akaa akpɛ yɛeyianyɛ, gɛ anɔdzɛ yɛamɔ yɛ, gɛ bamɔ yɛ nɛ ɔsha niesi,

english_verse: so he could set us free from the Law, and we could become God's children.
translation_verse: asɛ tɛ ebu bagɛ akpasɛ ɔshaa niesi, tɛ bɔyɛkɛ kɛgɛ lɛ ɔkɔ beyì anyɛ.

english_verse: Now that we are his children, God has sent the Spirit of his Son into our hearts. And his Spirit tells us that God is our Father.
translation_verse: Gɛ beyì anyɛ nonu soɔ, Owusoso Akaa asɛsɛ Yɛeyianyɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ nɛ bɔlɔ etúɔ mɛ, Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ gɛ okulu bɔdɔ̃ ɔsɛ, “Mɛakaa.”

english_verse: You are no longer slaves. You are God's children, and you will be given what he has promised. Paul's Concern for the Galatians
translation_verse: Klisoɔ, oti konu anɔ dzidzi, pɔ eyì anyɛ wɔonu, kɛlɛ gɛ eyì anyɛ wɔonu soɔ, Owusoso Akaa abhɛtɛ wɔ tsyɛ ɔsɛ tɛ odzì kaŋãsi elete. Kɛgɛ Paulo Alɔkɔ Nɛ Ɛlɛ Mɛ Akɔ Galatianɛ

english_verse: Before you knew God, you were slaves of gods that are not real.
translation_verse: Kibeɛ gɛ bafala, pɛ nɔogitì Owusoso Akaa, nɔodzi banɔ dzidzi ɔkɔ bɔdɔ̃ tuli gɛinu ibudì.

english_verse: But now you know God, or better still, God knows you. How can you turn back and become the slaves of those weak and pitiful powers?
translation_verse: Pɔ otsiɛ xɔnɔ gɛ nɔonyi Owusoso Akaa, alo gɛ Owusoso Akaa tsyɛ enyi ɔnɔ soɔ, kiɖe so pɛ nozo otsyina ɔvɛ ebuiɛ gɛ ama kɛnanà mɛ pɛ leku ashagabuiɛ shuɔ dza? Nɔkɔ pɛ ɔsɛ tɛ nowlui odzi banɔ dzidzi ɔkɔ alɛ dza?

english_verse: You even celebrate certain days, months, seasons, and years.
translation_verse: Nɔ olu awɔ̃ oɖi ewuiɛ nɛ̀ bewoele nɔ, kibeɛ nɛ̀ agɔɔ gɛetsyina xɔɔ!

english_verse: I am afraid I have wasted my time working with you.
translation_verse: Isi ifu kɔ ɔnɔ, ɔsɛ ɔdɛ tsyɛ izĩ mɛibe nɛ wɔnɔ ɛtakpu.

english_verse: My friends, I beg you to be like me, just as I once tried to be like you. Did you mistreat me
translation_verse: Mɛadzya alɛ mɔ, ɛkɔ ɔnɔ ɛtakpu ɔsɛ, nɔzã asɛ mɛ ana, ɔmɔsɛ ɛtɛzã asɛ wɔnɔ oɖi. Nɔgɛ bhɛtɛ kibui kpa nanà mana mɛ ɔkɔ.

english_verse: when I first preached to you? No you didn't, even though you knew I had come there because I was sick.
translation_verse: Asɛ gɛ nɔonyi ana, bɔnyã bɔlɛkɔ so pɛ iputɔ ɖɔ kibui gbangbã ɛlɛ tseɖe ɔnɔ.

english_verse: My illness must have caused you some trouble, but you didn't hate me or turn me away because of it. You welcomed me as though I were one of God's angels or even Christ Jesus himself.
translation_verse: Gɛ mɛ bɔnyã dɔdɔ dzì tifefekeɖi ɔkɔ wɔnɔ tsyɛ elo, ɛgɛ iku wɔnɔ ɛnyɛnyɛ̃. Pɔ, nɔyɛkɛ mɛ asɛ onupɛ Owusoso Akaa axɔ̃ɛdɛ mɛinu dza, asɛ gɛ onupɛ mɛinu Kristo Yesuɔ dza.

english_verse: Where is that good feeling now? I am sure if it had been possible, you would have taken out your own eyes and given them to me.
translation_verse: Kiɖuwoe gɛ ba nɛ wɔnɔ same mɔmɔ petee abha dza? Ɛba là dalɛ ɛlɛ mɛ ɔsɛ, pɛ otɛ ɔsɛ nɔbhɛtɛ anaa tsyɛ alo, nɔtɔ obù ɔnɔ awuhũɔ ɔlɔkɔ kɔ mɛ.

english_verse: Am I now your enemy, just because I told you the truth?
translation_verse: Otsi alɛ idzì anɔ gɛ atɛa dɛmɛ wɔnɔ ibui so pɛ itseɖe ɔnɔ kibuiɛ gɛlɛ ɛlɛ ɔzãkɔ dza?

english_verse: Those people may be paying you a lot of attention, but it isn't for your good. They only want to keep you away from me, so you will pay them a lot of attention.
translation_verse: Banɔ alɛ apɛɛ gagalĩi asɛ tɛ baŋã wɔnɔ abha, pɔ gɛnɛ kɔ kibui gbangbã nanà so. Kɛgɛ bapɛ kilɛinu ɔsɛ tɛ bebu wɔnɔ ɔtɔ nɛ ɔlɔ shu, ɔsɛ tɛ nɔzã gagalĩi ɔplɛnɔ alɛ.

english_verse: It is always good to give your attention to something worthwhile, even when I am not with you.
translation_verse: Kɛpɛ̃ ɔsɛ tɛ nɔzã gagalĩi, gɛ onupɛ kɛgɛ ɛlɛ nɔtɔɖɔ pɛ̃, kɛlɛ nɔzã ɛlɛ abha ana kiwui nyia wui, gɛnɛ gɛ onupɛ ɛlɛ ɔnɔ bha ete.

english_verse: My children, I am in terrible pain until Christ may be seen living in you.
translation_verse: Mɛeyi alɛ gɛ ɛdɛmɛ nɔɔ, gɛ wɔnɔ shu so pɛ ɛya ɛlɛ katã mɛ odiyinɔ kibeɛ gɛ Kristo afala eku wɔnɔ mɛ flogo,

english_verse: I wish I were with you now. Then I would not have to talk this way. You really have me puzzled. Hagar and Sarah
translation_verse: tsyɛ alo ɛpɛ sɛ tɛ mɛzã ɔnɔ ɔbha otsiɛ tɛ mɛitsyina mɛebui ɖɔɖɔ flogo, ɔmɔsɛ mɛ ekpeɖekpeɖe mɛ aka dɔ nɛ wɔnɔ shu! Hagar Nɛ̀ Sara

english_verse: Some of you would like to be under the rule of the Law of Moses. But do you know what the Law says?
translation_verse: Nɔotseɖe mɛ, wɔnɔ bagɛ ɔpɛ ɔsɛ tɛ nɔzã ɔsha niesi, pɔ nɔotinyi kɛgɛ ɔshaa ɔɖɔɔ dza?

english_verse: In the Scriptures we learn that Abraham had two sons. The mother of one of them was a slave, while the mother of the other one had always been free.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ bafɔnɔ elu asɛ, beyianyɛ taabha azã Abraham ahɔɛ, adzɛ dzidziɛ amɔ̃ tenikpo, kɛlɛ adzɛ gɛ alɛ yɛishu ahɔɛ mɛ tsyɛ amɔ̃ tenikpo.

english_verse: The son of the slave woman was born in the usual way. But the son of the free woman was born because of God's promise.
translation_verse: Bamɔ̃ yɛeyianyɛ gɛ ede ekoeyi nɛ adzɛ dzidziɛ mɛ, asɛ gɛ bamɔ anɔ ana, pɔ yɛeyianyɛ gɛ ede ekoeyi nɛ adzɛ gɛ azã yɛishu ahɔɛ mɛ xɔnɔɔ, bamɔɛ nɛ kibui gɛ batɔɖɔ eluɔ shu.

english_verse: All of this has another meaning as well. Each of the two women stands for one of the agreements God made with his people. Hagar, the slave woman, stands for the agreement that was made at Mount Sinai. Everyone born into her family is a slave.
translation_verse: Babà lɔkɔ ebui alɛ asɛ bubuti ebui ɖɔɖɔ, ɔmɔsɛ badzɛ taabha azã olugbũnyanyã tɛbha ulũ. Olugbũ nyanyã tonikpo ode Sinai Ketukpɔ nabha kɛlɛ lɔ mɔ beyiɛ gɛ kɛlɛ sɛ bedzi banɔ dzidzi. Hagar nɛ alɛ.

english_verse: Hagar also stands for Mount Sinai in Arabia and for the present city of Jerusalem. She and her children are slaves.
translation_verse: Otsiɛ nɔ, Hagar ashɔnɔ Sinai Ketukpɔ nɛ Arabia gɛ abhɛtɛ tenikpo nɛ̀ obo ɔma gbagbla Yerusalem, ɔmɔsɛ yɛ nɛ̀ yɛeyi alɛ edzì banɔ dzidzi.

english_verse: But our mother is the city of Jerusalem in heaven above, and she isn't a slave.
translation_verse: Pɔ Yerusalem ɔma gɛɔlɛ kititíɛ xɔnɔ ɔlɛ ishu ahɔɛ mɛ, kɛlɛ yɛenu bɔlɔ elĩɛ.

english_verse: The Scriptures say about her, “You have never had children, but now you can be glad. You have never given birth, but now you can shout. Once you had no children, but now you will have more children than a woman who has been married for a long time.”
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ bafɔnɔ elu asɛ, “Mɔ same, O adzɛ gɛ ɔtɛ ɔmɔ eyi, adzɛ gɛ atɛ ɔmɔ beyi nanà, lɔ tsiɔ tɛ ɔkpɛ ozĩ gagalĩ, wɔ gɛ ɛya katã wɔ oɖi, ɔmɔsɛ adzɛ gɛ yɛishu bashɛnɛ ahɔɛ yɛeyi alɛ ekpè ɔdɛ adzɛ gɛ anyɛ alɛ yɛahɔɛ.”

english_verse: My friends, you were born because of this promise, just as Isaac was.
translation_verse: Mɛadzya alɛ mɔ, otsiɛ nɔ, asɛ Isak ana, nɔonu beyi gɛ batɔɖɔ kibui elu akɔ.

english_verse: But the child who was born in the natural way made trouble for the child who was born because of the Spirit. The same thing is happening today.
translation_verse: Nɛ kibe ɛlɛ, eyianyɛ gɛ bamɔ asɛ gɛ bamɔ̃ kanɔ mɛ anɔ ana, atɔnɔ eyianyɛ gɛ bamɔ edenɔ Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ ɔlɛ ohumi mɛ niede. Kana kidzi otsiɛ tsyɛ.

english_verse: The Scriptures say, “Get rid of the slave woman and her son! He won't be given anything. The son of the free woman will receive everything.”
translation_verse: Pɔ kiɖuwoe Kibuiɛ gɛ bafɔnɔ luɔ ɖɔ dza? “Kakà adzɛ dzidziɛ nɛ̀ yɛeyianyɛ oyinɔ, ɔmɔsɛ adzɛ zidziɛ yɛeyianyɛ atɛ ba mɔ ɔŋãkɔ nɛ kaŋãsiɛ mɛ ɔplɛnɔ adzɛ gɛ alɛ yɛishu ahɔɛ mɛ yɛeyianyɛ eɖi.”

english_verse: My friends, we are children of the free woman and not of the slave.
translation_verse: Klisoɔ, mɛadzya alɛ mɔ, butinu beyi ɔkɔ adzɛ dzidziɛ, pɔ bunu beyianyɛ ɔkɔ adzɛ gɛ alɛ yɛishu ahɔɛ mɛ.

english_verse: Christ has set us free! This means we are really free. Now hold on to your freedom and don't ever become slaves of the Law again.
translation_verse: Ishu ahɔɛmɛ zazã shu so pɛ Kristo akɔ ɔlɔ ishu ahɔɛmɛ zazã. Kliso nɔzã gagalĩi, kɛlɛ nɔtɛ ɔkɔ kɔ ɔwɔ̃nyɔnɔ zã wɔnɔ mɛ nɛ banɔ dzidzi ɛkɔɔ shu.

english_verse: I, Paul, promise you that Christ won't do you any good if you get circumcised.
translation_verse: Nɔ olu awɔ̃ nɛ̀ mɛebuiɛ! Mɛ, Paulo, itseɖe ɔnɔ sɛ onupɛ nɔkɔ bebhui kɛhɔ̃a akɔ ɔnɔɔ, Kristo ezeke edzi kɛdɔ̃ nanà akɔ ɔnɔ kuraa.

english_verse: And if you try to please God by obeying the Law, you have cut yourself off from Christ and his gift of undeserved grace.
translation_verse: Wɔnɔ bagɛ nɔpɛ ɔsɛ tɛ balɔkɔ ɔshaa bhui bɔtsyɔa akɔ ɔnɔ ɔnɔɔ, nobu ishu ɔtɔ nɛ Kristoɔ shu, nɔshɛ nɛ ɔnɔ tibhui huibuɔ niede.

english_verse: But the Spirit makes us sure God will accept us because of our faith in Christ.
translation_verse: Pɔ nɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛɛ, budzi ipo tɛzazã piapi nɔ gɛ buɖi kidzo mɛ ɔkɔ ɔganà Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ mɛ gagalĩ.

english_verse: If you are a follower of Christ Jesus, it makes no difference whether you are circumcised or not. All that matters is your faith that makes you love others.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ nɛ Kristoɔ Yesu mɛɛ, kɛnanà ma kɛhɔ̃a bhui bhui alo kɛhɔ̃a mabhui mabhui mɛ. Kɛgɛ ɛlɛ sɛ beɖiɛ kɛlɛinu tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã gɛibu ishu kasɛ ɔgana tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ mɛ.

english_verse: You were doing so well until someone made you turn from the truth.
translation_verse: Nɔzɔ̀ dɛ kɛxɔ̃ɛ gbangbã. Pɔ afana gɛ akpɛ ɛlɛ nɛ wɔnɔ mɛ ɔsɛ tɛ nɔ otolũ atɔ̃ ɔkɔ kibuiɛ gɛlɛ ɛlɛ ɔzãkɔ dza?

english_verse: And that person was certainly not sent by the one who chose you.
translation_verse: Bulũ wɔɛwɔɛ ɛlɛ nɛidi gɛide agɛ yɛekulu ɔnɔ ɔkɔ.

english_verse: A little yeast can change a whole batch of dough,
translation_verse: “Kɛdɔ̃ɛyi bhlui nɔ gɛ nyanyã ɔdɔ̃, kɛlɛ kɔ pɛ mɔwɔɛ nɔ petee anyanyã.”

english_verse: but you belong to the Lord. This makes me certain you will do what I say, instead of what someone else tells you to do. Whoever is causing trouble for you will be punished.
translation_verse: Ilù awɔ̃ nɛ Apalete nabha sɛ nɔtɛ bɔmɔ kibui ɛlɛ ɔgana kidzo bhlatɔ abha. Anunɔ gɛ etsyinà ɔnɔ ekpeɖekpeɖeɛ mɛ, aba buia ɛlɛ shu ɛbhɛ, anunɔ ɛdi gɛ yɛenu tsyɛɛ o.
