translation_verse: Ebuiɛ ekpè gɛ kɛlɛ sɛ tɛ bɔɖɔ nɛ alɛ shu, pɔ kinu kibui gagalĩ ɔkɔ alɛ mɛ tibubú, ɔmɔsɛ kɛbɔɛbɔɛ lũ nɔkpasɛ ɔdɔ̃.

english_verse: By now you should have been teachers, but once again you need to be taught the simplest things about what God has said. You need milk instead of solid food.
translation_verse: Kibui gbã kinu, sɛ tɛ nodzi badɔ̃kasɛ flogo, pɔ kɛlɛ sɛ tɛ anɔ bhlatɔ teli edzinɔ kɛkpɛ gɛ Owusoso Akaa yɛibuiɛ kpɛ esiɛ pete akasɛ ɔnɔ. Kɛmɔlĩ kɛlɛ pɛ kɔ ɔnɔ, anɛ kɛŋaŋã gagalĩ!

english_verse: People who live on milk are like babies who don't really know what is right.
translation_verse: Anɔ nyia anɔ gɛ aamɔ ɛmɔɔ, anuvɔ ete yɛeke nu gɛ etinyi bɔdɔ̃ kakasɛ nanà nɛibui dadalɛɛ shu.

english_verse: Solid food is for mature people who have been trained to know right from wrong.
translation_verse: Banɔ sheshè balɛ aŋã kɛŋaŋã gagalĩ, gɛ bakpala alɛ aganà ebui gelee mɛ soɔ benyi ibui gbangbã nɛ̀ kizizĩ.

english_verse: We must try to become mature and start thinking about more than just the basic things we were taught about Christ. We shouldn't need to keep talking about why we ought to turn from deeds that bring death and why we ought to have faith in God.
translation_verse: Kliso nɔkɔ bɔshɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ kesi kpɛkpɛ ɔdɔ̃ kakasɛ nɛ Kristo shuɔ lu, tɛ bushe tɔɖɔ. Nɔtɛ ɔkɔ buwlui zɛkasɛ ɔdɔ̃ nɛ kitú mɛ tsyitsyina ebuiɛ gɛ lagbana anɔ akpɛ butsí mɛɛ shu, nɛ̀ ɔdɔ̃ kakasɛ vɔvɔ nɛ kɛyɛkɛ gɛ bɔba yɛkɛ Owusoso Akaa nabha ɔŋã shu,

english_verse: And we shouldn't need to keep teaching about baptisms or about the laying on of hands or about people being raised from death and the future judgment.
translation_verse: bɔdɔ̃ kakasɛ nɛ bulĩkpɛkpɛ ɔkɔ anɔ, ahɔɛ tɔtɔɖɔ nɛ banɔ abha, tɛlɔlɔ odenɔ bekpu mɛ nɛ̀ kebhu gɔgɔɛ tikukulu shu.

english_verse: Let's grow up, if God is willing.
translation_verse: Onupɛ Owusoso Akaa adɛmɛɛ, bɔba bhɛtɛ ɛlɛ ana.

english_verse: But what about people who turn away after they have already seen the light and have received the gift from heaven and have shared in the Holy Spirit? What about those who turn away after they have received the good message of God and the powers of the future world? There is no way to bring them back. What they are doing is the same as nailing the Son of God to a cross and insulting him in public!
translation_verse: pɛ bashɛrɛrɛ avɛ ide, pɛ kɛlɛ sɛ tɛ bewlui akɔ betsyina itú mɛɛ, nu ibui gɛ gagalĩ. Ɔmɔsɛ kɛgɛ kɛlɛ shu bashɛnɛ ahɔɛ, lɛ asɛ gɛ bewlui aka akpalà Owusoso Akaa Yɛeyiɛ nɛ butsí osíɛ shu, pɛ bakpɛ yɛawuĩ nɛ banɔɛishi.

english_verse: A field is useful to farmers, if there is enough rain to make good crops grow. In fact, God will bless such a field.
translation_verse: Kesiakpa nabha gɛ subha adɔ pɛ kamɔ tɛyɔyɔ ibe nyiɛ be, pɛ kakɔ ɔdɔ̃ gbangbã yu nɛ alɛ abha ɔkɔ bayadɛɛ, kamɔ Owusoso Akaa tiyuyula.

english_verse: But land that produces only thornbushes is worthless. It is likely to fall under God's curse, and in the end it will be set on fire.
translation_verse: Kesiakpa nɔnɔ́ pɔ gɛ kanyi nɛ̀ egbegbe ete alɛ adɔ nɛ alɛ abhaa, kagɛpɛ akɔ kɛdɔ̃ nanà, pɔ kedzi esiakpa gɛ Owusoso Akaa aɖɔ ibui akpɛ. Kɛlɛ gɔgɔɛ nɔɔ, bebètsu alɛ ifu.

english_verse: My friends, we are talking this way. But we are sure that you are doing those really good things people do when they are being saved.
translation_verse: Mɛɔbhia alɛ mɔ, gɛ bɔɖɔ ebui alɛ nanà tsyɛ elo, wɔnɔ xɔnɔ bɔyɛkɛ wɔnɔ abha ŋã nɛ wɔnɔ ibui gbangbã-nɛ̀ ebuiɛ gɛ aplɛnɔ wɔnɔ tibubuɔ shu.

english_verse: God is always fair. He will remember how you helped his people in the past and how you are still helping them. You belong to God, and he won't forget the love you have shown his people.
translation_verse: Owusoso Akaa etinu asɛnɛ elete, pɛ abadzɛkɛ wɔnɔ axɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa nɛ̀ wɔnɔ tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ nɛ̀ banɔ gɛ nobu ɔkasɛ ɔgana nɛ tɛpɛplɛnɔɔ gɛ nɔkɔ yɛaxɔ̃ɛwɔa. Gɛ notsiɖi ɛlɛ abha ɔkɔ yɛanunɔ tɛpɛplɛnɔ ɔmana otsiɛ.

english_verse: We wish each of you would always be eager to show how strong and lasting your hope really is.
translation_verse: Bɔpɛ sɛ tɛ wɔnɔ mɛ aka nyia aka avɛ emi kpɛkpɛ alɛ nɛidi abha ɔvɛnɔ kibe gɔgɔ̃, sɛ tɛ nɔmɔ kɛgɛ ɛlɛ idzo mɛ noɖiɛ.

english_verse: Then you would never be lazy. You would be following the example of those who had faith and were patient until God kept his promise to them. God's Promise Is Sure
translation_verse: Bɔgɛ pɛ sɛ nodzi banɔ zhɔzhɔrɛ, pɔ nɔkpasɛ bagɛ azã nɛ̀ ɛkpã ɔgana tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã nɛ̀ tɛdɔdɔ fɔ̃ɔ mɛ, pɛ bayɛkɛ kɛgɛ batɔɖɔ eluɔ. Owusoso Akaa Atɔɖɔ Kɛklagbu Nɛ Yɛtɛtɔtɔɖɔ Nabha

english_verse: No one is greater than God. So he made a promise in his own name when he said to Abraham,
translation_verse: Gɛ Owusoso Akaa abhɛtɛ yɛtɛtɔtɔɖɔ akɔ Abrahamuɔ, atanà yɛishu akɔɛ, ɔmɔsɛ anɔ nanà ama gɛ egbogblo adɛ yɛ pɛ abatanà so,

english_verse: “I, the Lord, will bless you with many descendants!”
translation_verse: kɛlɛ aɖɔ asɛ, “Ɛbayula wɔ kokoko, kɛlɛ ɛbakɔ wɔ beshui gelee.”

english_verse: Then after Abraham had been very patient, he was given what God had promised.
translation_verse: So gɛ Abraham adɔ fɔ̃ pɛ epoɔ, abayɛkɛ kɛgɛ batɔɖɔ eluɔ.

english_verse: When anyone wants to settle an argument, they make a vow by using the name of someone or something greater than themselves.
translation_verse: Banɔ atanà anunɔ gɛ egbogblo adɛ alɛɛ, bɔdɔ̃ tatanà tɔɖɔ kɛklagbu nɛ kibui tili gɛ baɖɔ elu nabha, kɛlɛ bɔlɔkɔ ebhui ibui wɔawlɔãkɛ mɛ nɔ.

english_verse: So when God wanted to prove for certain his promise to his people could not be broken, he made a vow.
translation_verse: Gɛ Owusoso Akaa apɛ asɛ tɛ akasɛ asɛ yɛtɛtɔtɔɖɔ ɛlɛ tɛbà tsyina ɔkɔ bagɛ abaŋã kaŋãsiɛ eɖi soɔ, atanà ɔdɔ̃ atɔɖɔ ɛlɛ abha.

english_verse: God cannot tell lies! And so his promises and vows are two things that can never be changed. We have run to God for safety. Now his promises should greatly encourage us to take hold of the hope that is right in front of us.
translation_verse: Owusoso Akaa abhɛtɛ ɛlɛ ana asɛ, nɛ ebui taabha alɛ gɛ atɛ balà etsyina, pɛ Owusoso Akaa ebedzi asɛnɛ eleteɛ, kɛlɛ sɛ ɔlɔ agɛ bú tɛtsɛɖɛ mɛ ba yɛdɔ̃ kɔkɔ gɛ bulu awɔ̃ nɛ ɛlɛ abha ɔkɔɔ, tɛ bɔkpɛ ishu emi.

english_verse: This hope is like a firm and steady anchor for our souls. In fact, hope reaches behind the curtain and into the most holy place.
translation_verse: Kidzo mɛ ɖiɖi ɛlɛ kɛlɛ nyana bɔlɔ atɔwuiɛ evunɔ asɛ kɛnyana gɛ sɛkɛ anyana kelĩ abha ohu evunɔ pɛ lɔtɛ hɔa ana. Agã kɛpɔtɛ gɛ balɔkɔ ebhui buvũɔ mɛ nɔ̀ mɛ. Etsoku ɔdadalɛ ɔkɔ pɛ avɛ kɛpɔtɛ niede,

english_verse: Jesus has gone there ahead of us, and he is our high priest forever, just like Melchizedek.
translation_verse: nɛ ɛkɛlɛ gɛ Yesu eputɔ editsoku nɛ ɔlɔ inye mɛ. Etsyina edzi banɔlũazazã gbagbla tihɔɔ ɔmɔ asɛ Melxizedek ana.

english_verse: Melchizedek was both king of Salem and priest of God Most High. He was the one who went out and gave Abraham his blessing, when Abraham returned from killing the kings.
translation_verse: Melxizedek alɛ edzi Salemanɛ ekusi nɛ̀ Owusoso Akaa Kitití Ayɛ anɔlũazazã. Atsyana Abraham nɛ kibeɛ gɛ edité bekusi teli pɛ etsyina abaa, kɛlɛ eyula nɛ yɛabha.

english_verse: Then Abraham gave him a tenth of everything he had. The meaning of the name Melchizedek is “King of Justice.” But since Salem means “peace,” he is also “King of Peace.”
translation_verse: Abraham ebu kɛka nyia ka gɛ evunɔɔ, kɛlɛ awɔ̃pɛ lɔfɔmiɛ akɔɛ. Yɛinye gɛiputɔ niesi nu ɔsɛ, “ekusi gɛ abhɛtɛ ibui gɛlɛ ɛlɛ ɔzãkɔ.” Kɛlɛ kɛbhalà gɛinu, “Salemanɛ ekusiɛ” niesi nu ɔsɛ, “ishu yɔyɔ ekusi.”

english_verse: We are not told he had a father or mother or ancestors or beginning or end. He is like the Son of God and will be a priest forever.
translation_verse: Akaa nɛ̀ elĩ agɛzã yɛahɔɛ. Kishopo kpɛkpɛ gɛzã yɛahɔɛ, agɛ kpɛ esi nɛ̀ kiwui nanà, kɛlɛ yɛkpã tsyɛ ɔtɔ̃kɔ nanà ɔgɛzã. Ɔmɔ asɛ Owusoso Akaa Yɛeyi anaa, enu banɔlũazazã tihɔɔ.

english_verse: Notice how great Melchizedek was! Our famous ancestor Abraham gave him a tenth of what he had taken from his enemies.
translation_verse: Noɖi anɔ gbagbla nɛidi gɛ ekusi alɛ edziɛ mɔ. Ashuĩ Abraham kura tsyɛ alɔkɔ ɔdɔ̃ gɛ ayalɛ edenɔ keyuɔ, awɔ̃pɛ lɔfɔ miɛ akɔɛ.

english_verse: The Law teaches that even Abraham's descendants must give a tenth of what they possess. And they are to give this to their own relatives, who are the descendants of Levi and are priests.
translation_verse: Nɛ ɔsha nulũɔ, balɔkɔ Levieyi edzu banɔlũazazã ɛzãkɔ, sɛ tɛ bayɛkɛ ɔdɔ̃ ɔlɛ ɔwɔ̃pɛ lɔfɔmiɛ nɛ banunɔ ahɔɛ-gɛ benu alɛ adzya alɛ-gɛ alɛ tsyɛ enu Abraham yɛeshui alɛ flogo.

english_verse: Although Melchizedek wasn't a descendant of Levi, Abraham gave him a tenth of what he had. Then Melchizedek blessed Abraham, who had been given God's promise.
translation_verse: Ekusi alɛ pɔ etinu Levieyi tsyɛ flogo, pɔ ayɛkɛ ɔdɔ̃ awɔ̃pɛ lɔfɔmiɛ nɛ Abraham ahɔɛ pɛ eyula atɔɖɔ anunɔ gɛ Owusoso Akaa abhɛtɛ tɛtɔtɔɖɔ elu akɔ nabha.

english_verse: Everyone agrees a person who gives a blessing is greater than the one who receives the blessing.
translation_verse: Kawlɔ̃akɛ ama ɛlɛ mɛ ɔsɛ, anunɔ gɛ yɛegbogblo adɛ yɛeyula nɛ ebhluibhlui nabha.

english_verse: Priests are given a tenth of what people earn. But all priests die, except Melchizedek, and the Scriptures teach that he is alive.
translation_verse: Nɛ banɔlũazazã ebui alɛ mɛɛ, banɔ gɛ etsíɛ ahɔɛ bazayɛkɛ ɔdɔ̃ awɔ̃pɛ lɔfɔmiɛ. Badalɛ ɛlɛ mɛ nɛibui bhalà mɛ xɔnɔ asɛ, anunɔ gɛ ayɛkɛ awɔ̃pɛ lɔfɔmi udɔ̃ enu anunɔ gɛ alɛ nɛ ɛkpã.

english_verse: Levi's descendants are now the ones who receive a tenth from people. We could even say that when Abraham gave Melchizedek a tenth, Levi also gave him a tenth.
translation_verse: Anɔ abalà aɖɔ asɛ Levi kura gɛ ayɛkɛ ɔdɔ̃ awɔ̃pɛ lɔfɔmiɛ tsyɛ, akɔ ɔdɔ̃ awɔ̃pɛ lɔfɔmiɛ ɔgãna Abraham abha,

english_verse: This is because Levi was born later into the family of Abraham, who gave a tenth to Melchizedek.
translation_verse: ɔmɔsɛ kibeɛ gɛ Melxizedekiɛ atsyana Abrahamuɔ, bakamɔ Levi nɛ kibe ɛlɛ. Azã Abraham yɛashɛ mɛ ko. Yesu Tɛlɔlɔkɔ Shɔnɔ Melxizedek

english_verse: Even though the Law of Moses says the priests must be descendants of Levi, those priests cannot make anyone perfect. So there needs to be a priest like Melchizedek, rather than one from the priestly family of Aaron.
translation_verse: Pɛ otɛ ɔsɛ Lewieyiɛ anɔlũzazã axɔ̃ɛ dɛdɛ anáa pɛpɛpɛɛ tsyɛ alo bɔtɔ ɖuwoe lu gɛtazã sɛ tɛ bewlui apɛ banɔlũazazã bhlatɔ gɛ alɛ asɛ Melxizedek ana, sɛ tɛ abazã Aron yɛeyi alɛ nɛinye mɛɛ dza?

english_verse: And when the rules for selecting a priest are changed, the Law must also be changed.
translation_verse: Onupɛ banɔlũazazã tɛŋaŋã ibuiɛ tsyinaa, kɛlɛ sɛ tɛ ɔsha tsyɛ tɛ litsyina kokoko.

english_verse: The person we are talking about is our Lord, who came from a tribe that had never had anyone to serve as a priest at the altar.
translation_verse: Anunɔ gɛ yɛishu baɖɔ ebui alɛ, kigbogbo bhlatɔ mɛ yɛede, kɛlɛ anɔ nanà ekede kigbogbo ɛlɛ mɛ abadɛ ɔdɔ̃ pɔpɔɛ ɛxɔ̃ɛ nɛ osubha ɔlɛ shu eɖi.

english_verse: Everyone knows he came from the tribe of Judah, and Moses never said priests would come from that tribe.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ mɛ dalɛ sɛ Yuda igbogboɔ mɛ bɔlɔ Apaleteɛ edekoeyì. Nɛ kigbogbo ɛlɛ shuɔ, Mose agɛɖɔ ibui nanà edenɔ balɛ anɔlũzazã tɛŋaŋã shu.

english_verse: All of this becomes clearer, when someone who is like Melchizedek is appointed to be a priest.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ kɛgɛ baɖɔ kura nɔ mɛ zɛdalɛ dɛ ɔsɛ, onupɛ banɔlũazazã asɛ Melxizedek ana ebekoeyìɛ,

english_verse: The Scriptures say about him, “You are a priest forever, just like Melchizedek.”
translation_verse: Pɔ badalɛ kɛlɛ mɛ asɛ, “Wonu banɔlũazazã tihɔɔ ɔmɔ asɛ Melxizedek ana.”

english_verse: In this way a weak and useless command was put aside,
translation_verse: Bashɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ idzo kukuɔ shu, ɔmɔsɛ ohumi nɛ̀ ibui gbã nanà gɛzã ɔlɛ shu

english_verse: because the Law cannot make anything perfect. At the same time, we are given a much better hope, and it can bring us close to God.
translation_verse: , bakɔ ɔlɔ idzo gbangbã bhlatɔ nɛ awɔ̃ lulú mɛ sɛ tɛ bɔlà tsoplɛnɔ Owusoso Akaa.

english_verse: God himself made a promise when this priest was appointed. But he did not make a promise like this when the other priests were appointed. The promise he made is, “I, the Lord, promise that you will be a priest forever! And I will never change my mind!”
translation_verse: Yesu xɔnɔ ebedzi banɔlũazazã nɛ bɔdɔ̃ tatanà ɔkɔɛ gɛ Owusoso Akaa aɖɔ etseɖe yɛasɛ, “Apaleteɛ atanà ɔdɔ̃ kɛlɛ etibetsyina yɛekpeɖekpeɖeɛ, ‘Wonu banɔlũazazã tihɔɔ.’ ”

english_verse: This means that Jesus guarantees us a better agreement with God.
translation_verse: Bɔdɔ̃ tatanà ɛlɛ soɔ, Yesu ebedzi anunɔ gɛ yɛahɔɛ mɛ olũgbu nyanyã gbangbã lɛ ɔlɛ.

english_verse: There have been a lot of other priests, and all of them have died.
translation_verse: Banɔlũazazã alɛ nɛidi bekpè nɛibe ɛlɛ, pɔ butsí gɛkɔ alɛ idzo sɛ tɛ bavɛ axɔ̃ɛ nabha.

english_verse: But Jesus will never die, and so he will be a priest forever!
translation_verse: Gɛ Yesu pɔ alɛ nɛ ɛkpã tihɔɔ soɔ, etsyina edzi banɔlũazazã ɔvɛnɔ tihɔ, tihɔ.

english_verse: He is forever able to save the people he leads to God, because he always lives to speak to God for them.
translation_verse: Kliso Yesu alà ebu bagɛ aba Owusoso Akaa ɔkɔ, ɔgana yɛabha, ɔmɔsɛ alɛ nɛ ɛkpã gɛ ayɛkɛ ebui nɛ alɛ ulũ.

english_verse: Jesus is the high priest we need. He is holy and innocent and faultless, and not at all like us sinners. Jesus is honored above all beings in heaven,
translation_verse: Banɔlũazazã gbagbla alɛ nɛidi yɛapɛ akɔ bɔlɔ ɔtɔ ebuiɛ, gɛenu anɔ dadalɛ, bamɛ tsitsí ibui ma yɛishu, yɛmɛ bhui, kɛlɛ bebui ɔtɔ nɛ ebui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ elete ishi, kɛlɛ balɔkɔ yɛedzu nɛ ɔɖaɖamakɔ gɛ ɔdɛ pete nɔ.

english_verse: and he is better than any other high priest. Jesus doesn't need to offer sacrifices each day for his own sins and then for the sins of the people. He offered a sacrifice once for all, when he gave himself.
translation_verse: Yesu xɔnɔ atɛ abhɛtɛ asɛ banɔlũazazã gbagbla alɛ gɛ kɛlɛ sɛ beputɔ alɔkɔ balɛishu udɔ̃ pɔpɔɛ nɔ akɔ kiwui nyiɛ wui nɛ balɛ ebui kpa ɛtakpu flogo tɛ bakɔ nɛ banɔ bhlatɔ nɛtakpu. Nɛ kibe tinikpo mɛɛ, alɔkɔ yɛishu akɔ asɛ ɔdɔ̃ pɔpɔɛ ana nɛ alɛ ebui kpa nɛtakpu.

english_verse: The Law appoints priests who have weaknesses. But God's promise, which came later than the Law, appoints his Son. And he is the perfect high priest forever.
translation_verse: Nɛ Mose yɛɔsha nulũɔ, banɔdzyrameɛ gɛ betinu ohumieleteɛ bazalɔkɔ ezedzu banɔlũazazã gbagbla, pɔ nɛ Owusoso Akaa yɛɔdɔ̃ gɛ atanà idegbaa shu soɔ, alɔkɔ Yɛeyiɛ gɛ ewoese piapii nɔ edzu yɛedzi banɔlũazazã gbagbla tihɔ, tihɔ.

english_verse: What I mean is we have a high priest who sits at the right side of God's great throne in heaven.
translation_verse: Kibui nɛiwuhũ gɛ bɔɖɔ nu ɔsɛ, Banɔlũazazã Gbagbla alɛ nɛidi alɛ bɔlɔ ahɔɛ, gɛ azã Ohumielete egbu nɛŋã mɛ nɛititíɛ,

english_verse: He also serves as the priest in the most holy place inside the real tent there in heaven. This tent of worship was set up by the Lord, not by humans.
translation_verse: pɛ adɛ axɔ̃ɛ nɛ ɔdadalɛ ɔkɔ, trɛwɔɛagba gbangbã gɛ Apaleteɛ alɔkɔ yɛahɔɛ edzu nɔ, gɛ ketinu anɔdzyrame yɛedzu alɛ.

english_verse: Since all priests must offer gifts and sacrifices, Christ also needed to have something to offer.
translation_verse: Bezèdzu banɔlũazazã gbagbla ka nyia ka sɛ tɛ azalɔkɔ bɔdɔ̃ kɔkɔ akɔ, tɛ azapɔɛ bɔdɔ̃ tsyɛ, kliso kinu awuhũ dzadzã ibui sɛ tɛ kɛdɔ̃ tili zã yɛtsyɛ ahɔɛ tɛ azakɔ.

english_verse: If he were here on earth, he would not be a priest at all, because here the Law appoints other priests to offer sacrifices.
translation_verse: Pɔ otɛ ɔsɛ kanɔ nɔ̀ mɛ yɛalɛɛ, etibedzi banɔlũazazã, ɔmɔsɛ banɔ alɛ, gɛ bakɔ ɔdɔ̃ nɛ ɔsha nulũ kukuku.

english_verse: But the tent where they serve is just a copy and a shadow of the real one in heaven. Before Moses made the tent, he was told, “Be sure to make it exactly like the pattern you were shown on the mountain!”
translation_verse: Badɛ axɔ̃ɛ nɛ trɛwɔɛagba ɔdadalɛ ɔkɔ, gɛ lodzi asɛ kitití ɔyɛ nɛ̀ kɛtɔwui nɛidi ana. Kliso pɛ Owusoso Akaa ebui ɛlɛ mɛ akɔ Mose nɛ trɛwɔɛagbaa tidzudzuɔ shu asɛ, “Ɖi kɛlɛ ɔkɔ ɔsɛ ɔbhɛtɛ kɛka nyia ka kɛna asɛ kɛgɛ bebu akasɛ wɔ nɛ butu nabha.”

english_verse: Now Christ has been appointed to serve as a priest in a much better way, and he has given us much assurance of a better agreement.
translation_verse: Kɛxɔ̃ɛ dɛdɛɛ pɔ gɛ Kristo ayɛkɛɛ, kigbogblo dɛ balɛ ɛyɛ, asɛ kigbogbloɔ gɛ yɛolugbũ nyanyã gɛ alɔkɔ evũ iboe nɛ bɔlɔ ishiɛ nɔ tsyɛ ogbogblo ɔdɛ balɛ ɔyɛ kukuɔ ana. Ɔmɔsɛ lode tɛtɔtɔɖɔ luɔ gɛpɛ̃ dɛ okukuɔ mɛ.

english_verse: If the first agreement with God had been all right, there would not have been any need for another one.
translation_verse: Pɛ otɛ ɔsɛ kibui nanà gizĩ nɛ olugbũ nyanyã gɛ oputɔ ɔlɛ shuɔ, tsyɛ alo bagɛ takpɛ ɔbhlatɔ nanà nɛ ɔlɛ nɛinye mɛ.

english_verse: But the Lord found fault with it and said, “I tell you the time will come, when I will make a new agreement with the people of Israel and the people of Judah.
translation_verse: Pɔ Owusoso Akaa amɔ esitata ɛbui nɛ banunɔ shu kɛlɛ aɖɔ asɛ, “Apaleteɛ adalɛ kɛlɛ mɛ asɛ, kibe lɛ ɛba, gɛ ɛbabhɛtɛ olugbũ nyanyã vɔvɔ nɛ̀ Israel upãa kɛlɛ nɛ̀ Yuda yɛɔpã.

english_verse: It won't be like the agreement that I made with their ancestors, when I took them by the hand and led them out of Egypt. They broke their agreement with me, and I stopped caring about them!
translation_verse: Kɛtɛ bazã asɛ olugbũ nyanyã gɛ ɛnyã nɛ̀ balɛ ashuĩ alɛ gɛ ɛgbana alɛ nɛ̀ ahɔɛ odenɔ nɛ Egipte ɔmɔsɛ bagɛ zabhɛtɛ nɛ olugbũɔ gɛ ɛnyã nɛ alɛ nabha, kɛlɛ itsyina nɛ alɛ ede, Apaleteɛ adalɛ kɛlɛ mɛ ana.

english_verse: “But now I tell the people of Israel this is my new agreement: ‘The time will come when I, the Lord, will write my laws on their minds and hearts. I will be their God, and they will be my people.
translation_verse: Olugbũ nyanyã gɛ ɛbanyã nɛ̀ Israel upã nu ɔlɛ Apaleteɛ adalɛ kɛlɛ mɛ asɛ, nɛibe ɛlɛ niedeɛ, ɛbakpɛ mɛ ɔshaa nɛ balɛ ekpeɖekpeɖeɛ mɛ kɛlɛ ɛbafɔnɔ ɛlɛ nɛ balɛ etú nabha. Ɛbadzi balɛ Owusoso Akaa, kɛlɛ bebèdzi mɛeyi alɛ.

english_verse: Not one of them will have to teach another to know me, their Lord.’ “All of them will know me, no matter who they are.
translation_verse: Otsi alɛ anɔ nanà azakasɛ yɛɔhagbo ɔdɔ̃, alo etseɖe yɛadzya asɛ, ‘Apaleteɛ nɛ alɛ,’ ɔmɔsɛ balɛ pete bebèti mɛ, odenɔ anuvɔ bhlui nabha ɔvɛnɔ esheshè nabha.

english_verse: I will treat them with kindness, even though they are wicked. I will forget their sins.”
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ ɛbalɔkɔ alɛ atakpu mɛ gagalĩ mɛ ebui tsye alɛ kɛlɛ ɛzabaka balɛ ebui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ iɖi.”

english_verse: When the Lord talks about a new agreement, he means that the first one is out of date. And anything that is old and useless will soon disappear.
translation_verse: Gɛ ekulu olugbũ nyanyã ɔlɛ asɛ, “ɔvɔvɔɔ,” kɛkasɛ ɔsɛ ɔgɛ oputɔ otsyina odzi okuku. Kɛlɛ kɛdɔ̃ nɔnɔ́ gɛ kishe pɛ kidzi ikukuɔ bɔyàla kɛyala.

english_verse: The first promise included rules for worship and a tent for worship here on earth.
translation_verse: Nɛ kibe ɛlɛ, ɔsha zã olugbũ nyanyã kukuɔ shu ɔkɔ ɛtakpu esi kpɛkpɛ nɛ esiakpa trɛwɔɛagba mɛ.

english_verse: The first part of the tent was called the holy place, and a lampstand, a table, and the sacred loaves of bread were kept there.
translation_verse: Bedzu trɛwɔɛagba. Nɛ kalɛ uvũ gɛiputɔ mɛɛ, bedzu kifu osi, ɔkpɔlɔ̃ nɛ̀ kifufulũ gɛ ɛlɛishu badalɛ. Bekulu ɛkɛlɛ asɛ Ɔdadalɛ Ɔkɔ.

english_verse: Behind the curtain was the most holy place.
translation_verse: Nɛ kɛpɔtɛ bhalà niedeɛ buvũ lɛ ɛkɛlɛ gɛ bekulu ɛkɛlɛ asɛ, Ɔdadalɛ Ɔkɔ Ɔdɛ.

english_verse: The gold altar for burning incense was in this holy place. The gold-covered sacred chest was also there, and inside it were three things. First, there was a gold jar filled with manna. Then there was Aaron's walking stick that sprouted. Finally, there were the flat stones with the Ten Commandments written on them.
translation_verse: Nɛ buvũ lɛ mɛɛ, kilifi ɔpɔɛkɔ osubha gɛ balɔkɔ sikã abhɛtɛ ɔlɛ nɔ, nɛ̀ olugbũ nyanyã aɖaka gɛ bedere sikã nɛ yɛishu alɛ. Nɛ aɖaka nɔ mɛɛ, sikãitsikpi gɛ manà akpasɛ ɛlɛ mɛ, Aron yɛosi vuvũ gɛ obu awlɔakpa, nɛ̀ aklagbu taabha nabha gɛ bafɔnɔ olugbũ nyanyã ebuiɛ atɔɖɔɔ azã.

english_verse: On top of the chest were the glorious creatures with wings opened out above the place of mercy. Now isn't the time to go into detail about these things.
translation_verse: Bekerubim alɛ aɖaka nabha gɛ baakasɛ Owusoso Akaa yɛishu dadalɛ, kɛlɛ balɛ ɔsɛ̀ fè tɔɖɔ ɛkɛlɛ gɛ bɔmɔ ashagabui ɖiɖi odenɔ nabha. Bɔtɛ balà bu ebui alɛ nyɔ̃ɔ mɛ ɔvɛnɔ otugbà otsiɛ.

english_verse: But this is how everything was when the priests went each day into the first part of the tent to do their duties.
translation_verse: Onupɛ bedzu udɔ̃ nɛ bɔlɛ ɔzãkɔ ebeke ana, kɛlɛ banɔlũazazã etsoku trɛwɔɛagba gɛ alɛ ɔtɔ̃ ɛbeɛ pɛ adɛ axɔ̃ɛ nɛ ɛkɛlɛ.

english_verse: However, only the high priest could go into the second part of the tent, and he went in only once a year. Each time he carried blood to offer for his sins and for any sins that the people had committed without meaning to.
translation_verse: Banɔlũazazã gbagbla ete ko yɛamɔ idzo etsoku ovuede uvũɔ mɛ, kɛlɛ ɛdɛ tinikpo nɛ kɛgɔ ka nyia ka mɛ. Onupɛ avɛɛ, alɔkɔ agã dzyosu evunɔ sɛ tɛ alɔkɔ akɔ nɛ yɛahɔɛ ɛtakpu nɛ̀ banunɔ ebui mati mati gɛ babhɛtɛ nɛ ɛtakpu.

english_verse: All of this is the Holy Spirit's way of saying no one could enter the most holy place while the tent was still the place of worship.
translation_verse: Nɛ kɛlɛ mɛɛ, Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã obu kɛlɛ ɔkasɛ ɔsɛ, gɛ trɛwɔɛuvũɔ gɛiputɔ lɛ ɛkalɛ koɔ, kɛkasɛ ɔsɛ bekebu idzoɔ gɛili ɔvɛ Ɔdadalɛ Ɔkɔ Ɔdɛ akasɛ.

english_verse: This also has a meaning for today. It shows we cannot make our consciences clear by offering gifts and sacrifices.
translation_verse: Kinu udɔ̃ kakasɛ ɔlɔkɔ shɔnɔ nɛ kibe ɛlɛ shu, sɛ ɔdɔ̃ kɔkɔ nɛ̀ bɔdɔ̃ pɔpɔɛ nɔ gɛlà dalɛ bagɛ azakpɛ ɛtakpu esiɛ etú mɛ ebuiɛ shu.

english_verse: These rules are merely about such things as eating and drinking and ceremonies for washing ourselves. And rules about physical things will last only until the time comes to change them for something better.
translation_verse: Bɔdɔ̃ ɔlɛ nu asɛ, bɔdɔ̃ ŋaŋã nɛ̀ ɔdɔ̃ wɔɛwɔɛ nɛ̀ ɛkɔ ediedi ebui tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ ana, alɛ nyɔ̃ɔ enu kanɔ mɛ ebui tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ gɛ lazã ɔvɛnɔ kibeɛ gɛ tɛtɔtɔɖɔ vɔvɔ ɛlɛ baba. Kristo Yɛdzyosu Nɔ

english_verse: Christ came as the high priest of the good things that are now here. He also went into a much better tent that wasn't made by humans and that doesn't belong to this world.
translation_verse: Gɛ Kristo abaa asɛ banɔlũazazã gbagbla ɔkɔ ɔdɔ̃ gbangbã gɛlɛ ɛkɛlɛɛ, agã afala nɛ trɛwɔɛagba gɛ egbogblo pɛ kaná ɔdɛ trɛwɔɛagba ka nyia ka, gɛ anɛ anɔdzyrame yɛahɔɛ yɛedzui alɛ nɔ mɛ. Gulũi nɔɔ, kegide kanɔɛ alɛ mɛ.

english_verse: Then Christ went once for all into the most holy place and freed us from sin forever. He did this by offering his own blood instead of the blood of goats and bulls.
translation_verse: Kristo agɛ lɔkɔ bepidzya nɛ̀ banɛtsyɔɛeyi dzyosu evunɔ flogo pɛ etsoku Ɔdadalɛ, Ɔkɔ Ɔdɛ ɔlɛ mɛ. Itsoku tunikpo nɛ ebeɛ pete ulũ yɛadɛ nɛ̀ yɛahɔɛ dzyosu, sɛ tɛ amɔ ɛkpã tihɔɔ akɔ ɔlɔ.

english_verse: According to the Law of Moses, those people who become unclean are not fit to worship God. Yet they will be considered clean, if they are sprinkled with the blood of goats and bulls and with the ashes of a sacrificed calf.
translation_verse: Bepidzya nɛ̀ banɛtsyɔɛ dzyosu, nɛ̀ banɛtsyɔɛelĩ pɔpɔɛ ɔtɔ̃ɔ gɛ balɔkɔ ahɔɛ nɛ bagɛ balɛishu gɛ dalɛ shuɔ sɛ tɛ balɛishu dalɛ nɛ ishupi mɛ ete.

english_verse: But Christ was sinless, and he offered himself as an eternal and spiritual sacrifice to God. This is why his blood is much more powerful and makes our consciences clear. Now we can serve the living God and no longer do things that lead to death.
translation_verse: Kiɖuwoe gɛ bakɔ ɔsɛ, Kristo gɛ agã Ɔfɔfuĩa tihɔɔ nɔ mɛ pɛ alɔkɔ yɛishu akɔ, pɛ bamɛ tsitsí nanà ma yɛishu nɛ Owusoso Akaa na mɛɛ, atɛ alà akɔ asɛ yɛdzyosu nɔ eplu bɔlɔ etú mɛ ebui kpa gɛ lagbana ɔlɔ akpɛ butsí mɛɛ, sɛ tɛ bɔmɔ idzo dɛ axɔ̃ɛ ɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa gɛenu enunuɔ mɔɔ dza!

english_verse: Christ died to rescue those who had sinned and broken the old agreement. Now he brings his chosen ones a new agreement with its guarantee of God's eternal blessings!
translation_verse: Nɛ kidzo ɛlɛ nabhaa, Kristo edzi iboe evuvũ akɔ olũgbu nyanyã vɔvɔ ɔlɛ, sɛ tɛ bagɛ bekuluɔ tɛ bamɔ kaŋãsi tihɔɔ ayɛkɛ nɛ tɛtɔtɔɖɔ nulũ, Kristo ebetsí sɛ tɛ ebu balɛ eyinɔ nɛ balɛ ebui kpaa gɛ babhɛtɛ nɛ olugbũ nyanyã gɛ oputɔ nɛibeɛ.

english_verse: In fact, making an agreement of this kind is like writing a will. This is because the one who makes the will must die before it is of any use.
translation_verse: Bawɔa kibui tɔtɔɖɔ lu ɛlɛ shu kɛxɔ̃ɛ onupɛ anunɔ gɛ atɔɖɔ ibuiɛ elu etsí pɛ abhama nɛ ɛkpãa,

english_verse: In other words, a will doesn't go into effect as long as the one who made it is still alive.
translation_verse: ɔmɔsɛ kibui tili gɛ anɔ teli atɔɖɔ eluɔ, kidzi ɛxɔ̃ɛwɔadɔ̃ onupɛ etsíɛ, kɛtɛ wɔa ɛxɔ̃ɛ onupɛ anunɔ akalɛ nɛ ɛkpã.

english_verse: Blood was also used to put the first agreement into effect.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ so onupɛ bagɛmɔ dzyosu nɔ, bagɛzɛ eshu olugbũ nyanyã gɛoputɔ ɔkɔ.

english_verse: Moses told the people all the Law said they must do. Then he used red wool and a hyssop plant to sprinkle the people and the book of the Law with the blood of bulls and goats and with water.
translation_verse: Kliso onupɛ Mose akalà ɔsha ka nyia ka akɔ banunɔ pete nɔ, azalɔkɔ banɛtsyɔɛeyi dzyosu, bulĩ nɛ̀ obhoshi tɛkã dzadzã ɔlɔkɔ plɛnɔ hisɔp shu pɛ alɔkɔ azahɔ̃ɛ nɛ keplukpa nɛ̀ banunɔ abha.

english_verse: He told the people, “With this blood God makes his agreement with you.”
translation_verse: Azaɖɔ asɛ, “Olugbũ nyanyã dzyosu nɔ gɛ Owusoso Akaa akpɛ elu akɔ ɔnɔ asɛ tɛ nɔnyana ɔlɛ mɛ ovunɔɔ, nɔ ɔlɛ.”

english_verse: Moses also sprinkled blood on the tent and on everything else used in worship.
translation_verse: Nɛidzo ɛlɛ nabhaa yɛazahɔɛ dzyosu nɔ nɛ trɛwɔɛagba nɛ̀ kɛka nyia ka shu axɔ̃ɛ gɛ bawɔa nɛ ɔkɔ oshukɔ nabha.

english_verse: The Law says that almost everything must be sprinkled with blood, and no sins can be forgiven unless blood is offered. Christ's Great Sacrifice
translation_verse: Kibui gbã kinu nɛ ɔsha nulũ ɔsɛ tɛbalɔkɔ dzyosu adalɛ kɛka nyia ka shu nɔ, kɛlɛ onupɛ dzyosu agɛ balɛ nɛ esiɛ, kibui tɛlɔlɔkɔ otsye nanà tsyɛ ma.

english_verse: These things are only copies of what is in heaven, and so they had to be made holy by these ceremonies. But the real things in heaven must be made holy by something better.
translation_verse: So awuhũ dzadzã ibui kidzi sɛ tɛ balɔkɔ ɔdɔ̃ pɔpɔɛ adalɛ ɔdɔ̃ gɛ budzi asɛ kitití uyɛ ɛtɔwuiɛ ana shu. Kitití udɔ̃ pɔɔ, bɔdɔ̃ gɛ balɔkɔ adalɛ bɔlɛishu nɔɔ, pɛ̀ dɛ ɔlɛ.

english_verse: This is why Christ did not go into a tent made by humans and was only a copy of the real one. Instead, he went into heaven and is now there with God to help us.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ Kristo egitsoku trɛwɔɛagba gɛ banɔ bedzu pɛ kenu kagbangbã ɔdɛ ɛtɔwui ɛyɛ mɛ. Kitití boŋ yɛavɛ pɛ alɛ Owusoso Akaa na mɛ nɛ bɔlɔ inye mɛ.

english_verse: Christ did not have to offer himself many times. He wasn't like a high priest who goes into the most holy place each year to offer the blood of an animal.
translation_verse: Kristo agɛvɛ ititíɛ sɛ tɛ ewlui adɛ̀ alɔkɔ yɛishu akɔ edzinɔ akɔ, ɔmɔ asɛ kitsoku gɛ banɔlũazazã gbagbla etsoku Ɔdadalɛ Ɔkɔ Ɔdɛ kɛgɔ nyiɛ gɔ nɛ̀ dzyosu gɛ etinu yɛahɔɛ ayɛ yɛenuɔ.

english_verse: If he had offered himself every year, he would have suffered many times since the creation of the world. But instead, near the end of time he offered himself once and for all, so he could be a sacrifice that does away with sin.
translation_verse: Pɛ otɛ ɔsɛ kana kɛlɛɛ, tsyɛ alo Kristo atazã bɔtɔ gelee odenɔ kanɔ mɛ esi kpɛkpɛ ibe. Pɔ otsiɛ nɔ Kristo aba kanɔ nɔ̀ mɛ nɛ tɛbaba tinikpo ete mɛ ko nɛ kibe gɔgɔɛ lɛ mɛ, sɛ tɛ alɔkɔ yɛishu akɔ, sɛ tɛ ebu ɔlɔ ebui kpa atɛ.

english_verse: We die only once, and then we are judged.
translation_verse: Asɛ ɛtɔɖɔ gɛ batɔɖɔ akɔ anɔdzyrame asɛ tɛ etsí titsitsí tinikpo, kɛlɛ nɛ ɛlɛ niedeɛ, bebèkulu ebhu nii,

english_verse: So Christ died only once to take away the sins of many people. But when he comes again, it will not be to take away sin. He will come to save everyone who is waiting for him.
translation_verse: kliso Kristo alɔkɔ yɛishu akɔ nɛ kibe tinikpo mɛ, sɛ tɛ ebu banɔ gelee ebui kpa atɛ, kɛlɛ azababa bɔbhalà, anɛ ɔlɔ ebui kpa tɛyɛyɛkɛ so pɛ azababa, pɔ ababá sɛ tɛ ebu bagɛ belu awɔ̃ nɛ yɛtɛbabá nabhaa.

english_verse: The Law of Moses is like a shadow of the good things to come. This shadow isn't the good things themselves, because it cannot free people from sin by the sacrifices offered year after year.
translation_verse: Kɛtɔwui ete ɔsha onu ɔkɔ ebui gbangbã gɛ laba shu-anɛ alɛ enu ebuiɛ gɛ bɔpɛ sɛ tɛ bɔmɔ. Kliso ɔsha tɛbɔlà gã kɛgɔ nyiɛ gɔ ɔdɔ̃ pɔpɔɛ mɛ pɛ lɔbɔbhɛtɛ bagɛ bakpɛ ɛtakpu esi akɔ Owusoso Akaa, sɛ tɛ bana piapii nɔ ɖa.

english_verse: If there were worshipers who already have their sins washed away and their consciences made clear, there would not be any need to go on offering sacrifices.
translation_verse: Pɛ otɛ ɔsɛ lɔbɔlà bhɛtɛ anɔ sɛ tɛ ana piapii nɔ tsyɛ alo batɛ yɔlɔ bɔdɔ̃ pɔpɔɛ nɔ xɔ̃ɔ. Tsyɛ alo bagɛ bakpɛ ɛtakpu esiɛ shu tabhuì nɛ kibe tinikpo abha ɔvɛnɔ tihɔɔ, kɛlɛ balɛ etúɔ tsyɛ ezebu alɛ ɛpɔ nɛ alɛ ebui kpaa shu.

english_verse: But the blood of bulls and goats cannot take away sins. It only reminds people of their sins from one year to the next.
translation_verse: ɔmɔsɛ banɛtsyɔɛ nɛ̀ bepidzya dzyosu atɛ balà ebu ebui kpa atɛ ɖa.

english_verse: When Christ came into the world, he said to God, “Sacrifices and offerings are not what you want, but you have given me my body.
translation_verse: Kliso gɛ Kristo aba kanɔ nɔ̀ mɛɛ, aɖɔ asɛ, “Anɛ bɔdɔ̃ pɔpɔɛ nɛ̀ bɔdɔ̃ kɔkɔ wɔpɛ, pɔ ishu gɛ owoese ɔkɔ mɛ,

english_verse: No, you are not pleased with animal sacrifices and offerings for sin.”
translation_verse: bɔdɔ̃ pɔpɔɛ kɔkɔ nɛ̀ ebui kpa bubútɛ ɔdɔ̃ kɔkɔ tiku wɔ same.

english_verse: Then Christ said, “And so, my God, I have come to do what you want, as the Scriptures say.”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ mɛɖɔ ɔsɛ, ‘Mɛ nɛ alɛ, bafɔnɔ nɛ keplukpa mɛ nɛ mɛishu asɛ, O, Owusoso Akaa, ɛba sɛ tɛ mɛbhɛtɛ nɛ wɔ tɛtɔtɔɖɔ abha.’ ”

english_verse: The Law teaches that offerings and sacrifices must be made because of sin. But why did Christ mention these things and say that God did not want them?
translation_verse: Aɖɔ eputɔ asɛ, “Ɔkɔ̀ bɔdɔ̃, nɛ̀ bɔdɔ̃ kɔkɔ, bɔdɔ̃ pɔpɔɛ kɔkɔ nɛ̀ ɔdɔ̃ kɔkɔ nɛ ebui kpa ɛtakpu tiku wɔ same, pɛ kɛba dzi sɛ ɔmɔ same nɛ bɔlɛishu” .

english_verse: Well, it was to do away with offerings and sacrifices and to replace them. This is what he meant by saying to God, “I have come to do what you want.”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ azaɖɔ asɛ, “Mɛ nɛ alɛ, ɛba sɛ tɛ mɛbhɛtɛ nɛ wɔtɛdɛdɛmɛ ulũ. “Ebu tɛtɔtɔɖɔ gɛiputɔɔ, pɛ akpɛ ɔbhalà nɛ ɔlɛinye mɛ.

english_verse: So we are made holy because Christ obeyed God and offered himself once for all.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ gana kibui tɔtɔɖɔ ɛlɛ mɛɛ, Owusoso Akaa abhɛtɛ ɔlɔ budzi banɔ dadalɛ ɔgana Yesu Kristo yɛishu tɛlɔlɔkɔ ɔkɔ nɛ ebeɛ pete ulũ.

english_verse: The priests do their work each day, and they keep on offering sacrifices that can never take away sins.
translation_verse: Nɛ kiwui nyiɛ wui abhaa, banɔlũazazã ka nyia ka ewlui avɛ yɛxɔ̃ɛ abha, pɛ balɔkɔ bɔdɔ̃ pɔpɔɛ tunikpo ɛlɛ nɛidi, gɛ tɛlà bu ebui kpa tɛ nɔ akɔ.

english_verse: But Christ offered himself as a sacrifice that is good forever. Now he is sitting at God's right side,
translation_verse: Pɔ gɛ Kristo abalɔkɔ yɛishu akɔ, bɔkɔ tunikpo, nɛ ebui kpa nɛ takpu, nɛ ebeɛ pete ulũɔ, adɛzã esi nɛ Owusoso Akaa nɛ ŋã mɛ.

english_verse: and he will stay there until his enemies are put under his power.
translation_verse: Denɔ kibe ɛlɛ ɔvɛnɔɔ, alɛ epo kiwui ɛlɛ nabha gɛ babà lɔkɔ bagɛ batɛ adɛmɛ niɛ abhɛtɛ yɛtɛtsɛɖɛ egbu,

english_verse: By his one sacrifice he has forever set free from sin the people he brings to God.
translation_verse: ɔmɔsɛ gana bɔdɔ̃ pɔpɔɛ tunikpo mɛɛ, abhɛtɛ bagɛ balɛishu badalɛɛ, bamɔ tɛdadalɛ tihɔɔ.

english_verse: The Holy Spirit also speaks of this by telling us that the Lord said,
translation_verse: Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã tsyɛ ɔdalɛ ibui mɛ ɔkɔ ɔlɔ nɛ kɛli shu. Aɖɔ eputɔ nɔ asɛ,

english_verse: “When the time comes, I will make an agreement with them. I will write my laws on their minds and hearts.
translation_verse: “Ɔmɔ olũgbu nyanyã gɛ ɛbanyã nɛ alɛ nɛ kibe ɛlɛ niedeɛ, Apaleteɛ aɖɔ asɛ, ɛbalɔkɔ mɔsha kpɛ alɛ etúɔ mɛ, kɛlɛ ɛbafɔnɔ ɛlɛ nɛ balɛ ekpeɖekpeɖeɛ mɛ.”

english_verse: Then I will forget about their sins and no longer remember their evil deeds.”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ azaɖɔ aplɛnɔ asɛ, “Balɛ ebui kpa nɛ̀ ɔsha abha mabhɛtɛ mabhɛtɛ ɛzabaka.”

english_verse: When sins are forgiven, there is no more need to offer sacrifices. Encouragement and Warning
translation_verse: Kliso gɛ balɔkɔ ebui alɛ pete etsyeɛ, kɛlɛ bɔdɔ̃ pɔpɔɛ sɛ tɛ bebu ebui kpa atɛ nɔ nanà abha ma. Buluhũ Huhu Ɔkɔ Emi Kpɛkpɛ

english_verse: My friends, the blood of Jesus gives us courage to enter the most holy place
translation_verse: Kliso mɛadzya alɛ mɔ, gɛ bɔyɛkɛŋã sɛ bɔbatsoku buvũɔ Ɔdadalɛ Ɔkɔ Ɔdɛ mɛ nɛ Yesu dzyosu nɔ mɛ soɔ,

english_verse: by a new way that leads to life! And this way takes us through the curtain that is Christ himself.
translation_verse: gɛ ɛkpã nɛ̀ idzo vɔvɔ ɛlɛ dalɛ kɔ ɔlɔ ɔgana kɛpɔtɛ gɛinu yɛishupiɛ mɛɛ,

english_verse: We have a great high priest who is in charge of God's house.
translation_verse: kliso otsi alɛ gɛ banɔlũazazã hohoe alɛ àlɛ Owusoso Akaa ɔpã nulũɔ,

english_verse: So let's come near God with pure hearts and a confidence that comes from having faith. Let's keep our hearts pure, our consciences free from evil, and our bodies washed with clean water.
translation_verse: nɔkɔ butso ɔplɛnɔ Owusoso Akaa nɛ̀ etúɔ gɛ akpɛ igu nɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã gagalĩi mɛ, etúɔ gɛ beplu alɛ mɛ ebui zizĩ eyinɔ nɛ bɔlɔ ishupiɛ gɛ bewulu nɔ piapii nɛ̀ bulĩ dadalɛ.

english_verse: We must hold tightly to the hope we say is ours. After all, we can trust the one who made the agreement with us.
translation_verse: Nɔkɔ bɔnyana kɛgɛ kɛlɛ mɛ bɔdalɛ, pɛ buuɖi kɛlɛ idzo mɛɛ vunɔ gagalĩi, ɔmɔsɛ anunɔ gɛ yɛatɔɖɔ kibuiɛ lɛ eluɔ abhɛtɛ ibui abha.

english_verse: We should keep on encouraging each other to be thoughtful and to do helpful things.
translation_verse: Nɔkɔ bɔkpɛ ɛtakpu mɛ nɛ kɛnyana gɛ bɔba nyana bagɔlɔ nɛ̀ tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ nɛ̀ ebui gbangbã tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ mɛ.

english_verse: Some people have given up the habit of meeting for worship, but we must not do that. We should keep on encouraging each other, especially since you know that the day of the Lord's coming is getting closer.
translation_verse: Nɔtɛkɔ bɔshɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ tɛtsyɔatsyɔa itsu shu, ɔmɔ asɛ kɛbhɛtɛ gɛ beteli baabhɛtɛ ana, pɔ nɔkɔ bɔkpɛ bagɔlɔ emi, kɛlɛ nɔbhɛtɛ ɛlɛ ɔtɔɖɔ ɔdɛ, gɛ nɔɔ mɔ ɔsɛ Kiwuiɛ tso ubaa.

english_verse: No sacrifices can be made for people who decide to sin after they find out about the truth.
translation_verse: Onupɛ bútì ibuiɛ gɛlɛ ɛlɛ ɔzãkɔ, flogo pɛ bɔkà tsodzi vɛ ebui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ abhaa, kɛlɛ bɔdɔ̃ pɔpɔɛ nanà bhama ɔkɔ ebui kpaa tibubu tɛ,

english_verse: They are God's enemies, and all they can look forward to is a terrible judgment and a furious fire.
translation_verse: kifu sisí tili idi mɛ ko bɔbazã pɛ bɔbaapo kɛpɔ bubú, nɛ̀ kifù hohoeɛ gɛ baapɔɛ bagɛ batɛ dɛmɛ nɛ̀ Owusoso Akaa.

english_verse: If two or more witnesses accused someone of breaking the Law of Moses, that person could be put to death.
translation_verse: Bezeyí anɔ nyia anɔ gɛ agɛ dɛmɛ atɔɖɔ Mose yɛɔsha nabhaa, nɛ̀ ashagabui maɖimaɖi. Onupɛ banɔ taabha alo tatá adalɛ ibui mɛ nɛ yɛishuɔ.

english_verse: But it is much worse to dishonor God's Son and to disgrace the blood of the promise that made us holy. And it is just as bad to insult the Holy Spirit, who shows us mercy.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛɛ batɔ̃ pɔpɔ̃ gagalĩ ɖuwoe lu gɛ bɔyɛkɛŋã sɛ babàkɔ anunɔ gɛ akpata Owusoso Akaa Yɛeyiɛ, pɛ agɛ lɔkɔɛ nanà nɛ dzyosu olugbũ nyanyã gɛ alɔkɔ adalɛ yɛishu nɔ mɛ, pɛ ebu olugbũ mɛ nɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ gɛ lobhui banɔ obuɔ shu oɖiɛ mɔɔ dza?

english_verse: We know that God has said he will punish and take revenge. We also know that the Scriptures say the Lord will judge his people.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ bunyi anunɔ gɛ aɖɔ asɛ, “Mɛinu anunɔ gɛ ibisi otití, ɛbabhɛtɛ kpɛ ɔlɛ inye mɛ,” kɛlɛ azaɖɔ ɔsɛ, “Apaleteɛ ebekulu ebhu nɛ̀ yɛamadiɛ.”

english_verse: It is a terrible thing to fall into the hands of the living God!
translation_verse: Kinu ibui gɛiku ifù sɛ tɛ anɔ teli atsɛ eku Owusoso Akaa enunuɔ ahɔɛ mɛ.

english_verse: Don't forget all the hard times you went through when you first received the light.
translation_verse: Nɔbaka ewuiɛ gɛ abafala pɛ nɔyɛkɛ tɛdadalɛ oɖi. Kibe ɛlɛ nɔzã piapii, nɔɔkpɛ emi ɔzɔwlɔãkɛ gagalĩi nɛ bɔtɔ gelee mɛ.

english_verse: Sometimes you were abused and mistreated in public, and at other times you shared in the sufferings of others.
translation_verse: Nɛ kibeɛ tili mɛɛ, baakwala ɔnɔ nɛ ɔmɛishi pɛ baatɔnɔ ɔnɔ ede. Kibeɛ tili pɔɔ, baalɔkɔ ɔnɔ akpɛ bagɛ balɛ tsyɛ azamɔ bɔtɔ ɔlɛ nɛidiɛ nɛishi.

english_verse: You were kind to people in jail. And you gladly let your possessions be taken away, because you knew you had something better, something that would last forever.
translation_verse: Nɔɔnyɔnɔ awɔ̃ nɛ bagɛ bakpɛ nɛ ɛɖavũ mɛɛ shu, kɛlɛ nɔdɛmɛ bayɛkɛ ɔnɔ ahɔɛ mɛ bɔdɔ̃ tsyɛ nɛ ɔnɔ ahɔɛ faa. Ɔmɔsɛ nonyi ɔsɛ bɔdɔ̃ gbangbã gɛdɛ ɔlɛ bazã ɔnɔ ahɔɛ tihɔɔ kiwui tili.

english_verse: Keep on being brave! It will bring you great rewards.
translation_verse: Kliso nɔtɛ shɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ wɔnɔ awɔ̃ luluɔ shu olu, ɔmɔsɛ kɛbhɛ babuia hohoe lɛ kɛlɛishu ɔkɔ ɔnɔ.

english_verse: Learn to be patient, so you will please God and be given what he has promised.
translation_verse: Nɔkpɛ emi, pɛ nɔbhɛtɛ nɛ Owusoso Akaa yɛtɛtɔtɔɖɔ lu abhaa, nɔbɔ yɛkɛ kɛgɛ atɔɖɔ elu asɛ abakɔ ɔnɔɔ.

english_verse: As the Scriptures say, “God is coming soon! It won't be very long.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ nɛ kibe gulũi mɛ koɔ, “Anɔ nɔ̀ gɛ yɛabaa, atɛ bawɔ ede.

english_verse: The people God accepts will live because of their faith. But he isn't pleased with anyone who turns back.”
translation_verse: Mɛ anɔ gbangbã pɔɔ, abazã nɛ̀ ɛkpã gana tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛ. Kɛlɛ etsodzi etsyina avɛ kideɛ, yɛibui zaazã mɛuli.”

english_verse: We are not like those people who turn back and get destroyed. We will keep on having faith until we are saved.
translation_verse: Bɔlɔ xɔnɔ bɔma banunɔ gɛ betsodzi etsyina avɛ ideɛ pɛ betsíɛ nɛishi, pɔ bɔlɔ nu bagɛ ayɛkɛ aŋã, pɛ bebu ɔlɔ.

english_verse: Faith makes us sure of what we hope for and gives us proof of what we cannot see.
translation_verse: Tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã nu kɛgɛ olu awɔ̃ nɛ ɛlɛ abha pɛ oɖi kɛdɔ̃ gɛ ɔtɛ ɔmɔ nɛidzo mɛ.

english_verse: It was their faith that made our ancestors pleasing to God.
translation_verse: Nɛidzo ɛlɛ nabhaa ebelianɛ nɛibui ku Owusoso Akaa awɔ̃.

english_verse: Because of our faith, we know that the world was made at God's command. We also know that what can be seen was made out of what cannot be seen.
translation_verse: Denɔ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛɛ, bulũ kɛlɛ mɛ ɔsɛ, kibui Owusoso Akaa aɖɔ alɔkɔ abhɛtɛ kanɔ nɔ̀ mɛ nɔ̀. Kliso kɛgɛ bɔmɔ otsiɛ nɔ, babhɛtɛ ɛlɛ edenɔ kɛgɛ bɔtɛlà mɔ mɛ.

english_verse: Because Abel had faith, he offered God a better sacrifice than Cain did. God was pleased with him and his gift, and even though Abel is now dead, his faith still speaks for him.
translation_verse: Nɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛɛ, Abel akɔ Owusoso Akaa ɔdɔ̃ pɔpɔɛ gbangbã ɔdɛ Kain. Gana tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛɛ, yɛibui ku Owusoso Akaa awɔ̃ kɛlɛ aɖɔaɖɔ gbangbã nɛ yɛɔdɔ̃ kɔkɔɔ shu. Abel etitsí, pɔ nɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛɛ, akaɖɔaɖɔ.

english_verse: Enoch had faith and did not die. He pleased God, and God took him up to heaven. This is why his body was never found.
translation_verse: Tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛ kide sɛ befeke Henox avɛnɔ itití nɛ yɛkpã mɛ, egitsí so bagɛlà amɔɛ, ɔmɔsɛ Owusoso Akaa yɛefeke ashɛnɔ. Flogo tɛ befeke yɛɛ, edzi anunɔ gɛ yɛibui ku Owusoso Akaa awɔ̃.

english_verse: But without faith no one can please God. We must believe that God is real and rewards everyone who searches for him.
translation_verse: Kɛba gagalĩ sɛ tɛ oku Owusoso Akaa awɔ̃, onupɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã ma wɔahɔɛ. Ɔmɔsɛ anɔ nyia anɔ gɛ aba yɛamɛɛ, kɛlɛ sɛ tɛ ayɛkɛ aŋã asɛ Owusoso Akaa alɛ, kɛlɛ abuia ɛbhɛ akɔ bagɛ apɛ yɛ nɛ̀ awuhũ dzadzã.

english_verse: Because Noah had faith, he was warned about something that had not yet happened. He obeyed and built a boat that saved him and his family. In this way the people of the world were judged, and Noah was given the blessings that come to everyone who pleases God.
translation_verse: Gana tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛɛ, gɛ bakɔ Noa yɛeti asɛ kɛgɛ kabaɖi bàba ɛlɛ mɛɛ, alɔkɔ atɔ̃ lulũ gbangbã afatɛ kelĩabha ohu pɛ alɔkɔ ebu yɛɔpã nɔ. Alɔkɔ yɛtɛyɛyɛkɛŋã ebu kanɔ mɛanɛ nɛpɔ nɔ̀, kɛlɛ edzi kaŋãsiɛelete gbangbã gɛide tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛ.

english_verse: Abraham had faith and obeyed God. He was told to go to the land that God had said would be his, and he left for a country he had never seen.
translation_verse: Tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã kɔ ɔsɛ gɛ betseɖe Abraham asɛ ashɛ avɛ esiakpa bhlatɔ abha tɛ kebe dzi yɛayɛ idegbaa, elũ atɔ̃, kɛlɛ avɛ, gɛ etinyi ɛkɛlɛ gɛ avɛ kura tsyɛ flogo.

english_verse: Because Abraham had faith, he lived as a stranger in the promised land. He lived there in a tent, and so did Isaac and Jacob, who were later given the same promise.
translation_verse: Nɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛɛ, Abraham ebhui bɔpã nɛ esiakpa gɛ kebe dzi yɛayɛ nabha, asɛ efu ana nɛ kɛmadi bhlatɔ mɛ. Abraham, Isak ɔlɔkɔ ɔplɛnɔ Yakob pete nɔ, apɔtɛ ɔgba mɛ baazã, gɛ balɛ tsyɛ enu kaŋãsi alɛ gɛ batɔɖɔ elu alɛ elete.

english_verse: Abraham did this, because he was waiting for the eternal city God had planned and built.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ ezeɖi idzo mɛ akɔ ɔma hohoe toli gɛ ɔlɛ tidzudzu esi kpɛkpɛ ɛklagbueleteɛ, nɛ̀ agɛ atá ɔma pɛ ebhui ɔlɛ, yɛenu Owusoso Akaa.

english_verse: Even when Sarah was too old to have children, she had faith that God would do what he had promised, and she had a son.
translation_verse: Nɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛɛ, gɛ Abraham edzì anyɛ sheshè, kɛlɛ Sara tsyɛ edzì anɔ gɛ bɔmɔ gɛzã yɛishu tsyɛ elo, ebedzi banɔ gelee akaa, ɔmɔsɛ ayɛkɛ Owusoso Akaa gɛ atɔɖɔ tɛtɔtɔɖɔ ɛlɛ akɔɛ asɛ enu anunɔ gɛ àbhɛtɛ kɛgɛ lɛ ɛlɛ ɔzãkɔ nabha aŋã.

english_verse: Her husband Abraham was almost dead, but he became the ancestor of many people. In fact, there are as many of them as there are stars in the sky or grains of sand along the seashore.
translation_verse: Kliso denɔ Abraham tenikpo alɛ gɛ asɛ etsí ebeke mɛɛ, yɛeyi mɔmɔ́ alɛ ebékpè asɛ awɔ̃ɛ nɛititíɛ kɛlɛ asɛ ayisɛ nɛ aƒutɔ̃, gɛ lama tɛkaklà mɛ ana.

english_verse: Every one of those people died. But they still had faith, even though they had not received what they had been promised. They were glad just to see these things from far away, and they agreed that they were only strangers and foreigners on this earth.
translation_verse: Banɔ alɛ pete nɔ, tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã ɛlɛ zã alɛ ahɔɛ flogo pɛ bétsí. Pɔ bagɛ yɛkɛ ɔdɔ̃ gɛ batɔɖɔ elu akɔ alɛ. Bamɔ bɔlɛ ete, kɛlɛ batsyana ɔlɛ edenɔ kitsí. Badɛmɛ atɔɖɔ ɛlɛ abha asɛ befu nɛ̀ baafafala ete benu nɛ esiakpa nabha.

english_verse: When people talk this way, it is clear they are looking for a place to call their own.
translation_verse: Bànɔ nɔ̀ gɛ baɖɔ ebui alɛ nɛidiɛ, kɛkasɛ ɔsɛ baapɛ esiakpa gɛenu balɛ ahɔɛ ayɛ.

english_verse: If they had been talking about the land where they had once lived, they could have gone back at any time.
translation_verse: Pɛ otɛ ɔsɛ baza baka esiakpa mɛ gɛ bedeɛ, tsyɛ alo batɛ mɔ ɛdzo etsyina avɛ.

english_verse: But they were looking forward to a better home in heaven. This is why God wasn't ashamed for them to call him their God. He even built a city for them.
translation_verse: Nɛ kɛlɛ nɛinye mɛɛ, baapɛ balɛ ahɔɛ esiakpa gɛ apɛ adɛ gɛ kenu itití ayɛ. Kliso kiwũi tiyi Owusoso Akaa asɛ bezekului asɛ balɛ Owusoso Akaa, ɔmɔsɛ ebhui ɔma hohoe toli elu akɔ alɛ.

english_verse: Abraham had been promised that Isaac, his only son, would continue his family. But when Abraham was tested, he had faith and was willing to sacrifice Isaac,
translation_verse: gɛ Owusoso Akaa aɖɔ etseɖe yɛasɛ, “Denɔ Isak mɛɛ, babàmɔ wɔɛeyi mɔmɔ́ alɛ.” tsyɛ elo

english_verse: because he was sure that God could raise people to life. This was just like getting Isaac back from death.
translation_verse: Abraham ayɛkɛ aŋã asɛ, Owusoso Akaa abalà akɔ anɔ alɔ ede bekpu mɛ, kliso kidzi ibui gbã sɛ edzinɔ Isak ayɛkɛ edzu nɛ ɛkpã.

english_verse: Isaac had faith, and he promised blessings to Jacob and Esau.
translation_verse: Nɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛɛ, Isak eyula tutu esi akɔ Yakob nɛ̀ Esau, nɛ kɛgɛ bebèdzi nɛ alɛ ɛkpã mɛ nabha.

english_verse: Later, when Jacob was about to die, he leaned on his walking stick and worshiped. Then because of his faith he blessed each of Joseph's sons.
translation_verse: Nɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛɛ, flogo tɛ Yakob adã olugbũɔ, eyula tutu esi akɔ Yosef yɛeyi alɛ, kɛlɛ gɛ edzyui paŋãa nɛ yɛosi vuvũ abhaa, akpɛ ɛtakpu esi akɔ Owusoso Akaa.

english_verse: And right before Joseph died, he had faith that God would lead the people of Israel out of Egypt. So he told them to take his bones with them.
translation_verse: Nɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛɛ, Yosef gɛ yɛitsíbe naa, aɖɔ ibui elu nɛ Israeleyiɛ tɛshɛshɛ nɛ Egipte shu, kɛlɛ ashɔrɔ ebui elu nɛ yɛaxɔa shu.

english_verse: Because Moses' parents had faith, they kept him hidden until he was three months old. They saw that he was a beautiful child, and they were not afraid to disobey the king's orders.
translation_verse: Nɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛɛ, Mose yɛakaa nɛ̀ yɛelĩ bekpũi nɛ bewoele tatá mɛ, nɛ kibeɛ gɛ bamɔɛ, edzi eyi gbangbã teli idi, kɛlɛ begisí ifu nɛ Farao yɛɔsha kpɛkpɛɛ shu.

english_verse: Then after Moses grew up, his faith made him refuse to be called the king's grandson.
translation_verse: Tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã kɔ ɔsɛ gɛ Mose esheɛ, agaadɛmɛ asɛ Farao yɛeshui yɛenu.

english_verse: He chose to be mistreated with God's people instead of having the good time that sin could bring for a little while.
translation_verse: Pɔ adɛmɛ asɛ tɛ amɔ bɔtɔ nɛ̀ Owusoso Akaa yɛeyi alɛ ɔdɛ ɔsɛ tɛ aŋã kanɔ mɛ ɛkpã dzyɛdzyɛ gɛ lɛtɛ bazã tihɔɔ nɔ.

english_verse: Moses knew that the treasures of Egypt were not as wonderful as what he would receive from suffering for the Messiah, and he looked forward to his reward.
translation_verse: Adɛmɛ asɛ tɛ aŋã kiwuĩ nɛ Kristo ɛtakpu, gɛ kɛyɛkɛ edzi ɔdɛ ehoeyi udɔ̃ gɛ abamɔ edenɔ Egipte, ɔmɔsɛ elu awɔ̃ nɛ kɛbhɛ gɛ bababuia akɔɛ idegbaa nabha.

english_verse: Because of his faith, Moses left Egypt. Moses had seen the invisible God and wasn't afraid of the king's anger.
translation_verse: Nɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛɛ, ashɛ nɛ Egipte, kɛlɛ ɔmɔpɔɛ nɔ gɛ ekusiɛ akpɛɛ logiku yɛifu, pɔ akpɛ emi avɛ ɛlɛ abha, ɔmɔsɛ azamɔ anunɔ gɛ alɛ tikpukpũ mɛɛ.

english_verse: His faith also made him celebrate Passover. He sprinkled the blood of animals on the doorposts, so that the first-born sons of the people of Israel would not be killed by the destroying angel.
translation_verse: Nɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛɛ, Mose akpɛ Tɛdɔdɔfala nɛ̀ dzyosu hɔɛhɔ̃ɛ ɔkɔɔ, sɛ tɛ anunɔ gɛ eyí beyikusiɛ nɛ Egipteɛ, etibeyí Israelanɛ xɔnɔ ayɛ.

english_verse: Because of their faith, the people walked through the Red Sea on dry land. But when the Egyptians tried to do it, they were drowned.
translation_verse: Tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛ kide sɛ Israelanɛ balà agãfala nɛ Aƒu Dzadzã mɛ, ɔmɔ asɛ kesiakpa kpakpa abha ana, pɔ gɛ Egipteanɛ tsyɛ apɛ asɛ tɛ batsakɛ anaa, bulĩɛ vũ alɛ.

english_verse: God's people had faith, and when they had walked around the city of Jericho for seven days, its walls fell down.
translation_verse: Nɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛɛ, Yeriko bɔkã egiliɛ atsaɖà ebu alɛ, gɛ Israelanɛ baagã akana ɔma nɛ ewui tegegene mɛ.

english_verse: Rahab had been a prostitute, but she had faith and welcomed the spies. So she wasn't killed with the people who disobeyed.
translation_verse: Nɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛ kɛkɔ sɛ kedulũazazã Raxab egitsí aplɛnɔ batɔ̃ gagalĩelete, ɔmɔsɛ ayɛkɛ Israelanɛ gɛ bakpɛ asɛ bedilu awɔ̃ eɖi udɔ̃ abaɖɔɔ.

english_verse: What else can I say? There isn't enough time to tell about Gideon, Barak, Samson, Jephthah, David, Samuel, and the prophets.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ kiɖuwoe lu mɛzɛ baɖɔ plɛnɔ? Kibeɛ mà mɛahɔɛ gɛ ɛbalɔkɔ ɖɔaɖɔ nɛ Gideon, Barak, Simson, Yefta, David, Samuel nɛ̀ ebuiɖɔluanɛ shu,

english_verse: Their faith helped them conquer kingdoms, and because they did right, God made promises to them. They closed the jaws of lions
translation_verse: kɛlɛ denɔ balɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛɛ, beté ɛmadi, bekulu bubhu, kɛlɛ bayɛkɛ kɛgɛ batɔɖɔ elu akɔ alɛ, kɛlɛ teli xɔnɔ badzyata ilugbũ bazanyana amɔnɔ̃,

english_verse: and put out raging fires and escaped from the swords of their enemies. Although they were weak, they were given the strength and power to chase foreign armies away.
translation_verse: beteli elĩ efuɔ gɛ azatã nɛ̀ ɔɖɛklɛmɛ nɔ, baabɛtɛ̀ ilugbũ tɛbha ŋaŋã gɛité alɛ, kɔ ɔsɛ ohumi ozoku bagɛ bebotsoɔ mɛ, pɛ bazawɔa bunye nɛ buyu ɔtakɔ pɛ beté bagɛ alɔkɔ ɔyu nɛ balɛishuɔ.

english_verse: Some women received their loved ones back from death. Many of these people were tortured, but they refused to be released. They were sure they would get a better reward when the dead are raised to life.
translation_verse: Banɔ teli anyɛmɛ beyi teli gɛ etsí flogo nɔ, pɛ bedzini alɛ akpɛ ahɔɛ akɔ balɛ elĩ alɛ. Bawɔa ɔfɔ̃ nɛ beteli shu, pɔ bagɛkɔ ɛtakpu asɛ tɛ bashɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ balɛishu, sɛ tɛ babamɔ tɛlɔlɔ gbangbã odenɔ bekpu mɛ.

english_verse: Others were made fun of and beaten with whips, and some were chained in jail.
translation_verse: Bebu olugbũ mɛ nɛ beteli shu pɛ behusi teli, banyã beteli pɔ nɛ̀ ɛɖahui pɛ bakpɛ alɛ nɛ ɛɖavũ mɛ.

english_verse: Still others were stoned to death or sawed in two or killed with swords. Some had nothing but sheep skins or goat skins to wear. They were poor, mistreated, and tortured.
translation_verse: Behu teli aklagbu kɛlɛ bayɛ teli mɛ akpa bɔwɔ̃pɛ tɔɔbha mɛ nɛ̀ saa, teli xɔnɔ beté alɛ eyí nɛ̀ babɛtɛ̀. Beteli pɔɔ, ibhoshi nɛ̀ bepidzyapi mɛ bazazã, bazafala akpakpa nɛ̀ bɔbhɛ, bazatɔnɔ̃ alɛ ede pɛ bazawɔa ɔfɔ̃ nɛ alɛishu,

english_verse: The world did not deserve these good people, who had to wander in deserts and on mountains and had to live in caves and holes in the ground.
translation_verse: kanɔ alɛ mɛ etinu ɔgbangbã ɔkɔ kɔ alɛ. Bazanyɔ̃ afala nɛ ixui nɛ̀ betu abha, bazafala nɛ ɛxɔɛishi nɛ̀ izĩ mɛ.

english_verse: All of them pleased God because of their faith! But still they died without being given what had been promised.
translation_verse: Banɔ alɛ nyɔ̃ɔ nɔ, tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã dalɛ balɛishu, pɔ balɛ mɛ anɔ nanà agɛ yɛkɛ kɛgɛ Owusoso Akaa atɔɖɔ eluɔ.

english_verse: This was because God had something better in store for us. And he did not want them to reach the goal of their faith without us.
translation_verse: Owusoso Akaa atɔɖɔ ibui gbangbã tili elu akɔ ɔlɔ, kɛlɛ kɛgɛ apɛɛ nɔ kɛli nu ɔsɛ, nɛ tenikpo bhɛbhɛtɛ mɛɛ, abakɔ kɛka nyia ka ba ɛlɛ mɛ, nɛ alɛ tɛtɔtɔɖɔ ulũ.

english_verse: Such a large crowd of witnesses is all around us! So we must get rid of everything that slows us down, especially the sin that just won't let go. And we must be determined to run the race that is ahead of us.
translation_verse: Gɛ bagazã pɛ amɔ ebuiɛ alɛ asɛ ɔmɔlɔ̃ dodo toli gɛ ɔkpɛ ɔkã kana ɔlɔ soɔ, nɔkɔ bubú kɛka nyia ka, nɛ̀ ebui kpa gɛ làkɔ bɔdɔ̃ oboeɛ lu, tɛ bubú tɛtsɛɖɛ mɛ bubuɔ gɛlɛ ɔlɔ otugbàa nɛ̀ emi.

english_verse: We must keep our eyes on Jesus, who leads us and makes our faith complete. He endured the shame of being nailed to a cross, because he knew later on he would be glad he did. Now he is seated at the right side of God's throne!
translation_verse: Nɔkɔ ɔlɔ awuhũ àzã Yesu abha, yɛ gɛ akpɛ ɛlɛ esi pɛ ewoese ɔlɔ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋãa, kɛlɛ gɛ enyi same nɔnɔ́ gɛ alɛ akɔɛ idegba soɔ, akpɛ emi nɛ butsí osiɛ abha zazã bɔtɔ mɛ, kɛlɛ butsí osi abha zazã gɛiku yɛiwũi nanà. Otsi alɛ ezhi azã Owusoso Akaa bukusiegbu nɛŋã mɛ.

english_verse: So keep your mind on Jesus, who put up with many insults from sinners. Then you won't get discouraged and give up.
translation_verse: Noɖi bɔtɔ gɛ anɔ alɛ akpɛ emi amɔ edenɔ banɔ kpa gɛ alɔɛtití nɛishuɔ ahɔɛ mɛɛ, sɛ tɛ anɔ nyia anɔ akpɛ emi tɛ yɛitú titsyina ɔtɔ.

english_verse: None of you have yet been hurt in your battle against sin.
translation_verse: Gɛ nɔgɔ̃lɛ nɛ̀ ebui kpa tsyɛ elo, kɛkà ba ɛlɛ mɛ sɛ tɛ bàbalɛ wɔnɔ dzyosu esi xɔnɔ.

english_verse: But you have forgotten that the Scriptures say to God's children, “When the Lord punishes you, don't make light of it, and when he corrects you, don't be discouraged.
translation_verse: Pɔ nɔ dzɛkɛ kemi kpɛkpɛ anɔ ibuiɛ gɛ balɔkɔ ekulu wɔnɔ nɔ̀ asɛ mɛeyianyɛ alɛ mɔɔ dza? Aɖɔ asɛ, “Mɛeyi, tɛɔlɔkɔ Apaleteɛ yɛatɔ̃ pɔpɔ ɔmɔ asɛ bɔwufu ibui kɛlɛ onupɛ etseɖe ni wɔɔ, tɛkɔ kɛpɔɛ wɔ,

english_verse: The Lord corrects the people he loves and disciplines those he calls his own.”
translation_verse: ɔmɔsɛ anunɔ gɛ Apaleteɛ adɛmɛ nɔɔ yɛ yɛapɔ batɔ̃ akɔ, kɛlɛ apɔ batɔ̃ akɔ eyi nɔnɔ́ gɛ ayɛkɛ asɛ eyiɛ.”

english_verse: Be patient when you are being corrected! This is how God treats his children. Don't all parents correct their children?
translation_verse: Nɔkpɛ emi nɛ wɔnɔ ɔtɔ gɛlɛ asɛ batɔ̃ pɔpɔ̃ ana mɛ, tɛ nɔyɛkɛ ɛlɛ ɔmɔ asɛ Owusoso Akaa yɛapɔ atɔ̃ akɔ yɛeyi alɛ ana. Pɔ eyi ɖuwoe lu gɛ alɛ pɛ yɛakaa atɛpɔ yɛatɔ̃ mɔɔ dza?

english_verse: God corrects all his children, and if he doesn't correct you, then you don't really belong to him.
translation_verse: Onupɛ bagɛpɔ wɔatɔ̃ , kɛlɛ notinu bɔpã mɛeyi, ɔma mɛ eyi nonu.

english_verse: Our earthly fathers correct us, and we still respect them. Isn't it even better to be given true life by letting our spiritual Father correct us?
translation_verse: Kɛgɛ zɛdɛɛ nu ɔsɛ bakaa alɛ ɔlɔ nyɔ̃ɔ ahɔɛ nɛ kanɔ nɔ̀ mɛ gɛ bapɔ atɔ̃ akɔ ɔlɔ, kɛlɛ bɔkpɛ alɛ inye nɛitití nɛ ɛlɛ takpu. Kiɖuwoe gɛkɔ ɔsɛ bɔtaa zhinɔ ishu esi kpɛ bɔlɔ ɛfɔfuĩa mɛ Akaa niesi pɛ bɔbazã nɛ ɛkpãa dza?

english_verse: Our human fathers correct us for a short time, and they do it as they think best. But God corrects us for our own good, because he wants us to be holy, as he is.
translation_verse: Nɛ kibe gulũi tili mɛɛ, bɔlɔ akaa alɛ akɔ ɔlɔ atɔ̃ pɔpɔ nɛ kɛpɛ gɛ bapɛ ulũ. Owusoso Akaa xɔnɔ àpɔ ɔlɔ atɔ̃ asɛ tɛ bɔpɛ, tɛ bɔbazã yɛtɛdadalɛ mɛ.

english_verse: It is never fun to be corrected. In fact, at the time it is always painful. But if we learn to obey by being corrected, we will do right and live at peace.
translation_verse: Same atɛzã batɔ̃ ɛpɔbe nanà mɛ, bɔtɔ ɛbe kinu. Nɛ kɛlɛ idegba pɔɔ, kɛmana tɛzazã piapii nɛ̀ ishu yɔyɔ ɔkɔ bagɛ bazã tɛgbagbana niesiɛ.

english_verse: Now stand up straight! Stop your knees from shaking
translation_verse: Kliso nɔtɛkɔ wɔnɔ ɛhɔɛ tsí, kɛlɛ wɔnɔ ekluisi nɔ tsyɛ eti botsò.

english_verse: and walk a straight path. Then lame people will be healed, instead of getting worse. Warning against Turning from God
translation_verse: “Nowoese edzoɔ tɛ ladɔ akɔ wɔnɔ ɛtsɛɖɛkpɔ,” sɛ tɛ banɔ gɛ agbã atɛgbã atɔɖɔ, pɔ tɛ bamɔ ishu mɛ gagalĩ. Tɛshɛ Nɛ̀ Ahɔɛ Nɛ Owusoso Akaa Shu

english_verse: Try to live at peace with everyone! Live a clean life. If you don't, you will never see the Lord.
translation_verse: Nɔgã idzo ka nyia ka abha tɛ nɔzã nɛ ishu yɔyɔ mɛ nɛ̀ anɔ ka nyia ka tɛ wɔnɔ shu dalɛ, ɔmɔsɛ onu anɛ tɛdadalɛ mɛɛ, anɔ nanà atɛ balà amɔ Apaleteɛ.

english_verse: Make sure no one misses out on God's wonderful kindness. Don't let anyone become bitter and cause trouble for the rest of you.
translation_verse: Nɔnyana ishu piapii sɛ tɛ noti tsodzi nɛ Owusoso Akaa tibhui bhuibuɔ shu. Nɛ kidzo ɛlɛ nabhaa anɔ nanà atɛzã asɛ tɛwɔa kɛtɛtɛ́ gɛ tishè pɛ tɛlɔkɔ ɛlɛ abɔɛ zĩ udɔ̃ gelee nɔ ana.

english_verse: Watch out for immoral and ungodly people like Esau, who sold his future blessing for only one meal.
translation_verse: Nɔnyana ishu piapii sɛ tɛ anɔ nanà etidzi ɔmamɛadɛdɛ elete alo anɔ gɛ akaa ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ ma yɛmɛ asɛ Esau ana, gɛ atsrakɛ yɛeyianyɛ ukusi ɔzãkɔ nɛ̀ kɛŋaŋã esugba tienikpo nɔ ana.

english_verse: You know how he later wanted it back. But there was nothing he could do to change things, even though he begged his father and cried.
translation_verse: Kidegba gɛ azapɛ tiyuyula asɛ eyianyɛ ekusi anaa, bagɛ kɔɛ. Gɛ alɔkɔ yoyo bibi tsyɛ elo, anɔ nanà agalà etsyina kibuiɛ gɛ atɔɖɔ eluɔ.

english_verse: You have not come to a place like Mount Sinai that can be seen and touched. There is no flaming fire or dark cloud or storm
translation_verse: Anɛ butuɔ gɛ nɔtɛ bɔlà ohunɔ shu nɔba, nɛ̀ butuɔ gɛ sɔlɛ ɛtã, otumũ kuku, kawlagoe alo fluflũ mɛ nɛ̀ kɛlɛ́ gagalĩ fufù,

english_verse: or trumpet sound. The people of Israel heard a voice speak. But they begged it to stop,
translation_verse: ɛkɛlɛ gɛ babɔ exoele nɛ̀ ozĩ kpɛkpɛ toli idi, kɛlɛ bagɛ elũɔ akɔ ɛtakpu asɛ batɛ aɖɔ ibui nanà etseɖe alɛ,

english_verse: because they could not obey its commands. They were even told to kill any animal that touched the mountain.
translation_verse: ɔmɔsɛ batɛ akalà azã ɔsha gɛ bakpɛ akɔ alɛ niesi. “Agã yɛete yɛehunɔ butuɔ tsyɛ elo, kɛlɛ sɛ tɛ behui aklagbu eyí.”

english_verse: The sight was so frightening that Moses said he shook with fear.
translation_verse: Kɛgɛ Mose amɔɔ ku yɛifu, so aɖɔ asɛ, “Ishuku nɛ̀ kifusisi.”

english_verse: You have now come to Mount Zion and to the heavenly Jerusalem. This is the city of the living God, where thousands and thousands of angels have come to celebrate.
translation_verse: Otsi alɛ nɔba Zion Butuɔ, gɛinu kitití Yerusalem, Owusoso Akaa enunuɔ yɛɔma gbagbla mɛ. Noboku baxɔ̃ɛdɛ ihotu, ihotuɔ nɛ̀ balɛ same mɔmɔ̃ ɛzaa mɛ.

english_verse: Here you will find all of God's dearest children, whose names are written in heaven. And you will find God himself, who judges everyone. Here also are the spirits of those good people who have been made perfect.
translation_verse: Nɔba, wɔnɔ ɔhamɛeyi gɛ oputɔ, bagɛ alɛ enye bafɔnɔ elu nɛititíɛ. Nɔba Owusoso Akaa, gɛ yɛekulu ebhu nɛ̀ anɔ nyia anɔ, nɛ̀ banɔ gbangbã ɛfɔfuĩa nɔ gɛ babhɛtɛ piapii nɔ,

english_verse: And Jesus is here! He is the one who makes God's new agreement with us, and his sprinkled blood says much better things than the blood of Abel.
translation_verse: Yesu gɛ evũ iboe nɛ̀ olugbũ nyanya vɔvɔ, nɛ̀ dzyosu hɔɛhɔ̃ɛ gɛ aɖɔaɖɔ gbangbã ɔdɛ Abel dzyosu nɔɔ.

english_verse: Make sure you obey the one who speaks to you. The people did not escape, when they refused to obey the one who spoke to them at Mount Sinai. Do you think you can possibly escape, if you refuse to obey the one who speaks to you from heaven?
translation_verse: Nolu awɔ̃ tɛ notitso dzi nɛibuiɛ gɛ anunɔ aɖɔ niede. Onupɛ bagɛlà ebu tɛtsɛɖɛ mɛ ashɛ nɛ anunɔ gɛ etseɖe nɔ nɛ̀ alɛ nɛ esiakpa alɛ nabhaa, kɛlɛ bɔlɔ pɔ balà bu tɛtsɛɖɛ mɛ onupɛ butso dzi nɛ anunɔ gɛ etseɖe nɔ ɔlɔ nɛ kititíɛ ɔbha?

english_verse: When God spoke the first time, his voice shook only the earth. This time he has promised to shake the earth once again, and heaven too.
translation_verse: Nɛ kibe ɛlɛ, yɛaɖɔ ezeshuku esiakpa, pɔ otsi alɛ atɔɖɔ elu asɛ, “Anɛ esiakpa ete mɛba suku, kititíɛ tsyɛ baplɛnɔ.”

english_verse: The words “once again” mean that these created things will someday be shaken and removed. Then what cannot be shaken will last.
translation_verse: Ebui alɛ asɛ, “Ɛzɛbabhɛtɛɛ” kɛkasɛ kibu gɛ bebèbu bɔdɔ̃ gɛ balà shukuɔ atɛɛ-gɛ bunu kanɔ mɛ ɔdɔ̃-sɛ tɛ kɛgɛ batɛ balà eshukuɔ tɛ kɛwɔ ɛlɛ ɔzãkɔ.

english_verse: We should be grateful we were given a kingdom that cannot be shaken. And in this kingdom we please God by worshiping him and by showing him great honor and respect.
translation_verse: Kliso gɛ bɔyɛkɛ bukusi ŋaŋã ɛlɛ gɛ bɔtɛbalà shukuɔ, nɔkɔ buhu ahɔɛ, tɛ bɔlɔkɔ ɛtakpu esi kpɛkpɛɛ gɛshɔnɔ, nɛ̀ kifu sisíɛ ɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa,

english_verse: Our God is like a destructive fire!
translation_verse: ɔmɔsɛ bɔlɔ Owusoso Akaa, enu kifu gɛpɔɛ bɔdɔ̃.”

english_verse: Keep being concerned about each other as the Lord's followers should.
translation_verse: Nɔvɛ ɛlɛ abha tɛ nɔɔdɛmɛ nɔ nɛ bagɔlɔ.

english_verse: Be sure to welcome strangers into your home. By doing this, some people have welcomed angels as guests, without even knowing it.
translation_verse: Nɔdɛmɛ befu yɛyɛkɛ ibui, ɔmɔsɛ nɛ mati matì mɛɛ, beteli bayɛkɛ Owusoso Akaa axɔ̃ɛdɛɛ.

english_verse: Remember the Lord's people who are in jail and be concerned for them. Don't forget those who are suffering, but imagine you are there with them.
translation_verse: Nɔbaka bagɛ alɛ ɔɖavũɔ mɛ, asɛ wɔnɔ nɛ alɛ bakpɛ nɛ ɔɖavũɔ mɛ ana, kɛlɛ bagɛ bawɔa alɛ ɔnyɔnyɔ̃ nɔ, ɔmɔ asɛ wɔnɔ tsyɛ wɔnɔ mɔ ɔtɔ ɔlɛ ana.

english_verse: Have respect for marriage. Always be faithful to your partner, because God will punish anyone who is immoral or unfaithful in marriage.
translation_verse: Wɔnɔ badzɛenyine nɛ̀ banyɛedziniɛ, nɔkpɛ ɛtakpu esi ɔkɛ bagɔlɔ shu nɛ̀ wɔnɔ ɔwɔ̃sɔkɔ tsyɛ, ɔmɔsɛ Owusoso Akaa ebekulu ebhu nɛ̀ agɛ adɛ ɔma mɛ nɛ̀ bagɛ baŋã agɔlɔ.

english_verse: Don't fall in love with money. Be satisfied with what you have. The Lord has promised that he will not leave us or desert us.
translation_verse: Tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ nɛ̀ ehoeyi pɛpɛ ɔfala nɛifu tɛzã ɔnɔ mɛ, pɔ nɔkɔ kɛgɛ ɛlɛ wɔnɔ ahɔɛ ku wɔnɔ awɔ̃, ɔmɔsɛ Owusoso Akaa aɖɔ asɛ, “Ɛtɛba shɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ wɔshu alo tɛ mɛshɛ̃ yɔlɔ wɔ ɖa.”

english_verse: This should make you feel like saying, “The Lord helps me! Why should I be afraid of what people can do to me?”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ bɔɖɔ nɛ̀ awɔ̃ lulu ɔsɛ, “Apaleteɛ yɛenu anunɔ gɛ aaplɛni mɛ, kifu tɛbayi mɛ. Kiɖuwoe anɔ abalà abhɛtɛ ni mɛ?”

english_verse: Don't forget about your leaders who taught you God's message. Remember what kind of lives they lived and try to have faith like theirs.
translation_verse: Nɔbaka banɔlũazazã gaɖɔ Owusoso Akaa ibuiɛ akɔ ɔnɔɔ. Nolũ awɔ̃ oɖi balɛ ɛkpã tɛzazã mɛ tɛ nɔkpasɛ alɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã.

english_verse: Jesus Christ never changes! He is the same yesterday, today, and forever.
translation_verse: Yesu Kristo tenikpo alɛ gɛ azã ivũɔ, yɛalɛ oboɔ nɛ̀ tihɔɔ.

english_verse: Don't be fooled by any kind of strange teachings. It is better to receive strength from God's gift of undeserved grace than to depend on certain foods. After all, these foods don't really help the people who eat them.
translation_verse: Nɔtɛkɔ ɔsɛ bɔdɔ̃ kakasɛ ka nyia ka ɛdi zɛgbana ɔnɔ. Kɛpɛ ɔkɔ ɔlɔ etúɔ asɛ tɛ lamɔ ohumi ɔgana tibhui bhuibu mɛ, pɔ anɛ bɔŋaŋã ɔsha gɛ ɛlɛ beshu pɛ lɛ tɛ plɛnɔ banunɔ gɛ baŋã ɔdɔ̃.

english_verse: But we have an altar where even the priests who serve in the place of worship have no right to eat.
translation_verse: Bɔdɔ̃ pɔpɔɛ osubha toli ɔlɛ ɔlɔ ahɔɛ gɛ bagɛ bakpɛ ɛtakpu esi nɛ trɛwɔɛgba mɛɛ, bagɛ mɔ idzo aŋã ɔlɛ abha ɔdɔ̃.

english_verse: After the high priest offers the blood of animals as a sin offering, the bodies of those animals are burned outside the camp.
translation_verse: Banɔlũazazã gbagbla alɔkɔ bagã dzyosu avɛnɔ Ɔdadalɛ Ɔkɔ Ɔdɛɛ, asɛ bɔdɔ̃ kɔkɔ́ nɛ ebui kpa ɛtakpu ana, bagã pɔɔ, baapɔɛ alɛ nɛ kagba niede.

english_verse: Jesus himself suffered outside the city gate, so his blood would make people holy.
translation_verse: Nɛidzo ɛlɛ nabhaa, Yesu tsyɛ amɔ̃ bɔtɔ nɛ ɔma ɔlɛ kakãdeɛ ɔsɛ tɛ alɔkɔ yɛdzyosu nɔ adalɛ banɔ shu nɔ.

english_verse: This is why we should go outside the camp to Jesus and share in his disgrace.
translation_verse: So nɔkɔ bukoeyi vɛ Yesu yɛɔbha nɛ buvũ niede tɛ bakpɛ ɔlɔ tsyɛ awuĩ nɛidi gɛ bakpɛ yɛ.

english_verse: On this earth we don't have a city that lasts forever, but we are waiting for such a city.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ nɛ kanɔ alɛ mɛɛ, ɔma nanà ɔma gɛ ɔbɔkpɛ emi ɔzã tihɔɔ, pɔ buuɖi ɔgɛ ɔbɔba nɛidzo mɛ.

english_verse: Our sacrifice is to keep offering praise to God in the name of Jesus.
translation_verse: Kliso gana Yesu abhaa, nɔkɔ bɔzɛ lɔkɔ kitití kpɛkpɛ gɛlɛ asɛ bɔdɔ̃ pɔpɔɛ anaa zɛkɔ Owusoso Akaa- gɛenu ebuiɛ gɛ baɖɔ adalɛ yɛinyeɛ shu nɔ.

english_verse: But don't forget to help others and to share your possessions with them. This too is like offering a sacrifice that pleases God.
translation_verse: Nɔtɛ dzɛkɛ ibui gbangbã tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ nɛ̀ kɛdɔ̃ kɔkɔ banɔ bhlatɔ, ɔmɔsɛ bɔdɔ̃ pɔpɔɛ ɛlɛ nɛidi ku Owusoso Akaa awɔ̃.

english_verse: Obey your leaders and do what they say. They are watching over you, and they must answer to God. So don't make them sad as they do their work. Make them happy. Otherwise, they won't be able to help you at all.
translation_verse: Nolũ atɔ̃ ɔkɔ bagɛ alɛ̀ ɔnɔ lũɔ, tɛ nɔkpɛ ɛtakpu esi ɔkɔ alɛ gɛ balɛ eɖi wɔnɔ abha, pɛ balɛ enu banunɔ gɛ babàkɔ akɔnta nɛ wɔnɔ shuɔ. Nolũ atɔ̃ nɛ alɛ, sɛ tɛ balɛ ɛxɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa etsyina edzi same ibui, pɔ nɔtɛkpɛ bɔtɔ gɛ tɛba mana ɔnɔ ibui gbangbã nanà nɔ.

english_verse: Pray for us. Our consciences are clear, and we always try to live right.
translation_verse: Nookulu Owusoso Akaa nɛ ɔlɔ ɛtakpu. Bɔyɛkɛ ŋã sɛ bɔlɛ nɛ etú dadalɛ mɛ, kɛlɛ bɔɔpɛ sɛ tɛ bɔzã nɛ̀ ɛkpã dadalɛ nɛidzo nyiɛ idzo abha.

english_verse: I especially want you to pray that I can visit you again soon. Final Prayers and Greetings
translation_verse: Ɛkpalà ɔnɔ ɔsɛ nokulu Owusoso Akaa sɛ tɛ adalɛ idzo akɔ mɛ tɛ mɛitso dzi tsyina ba ɔnɔ ɔbha nɛibe gulũi mɛ.

english_verse: God gives peace, and he raised our Lord Jesus Christ from death. Now Jesus is like a Great Shepherd whose blood was used to make God's eternal agreement with his flock.
translation_verse: Tɛ ishu yɔyɔeleteɛ gɛenu Owusoso Akaa, anunɔ gɛ gana olugbũ nyanyã tihɔɔ dzyosu alɛ mɛ, pɛ akɔ Apalete Yesu alɔ ede bekpu mɛ, gɛ yɛenu Ibhoshi agbagbanaelete gbagblaa,

english_verse: I pray God will make you ready to obey him and that you will always be eager to do right. May Jesus help you do what pleases God. To Jesus Christ be glory forever and ever! Amen.
translation_verse: alɔkɔ ɔdɔ̃ gbangbã ka nyia ka idi akɔ ɔnɔ sɛ tɛ nɔbhɛtɛ nɛ yɛibui tɔtɔɖɔ nabha, tɛ abhɛtɛ kɛgiku yɛawɔ̃ nɛ bɔlɔ mɛ ɔgana Yesu Kristo abha. Yɛyɛ́ kɛlinu ishu dadalɛ odenɔ otsiɛ ɔvɛnɔ tihɔ, tihɔ. Amen.

english_verse: My friends, I have written only a short letter to encourage you, and I beg you to pay close attention to what I have said.
translation_verse: Mɛadzya alɛ mɔ, ɛɛkpalà ɔnɔ gagalĩi sɛ tɛ nɔyɛkɛ uluhũɔ gɛihu kɔ ɔnɔ gana eplukpa gulũi alɛ gɛ ɛfɔnɔ sɛsɛ ɔnɔ mɛ.

english_verse: By now you surely must know that our friend Timothy is out of jail. If he gets here in time, I will bring him with me when I come to visit you.
translation_verse: Ɛkɔ ɔnɔ tititì sɛ bashɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ ɔlɔ adzya Timoteo shu, kɛlɛ kibeɛ gɛ anya ebeku mɛɔbha koɔ, bɔnɛ yɛ babá tɛ ɛbaɖi unɔ.

english_verse: Please give my greetings to your leaders and to the rest of the Lord's people. His followers from Italy send you their greetings.
translation_verse: Nɔsɛnɔ bagɛ agbana ɔnɔ nɛ̀ Owusoso Akaa anɔ petee. Bagɛ bede Italia aba pete asɛnɔ ɔnɔ.

english_verse: I pray that God will be kind to all of you!
translation_verse: Owusoso Akaa tibhui bhuibuɔ zã plɛnɔ wɔnɔ petee.

english_verse: From James, a servant of God and of our Lord Jesus Christ. Greetings to the twelve tribes scattered all over the world. Faith and Wisdom
translation_verse: Keplukpa alɛ kede mɛ Yakobo, Owusoso Akaa nɛ̀ Apalete Yesu Kristo yɛaxɔ̃ɛdɛ ɔkɔ. Ɛfɔnɔ alɛ sɛsɛ egbogbo lɔfɔ nɛ̀ taabha gɛ apɔɛ afalanɔ pɛ bakpasɛ ɛmadi bhlatɔ mɛ. Nɔyɛkɛ mɛ ɔsɛsɛnɔo. Tifefekeɖi Nɛ̀ Anɔ Tɛgbagbana Kpɛ Ebui Kpa Mɛ

english_verse: My friends, be glad, even if you have a lot of trouble.
translation_verse: Mɛadzya alɛ mɔ, onupɛ tifefekeɖi ediedi aba ɔnɔ abhaa, nɔkɔ ilidzi same ibui kɔ ɔnɔ,

english_verse: You know you learn to endure by having your faith tested.
translation_verse: ɔmɔsɛ nonyi ɔsɛ gana ɔnɔ tifefekeɖi mɛɛ, wɔnɔ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã zɛiwlui mɔ emi kpɛkpɛ ɔtɔɖɔ.

english_verse: But you must learn to endure everything, so you will be completely mature and not lacking in anything.
translation_verse: Nɔkɔ wɔnɔ emi kpɛkpɛ azã wɔnɔ ɛkpã mɛ ɔdɛtɔ̃, tɛ nodzi banɔ gɛ eshe, pɛ nɔna nɛ kɛka nyia ka mɛ, gɛ nɔtɛ bɔpɛ ɛdɔ̃ nanà.

english_verse: If any of you need wisdom, you should ask God, and it will be given to you. God is generous and won't correct you for asking.
translation_verse: Onupɛ bubutì ma wɔnɔ ɔmayɛmɛ teli ahɔɛɛ, ebisi Owusoso Akaa gɛ àkɔ anɔ nyɔ̃ɔ ɔdɔ̃ nɛ̀ ɛyamɛ fãa nɔ, pɛ etiɖi ibui kpa nɛ anɔ shu flogo nɔ, kɛlɛ abalɔkɔ akɔɛ.

english_verse: But when you ask for something, you must have faith and not doubt. Anyone who doubts is like an ocean wave tossed around in a storm.
translation_verse: Pɔ onupɛ ebisiɛ, ayɛkɛ aŋã, anɛ sɛ tɛ akpɛ ɛlɛ ɔkpɛ, ɔmɔsɛ anɔ gɛ akpɛ ɔkpɛ, alɛ asɛ aƒulĩ gɛitsu guɖaguɖa gɛ kɛlɛ́ fu falanɔ ɔvɛnɔ ɛkɛlɛ odzinɔ mana ana.

english_verse: Any of God's people who are poor should be glad he thinks so highly of them.
translation_verse: Adzya nɔnɔ́ gɛ elu ishu esiɛ, tɛ efeke ishu nɛ yɛɔɖaɖama ɔkɔ shu.

english_verse: But any who are rich should be glad when God makes them humble. Rich people will disappear like wild flowers
translation_verse: Adzya gɛenu ehoeyielete pɔɔ, tɛ efeke ishu nɛ yɛesi ɔzãkɔ bhlui nɔ shu, ɔmɔsɛ abashɛ nɛ kanɔ alɛ mɛ asɛ kifutu mɛ usi efufu ana.

english_verse: scorched by the burning heat of the sun. The flowers lose their blossoms, and their beauty is destroyed. This is how the rich will disappear, as they go about their business. Trials and Temptations
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ iwiɛ tu nɛ̀ kɛlɛ tinyenye gagalĩɛ, kɛkɔ ɔsɛ busiɛ atã pɔɛ, awlɔakpa wɔwɔ tsyɛ atãpɔɛ, kɛlɛ latɛ kapɔ ɖi. Nɛidzo ɛlɛ nabhaa ehoeyieleteɛ nulũ bafala, gɛ abazã yɛaxɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa abha tsyɛ flogo.

english_verse: God will bless you, if you don't give up when your faith is being tested. He will reward you with a glorious life, just as he rewards everyone who loves him.
translation_verse: Bebè yula akɔ anyɛ nɔnɔ gɛ akpɛ emi nɛ yɛtifefekeɖi mɛ, ɔmɔsɛ alàkpɛ emiɛ avɛnɔ adɛtɔ̃ɔ, abayɛkɛ ɛkpã ɛkutuɔ gɛ Owusoso Akaa atɔɖɔ elu akɔ bagɛ adɛmɛ niyɛ.

english_verse: Don't blame God when you are tempted! God cannot be tempted by evil, and he doesn't use evil to tempt others.
translation_verse: Onupɛ befeke anɔ teli eɖiɛ, atɛɖɔ asɛ, “Owusoso Akaa yɛefeke mɛ eɖi.” Ɔmɔsɛ kibui zizĩ tɛbalà feke Owusoso Akaa eɖi, alo tɛ yɛtsyɛ etɛefeke anɔ nanà eɖi,

english_verse: We are tempted by our own desires that drag us off and trap us.
translation_verse: pɔ anɔ nyia anɔ, yɛibui zizĩɛ gɛ apɛ asɛ tɛ abhɛtɛɛ, kɛlɛ gba nii pɛ kɛwɔɛ yɛakpɛ tifefekeɖi mɛ.

english_verse: Our desires make us sin, and when sin is finished with us, it leaves us dead.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ onupɛ wɔ tɛpɛpɛ nɔ munɔ ɛya bekeɛ, kɛmɔ kibui kpa, kɛlɛ onupɛ kibui kpa shè piapii nɔ, kɛmɔ butsí.

english_verse: Don't be fooled, my dear friends.
translation_verse: Mɛadzya alɛ gɛ ɛdɛmɛ nɔ, nɔtɛkɔ anɔ nanà awɔɛ ɔnɔ.

english_verse: Every good and perfect gift comes down from the Father who created all the lights in the heavens. He is always the same and never makes dark shadows by changing.
translation_verse: Bɔdɔ̃ kɔkɔ gbangbã nɛ̀ bɔgɛ na piapii nɔ, kitití bude, nɛ kitití udɔ̃ gɛitseɛ bɔlɛ Akaa ɔkɔ, anunɔ gɛ etitsyina asɛ atɔwui gɛ tsrakɛ ɔzãkɔ ana.

english_verse: He wanted us to be his own special people, and so he sent the true message to give us new birth. Hearing and Obeying
translation_verse: Adɛmɛ ayɛkɛ ɔlɔ amɔ aganà yɛibuiɛ gɛlɛ ɛlɛ ɔzãkɔ mɛ, sɛ tɛ budzi asɛ yɛɔdɔ̃ eyuyu gɛiputɔ odenɔ yɛɔdɔ̃ bhɛbhɛtɛ nɛishi. Kibui Tilulũ Nɛ̀ Kɛlɛ Abha Tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ

english_verse: My dear friends, you should be quick to listen and slow to speak or to get angry.
translation_verse: Mɛadzya alɛ gɛ ɛdɛmɛ nɔ, nolu awɔ̃ nɛ̀ ibui ɛlɛ ɔsɛ, anɔ nyia anɔ àhɔa nɛ ibui tilulũ mɛ, tɛ adɔfɔ̃ nɛ kaɖɔ ɖɔɖɔ nɛ̀ ɔmɔpɔɛ kpɛkpɛ mɛ,

english_verse: If you are angry, you cannot do any of the good things God wants done.
translation_verse: ɔmɔsɛ ɔmɔpɔɛ ɔtɛkɔ ɔsɛ anɔ azã nɛ ɛkpã gbangbã gɛ Owusoso Akaa adɛmɛ nɔɔ.

english_verse: Obey God's message! Don't fool yourselves by just listening to it.
translation_verse: Notɛ odzi kibuiɛ elulũelete ete, tɛ nɔzɔwɔɛ ɔnɔ shu. Nɔzɔbhɛtɛ kɛgɛ ibuiɛ tseɖe ɔnɔɔ.

english_verse: If you hear the message and don't obey it, you are like people who stare at themselves in a mirror
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ anɔ nyia anɔ gɛ eelũ ibui pɛ atɛ bhɛtɛ ɛlɛ abhaa, alɛ asɛ anyɛ teli gɛ eɖi yɛamɛ nɛ ahuhɔ̃ɛ mɛ,

english_verse: and forget what they look like as soon as they leave.
translation_verse: kɛlɛ tsĩɔ ɔlɛ gɛ etsyina ashɛ koɔ, atɛ dzɛkɛ kidzi gɛ edziɛ kukuuku.

english_verse: But you must never stop looking at the perfect law that sets you free. God will bless you in everything you do, if you listen and obey, and don't just hear and forget.
translation_verse: Anunɔ pɔ gɛ elu awɔ̃ nɛ̀ ɔsha gɛɔná, pɛ lɔkɔ anɔ ishu ahɔɛ mɛ zazã, pɛ avɛ ɛlɛ abha, atɛ dzɛkɛ pɛ adɛmɛ aabhɛtɛ nɛ kɛgɛ elũ nabhaa, bebeyulà atɔɖɔ kɛka nyia ka gɛ abhɛtɛ nabha.

english_verse: If you think you are being religious, but can't control your tongue, you are fooling yourself, and your religion is useless.
translation_verse: Onupɛ anɔ teli akpɛ ɛtakpu esi akɔ Owusoso Akaa, pɔ pɛ atɛ nyana yɛinike piapii nɛ kaɖɔ ɖɔɖɔ mɛɛ, awɔɛ yɛishu kɛlɛ yɛtakpu esi kpɛkpɛɛ ma kɛnanà mɛ.

english_verse: Religion that pleases God the Father must be pure and spotless. You must help needy orphans and widows and not let this world make you evil.
translation_verse: Kɛtakpu esi kpɛkpɛ gɛ kɛli shu bhui pɛ kibo nanà ma kɛlɛishu, nɛ bɔlɔ Owusoso Akaa namɛɛ nu ɔsɛ tɛ nɔplɛnɔ batsyãnbhi nɛ̀ bavɛvɛ edzini nɛ balɛ ɔtɔ mɛ. Kɛlɛ tɛ anɔ alà yɛishu ebu nɛ kanɔ mɛ ebuiɛ gɛ letuturu anunɔɔ mɛ.

english_verse: My friends, if you have faith in our glorious Lord Jesus Christ, you won't treat some people better than others.
translation_verse: Mɛadzya alɛ mɔ, wɔnɔ asɛ banɔ gɛ ɔyɛkɛ ishu dadalɛ anɔ Apalete Yesu Kristo abha ɔŋãa, nɔotɛ oɖi anɔ amɛ.

english_verse: Suppose a rich person wearing fancy clothes and a gold ring comes to one of your meetings. And suppose a poor person dressed in worn-out clothes also comes.
translation_verse: Kɛba ɛlɛ mɛ ɔsɛ anɔ teli akpɛ sikã ɛsikpɛ pɛ adã ɛpɔtɛ gbangbã amana wɔnɔ ɔtsyɔakɔ, pɛ etsyuitsyui teli tsyɛ akpɛ ɔdɔ̃ tsatsã amana,

english_verse: You must not give the best seat to the one in fancy clothes and tell the one who is poor to stand at the side or sit on the floor.
translation_verse: onupɛ anunɔ nɛ yɛehoeyiudɔ̃ kpɛkpɛ so pɛ notseɖe yɛɔsɛ, “Zã ɔgbangbãkɔ ɔlɛ,” pɔ notseɖe etsyuitsyuiɛ xɔnɔ ɔsɛ, “Wɔ xɔnɔ lɔ itití nɛ ɛkɛlɛ,” alo “Zã esiakpa nɛ mɛotugbà, nɛ ɛkɛlɛ.”

english_verse: This is the same as saying that some people are better than others, and you would be acting like a crooked judge.
translation_verse: Nɔbhɛtɛ ana pɔ, anɛ ɔtɔ nɔkpɛ nɛ bagɔlɔ ishi, pɛ nɔlɔkɔ ekpeɖekpeɖe mɛ zizĩ okulu ebhu nɔ mɔɔ dza?

english_verse: My dear friends, pay attention. God has given a lot of faith to the poor people in this world. God has also promised them a share in his kingdom that he will give to everyone who loves him.
translation_verse: Mɛdzya alɛ gɛ ɛdɛmɛ nɔ, nɔtɛ atɔ̃ tɛ nolũ. Pɛnɔsɛ anɛ bagɛ beɖì asɛ balɛ enu betsyutsyuiɛ, balɛ Owusoso Akaa xɔnɔ ebú bedzi ehoeyielete nɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛ, kɛlɛ anɛ balɛ abaŋã itití ukusiɛ gɛ atɔɖɔ elu akɔ agɛ adɛmɛ yɛibuiɛ mɔɔ dza?

english_verse: You mistreat the poor. But isn't it the rich who boss you around and drag you off to court?
translation_verse: Wɔnɔ pɔ nɔkwɔala betsyutsyuiɛ. Pɛnɔsɛ anɛ ehoeyieleteɛ balɛ ezhini ɔnɔ ɔkpɛ esiɛ mɔɔ dza? Kɛlɛ anɛ balɛ enu banunɔ gɛ agbana ɔnɔ avɛnɔ ebhu okulukɔ mɔɔ dza?

english_verse: Aren't they the ones who make fun of your Lord?
translation_verse: Anɛ ehoeyielete alɛ balɛ ebu olugbũ mɛ nɛinye gbangbã gɛ nɔyɛkɛ odenɔ Yesu ede zazã mɛ mɔɔ dza?

english_verse: You will do all right, if you obey the most important law in the Scriptures. It is the law that commands us to love others as much as we love ourselves.
translation_verse: Onupɛ nɔnyana ukusi ɔŋãkɔ ɔsha, nɛ kibuiɛ gɛ bafɔnɔ eluɔ mɛ ɔsɛ, “Dɛmɛ nɛ wɔhagbo asɛ kɛdɛmɛ gɛ ɔdɛmɛ nɔ nɛishu anaa,” kɛlɛ nɔbhɛtɛ piapii.

english_verse: But if you treat some people better than others, you have done wrong, and the Scriptures teach that you have sinned.
translation_verse: Pɔ onupɛ noɖi anɔ amɛɛ, nɔbhɛtɛ ibui kpa, kɛlɛ nɔŋã ɛpɔ asɛ banunɔ gɛ batɛ bhɛtɛ ɔsha abha ana.

english_verse: If you obey every law except one, you are still guilty of breaking them all.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ anunɔ gɛ abhɛtɛ ɔsha pete abha, pɔ pɛ atá esi nɛ toli shuɔ, kɛlɛ ata esi nɛ ɔlɛ pete shu.

english_verse: The same God who told us to be faithful in marriage also told us not to murder. So even if you are faithful in marriage, but murder someone, you still have broken God's Law.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ anunɔ gɛ àɖɔ asɛ, “Tɛɔdɛ ɔma mɛɛ,” yɛɛ ke yɛazaɖɔ asɛ, “Tiyí anɔ.” Onupɛ ɔgɛdɛ ɔma mɛ, pɔ oyì anɔ xɔnɔɔ, kɛlɛ odzi anunɔ gɛ ɔgɛbhɛtɛ nɛ ɔsha abha.

english_verse: Speak and act like people who will be judged by the law that sets us free.
translation_verse: Nɔɖɔaɖɔ tɛ nɔbhɛtɛ ibui asɛ banunɔ gɛ babàlɔkɔ ɔsha gɛ lɔkɔ anɔ ishu mɛ ahɔɛ zazã ekulu ebhu akɔ nɔ nɔ̀,

english_verse: Do this, because on the day of judgment there will be no pity for those who have not had pity on others. But even in judgment, God is merciful! Faith and Works
translation_verse: ɔmɔsɛ babalɔkɔ ashagabui maɖimaɖi ekulu ebhu akɔ bagɛ etiɖi anɔ ashagabuiɛ. Yɛ pɔɔ, ashagabui ɖiɖi yɛenu yɛtɛtɔtɔ ɖɔ nɛ yɛebhu kukulu mɛ. Ashagabui ɖiɖi pɔ yɛaŋã abha nɛ yɛebhu kukulu mɛ. Tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã Nɛ̀ Ebui Tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ

english_verse: My friends, what good is it to say you have faith, when you don't do anything to show you really do have faith? Can this kind of faith save you?
translation_verse: Mɛadzya alɛ mɔ, kɛgbangbã ɖuwoe lu kinu, onupɛ anɔ teli aɖɔ asɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã lɛ yɛahɔɛ, pɛ ɛxɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa nanà pɔ ma kɛlɛishuɔ, kɛlɛ́ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã ɛlɛ nɛidiɛ balà bui mɔ dza?

english_verse: you shouldn't just say, “I hope all goes well for you. I hope you will be warm and have plenty to eat.” What good is it to say this, unless you do something to help?
translation_verse: onupɛ wɔnɔ mɛ anɔ teli aɖɔ etseɖe yɛasɛ, “Gavɛ nɛ ishu yɔyɔ mɛ, tɛ kɛkpɛkpɛ ku wɔ mɛ, kɛlɛ tɛ ɔŋã ɔdɔ̃ tɛ oxuiɛ,” tixuxui ɖuwoe luidi yɛabamɔ, onupɛ agɛkɔɛ tɛpɛplɛnɔ nanà nɛ yɛɔtɔ mɛ pɔɔ dza?

english_verse: Faith that doesn't lead us to do good deeds is all alone and dead!
translation_verse: Kidzo ɛlɛ nabha anɔ yɛtɛyɛyɛkɛŋã lɛ́, onupɛ kɛxɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa ma kɛlɛishuɔ, kinu kɛlɛ́ mɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã.

english_verse: Suppose someone disagrees and says, “It is possible to have faith without doing kind deeds.” I would answer, “Prove that you have faith without doing kind deeds, and I will prove that I have faith by doing them.”
translation_verse: Anɔ teli abaɖɔ asɛ, “Tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã lɛ wɔahɔɛ, kɛlɛ mɛ pɔ kɛxɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa kɛlɛ ɛlɛ mɛahɔɛ.” Ɛbabu kɛliulũ kɔɛ ɔsɛ, kasɛ mɛ wɔtɛyɛyɛkɛŋã nɛ̀ ɛxɔ̃ɛ mawɔa mawɔa, tɛ mɛ tsyɛ ɛkasɛ wɔ mɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã gana mɛxɔ̃ɛ gɛ ɛwɔaa mɛ.

english_verse: You surely believe there is only one God. That's fine. Even demons believe this, and it makes them shake with fear.
translation_verse: Nɔyɛkɛ ɔŋã ɔsɛ Owusoso Akaa tenikpo ko yɛalɛ. Gɛ kɛpɛ ana tsyɛ! Ɛfɔfuĩa zizĩɛ tsyɛ yɛkɛŋã ana-kɛlɛ ɛkɔ ɔsɛ tɛ lishuku wawawa.

english_verse: Does some stupid person want proof that faith without deeds is useless?
translation_verse: Awɔaga, ɔpɛ ɔsɛ bebu akasɛ wɔ asɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã gɛ ɛxɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa nanà ma kɛlɛishuɔ ma kɛnanà mɛɛ dza?

english_verse: Well, our ancestor Abraham pleased God by putting his son Isaac on the altar to sacrifice him.
translation_verse: Kiɖuwoe gɛkɔ ɔsɛ balɔkɔ ɔlɔ ashuĩ Abraham asɛ anɔ gbangbã yɛenu? Anɛ gɛ alɔkɔ yɛeyi Isak avɛnɔ bɔdɔ̃ pɔpɔɛ osubha nabha soɔ dza?

english_verse: Now you see how Abraham's faith and deeds worked together. He proved his faith was real by what he did.
