translation_verse: Axɔ̃ɛdɛ alɔkɔ kilifi itsikpi nɔ, ebu kifu nɛ bɔdɔ̃ pɔpɔɛ osubha mɛ akpɛ itsikpi nɔ mɛ, kɛlɛ awãnya ɛlɛ akpɛ kesiakpa nabha. Kɛlɛ subha lɛlɛ́, subha gugũ, subha ifu bubú nɛ̀ kesiakpa tishushukù ba ɛlɛ mɛ. Buxoele Tugegeneɛ Tɛbɔbɔ̀

english_verse: The seven angels now got ready to blow their trumpets.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ baxɔ̃ɛdɛ tegegeneɛ ahɔɛ gɛ buxoele tugegeneɛ zãa, babálɛ ishu asɛ tɛ babɔ buxoele nɔ.

english_verse: When the first angel blew his trumpet, hail and fire mixed with blood were thrown down on the earth. A third of the earth, a third of the trees, and a third of all green plants were burned.
translation_verse: Gɛ axɔ̃ɛdɛ gɛeputɔ abɔ̀ yɛexoele nɔ, amɔklagbu nɛ̀ kifu gɛ batsa nɛ̀ dzyosu ezhi abalɛ esiakpa nabha. Kɛlɛ kanɔ nɔ̀ mɛ awɔ̃pɛ tramiɛ atã pɔɛ, beesi nɛ awɔ̃pɛ tramiɛ nɛ̀ tɛwɔa wɔwɔ pete tsyɛ tãpɔɛ.

english_verse: When the second angel blew his trumpet, something like a great fiery mountain was thrown into the sea. A third of the sea turned to blood,
translation_verse: Axɔ̃ɛdɛ bhalà abɔ yɛexoele nɔ, kɛlɛ kɛdɔ̃ tili gɛlɛ ɔmɔ asɛ butu hohoe gɛ bɔtã ana, awãnya akpɛ aƒuɔ mɛ. Aƒuɔ yɛawɔ̃pɛ tramiɛ etsyina edzi dzyosu,

english_verse: a third of the living creatures in the sea died, and a third of the ships were destroyed.
translation_verse: bɔdɔ̃ gɛlɛ nɛ ɛkpã nɛ aƒuɔ mɛ awɔ̃pɛ tramɛ etsí, kɛlɛ kidzo ɛlɛ nabha kelĩabhahuɔ awɔ̃pɛ tramiɛ gɛzã ɛkɛlɛ lɛyɛ.

english_verse: When the third angel blew his trumpet, a great star fell from heaven. It was burning like a torch, and it fell on a third of the rivers and on a third of the springs of water.
translation_verse: Axɔ̃ɛdɛ tramɛ abɔ̀ yɛexoele nɔ, kɛlɛ kɛwɔ̃ɛ hohoe tili gɛezebu ifu asɛ ɔbía nɔ obozhi oku bulĩkekpu nɛ̀ belĩɛ gɛ bedekoeyì nɛ ɛxɔa mɛɛ, ɛlɛ ɔwɔ̃pɛ tɔɔta,

english_verse: The name of the star was Bitter, and a third of the water turned bitter. Many people died because the water was so bitter.
translation_verse: kɛwɔ̃ɛ nɛinye nu ɔsɛ, Osì Kɛtɛtɛ́. Belĩɛ ɔwɔ̃pɛ tɔɔta tsyina dzi belĩ kɛtɛtɛ́, kɛlɛ banɔ gelee etsí ɔgana belĩ alɛ titsyitsyina mɛ.

english_verse: When the fourth angel blew his trumpet, a third of the sun, a third of the moon, and a third of the stars were struck. They each lost a third of their light. So during a third of the day there was no light, and a third of the night was also without light.
translation_verse: Axɔ̃ɛdɛ lɛlɛ̃mɛ abɔ yɛexoele nɔ gɛ kalɛ ozĩɛ odihu kiwiɛ, woele nɔ nɛ̀ awɔ̃ɛ, alɛ ɔwɔ̃pɛ tramiɛ, gɛ kɛkɔ ɔsɛ balɛ ɔwɔ̃pɛ tramɛ ɛlɛ tsyina dzi otumũ. Nɛ kiwuiɛ awɔ̃pɛ tramɛ mɛɛ kiwi nyenye gɛzã, kɛlɛ elishiɛ awɔ̃pɛ tramɛ tsyɛ otumũɔ ozodo ɔtɔɖɔ.

english_verse: Then I looked and saw a lone eagle flying across the sky. It was shouting, “Trouble, trouble, trouble to everyone who lives on earth! The other three angels are now going to blow their trumpets.”
translation_verse: Gɛ ilu awɔ̃ pɛ iɖiɛ, ilũ odi toli gɛ lozopruru ɔfala nɛ kɛlɛ́ nɔ mɛ pɛ lɔzɔɖɔ gagalĩi ɔsɛ, “Kɛba gagalĩ! Kɛba gagalĩ! Kɛba gagalĩ ɔkɔ wɔnɔ esiakpa abha anɛ, nɛ buxoele nɔ gɛ baxɔ̃ɛdɛ tatá gɔgɔɛ nɔ abàbɔ nɛ idegba shu so!”

english_verse: When the fifth angel blew his trumpet, I saw a star fall from the sky to earth. It was given the key to the tunnel that leads down to the deep pit.
translation_verse: Axɔ̃ɛdɛ trimiɛ abɔ yɛexoele nɔ, kɛlɛ ɛmɔ kɛwɔ̃ɛ tili gɛ wɔlɛ de ititíɛ pɛ kɛbadɔ esiakpa nabha. Balɔkɔ ozĩ dzudzu opupuiakɔ akpɛ ahɔɛ akɔ kɛwɔ̃ɛ.

english_verse: As it opened the tunnel, smoke poured out like the smoke of a great furnace. The sun and the air turned dark because of the smoke.
translation_verse: Gɛ adalɛ ozĩ dzudzu nulũɔ, ɔnyɔ̃ dodó toli ode okoeyi ɔmɔ asɛ kifu gɛtã nɛ̀ ɔɖɛklɛmɛ ana. Ɔnyɔ̃ gɛ odekoeyì nɛ ozĩɛ mɛɛ, ɔkɔ ɔsɛ kiwiɛ nɛ̀ kɛlɛ́ nɔ mɛ ku otumũ.

english_verse: Locusts came out of the smoke and covered the earth. They were given the same power that scorpions have.
translation_verse: Ahɔaye ekoeyì ode ɔnyɔ̃ mɛ ezhi aba kesiakpa nabha, kɛlɛ bakɔ alɛ ohumi ɔmɔ asɛ esiakpa abha katabuiadzyɔ ɔyɛ ana.

english_verse: The locusts were told not to harm the grass on the earth or any plant or any tree. They were to punish only those people who did not have God's mark on their foreheads.
translation_verse: Bagɛkɔ alɛidzo asɛ tɛ bezĩ tɛwɔa nanà, bɔdɔ̃eyi alo beesi nanà nɛ esiakpa nabha, pɔ bagɛ begitũ Owusoso Akaa ɛnyeɛ nɛ alɛ utuɔ ete.

english_verse: The locusts were allowed to make them suffer for five months, but not to kill them. The suffering they caused was like the sting of a scorpion.
translation_verse: Bagɛ kɔ alɛ ohumi ɔsɛ tɛ beyí alɛ, pɔ sɛ tɛ bawɔa alɛ ɔnyɔnyɔ̃ nɛ bewoele teti mɛ ete. Kɛlɛ ɔwɔ̃nyɔnɔ gɛ bamɔɔ, ɔzã asɛ ishu gɛtã anɔ onupɛ katabuiadzyɔ atá anɔ teli ana.

english_verse: In those days people will want to die, but they will not be able to. They will hope for death, but it will escape from them.
translation_verse: Nɛ kibe ɛlɛ banɔ abaapɛ butsí asɛ tɛ betsí, pɔ batɛ bamɔ ɔlɛ, baba pɛ asɛ tɛ betsí, pɔ butsí tɛba dɛmɛ ɔkɔ alɛ.

english_verse: These locusts looked like horses ready for battle. On their heads they wore something like gold crowns, and they had human faces.
translation_verse: Ahɔaye nɔ azã ɔmɔ asɛ basɔ nɔ gɛ babálɛ alɛ àkɔ keyu tatá ana. Kɛdɔ̃ tili zã alɛ atakpuɔ ɔmɔ asɛ sikã ekusikutu bekumu ana, kɛlɛ balɛ amɛ azã ɔmɔ asɛ anɔdzyrame ayɛ ana.

english_verse: Their hair was like a woman's long hair, and their teeth were like those of a lion.
translation_verse: Balɛ tɛkã dzi ɔmɔ asɛ adzɛ tɛkã, kɛlɛ balɛ eneɛ eyi dzyata ayɛ.

english_verse: On their chests they wore armor made of iron. Their wings roared like an army of horse-drawn chariots rushing into battle.
translation_verse: Bupiɛ nɛ balɛitéɛ mɛ zã asɛ ɔɖa apɛ nɛidi gɛ buyuelete atɔɖɔ kité mɛ ana, kɛlɛ ozĩɛ gɛ oyi ode balɛ ɔsɛɛ mɛɛ, oyi basɔ gelee tɛlɛlɛ̀ ozĩ, kɛlɛ ɔmɔ asɛ apaka ihu gɛ bege avɛnɔ keyutu.

english_verse: Their tails were like a scorpion's tail with a stinger that had the power to hurt someone for five months.
translation_verse: Balɛ itseɛ nɛ̀ kiwuɔ gɛ batà anunɔ lɛ asɛ katabuiadzyɔ ɛyɛ ana, kɛlɛ balɔkɔ alɛ itseɛ gɛ ohumi ɔkpasɛ ɛlɛ mɛ awɔa ɔfɔ̃ nɛ̀ banunɔ nɛ bewoele teti mɛ.

english_verse: Their king was the angel in charge of the deep pit. In Hebrew his name was Abaddon, and in Greek it was Apollyon.
translation_verse: Ekusiɛ galɛ alɛ ahɔɛ yɛenu axɔ̃ɛdɛ galɛ ozĩ dzudzu nulũ. Nɛ Hebrianɛ uluhũɔ mɛɛ, bekului asɛ Abadon, kɛlɛ nɛ Helanɛ ɔyɛ mɛ pɔɔ, bekului asɛ Apolion.

english_verse: The first horrible thing has now happened! But wait. Two more horrible things will happen soon.
translation_verse: Bɔtɔ gagalĩɛ gɛiputɔ lũ bafala, pɔ bɔtɔ gagalĩ bhlatɔ tɔɔbha lɛ gɛzɛ babá.

english_verse: Then the sixth angel blew his trumpet. I heard a voice speak from the four corners of the gold altar that stands in the presence of God.
translation_verse: Axɔ̃ɛdɛ hlomiɛ abɔ yɛexoele nɔ, kɛlɛ ilũ ozĩ oyi odenɔ asha galɛ sikãosubha gɛ ɔlɛ Owusoso Akaa namɛɛ mɛ.

english_verse: The voice spoke to this angel and said, “Release the four angels who are tied up beside the great Euphrates River.”
translation_verse: Etseɖe axɔ̃ɛdɛ hlomiɛ gɛ evunɔ kexoele nɔ asɛ, “Boele baxɔ̃ɛdɛ talɛ̃ gɛ banyã elu nɛ Frat kelĩkekpu hohoe nɔ tɔ̃ɔ.”

english_verse: The four angels had been prepared for this very hour and day and month and year. Now they were set free to kill a third of all people.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ beboele baxɔ̃ɛdɛ talɛ̃ gɛ babálɛ elu akɔ ɔɖatatá ɔlɛ, kiwui, woele nɛ̀ kɛgɔɔ mɛ sɛ bèyí banɔdzyrame kawɔ̃pɛ tramiɛ mɛ.

english_verse: By listening, I could tell there were more than
translation_verse: Ilũ sɛ bagɛ azã basɔ nabha akɔ keyu tatá tɛkakalà mɛ zã ihotu edi ihotu akpã taabha.

english_verse: In my vision their riders wore fiery-red, dark-blue, and yellow armor on their chests. The heads of the horses looked like lions, with fire and smoke and sulfur coming out of their mouths.
translation_verse: Bebu basɔ nɔ nɛ̀ agɛ azã alɛ abha akasɛ mɛ nɛ bɔlala mɛ anii. Balɛ itelũ ɔɖa dzã hanyãa, ɛkɔ tili kpɛ blu tutũ kɛlɛ ɛkɔ tili tsyɛ linu ɔkɔkɔbɔ. Basɔ nɔ atakpuɔ edzi ɔmɔ asɛ badzyata ayɛ, kɛlɛ kifu, ɔnyɔ̃ nɛ̀ iɖuaklagbu ekoeyì odenɔ balɛ ilugbũɔ mɛ.

english_verse: One third of all people were killed by the three terrible troubles caused by the fire, the smoke, and the sulfur.
translation_verse: Balɔkɔ banɔ yiyíudɔ̃ tɔɔta ɛlɛ̀ gɛinu kifu, ɔnyɔ̃ nɛ̀ kifu aklagbu gɛ ezekoeyì edenɔ basɔ nɔ ilugbũɔ mɛɛ, eyí banɔdzyrameɛ tɛkaklà bɔwɔ̃pɛ tɔɔta nɔ.

english_verse: The horses had powerful mouths, and their tails were like poisonous snakes that bite and hurt.
translation_verse: Ilugbũ nɛ̀ itseɛ mɛ ihumi nɔ zã ɔkɔ basɔ̃nɔ. Balɛ itseɛ zã ɔmɔ asɛ beblidzya, kɛlɛ atakpu nɛidi gɛzã alɛ ahɔɛ, bazalɔkɔ akpɛ ebi nɛ banɔdzyrameɛ shu nɔ.

english_verse: The people who lived through these terrible troubles did not turn away from the idols they had made, and they did not stop worshiping demons. They kept on worshiping idols that were made of gold, silver, bronze, stone, and wood. Not one of these idols could see, hear, or walk.
translation_verse: Banunɔ gɛ agɔ̃, pɛ banɔ yiyíudɔ̃ ɔlɛ giyí alɛ tsyɛ elo, begitsyina nɛ balɛ ahɔɛ ɔdɔ̃ bhɛbhɛtɛ niede. Bagɛ yɔlɔ ɛtakpu esi ukpɛ ɔkɔ ɛfɔfuĩa kpa, nɛ̀ ibudi gɛ balɔkɔ sikã, sikã fufu, ɔɖa dzadzã, aklagbu nɛ̀ isí abhɛtɛ nɔ—ibudiɛ gɛ lɛtɛlà mɔ̃ nɛ̀ awuhũ, alo lilũ, alo lɛgã.

english_verse: No one stopped murdering or practicing witchcraft or being immoral or stealing.
translation_verse: Begitsyina etú mɛ nɛ banɔ yiyí niede, alo ihui yɛyɛkɛ ebui, ɔmamɛ dɛdɛ alo tizuzru mɛ.

english_verse: I saw another powerful angel come down from heaven. This one was covered with a cloud, and a rainbow was over his head. His face was like the sun, his legs were like columns of fire,
translation_verse: Ɛzɛmɔ axɔ̃ɛdɛ ohumielete bhlatɔ gɛ ede ititíɛ ezezhi. Balɔkɔ ɔmɔlɔ̃ akpɛkpɛ dzlolo akpɛ akɔɛ, kɛlɛ enegè eyi akanà yɛtakpuɔ, yɛamɛ mɛ̀ azã asɛ kiwi, kɛlɛ yɛtɛtsɛɖɛ zã ɔmɔ asɛ kifu gɛtã nɛ̀ ɔɖɛklɛmɛ.

english_verse: and with his hand he held a little scroll that had been unrolled. He stood there with his right foot on the sea and his left foot on the land.
translation_verse: Evunɔ eplukpa bhlui tieli gɛ kadalɛ edzi nɛ yɛahɔɛ mɛ. Edzu yɛŋã ɔtsɛɖɛ nɛ aƒu nabha, kɛlɛ kɛblɛkpɛ ɔyɛ nɛ esiakpa,

english_verse: Then he shouted with a voice that sounded like a growling lion. Thunder roared seven times.
translation_verse: kɛlɛ akpɛ ozĩ gagalĩ ɔmɔ asɛ dzyata ɔyɛ kpɛkpɛ ana. Gɛ akpɛ ozĩɛ, subha lɛlɛ̀ tegegeneɛ aɖɔaɖɔ.

english_verse: After the thunder stopped, I was about to write what it had said. But a voice from heaven shouted, “Keep it secret! Don't write these things.”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ gɛ subha lɛlɛ̀ tegegeneɛ aɖɔaɖɔɔ, ɛpɛ sɛ tɛ mɛfɔnɔ alɛ lu, pɔ ilũ kaɖɔ odenɔ ititíɛ ɔsɛ, “Dere kɛgɛ subha lɛlɛ̀ tegegeneɛ aɖɔɔ, pɔ tɛfɔnɔ kɛlɛ olu.”

english_verse: The angel I had seen standing on the sea and the land then held his right hand up toward heaven.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ axɔ̃ɛdɛ gɛ ɛmɔ yɛalɔɛtití nɛ aƒuɔ nɛ̀ kesiakpa nabhaa, ebu yɛŋã ɔhɔɛ akpɛ ititíɛ.

english_verse: He made a promise in the name of God who lives forever and who created heaven, earth, the sea, and every living creature. The angel said, “You won't have to wait any longer.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ atanà anunɔ gɛ alɛ nɛ̀ ɛkpã tihɔɔ, pɛ yɛabhɛtɛ itití nɛ̀ bɔdɔ̃ gɛlɛ ɛkɛlɛ́, nɛ̀ kesiakpa nɛ̀ alɛ mɛ ɔdɔ̃, aƒu nɛ̀ yɛmɛ ɔdɔ̃, aɖɔ asɛ, “Kɛzɛbadze kura!

english_verse: God told his secret plans to his servants the prophets, and it will all happen by the time the seventh angel sounds his trumpet.”
translation_verse: Pɔ nɛ kiwui nabha gɛ axɔ̃ɛdɛ gegenemiɛ ababɔ yɛexoele nɔɔ, kɛlɛ Owusoso Akaa ebui kpukpũɔ nyɔ̃ɔ ababá alɛ mɛ, asɛ kɛɖɔ gɛ aɖɔ etseɖe yɛaxɔ̃ɛdɛ gɛenu ebuiɖɔluanɛ.”

english_verse: Once again the voice from heaven spoke to me. It said, “Go and take the open scroll from the hand of the angel standing on the sea and the land.”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ kaɖɔ gɛ itilũ odenɔ ititíɛ bafalanɔ zɛetseɖe mɛasɛ, “Gavɛ, tɛ ɔyɛkɛ eplukpa gɛ adalɛ edzi nɛ axɔ̃ɛdɛ gɛ eli itití nɛ aƒuɔ nɛ̀ esiakpa nabha ahɔɛ.”

english_verse: When I went over to ask the angel for the little scroll, the angel said, “Take the scroll and eat it! Your stomach will turn sour, but the taste in your mouth will be sweet as honey.”
translation_verse: Kliso ɛvɛ̀ axɔ̃ɛdɛ ɔkɔ sɛ alɔkɔ eplukpa bhlui nɔ akɔ mɛ. Etseɖe mɛasɛ, “Yɛkɛ tɛ ɔtã alɛ. Kabazã nyagbãnyagba nɛ wɔya mɛ, wɔolugbũɔ mɛ pɔɔ, kabazã dzyɛdzyɛɛdzyɛ ɔmɔ asɛ betsí ana.”

english_verse: I took the little scroll from the hand of the angel and ate it. The taste was sweet as honey, but my stomach turned sour.
translation_verse: Ɛyɛkɛ keplukpa bhlui nɔ nɛ axɔ̃ɛdɛ ahɔɛ kɛlɛ ɛtã alɛ. Kamɔ ɔdzyɛ nɛ mɛolugbũɔ mɛ asɛ betsí. Pɔ gɛ ɛtã alɛ bekeɛ, kazã nyagbãnyagba nɛ mɛ ɛyamɛ.

english_verse: Then some voices said, “Keep on telling what will happen to the people of many nations, races, and languages, and also to kings.”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ etseɖe mɛasɛ, “Kɛlɛ sɛ owlui ɔdɛɖɔ ibui olu nɛ banɔ, amadi, beluhũ nɛ̀ bekusi gelee shu.”

english_verse: But don't measure the courtyard outside the temple building. Leave it out. It has been given to those people who don't know God, and they will trample all over the holy city for
translation_verse: Pɔ etseɖe mɛasɛ iti dzyidzyi kevude ɔkɔ, ɔmɔsɛ batɛ lɔkɔ ɛkɛlɛ akɔ bagɛ etinu Yudanɛ. Kɛlɛ nɛ bewoele afɛtalɛ nɛ̀ taabha mɛɛ, bebètũ ɛtsɛɖɛ nɛ ɔma dadalɛ ɔlɛ nabha.

english_verse: My two witnesses will wear sackcloth, while I let them preach for
translation_verse: Ɛbakɔ mɛibui mɛadadalɛelete taabhaa ohumi tɛ baɖɔ ebui elu nɛ ewui ohotu zɛzɛ̃, akpã taabha nɛ̀ afɛtehlo (

english_verse: These two witnesses are the two olive trees and the two lampstands that stand in the presence of the Lord who rules the earth.
translation_verse: Balɛ enu bɔbɔ̀ isí tɛɛbhaa nɛ̀ ifu isíi tɛɛbhaa gɛzã kesiakpa Apalete namɛɛ.

english_verse: Any enemy who tries to harm them will be destroyed by the fire that comes out of their mouths.
translation_verse: Onupɛ anɔ teli apɛ asɛ tɛ awɔa ɔfɔ̃ nɛ alɛɛ, kifu koeyi odenɔ alɛ ilugbũɔ mɛ pɛ kɛpɔɛ bagɛ batɛ dɛmɛ nɛ̀ alɛ. Kidzo ɛlɛ nabha anunɔ gɛ apɛ asɛ awɔa ɔfɔ̃ nɛ̀ alɛɛ ebetsì.

english_verse: They have the power to lock up the sky and to keep rain from falling while they are prophesying. And whenever they want to, they can turn water to blood and cause all kinds of terrible troubles on earth.
translation_verse: Ohumi ɔlɛ balɛ ahɔɛ, sɛ tɛ badã kititíɛ akpɛ subha dɔdɔ nɛ kibeɛ gɛ baaɖɔ ebui eluɔ, kɛlɛ nɛ ohumi ɔlɛ nulũɔ, balà bhɛtɛ bulĩ butsyina dzi dzyosu, kɛlɛ bakɔ banyã kpa ka nyia ka idi azaba esiakpa nabha, asɛ kɛpɛ gɛ bapɛ.

english_verse: After the two witnesses have finished preaching God's message, the beast that lives in the deep pit will come up and fight against them. It will win the battle and kill them.
translation_verse: Onupɛ badalɛ alɛ ebuiɛ mɛ ebekeɛ, agãhoeɛ gɛ ekoeyì ede ozĩ dzudzuɔ mɛɛ, aba bágɔ̃lɛ nɛ̀ alɛ, gɛ aŋã alɛ abha, kɛlɛ eyí alɛ.

english_verse: Their bodies will be left lying in the streets of the same great city where their Lord was nailed to a cross. And this city is spiritually like the city of Sodom or the country of Egypt.
translation_verse: Balɛ etsitsíɛ abawɔ tipɔ nabha nɛ ɔma gbagbla ɔlɛ gɛ bekulu ɔlɛ inye nɛ sahɔ̃ɛ aɖɔ mɛ asɛ, Sodom alo Egipte, nɛ̀ ɛkɛlɛ tsyɛ gɛ bakpala balɛ Apaleteɛ nɛ butsí osí shuɔ.

english_verse: For three and a half days the people of every nation, tribe, language, and race will stare at the bodies of these two witnesses and refuse to let them be buried.
translation_verse: Nɛ ewui tata nɛ̀ apɛ mɛɛ, banɔ bebède ɔkɔ nyia ɔkɔ, egbogbo, beluhũ nɛ̀ amadi ababá ebeɖi banɔ tsitsíɛ, pɔ batɛ bakɔ ɛdzo asɛ tɛ bavɔbɔ alɛ.

english_verse: Everyone on earth will celebrate and be happy. They will give gifts to each other, because of what happened to the two prophets who caused them so much trouble.
translation_verse: Kanɔ mɛ anɛ pete abamɔ same, aŋã iwui nɛ banɔ taabha alɛ utsíɛ shu, kɛlɛ bebebu alɛ same mɔmɔ akasɛ ɔgana bɔdɔ̃ kɔkɔ́ bagɔlɔ mɛ, ɔmɔsɛ ebuiɖɔluanɔ taabha alɛ awɔa ɔfɔ̃ nɛ bagɛ alɛ kanɔ nɔ̀ mɛɛ, shu fanɔ.

english_verse: But three and a half days later, God will breathe life into their bodies. They will stand up, and everyone who sees them will be terrified.
translation_verse: Pɔ nɛ ewui tata nɛ̀ apɛ niedeɛ, Owusoso Akaa akɔ yɛɔfɔfuĩa nɔ oboku alɛ mɛ, kɛlɛ bamɛ ɛkpã alɔ. Kɛlɛ kifu dɛ bagɛ amɔ kɛgɛ ba ɛlɛ mɛɛ.

english_verse: The witnesses then heard a loud voice from heaven, saying, “Come up here.” And while their enemies were watching, they were taken up to heaven in a cloud.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ ebuiɖɔluanɛ taabha belũ ozĩ gagalĩ odenɔ ititíɛ ɔsɛ, “Nowu ɔba ɛkɛlɛ.” Kɛlɛ bewu avɛ ititíɛ nɛ ɔmɔlɔ̃ mɛ, nɛ kibeɛ gɛ bagɛ atɛ adɛmɛ nɛ alɛ ezeɖi alɛ asɛsɛ.

english_verse: At that same moment there was a terrible earthquake that destroyed a tenth of the city. Seven thousand people were killed, and the rest were frightened and praised the God who rules in heaven.
translation_verse: Nɛ ɔɖa tatá ɔlɛ tututu mɛɛ, kesiakpa eshuku gagalĩ kɛlɛ ɔma ɔlɛ awɔ̃pɛ lɔfɔmiɛ ɔyɛ obho. Banɔ ihotu tigegene etsí nɛ esiakpaa tishushukùɔ mɛ. Kifu dɛ bagɛ azã nɛ ɛkpãa, kɛlɛ bakpɛ Owusoso Akaa nɛinye nɛitití.

english_verse: The second horrible thing has now happened! And the third one will be here soon. The Seventh Trumpet
translation_verse: Bɔtɔ bhalà gagalĩ ɛlɛ bafala, pɔ bɔtramɛ babà gulũi. Kexoele Gegenemiɛ Tɛbɔbɔ̀

english_verse: At the sound of the seventh trumpet, loud voices were heard in heaven. They said, “Now the kingdom of this world belongs to our Lord and to his Chosen One! And he will rule forever and ever!”
translation_verse: Axɔ̃ɛdɛ gegenemiɛ abɔ yɛexoele nɔ, kɛlɛ izĩ kpɛkpɛ gagalĩ yi nɛititíɛ ɔsɛ, “Kanɔ mɛ ukusi ŋaŋãa tsyina dzi bɔlɔ Apaleteɛ, nɛ̀ yɛ Kristo ɔyɛ, kɛlɛ yɛabaŋã ukusiɛ ovɛnɔ tihɔ, tihɔ.”

english_verse: They said, “Lord God All-Powerful, you are and you were, and we thank you. You used your great power and started ruling.
translation_verse: Pɛ bazaɖɔ asɛ, “Buhu wɔahɔɛ Apalete Owusoso Akaa Ohumielete, anunɔ gɛ alɛ pɛ atɛzã eɖi, ɔmɔsɛ ɔlɔkɔ wɔohumi gbagbla ɔkpɛ wɔkusi ŋaŋã niesi nɔ.

english_verse: When the nations got angry, you became angry too! Now the time has come for the dead to be judged. It is time for you to reward your servants the prophets and all your people who honor your name, no matter who they are. It is time to destroy everyone who has destroyed the earth.”
translation_verse: ɛmadiɛ kpɛ ɔmɔpɔɛ, kɛlɛ wɔmɔpɔɛ nɔ ɔba. Kebhu kukulu ɔkɔ banɔ tsitsí nɛibe na, kɛlɛ kɛbhɛ buiabe na ɔkɔ baxɔ̃ɛdɛ gɛ benu ebuiɖɔluanɛ nɛ̀ wɔanunɔ dadalɛ nɛ̀ bagɛ elũ atɔ̃ akɔ wɔɛinyeɛ, bunuvɔɛ nɛ̀ besheshè- kɛlɛ kibe na sɛ tɛ beyí banunɔ gɛ bezĩ esiakpa.”

english_verse: The door to God's temple in heaven was then opened, and the sacred chest could be seen inside the temple. I saw lightning and heard roars of thunder. The earth trembled and huge hailstones fell to the ground.
translation_verse: Badalɛ Owusoso Akaavũɔ nɛititíɛ, kɛlɛ nɛ yɛuvũɔ mɛɛ, bamɔ yɛolugbũ nyanyã aɖaka nɔ. Kɛba ɛlɛ mɛɔsɛ, subhaɛfu ziboele, ɛkɛlɛ ozogũ kɛlɛ subha azalɛ́ɛ, kɛlɛ kesiakpa ezeshuku pɛ amɔklagbu hohoe azadɔ.

english_verse: She was about to give birth, and she was crying because of the great pain.
translation_verse: Adzɛ emunɔ ɛya, kɛlɛ azakpɛ ozĩ gagalĩ gɛ ɛya zɛtãɛ pɛ avɛ ɔmɔkɔɔ.

english_verse: Something else appeared in the sky. It was a huge red dragon with seven heads and ten horns, and a crown on each of its seven heads.
translation_verse: Kɛdɔ̃ bhlatɔ tsyɛ zibu ishu kasɛ nɛititíɛ. Kɛtsɔpɛ dzadzã hohoe tili gɛ kɛtá atakpu tegegene pɛ kidzu asha lɔfɔɔ, kumu bekusi ekutu tegegene nɛ atakpuɔ.

english_verse: With its tail, it dragged a third of the stars from the sky and threw them down to the earth. Then the dragon turned toward the woman, because it wanted to eat her child as soon as it was born.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ otse hohoeɛ xoele awɔ̃ɛ nɛawɔ̃pɛ tramɛ afalanɔ nɛ ititíɛ atsɛ akpɛ esiakpa. Kɛtsɔpɛ lɔɛtití nɛ adzɛ gɛ azã kamɔ̃tuɔ otugbà sɛ tɛ tsĩɔ ɔlɛ gɛ amɔ̃ eyiɛ koɔ tɛ alɔkɔɛ amɛnɔ.

english_verse: The woman gave birth to a son, who would rule all nations with an iron rod. The boy was snatched away. He was taken to God and placed on his throne.
translation_verse: Adzɛ amɔ̃ eyianyɛ. Yɛenu anunɔ gɛ abalɔkɔ ɔɖaɛshi agbana ɛmadiɛ pete nɔ. Kɛlɛ babhɛ eyiɛ nɛ adzɛ ahɔɛ ewunɔ avɛnɔ Owusoso Akaa nɛ eyiɛ ekusiegbuɔ shu.

english_verse: The woman ran into the desert to a place that God had prepared for her. There she would be taken care of for
translation_verse: Adzɛ ebu tɛtsɛɖɛ mɛ avɛ oxui, nɛ ɛkɛlɛ gɛ Owusoso Akaa abálɛ elu akɔɛ, kɛlɛ beɖi yɛabha nɛ ɛkɛlɛ, nɛ ewui ohotu zɛzɛ̀, akpã taabha nɛ̀ tehlo mɛ (

english_verse: A war broke out in heaven. Michael and his angels were fighting against the dragon and its angels.
translation_verse: Bakpa keyu tá nɛititíɛ. Mixael nɛ̀ yɛaxɔ̃ɛdɛ alɔkɔ eyu nɛ kɛtsɔpɛ hohoeɛ shu, kɛlɛ kɛtsɔpɛ hohoeɛ nɛ̀ yɛaxɔ̃ɛdɛ abatsyɔa nɛ̀ alɛ ɔkɔ keyuɔ tɛtatá.

english_verse: But the dragon lost the battle. It and its angels were forced out of their places in heaven
translation_verse: Pɔ ohumi ɔgɛzã kɛtsɔpɛ nɛ̀ kɛlɛ axɔ̃ɛdɛ ahɔɛ, kliso balɛ ɔzãkɔ ɔyala nɛititíɛ.

english_verse: and were thrown down to the earth. Yes, that old snake and his angels were thrown out of heaven! That snake, who fools everyone on earth, is known as the devil and Satan.
translation_verse: Balɔkɔ kɛtsɔpɛ hohoeɛ awɔanyã akpɛ esiakpa, eblidzya alɛ gɛ bekulu asɛ Abosam alo Satana pɛ agbana kanɔ nɔ̀ pete aganà kidzo kpa abhaa, balɔkɔ yɛ nɛ̀ yɛaxɔ̃ɛdɛ awɔanyã akpɛ esiakpa.

english_verse: Then I heard a voice from heaven shout, “Our God has shown his saving power, and his kingdom has come! God's own Chosen One has shown his authority. Satan accused our people in the presence of God day and night. Now he has been thrown out!
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ ilũ ozĩ gagalĩ loyi odenɔ kititíɛ ɔsɛ, “Otsi alɛ bɔlɔ tibubu, ohumi nɛ̀ ɔlɔ Owusoso Akaa ukusi ŋaŋã ba, nɛ̀ yɛ Kristo ukusi ŋaŋã. ɔmɔsɛ anunɔ gɛ ebhui ɔlɔ adzya alɛ nulũɔ, anunɔ gɛ ebhui alɛ ulũ nɛ Owusoso Akaa na mɛ elishi nɛ̀ emuzeɛ, bawɔanyã yɛakpɛ esiakpa.

english_verse: “Our people defeated Satan because of the blood of the Lamb and the message of God. They were willing to give up their lives.
translation_verse: Baxɔ̃ɛdɛ aŋã kɛtsɔpɛ nabha ɔgana Obhoshieyiɛ dzyosu nɔ mɛ nɛ̀ yɛibui gbangbã tɛdadalɛ mɛ, bagɛ adɛmɛ nɔ nɛ̀ balɛ ɛkpã adɛ pɛ besì butsí ifu.

english_verse: “The heavens should rejoice, together with everyone who lives there. But pity the earth and the sea, because the devil was thrown down to the earth. He knows his time is short, and he is very angry.”
translation_verse: Kliso wɔnɔ ititíeleteɛ nɔmɔ same nɛ̀ wɔnɔ agɛ ɔlɛ alɛ mɛɛ! Pɔ kɛba gagalĩ ɔkɔ kesiakpa nɛ̀ aƒuɔ, ɔmɔsɛ Abosam ezhi aba ɔnɔ ɔkɔ! Ɔmɔpɔɛ nyɔ̃ɔ ɔlɔ ɔkpasɛ yɛmɛ, ɔmɔsɛ enyi asɛ yɛibe ɛlɛ gulũi.”

english_verse: When the dragon realized it had been thrown down to the earth, it tried to make trouble for the woman who had given birth to a son.
translation_verse: Gɛ kɛtsɔpɛ hohoeɛ mɔ ɔsɛ balɔkɔ yɛ atɛ akpɛ kesiakpaa soɔ, atɔ̃nɔ adzɛ gɛ amɔ eyianyɛ niede.

english_verse: But the woman was given two wings like those of a huge eagle, so she could fly into the desert. There she would escape from the snake and be taken care of for a time, two times, and half a time.
translation_verse: Balɔkɔ bɔsɛ gbagbla tɔɔbha gɛ buyi odi ɔyɛ nɛidiɛ akɔɛ asɛ tɛ alɔkɔ epruru avɛ ɛkɛlɛ gɛ babálɛ elu akɔɛ nɛ oxuiɛ, sɛ tɛ beɖi yɛabha nɛ ɛkɛlɛ nɛ agɔ tata nɛ̀ kapɛ mɛ. Kɛbakɔ ɔsɛ kɛtsɔpɛ tɛbamɔ adzɛ.

english_verse: The snake then spewed out water like a river to sweep the woman away.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ kɛtsɔpɛ hohoeɛ pí bulĩ odenɔ olugbũɔ mɛ asɛ kelĩkekpu ana sɛ tɛ bulĩɛ bhui adzɛ nulũ tɛ buvũi.

english_verse: But the earth helped her and swallowed the water that had come from the dragon's mouth.
translation_verse: Pɔ kesiakpa ebhui adzɛ ebu. Kesiakpa yaka olugbũ mɛ, pɛ kamɛnɔ̀ bulĩɛ gɛ kɛtsɔpɛ pí sɛsɛ̀.

english_verse: This made the dragon terribly angry with the woman. So it started a war against the rest of her children. They are the people who obey God and are faithful to what Jesus did and taught.
translation_verse: Kɛtsɔpɛ hohoeɛ kpɛ ɔmɔpɔɛ nyagã nɛ adzɛ shu, kɛlɛ kikoeyi vɛ sɛ dɛtá eyu nɛ̀ adzɛ yɛeyi alɛ gɔgɔ̃ɛ nɔ-gɛ abhɛtɛ nɛ Owusoso Akaa yɛɔsha nabha pɛ banyana ebui dadalɛ mɛ nɛ Yesu shu evunɔɔ.

english_verse: The dragon stood on the beach beside the sea.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ kɛtsɔpɛ hohoeɛ lɔɛtití nɛ aƒu nɔ ɔtɔ̃.

english_verse: I looked and saw a beast coming up from the sea. This one had ten horns and seven heads, and a crown was on each of its ten horns. On each of its heads were names that were an insult to God.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ agãhoeɛ alɔɛtití nɛ aƒu nɔtɔ̃. Agãhoe Edekoeyì Nɛ aƒuɔ Mɛ Kɛlɛ ɛmɔ agãhoe teli gɛ edekoeyì nɛ aƒuɔ mɛ. Edzu ashà lɔfɔ, atakpu tegegene alii kɛlɛ ekusikutu itsiɖi kɔsha ka nyia ka abha. Bafɔnɔ kibusoinye nɛ kɛtakpuɔ ka nyia ka shu.

english_verse: The beast I saw had the body of a leopard, the feet of a bear, and the mouth of a lion. The dragon handed over its own power and throne and great authority to this beast.
translation_verse: Agãhoeɛ gɛ ɛmɔ̃ eyì awɔɛkɔ, pɔ yɛtsɛɖɛ xɔ̃nɔ zã asɛ agudu ɛyɛ, kɛlɛ olugbũɔ oyi dzyata ɔyɛ. Kɛtsɔpɛ hohoeɛ shɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ yɛohumi, ekusiegbuɔ nɛ̀ bukusi ŋaŋãa shu akɔ agahoeɛ.

english_verse: One of its heads seemed to have been fatally wounded, but now it was well. Everyone on earth marveled at this beast,
translation_verse: Nɛ bɔdɔ̃ ɖiɖi ulũɔ, agãhoe alɛ atɛ nyã kɛpɔ̃ hohoe tili nɛ yɛtakpuɔ tinikpo eɖi, flogo pɛ kibe ɛlɛ babá. Kɛbhɛtɛ kanɔ mɛ anɛ pete ibi, kɛlɛ bazã agahoeɛ niede.

english_verse: and they worshiped the dragon who had given its authority to the beast. They also worshiped the beast and said, “No one is like this beast! No one can fight against it.”
translation_verse: Banɔ akpɛ ɛtakpu esi akɔ kɛtsɔpɛ, ɔmɔsɛ kɛlɔkɔ ɛlɛ ukusi ŋaŋã akɔ agãhoeɛ. Bazakpɛ ɛtakpu esi akɔ agãhoeɛ pɛ bezebisi asɛ, “Afana gɛ alɛ asɛ agãhoe alɛ dza? Afana gɛ abalà ata keyu nɛ̀ yɛ dza?

english_verse: The beast was allowed to brag and claim to be God, and for
translation_verse: Bebuidzo akɔ agãhoeɛ asɛ tɛ aɖɔ ishu fefeke nɛ̀ ebusoebui, kɛlɛ tɛ aŋã bukusi nɛ bewoele afɛtalɛ nɛ̀ taabha mɛ.

english_verse: It was allowed to fight against God's people and defeat them. It was also given authority over the people of every tribe, nation, language, and race.
translation_verse: Bakɔɛ ohumi sɛ tɛ alɔkɔ keyu nɛ banɔ dadalɛ shu, tɛ eté alɛ. Kɛlɛ ayɛkɛ bukusi ŋaŋã ohumi nɛ egbogbo, banɔ, beluhũ nɛ̀ ɛmadi ka nyia ka ahɔɛ.

english_verse: The beast was worshiped by everyone whose name wasn't written before the time of creation in the book of the Lamb who was killed.
translation_verse: Kesiakpa abha anɛ pete abakpɛ ɛtakpu esi akɔ agãhoeɛ, bagɛ alɛ enye bagɛ fɔnɔ akpɛ ɛkpã eplukpa gɛenu Obhoshieyiɛ gɛ beyiɛ ayɛ mɛ, denɔ kanɔ mɛ esi kpɛkpɛ mɛ.

english_verse: If you have ears, then listen!
translation_verse: Anunɔ gɛ batɔ̃ alɛ yɛahɔɛ, tɛ elũ.

english_verse: If you are doomed to be captured, you will be captured. If you are doomed to be killed by a sword, you will be killed by a sword. This means God's people must learn to endure and be faithful!
translation_verse: Onupɛ kɛlɛ sɛ tɛ anɔ teli azã anɔ esiɛ, anɔ esi yɛbavɛ adɛzã. Onupɛ kɛlɛ sɛ bɛtɛ̀ balɔkɔ tɛ beyí anɔ teli nɔɔ, bɛtɛ̀ babàlɔkɔ eyí nɔ. Baɖɔ kɛlɛ ana, sɛ tɛ bagɛ badalɛ balɛishuɔ tɛ baadɔ fɔ̃ɔ nɛ̀ kemi kpɛkpɛ nɛ̀ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛ. Agãhoe Ekoeyì Ede Kesiakpa Mɛ

english_verse: I now saw another beast. This one came out of the ground. It had two horns like a lamb, but spoke like a dragon.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ ɛzɛmɔ agãhoe bhlatɔ gɛ ekoeyì ede kesiakpa mɛ. Edzu asha taabha asɛ obhoshieyi, pɔ azaɖɔaɖɔ gɛ keyì kɛtsɔpɛ ayɛ.

english_verse: It worked for the beast whose fatal wound had been healed. And it used all its authority to force the earth and its people to worship that beast.
translation_verse: Awɔa ohumi nɔ gɛ ɔzã agãhoe gɛ eputɔ shu axɔ̃ɛ, nɛ yɛinye mɛ. Akɔ asɛ kesiakpa nɛ̀ kalɛ mɛ anunɔ nyɔ̃ɔ akpɛ ɛtakpu esi akɔ agãhoe gɛeputɔ gɛ kɛpɔ̃bi zã yɛishuɔ.

english_verse: It worked mighty miracles, and while people watched, it even made fire come down from the sky.
translation_verse: Agã alɛ abhɛtɛ ohumi nɛ̀ atsyindzye ebui akasɛ, gɛ alà ekulu kifu edenɔ kititíɛ ɔmana kesiakpa gɛ banɔ bazamɔ.

english_verse: This second beast fooled people on earth by working miracles for the first one. Then it talked them into making an idol in the form of the beast that did not die after being wounded by a sword.
translation_verse: Nɛ bɔdɔ̃ bubú kasɛ ɛlɛ shu soɔ bakɔɛ ohumi sɛ tɛ azawɔa axɔ̃ɛ nɛ agãhoeɛ gɛeputɔ nɛinye mɛ, kɛlɛ azawɔɛ banunɔ nɛ esiakpa nabha. Akɔ balɛ ɔsha asɛ bafatɛ osí nɛ agãhoe gɛ beté nɛ̀ bɛtɛ̀ pɛ akalɛ nɛ̀ ɛkpãa, yɛtɛzazã mɛ.

english_verse: It was allowed to put breath into the idol, so it could speak. Everyone who refused to worship the idol of the beast was put to death.
translation_verse: Bakɔɛ ohumi asɛ tɛ akɔ agãhoeɛ gɛeputɔ yɛafafatɛ ɔfɔfuĩa sɛ tɛ aɖɔaɖɔ, tɛ akɔ asɛ anunɔ gɛ agɛ dɛmɛ asɛ tɛ akpɛ ɛtakpu esi akɔ afafatɛɛ, tɛ beyìi.

english_verse: All people were forced to put a mark on their right hand or forehead. Whether they were powerful or weak, rich or poor, free people or slaves,
translation_verse: Kidzi ɔsha nyanyã ɔsɛ, anuvɔ nɛ̀ esheshè, ehoeyielete nɛ̀ etsyuitsyui, anɔ gɛ alɛ yɛishu ahɔɛ mɛ nɛ̀ anɔ dzidzì, sɛ tɛ batsa ɛná akɔɛ nɛ kɛŋã ɔhɔɛ alo nɛ ketuɔ,

english_verse: they all had to have this mark, or else they could not buy or sell anything. This mark stood for the name of the beast and for the number of its name.
translation_verse: sɛ tɛ anɔ nanà atɛmɔ ɛdzo edzi alo adɛ kɛdɔ̃ nanà, onupɛ agɛtsa ɛná gɛinu agãhoe nɛinye alo kɛlɛ tɛkakalàa mɛ.

english_verse: You need wisdom to understand the number of the beast! But if you are smart enough, you can figure this out. Its number is
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ sɛ tɛ balɔkɔ bubutì agã ibui ɛlɛ mɛ nɔ. Anunɔ gɛelũ ebui esiɛ, tɛ avɛ ekpeɖekpeɖe mɛ nɛ aná alɛ tɛkakalà shu eɖi, gɛ anɔ teli udɔ̃ kakalà ete bunu. Yɛanà tsatsà tɛkakalà mɛ lɛ akpã tehlo, afɛtehlo nɛ̀ tehlo (

english_verse: Then I heard a sound from heaven that was like a roaring flood or loud thunder or even like the music of harps.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ ilũ kaɖɔ odenɔ ititíɛ ɔmɔ asɛ bulĩ gɛɛtsɛ̀ gagalĩ, nɛ̀ asɛ subha tɛlɛlɛ̀ɛ ana. Kɛlɛ ozĩɛ gɛ izilũɔ oyi asɛ ihui asangũelete gɛ bebho balɛ asangũ ana.

english_verse: And a new song was being sung in front of God's throne and in front of the four living creatures and the elders. No one could learn that song, except the
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ bazhã ɔdzɛ̃ vɔvɔ nɛ ekusiegbuɔ, bagã talɛ nɛ̀ banɔ sheshèɛ otugbà. Anɔ nanà agɛlà akpasɛ ɔdzɛ̃ ɔdɛ banɔ ohotu ikpã zɛzɛ̃ nɛ̀ afɛtalɛ nɛ̀ talɛ ete ko, gɛ balɛ enu bagɛ bebhui ebu nɛ esiakpa nabha.

english_verse: All of these are pure virgins, and they follow the Lamb wherever he leads. They have been rescued to be presented to God and the Lamb as the most precious people on earth.
translation_verse: Balɛ enu anɔ gɛ bagɛ dɛ badzɛ̃ shu eɖi, kɛlɛ bazã ishu dadalɛ mɛ. Bazã Obhoshieyi niede avɛ ɔkɔ nyia ɔkɔ gɛ avɛɛ. Bedzi alɛ edenɔ banɔ gelee ishi, kɛlɛ balɔkɔ alɛ akɔ Owusoso Akaa nɛ̀ Obhoshieyiɛ, ɔmɔ asɛ busieyuyu gɛ bapɔ eputɔ ana.

english_verse: They never tell lies, and they are innocent. The Messages of the Three Angels
translation_verse: Asɛnɛ ɛbui agɛzã balɛ olugbũ mɛ oɖi, kɛlɛ bamɛ tsitsí ibui nanà tsyɛ ma balɛishu. Baxɔ̃ɛdɛ Tatá Teli

english_verse: I saw another angel. This one was flying across the sky and had the eternal good news to announce to the people of every race, tribe, language, and nation on earth.
translation_verse: Ɛmɔ axɔ̃ɛdɛ bhlatɔ teli gɛ ezepruru afala nɛ kɛlɛ́ nɔ mɛ gɛ evunɔ Kibui Gbangbã gɛbazã tihɔɔ sɛ tɛ adɛɖɔ etseɖe esiakpa abhanɛ kɛmadi ka nyia ka, egbogbo, beluhũ nɛ̀ banunɔ pete.

english_verse: The angel shouted, “Worship and honor God! The time has come for him to judge everyone. Kneel down before the one who created heaven and earth, the oceans, and every stream.”
translation_verse: Akpɛ ozĩ gagalĩ asɛ, “Nosì Owusoso Akaa nɛifu, tɛ nɔkɔɛ ishu dadalɛ, ɔmɔsɛ yɛebhu kukulu ibeɛ na ɛkɛlɛ́. Nɔkpɛ ɛtakpu esi ɔkɔ yɛ gɛ abhɛtɛ itití, kesiakpa, aƒu nɛ̀ belĩɛ gɛ bepí edekoeyì nɛ ɛxɔa nɛishiɛ.”

english_verse: A second angel followed and said, “The great city of Babylon has fallen! This is the city that made all nations drunk and immoral. Now God is angry, and Babylon has fallen.”
translation_verse: Axɔ̃ɛdɛ bhalà tsyɛ azaba, kɛlɛ aɖɔ asɛ, “Ayɛmɔo! Babilonia Gbagbla ayɛ mɔo. Yɛ gɛ akɔ ɛmadi bazabhɛtɛ ɔmamɛdɛdɛ ebui, ɔmɔ asɛ kibuku gɛ bunu buku anɔ ana.”

english_verse: Finally, a third angel came and shouted: Here is what will happen if you worship the beast and the idol and have the mark of the beast on your hand or forehead.
translation_verse: Axɔ̃ɛdɛ tramɛ tsyɛ azaba nɛ taabha alɛ niede kɛlɛ akpɛ ozĩ gagalĩ asɛ, “Onupɛ anɔ teli akpɛ ɛtakpu esi akɔ agãhoeɛ nɛ̀ yɛtɛzazã mɛ fafatɛɛ pɛ atsà yɛná nɛ ketúɔ alo nɛ ɔhɔɛɛ,

english_verse: You will have to drink the wine that God gives to everyone who makes him angry. You will feel his mighty anger, and you will be tortured with fire and burning sulfur, while the holy angels and the Lamb look on.
translation_verse: anɔ alɛ tsyɛ abawɔ̃ɛ Owusoso Akaa ɔmɔpɔɛ wain gɛ bakɔ akpɛ nɛ̀ ohumi pɛ keyi pɔɛ nɛ ɔmɔpɔɛ kayintse nɔ mɛ. Babàwɔa anɔ alɛ ɔnyɔnyɔ̃ nɛ kifu aklagbuɔ, nɛ baxɔ̃ɛdɛ dadalɛ nɛ̀ Obhoshieyi namɛ.

english_verse: If you worship the beast and the idol and accept the mark of its name, you will be tortured day and night. The smoke from your torture will go up forever and ever, and you will never be able to rest.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ ɔnyɔ̃ gɛ obokoeyi ode balɛ bɔtɔ ɔmɔ ɔkɔɔ, lɔbɔ vɛ abha ana tihɔ, tihɔ. Elishi nɛ̀ kemuzeɛ, timoemoele tɛbazã ɔkɔ bagɛ akpɛ ɛtakpu esi akɔ agãhoe nɛ̀ yɛtɛzazã mɛ fafatɛ alo anɔ nyia anɔ gɛ agãhoe nɛná nɛ̀ yɛinye lɛ yɛishuɔ.

english_verse: God's people must learn to endure. They must also obey his commands and have faith in Jesus.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ sɛ tɛ bagɛ balɛishu badalɛ, pɛ babhɛtɛ nɛ Owusoso Akaa yɛɔsha nabha, pɛ begi tsodzi nɛ Yesu edeɛ, tɛbadɔ fɔ̃ɔ nɛ kemi kpɛkpɛ mɛ.

english_verse: Then I heard a voice from heaven say, “Put this in writing. From now on, the Lord will bless everyone who has faith in him when they die.” The Spirit answered, “Yes, they will rest from their hard work, and they will be rewarded for what they have done.” The Earth Is Harvested
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ ilũ ozĩ loyi odenɔ ititíɛ ɔsɛ, “Fɔnɔ ɔsɛ, beyula bagɛ bazã Apaleteɛ mɛ flogo pɛ betsíɛ, denɛ otsiɛ ɔvɛnɔ.” Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ ɔɖɔ ɔsɛ, “Ee, bebèmoelɛ odenɔ balɛ axɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa mɛ, ɔmɔsɛ balɛ axɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa abazã alɛ ede avɛ.” Kesiakpa Abha Bɔdɔ̃ Pɔpɔ̃

english_verse: I looked and saw a bright cloud, and someone who seemed to be the Son of Man was sitting on the cloud. He wore a gold crown on his head and held a sharp sickle in his hand.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ ilu awɔ̃ iɖi kɛlɛ ɛmɔ ɔmɔlɔ̃ fufu toli, kɛlɛ anɔ teli azã ɔlɛ abha “asɛ anɔdzyrame yɛeyi ana” gɛ ekumu sikã ekusikutu kɛlɛ ɛhɛakpa gɛ bapɔɛ amɔ nɔ azã yɛahɔɛ.

english_verse: An angel came out of the temple and shouted, “Start cutting with your sickle! Harvest season is here, and all crops on earth are ripe.”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ axɔ̃ɛdɛ bhlatɔ tsyɛ ezekoeyì ede owusoso akaavũɔ mɛ, kɛlɛ akpɛ ozĩ agagalĩ ekulu anunɔ gɛ etsiɖi ɔmɔlɔ̃ nabha asɛ “Lɔkɔ wɔhɛakpa, tɛ ɔpɔɛ bɔdɔ̃, ɔmɔsɛ kibe na ɔkɔ bɔdɔ̃ tɛpɔpɔ̃ nɛ kanɔ nɔ̀ mɛ, ɔmɔsɛ bɔdɔ̃ ɖaɖa nɛ esiakpa nabha shè.

english_verse: The one on the cloud swung his sickle and harvested the crops.
translation_verse: Kliso anunɔ gɛ azã ɔmɔlɔ̃ nabha awɔa yɛhɛakpa shu axɔ̃ɛ akana afala nɔ nɛ esiakpa nabha, kɛlɛ apɔ̃ kesiakpa abha ɔdɔ̃ eyuyuɔ.

english_verse: Another angel with a sharp sickle then came out of the temple in heaven.
translation_verse: Axɔ̃ɛdɛ bhlatɔ ezekoeyì ede owusoso akaavũɔ mɛ nɛititíɛ kɛlɛ ɛhɛakpa ŋaŋã teli azã yɛtsyɛ ahɔɛ.

english_verse: After this, an angel with power over fire came from the altar and shouted to the angel who had the sickle. He said, “All grapes on earth are ripe! Harvest them with your sharp sickle.”
translation_verse: Axɔ̃ɛdɛ bhlatɔ gɛ bakɔɛ ohumi nɛ kifu abha tsyɛ ekoeyì ede bɔdɔ̃ pɔpɔɛ osubha shu, kɛlɛ akpɛ ozĩ gagalĩ etseɖe axɔ̃ɛdɛ gɛ evũ nɔ ɛhɛakpa asɛ, “Lɔkɔ wɔhɛakpa ŋaŋãa tɛ ɔpɔ̃ wain asa nɛ wain aya mɛ nɛ esiakpa, ɔmɔsɛ wain eshè akɔ tɛpɔpɔ̃.”

english_verse: The angel swung his sickle on earth and cut off its grapes. He threw them into a pit where they were trampled on as a sign of God's anger.
translation_verse: Axɔ̃ɛdɛ awɔ̃anya yɛhɛakpa afalanɔ nɛ kesi nabha, eshogò yɛɔdɔ̃ pɔpɔ̃ pɛ abalɛ bɔlɛ akpɛ Owusoso Akaa yɛɔmɔpɔɛ wain izitɔ mɛ.

english_verse: The pit was outside the city, and when the grapes were mashed, blood flowed out. The blood turned into a river that was about
translation_verse: Bezhi eku izitɔ mɛ pɛ betũ ɛtsɛɖɛ nɛ wain nabha nɛ ɔma nɛakãdeɛ kɛlɛ dzosu akpɛ ekoeyì ede kizĩɛ mɛ. Dzyosu nɔ mɛ edzu ɔmɔ asɛ bɔtsɛɖɛkpɔ tuti, kɛlɛ akpɛ avɛ asɛ ɛtsɛkɔ ɛkpã zɛzɛ̃ nɛ̀ afɛtasɛ.

english_verse: After this, I looked at the sky and saw something else that was strange and important. Seven angels were bringing the seven last terrible troubles. When these are ended, God will no longer be angry.
translation_verse: Ɛmɔ ibui hohoe bhlatɔ tili nɛititíɛ gɛ kɛbhɛtɛ ibi popopo. Ɛmɔ baxɔ̃ɛdɛ tegegene gɛ bevunɔ utɔ ebui tegegene gɔgɔɛ nɔ, lenu bɔtɔ ebui gɔgɔɛ, ɔmɔsɛ alɛ bu Owusoso Akaa yɛɔmɔpɔɛ gɔgɔɛ nɔ ɔkasɛ.

english_verse: Then I saw something that looked like a glass sea mixed with fire, and people were standing on it. They were the ones who had defeated the beast and the idol and the number that tells the name of the beast. God had given them harps,
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ ɛmɔ aƒu teli idi gɛ alɛ asɛ ahuhɔ̃ɛ ana gɛ kifu kpasɛ yɛmɛ kɛlɛ ɛmɔ bagɛ aŋã agãhoeɛ, yɛafafatɛ nɛ̀ yɛinye nabha, alɔɛtití nɛ aƒu nɔtɔ̃ nɛ̀ bagɛ aŋã yɛenye nabha. Basangũ gɛ Owusoso Akaa akɔ alɛ azã alɛ ahɔɛ

english_verse: and they were singing the song his servant Moses and the Lamb had sung. They were singing, “Lord God All-Powerful, you have done great and marvelous things. You are the ruler of all nations, and you do what is right and fair.
translation_verse: kɛlɛ bazazhã Owusoso Akaa axɔ̃ɛdɛ Mose nɛ̀ Obhoshieyiɛ ɔdzɛ asɛ, “Wɔebui bhɛbhɛtɛ egbogblo kɛlɛ labhɛtɛ ibi, Apalete Owusoso Akaa Ohumielete wɔebuiɛ alɛ àlɛ ɔzãkɔ kɛlɛ wɔedzoɔ enu ebui gbã, Wonu Ekusi galɛ odenɔ kuku ibe.

english_verse: Lord, who doesn't honor and praise your name? You alone are holy, and all nations will come and worship you, because you have shown that you judge with fairness.”
translation_verse: O Apalete, afana gɛ etibesi wɔɛfu, pɛ atɛ badalɛ wɔɛnyeɛ shu? Ɔmɔsɛ wɔ ete wonu anɔ dadalɛ. Ɛmadiɛ pete ababá abakpɛ ɛtakpu esi nɛ wɔotugbà, ɔmɔsɛ bebu wɔebui gbangbã tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ akasɛ.”

english_verse: After this, I noticed something else in heaven. The sacred tent used for a temple was open.
translation_verse: Kidegba ilu awɔ̃ ɖi kɛlɛ ɛmɔ akaavũɔ nɛititíɛ gɛ bunu Trɛwɔɛagbaa mɛ gɛ baɖɔ Kibui Dadalɛ nɛ alɛ mɛɛ, kadalɛ azã.

english_verse: And the seven angels who were bringing the terrible troubles were coming out of it. They were dressed in robes of pure white linen and wore belts made of pure gold.
translation_verse: Denɔ akaavũɔ mɛɛ, baxɔ̃ɛdɛ tegegeneɛ nɛ̀ bɔtɔ ebui tegegeneɛ ekoeyì aba. Bazã bɔdɔ̃ kpɛkpɛ gɛ bɔlɛishu dalɛ pɛ buzitsé ŋelĩŋelĩɛ mɛ, kɛlɛ batsà sikã ɛpɔtɛ ɛwɔ̃ atɔɖɔ nɛ kitéɛ mɛ.

english_verse: One of the four living creatures gave each of the seven angels a bowl made of gold. These bowls were filled with the anger of God who lives forever and ever.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ bagã talɛ ɔmayɛmɛ tenikpo alɔkɔ sikã buyintse tugegeneɛ mɛ gɛ Owusoso Akaa gɛ alɛ nɛkpã tihɔɔ tihɔɔ ɔmɔpɔɛ oyukuɔ, akɔ baxɔ̃ɛdɛ tegegeneɛ.

english_verse: The temple quickly filled with smoke from the glory and power of God. No one could enter it until the seven angels had finished pouring out the seven last troubles.
translation_verse: Ɔnyɔ̃ gɛ ode okoeyi nɛ Owusoso Akaa tɛdadalɛ ohumi nɔ mɛɛ oyuku akaavũɔ mɛ, kɛlɛ anɔ nanà aga là ɔlɛ mɛ etsoku eɖi, ɔlɔkɔ ɔvɛnɔ kibeɛ gɛ baxɔ̃ɛdɛ tegegene bɔtɔ ebui tegegeneɛ aba alɛ mɛ ebeke.

english_verse: From the temple I heard a voice shout to the seven angels, “Go and empty the seven bowls of God's anger on the earth.”
translation_verse: Izilũ ozĩ gagalĩ toli odenɔ akaavũɔ mɛ lozo otseɖe baxɔ̃ɛdɛ tegegeneɛ asɛ, “Nokoeyi ɔvɛ oditsyina Owusoso Akaa ɔmɔpɔɛ buyintse tugegeneɛ ɔbalɛ esiakpa nabha.”

english_verse: The first angel emptied his bowl on the earth. At once ugly and painful sores broke out on everyone who had the mark of the beast and worshiped the idol.
translation_verse: Axɔ̃ɛdɛ gɛ eputɔ avɛ adɛ balɛ̀ yɛɔmɔpɔɛ kayintse nɔ nɛ esiakpa nabha kɛlɛ bagɛ batsà agã yɛná pɛ bazakpɛ ɛtakpu esi akɔ yɛ tɛzazã afafatɛɛ, badɔ ebi kpa teli idi nɛ̀ apɔ̃ zizĩ.

english_verse: The second angel emptied his bowl on the sea. At once the sea turned into blood like that of a dead person, and every living thing in the sea died.
translation_verse: Axɔ̃ɛdɛ bhalà avɛ adɛ balɛ̀ yɛɔmɔpɔɛ kayintse nɔ akpɛ aƒuɔ mɛ, kɛlɛ etsyina edzi dzyosu ɔmɔ asɛ anɔ tsitsí ayɛ ana, kɛlɛ ɛkpã ɛdɔ̃ ka nyia ka nɛ aƒuɔ mɛ tsí.

english_verse: The third angel emptied his bowl into the rivers and streams. At once they turned to blood.
translation_verse: Axɔ̃ɛdɛ tramɛ abalɛ̀ yɛayintse nɔ akpɛ bulĩkekpuɔ nɛ̀ belĩɛ gɛ ekoeyì edenɔ ɛxɔɛishiɛ mɛ kɛlɛ betsyina edzi dzyosu.

english_verse: Then I heard the angel, who has power over water, say, “You have always been, and you always will be the holy God. You had the right to judge in this way.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ ilũ axɔ̃ɛdɛ gɛ ezeɖi belĩ nabha yɛaɖɔ asɛ, “Obhui ebui mɛ piapii nɛ wɔbhu kukulu ulɛ mɛ, wɔ gɛ ɔlɛ pɛ ɔtɛzã oɖi, pɛ onu Anɔ Dadalɛɛ, ɔmɔsɛ wɔebhu kukluɔ alɛ kàlɛ ɔzãkɔ,

english_verse: They poured out the blood of your people and your prophets. So you gave them blood to drink as they deserve!”
translation_verse: ɔmɔsɛ balɛ eyí wɔanɔ dadalɛ nɛ̀ ebuiɖɔluanɛ, kɛlɛ wɔkɔ alɛ dzyosu ɔsɛ tɛ bawɔ̃ɛ asɛ gɛ kɛshɔ̃ nɔ kɔ alɛ.”

english_verse: After this, I heard the altar shout, “Yes, Lord God All-Powerful, your judgments are honest and fair.”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ ilũ kaɖɔ odenɔ bɔdɔ̃ pɔpɔɛ osubha mɛ ɔsɛ, “Kɛgbã Apalete Owusoso Akaa Ohumielete, kɛgbã wɔ bubhu kukuluɔ mɛ dalɛ.”

english_verse: The fourth angel emptied his bowl on the sun, and it began to scorch people like fire.
translation_verse: Axɔ̃ɛdɛ lɛlɛ̃mɛ abalɛ̀ yɛɔmɔpɔɛ kayintse nɔ nɛ kiwi nabha, kɛlɛ kiwiɛ yɛkɛ ohumi ɔsɛ tɛ kinye pɔɛ anɔ asɛ kifu.

english_verse: Everyone was scorched by its great heat, and all of them cursed the name of God who had power over these terrible troubles. But no one turned to God and praised him.
translation_verse: Kɛkpɛkpɛ́ɛ gagalĩɛ gɛide kiwiɛ mɛ tã banunɔ, kɛlɛ bazaɖɔ ebui akpɛ Owusoso Akaa nɛinyeɛ, anɔ gɛ yɛahɔɛ ohumi ɔlɛ nɛ bɔtɔ ebui alɛ nulũɔ, pɔ bagɛ dɛmɛ etsyina pɛ bákpɛ ɛtakpu esi akɔɛ.

english_verse: The fifth angel emptied his bowl on the throne of the beast. At once darkness covered its kingdom, and its people began biting their tongues in pain.
translation_verse: Axɔ̃ɛdɛ trimiɛ abalɛ̀ yɛɔmɔpɔɛ kayintse nɔ atɔɖɔ agãhoe ekusiegbu nabha, kɛlɛ otumũ oku nɛ yɛukusiaŋãsiɛ mɛ. Banɔ azatã balɛ enike nɛ̀ ɔwɔ̃nyɔnɔ,

english_verse: And because of their painful sores, they cursed the God who rules in heaven. But still they did not stop doing evil things.
translation_verse: kɛlɛ bazaɖɔ ebui kpa akpɛ Owusoso Akaa, nɛ balɛ ɔtɔ nɛ̀ apɔ̃ gɛ bazanyãa shu, pɔ bagɛ dɛmɛ etsyina eté mɛ nɛ ebui kpa gɛ baza bhɛtɛ shu.

english_verse: The sixth angel emptied his bowl on the great Euphrates River, and it completely dried up to make a road for the kings from the east.
translation_verse: Axɔ̃ɛdɛ hlomiɛ abalɛ̀ yɛɔmɔpɔɛ kayintse nɔ akpɛ Frat Kelĩkekpu hohoeɛ mɛ, kɛlɛ kalɛ mɛ bulĩɛ pete butsì, sɛ tɛ olĩdiɛ otsyina odzi kidzo ɔkɔ bekusiɛ gɛ bede kiwiɛ otukɔɔ.

english_verse: An evil spirit that looked like a frog came out of the mouth of the dragon. One also came out of the mouth of the beast, and another out of the mouth of the false prophet.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ ɛmɔ ɛfɔfuĩa zizĩ tɛtá gɛ leyì avɔbɔ, gɛ lekoeyì ede kɛtsɔpɛ, agãhoeɛ nɛ̀ asɛnɛ elete ebuiɖɔluanunɔ ilugbũɔ mɛ.

english_verse: These evil spirits had the power to work miracles. They went to every king on earth, to bring them together for a war against God All-Powerful. But that will be the day of God's great victory.
translation_verse: Linu ɛfɔfuĩa zizĩ gɛ lɛbhɛtɛ atsyindzye ebui akasɛ, kɛlɛ likoeyi vɛ dɛgã kanɔ nɔ̀ mɛ ekusi nabha, sɛ tɛ eshogò alɛ ɔkɔ keyu tɛtatá nɛ̀ Owusoso Akaa ohumielete nɛiwui gbagbla nabha.

english_verse: Remember Christ says, “When I come, it will surprise you like a thief! But God will bless you, if you are awake and ready. Then you won't have to walk around naked and be ashamed.”
translation_verse: “Ɖio, ɛba ɔmɔ asɛ ezĩ ana! Beyula anunɔ gɛ alɛ nɛ awɔ̃, pɛ alɔkɔ yɛɔdɔ̃ kpɛkpɛ elu yɛɔbha sɛ tɛ atɛzã bɔdɔ̃ makpɛ makpɛ, sɛ tɛ etibu yɛishu elu nɛ awuĩ mɛ.”

english_verse: Those armies came together in a place that in Hebrew is called Armagedon.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ ɛfɔfuĩa nɔ gbanà bekusiɛ edi shogò nɛ ɔkɔ toli gɛ bekulu nɛ Hebrianɛ uluhũɔ mɛ asɛ, Armagedon.

english_verse: As soon as the seventh angel emptied his bowl in the air, a loud voice from the throne in the temple shouted, “It's done!”
translation_verse: Axɔ̃ɛdɛ gegenemiɛ abalɛ̀ yɛɔmɔpɔɛ kayintse nɔ akpɛ kɛlɛ́ nɔ mɛ, kɛlɛ ozĩ gagalĩ toli oyi odenɔ akaavũɔ mɛ ɔsɛ, “Kibele oo!”

english_verse: The great city of Babylon split into three parts, and the cities of other nations fell. So God made Babylon drink from the wine cup filled with his anger.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ ɔma hohoeɛ ɔyɛ ɔkpɛ bɔwɔ̃pɛ tɔɔta mɛ, kɛlɛ ɛmadi ɛlɛ ɛma hohoeɛ lɛyɛ zĩ. Owusoso Akaa abakà Babilonia ɔma Gbagbla kɛlɛ alɔkɔ yɛɔmɔpɔɛ wain kayintse nɔ gɛ keyiɛ akɔɛ nɛ yɛɔmɔpɔɛ kpɛkpɛ gagalĩɛ mɛ.

english_verse: Every island ran away, and the mountains disappeared.
translation_verse: Bulĩmɛetukpɔ ka nyia ka yala, kɛlɛ ɛgamɔ betu nanà.

english_verse: One of the seven angels who had emptied the bowls came over and said to me, “Come on! I will show you how God will punish that shameless prostitute who sits on many oceans.
translation_verse: Baxɔ̃ɛdɛ tegegene gɛ ɔmɔpɔɛ buyintse zã alɛ ahɔɛ ɔmayɛmɛ tenikpo aba pɛ ebetseɖe mɛasɛ, “Ba, ɛbakasɛ wɔ batɔ̃ pɔpɔ̃ ɔkɔ adzɛ kedulũ azazã gbagbla, gɛ ezhi azã belĩ gelee abhaa.

english_verse: Every king on earth has slept with her, and her shameless ways are like wine that has made everyone on earth drunk.”
translation_verse: Gana adzɛ alɛ abhaa, kesiakpa abha ekusiɛ abhɛtɛ ɔmamɛ dɛdɛ ebui, kɛlɛ kanɔ mɛanɛ awɔ̃ɛ, yɛɔmamɛ dɛdɛ unuɔ ebuku.”

english_verse: With the help of the Spirit, the angel took me into a desert, where I saw a woman sitting on a red beast. The beast was covered with names that were an insult to God, and it had seven heads and ten horns.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ axɔ̃ɛdɛ agbana mɛ nɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa mɛ avɛnɔ oxui toli. Ɛkɛlɛ mɛdɛ mɔ adzɛ teli gɛ ezhi azã agãhoe dzadzã teli gɛ ebuso enye edi, edi azã yɛishuɔ akana. Atakpu tegegene alɛ azã yɛishu kɛlɛ edzu asha lɔfɔ.

english_verse: The woman was dressed in purple and scarlet robes, and she wore jewelry made of gold, precious stones, and pearls. In her hand she held a gold cup filled with the filthy and nasty things she had done.
translation_verse: Adzɛ akpɛ akpɛkpɛ teli gɛ yɛɔkɔ toli okpelĩ kɛlɛ ɔkɔ toli xɔ̃nɔ ɔdzã, kɛlɛ ezetsé edenɔ sikã ɔdɔ̃ nɛ̀ aklagbu gbangbã nɛ̀ bakukɔliɛ gɛ ayɛkɛ edzi mɛ. Sikãiyintse azã yɛahɔɛ gɛ keyukù nɛ̀ atsɔ̃bui nɛ̀ ebuitsyutsyulĩɛ gɛ amɔ edenɔ yɛɔmamɛ dɛdɛ axɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa mɛ.

english_verse: On her forehead a mysterious name was written: I AM THE GREAT CITY OF BABYLON, THE MOTHER OF EVERY IMMORAL AND FILTHY THING ON EARTH.
translation_verse: Bafɔnɔ kinye ɛlɛ gɛ abhɛtɛ akɔ yɛishuɔ nɛ kɛtakpuɔ asɛ, KIBUI ƐLƐ KPŨ BABILONIA GBAGBALA BUDULŨ AZAZÃELETE ELĨƐ NƐ̀ KANƆ MƐ PETEE ATSƆ̃BUI ELETE.

english_verse: I could tell that the woman was drunk on the blood of God's people who had given their lives for Jesus. This surprising sight amazed me,
translation_verse: Ɛmɔ sɛ adzɛ ebuku ɔgana banɔ dadalɛ nɛ̀ bagɛ bazaɖɔ kaɖɔ nɛ Yesu shuɔ, balɛ dzyosu tɛwɔɛwɔɛ mɛ, banɔ alɛ gɛaza dalɛ Yesu shuɔ dzyosu nɔ. Gɛ ɛmɔ yɛ, kɛbhɛtɛ mɛibi popopo.

english_verse: and the angel said: Why are you so amazed? I will explain the mystery about this woman and about the beast she is sitting on, with its seven heads and ten horns.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ axɔ̃ɛdɛ ebisi mɛasɛ, “Kiɖeso pɛ kɛbhɛtɛ wɔɛibi? Ɛbaɖɔ kibui kpukpũɔ nɛ adzɛ alɛ nɛ̀ agãhoe nabha gɛ etsiɖi, pɛ atakpu tegegene nɛ̀ asha lɔfɔ alɛ yɛishuɔ tseɖe wɔ.

english_verse: The beast you saw is one that used to be and no longer is. It will come back from the deep pit, but only to be destroyed. Everyone on earth whose names were not written in the book of life before the time of creation will be amazed. They will see this beast that used to be and no longer is, but will be once more.
translation_verse: Agãhoeɛ gɛ ɔmɔɔ, atɛzã eɖi, otsiɛ xɔnɔ abha ma, kɛlɛ ebekoeyì ede ozĩ dzudzuɔ mɛ aba pɛ bebèyíi. Banunɔ gɛ alɛ esiakpa nabha, pɛ bagɛ fɔnɔ balɛ enye akpɛ ɛkpãelete nɛeplukpa mɛ odenɔ kibeɛ gɛ kanɔ alɛ akpɛ esiɛ, kɛbabhɛtɛ alɛ ibi, onupɛ bamɔ agãhoeɛ. Ɔmɔsɛ atɛzã eɖi kɛlɛ abha ma, pɔ azababa.

english_verse: Anyone with wisdom can figure this out. The seven heads that the woman is sitting on stand for seven hills. These heads are also seven kings.
translation_verse: “Kɛlɛ sɛ bɔlɔkɔ ekpeɖekpeɖe mɛ nɛ̀ bubutì olu awɔ̃ oɖi kibui ɛlɛ mɛ. Atakpu tegegeneɛ enu butukpɔ tugegeneɛ gɛ bɔlɛ abha adzɛ ezhi azã.

english_verse: Five of the kings are dead. One is ruling now, and the other one has not yet come. But when he does, he will rule for only a little while.
translation_verse: Balɛ tsyɛ enu bekusi tegegene. Teetì nɛ alɛ mɛ abha ma, tenikpo nɔ alɛ, pɔ tenikpo nɔ aka aba otsiɛ. Pɔ onupɛ abaa, abazã nɛ kibe gulũi mɛ ete.

english_verse: You also saw a beast that used to be and no longer is. That beast is one of the seven kings who will return as the eighth king, but only to be destroyed.
translation_verse: Agãhoeɛ gɛ atɛzã eɖi pɛ abha ma, yɛenu ekusi asɛmiɛ. Enu bekusi tegegeneɛ mɛ anɔ tenikpo gɛ etsiɖi idzo mɛ avɛ yɛutsi mɛ.

english_verse: The ten horns you saw are ten more kings, who have not yet come into power, and they will rule with the beast for only a short time.
translation_verse: “Asha lɔfɔɔ gɛ ɔmɔɔ, enu bekusi lɔfɔ gɛ bakamɔ bukusi ɔŋãkɔ kaku alɛ ahɔɛ, pɔ nɛ ɔɖa tatá tonikpo mɛɛ, babà yɛkɛ ohumi sɛ tɛ bedzi bekusi aplɛnɔ agãhoeɛ.

english_verse: They all think alike and will give their power and authority to the beast.
translation_verse: Tɛtɔtɔɖɔ tinikpo kɛlɛ lɛ̀ alɛ ahɔɛ sɛ tɛ balɔkɔ balɛ ohumi nɛ̀ bukusi ŋaŋã akɔ agãhoeɛ.

english_verse: These kings will go to war against the Lamb. But he will defeat them, because he is Lord over all lords and King over all kings. His followers are chosen and special and faithful.
translation_verse: Babà lɔkɔ eyu nɛ Obhoshieyiɛ shu, pɔ Obhoshieyiɛ abaŋã alɛ abha, ɔmɔsɛ yɛenu Apalete akɔ bapaleteɛ nɛ̀ Ekusi akɔ bekusiɛ-bagɛ babà plɛni yɛɛ, balɛ enu bagɛ ekuluɔ, bagɛ ebuɔ nɛ̀ bagɛ ayɛkɛ yɛabha aŋãa.”

english_verse: The oceans you saw the prostitute sitting on are crowds of people from all races and languages.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ axɔ̃ɛdɛ etseɖe mɛasɛ, “Belĩɛ gɛ ɔmɔ pɛ kedulũazazã eleteɛ ezhi azã balɛ abhaa, benu banɔ aza, ɛmadi nɛ̀ beluhũ edi edi.

english_verse: The ten horns and the beast will start hating the shameless woman. They will strip off her clothes and leave her naked. Then they will eat her flesh and throw the rest of her body into a fire.
translation_verse: Agãhoeɛ nɛ̀ asha lɔfɔ gɛ ɔmɔɔ, atɛ badɛmɛ nɔ̀ nɛ̀ kedulũ azazã eleteɛ nɛibui. Babà gbani yɛakpɛ tizizĩ mɛ, kɛlɛ babà shɛ ayɔlɔ yɛ kɛdɔ̃ makpɛ makpɛ nɛishu. Babàtã yɛɔdzya kɛlɛ babàpɔɛ yɛ nɛ̀ kifu.

english_verse: God is the one who made these kings all think alike and decide to give their power to the beast. And they will do this until what God has said comes true.
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ Owusoso Akaa akpɛ ɛlɛ nɛ alɛ etúɔ mɛ asɛ tɛ babhɛtɛ nɛ kɛgɛ atɔɖɔ eluɔ nabha, sɛ tɛ badɛmɛ tɛ balɔkɔ balɛ ohumi nɔ akɔ agãhoeɛ tɛ alɔkɔ aŋã ukusiɛ nɔ, ɔvɛnɔ kibeɛ gɛ Owusoso Akaa yɛibuiɛ babà ɛlɛ mɛ.

english_verse: The woman you saw is the great city that rules over all kings on earth.
translation_verse: Adzɛ gɛ ɔmɔɔ, yɛenu ɔma gbagbla gɛ lɔŋã bukusi nɛ kesiakpa bekusi nabha.”

english_verse: I saw another angel come from heaven. This one had great power, and the earth was bright because of his glory.
translation_verse: Nɛ kɛlɛ niedeɛ ɛmɔ axɔ̃ɛdɛ bhlatɔ gɛ ezhi ede ititíɛ àba. Kabha ŋaŋã ohumi ɔzã yɛahɔɛ, kɛlɛ yɛ tɛdadalɛ kɔ ɔsɛ kesiakpa adalɛ.

english_verse: The angel shouted, “Fallen! Powerful Babylon has fallen and is now the home of demons. It is the den of every filthy spirit and of all unclean birds, and every dirty and hated animal.
translation_verse: Akpɛ ozĩ gagalĩ asɛ, “Ayɛ mɔo! Babilonia hohoeɛ ayɛmɔo! Etsyina edzi ɔma ɔkɔ ɛfɔfuĩa zizĩ nɛ̀ okpukpũ ɔkɔ, ɔkɔ ɛfɔfuĩa zizĩ ka nyia ka edzì ɔzãkɔ ɔkɔ bagã nɛ̀ bɔzhɔɛ gɛ batɛtã okpũkɔ.

english_verse: Babylon's evil and immoral wine has made all nations drunk. Every king on earth has slept with her, and every merchant on earth is rich because of her evil desires.”
translation_verse: Ɔmɔsɛ amadiɛ pete awɔ̃ɛ yɛunuɔ ebuku yɛegugù unuɔ gɛ amɔ edenɔ yɛɔmamɛ dɛdɛ mɛ. Kesiakpa abha ekusiɛ awɔ̃sɔ nɛ̀ adzɛ alɛ, kɛlɛ kesiakpa abha edzinigã bedzi ehoeyielete ɔgana yɛehoeyiudɔ̃ kpekpèɛ gɛzɛ pɔɖiɛ mɛ.”

english_verse: Then I heard another voice from heaven shout, “My people, you must escape from Babylon. Don't take part in her sins and share her punishment.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ izilũ ozĩ odenɔ ititíɛ gɛɔɖɔ ɔsɛ, “Nokoeyi nɛ yɛ mɛ, mɛeyi alɛ mɔ sɛ tɛ notɛ bodzi ebui kpa abhɛbhɛtɛelete asɛ yɛ ana, sɛ tɛ yɛɔtɔ̀ kpa nanà tɛbàba ɔna abha,

english_verse: Her sins are piled as high as heaven. God has remembered the evil she has done.
translation_verse: ɔmɔsɛ yɛebui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ ewu adɛtɔ̃ ititíɛ, kɛlɛ Owusoso Akaa abakà yɛ ɛtakpumɛ gagalĩɛ.

english_verse: Treat her as she has treated others. Make her pay double for what she has done. Make her drink twice as much of what she mixed for others.
translation_verse: Nɔbhɛtɛ ɔkɔɛ asɛ ɛbhɛtɛ gɛ abhɛtɛ akɔ ɔnɔɔ, nɔbuia ɛbhɛ kɛlɛ edi taabha ɔkpɛ yɛyɛ́ nulũ ɔkɔɛ. Noshì kayintse nɔ gɛ eshi akɔ ɔnɔɔ, edi taabha ɔkpɛ bɔlɛulũ ɔkɔɛ.

english_verse: That woman honored herself with a life of luxury. Reward her now with suffering and pain. “Deep in her heart Babylon said, ‘I am the queen! Never will I be a widow or know what it means to be sad.’
translation_verse: Nɔhɔhɔli piapii tɛ amɔ ɔwɔ̃nyɔnɔ nɛ yɛishu dadalɛ ɔdɔ̃ nɛ̀ ehoeyiudɔ̃ gɛ abhɛtɛ akɔ yɛishuɔ. Efeke ishu nɛ yɛitúɔ mɛ, ‘Ɛzã esi asɛ adzɛekusi ana, itinu avɛvɛ yɛedzini, kɛlɛ yoyo bibí tɛbàba mamɛ oɖi.’

english_verse: And so, in a single day she will suffer the pain of sorrow, hunger, and death. Fire will destroy her dead body, because her judge is the powerful Lord God.”
translation_verse: Klisoɔ bɔtɔ zizĩ babà yɛabha kiwui tili, gɛinu butsí, yoyò bibi nɛ̀ bɔbhɛ. Kifu bapɔɛ yɛ, ɔmɔsɛ ohumielete yɛenu Apalete Owusoso Akaa gɛ ekulu ebhu nii.

english_verse: Every king on earth who slept with her and shared in her luxury will mourn. They will weep, when they see the smoke from that fire.
translation_verse: “Onupɛ kesiakpa abha ekusiɛ gɛ baza wɔ̃sɔ nɛ̀ adzɛ alɛ pɛ baza amɔ same nii amɔ ɔnyɔ̃ gɛ ode okoeyi gɛ onupɛ atãa, bebèbi yoyo pɛ babàmɔ ɔwɔ̃nyɔnɔ nɛ yɛtakpu.

english_verse: Her sufferings will frighten them, and they will stand at a distance and say, “Pity that great and powerful city! Pity Babylon! In a single hour her judgment has come.”
translation_verse: Gɛ kifù badɛ alɛ nɛ yɛɔtɔ̀ shu soɔ, babazã itsí pɛ bebèebi yoyò akɔɛ. “ ‘Kɛba gagalĩ! Kɛba gagalĩ, ɔkɔ wɔ ɔma hohoe, Ɔma hohoe Babilon gɛ ohumi ɔzã ɔlɛ ahɔɛ! Nɛ ɔɖa tatá tonikpo mɛ koɔ kɛlɛ bagbã wɔakpɛ!’

english_verse: Every merchant on earth will mourn, because there is no one to buy their goods.
translation_verse: “Kesiakpa abha edzinigã ebebi yoyò nɛ̀ ɔwɔ̃nyɔnɔ ɔmɔsɛ anɔ nanà etikedzi balɛ ɔdɔ̃ dɛdɛɛ,

english_verse: There won't be anyone to buy their gold, silver, jewels, pearls, fine linen, purple cloth, silk, scarlet cloth, sweet-smelling wood, fancy carvings of ivory and wood, as well as things made of bronze, iron, or marble.
translation_verse: kedzi gagã ɔdɔ̃ gɛinu sikã, sikãfufu, aklagbu gɛ ayɛkɛ edzì nɛ̀ bɔdɔ̃ gbangbã, aklalɛ kplɔkpɔlɔ nɛ̀ blɔɔ apɔtɛ, apɔtɛ gɛ ayɛkɛ edzi, silikì nɛ̀ apɔtɛ dzadzã. Bɔdɔ̃ bhlatɔ tsyɛ nu besi gɛ alɔ̃ huhuhù edi edi, nɛ̀ bɔdɔ̃ gɛ balɔkɔ kifutu mɛenyi yɛ onotsó afatɛ nɔ, isí gɛ lɛ yɛkɛ edzi, ɔɖa dzadzã nɛ̀ ɔɖa tutũ nɛ̀ aklagbuɔ gɛ ayɛkɛ edzí,

english_verse: No one will buy their cinnamon, spices, incense, myrrh, frankincense, wine, olive oil, fine flour, wheat, cattle, sheep, horses, chariots, slaves, and other humans.
translation_verse: etuku gɛenu osí huhuhù toli gɛ bekulu asɛ sinamɔn nɛ̀ babɔ̀ huhuhù gɛ basara, pepere, kilifi nɛ̀ ahaliwɔɛ, wain nɛ̀ bɔbɔ́ ŋaŋã, bɔna hɔhɔ nɛ̀ agɔlɛ, banɛtsyɔɛ nɛ̀ ibhoshi, basɔɔ nɛ̀ bapakahu, ɔlɔkɔ plɛnɔ banɔdzyrame nɛ̀ balɛ atɔwuiɛ.

english_verse: Babylon, the things your heart desired have all escaped from you. Every luxury and all your glory will be lost forever. You will never get them back.
translation_verse: “Bedzinigã abaɖɔ asɛ, ‘Beesi eyuyuɔ gɛ ɔdɛmɛ nɔ koeyì nɛ wɔahɔɛ. Wɔehoeyi udɔ̃ pete nɛ̀ kana gɛ otséɛ ɔyala, gɛ ɔzɛɔmɔ ɔlɛ oɖi.’

english_verse: The merchants had become rich because of her. But when they saw her sufferings, they were terrified. They stood at a distance, crying and mourning.
translation_verse: Bedzinigã gɛ bazadɛ bɔdɔ̃ ɔlɛ pɛ bazamɔ ehoeyiudɔ̃ edenɔ adzɛ ɔkɔɔ, babazã kitsí, nɛifusisí mɛ ɔkɔ yɛatɔ̃ pɔpɔ̃ɔ. Bebèbi yoyò pɛ babà nyɔnɔ awɔ̃.

english_verse: Then they shouted, “Pity the great city of Babylon! She dressed in fine linen and wore purple and scarlet cloth. She had jewelry made of gold and precious stones and pearls.
translation_verse: kɛlɛ babà kpɛ ozĩ asɛ, “ ‘Kɛba gagalĩ! Kɛba gagalĩ, Wɔ ɔma hohoe, wɔ gɛ ɔɔzã apɔtɛ kplɔkpɔlɔ, apɔtɛ gɛ ayɛkɛ edzi nɛ̀ apɔtɛ dzadzã mɛ, pɛ ozotsé nɛ sikãdɔ̃ nɛ̀ aklagbu gɛ ayɛkɛ edzi nɛ̀ bakukɔli gbangbã mɛɛ!

english_verse: Yet in a single hour her riches disappeared.” Every ship captain and passenger and sailor stood at a distance, together with everyone who does business by traveling on the sea.
translation_verse: Nɛ ɔɖa tatá tonikpo mɛɛ, ehoeyiudɔ̃ ɛlɛ nyɔ̃ɔ zĩ!’ “Kelĩabha ihu anɔ gbagbla, bagɛ azazã ɛlɛ mɛ nɛ̀ bagɛ bezege ɛlɛ nɛ̀ banunɔ nyɔ̃ɔ gɛ azamɔ alɛ ehoeyiudɔ̃ edenɔ aƒu nabha, abàzã itsí.

english_verse: When they saw the smoke from her fire, they shouted, “This was the greatest city ever!”
translation_verse: Onupɛ bamɔ ɔnyɔ̃ gɛ okoeyi nɛ ɔma gɛ ɔtã mɛɛ, bebee bisi asɛ, ‘Ɔma hohoe toli ɔtɛzã asɛ ɔma hohoe ɔlɛ nɛidi oɖi dza?’

english_verse: They cried loudly, and in their sorrow they threw dust on their heads, as they said, “Pity the great city of Babylon! Everyone who sailed the seas became rich from her treasures. But in a single hour the city was destroyed.
translation_verse: Babà yalɛ osubhududu awɔ̃anya akpɛ atakpuɔ mɛ kɛlɛ nɛ̀ yoyò bibí nɛ̀ ɔwɔ̃nyɔnɔ mɛɛ, babàkpɛ ozĩ gagalĩ asɛ, “ ‘Kɛba gagalĩ! Kɛba gagalĩ, O ɔma hohoeɛ, ɛkɛlɛ gɛ banɔ gelee ahɔɛ gɛ kelĩabha ihu zã nɛ aƒu nabha bamɔ ehoeyiudɔ̃ edenɔ yɛ ehoeyiudɔ̃ mɔmɔ mɛɛ! Nɛ ɔɖa tatá tonikpo mɛ koɔ yɛka nyia ka zĩ!

english_verse: The heavens should be happy with God's people and apostles and prophets. God has punished her for them.”
translation_verse: O kititíanɛ, nɔmɔ same nɛ Babilonia tɛyɛyɛ nɔ shu! Nɔmɔ samɛ banɔ dadalɛ, nɛ̀ banɔ kpɛkpɛ nɛ̀ ebuiɖɔluanɛ! Owusoso Akaa ekulu kebhu nii nɛ kana gɛ awɔa ɔfɔ nɛ ɔnɔ.’ ”

english_verse: A powerful angel then picked up a huge stone and threw it into the sea. The angel said, “This is how the great city of Babylon will be thrown down, never to rise again.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ axɔ̃ɛdɛ ohumielete teli alɔkɔ kɛklagbu hohoe tili gɛ kɛlɛ asɛ bɔhɔ hohoe tuli anaa, awɔanyã atɛ́ akpɛ aƒuɔ mɛ, pɛ aɖɔ asɛ, “Gana agbagbà ibui tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ ɛlɛ nɛidi mɛɛ Babà wɔanyã Babilonia ɔma hohoeɛ atɛ́ ehu esi, gɛ baza amɔ ɔlɛ eɖi.

english_verse: The music of harps and singers and of flutes and trumpets will no longer be heard. No workers will ever set up shop in that city, and the sound of grinding grain will be silenced forever.
translation_verse: Ɛdzɛ gɛ sangũelete, ɛdzɛ afafatɛelete, babe elete nɛ̀ buxoele abɔbɔ̀ azabɔɔ, bezelũ ɛlɛ odenɔ wɔ mɛ eɖi ɖa. Ahɔɛmɛ axɔ̃ɛwɔa nanà azawɔa ɛxɔ̃ɛ bazamɔ alɛ nɛ wɔ mɛ eɖi ɖa. Bɔdɔ̃ hɔhɔ gɛ bezelũɔ bezelũ nɛ wɔ mɛ eɖi ɖa.

english_verse: Lamps will no longer shine anywhere in Babylon, and couples will never again say wedding vows there. Her merchants ruled the earth, and by her witchcraft she fooled all nations.
translation_verse: Kani mɛifu zatã nɛ wɔ mɛ oɖi. Adzɛenyine nɛ̀ anyɛedzini aɖɔ bezelũ alɛ nɛ wɔ mɛ oɖi. Wɔedzinigã balɛ edzì kanɔ nɔ̀ mɛ anɔ gbagbla. Gana wɔ ihui ebui bhɛbhɛtɛ mɛɛ, ɔgbana ɛmadiɛ pete ɔtsrakɛ idzo.

english_verse: On the streets of Babylon is found the blood of God's people and of his prophets, and everyone else.”
translation_verse: Nɛ yɛ mɛɛ, bamɔ ebuiɖɔluanɛ nɛ̀ banɔ dadalɛ dzyosu nɔ, nɛ̀ banunɔ nyɔ̃ɔ gɛ beyí nɛ esiakpa nabha.”

english_verse: After this, I heard what sounded like a lot of voices in heaven, and they were shouting, “Praise the Lord! To our God belongs the glorious power to save,
translation_verse: Nɛ kɛlɛ niedeɛ, ilũ ozĩ hohoe toli gɛ ɔmɔ asɛ banɔɛza gbagbla tili odenɔ kititíɛ gɛ baakpɛ ɔlɛ asɛ, “Nɔkpɛ yɛinye nɛitití! Tibubu, ishu dadalɛ nɛ̀ ohumi onu bɔlɔ Owusoso Akaa ɔyɛ,

english_verse: because his judgments are honest and fair. That filthy prostitute ruined the earth with shameful deeds. But God has judged her and made her pay the price for murdering his servants.”
translation_verse: yɛebhu kukulu nɛ̀ ebui mɛ bhui bhui lɛ alɛ ɔzãkɔ. Ebu kedulũ azazã gbagbla nepɔ gɛ ezĩ esiakpa nɛ̀ yɛedulũ zazã axɔ̃ɛ. Ebisi yɛaxɔ̃ɛdɛ dzyosu nɔ otití odenɔ yɛɔkɔ.”

english_verse: Then the crowd shouted, “Praise the Lord! Smoke will never stop rising from her burning body.”
translation_verse: Bazakpɛ ozĩ bɔbhalà asɛ, “Nɔkpɛ yɛinye nɛitití! Ɔnyɔ̃ gɛ okoeyi ode adzɛ mɛɛ, obowu abha tihɔ, tihɔ.”

english_verse: From the throne a voice said, “If you worship and fear our God, give praise to him, no matter who you are.”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ ozĩ oyi odenɔ ekusiegbuɔ shu ɔsɛ, “Nɔkpɛ bɔlɔ Owusoso Akaa nɛinye nɛitití, wɔnɔ yɛaxɔ̃ɛdɛ nyɔ̃ɔ, wɔnɔ agɛ osi yɛifuɔ bunuvɔɛ nɛ̀ esheshè!”

english_verse: Then I heard what seemed to be a large crowd that sounded like a roaring flood and loud thunder all mixed together. They were saying, “Praise the Lord! Our Lord God All-Powerful now rules as king.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ ilũ ozĩ gɛ oyi asɛ banɔɛza kpekpè tili ɔyɛ ana, nɛ̀ asɛ ɔtsɛtsɛ toli gɛ ɔtsɛ, nɛ̀ subha gɛ azalɛ́ɛ nɔ, aɖɔ asɛ, “Nɔkpɛ yɛinye nɛitití! Ɔmɔsɛ bɔlɔ Apalete Owusoso Akaa Ohumielete yɛaŋã ukusiɛ.

english_verse: So we will be glad and happy and give him praise. The wedding day of the Lamb is here, and his bride is ready.
translation_verse: Nɔkɔ bɔkpɛ same tɛ bɔmɔ same tɛ bɔkɔɛ ishu dadalɛ! Ɔmɔsɛ Obhoshieyiɛ adzɛ gbagbana ɛbeɛ na, kɛlɛ adzɛ gɛ akpɛ uluwɔ̃a eluɔ, etiwoese yɛishu ebeke.

english_verse: She will be given a wedding dress made of pure and shining linen. This linen stands for the good things God's people have done.”
translation_verse: Balɔkɔ aklalɛ kplɔkpɔlɔ gɛ etse pɛ yɛamɛ ebhuiɛ, balɔkɔ akɔɛ asɛ tɛ akpɛ.”

english_verse: Then the angel told me, “Put this in writing. God will bless everyone who is invited to the wedding feast of the Lamb.” The angel also said, “These things God has said are true.”
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ axɔ̃ɛdɛ etseɖe mɛasɛ, “Fɔnɔ olu ɔsɛ, ‘Beyula akɔ bagɛ bekulu alɛ amana Obhoshieyiɛ adzɛ gbagbana ɔwlɔago ɔdɔ̃ ɔŋãkɔɔ!’ ” Kɛlɛ aɖɔ aplɛnɔ asɛ, “Owusoso Akaa, yɛolugbũ mɛ ebuiɛ gɛenu ɛgbã nu alɛ.”

english_verse: I knelt at the feet of the angel and began to worship him. But the angel said, “Don't do that! I am a servant, just like you and everyone else who tells about Jesus. Don't worship anyone but God. Everyone who tells about Jesus does it by the power of the Spirit.” The Rider on the White Horse
translation_verse: Nɛ yɛibui ɛlɛ shuɔ, idzu ekluisi esi sɛ tɛ mɛkpɛ ɛtakpu esi kɔɛ, pɔ etseɖe mɛasɛ, “Tɛbhɛtɛ ibui ɛlɛ! Wɔhagbo axɔ̃ɛdɛ mɛinu plɛnɔ wɔ, ɔlɔkɔ ɔplɛnɔ wɔadzya alɛ gɛ baɖɔaɖɔ adalɛ Yesu shuɔ. Kpɛ ɛtakpu esi ɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa! Ɔmɔsɛ ebuiɛ gɛ baɖɔ adalɛ Yesu shuɔ alɛ enu ɔfɔfuĩa mɛ ebui gɛ baɖɔ eluɔ.” Anɔ Nɔ̀ Gɛ Azã Ɛsɔ Fufu Nabha

english_verse: I looked and saw heaven was open, and a white horse was there. Its rider was called Faithful and True, and he is always fair when he judges or goes to war.
translation_verse: Ɛmɔ ititíɛ dalɛ zã kɛlɛ ɛmɔ ɛsɔ fufu teli, gɛ bekulu anunɔ gɛ azã yɛabha asɛ, Kibui gɛlɛ ɛlɛ ɔzãkɔelete nɛ̀ Kibui gbã elete. Alɔkɔ bamɛ maɖi maɖi ekulu ebhu pɛ atà eyu nɔ.

english_verse: He had eyes like flames of fire, and he was wearing a lot of crowns. His name was written on him, but he was the only one who knew what the name meant.
translation_verse: Yɛawuhũɔ alɛ asɛ kifu ɔɖɛklɛmɛ ana, kɛlɛ bukusi ekutu gelee alɛ yɛtakpuɔ. Bafɔnɔ kinye nɛishu gɛ anɔ nanà etinyi, pɔ yɛ ete ko kɛlɛ̀ tititì mɛ ɔkɔ.

english_verse: The rider wore a robe that was covered with blood, and he was known as “The Word of God.”
translation_verse: Akpɛ akpɛkpɛ dzlolo teli gɛ batɔɛ nɛ dzyosu mɛ, kɛlɛ bekulu yɛasɛ Owusoso Akaa Yɛibui.

english_verse: He was followed by armies from heaven that rode on white horses and were dressed in pure white linen.
translation_verse: Kitití uyuaza azã basɔ fufu abha nɛ yɛede. Bakpɛ aklalɛ kplɔkpɔlɔ, gɛ efuli pɛ yɛishu dalɛ piapii nɔ.

english_verse: From his mouth a sharp sword went out to attack the nations. He will rule them with an iron rod and will show the fierce anger of God All-Powerful by trampling the grapes in the pit where wine is made.
translation_verse: Bɛtɛ̀ ŋaŋã teli edekoeyì nɛ yɛolugbũɔ mɛ, gɛ abalɔkɔ eté ɛmadiɛ nɔ. “Abaŋã ukusi nɛ alɛ abha nɛ̀ ɔɖaɛshi.” Alɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa ɔmɔpɔɛ kpɛkpɛ, agã alɛ abha asɛ ɛgɛ gɛ bagã waian eyuyu abha nɛ yɛɔkãkɔ ana.

english_verse: On the part of the robe that covered his thigh was written, “KING OF KINGS AND Lord OF LordS.”
translation_verse: Bafɔnɔ kinye ɛlɛ nɛ yɛakpɛkpɛ dzlolo nɛ̀ ɛtsrɛta nabha asɛ, BEKUSI ABHA EKUSI NƐ̀ BAPALETE ABHA APALETE.

english_verse: I then saw an angel standing on the sun, and he shouted to all the birds flying in the sky, “Come and join in God's great feast!
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ ɛmɔ axɔ̃ɛdɛ teli yɛalɔɛtití nɛ kiwi nabha. Akpɛ ozĩ gagalĩ etseɖe bɔzhɔɛ nɔ nɛ kɛlɛ́ nɔ mɛ asɛ, “Nobo shogò ɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa yɛɔwlɔago ɔdɔ̃ ŋaŋã hohoeɛ,

english_verse: You can eat the flesh of kings, rulers, leaders, horses, riders, free people, slaves, important people, and everyone else.”
translation_verse: sɛ tɛ nɔbɔtã bekusi, buyuaza lũazazãnɔ gbagbla nɛ̀ ohumi anɔ gbagbla, basɔ nɛ̀ bagɛ azã alɛ abha, nɛ̀ banɔ edi edì, banɔ gɛ alɛ ishu ahɔɛ mɛ nɛ̀ banɔ dzidzi, bunuvɔɛ nɛ̀ besheshè ɔdzya.”

english_verse: I also saw the beast and all kings of the earth come together. They fought against the rider on the white horse and against his army.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ ɛmɔ agãhoeɛ nɛ̀ esiakpa abha ekusiɛ nɛ̀ balɛ buyuaza gɛ eshogò asɛ tɛ batá eyu nɛ̀ anunɔ gɛ etsiɖi ɛsɔ nabhaa, nɛ̀ yɛeyuiza.

english_verse: But the beast was captured and so was the false prophet. This is the same prophet who had worked miracles for the beast, so he could fool everyone who had the mark of the beast and worshiped the idol. The beast and the false prophet were thrown alive into a lake of burning sulfur.
translation_verse: Pɔ bevũ agãhoeɛ, nɛ̀ yɛasɛnɛ ebuiɖɔluanɔ gɛ azabhɛtɛ atsyindzye ebui akasɛ nɛ yɛinye mɛɛ. Bɔdɔ̃ bubú kasɛ ɛlɛ yɛalɔkɔ awɔɛ bagɛ ayɛkɛ agãhoe ɛná tsatsà, pɛ bazakpɛ ɛtakpu esi akɔ yɛafafatɛɛ. Balɔkɔ banɔ alɛ enunuɔ taabha atɛ́ akpɛ aklagbuifuɔ, gɛtã nɛ̀ ɔɖɛklɛmɛ nɔ mɛ.

english_verse: But the rest of their army was killed by the sword that came from the mouth of the rider on the horse. Then birds stuffed themselves on the dead bodies.
translation_verse: Bagɔgɔɛ nɔ xɔ̃nɔ beyí alɛ nɛ̀ bɛtɛ̀ɛ gɛ ekoeyì ede anunɔ gɛ azã ɛsɔ nabha olugbũɔ mɛ, kɛlɛ bɔzhɔɛ nɔ petee batsyuĩ alɛ ɔdzya aŋã.

english_verse: I saw thrones, and sitting on those thrones were the ones who had been given the right to judge. I also saw the souls of the people who had their heads cut off because they had told about Jesus and preached God's message. They were the same ones who had not worshiped the beast or the idol, and they had refused to let its mark be put on their foreheads or hands. They will come to life and rule with Christ for
translation_verse: Ɛmɔ bagɛ bakɔ alɛidzo asɛ tɛ bekulu ubhuɔ bazã beekusi ugbu abha. Kɛlɛ ɛmɔ bagɛ alɛ atakpu bebhuiɛ atɔwuiɛ, bagɛ bazaɖɔaɖɔ adalɛ Yesu nɛ̀ Owusoso Akaa yɛibuiɛ shu soɔ. Bagɛ kpɛ ɛtakpu esi akɔ agãhoeɛ alo yɛafafatɛ, kɛlɛ bagɛ yɛkɛ yɛná tsatsà nɛ butúɔ alo ɛhɔɛ tsyɛ. Bazã nɛ̀ ɛkpã, kɛlɛ baŋã ukusi aplɛnɔ Kristo agɔ ohotu zɛzɛ̀.

english_verse: These people are the first to be raised to life, and they are especially blessed and holy. The second death has no power over them. They will be priests for God and Christ and will rule with them for
translation_verse: Tiyuyulà nɛ̀ tɛdadalɛ ɔkɔ bagɛ amɔ tɛlɔlɔ́ gɛiputɔ ɔlɛ. Butsí bhalà tɛbamɔ ohumi nɛ alɛ abha, pɔ bebèdzi Owusoso Akaa nɛ̀ Kristo balɛ anɔlũazazã, kɛlɛ babàŋã ukusi aplɛnii agɔ ohotu zɛzɛ̀. Beté Satana

english_verse: At the end of the
translation_verse: Onupɛ agɔ ohotu zɛzɛ̀ ɔbɔfalaa, bebèbu Satana odenɔ yɛɔɖavũɔ mɛ

english_verse: He will fool the countries of Gog and Magog, which are at the far ends of the earth, and their people will follow him into battle. They will have as many followers as there are grains of sand along the beach,
translation_verse: kɛlɛ azabavɛ adɛwɔɛ ɛmadiɛ nɛ kesiakpa idekrekpu tɛlɛ nabha, gɛenu Gɔg nɛ̀ Magɔg tɛ edi shogò alɛ akɔ keyu tatá. Balɛ tikpekpè mɛ lɛ asɛ aƒutɔ̃ ayisɛ ana.

english_verse: and they will march all the way across the earth. They will surround the camp of God's people and the city God loves. But fire will come down from heaven and destroy the whole army.
translation_verse: Batsà ɛha afala nɛ kanɔ nɔ̀ mɛ, kɛlɛ bebu kɛma akpɛ Owusoso Akaa anɔ dadalɛ nɛ̀ ɔma hohoeɛ gɛ adɛmɛ nɔ. Pɔ kifù wɔlɛ ide kititíɛ pɛ kɛpɔɛ alɛ.

english_verse: Then the devil who fooled them will be thrown into the lake of fire and burning sulfur. He will be there with the beast and the false prophet, and they will be in pain day and night forever and ever. The Judgment at the Great White Throne
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ balɔkɔ abosamuɔ gɛ awɔɛ alɛ, awɔanyã atɛ́ akpɛ aklagbuifuɔ gɛ ɛtã mɛ, nɛ ɛkɛlɛ gɛ batɛ́ɛ agãhoeɛ nɛ̀ asɛnɛ ebuiɖɔluanunɔ akpɛ. Babà hɔhɔlɛ alɛ elishi nɛ̀ emuze ɔvɛnɔ tihɔ, tihɔ. Kebhu Gɔgɔɛ Kukulu Nɛ̀ Bekpuɔ

english_verse: I saw a great white throne with someone sitting on it. Earth and heaven tried to run away, but there was no place for them to go.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ ɛmɔ ekusiegbu hohoe fufú tieli, nɛ̀ anunɔ gɛ azã alɛ abhaa. Kesiakpa nɛ̀ kitití shɛ̃ nɛ yɛamɛ, kɛlɛ ɔzãkɔ nanà ɔgɛ ɔzã ɔkɔ alɛ.

english_verse: I also saw all the dead people standing in front of that throne. Every one of them was there, no matter who they had once been. Several books were opened, and then the book of life was opened. The dead were judged by what those books said they had done.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ ɛmɔ banɔ tsitsí, besheshè nɛ̀ bunuvɔɛ gɛ bazã itití nɛ ekusiegbuɔ otugbà, kɛlɛ badalɛ uplukpa mɛ elu. Baza dalɛ eplukpa bhlatɔ tsyɛ gɛenu ɛkpã eplukpa. Bekulu ebhu nɛ̀ banɔ tsitsíɛ nɛ kɛzã gɛ bazã nɛ ɛkpã, pɛ bafɔnɔ elu nɛ buplukpa mɛ nulũ.

english_verse: The sea gave up the dead people who were in it, and death and its kingdom also gave up their dead. Then everyone was judged by what they had done.
translation_verse: Aƒu ebu bagɛ betsí nɛ yɛmɛ amana, kɛlɛ butsí nɛ̀ bekpuɔ ɔzãkɔ tsyɛ obu banɔ tsitsíɛ gɛ balɛ alɛ mɛɛ amana. Kɛlɛ bekulu ebhu akɔ anɔ nyia anɔ nɛ kɛgɛ yɛabhɛtɛ nulũ.

english_verse: Afterwards, death and its kingdom were thrown into the lake of fire. This is the second death.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ balɔkɔ butsí nɛ̀ bekpuɔ ɔzãkɔ atɛ́ akpɛ kifu ɔɖɛklɛmɛ nɔ mɛ. Kifu ɔɖɛklɛmɛ nɔ ɔlɛ nu butsí bhalà.

english_verse: Anyone whose name wasn't written in the book of life was thrown into the lake of fire.
translation_verse: Onupɛ bágɛmɔ anɔ teli yɛinye sɛ bafɔnɔ ɛlɛ akpɛ ɛkpãeplukpa mɛɛ, balɔkɔ yɛatɛ́ akpɛ kifu ɔɖɛklɛmɛ nɔ mɛ.

english_verse: I saw a new heaven and a new earth. The first heaven and the first earth had disappeared, and so had the sea.
translation_verse: Kidegbaa ɛmɔ itití vɔvɔ nɛ̀ esiakpa vɔvɔ. Ɔmɔsɛ kititíɛ gɛiputɔ nɛ̀ esiakpa gɛeputɔ ulũ tɛba fala, kɛlɛ aƒu nanà tsyɛ agabazã.

english_verse: I heard a loud voice shout from the throne: God's home is now with his people. He will live with them, and they will be his own. Yes, God will make his home among his people.
translation_verse: Kɛlɛ ilũ ozĩ loyi gagalĩ ode ekusiegbuɔ shu ɔsɛ, “Otsi alɛ Owusoso Akaa ɔzãkɔ lɛ banɔdzyrame ishi, kɛlɛ abazã aplɛnɔ alɛ. Bebedzi yɛanɔ, kɛlɛ Owusoso Akaa abazã balɛ ɔbha, pɛ yɛebedzi alɛ Owusoso Akaa.

english_verse: He will wipe all tears from their eyes, and there will be no more death, suffering, crying, or pain. These things of the past are gone forever.
translation_verse: Ebetsuru ɛwɔ̃mɛ ka nyia ka nɛ alɛ amɛ. Butsí, ɔwɔ̃nyɔnɔ, yoyò bibi alo bɔtɔ nanà zɛbazã, ɔmɔsɛ ebui kukuɔ gɛazã ulũ tɛbafala.”

english_verse: Then the one sitting on the throne said: I am making everything new. Write down what I have said. My words are true and can be trusted.
translation_verse: Anɔ nɔ̀ gɛ azã ekusiegbu nabha aɖɔ asɛ, “Idzinɔ kɛka nyia ka bhɛtɛ ɛdɔ̃ vɔvɔ.!” Kɛlɛ azaɖɔ asɛ, “Fɔnɔ ɛlɛ olu, ɔmɔsɛ ebuiɛ alɛ àlɛ, alɛ ɔzãkɔ kɛlɛ lɛenu kɛgbã.”

english_verse: Everything is finished! I am Alpha and Omega, the beginning and the end. I will freely give water from the life-giving fountain to everyone who is thirsty.
translation_verse: Etseɖe mɛasɛ, “Kɛka nyia ka na. Mɛinu anɔ gɛ eputɔ, pɛ ɛka wlui nu Agɔgɔɛ nɔ tsyɛ, mɛinu Kesikpɛkpɛ nɛ̀ Ɔtɔ̃kɔɔ. Anunɔ gɛ olũwɔɛ oyi yɛ ɛbakɔ yɛulĩ yɛawɔɛ odenɔ bulĩɛ gɛ bukoeyi odenɔ ɛkpã bulĩ ozĩɛ mɛ, gɛ atɛ babuia ɔlɛ ɛbhɛ.

english_verse: All who win the victory will be given these blessings. I will be their God, and they will be my people.
translation_verse: Anunɔ gɛ aŋã abhaa, bɔdɔ̃ ɛlɛ nyɔ̃ɔ badzi yɛɔyɛ, kɛlɛ ɛbadzi yɛ Owusoso Akaa, kɛlɛ yɛtsyɛ ebedzi mɛeyi.

english_verse: But I will tell you what will happen to cowards and to everyone who is unfaithful or dirty-minded or who murders or is sexually immoral or uses witchcraft or worships idols or tells lies. They will be thrown into that lake of fire and burning sulfur. This is the second death. The New Jerusalem
translation_verse: Pɔ kifusisíelete, bagɛ agɛyɛkɛ aŋã, atsɔ̃bui abhɛbhɛtɛelete, bagɛ beyì anɔ, aglɔŋaŋãelete, behuinɛ, ibudielete nɛ̀ asɛnɛ sɛsɛelete peteɛ xɔ̃nɔɔ-balɛ ɔzãkɔ obodzi kifu gɛtã nɛ̀ aklagbuifu ɔɖɛklɛmɛ nɔ mɛ. Kɛlinu butsi bhalàa.”

english_verse: I saw one of the seven angels who had the bowls filled with the seven last terrible troubles. The angel came to me and said, “Come on! I will show you the one who will be the bride and wife of the Lamb.”
translation_verse: Baxɔ̃ɛdɛ tegegene gɛ bevunɔ buyintse tugegene gɛ buyuku nɛ̀ bɔtɔ gɔgɔ̃ɛ nɔ gɛ bàba, ɔmayɛmɛ tenikpo aba kɛlɛ etseɖe mɛasɛ, “Ba, ɛbakasɛ wɔ adzɛ gɛ bakpɛ buluwɔ̃a eluɔ, gɛenu Obhoshieyiɛ yɛedzini.”

english_verse: Then with the help of the Spirit, he took me to the top of a very high mountain. There he showed me the holy city of Jerusalem coming down from God in heaven.
translation_verse: Nɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa mɛɛ, efeke mɛ adɛ tɔɖɔ butu hohoe, ɖaɖama tuli abha, kɛlɛ ebu Ɔma Dadalɛ, gɛonu Yerusalem gɛode Owusoso Akaa ɔkɔ nɛititíɛ ozhiɛ akasɛ mɛ.

english_verse: The glory of God made the city bright. It was dazzling and crystal clear like a precious jasper stone.
translation_verse: Owusoso Akaa ishu dadalɛ zitse nɛ ɔlɛ mɛ, kɛlɛ ɔlɛ titsetseɛ zã ɔmɔ asɛ yaspa ɛklagbu, gɛ kɛlɛ mɛ dalɛ ɔmɔ asɛ ahuhɔ̃ɛ mɛ.

english_verse: The city had a high and thick wall with twelve gates, and each one of them was guarded by an angel. On each of the gates was written the name of one of the twelve tribes of Israel.
translation_verse: Babhɔ egli dodó, ɖaɖama akana ɔmaa, gɛ budulũ lɔfɔ nɛ̀ tɔɔbha zã alɛishu. Baxɔ̃ɛdɛ lɔfɔ nɛ̀ taabha azã budulũɔ. Nɛ budulũɔ shuɔ, bafɔnɔ Israel egbogbo lɔfɔ nɛ̀ taabha enye atɔɖɔ.

english_verse: Three of these gates were on the east, three were on the north, three more were on the south, and the other three were on the west.
translation_verse: Bebu budulũ tɔɔta nɛ kiwiɛ otukɔ nɛibe, tɔɔta lɛ piabhaɛbeɛ, tɔɔta lɛ pialɛ ibe kɛlɛ tɔɔta tsyɛ lɛ kiwiɛ ɔdɔkɔ nɛibe.

english_verse: The city was built on twelve foundation stones. On each of the stones was written the name of one of the Lamb's twelve apostles.
translation_verse: Buvũ esikpɛkpɛ egli edi lɔfɔ nɛ̀ taabha babhɔ akɔ ɔma egiliɛ. Kɛlɛ nɛ kɛka nyia aka abhaa, bafɔnɔ Obhoshieyiɛ yɛanɔ kpɛkpɛ lɔfɔ nɛ̀ taabha enyeɛ atɔɖɔ.

english_verse: The angel who spoke to me had a gold measuring stick to measure the city and its gates and its walls.
translation_verse: Axɔ̃ɛdɛ gɛ aɖɔaɖɔ nɛ mɛɛ, evunɔ sikãishi tili idi gɛ alɔkɔ edzyidzyi ɔmaa, ɔlɛ budulũɔ nɛ̀ egiliɛ nɔ.

english_verse: The city was shaped like a cube, because it was just as high as it was wide. When the angel measured the city, it was about
translation_verse: Ɔma gɛ bebhuiɛ, ɔlɛ tidzedze mɛ nɛ̀ ɔlɛ tɛnɛnɛ̀ɛ mɛ pete ashɔ̃. Axɔ̃ɛdɛ edzyidzyi ɔma ɔlɛ kɛwɔã zɛ̀zɛ nɛ̀ ɔɖɛishì nɔ, kɛlɛ ɔlɛ tidzedzeɛ mɛ lɛ asɛ ɛtsɛkɔ ohotu zɛzɛ̀ nɛ̀ akpã talɛ̃. Ɔma ɔlɛ tidzedzeɛ, nɛ̀ tɛnɛnɛ̀ mɛ̀ pete shɔ̃.

english_verse: Then the angel measured the wall, and by our measurements it was about
translation_verse: Axɔ̃ɛdɛ edzyidzyi egiliɛ tsyɛ kɛlɛ alɛ tɛɖaɖama mɛ zã asɛ bɔtsrɛkpɔ akpã taabha, kishi nɔ gɛ axɔ̃ɛdɛ alɔkɔ nu anɔdzyrame ɔdɔ tidzyi dzyidzyí ɔyɛ.

english_verse: The wall was built of jasper, and the city was made of pure gold, clear as crystal.
translation_verse: Balɔkɔ yaspa aklagbu abhɔ egiliɛ nɔ, kɛlɛ babhɔ ɔma nɛ̀ sikã gbangbã gɛ yɛmɛ adalɛ asɛ ahuhɔ̃ɛ ana.

english_verse: Each of the twelve foundations was a precious stone. The first was jasper, the second was sapphire, the third was agate, the fourth was emerald,
translation_verse: Balɔkɔ aklagbu gbangbã edi edi ewoese kesikpɛkpɛ egiliɛ nɔ. Kesikpɛkpɛ aklagbuɔ gɛeputɔ enu yaspa, tɛbhɔbhɔ tɔɖɔ ɔbhalà nu safir, atramɛ enu kalkedon, kɛlɛ alɛlɛmɛ enu emaragad,

english_verse: the fifth was onyx, the sixth was carnelian, the seventh was chrysolite, the eighth was beryl, the ninth was topaz, the tenth was chrysoprase, the eleventh was jacinth, and the twelfth was amethyst.
translation_verse: etrimiɛ enu sardonik, ehlomiɛ enu sardis, egegenemiɛ enu krisolit, asɛmiɛ enu beril, ezhitamiɛ enu topas, kɛfɔ̀miɛ nu krisopras, kɛfɔ̀ nɛ̀ toliɛ nu hiakink kɛlɛ kɛfɔ̀ nɛ̀ taabhaa nu ametist.

english_verse: Each of the twelve gates was a solid pearl. The streets of the city were made of pure gold, clear as crystal.
translation_verse: Balɔkɔ bɔdɔ̃ gɛitse ŋelĩŋelĩɛ ewoese ɔma ɔlɛ budulũ lɔfɔ nɛ̀ tɔɔbhaa nɔ. Sikã gbangbã adɛ gɛ ɔbɔlà ɔmɔ bɔdɔ̃ ɔgana yɛmɛ asɛ ahuhɔ̃ɛ anaa, yɛ balɔkɔ akpɛ ɔma tipɔ hohoeɛ nɔ.

english_verse: I did not see a temple there. The Lord God All-Powerful and the Lamb were its temple.
